treatment.title: Overview
treatment.description: A brain tumor is a growth of cells in the brain or near it. Brain tumors can happen in the brain tissue. Brain tumors also can happen near the brain tissue. Nearby locations include nerves, the pituitary gland, the pineal gland, and the membranes that cover the surface of the brain.

treatment.title: Types
treatment.description: *Gliomas and related brain tumors
*Choroid plexus tumors

treatment.title: Symptoms
treatment.description: The signs and symptoms of a brain tumor depend on the brain tumor's size and location. Symptoms also might depend on how fast the brain tumor is growing, which is also called the tumor grade. General signs and symptoms caused by brain tumors may include: i.Headache or pressure in the head that is worse in the morning. ii.Headaches that happen more often and seem more severe. iii.Headaches that are sometimes described as tension headaches or migraines.

treatment.title: Brain tumor headaches
treatment.description: Headaches are the most common symptom of brain tumors. Headaches happen in about half of people with brain tumors. Headaches can happen if a growing brain tumor presses on healthy cells around it. Or a brain tumor can cause swelling in the brain that increases pressure in the head and leads to a headache.

treatment.title: Brain tumor symptoms by location
treatment.description: The main part of the brain is called the cerebrum. Brain tumors in different parts of the cerebrum might cause different symptoms. 1.Brain tumors in the front of the brain. 2.Brain tumors in the middle of the brain. 3.Brain tumors in the back of the brain.

treatment.title: When to see a doctor
treatment.description: Make an appointment with your health care provider if you have persistent signs and symptoms that worry you.

treatment.title: Lifestyle Changes
treatment.description: Adopting a heart-healthy diet, regular exercise, quitting smoking, and reducing alcohol intake can help manage CAD and improve overall cardiovascular health.

treatment.title: Medications
treatment.description: Drugs like statins to lower cholesterol, beta-blockers to reduce blood pressure, and aspirin to prevent blood clots can be prescribed to manage CAD symptoms and prevent complications.

treatment.title: Angioplasty and Stent Placement
treatment.description: A procedure where a balloon is used to open up blocked arteries, followed by the placement of a stent to keep the artery open, improving blood flow to the heart.

treatment.title: Medications
treatment.description: Diuretics to reduce fluid buildup, ACE inhibitors to lower blood pressure and reduce heart strain, and beta-blockers to slow the heart rate and reduce blood pressure.

treatment.title: Lifestyle Changes
treatment.description: Reducing salt intake, engaging in regular physical activity, managing stress, and monitoring fluid intake can help manage heart failure symptoms and improve quality of life.

treatment.title: Implantable Devices
treatment.description: Devices like pacemakers to help control abnormal heart rhythms or implantable cardioverter-defibrillators (ICDs) to prevent sudden cardiac death by correcting dangerous arrhythmias.

treatment.title: Medications
treatment.description: Antiarrhythmic drugs to control heart rate and rhythm, beta-blockers to slow the heart rate, and anticoagulants to prevent blood clots in patients with certain types of arrhythmias.

treatment.title: Electrical Cardioversion
treatment.description: A procedure where an electric shock is delivered to the heart to reset its rhythm, typically used for atrial fibrillation or other serious arrhythmias.

treatment.title: Catheter Ablation
treatment.description: A minimally invasive procedure where a catheter is used to destroy small areas of heart tissue that are causing abnormal electrical signals and arrhythmias.

treatment.title: Medications
treatment.description: ACE inhibitors to lower blood pressure, beta-blockers to reduce heart rate, and diuretics to remove excess fluid from the body, all aimed at improving heart function and symptoms.

treatment.title: Lifestyle Changes
treatment.description: Regular exercise, a healthy diet, avoiding alcohol and recreational drugs, and managing stress can help manage symptoms and slow the progression of cardiomyopathy.

treatment.title: Implantable Devices
treatment.description: Devices such as pacemakers to regulate heartbeats or ICDs to prevent sudden cardiac arrest can be used in severe cases of cardiomyopathy.

treatment.title: Medications
treatment.description: Diuretics to reduce fluid buildup, anticoagulants to prevent blood clots, and beta-blockers to manage blood pressure and heart rate, helping to alleviate symptoms of valve disease.

treatment.title: Lifestyle Changes
treatment.description: A heart-healthy diet, regular exercise, and avoiding smoking and excessive alcohol intake can help manage symptoms and prevent worsening of valve disease.

treatment.title: Valve Repair or Replacement
treatment.description: Surgical procedures to repair or replace damaged heart valves, improving blood flow and heart function. This can be done through traditional open-heart surgery or minimally invasive techniques.

Cough > 2months ± Hemoptysis PTB CXR : Apical cavitation. Hemoptysis, Clubbing, Weight loss Bronchial Ca Sudden onset, tracheal shift Pneumothorax Resonant hemithorax LS : Diminished breath sound / Silent chest CXR : Hyperlucent c̄ collapsed lung margin Fever, cough Pneumonia (Consolidation) & RTI ± pleuritic chest pain (pain with respiration) LS : Bronchial breath sound CXR : Consolidation / Inflammatory infiltration Sore throat ± Runny nose URTI Breathlessness with – Clear lungs with no added sound Acidosis (DKA) Uremia (CKD/ARF) GAD / HCR Always exclude EMERGENCY possibilities first : • Orthopnoca, Basal creps : Heart Failure. • Episodic, Specific triggers, early onset : Asthma. • Pushed trachea, hyper-resonant percussion : Pneumothorax.

treatment.title: Hospitalization criteria
treatment.description: Immediately refer ‘any SOB patient’ to higher center, advise hospitalization, never to treat in outdoor basis if : • Cannot complete sentences • Restless, agitated • SpO 2 • Respiratory rate (RR) : Adult : > 30/ min. Child : Fast breathing (in breaths/min) : ≥ 60; 2–11 months: ≥ 50; 1–5 years: ≥ 40. • Pulse (P) > 120/ min or, 160/min for infants) • PEF • Confusion / Drowsiness / Coma • Silent chest on auscultation • Cyanosis Therapies not recommended during acute SOB attack : • Sedatives • Antitussive drugs • Chest physiotherapy (may increase patient discomfort) • Hydration with large volumes of fluid (may be necessary for younger children and infants) • Antibiotics: If overt infection. • Antihistamines

treatment.title: Primary Skin Lesions
treatment.description: Primary Lesions / Lesion types 1. Macule (Spot / colour change) : a circumscribed flat area of skin of ≤ 1cm diameter that appears different from the skin surrounding it, usually because of different colour. e.g. freckle. 2. Patch : the same as a macule but larger. (up to 15-20 cm) 3. Papule : a discrete elevation of skin of ≤ 1cm diameter. (Size up to 0.5cm) 4. Nodule : similar to a papule but having significant depth (into dermis or even subcutaneous layer) and usually > 1 cm in diameter. 5. Tumours. 6. Plaque : a raised area of skin with a flat top, >1 cm in diameter. e.g. psoriasis. 7. Vesicle / Blister : a small ( ≤ 1 cm) fluid-filled blister. e.g. pemphigoid. 8. Bulla / Bullae : and a larger (> 1 cm in diameter) fluid-filled blister. e.g. pemphigoid. 9. Pustule / pimples : a visible accumulation of pus in a blister. 10. Cyst : Vesico-pustule / Papulo-pastule. 11. Weal / Hives : an evanescent discrete area of dermal oedema, often centrally white due to masking of local blood supply by fluid; weals can be papules, macules, patches and plaques, and are the hallmark of urticarial. e.g. a nettle sting. 12. Abscess : a localised collection of pus in a cavity. 13. Burrow : a linear or curvilinear papule, caused by a burrowing scabies mite. 14. Comedone : a plug of keratin and sebum in a dilated pilosebaceous orifice. 15. Papilloma : A projecting nipple like mass. e.g. skin tag. 16. Petechiae are pinhead-sized, flat macules of extravascular blood in the dermis; 17. Purpura a re larger and may be palpable; 18. Ecchymosis (‘bruise’) is the term used to describe bleeding that involves deeper structures. 19. Telangiectasia : visible dilatation of small cutaneous blood vessels. e.g. psoriasis.

treatment.title: Secondary Skin Lesions
treatment.description: Secondary lesions / Other terms : 1. Atrophy : an area of thin, translucent skin caused by loss of epidermis, dermis or subcutaneous fat, e.g. secondary to excess topical corticosteroids. 2. Crust / Scabs : dried exudate of blood or serous fluid. e.g. eczema. 3. Erosion : an area of skin denuded by complete or partial loss of the epidermis. 4. Excoriation / Scratch mark / Abrasion : a linear ulcer or erosion resulting from scratching. 5. Fissure / Cracks / Cleft : a slit-shaped deep ulcer, e.g. in irritant dermatitis of the hands. 6. Microscopic fissure / Chap / Chapping. 7. Scale / Exfoliation : a flake arising from the stratum corneum. Any condition with a thickened stratum corneum, i.e. hyperkeratosis, can cause scaling. An immature thickened and cohesive stratum corneum, e.g. as in psoriasis or Bowen’s disease, gives characteristic optical and physical properties that cause a different scale from that due to a thickened, but otherwise normal, stratum corneum. 8. Scar / Cicatrices : replacement of normal structures by fibrous tissue at the site of an injury. 9. Sinus : a cavity or channel that permits the escape of pus or fluid. 10. Stria : a linear, atrophic, pink, purple or white band caused by connective tissue changes. e.g. Cushing syndrome. 11. Ulcer : an area from which the epidermis and at least the upper part of the dermis have been lost. [Ref – Modified from, Davidson’s Medicine 24 th , Page 1211 & Andrews’ Diseases of the Skin, 12th , Page 11-14]

treatment.title: Pharmacology & Consideration in Dermatology
treatment.description: Mild potent : Hydrocortisone Acetate Topicort (sq) / Zocort (aci) / Cortider (skf) Desonide Desotop (aci) Moderately potent : Hydrocortisone Butyrate Eczacort (zis) Clobetasone Butyrate Ezex (sq) / Clobecort (ops) Triamcinolone Acetonide Trialon (drg) / Trimcort (che) / Aristocort (aris) Betamethasone Valerate Betaderm (incp) / Bet-A (acm) / Bantovet (dbl) Highly potent : Betamethasone Dipropionate Betameson (sq) / Betason (ops) Mometasone Furoate Soneta (aris) / Mometa (pop) / Momeson (incp) Super Potent : Clobetasol Propionate Exovate (bex) / Dermasol (sq) / Clovate (aci) / Xenovate (uni) / Topiclo (skf) / Nyclobate (incp) Halobetasol Propionate Halobet (sq) / Ulticort (incp) / Halocort (aci) / Haloxa (ops) Halcinonide Dermalog (jay) / Zemalog (gaco) General Principles of use of topical steroid : O Potent and super potent steroid should not be used for prolong period (maximum 2 – 3 weeks). O Super potent steroid should be avoid in case of children & over thin skin of adult person like eyelid, face, genitalia, mucous membrane, already atrophied skin. O For prolong use of steroid (eg. Eczema / LSC), potent/super potent steroid is followed by moderate to mild potent steroids. O For chronic inflammatory conditions like AD, Psoriasis, LP, steroid substitute should be used after considerable time period to prevent steroid induced side effects (Topical tacrolimus ointment or pimecrolimus cream). O For widespread lesions – use a moderate or mild potent steroid. O Lesions over scalp – use moderately potent or mild agents or lotion base. O For oral lesion – moderately potent steroid in an orabase / oral paste. O For secondarily infected lesions, combination with topical antibiotics is prudent. O Corticosteroids can be applied under occlusion to augment their potency. Wrape polyethelene around the lesion, after steroid application. O Intermittent use of potent topical steroids has not been associated with side effects and can be employed in steroid responsive dermatoses. (weekend therapy/ intermittent therapy). O Avoid abrupt discontinuation of potent/super potent topical steroids, rather taper to less potent steroid & then stop to prevent flare of the dermatoses. O Should not use super potent steroid when mild or mid topical steroid brings clearance of the lesions. O Additives preparation may be used to augment the potency of topical steroids like propylene glycol, urea, and salicylic acid. Clobetasol Propionate + Salicylic Acid. O For wide spread lesions and hairy parts of the body lotion, spray, foam formulations should be used. Topical Ratinoid : Tacrolimus : 0.03% & 0.1% Strength 0.03% used in child Sun screen : For outdoor sun Protection Factor (SPF) should be > 50 & within room (indoor) SPF SunMask Cream (sq) / Depiwhite.M SPF 50+ Lotion (uni). Anti-staphylococcal antibiotics of skin & soft tissue infections : Penicillins : Flucloxacillin, Cloxacillin, Amoxicillin + clavulanic. First generation cephalosporin : Cephapirin, Cefadroxil. Tetracyclines : Doxycycline, Minocycline. Macrolides : Clarithromycin. Lincosamide : Clindamycin (with anaerobic coverage). Oxazolidinone : Linezolid (with MRSA & VRSA coverage). Antibiotics with anaerobic coverage : clindamycin, penicillin with beta-lactamase inhibitor combination (amoxiclav), second-generation cephalosporins, carbapenems, and quinolones. Metronidazole has broad-spectrum anaerobic activity. When to refer to a dermatolgist : 1. When diagnosis is not established / doubtful. 2. Widespread lesions affecting job / social life & psychology adversely. 3. Dermatological emergencies : Toxic epidermal necrolysis and Stevens-Johnson syndrome (SJS), Eczema herpeticum, wide spread Erythroderma and Pemphigus vulgaris etc. 4. Failed topical treatment after 1 – 3 months. Normal cellular trunover of skin is 1 – 3 months. So any topical agent exerts it effect within 2 months. No patient should treat by an GP more than 3 months, if no improvement. 5. Co-existing / underlying connective tissue disorders must be referred to a rheumatologist. 6. Prescribung hazardous drugs : cytotoxic drugs etc. Regular follow up of liver function tests are monitored.

treatment.title: Acne : Grading, Features & Topicals
treatment.description: Acne vulgaris, is a long-term skin disease that occurs when hair follicles are clogged with dead skin cells and oil from the skin. Acne global grading system (GAGS) score : Face & trunk divided in 6 parts. Forehead, right cheek, left cheek, nose, chin & trunk (chest & back). Grading for each region lesions : Grade 1 : Comedones (Whitehead & Blackhead/Millia) Grade 2 : Papules, sometimes pustules (less than 5 in number). Grade 3 : Pustules (more than 5 in number). Grade 4 : Nodules & cysts. According to morphology : Mild acne : Comedone present, occasionally papule & pustule. ↓ Moderate acne : Papule & Pustule present. ↓ Severe acne : Nodular & Cystic lesion present. Clinical feature : 1. Adolescent & Female > Male. 2. Background is seen greasy with prominent follicular opening. 3. Eruption of papules, pustules, nodules and cysts, lesions heal with typical scaring. 4. Presence of comedones with typical distribution in face, upper part of the trunk (chest & back) and shoulder. Drugs Used in Acne A. Comedolytic Agents : ● Azelaic Acid 20 % / Azelec (acm) Cream ● Salicylic acid 2 % / Kerasol FCL lotion (incp) ● Benzoyl Peroxide 4 % / Oxigel Wash Topical gel (uni) / Benzac (drg) Cream. (safe in pregnancy) : Apply to clean skin initially once daily on alternate days then twice a day (or more) for 4- 6 weeks. B. Topical Retinoids : Anti proliferative, Anti Comedolytic & anti inflammatory effects but no anti-bacterial effect. ● Gel sotretinoin 0.05% / Retigel (uni) / Aknil (incp) ● Cap. Isotretinoin 10 or 20 mg/Retigel (uni)/Aknil (incp) ● Cream Tretinoin 0.025% / Cosmotrin (bex) / Ratin-A (syn) [less available] ● Cream Adapalene 0.1 % / 0.3 % / Fona (sq) / Adapel (hc) / Aclene (drg) / Adgar (aci) C. Topical Antibiotics : ● Clindamycin 1 % Lotion / Clindacin (incp) / Clindax (ops) / Lindamax (skf) Lotion, 2 – 3 times daily & kept at-least 3 hours for 6-12 weeks. D. Anti-androgen : Cryproterone, Spironolactone 50-100 mg daily.

treatment.title: Step wise treatment of acne
treatment.description: Step wise treatment of acne : Mild (Comedones ± Inflammation) : ► Adapalene 0.1% + Benzoyl Peroxide 2. 5 % / Fona plus (sq) / Acnegel (acm) / Freshlook (zis) / Adagel plus (uni), মুখ পরিষ্কার করে রাতে লাগাবেন এবং সকালে মুখ ধুয়ে নিবেন ------ ২ মাস ± ► Anti-acne soap (Zit bar or Acne bar) ---------- Continue, Clean face with this soap BD (keep 5 min & then wash out) ↓ Moderate (Papule/Pustule or failed Tx of mild acne) : ► Adapalene 0.1% + Benzoyl Peroxide 2.5% / Fona plus (sq) / Acnegel (acm) / Freshlook (zis) / Adagel plus (uni), মুখ পরিষ্কার করে রাতে লাগাবেন এবং সকালে মুখ ধুয়ে নিবেন ------ ২ মাস + ► Clindamycin 1 % Lotion / Clindacin (incp) / Clindax (ops) / Lindamax (skf) Lotion. দিনে ২ বার লাগাবেন ---------------- ২ মাস (কমপক্ষে ৩ ঘন্টার মধ্যে মুখ ধোয়া উচিত না) Severe acne (Nodule/Cystic lesion or failed topical treatment) : ► Adapalene 0.1% + Benzoyl Peroxide 2.5% / Fona plus (sq) / Acnegel (acm) / Freshlook (zis) / Adagel plus (uni), মুখ পরিষ্কার করে রাতে লাগাবেন এবং সকালে মুখ ধুয়ে নিবেন ---- ২ – ৩ মাস + ► Pulse Therapy : Tab. Azithromycin 500 mg / Zimax (sq) / Zithrin (ren) / Azimex (drg) 500 mg, সপ্তাহে পর পর তিনদিন খাবেন। যেমন – শুক্রবার, শনিবার, রবিবার। পরর্বতী শুক্রবারে আবার শুরু করবেন। একই নিয়মে খেতে থাকবেন। --- দু্‌ই থেকে ছয় মাস + Under specialist supervision : Adapalene causes less irritation, so may be given at morning also, but Isotretinoin causes more irritation, so it is used at night only. Any one – ± ► Cap. Isotretinoin 10 or, 20 mg / Reticap (uni) / Aknil (incp) / Roaccutane (roche) / Akno (drg), 1 + 0 + 1 –––––– 2 – 3 months. অথবা, ± ► Topical gel Isotretinoin 0.05% / Retigel (uni) / Aknil (incp), মুখ পরিষ্কার করে রাতে লাগাবেন এবং সকালে মুখ ধুয়ে নিবেন ------ ২ মাস কাউন্সিলিং : ● ব্রনের চিকি ৎ সা দীর্ঘমেয়াদী (নূন্যতম ৬ থেকে ১২ মাস প্রয়োজন)। প্রতি 2 মাস পর পর ফলোআপে আসা আবশ্যক। ওষুধের প্রতিক্রিয়া সন্তোষজনক হলে কমপক্ষে পরবর্তী ৪ থেকে ৬ মাস ওষুধ চালিয়ে যেতে হবে (Maintenance Therapy)। কিন্তু সন্তোষজনক না হলে ফলোআপে নতুন ওষুধ যুক্ত করা হয় এবং তার ২ মাস পরে আবার ফলোআপে আসতে বলা হয়। ● এই চিকি ৎ সাতে ধৈর্য্য রাখতে হবে, কারণ দৃশ্যমান ফলাফল পেতে অনেক সময় প্রয়োজন। এতে নূন্যতম ৬ থেকে ১২ মাস প্রয়োজন। ধৈর্য্যর সাথে নিয়মিত ঔষধ গ্রহণ ও উপদেশ না মানলে চিকি ৎ সা গ্রহণ কার্যকরী হয় না। পরামর্শ : ১। মুখের কোন প্রসাধনী ব্যবহার এড়িয়ে চলুন, চুলকানী বা চিমটি করা এড়িয়ে চলুন। ২। মুখে সাবান (Dove / Cosco Soap / Acne Bar / Zit bar) বা গ্লিসারিন বেইজড ফেসওয়াশ ব্যবহার করে বার বার মুখ পরিষ্কার করবেন । পরিষ্কার করে মুখে ঔষধ ব্যবহার করবেন। ৩। টপিকাল রেটিনয়েড বা অন্যান্য ক্রিম/জেল ব্যবহারের পর ত্বক শুষ্ক হলে ভ্যাসলিন / ইমোলিয়েন্টের মতো ময়েশ্চারাইজার ব্যবহার করুন। ৪। ব্যবস্থাপত্রের কোন ওষুধ ক্রিম বা লোশন মুখে লাগানোর পরে যদি মুখ অতিরিক্ত জ্বালাপোড়া করে বা সমস্যা মনে হয়, তবে দুই থেকে তিন ঘন্টা পরে তা ধুয়ে ফেলা যাবে এবং তা চিকিৎসকে জানাবেন। সাধারণত রেটিনয়েডগুলো মুখে সমস্যা করে কিন্তু তা অধিক কার্যকরী। ৫। ভাল অভ্যাস গড়ে তুলুন। যেমন : তাড়াতাড়ি ঘুমাতে যাবেন, পর্যাপ্ত ঘুমাবেন, কোষ্ঠকাঠিন্য মুক্ত থাকবেন, ফাস্টফুড ও চর্বিযুক্ত খাবার এড়িয়ে চলুন, প্রচুর পানি পান করুন (> ২.৫ লিটার/দিন)।

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: Ø In follow up of well responded cases, all the drugs should be omitted & continue only topical Adapalene ± Benzoyl Peroxide OD at night for next 3-6 months as maintenance therapy. Ø Oral antibiotic is indicated if nodules/cysts are present or, involves >75% of face. Used antibiotics (any one) Doxycycline, Minocycline, Azithromycin, Erythromycin. Doxycycline is the most cost effective but causes sunlight sensitivity, especially in summer. Alternative antibiotic : ● Cap. Doxycycline 100 mg / Doxicap (ren) / Doxin (ops) / Doxacil (sq) 100 mg, 1 + 0 + 0 ––––– 3 months. Or, ● Tab. Erythromycin 500 mg / Eromycin DS (sq) / Erosa (bio) / A-Mycin (aris) / Mycin (amb) / Erybac (aris) / Erocin (acm) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 –––––– 2 – 3 months. Ø Oral isotretinoin : If severe acne : Topical isotretinoin + systemic antibiotic + oral isotretinoin. Oral isotretinoin is a hazardous drug & is contra-indicated in pregnancy & breastfeeding. Dose (average 0.5mg/kg/day). (Check fasting lipid profile, SGPT before starting the drug. Stop two/three months before conceive & during pregnancy. ● Cap. Isotretinoin 10 or, 20 mg / Reticap (uni) / Aknil (incp) / Roaccutane (roche) / Akno (drg), 1 + 0 + 1 –––––– 2 – 3 months. Ø If more severe acne, e.g., Acne Conglobata. Treatment : Topical iso-tretinoin + systemic antibiotic + oral iso-tretinoin (average 0.5 mg / kg / day) + Anti-inflammatory (steroids to reduce inflammation). ● Tab. Prednisolone/ Cortan (incp) 10 mg, 2 + 1 + 0 (after meal) for 2 weeks then tapper slowly over 2-4 weeks. Ø Steroid acne : Nodular lesion followed by topical steroid application. Treatment : avoid steroids & treat accordingly (moderate / severe acne). Ø Referral to dermatology : 1. Acne fulminans (ulceration & scarring) : Seen in adolescent males; severe acne is associated with fever, arthritis, and vasculitis. 2. Severe acne or painful, deep nodules or cysts and could benefit from oral isotretinoin. More severe cases (acne conglobata) : Nodular abscess and more widespread scarring as well as non responsive cases. 3. Severe social / psychological sequelae. 4. At risk of / developing scarring despite primary care remedies. 5. Poor treatment response. 6. Suspected underlying cause for acne e.g. PCOS.

treatment.title: Acneiform eruptions
treatment.description: Acneiform eruptions are acne vulgaris-like dermatoses. There are papulopustular, nodular, and cystic lesions. Acne vulgaris is characterized by comedones, whereas acneiform eruptions clinically lack comedones. Acneiform eruptions demonstrate an acute onset and can affect any age group. May affects trunk, face and neck. Acneiform eruptions on trunk are described as folliculitis, which may be due to infection, obstruction, or unknown irritant factors. Treatment : Topical medication ( retinoids, azelaic acid, benzoyl peroxide, and topical antibiotics ) should be the first-line treatment. ↓ Oral medications ( systemic antibiotics, hormone replacement, oral contraceptives, and especially isotretinoin ) should be used for severe acne if initial treatment fails. Oral tetracycline antibiotics are the most effective preventive treatment for acne-like skin rashes caused by EGFR inhibitors.

treatment.title: Mask Acne (Maskne)
treatment.description: Clinically Maskne resembles classical acne vulgaris with presence of comedones and inflammatory papules in the characteristic distribution involving area of face covered by mask in both de novo cases and those having previous history of acne. Maskne is a form of mechanical acne, resulting from continuous textile–skin adherence and friction. Furthermore, prolonged mask use determines a hot-humid microclimate on the skin surface, thus modifying sebum production and consequently microorganisms. Treatment : Advice to avoid wearing masks, new one (mask) if needed. Drugs therapy is same as acne vulgaris.

treatment.title: Fungal acne
treatment.description: Fungal acne / Malassezia folliculitis / Pityrosporum folliculitis Malassezia folliculitis presents as small, uniform, itchy papules and pustules particularly on the upper back and chest. Other sites involved can include the forehead/hair line, chin, neck, and extensor aspect of the upper limbs. This is a monomorphic eruption and comedones are not seen, distinguishing this condition from acne vulgaris. An other clue is this condition may persist for years without complete resolution with typical acne medications. Flare up in hot humid climate/summer. May be combined with acne vulgaris (classic / regular acne). Topical treatments such as selenium sulfide shampoo, econazole solution and topical ketoconazole are effective in the majority of cases but may require a longer course than systemic agents (Fluconazole). Ongoing weekly application may be particularly useful as maintenance therapy to prevent recurrence. If confused can continue treatment of acne vulgaris (Adapalene ± Benzoyl Peroxide) plus – ► Topical ketoconazole (shampoo / medicated soap bar) / Dermazole Bar (der) / Kenzo F Soap (rah) / Ketozole (zas), Rub gently on the affected part (overall body for Pityriasis versicolor) to produce lather. Leave for 4-5 minutes. Rinse of thoroughly with water ---------- 1 – 3 months

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Features of lesion : ● Persistant erythematous & some popular lesion presents on both cheek including nose. ● Lesion persisting at least 3 months. ● Common in age group 30-50 years. ● Common in menopausal women. D/D : Acne vulgaris, SLE (Malar Rash).

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment : Topical+ systemic + oral isotretinoin. ► Azelaic Acid 20 % / Azelec (acm) Cream, 2 times daily -------- 3-4 months Or, ► Topical Ivermactin / Lotion Alice (sq) / Licnil (skf), Once daily at night ----------------- 4 months ► Cap. Doxycycline / Doxicap (ren) / DXC (uni) 100 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 --- 8-12 weeks Or, ► Cap. Oxytetracycline / Renamycin (ren) / Oxytetracycline (reli) 250 mg, 2 + 0 + 2 ----------- 2-3 months ± ► Tab. Clonazepam / Rivotril (rad)/ Pase (ops)/ Epitra (sq) 0.5 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ------- 2-4 weeks (for anxiety, stress) If still not responsive then, may use oral isotretinoin (0.5mg/kg/day). In 60 kg patient, ± ► Cap. Isotretinoin / Reticap 10 mg, 1 ½ +0+1 ½ ------ 4-6 months, (Check fasting lipid profile, SGPT before starting drug, stop isotretinoin 2 month before conceive & during pregnancy.) Advice : ● Use sunscreen like solascren 30 min before exposure to sunlight / heavy work 2-3 month ● Drink plenty of water. ● Ensure adequate sleep & avoid stress. ● Avoid coffee, spicy food, alcohol etc.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Dyshidrotic eczema (pompholyx) is a chronic, recurrent condition and appears as small, deep-seated, fluid-filled tiny blisters (vesicles) on the sides of the fingers, hands, and feet. The vesicles are small (1–2mm), often symmetrical, with no associated erythema, and they do not occur elsewhere on the body. More in middle aged & summer, often associated with hyperhidrosis. Relapsing –remitting course, each episode resolving within 2-3 weeks. Recurrent episode of bland vesicles with intense itching. Increased itchiness by touching water / soap / detergent.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment : Topical corticosteroid cream or ointment, anti-histamine (for itching) and antibiotic (if secondary infection). ► Betamethasone 0.1% / Betaderm (incp) / Bet-A (acm) / Sinacort (ibn)/ Bantovet (dbl)/ Betameson (sq-0.05%)/ Betason (ops-0.05%), Or, Cream Clobetasol 0.05% / Exovate (bex) / Dermasol (sq) / Clovate (aci) / Xenovate (uni) / Topiclo (skf), Apply in affected part BD ––––––– 14 days ► Tab. Levocetrizine 5 mg / Alcet (hc) / Seasonix (incp) / Sinacet (ibn), 0 + 0 + 1 ––––––– 10 – 14 days ± ► Tab. Azithromycin 500 mg / Azin (acm) / Romycin (ibn) / Rozith (hc) / Tridosil (incp) / Zithrin (ren) / Azicin (ops), 1 + 0 + 0 ––––––– 5 – 7 days

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: A callosity (syn. callus) is a plaque of thickened skin. Painless localized thickenings of the keratin layer-protective response to friction / pressure. A corn is a sharply demarcated callosity of smaller size. Corn may be soft or hard & often mild painful. Treatment (any one of keratolytic agents) : ☼ Cream Salicylic acid 6 % or, 12 % / Kerasol (incp) / Salicid (sq), Apply once daily -------------- 1 month Or, ☼ Oint. Salicylic Acid 3% + Benzoic Acid 6% / Whitfield (unique / millat) / Dermin (jay) / Bensal (gaco), Apply 2 – 3 times daily -------- 1 month Soak your hands or feet. Soaking corns and calluses in warm, soapy water softens them. This can make it easier to remove the thickened skin with a pumice stone (ঝামা / ঝামাপাথর). Moisturize your skin. Use moisturizer on your hands and feet regularly. Wear comfortable shoes and socks.

treatment.title: Treatment & Advice
treatment.description: Treatment & advise : ¤ Soaking in soapy warm water for 10 minutes till it became soft, then rub with pumice stone/rough clothing. ¤ Vaseline apply 4-6 times daily to keep it moist. ¤ Salicylic acid 12% BD for 8-12 weeks ¤ Drinking adequate water daily. ( ≥ 2.5 L/day) ¤ Topical steroid, if severely painful & cracked skin/palm/sole.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Viral warts (Verruca) / (আঁচিল) A viral wart is a very common benign lesion caused by infection with human papillomavirus (HPV). About 60% of common warts resolve spontaneously. Cryosurgery / CO 2 laser / Electro-cauterization are the treatment of choice. Features of lesion : Single /multiple, circumscribed, firm, dome shaped papules with verrucous dry surface, stippled with black dots. May be arranged linearly due to auto inoculation. Paring reveals capillary loops that distinguish plantar warts from corns. Black dots / capillary dots within viral warts. Verruca Vulgaris / Common Wart : ● Topical Solution Salicylic Acid 16.7% + Lactic Acid 16.7% / Salitic (zis) / D-film (uni) / Duofilm (stie) / Cylac (phar), Apply at affected part once daily (OD) at night ----- 1 to 3 months

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Keratolysis exfoliativa also called lamellar dyshidrosis / dyshidrosis lamellosa sicca / recurrent palmar peeling. While generally asymptomatic, but initial mild burning or tingling sensation and pain upon blister rupture, involved surfaces are often associated with hyperhidrosis. Blistering is exacerbated by friction, moist, warm environments, salty or chemical irritation. Normally in winter season. In mild cases no extra attention or treatment need, but in severe cases following treatment should be applied, local steroids may also be used (but not recommended). Treatment, ¤ Topical Cream Urea 10% / Equra (sq) / Eucera (uni), Apply locally BD (2 – 4 weeks) ¤ Emollient, Oil or Vaseline , Apply 4 – 6 hourly daily ¤ Plenty of fluid intake. ¤ Avoid triggering factors.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Hyperhidrosis is abnormal excessive sweating, not necessarily related to heat or exercise. This type of heavy sweating causes social anxiety and embarrassment. Usually affects the palm, sole, underarms or face causes at least one episode in a week, that disrupts patient’s normal daily activity. The most common form of hyperhidrosis is primary focal (essential) hyperhidrosis, where over-activity of sweat glands has no medical cause. Onset is typically in the teenagers. Secondary hyperhidrosis occurs when excess sweating due to a medical condition. May be due to hyperthyroidism, menopausal hot flashes, some emergencies like – hypoglycemia, AMI, sympathetic over activity etc. Adv : S. TSH, RBS (if suspected).

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment : Anti-perspirant / Anti-sweating agent : ► Solution Aluminum chloride hexahydrate 20% / Solution Dricare (zis) / Sudor-X (uni) / Apirent (ops), After cleaning affected area, apply at night & wash off in morning for 3 – 7 days then, use (maintenance therapy) once or twice weekly as per desired effect. ± ► Tab. Propantheline 15 mg / Prokind (bacn) / Thelin (incp) / Propa (lab), 1 + 1 + 1 ---------- 2 – 4 weeks If not responding : ¤ Iontophoresis / DC electricity therapy : May suggest patient to buy iontophoresis device / machine from scientific stores. ১০ মিনিট পজিটিভ তারপরে ১০ মিনিট নেগেটিভ, দিনে ১ বার ---- ১৫ দিন, তারপরে, ১০ মিনিট পজিটিভ তারপরে ১০ মিনিট নেগেটিভ, সপ্তাহে ১ – ২ বার ----- ১–৬ মাস। (প্রাপ্ত কার্যকারিতা বা উপকার অনুযায়ী মেশিন সপ্তাহে কতবার ব্যবহার করবে এবং কতদিন পর্যন্ত ব্যবহার করবে তা ঠিক করে নিতে হবে।) ¤ Refer to dermatologist for better management. Laser destruction of sweat glands, Endoscopic transthoracic sympathectomy (ETS), Botulinum toxin injections. ¤ GENERALIZED HYPERHIDROSIS : Excessive sweating over whole body. ► Tab. Propantheline 15 mg / Prokind (bacn) / Thelin (incp) / Propa (lab), 1 + 1 + 1 ---------- 2 – 4 weeks

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : Ø Severe itching, frequently followed by secondary bacterial regional lymphadenopathy and eczema- tization, resulting in matting of hair. Ø Transmission occurs by head-to-head contact, sharing of combs and caps, infested clothing of bedding and poor personal hygiene Ø Transmission of pubic lice is by sexual contact. Treatment : Any anti-louse preparation. ► Topical Ivermectin / Lotion Alice (sq) / Licnil (skf), Apply in scalp 10 minutes before shower ----------------------- 2 applications 7 days apart Or, each after one day for 7 days. ± ► Shampoo Ketoconazole 2% / ☼ Shampoo Nizoder (uni) / Dancel (incp) / Select plus (sq) / Odessy (ethi) / Resolve (bex), Apply the shampoo over affected areas. Use fingers to rub the shampoo until it forms lather. Leave the shampoo on skin for 10 minutes. Then rinse the shampoo off with water. Use the shampoo 2 days interval weekly -------------------- 1 month (or as per response) ± ► Tab. Cetirizine 10 mg (If persistent itching), 0+0+1 ------ 7 days [2-6 years = ½ tab or 5mg, >6 years = 10mg] Advice : 1. Comb thehair with a fine toothed comb until all lice are removed and repeat at 2-3 days intervals for 2-3 weeks. 2. Infested clothing and bedding should be washed properly in hot water and dried in sunlight. 3. In pubic lice infestation, sex partner should be treated as well. 4. Clothes should be ironed from inside with special attention to seamline.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Scabies (খোস-পাঁচড়া / খাউজ / খুজলি / চুলকানি) • Acute itchy condition of short duration/sudden onset. • Caused by the female mite Sarcoptes scabiei var. hominis . • Intermittent, pleasant, intense itching at night. Scabies is the only itchy skin disease where patient may complaint that he/she had not sleept well, due to nocturnal itching. • Burrow present. Scratch mark / excoriations present. • More incidence in winter season. Usually decreases sweating. • Infants : scalp and face are often involved, spares in adults. • Due to delayed type / type IV hypersensitivity reaction, after 4 – 6 weeks inoculation a scabies patient develops intense itch and rash.

treatment.title: Site : CIRCLE OF HEBRA
treatment.description: Common sites : Excoriated papules with burrows found on CIRCLE OF HEBRA. Most common site is inter-digital clefts. Must involve atleast any one of the following four sites – • Finger web • Umbilicus • Scrotum and penis • Toe web ± Other possible sites : • Wrists • Axilla / intertriginous areas • Nipple and Areola • Lower abdomen Buttock • Perineal area • Foot • Palms and soles • Face and scalp in children only • Around and underneath nails Type : ● Classical scabies ● Nodular scabies ● Crusted (Norwegian) scabies ● Bullous scabies

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment : Scabicidal / Ectoparasiticide agent & Anti-Histamine for itching. ± Oral ivermectin, ± Antibiotics (if pus forming lesions) Scabicidal agent : Local scabicidal agent : Cream Permethrin 5% or, Lotion Permethrin 5% + Crotamiton 10% or, Benzyl Benzoate 25% ↓ Tx failure or, recurrent Monosulfiram 25% Solution [Monosulfiram is better option. It is safe in pregnancy & infancy.] ↓ Tx failure or, recurrent Local scabicidal agent + Oral ivermectin Anti-Histamine is a must for itching to avoid scratching. ± Antibiotics (if pus forming lesions). First Option : ● Permethrin 5% Cream / ☼ Cream Lorix (ops) / Scabex (sq) / Perosa (skf) Or, ● Lotion Permethrin 5% + Crotamiton 10% / ☼ Unix-C (uni) / Lorix Plus (ops) / Eliminate Plus (incp) / Crotam Plus (ethi) ব্যবহারবিধি : ১। গোসলের পর পর্যাপ্ত পরিমান লোশন সারা শরীরে লাগিয়ে (পা ও হাতের তালু সহ, বাচ্চাদের মাথা ও মুখেও লাগাতে হবে) ৮ থেকে ১২ ঘন্টা পর গোসল করবেন। ২। সপ্তাহে একদিন করে (সাত দিন পর পর), অর্থাৎ ঐ মাসে মোট চারবার ব্যবহার করবেন। পরিবারের সকল সদস্য বা সকল রুমমেটরা একই নিয়মে লাগাবেন। ৩। প্রথম ওষুধ ব্যবহারের দিনে অবশ্যই ব্যবহার্য কাপড়-চোপড় গরম-সোডা পানি দিয়ে ধুয়ে ফেলবেন। লেপ, তোষক যা ধৌত করা যায় না তা রোদে দিয়ে রাখবেন ও নূন্যতম ৩ দিন ঔগুলো ব্যবহার থেকে বিরত থাকবেন । পারতপক্ষে পরবর্তী ওষুধ ব্যবহারের দিনে গুলোতেও ব্যবহার্য কাপড় চোপড় ধৌত করবেন । ৪। পরিবারের সকল সদস্যরা / রুমমেটরা নিয়মটি না মেনে চললে আরোগ্য লাভ করা যায় না বা ঘন ঘন খোস-পাঁচড়াতে আক্রান্ত হতে থাকতে হয়। Note – Lotion (Permethrin 5% + Crotamiton 10%) sufficient amount to cover whole body. But advised amount for use of cream permethrin 5% according age – ● 2 months – 1 yrs : One eighth of the tube. ● 1 – 5 years : One fourth of the tube. ● 6 – 12 yrs : Half of the tube. ● above 12 yrs : 1 - 2 full tube. Or, Next Option : ● Monosulfiram 25% Solution / ☼ Tetrasol Lotion (aci) ব্যবহারবিধি : ১। গোসলের পর পর্যাপ্ত পরিমান লোশন সারা শরীরে লাগিয়ে (পা ও হাতের তালু সহ। বড়দের মাথা ও মুখ বাদে কিন্তু বাচ্চাদের মাথা ও মুখেও লাগাতে হবে), পরের দিন প্রায় ১৮ - ২৪ ঘন্টা পর গোসল করবেন। পরবর্তী দুইদিন গোসলের পর পর আবার ঔষধটি লাগাবেন। ২। মিশ্রণ পদ্ধতি : বড়দের ক্ষেত্রে এক চামচ ওষুধের সাথে দুই চামচ পানি এবং বাচ্চাদের ক্ষেত্রে এক চামচ ওষুধের সাথে এক চামচ পানি মিশিয়ে ব্যবহার করবেন। ৩। সপ্তাহে পর পর তিন দিন ব্যবহার করবেন। পরের সপ্তাহে অবার পর পর তিন দিন ব্যবহার করবেন। অর্থাৎ মোট ছয় দিন ব্যবহার করবেন। পরিবারের সকল সদস্য বা সকল রুমমেটরা একই নিয়মে লাগাবেন। ৪। প্রথম ওষুধ ব্যবহারের দিনে অবশ্যই ব্যবহার্য কাপড়-চোপড় গরম-সোডা পানি দিয়ে ধুয়ে ফেলবেন। লেপ, তোষক যা ধৌত করা যায় না তা রোদে দিয়ে রাখবেন ও নূন্যতম ৩ দিন ঔগুলো ব্যবহার থেকে বিরত থাকবেন । পারতপক্ষে পরবর্তী ওষুধ ব্যবহারের দিনে গুলোতেও ব্যবহার্য কাপড় চোপড় ধৌত করবেন । ৫। পরিবারের সকল সদস্যরা / রুমমেটরা নিয়মটি না মেনে চললে আরোগ্য লাভ করা যায় না বা ঘন ঘন খোস-পাঁচড়াতে আক্রান্ত হতে থাকতে হয়। Oral scabicidal agent : Ivermectin – Incase of failed local treatment / recurrent scabies : ● Tab. Ivermectin 6 mg or 12 mg [For adults 12 mg & for child 0.2 mg/kg/dose] / ☼ Scabo (del) / Alice (sq) / Ivera (bex) / Ivtin (drg) 12 mg, প্রতি শুক্রবার ও শনিবার রাতে একটি করে ট্যাবলেট খাবেন ------------- পর পর তিন সপ্তাহ [Norwegian or crusted scabies (rare) affects only immunocompromised (have a weak immune system), elderly, disabled, or debilitated. In this case oral ivermectin is indicated twice dosing in a week for 4 – 6 weeks. In classic scabies once weely for 1-2 weeks is enough. Above ivermectin use is over dosing in case of classic scabies, but may prescribe as it is practically more effective. Child above 15 kg weight should receive tablet according to weight (200 mcg/kg). Ivermectin is contraindicated in pregnancy & infancy.] Anti-Histamine : It is a must, as scratching spreads the mites through nail dirt. 2 nd / 3 rd generation, if itching not controlled then 1 st generation anti-histamine. Child : ● Syp. Hydroxyzine 10mg / 5ml / ☼ Syrp. Artica (aci) / Xyril (ops) / Urtizin (uni), ½ চামচ রাতে ১ বার / দিনে ২ বার ------ ৫ – ১৪ দিন Adult : ● Tab. Levo-cetrizine 5 mg / ☼ T. Alcet (hc) / Seasonix (incp) / Sinacet (ibn), ০ + ০ + ১ ------ ৫ – ১৪ দিন

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: Discussion / Other plan : Other drug options – ● Benzyl Benzoate 25% Emulsion / ☼ Scabex (supreme) / Ascabiol (syn) / Scabisol (jay) গোসলের পর পর্যাপ্ত পরিমান লোশন সারা শরীরে লাগিয়ে (মাথা ও হাতের তালু সহ) পর পর তিন (০৩) রাত্রি লাগিয়ে চতুর্থ দিনে গোসল করবেন। পরিবারের সকল সদস্য একই নিয়মে লাগাবেন। ব্যবহার্য কাপড় চোপড় গরম পানি দিয়ে ধুয়ে ফেলবেন। লেপ, তোষক যা ধৌত করা যায় না তা রোদে দিয়ে রাখবেন, তিন (০৩) দিন পর ব্যবহার করা যাবে। সাত দিন (০৭) পর পুনরায় একই নিয়মে পরিবারের সবাই লোশন লাগাবেন ও ব্যবহার্য কাপড় চোপড় ধৌত করবেন । Note – ► Topical low potent steroid may prescribed if severe inflammatory reaction or itching or eczematization. Though role of steroid in scabies is controversial. ► Topical antibiotic, systemic antibiotic, topical steroid and topical keratolytic agents like salicylic acid is required for crusted scabies. May use oral antibiotic if secondary infection present. ► Incase of severe / failed topical treatment / immuno-compromized (crusted or Norwegian scabies) then use oral anti scabies drug / Severe case – If immunecompromise, oral ivermectin 200 μ g/kg per dose given orally for single dose (repeat dosing may be needed at a week or two week(s) interval), not indicated for children under 15 kg. [May use 2 to 5 doses, 1 to 2 weeks apart.] ► Ivermectin (200 mcg/kg) : It is safe for children above 15 kg body weight. Not recommended in pregnancy & lactation Usuially well tolerated. Side effects – diarrhoea, nausea, vomiting, tachycardia, hypotension are known adverse effects but very rare. Best to use in combination with topical scabicidal agent. ► Scabies mite still responses to all the scabicidal drugs (topical & oral). Main reason for treatment failure is incorrect technique of applying drugs, that don’t cover whole body, not washing of cloths & belongings, spare asymptomatic family members. ► Further treatment options of nodular & crusted scabies : • Intralesional triamcinolone acetonide injection for nodular scabies. Generally scrotal nodular scabies also response in conventional topical & oral anti-scabies agents, so intra-lesional injection is for non healing nodules. Nodules acts as source of recurrent scabies infection. • Crusted scabies is treated with oral ivermectin on two consecutive days each week for 4 to 6 weeks with topical permethrin for same duration. ► DD of scabies : Papular urticaria, Miliaria, Viral Exanthem, Drug Rash, Prurigo nodularis and insect bites. These are not scabies, as – ● Family members not affected. ● Itching throughout the day / not nocturnal itching. ● Finger and toe web / umbilicus / genitalia not involved. ► Incase of infected scabies & pustules : Add any one antibiotic (adult dose in mg - dosing interval) : Flucloxacillin (500-QDS), Cefadroxil (500-BD), Cephradine (500-QDS), Amoxicillin (500-TDS), Cefuroxime (500-BD). Child : ● Syp. Flucloxacillin 125mg/5ml / Flupen (drg) / Flubex (bex) / Fluclox (aci) / Phylopen DS (sq) / Flubac (pop), (wt/10) চামচ দিনে ৪ বার ------ ৭ – ১৪ দিন Adult : ● Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg / Flux (ops) / A-Flox (acm) / Flubex (bex) / Fluclox (aci) / Phylopen DS (sq) / Flustar (ren), ১ + ১ + ১ + ১ ---------------- ৭ থেকে ১৪ দিন

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Papular urticaria Papular urticaria is a common and often annoying disorder manifested by chronic or recurrent papules caused by a hypersensitivity reaction (itchy & looks like scabies). Mostly arms and legs involved. Long relapsing history. Could be associated with asthma, rhinitis. Treatment – as urticaria (below / next diseases). Treatment for children, ● Syrup Hydroxyzine 10 mg/5 ml / ☼ Syp. Artica / Xyril, wt/ 15 চামচ দিনে ৩ বার ....... ৭ দিন ● Sus. Famotidin 40 mg/5 ml / ☼ Sus. Famotid / Famodin / Famotack / Peptid, ½ চামচ দিনে ২ বার .... ৭ দিন Treatment for adults, H 1 Blocker : (Any one, but may use two anti-histamine in severe cases or failed response.) ● Tab. Levo-cetrizine 5 mg / Alcet (hc) / Seasonix (incp) / Sinacet (ibn), 1 + 0 + 0 –––––– 15 – 30 days ● Tab. Hydroxyzine 25 mg or 10 mg / Artica (aci) / Xyril (ops) / Urtizin (uni) / Roxyzin (incp) 10 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 –––––– 15 – 30 days H 2 Blocker : ● Tab. Famotidine 20 or, 40 mg / Famomax (bex) / Famotid (drg) / Famotack (sq) / Famodin (acm) / Peptid (ops) 40 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ( ½ hr B/M) –––––– 15 – 30 days ● Patients must be advised to avoid allergens. ● If severe itching, Calamine Lotion (amico) / Itchnil (skf) / Itchsol (incp), Apply in the body BD -------- till itching subsides. ● In severe cases, add oral corticosteroid with anti-ulcerants. Tab. Prednisolone 10 or 20 mg / Cortan (incp) / Predimax (acm) / Precodil (ops) / Deltasone (ren) / Deltapred (zis) 20 mg, 0 + 1 + 0 or, 1 + 1 + 0 or, 0 + 2 + 0 (after meal) ------ 7 – 10 days or, Tab. Deflazacort 6 mg / Deflacort (sq) / Xalcort (bacn) / Zodef (ren) / Cortimax (acm) / Deflazit (incp) 6 mg, 0 + 1 + 0 or, 1 + 1 + 0 or, 0 + 2 + 0 (after meal) ------ 7 – 10 days ► If non-responsive within 2 weeks, may add 1 st generation antihistamine at night and second / newer generation at morning. Newer anti-histamine options : ± ● Tab. Ebastine 10 mg / Ebatin (pop) / Ebatrol (del) / Tebast (sq) ± ● Tab. Bilastine 20 mg / Bilastin (pop) / Biltin (skf) / Billi (bex) / Bilan (acm) / Zilas (hc) / Bilamin (ibn) ± ● Tab. Mizolastine 10 mg / Mastel MR (aci) / Alergix MR (incp) / Rhinor MR (ops) As per need : 1 + 0 + 0 or, 0 + 0 + 1 –––––– 5 to 60 days. ► In severe cases : ● Inj. Pheniramine 45.5 mg/2 ml / Avil (syn) / Amarin (ops), ½ or, 1 amp I/M (or I/V slowly) stat on deltoid (adult) or buttock / lateral aspect of thigh (child). ± ● Inj. Hydrocortisone 100 mg/2 ml / Cotson (ops) / Intasone (incp) / Cortef (aris) / Cortinex (zis), 1 amp I/M (or I/V slowly) stat on deltoid (adult), Or, ½ amp I/M on buttock / lateral aspect of thigh (child). ► A severe allergic reaction, known as anaphylaxis, is a rare, life-threatening emergency. • Adrenaline 1 : 1000 (0.01 ml/kg IM provides rapid relief). • Steroid : I/V or I/M Hydrocortisone as per severity.

treatment.title: Features & Tx options
treatment.description: Features : • Constant itching with characteristic “wheal” and flare lesion. • Restless and irritable. • History of exposure to cold, heat, sun, allergic food. • Family history of allergy. Urticaria : Urticaria is caused by localized dermal oedema. Types : 1. Acute ( 2. Chronic > 6 weeks) 3. Urticarial Vasculitis Acute urticaria : Sharply defined, pruritic, transient wheals in various size and shape lasts Chronic urticaria more than 6 weeks duration, with daily or episodic wheals. Investigations : 1. CBC (eosinophilia in parasitic diseases), ESR (high in vasculitis) 2. Total Ig E and specific Ig E (if available) to possible allergens 3. If systemic diseases suspected: a. Urea and electrolytes b. Thyroid Function Tests c. Liver function tests d. ANA Treatment options : At first visit start 2nd-generation antihistamine (H 1 blocker) Note – • 1 st generation anti-histamine is better against itching & cough suppression, but short half-life & sedative enough to hamper daily activities. • H 2 blocker. Both H1 blockade (by anti-histamine) & H 2 blockade (by H 2 blocker) potentiates anti-allergic action. F/U after 2 wks ↓ if insufficient control Titrate (same) second-generation antihistamine to two to four times (2 × to 4 × ) normal dose. F/U after 4 wks ↓ if insufficient control Consider adding one of the following : ● H 2 blocker. ● First-generation antihistamine at night. ● Leukotriene receptor antagonist (Montelukast). ● Brief burst of oral corticosteroids (Prednisolone / Dexamethasone / Methylprednisolone / Deflazacort for 3 to 10 days in tapered dose). Or, Switch to different (poetnt) second-generation antihistamine. F/U after 4 wks ↓ if insufficient control Try another option listed above, Or, Consider referral for second-line therapies such as hydroxychloroquine (Plaquenil) or tacrolimus (Prograf), Ciclosporin, Methotrexate, Cyclophosphamide etc. ↓ Omalizumab (anti-IgE), Plasmapheresis, Intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG)
