Propantheline 15 mg / Prokind, 1 + 1 + 1 (½ hr B/M) ☼ Inj. Atropine 0.6mg/ml / G-Atropine (gono), 2 amp I/V stat. If still HR If still not responding : ☼ Inj. Isoprenaline / Isoproterenol / Isuprel 0.2mg/ml, 10 ml / 1 amp + Inf. 5% DA 500 ml, I/V stat @ 8 d/m.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: ATRIAL FIBRILLATION (AF) (Most common) (Refer to cardiologist / hospitalization) C / C : • Palpitation (Patient says heart jumping about or racing). • Breathlessness / dyspnoea & fatigue. • May be asymptomatic. (Paroxysmal type is discrete self-limiting episodes). O / E : • Pulse — irregularly irregular. • BP — May be hypertensive. Treatment : Rate control : Never prescribe beta blocker and rate limiting CCB (such as diltiazem or verapamil) simultaneously. ► Tab. Metoprolol 50 mg / Betaloc (drg) / Selomet (uni) 50 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 Or, ► Tab. Diltiazem 30 mg / Cardil (ibn) / Cardizem (drg) 30 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 Or, ► Tab. Digoxin / Centoxin (ops) / Agoxin (aris) 0.25 mg, 3 + 0 + 0 for 5 days, then, 1 + 0 + 0 (Friday & Saturday off) + Rhythm control : Stable patient with no structural heart disease presenting within 48 hours : ► Inj. Flecainide [2mg/kg over 30 min]. Or, Patient with structural or ischemic heart disease : ► Inj. Amiodarone, 2 amp mixed with 100 ml, I/V very slowly over 10 min, Then, Inf. 5%DA 500ml + Inj. Amiodarone 6 amp, I/V @32 microdrops/min. + Thrombo-prophylaxis : ► Tab. Warfarin (incp) / Unifarin (uni) / Farin (con) 5 mg, 1+0+1 – 2 days then, 0+0+1 Up to target INR 2-3 and continue Or, ► Tab. Rivaroxaban 20 mg / Rivarox (skf) / RivaXa (sq) 20 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ---- continue Continuous monitoring & maintenance of rate and rhythm, Identification of cause & treat the underlying cause(s) ► Digoxin contra-indicated in AMI, as Digoxin (Cardiac glycoside) increases force of contraction → increases O 2 demand → Aggravates ischemia. β -blockers may be used. Specialist supervision needed to use digoxin in AMI c̄ AF. ► Anticoagulation is indicated in patient with atrial fibrillation having risk factors for thromboembolism. Aspirin may also be used instead of warfarin. Age

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: VENTRICULAR FIBRILLATION (VF) (Uncommon) (Refer to cardiologist / hospitalization) C / C c̄ O / E : • Pulse — absent. • BP — not recordable. • Respiration — ceases or absent. • Patient — unconscious. • Pupil — dilated, less or no reaction to light. • Heart sounds — absent. ECG : VF (Lead II) : QRS—Chaotic, wide, bizarre, irregular. Treatment : ☼ Immediate defibrillation / DC Shock 200 joules. If not controlled – ☼ DC Shock another 200 joules. If not controlled – ☼ DC Shock another 360 joules. If not controlled – ☼ Inj. 2% Lignocaine / Jasocaine 50 ml + Inf. 5% DA 400 ml or cc. 2 vial (100mL) I/V stat @ 8 d/m for 24 hrs. ☼ After 24 hrs – Tab. Amiodarone / Cardiron (drg) / Pacet (bex) 200 mg, 1 + 1 + 1. Discussion : ► If defibrillator is not available — cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) should be given. ► If three shocks unsuccessful — adrenaline is given IV, followed by cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR). ► Effective circulation and ventilation must be obtained within 4 minutes to prevent, if irreversible brain damage. ► The patient who survives from VF in the absence of identifiable cause is at high-risk of sudden death. This case is treated with implantable cardioverter defibrillator.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: VENTRICULAR TACHYCARDIA (VT) (Refer to cardiologist / hospitalization) C / C : (Patient may say heart is racing / fluttering.) - Palpitation, - Dizziness, - Breathlessness. O / E : - HR : 140 – 220 b/m. - BP & Pulse : Un-recordable. ECG : - Absent P wave. - QRS broad (>0.14 sec), abnormal or bizarre pattern. - Rate > 100 beats /minute. Treatment : ☼ DC Shock 200 joules. ☼ Inj. 2% Lignocaine / Jasocaine 50 ml, 3-5 mL I/V stat over 1 min. After 5-10 min if not controlled – ☼ Inj. 2% Lignocaine / Jasocaine 50 ml, 3-5 mL I/V stat over 1 min. If controlled then maintain, ☼ Inj. 2% Lignocaine / Jasocaine 50 ml + Inf. 5% DA 400 cc, 2 vial (100mL) I/V stat @ 8 d/m for 24 hrs. ☼ Tab. Amiodarone / Cardiron (drg) / Pacet (bex) 200 mg, 1 + 1 + 1. ☼ If no improvement – DC shock (Cardioversion). Discussion : ► Ventricular tachycardia is more common than SVT. ► If the patient is hemodynamically unstable (such as hypotension, systolic ► If all fail — automatic implantable cardioverter defibrillator device (ICD) or radiofrequency ablation of focus of ventricular tachycardia should be done. ► Correction of hypokalemia, hypomagnesemia, hypoxemia and acidosis should be done. ► Discharge for VT : Ø Tab. Carvedilol / Carvista (incp) / Cardex(ops) 6.25 mg, ½ + 0 + ½ বা ১ + ০ + ১ ...... চলবে Ø Tab. Amiodarone / Cardiron (drg) / Pacet (bex) 200 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ ...... ০৭ দিন, তারপরে, ১ + ০ + ১ ...... ০৭ দিন, তারপরে, ১ + ০ + ০ ...... চলবে

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: SUPRAVENTRICULAR TACHYCARDIA (SVT) (Refer to cardiologist / hospitalization) C / C : Palpitation (Patient says heart is racing / fluttering), Dizziness, Syncope. O / E : HR : 150 – 250 b/m. BP & Pulse : Un-recordable. Inv. : ECG : - Absent P wave. - Regular rhythm. - Narrow QRS complex. Treatment : ☼ Continuous ECG monitoring & continuous carotid massage. ☼ Inj. Diazepam 10 mg / 2 ml / Sedil (sq), 1 amp I/V stat. ☼ Inj. Adenosine 6 mg / 2 ml / Adecard / Osiden, Flush with 20 ml Inf. N/S then, 2 amp I/V stat within 1-3 sec. If no response within 2 min then – ☼ Inj. Verapamil 5mg/2ml / Veracal (incp), 2 amp I/V stat slowly over 5-10 min. If patient is hemodynamically unstable (hypotension, pulmonary edema) / no response within 2 min then – ☼ DC shock. ► Carotid sinus massage or valsalva maneuver. It acts by increasing the vagal tone. Look for carotid bruit before carotid sinus massage. Otherwise, thrombus may be dislodged and may cause cerebral embolism. ► Treat & discharge according to cause. Avoid anxiety, tea, alcohol, coffee. ► Sinus tachycardia neither starts abruptly nor stops abruptly. ► IV Verapamil should be avoided if QRS (wider) > 0.12 second or history of WPW syndrome or if the patient is on β -blocker. • In WPW syndrome — transvenous radiofrequency catheter ablation is the treatment of choice. • In some cases, if there is no response — antitachycardial pacing is done (overdrive atrial pacing). Discharge for SVT : Ø Tab. Carvedilol / Carvista (incp) / Cardex(ops) 6.25 mg, ½ + 0 + ½ বা ১ + ০ + ১ ...... চলবে ± Ø Tab. Verapamil / Veracal (incp)/ Angimil (medi) 80 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ (খাবার পরে) ..... চলবে ± Ø Tab. Digoxin 250 μ g / Agoxin (aris) / Centoxin (ops) / Cap. Digoxin 200 μ g / Digoxen (drg), ১ + ০ + ০ (শুক্রবার বাদে খাবেন) ....... চলবে Ø Cap. Omeprazole / PPI (acm) / Xelopes (bacn) 20 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার আধাঘন্টা পূর্বে) ........ ১৫ দিন Ø Tab. Clonazepam / Rivotril (rad) 0.5 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ........ ১৫ দিন Advice : Please see “HTN chapter advice in bangla portion”.

treatment.title: Drug Dilution
treatment.description: Drug Dilution Modules in cardiology : ► Inj. Morphine : 1 ampoule contains 15 mg in 1 ml, 1 ml to be mixed with 14 ml distilled water, so the final strength is 1 mg/ml. ► Inj. Nitroglycerin, 50 mg/10 ml : 1 ampoule to be mixed with 40 ml of 5% dextrose in aqua, and to be infused with an infusion pump @ 0.4 ml - 1.4 ml/min, OR, 1 ampoule to be mixed with 500 ml of 5% dextrose in aqua, and to be infused @ 10- 30 drop/ min, usually not more than 24 hours. ► Inj. Streptokinase : If cardiac chest pain is less than 12 hours in STEMI, then the following thrombolytic agent may be used – ☼ Inj. Hydrocortisone 100 mg / Cotson (ops), 2 vial I/V stat before streptokinase. ☼ Inj. Streptokinase 15 lac IU / Eptase (bacn) / STK (incp) S-Kinase (pop), 1 vial dilute with 5 ml N/S (normal saline) & mixed further with 95 ml N/S then – I/V stat @ 25 d/m (single dose). ► Inj. Heparin (25,000IU/5ml) / Heparon (bacn) / Heparin Panpharma (city) / G-Heparin (gono), 1.5 ml I/V stat (loading dose in ACS/AMI) then, 1 vial (5 ml) + 500 ml 5% DA, I/V stat @ 20 μ d/m. [Heparin loading dose 100 unit / kg bolus.] ► Inj. Dopamine 200 mg/5 ml (5 ml ampoule) ¤ Inf. 5% DA or, N/S 500 ml + Inj. Dopamine 2 amp, I/V stat @ 8 d/m or @ 32 μ d/m (if in microburette) [Here, 4 μ d/m or, microdrops/min = 1 drops/min] ► Inj. Dobutamine 12.5 mg/ml (20 mL vial) ¤ Inf. 5% DA or, N/S 500 ml + Inj. Dobutamine 2 amp, I/V stat @ 8 d/m or @ 32 μ d/m (if in microburette) [Here, 4 μ d/m or, microdrops/min = 1 drops/min]

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: Acute Left Ventricular Failure (ALVF) / Acute Pulmonary Oedema
treatment.description: ACUTE LEFT VENTRICULAR FAILURE (ALVF) / ACUTE PULMONARY OEDEMA (Referred for hospitalization & cardiologist) Look for symptoms and signs : Symptoms : Sudden onset of dyspnoea at rest that rapidly progress to respiratory distress, orthopnea and prostration. Symptoms secondary to pulmonary oedema : SOB c̄ cough (sometimes with pink froth), hemoptysis, wheeze (cardiac asthma). o Severe breathlessness (SOB) and productive cough. o Pink frothy sputum may be mucoid or blood stained. o Chest tightness / chest pain. o Orthopnea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea (PND). o Usually with H/O IHD or AKI or CKD or HTN or DM etc. Signs : o Central cyanosis. o Pulsus alternans – pulse low volume, rapid. o BP – usually low. o Gallop rhythm (may be present in left parasternal area). o Bilateral fine basal crepitation present (on lung base). Initial investigations : o ECG – abnormal (may shows IHD). o Chest X-ray – Pulmonary oedema.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.disease_name: Acute Left Ventricular Failure (ALVF) / Acute Pulmonary Oedema
treatment.description: Management : ✔ Admission in hospital, preferably in CCU. ✔ Bed rest in propped up position ✔ O 2 inhalation, high flow 4-6 L/minute via face mask. ✔ Furosemide, 20-80 mg IV stat and twice daily, 8 am and 4 pm. Injection frusemide (20 mg/2 ml) can be given more frequently. ✔ Morphine, 3 mg (2.5-5 mg) IV stat, and repeated in small aliquot if required, PLUS, Prochlorperazine (vergon) 25 mg (or, ondansetron 8 mg) IV stat with the first dose of morphine. It can be considered and repeated if necessary. ✔ Glyceryl trinitrate (GTN) continuous infusion either by infusion pump or in IV drip, 9-200 µg/min as required, or buccal GTN 2 – 5 mg titrated upwards every 10 min, until clinical improvement occurs or systolic BP falls to less than 110 mm of Hg. ✔ Management of cardiogenic shock if indicated. Ionotropic agent : an intravenous infusion of an inotrope (e.g. dobutamine : 2.5-10 μ g/kg/min) should be considered in patients with hypotension (systolic blood pressure Subsequent management : ● Monitoring of the patient with – (a) Cardiac monitor (development of arrythmia); (b) BP & pulse oximetry (SpO 2 ). ● Insertion of intra-aortic balloon pump – in patient with acute pulmonary edema and cardiogenic shock. ● Do further investigations, e.g., echocardiography, and others, if available and as needed. ● Diagnose & treat underline causes e.g., revascularization for MI or, ischemia/IHD (aspirin, carvidelol, atorvastatin etc.), control hypertension, treat valvular heart disease etc. ● Add ACE inhibitor on discharge. ● Add other first-line heart failure drugs (see HF portion, below), if indicated. ● Refer to appropriate center for further evaluation and management.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: HEART FAILURE (HF) / CHRONIC HEART FAILURE (CHF) (Referred to cardiologist) History, clinical examination and initial investigations History : ● Poor effort intolerance, fatigue and lassitude. ● Exertional dyspnoea, orthopnoea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea. ● Known coronary artery disease, hypertension. Physical examination : ● Dependent oedema (+/++/+++). ● Raised JVP. ● Lung crepitation / Bilateral basal crepitation. ● Shifted and diffuse apex beat. ● Murmur. ● Tender hepatomegaly. Initial investigations : ● ECG (any abnormality, e.g., IHD). ● Chest X-ray (pulmonary oedema, cardiomegaly). ● Echocardiography (Echo), access function of the left ventricle. Reduced ejection fraction (normal ejection fraction 55-70%).

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Treatment : ► General measure : 1. Education of the patient. 2. Diet : - fluid and salt restriction. - Good general nutrition. - Weight reducing diet for obese. 3. Alcohol : - Moderation or elimination. - Abstinence in alcohol induced cardiomyopathy. 4. Smoking cessation & avoid any type of drug addiction. 5. Limitation of activity. Exercise is recommended, regular moderate aerobic exercise with in limit of symptoms. 6. Vaccination - influenza & pneumococcal. ► Pharmacological therapy : first-line drugs (± Frusemide) : ● ACEi/ARNI/ARB : Tab. Ramipril 1.25 mg, 0+0+1 --- continue ● Beta-blockers : Tab. Carvedilol 6.25 mg, ½ +0+ ½ --- continue ● Spironolactone : Tab. Spironolactone 25 mg, 1+1+0 -- continue ● SGLT2i : Tab. Empagliflozin 10 mg, 1 + 0 + 0 ---- continue ± ● Loop diuretic : Tab. Frusemide 40 mg, 1+0+0 ---- continue Look for features of congestion, e.g., bilateral lung crepitations, pulmonary oedema in CXR : ↓ ↓ "Wet patient" = Congestion present "Dry patient" = Congestion absent ↓ ↓ Start with ACEi/ARNI/ARB + Frusemide Start with Beta-blocker ± Frusemide As per need, add other first-line drugs. HF Drug Discussion : + Diuretic therapy – (any one or two if needed) ✔ Loop diuretics (frusemide) : Frusemide, intravenous in patient with severe chronic HF particularly in presence of chronic renal impairment in which edema persist despite oral (frusemide) loop diuretics. ✔ Aldosterone receptor antagonist e.g. spironolactone & Eplerenone. They improve long term clinical outcome in patient with severe HF or HF following acute MI. + ACEi/ARNI/ARB – (any one) ✔ ACEi – Can easily be started in otherwise stable patient & SBP more than 100 mmHg. In other patient diuretics should be stopped for 24 hours & then ACEi start at low dose. ✔ ARB – similar effect to that of ACEi (should be given if ACEi cannot tolerate or side effects occurs). ✔ Indication of combined ACEi & ARB – HF patient in those with recurrent hospitalization for HF (better not given due to risk of hyperkalaemia) ✔ Neprilysin inhibitors – sacubutril which decrease breakdown of natural diuretic (ANP. BNP). If used in combination with the ARB in an initial oral dose of 24 mg sacubitril and 26 mg valsartan daily, it produces additional symptomatic and mortality benefit over ACE inhibitor. + (any one) ✔ SGLT2 inhibitors (empagliflozin, dapagliflozin) – reduced hospitalizations for heart failure and lower mortality in patients with heart failure irrespective of the presence of diabetes. + (any one) ✔ Beta blocker therapy : More effective than ACEi in reducing mortality. Bisoprolol starting dose at 1.25 mg daily increase gradually over 12 weeks to a target maintenance dose of 10 mg daily or Carvidelol 6.25-25 mg daily can be used. ✔ Ivabradine – Typical dosages are 2.5-5 mg twice daily, increasing to 7-5 mg twice daily if necessary. In trials, its effects were most marked in patients with a relatively high heart rate (over 77/min). Indicated in- patients who cannot take β -blockers or whose heart rate remains high despite β -blockade. ± (only if strongly indicated) ✔ Vasodilator therapy – Nitrates in IHD. ✔ Digoxin – to provide rate control and if significant activity limitation (NYHA II, IV). ✔ Amiodarone – Effective in patient of symptomatic arrhythmia & should not be used in asymptomatic arrhythmia. Table : Dose of selected drugs used in heart failure. Drug Starting Dose Target Dose ACEi : Ramipril 1.25 mg daily 10 mg daily Enalapril 2.5 mg 12 hourly 10 mg 12 hrly Captopril 6.25 mg 3 times daily 50 mg 3 times daily ARB : Losartan 25–50 mg daily 150 mg daily Valsartan 40 mg twice daily 160 mg twice daily Valsartan-Sacubitril 50 mg twice daily, [25 mg twice daily-if low BP] 100 mg twice daily (neprilysin inhibitor) β blocker : Metoprolol succinate 12.5–25 mg daily 200 mg daily Carvedilol 3.125 mg twice daily 25 mg twice daily Bisoprolol 1.25 mg once daily 10 mg once daily SGLT2i : Empagliflozin 10 mg daily 10 mg daily Dapagliflozin 10 mg daily 10 mg daily Diuretic : Spironolactone 12.5–25 mg daily 50 mg daily Frusemide 20–40 mg daily 120 mg daily [Ref – Table-7, Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 57] Here, ○ ACEI = angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor, ○ ARB = angiotensin receptor blocker, ○ ARNI = angiotensin receptor-neprilysin inhibitor, ○ SGLT2 = sodium-glucose cotransporter-2.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ CHF / ALVF Adv. : ▪ ECG ▪ CXR P/A view ▪ Echo If needed – ▪ S. electrolytes ▪ Lipid profile ▪ S. Creatinine ▪ Hb% ▪ Coronary angiography Tx, ✔ Diet : Liquid. ✔ O ₂ inhalation stat & sos. [High flow (4-6 L/min)] ✔ Inj. Frusemide 20mg/2ml / Lasix (syn), 2 amp I/V stat and BD (8am and 4pm) ✔ Spray Nitroglycerin (400 mcg/spray) / Nidocard (drg) / GTN (skf) Spray, 2 puffs stat & every 5-10 min until systolic BP ✔ Tab. Ramipril / Ramoril (incp) / Ripril (sq) / Ramace (ops) 1.25 or 2.5mg, 0 + 0 + 1. (Use any one of – ACEI / ARNI / Vasodilator) ✔ Tab. Spironolactone / Spirocard (pop) / Inospiron (incp) / Spiretic (drg) 50 or 25 mg, (1 + 0 + 0) or (1 + 1 + 0) ✔ Tab. Carvedilol / Cardex (ops) / Carvista (incp) / Raditrend (rad) 3.125 mg (1+0+1) or 6.25 mg ( ½ +0+ ½ ) (after meal), may increase dose at least 2 weeks interval to 6.25 mg, then to 12.5 mg, then to 25 mg, twice daily.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Antibiotics like Tab. Azithromycin / Azin 500 mg (0+1+0) or Ceftriaxone 1g (1 vial I/V stat & BD) may also be used. ► If BP is normal (SBP > 90 mmHg) / higher than furosemide can be used more frequently up to 8 amp / 160 mg per day even more (if respiratory distress continues.) ► Nebulization with Salbutamol & Ipratropium / Windel plus stat & 6 hourly may added for symptomatic relief. ► If pathological Q in ECG then LVF due to IHD & diagnosis is OMI (ant) c̄ LVF, Add – ☼ Tab. Aspirin / Carva (sq) 75 mg, 0 + 1 + 0 (A/M). ☼ Tab. Clopidogrel / Odrel (bex) 75 mg, 0 + 1 + 0 (A/M). ☼ Tab. Glyceryl Trinitrate / Nitrin SR (hc) 2.6 mg, 1+0+1+0. ☼ Tab. Ramipril / Ripril (sq) 2.5 mg, 1 + 0 + 1. ☼ Tab. Carvedilol / Carvista (incp) 6.25 mg, ½ + 0 + ½. ☼ Tab. Atorvastatin / Atova (bex) 20 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 (B/M). ± ☼ Tab. Trimetazidine 35 mg / Metacard MR, 1 + 0 + 1.

treatment.title: Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Model Prescription : (+) Exertional SOB (+) Bi-pedal edema. (+) Orthopnea, Paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea / PND ( ± ) IHD, DM, HTN. Inv : • ECG. • CXR P/A view. • CBC with ESR. • S. Creatinine – Normal. • Echo 2D & M – mode Antero-septal hypokinesia. Treatment : ► Tab. Aspirin / Ecosprin (acm) 75mg 0+1+0 – continue (A/M) ► Tab. Pantoprazole / Pantid (ops) 20 mg, 1+0+1 – 1 month (B/M) ► Tab. Furosemide + Spironolactone / Edeloss (incp) 20/50 mg or 40/50 mg, 1+1+0 – 7 days. ► Tab. Ramipril / Ramoril (incp) 2.5 mg, 0+0+1 – continue ► Tab. Carvedilol / Carvista (incp) 25 mg, ½ + 0 + ½ - continue ► Tab. Nitroglycerin / Anril SR (sq) / Trocer SR (incp) 2.6 mg, 1+1+0 or, 1+0+1+0 – continue. ► Tab. Atorvastatin / Tiginor (incp) 10 mg, 0+0+1 – continue (B/M) Relative contraindication for amlodipine is – Heart failure.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: VALVULAR HEART DISEASE (Refer to cardiac surgeon / CTVS department) Patient complaints of – ○ palpitation, ○ exertion dyspnea / SOB, cough, ○ ± hemoptysis, ○ ± chest pain. On CVS examination : MITRAL STENOSIS (MS) – ○ Pulse low volume, ○ Irregularly irregular if atrial fibrillation, ○ Mid diastolic murmur in mitral area. MITRAL REGURGITATION (MR) – ○ Pulse high volume, ○ Irregularly irregular if atrial fibrillation, ○ Pansystolic murmur in mitral area radiates to left axilla. AORTIC STENOSIS (AS) – ○ Pulse low volume, ○ Narrow pulse pressure (low systolic BP), ○ Ejection systolic murmur in aortic area radiates to right side of the neck. AORTIC REGURGITATION (AR) – ○ Pulse high volume, ○ BP-high systolic and low diastolic, ○ Early diastolic murmur in left parasternal area best heard in sitting and leaning forward position of the patient, breath hold in expiration, there may be an ejection (mid) systolic murmur present in aortic area.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Definite Management: Refer the patient to cardiac surgeon (CTVS) Medical management : For all the valvular diseases is almost same. 1. Diet – normal. 2. Avoid strenuous exercise. 3. O2 inhalation SOS. 4. Tab. Phenoxymethyl penicillin (250mg) 1 tab 12 hourly (if rheumatic in origin). 5. Tab. Frusemide (40 mg, 1 + 1 + 0) (if patient complaint SOB and features of heart failure). 6. Tab. Digoxin 0.25 mg 1 tab at night for 6 doses in a week if fast atrial fibrillation. Digoxin decreases heart rate. 7. Tab. Aspirin (75 mg) daily if atrial fibrillation. 8. In MR and AR – if systemic hypertension is present, it should be treated with vasodilators such as ACE inhibitors or ARBs.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ VHD / MS Adv. : ▪ ECG ▪ CXR ▪ Echo Tx, ☼ Diet : Liquid/Normal (Salt restricted). ☼ Bed rest & avoid strenuous exercise. ☼ High flow O 2 inhalation stat & sos. If rheumatic in origin : ☼ Tab. Phenoxymethyl Penicillin (V) / Oracyn-K (syn) / Penvik (sq) 250 mg, 1+0+1, [not practiced now] If HF [shortness of breath (SOB)] : ☼ Tab. Frusemide / Fusid (sq) / Lasix (syn) 40mg, 1+1+0 Or, ☼ Tab. Frusemide 40 mg + Sopironolactone 50 mg / Frulac-40 (ori) / Edeloss Plus (incp) / Diretic-DS (drg), 1+0+0. ☼ Tab. Ramipril / Ramoril (incp) 2.5 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 If AF : ☼ Tab. Digoxin 0.25 mg / Agoxin (aris) / Centoxin (ops), 0 + 0 + 1 (except Friday). ± ☼ Tab. Metoprolol / Betaloc (drg) / Selomet (uni) 50 mg, 1+0+1. ☼ Tab. Aspirin / Ecospirin (acm) 75 mg, 0 + 1 + 0 (A/M) Or, ☼ Tab. Rivaroxaban / Rivarox (skf) 10 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 (A/M). Never prescribe beta blocker (Metoprolol) and rate limiting CCB (Diltiazem) simultaneously. Choose any one among – Metoprolol, D iltiazem & Digoxin.

treatment.title: Features & Jones Criteria
treatment.description: Approach to a patient with suspected acute rheumatic fever. Fever, joint pain, skin rash. ↓ Acute rheumatic fever suspected on the basis of history and clinical examination. Do relevant investigations : CBC, ESR, CRP, ASO titre, ECG, Throat culture, if feasible. ↓ Apply Revised Jones Criteria : Major criteria : ● Carditis (Clinical and/or subclinical (echocardiographic valvulitis) ● Arthritis: Monoarthritis or polyarthritis, polyarthralgia ● Chorea ● Erythema marginatum ● Subcutaneous nodules Minor criteria ● Mono-arthralgia ● Fever ( ≥ 38°C) ● ESR ≥ 30 mm/1 st hr and/or CRP ≥ 3.0 mg/dL. ● Prolonged PR interval on ECG Evidence of preceding group A streptococcal infection : ● Increased or rising ASO titer, or ● Positive throat culture for group A β -hemolytic streptococci. ↓ Diagnose : ► Initial acute rheumatic fever : 2 major manifestations or 1 major plus 2 minor manifestations PLUS evidence of preceding group A streptococcal infection. ► Recurrent acute rheumatic fever : 2 major or 1 major and 2 minor or 3 minor manifestations PLUS evidence of preceding group A streptococcal infection. Note : Joint manifestations can only be considered in either the major or minor categories but not both in the same patient. Here, CBC = complete blood counts, CRP = C-reactive protein, ESR = erythrocyte sedimentation rate, ASO = Antistreptolysin O, ECG = electrocardiogram.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment of rheumatic fever (RF) : Acute rheumatic fever is diagnosed. ↓ ► General management including rest and nutrition. ► Eradicate streptococcal infection : ● Phenoxymethylpenicillin 500 mg (child: 15 mg/kg up to 500 mg) orally 6-hourly for 10 days, Or, ● Azithromycin 500 mg (child: 12 mg/kg up to 500 mg) orally daily for 5 days, if allergic to penicillin. ► Treat arthritis : ● Naproxen 250-500 mg (child 10–20 mg/kg/day) orally twice daily, up to a maximum of 1250 mg daily, Or, ● Ibuprofen 200-400 mg (child 5-10 mg/kg) orally three times daily, up to a maximum of 2400 mg daily, Or, ● Aspirin 50-60 mg/kg/day orally, in four to five divided doses, up to a maximum of 80-100 mg/kg/day. ► Treat carditis : ● Prednisone/prednisolone 1 to 2 mg/kg up to a maximum of 80 mg orally, once daily or in divided doses, for severe carditis. ● Standard mx of heart failure, including ACE inhibitor, frusemide, spironolactone, and nitrate in symptomatic cases. ↓ Follow up : Look for wellbeing of the patient, symptoms and side effect of drugs. Do investigations : CBC including ESR, CRP, ECG, and echocardiography, if available. Look for complications. ↓ Offer secondary prophylaxis : ● Benzathine benzylpenicillin G (BPG) 1,200,000 units (child ≥ 20 kg : 1,200,000 units) deep IM every 21 days, Or, ● Phenoxymethylpenicillin 250 mg orally 12-hourly 1 hour before meal, Or, ● Erythromycin 250 mg orally twice daily, if allergic to penicillin. Duration of secondary prophylaxis (Australian guideline 2020): ● Definite acute rheumatic fever with no cardiac involvement: Minimum of 5 years after most recent episode of acute rheumatic fever, or until age 21 years (whichever is longer). ● Definite acute rheumatic fever, with cardiac involvement: Minimum of 10 years after the most recent episode of acute rheumatic fever or until age 40 years (whichever is longer). ↓ Refer, if any complications, e.g., refractory heart failure, arrhythmia, or Sydenham’s chorea.

treatment.title: Evaluation
treatment.description: Palpitations are a sensory symptom and are often described as a skipped beat, rapid fluttering in the chest, pounding sensation in the chest or neck, or a flip-flopping in the chest. Most common causes : ○ Anxiety. ○ Anemia. ○ Drugs – salbutamol, theophylline etc. ○ Thyrotoxicosis. ○ Rarely patient with heart failure / valvular heart disease / SVT / AF may present with solo complaint of palpitation. History : ► Menstrual loss/ less dietary intake/ recent pregnancy/symptoms hypothyroidism/bleeding hemorrhoids → Anemia. ► Heat intolerance, weight loss, tremor, excessive sweating → thyrotoxicosis. Questions to differentiate cardiac causes : • Continuous — sinus tachycardia, hyperdynamic circulation, persistent AF. • Intermittent — SVT, VT, paroxysmal AF. • Heart beat regular — SVT, VT, sinus tachycardia, hyperdynamic circulation. • Heart beat irregular — ectopic beats, AF. • Abrupt onset — SVT, VT. • Terminates with vomiting, defecation, valsalva — SVT. • Associated with chest pain, lightheadedness, polyuria–SVT. • History of mitral valvular disease — AF. • History of CAD, previous MI, cardiomyopathy — VT.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Tension type headache is the most common (70%) cause of headache. Must fulfill the following criteria of headache : - Episodic or chronic. - Gradual onset, radiate forward from occipital zone. - Bilateral, dull, tight, band like pain. - Less in morning, pain increase as day goes on (evening). - No accompanying nausea, vomiting, throbbing ache, sensitivity to light, sound or movement. Model Prescription : During pain : ► Tab. Paracetamol 500mg, 1+1+1 or, 2+2+2 (after meal) - 5 days If not controlled with paracetamol : ► Tab. Tolfenamic acid 200mg / Tufnil (skf) / Tolfem (sq) / Arain (ops) / Mygan (che), 1+0+1 (after meal) ---- 1 – 3 days ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Seclo (sq) / Proceptin (bex) 20 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 (30 min before meal) ---- 1 – 3 days If accompanied with insomnia : ► Tab. Bromazepam 3 mg / Bopam (sq) / Laxotanil (rad) / Laxyl (sq) / Tenil (acm), 0 + 0 + 1 --- 5 – 30 days In recurrent headache & anxiety disorder : ► Tab. Propranolol 20 or 40 mg/ Indever (aci)/ Propranol (ops), 1 + 0 + 1 ---- 3 months For chronic tension headache : ► Tab. Amitriptyline / Tryptin (sq) / Amit (gen) 10 or 25 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 or, 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 3 – 6 months May also add muscle relaxant : E.g. baclofen 5 to 10 mg 8 hourly for 7 to 10 days.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: To diagnose must fulfill the following criteria of headache : ● Severe unilateral and throbbing headache, usually persist for 4-72 hours, associated with nausea, vomiting, photophobia etc. ● Patient usually bed bound during an acute attack, movement makes the pain worse, patients prefer to lie in a quiet, dark room. Three times more common in women, after puberty to middle age. Usually during pregnancy, migraine status is improved. Precipitating factors : Stress, food (chocolate, cheese), alcohol, menstruation, OCP, caffeine. Model Prescription : Acute attack management : ► Tab. Zolmitriptan 2.5 mg / Nomi (sq), 1+0+1 ---- 3 days [If not controlled repeat after 2 hours of 1st dose. Total 4 tabs can be given in 24 hrs.] ► Tab. Tolfenamic acid 200 mg / Tufnil (skf) / Tolfem (sq), 1+0+1 (A/M) ---- 3 days ► Tab. Domperidone 10mg / Deflux (bex) / Domin (ops), 1+0+1 (½ hr B/M) ---- 3 days ± ► Tab. Bromazepam 3 mg / Bopam (sq) / Laxotanil (rad), 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 3 – 7 days Migraine Prophylaxis : If attacks are frequent (>2 attacks per month), use any one : ► Tab. Flunarizine / Flurizin (sq) / Minium (ops) / Fluzin (acm) / Fluver (aci) / Norium (skf) 10 mg, (sedative effect), 0 + 0 + 1 ----- 3 – 12 months [5-10 mg OD] Or, ► Tab. Propranolol 40 mg Indever (aci) / Propranol (ops), 1 + 0 + 1 ----- 3 – 12 months [40-120mg BD] [Use if associated with anxiety & palpitation, no sexual urgency] Or, ► Tab. Pizotifen / Migranil (sq) / Pizo-A (acm) / Pizofen (nav) / Zofen (aris) 1.5 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 3 – 12 months Or, ► Tab. Amitriptyline / Tryptin (sq) / Amit (gen) 25 mg, 0+0+1 ---- 3 – 12 months [10-75 mg at night] (sedative effect) Or, ► Tab. Topiramate / Topirva (incp) / Etopira (uni) / Topmate (ren) 25 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ---- 3 – 12 months [25-50mg BD] [Teratogenic, interferes with OCP efficacy] Discussion : ► When pain is about to start 5-HT agonists (Triptans) are use. Sumatriptan may use instead of Zolmitriptan. ○ Tab. Sumatriptan 100 mg / Nomigran (ambee) – 1 tab stat. Or, Sumatriptan nasal spray / Suma (acm) – 1 puff stat. Contraindications of all triptans : IHD, CVD, uncontrolled HTN, SSRIs, ergot.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : 1. This is characterized by unilateral lancinating facial pain, most commonly involving the second and/or third divisions of the trigeminal nerve territory, usually in patients over the age of 50 years. 2. The pain is repetitive, severe and very brief (seconds or less). It may be triggered by touch, a cold wind or eating. Management : 1. Carbamazepine up to 1200 mg daily should be started at low dose. Who cannot tolerate it add gabapentin or pregabaline. 2. Injection of alcohol or phenol in to a peripheral branch of the nerve. 3. Decompression of the vascular loop encroaching on the trigeminal root. ► Tab. Pregabalin / Lyrinex CR (nipro) / Gabarol CR (aci) / Neurolin CR (sq) / PG CR (skf) / Gapa-P CR (ren) Nervalin CR (bex) 82.5 or, 165 or, 330 mg, 0 + 0 + ½ ––– 7 days, then, 0 + 0 + 1 –– 1 – 3 months. Pregabalin tablet total daily dose (dosed 2 or 3 times daily): Pregabalin CR tablet/capsule dose (dosed once a day).

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Bell's palsy / Facial nerve palsy Possible symptoms of Bell's palsy : ▪ Droopy eyelid, dry eye, or excessive tears. ▪ Facial paralysis twitching, or weakness. ▪ Drooping corner of mouth, dry mouth, impaired taste. Treatment : ● Patient should wear spectacle when go outside. ● Eye should be tapped shut overnight. ● Artificial tear & ointment. ¤ Tearfresh liquigel 10mg/ml, 1 drop in affected eye 4-6 times daily or if dry eye sensation ---- 4 weeks ● Prednisolone 40-60 mg (1 mg kg in morning) daily for 7 – 14 days (should be started within 72 hours) : ¤ Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan (incp) / Deltasone (ren) / Precodil (ops) / Predimax (acm) 20 mg, 2+1+0 (A/M) ----- 7 – 14 days ¤ Cap. Omeprazole / Seclo (sq) / Ometid (ops) / Losectil (skf) / Procetin (bex) 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ----- 7 – 14 days ± ● Anti-viral (traditional but not recommended) : ¤ Tab. Acyclovir 400 mg / Virus (sq) / Novirux (drg) / Xovir (bacn) 400 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ---- 10 days If no recovery within 3 weeks or being complicated refer to neurologist or ophthalmologist or plastic surgeon.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Alzheimer's disease / Dementia Alzheimer's disease is the most common cause of dementia — a gradual decline in memory, thinking, behavior and social skills. - Common in old age ( - Unable to remember recent events. - Temporary loss of remembering address, phone number etc. Investigations : - CT scan : cerebral senile atrophy. Treatment, ► Tab. Donepezil 5 mg / Dimenta (hc) / Ameloss (incp), 0+0+1 --- 1 month, then, 0+0+2 ---- continue. ► Antidepressant in depressive patient.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Parkinson's Disease (Refer to a specialist) ¤ Older patient with insidious onset of resting tremor, bradykinesia, rigidity and festinate gait. Patient present with weakness, difficulty in walking. ¤ Rigidity of the limbs present, patient walk by short strides and turning of the patient will be slow. On examination – Appearance: Masked face, Cogwheel rigidity, normal sensory, cerebellar and planter responses. Treatment : For rigidity and bradykinesia : ► Tab. Levodopa 100 mg + Carbidopa 10 mg / Syndopa (sun) / Perkidopa (sq) / Co-dopa (uni) / D-dopa (drg), 1 + 1 + 1 ----- 1 month then titrate according to response 8 or 12 hourly increased up to 1000 mg/day (May start with 50 mg). For tremor : Anticholinergic to improve tremor & rigidity. ► Tab. Trihexyphenidyl / Hexinor (bacn) / Trihexy (incp) 2 mg, 1 + 0 + 0 --- 5 days, then, 1 + 0 + 1 --- 5 days, then, 1 + 1 + 1 ---- continue, then titrate according to 6-10 mg/day, max up to 15 mg/day. Discussion & options – ● Physiotherapy & speech therapy. ● Anticholinergic-improve tremor & rigidity. ¤ Trihexyphenidyl (1-4 mg 8 hourly) [available 1 mg, 2 mg, 4 mg]. ¤ Orphenadrine 50-100 mg 8 hourly. ● Dopamine receptor agonist : ¤ Bromocryptine 1 mg initially then 2.5 mg 8 hourly up to 30 mg/day. ¤ Pergolide 50 µgm (starting dose) increased to 250 µgm 8 hourly up to 3000 μ g/day. ● Anti-dyskinetic medicine : Amantadine (dose 100 mg, 8 or 12 hrly), to control dyskinesia produced by dopaminergic treatment. ● COMT inhibitors : Entacapone 200 mg with each dose of levodopa. This prolonged the effect of levodopa & allows levodopa dose to be reduced & given less frequently.

treatment.title: Extrapyramidal Syndrome (EPS) / Drug-induced movement disorders
treatment.description: C/C : - Spasm of muscle of tongue, neck, face etc. - Drug history like antipsychotic (Haloperidol). Treatment, ► Stop the drug causes EPS. ► Tab. Procyclidine 5 mg / Perkinil (sq) / Cyclid (incp), ½ + ½ + ½ ---- 3 days For more, please see Manic attack of BPD in psychiatric patient.

treatment.title: Tardive Dyskinesia
treatment.description: Tardive Dyskinesia C/C : Involuntary movement of tongue. Treatment, ► Tab. Tetrabenzine / Revocon (sun) / Tetrazin (bacn) 12.5 mg, 1+0+0 --- continue

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: ● Unpleasant sensations in the legs that are relieved by moving. Sensations are temporarily relieved by movement, but returns upon cessation of movement. Partially or totally relieved by movement such as walking or stretching, at least as long as the activity continues. ● It can sometimes involve both arms, typically legs bilaterally. ● It occurs when the patient is tired in the evenings and at the onset of sleep (worse in late evening and earlier part of night). ● Patient can present with daytime somnolence due to disturbed night-time sleep. Treatment : ► Tab. Ropinirole / Perkirol (sq) / Ropinol (incp) 0.25 mg or 2 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 1 to 3 hour before bedtime, increase dose (increment of 0.25mg to 4mg) every 7 days until relief of symptoms or maximum dose (4mg/day) is reached. ± ► Tab. Clonazepam (0.5-2 mg) at night for 15 days. Other options : (Any one) ¤ Tab. Levodopa 100-200 mg or dopamine agonist at night. ¤ Tab. Pramipexole 180 mcg / Parixol (aci), 0+0+1 --- 1 hour before bedtime. Increase dose every 5-7 days until relief of symptoms or maximum dose (540 micrograms daily) is reached. ¤ Tab. Gabapentin 600 mg, 0+0+1 --- 1 hour before bedtime, Increase dose every 5-7 days until relief of symptoms or maximum dose (2400mg) is reached. ¤ If ferritin

treatment.title: AFEBRILE UNCONSCIOUSNESS
treatment.description: AFEBRILE UNCONSCIOUSNESS (Refer & hospitalization) Focal Neurological Deficit (e.g., Hemiparesis, Extensor Plantar Response, Sudden / Insidious Onset) → Stroke / TIA H/O Associated convulsions → Epilepsy Neck Stiffness ± H/O Headache, Vomiting → Sub-Arachnoid Hemorrhage (SAH) Circumstantial evidence of poisoning (Drug Strips, Smell, H/O ingestion) / Trauma → Poisoning / Head Injury Dehydration, abdominal distension (discomfort) & Hyperglycemia (usually >24mmol/L) → DKA / HHS Acidotic breathing and low urine output → Uremic Encephalopathy Jaundice & stigmata of CLD (ascites) → Hepatic Encephalopathy H/O Vomiting, diarrhea, H/O diuretics → Hyponatremia Young lady with moving eyeball & eyelids, no systemic abnormality. → Malingering / HCR

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: EPILEPSY / STATUS EPILEPTICUS (মৃগীরোগ / খিঁচুনি) (Refer to neuro-medicine specialist) Usually previously diagnosed case of epilepsy, patient usually present with convulsion which is not going in remission or convulsion continued without patient regaining consciousness. Patient may have history of sudden stop of anticonvulsant drug. Recurrent Seizures occur unpredictably at any time and without any relationship to posture or ongoing activities. Nonspecific changes such as headache, mood alterations, lethargy, and myoclonic jerking hours before seizure occurs. Usually no H/O Fever and nonspecific infections. Investigation – diagnosis is clinical. To exclude secondary cause – blood glucose, serum calcium, serum electrolytes should be done.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ EPILEPSY Adv. : ▪ RBS ▪ S. Electrolytes with Calcium and Magnesium According to suspect : ▪ SGPT ▪ Serum bilirubin ▪ S. Urea ▪ CBC ▪ ECG Treatment, ✔ Maintain Airway, Breathing and Circulation ✔ O ₂ Inhalation stat and sos. ✔ Change Posture 2 hourly. ✔ NPO-TFO. ✔ Inf. N/S 1L, I/V @ 10 d/m. [Don't use glucose containing fluid] ✔ Inj. Diazepam (10mg/2ml) / Sedil (sq) / Easium (ops), 1 amp I/V stat and repeat once only, after 15 min if no response. Or, ✔ Inj. Lorazepam (4mg/1ml) / Lorazepam (incp) / Lorapam (pop), 1 ml slow I/V stat and repeat once only if no response after 15 min. Or, ✔ Tab. Midazolam 7.5 mg / Milam (skf) / Dormicum (rad) / Hypnofast (incp) / Dormitol (sq), 1 ½ tab stat buccally or intra-nasally. ► No value of giving I/M Inj. Or oral formulation in active convulsion.

treatment.title: Emergency Mx (Status epilepticus)
treatment.description: Emergency Mx (Status epilepticus) : If seizure continues after 30 minutes. Resuscitation / Clearing the airway, O 2 inhalation ↓ If seizures continue for > 5 mins : Give Midazolam (7.5 mg tablet) 10 mg (1 ½ tab), buccally or nasally. Or, Lorazepam 4mg/ml (1 amp) IV if access available. Or, Diazepam 10mg/2ml (1 amp) rectally or IV if necessary; ↓ Repeat once only after 15 min. ↓ If seizure continued after 30 min then, IV Fosphenytoin 15 mg/kg at 5O mg/minute. Or, IV Phenobarbital 10 mg/kg at 100 mg/minute. ↓ If seizure still continued after 30 min then, Start treatment for refractory status with intubation with general anaesthesia with propofol or thiopental sodium. ↓ Once status controlled-identify and treat the cause and start longer term anticonvulsant medication with one of the following – a) Sodium valproate 10 mg/kg, IV over 3-5 min, then 800-2000mg/day. b) Carbamazepine 400 mg by NG tube then 400-1200mg/day.

treatment.title: Types of seizure
treatment.description: ► Difference between seizure and pseduoseizure. True seizure / epilespsy have – • Tongue bite, • Incontinence (Urinary / fecal), • Post ictal amnesia, • Fixed eyeball. Pseudo-seizure needs psychiatric management. But in emergency diazepam is enough. ► • Recurrent Seizure / Epilepsy = Sensory + Motor component. • Absence Seizure / Petit-mal = Sensory component only. • Pesudo-convulsion = Motor component only (Psychiatric). Sensory manifestation / Absence Seizure / Petit-mal : - Lack of movement / Behavior arrest. - Lapse in awareness. - Blank stare. - Especially in childhood. Motor Manifestations : Clonic : Rhythmical jerking movements. Tonic : Tense & rigid muscle. Atonic : Weak & limp muscle. Myoclonus : Brief muscle twisting. Epileptic spasm : Body flexes & extends repeatedly. So, abnormal movements are not necessary to prove Recurrent Seizure / Epilepsy. Abnormal movement of body is motor involvement is called convulsion that may be due to organic cause / Psychiatric cause (Malingering / GAD / HCR). Please see psychiatry portion of GP Diary. ► For drug selection divide Epilepsy in above three types. 1. Sensory + Motor component = Recurrent Seizure / Epilepsy. (i) Without obvious pathology = General / Primary seizures : 1 st choice : Sodium-Valproate. (ii) With obvious brain (localized) pathology = Partial / Focal / Secondary seizures : 1 st choice : Carbamazepine. 2. Sensory component only = Absence Seizure / Petit-mal : 1 st choice : Ethosuximide. 3. Motor component only = Exclude organic causes, if HCR then Psychiatric Mx. Simple guideline for choice of antiepileptic drugs : 1. Primary seizure/generalized seizure – sodium valproate. 2. Secondary/partial seizure – carbamazepine. 3. Absence seizure – ethosuximide.

treatment.title: Discharge Notes
treatment.description: Discharge of Epilepsy : Primary Generalized seizure : ○ Tab. Sodium valproate / Epilim Chrono (syn) / Valex CR (incp) 200 mg or 300 mg, 1+0+1 --- continue ○ Withdrawing antiepileptic therapy, seizure-free at least 2 yrs. ○ Side effects : Nausea, teratogenicity, pancreatitis, liver failure, hair loss, ataxia, tremor, thrombocytopenia. ○ Follow up : SGPT, SGOT, S. bilirubin. Secondary Partial seizure : ○ Tab. Lamotrigine 50mg, ½ +0+0 – 7 days, then, 1+0+0 – continue. ○ Withdrawing antiepileptic therapy, seizure-free at least 2 yrs. ○ Side effects : Diplopia, blurred vision, photosensitivity, tremor, agitation, vomiting. Or, ○ Tab. Carbamazepine / Tegretol CR (nova) / Zeptol CR (sun) 200 mg, 1+0+1 --- continue ○ Tab. Phenobarbitone / Barbit 30 mg (incp), 1+0+1 ---- continue ○ Withdrawing antiepileptic therapy, seizure-free at least 2 yrs. ○ Side effects : Leucopenia, diplopia, blurred vision, drowsiness, impaired balance, rash, SIADH. Choice of drug during pregnancy : ○ Carbamazepine or, Lamotrigine. Absence Seizure / Petit-mal (in child) : ○ Syp. Ethosuximide (250 mg/5 ml) / Serontin (aci), ৩ - ৬ বছর পর্যন্ত : ১ চামচ × ১ বার ...... চলবে ৬ বছরের পর : ১ চামচ × ২ বার ........ চলবে উপদেশ : (১) পানি, আগুন ও ধারালো বস্তু থেকে যথাসম্ভব দূরে থাকবেন। (২) সাঁতার কাটা, গাড়ি চালানো, নিরাপত্তা বেল্ট ছাড়া উচ্চস্থানে কাজ করা থেকে বিরত থাকবেন। সহকর্মী বা সহপাঠীদের অসুখ সম্পর্কে জানিয়ে রাখুন, যেন খিঁচুনির সময় তারা ভীত না হয়ে আপনাকে নিরাপদভাবে রাখতে পারেন। (৩) নিয়মিত ঔষুধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে এই সমস্যার জন্য পূর্বের সব কাগজপাতিসহ নিউরো-মেডিসিন বা নিউরোলজি বহি:বিভাগে দেখা করবেন। পরিবারের সদস্যদের প্রতি উপদেশ : (১) খিঁচুনির সময় পানি, আগুন ও ধারালো বস্তু থেকে যথাসম্ভব দূরে রাখবেন। (২) অল্পসময়ের মধ্যে খিঁচুনি ভালো হবে, সময়টুকু ধৈর্য্য ধরুন ও রোগীকে মাটিতেই জড়িয়ে ধরে রাখুন। (৩) খিঁচুনির সময় জিহবাতে কামড় লাগলেও জোর করে চামচ বা অন্য কিছু ঢুকাবেন না। স্যান্ডেল বা মোজা শুঁকানো থেকে বিরত থাকুন।

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Transient ischemic attack (TIA) may be defined as a brief episode of neurological dysfunction caused by focal brain or retinal ischemia, with clinical symptoms typically lasting less than one hour and without evidence of acute infarction. Whereas stroke may be defined as rapidly developing clinical signs of focal (or global) disturbance of cerebral function, with symptoms lasting 24 hours or longer or leading to death, with no apparent cause other than of vascular origin. Presenting features of TIA : ● Transient weakness, numbness or paralysis of face, arm or leg, typically on one side of your body. ● Transient slurred or garbled speech or difficulty in understanding others. ● Transient blindness in one or both eyes or double vision. ● Curtain like appearance in front of eye (Amaurosis fugax). ● Transient dizziness or loss of balance or coordination. [Ref – National guidelines on management of stroke at primary health care level, NCDC, DGHS, 2021-22, Page – 5]

treatment.title: Types of Stroke
treatment.description: The stroke classifications may be : Stroke (vascular origin) – • Arterial >99% • Venous Arterial Stroke – • Ischemic 85% • Hemorrhage 15% Ischemic stroke is again classified as - a. Total anterior Circulation Stroke (TACS) (65%) • Partial anterior circulation stroke (PACS) 30% • Lacunar syndrome (LACS) 20% • Posterior circulation syndrome (POCS) 15% b. Posterior Circulation (20%) • Posterior circulation stroke (20%) Hemorrhagic stroke is again classified as - • Intra-cerebral hemorrhage (10%) • Sub-arachnoid hemorrhage (5%) [Ref – National guidelines on management of stroke at primary health care level, NCDC, DGHS, 2021-22, Page – 6]

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Acute stroke (Refer for hospitalization & neurologist intervention) “FAST” approach of stroke recognition & seek timely care – • Facial drooping / asymmetry. • Arm weakness. • Speech difficulty / slurring. • Time (hospitalization without delay). Presenting features of stroke : ¤ Sudden numbness or weakness in the face, arm or leg, especially on one side of the body. ¤ Sudden onset of aphasia, slurring of speech, ¤ Impairment or Loss of consciousness (LOC). ¤ Sudden trouble in seeing in one or both eyes. ¤ Severe headache with no known cause. ¤ Sudden trouble walking, dizziness, loss of balance or lack of coordination. [Ref – National guidelines on management of stroke at primary health care level, NCDC, DGHS, 2021-22, Page – 5] On examination – No history of fever & trauma, hemiplegia and unilateral planter extensor / equivocal. Investigation : • Immediate CT scan of brain / head to confirm stroke and its type: - Ischemic, Hemorrhagic or Subarachnoid hemorrhage. CT scan of (MRI with DWI scan, if presented after 7 days). • If CT not available, clinical suspicion : ✓ Ischemic : acute neurological deficit, that may be transient ( ✓ Hemorrhagic : as above and commonly associated with headache, vomiting. ✓ Sub-arachnoid hemorrhage (SAH) : thunderclap headache, neck rigidity. Others – RBS, S. creatinine, Lipid profile, ECG. Diagnosis : Acute stroke (haemorrhagic / ischemic) c̄ left/right sided hemiparesis c̄ ………… Differentiate ischemic vs hemorrhagic stroke : Trait Ischemic stroke Hemorrhagic stroke Onset Slowly usually during sleep. Sudden usually during excitement or exertion. Headache No / Less Severe Vomiting Less Common Blood pressure High (or normal) High Morality rate Lower Higher Paralysis More Less Hospitalization Neuro-surgery Neuro-medicine ward

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Management : ✔ If patient unconscious and/or dysphagia persists for more than 48 hours – NG tube feeding 200 ml 2 hourly in sitting posture and maintain the posture for 10-20 minutes after feeding. NG tube should be given after 24 hours and should not be given if patient have any SOB or aspiration. ► In conscious patient no dietary restriction needed. But semi-conscious / un conscious patient must need NG tube feeding 2L / per day, but in Hemorrhagic stroke with ventricular extension need more fluid 2.5 L – 3 L/ day because increased CSF will drain the blood. In case of cerebral edema fluid be restricted to 1.5 L/day, because not no worsen the edema. ✔ Change the posture 2 hourly. ✔ Continue catheterization : If acute retention or incontinence. ✔ Care of mouth, eye & bowel. Miconazole oral gel, apply TDS on oral cavity. Eye drop Chloramphenicol 5mg/ml [If patient can't close his eyes, 1 drop in each eye 3-6 times daily]. ✔ O 2 inhalation 2-4 L/minute (only if SpO 2 ✔ IV normal saline 1L is only to maintain IV access [IV @ 10 drops/min], 5% DA is contraindicated as it become hypotonic when metabolized. Any hypotonic solution will raise blood glucose and increase size of infarction, and also aggravate cerebral edema. Use only Normal Saline (N/S). ✔ Inj. Ceftriaxone 1 g, IV BD or, Syrup. Amoxicillin 2 TSF TDS. ✔ Omeprazole (PPI) injection 40 mg BD & Omeprazole sachet 1 sachet dissolve in ½ glass water then take via NG tube 12 hourly. ✔ Paracetamol if fever. ✔ Care of the mouth, eye, bowel. ✔ Treatment of the risk factors like DM, dyslipidaemia etc. ✔ If patient is diabetic then start insulin and if not known diabetic but blood sugar is >11.1 mmol/L then start short acting insulin. ✔ After getting CT scan report : ischaemic stroke : Loading dose : Aspirin 75mg 4-tab (300 mg) stat or, (Clopidogrel 75 mg (4 tab) stat if aspirin in contraindicated, intolerant or allergic) then, ⦸ Tab. Aspirin 75 mg, 0+1+0 (A/M) ± ⦸ Tab. Clopidogrel 75 mg, 0+1+0(A/M) upto 21 days. • Ischaemic stroke with atrial fibrillation should be treated with aspirin 300 mg for the first 2 weeks followed by anticoagulation treatment. Or, ✔ After getting CT scan report : haemorrhagic stroke : ⦸ Refer to neurosurgery for burr hole evacuation. If haemorrhagic stroke with ventricular extension : (blood within brain spaces / ventricles) add – ⦸ Tab. Nimodipine 30 mg / Nimocal (sq), 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 [300-360 mg/day] • Oral nimodipine should be administered to all patients with Acute Subarachnoid Hemorrhage [SAH], 60 mg 4 hourly and continued for 21 days to prevent delayed cerebral ischemia (DCI). • Withhold all anticoagulant and antiplatelet drugs. Anticoagulant effect of warfarin should be reversed immediately with appropriate agents – Inj. Vitamin K & Heparin induced bleeding should be reversed with protamine sulfate. Life threatening intracerebral hemorrhage (warfarin heparin induced) should managed by fresh frozen plasma / fresh blood. ✔ Immediate initiation of statin treatment is not recommended in people with acute stroke but continue statin treatment in people with acute stroke who are already receiving statins. Statin should be given on discharge of both type of stroke. ⦸ Tab. Atorvastatin 20–40 mg or, Rosuvastatin 10–20 mg, daily for life long, irrespective of the lipid profile, as they decrease TG+LDL as well as plaque stabilizer. ✔ If hypoglycemia (RBS ✔ If patient is restless: ⦸ Inj. Haloperidol 5mg/1ml, 1 amp IV stat. ⦸ Inj. Procyclidine 10mg/2ml, 1 amp IV stat.

treatment.title: BP management
treatment.description: ✔ BP management : If patient is known hypertensive and taking antihypertensive drug regularly then continue taking the drug. BP Target (in acute stroke) : Ischemic Stroke : Do not lower BP unless >180/110 mmHg. Hemorrhagic Stroke : Lower BP to [ Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 75] ► Ischemic stroke : Not to lower blood pressure abruptly. Antihypertensive should be given in the following condition in ischaemic stroke if BP >220/110 mmHg and if patient has heart failure, acute renal failure, evidence of hypertensive encephalopathy, aortic dissection as per neurologist consultation. Target in BP Mx in acute stroke : • For acute ischemic stroke patients, if admission BP is >220/110 mmHg, target BP lowering by 15% during 1st 24 hours using parenteral anti-hypertensive (IV labetalol, GTN infusion). • If BP is less than the upper limit (220/110 mmHg) observe up to 72 hours then start oral anti-hypertensive if BP remains above 140/90 mmHg. Target BP should be less than 130/80 mmHg. • Early treatment of hypertension is indicated if associated with impending target organ damage (e.g., concomitant acute coronary event, acute heart failure, aortic dissection, or preeclampsia / eclampsia).

treatment.title: Haemorrhagic stroke : ICH & SAH
treatment.description: ► Haemorrhagic stroke : Intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH) : For patients with hemorrhagic stroke target blood pressure should be maintained 220 mmHg. Parenteral antihypertensive (Labetalol, Hydralazine, sodium nitroprusside, GTN infusion) should be used for aggressive BP reeducation. Patients presenting beyond 6 hours with elevated BP oral antihypertensive should be given to maintain the target blood pressure. Acute Subarachnoid hemorrhage [SAH] : • Avoid hypo and hypertension (target SBP • Preferably should be referred to higher center as early as possible where expert interventional neurologist and neurovascular surgeon are available with appropriate setup. For aneurysmal SAH, endovascular coiling or surgical clipping is the definitive treatment option and should be performed within 24 hours or as early as possible to prevent rebleeding. • Maintenance of euvolemia with normal saline. • Ensure adequate analgesia (paracetamol 500mg 4 hourly. tramadol suppository) and sedation. • Anti-fibrinolytic agents (tranexamic acid, aminocaproic acid) me for less than 72 hours to prevent early re-bleeding, if aneurysms delayed. ✔ If convulsion occurs : Tab. Phenytoin 100 mg, 3+0+0 (as per neurologist consultation).

treatment.title: Raised intracranial pressure (ICP)
treatment.description: ► Signs of raised intracranial pressure (ICP) : 1. Papilloedema (By - Fundoscopy). 2. Sixth nerve palsy. 3. Impaired up gaze. 4. Focal neurological de ﬁ cits. 5. Impaired conscious level. ✔ If there is midline shifting / Mass effect / Brain atrophy with oedema / Features of raised ICP, add – ¤ Inf. Mannitol 20% [Ensure catheterization before administration] 300 ml IV stat running, then, 150 ml running TDS for 3 days [1 gm/kg IV stat and 0.5 gm/kg 6 hourly]. • Use of osmotic therapy for patients with clinical deterioration from cerebral swelling associated with cerebral infarction is reasonable. ✔ Corticosteroids should not be administered for the treatment of cerebral edema and increased intracranial pressure. • Mannitol and hypertonic saline may be used to reduce raised ICP. • IV dexamethasone is not recommended as ICP lowering agent. • Inj. Dexamethasone / Decason (ops)/ Roxadex (nuv / Dexa (ren), 1 amp I/V stat & TDS (or BD).

treatment.title: DVT Prophylaxis and Treatment
treatment.description: ✔ Deep venous thrombosis (DVT) Prophylaxis and Treatment : Early mobilization and optimal hydration should be maintained for all acute stroke patients. In long tome bed ridden patient DVT compression stockings (পায়ের মোজা) to reduce swelling and improve blood flow. Early mobilization : All patients should be referred to a physiotherapist as soon as possible, preferably within 24 to 48 hours of admission when the vitals are stable. Passive full-range-of-motion exercises for paralyzed limbs can be started during the first 24 hours. Ischemic stroke patient low-molecular weight heparin (LMWH) (40 mg subcutaneously, S/C daily) may be given to prevent DVT of paralyzed limbs. Higher dose of Low molecular weight heparin (60 mg bid) should be used for treatment of established DVT. Or, Hemorrhagic stroke patients with ICH and SAH routine physiotherapy and early mobilization should be carried out to prevent leg vein thrombosis.

treatment.title: Therapeutic options
treatment.description: Therapeutic options at tertiary care stroke hospital for different stroke subtypes : Ischemic stroke (hyper-acute) – ○ IV thrombolysis with r-tPA – window period 4.5 hours. ○ Endovascular thrombectomy (EVT) – window period 6 hours (proximal occlusion of MCA and ICA). ○ Decompressive craniectomy – large MCA territory infarct with mass effect (Malignant MCA infarct). Eligibility criteria for intravenous thrombolysis : 1. Symptoms of acute stroke. 2. Onset to needle time 3. Clinically measurable neurological deficit. 4. Age 18 – 80 year. 5. Absence of hemorrhage or stroke mimics in baseline CT-head. Intracerebral Hemorrhage (ICH) – • Usually treated conservatively with good control of BP. • ICH score may be done for prognostic evaluation. • Hematoma evacuation and diversion surgery indicated in the following conditions : ▸ Lesions with marked mass effect. ▸ Haematoma volume : 30-60 ml. ▸ Persistently elevated ICP (failure with medical management). ▸ Rapid neurological deterioration. ▸ Young patients ( ▸ Favorable location : Lobar, Cerebellar (GCS ≤ 13 or hematoma > 4 cm), Non dominant hemisphere. • Surgery is usually not required in following conditions- ▸ Small deep hemorrhages. ▸ Lobar hemorrhage without hydrocephalus and rapid neurological deterioration ▸ A score on the GCS of below 8 unless this is because of hydrocephalus. Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (SAH) – • Endovascular coiling or clipping for aneurysmal SAH. Venous stroke (CVST) – • Low molecular weight heparin followed by oral anticoagulant. [Ref – National guidelines on management of stroke at primary health care level, NCDC, DGHS, 2021-22, Page – 23 & 24]

treatment.title: NG Feeding
treatment.description: NG Feeding For NG tube insertion please see “Procedure” portion. Requirements : ╠ BMI feeding / NG tube 14 + 16 Fr – (1+1). ╠ 50cc syringe – (1) ╠ Micropore 1” – (1) ╠ Sterile gloves 7” – (1) ╠ Paraffin / Jesocaine jelly – (1) Advise to patient attendants : 1. First to bring the patient in sitting position by holding from back or, by cushion bring the patient to propped up position. 2. Then attach 50 cc syringe to the NG tube port. 3. Pour liquid rice / jaw vat, suji, milk, soften powder / biscuit, water etc., avoid high price formulated canned foods. 4. Pour liquid food full of 50 cc syringe 4 times after each 2 hours. 5. Finally in each time pour 20 cc fresh water to prevent tube blockage. 6. During feeding the 50 cc syringe is to hold up to the nose level, so that the liquid food will spontaneously go into stomach, never to use piston of the syringe for pushing. 7. If liquid food does not go spontaneously then a mild push with the piston to the liquid food filled syringe. 8. Keep the patient even after finishing the 4 syringe food for 15 min to prevent aspiration. 9. Opening port of NG tube is opened or covered is not important, but kept close to avoid aspiration outside. 10. If patient tries to pull off the NG tube then make balls of paper / take small balls, then tie then with both palm of patient, so that patient cannot make fist or grasp anything. Or, Tie with bed.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: HOSPITAL Mx OF ACUTE STROKE 1. H/O : – Right / Left sided & generalized weakness. – Semiconscious or, unconscious, may be conscious. – ± Aphasia (unable to talk), Facial deviation. 2. O/G/E : - BP : hypertension. - Planter response (Rt & Lt) – Unilateral Extensor or Equinus*** & flexor or normal in other. Δ Acute stroke (?) c̄ right sided hemiparesis c̄ ….... c̄ ….… Adv. : ▪ CT scan of head ▪ RBS ▪ S. creatinine ▪ S. electrolytes ± ▪ Lipid profile ± ▪ ECG ✔ Diet – Normal / Diabetic. ✔ O 2 inhalation sos, if respiratory distress. ✔ Change posture 2 hourly. ✔ Pneumatic bed if prolonged disability. ✔ Catheterization (if retention or incontinence). ✔ NG tube feeding 200 ml, 2 hrly from 8 am to 10 pm in sitting posture and maintain the posture at least 20 minutes (see advice above). For maintenance of IV channel : ✔ Inj. N/S (osl) / Normasol (libra) / Saloride (bex) 1000 mL, I/V stat @ 10 d/m. ✔ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftron (sq) / Arixon (bex) / Ceftizone (ren) 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. Or, Syp. Amoxicillin (125 mg/5 ml) / Moxacil (sq) / Fimoxyl (syn), 2 TSF TDS. ✔ Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Omep (ops) / PPI (acm) / Omenix (incp), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. Or, Omeprazole sachet / Oral powder Losectil (skf) / Omenix (incp) (20 or 40 mg/sachet), 1 sachet dissolve in ½ glass water then take via NG tube 12 hourly. If diabetic & RBS >11.1 mmol/l : ✔ Inj. Regular insulin 100U / Actrapid (skf) / Ansulin R (sq) / Maxsulin R (incp), trice daily (30 min before meal) ± Basal insulin 100U (long acting) Inj. Abasaglar (hc) / Lantus (syn) / Vibrenta (incp) at night (10 PM). If patient can't close his eyes : ✔ Eye drop Chloramphenicol 0.5% / Cloram (ibn) / A-Phenicol (acm) / Opsophenicol (osl), 1 drop in each eye 4-6 times daily. ✔ Miconazole 2% / Micoral (aci) / Dakrin (gen) oral gel, apply on oral cavity TDS, if patient is unconscious. If restless : ✔ Inj. Haloperidol 5mg/1ml / Halopid (incp), 1 amp I/M stat and sos. ✔ Inj. Procyclidine 10mg/2ml / Perkinil (sq), 1 amp I/V stat and sos. After getting CT scan report – Please Add (P/A) : If CT scan of brain shows hypodense area or normal CT scan with obvious symptoms then, diagnose it as Infractive / Ischemic stroke. Ischemic stroke : ✔ Tab. Aspirin 75 mg (or Clopidogrel 75 mg if aspirin in contraindicated) 4 tab stat then, Tab. Aspirin 75mg, 0+1+0 (A/M) – lifelong. ✔ T. Clopidogrel 75mg, 0+1+0 (A/M) – 21 days ✔ Tab. Atorvastatin 20mg, 0+0+1 – lifelong. ± ✔ Tab. Ramipril 2.5 or 5 mg, 0 + 0 + 1. ± ✔ Tab. Vinpocetine 5 mg, 1 + 1 + 1. Hemorrhagic (Hg) Stroke : If CT scan of brain shows hyperdense / white area / hematoma then, diagnose it as haemorrhagic stroke & refer to neurosurgery. ✔ Syp. Lactulose/ Osmolax (sq)/ Avolac (aris), 3 TSF for TDS. ✔ Supp. Tramadol 100 mg / Anadol (sq) / Utramal (uni), 1 stick P/R stat & BD. If Sub-arachnoid hemorrhage (SAH) or, with ventricular extension (VE), add – ✔ Tab. Nimodipine 30 mg / Nimocal (sq), 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 [300 – 360mg/day] If convulsion occurs : ✔ Tab. Phenytoin 100 mg, 3+0+0. If midline shifting / Mass effect / Brain atrophy with Oedema / Features of raised ICP, add – ✔ Inf. 20% Mannitol / Osmosol 500 ml, 300 ml I/V stat running then, 150 ml I/V running TDS for 3-5 days. Catheterization before mannitol infusion. - If infarction is massive (large hypo-dense area in CT scan) not to give Aspirin ± Clopidogrel, seek senior advice. Vinpocetine is a cerebral oxygenotor, T. Piracetam 800mg/Neurolap is an alternate. ► Signs of raised intracranial pressure (ICP) : 1. Papilloedema (By – Fundoscopy). 2. Sixth nerve palsy. 3. Impaired upgaze. 4. Focal neurological de ﬁ cits. 5. Impaired conscious level.

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment on discharge : -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- ISCHEMIC STROKE Ø Cap. Cefixime / Cef-3 (sq) / Triocim (bex) 200 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ....... ৭ দিন Ø Tab. Aspirin / Ecosprin (acm) / Carva (sq) 75 mg, ০ + ১ + ০ (ভরাপেটে) ..... চলবে Ø Tab. Atorvastatin / ATV (del) / Xerova (bacn) 10 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ...... চলবে Ø Tab. Ramipril 2.5 or 5mg / Ripril (sq) / Ramace(ops) 2.5 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ...... চলবে ± Ø Tab. Vinpocetine 5 mg / Camiton (drg) / Cerevas (sq), ১ + ১ + ১ ...... ৩ মাস Ø Cap. Omeprazole / Prevas (gen) / OP (glob) 20 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (আহারের আধাঘন্টা পূর্বে) ...... ১৫ দিন Ø Tab. Clonazepam / Clonatril (hc) / Rivotril (rad) 0.5 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ...... ১৫ দিন HEMORRHAGIC STROKE Or, Intra-Cranial Hg (ICH) Ø Cap. Cefixime / Orcef (ren) / Denvar (hc) 200 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ........ ৭ দিন Ø Cap. Tramadol / Anadol (sq) / Utramal (uni) 50 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ (ব্যাথা থাকলে খাবেন) ....... ১৪ দিন Ø Syp. Lactulose / Osmolax (sq) / Dlac (drg), ৩ চামচ × ৩বার (পায়খানা নরম রাখবেন) ..... ১৪ দিন Ø Tab. Nimodipine 30 mg / Nimocal (sq), ২ + ২ + ২ + ২ + ২ + ২ ....... ২১ দিন Ø Cap. Omeprazole / Prevas (gen) 20 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (আহারের আধাঘন্টা পূর্বে) ...... ১৪ দিন Ø Tab. Clonazepam / Clonatril (hc) / Rivotril (rad) 0.5 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ........ ১৪ দিন ± Ø Tab. Dexamethasone / Decason (ops) / Dexonex(sq) 0.5 mg, ৩ + ৩ + ৩ (ভরাপেটে) ......... ৭ দিন ± Ø Tab. Phenytoin 100 mg / Diphedan (ambee), ২ + ০ + ১ / ৩ + ০ + ০....... ১ মাস উপদেশ (Ischemic + Hemorrhagic) : নিয়মিত ঔষুধ খাবেন। রক্তচাপ থাকলে তা নিয়ন্ত্রনে রাখবেন। ডায়াবেটিকস থাকলে তা নিয়ন্ত্রনে রাখবেন। স্থানীয় ডায়াবেটিকস হাসপাতালে নিবন্ধিত হবেন ও নিয়মিত চেকআপে থাকবেন। অচেতন বা বিছানাগত রোগীর জন্য – (১) নাকের নল দিয়ে শিখানো নিয়মে খাওয়াবেন ২০০ মি. লি. করে দিনে ১০ বার। নাক নল করা থাকলে ২ সপ্তাহ পর পর পরিবর্তন করবেন। (২) দুই (০২) ঘন্টা পর পর শোবার পাশ পরিবর্তন করাবেন। সারাদিন চিৎ হয়ে শুইয়ে রাখলে পিঠে বা নিতম্বে ঘাঁ হয়ে যাবে। নিউমেটিক বা ফোমের বেড ব্যবহার করতে পারেন। হাত ও পায়ের ব্যায়াম, নিয়মিত গোসল করাবেন। (৩) ক্যাথেটার করা থাকলে ২ – ৩ সপ্তাহ পর পর পরিবর্তন করবেন। রোগী প্রসাব ধরে রাখার অনুভূতি ফিরে আসলে বা স্বাভাবিক হলে আর ৭ দিনের বেশি মুত্রনালীতে নল বা ক্যাথেটার থাকলে, ক্যাথেটার ট্রায়াল দিয়ে কোনো ডাক্তারের তত্ত্বাবধানে খুলবেন। (৪) দুর্বল পাশে শেখানো নিয়মে ব্যায়াম করবেন যাতে প্রত্যেক জয়েন্টের মুভমেন্ট করাবেন। প্যারালাইসিস এর পরবর্তীতে ফিজিওথ্যারাপি বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Guillain-Barré syndrome (GBS) (Hospitalization & specialist intervention) ● Patient present with ascending type of paralysis (symmetrical) of all four limbs, bowel and bladder not affected, consciousness intact. Ascending type of paralysis (progressive & symmetrical). No sensory disturbance. No bowel and bladder involvement. May have past history H/O diarrhea. ● On examination – lower motor neuron lesion of the all four limbs, sensory normal. ● Treatment is mainly supportive & I/V Ig, but must avoid corticosteroid in GBS. Plasma exchange or IVIg (should be started within 14 days). ¤ Inj. Human immunoglobulin (IVIg) 400mg/kg IV – 5 days. ¤ Bed rest : Change posture 2 hourly. Prevention of pressure sore. ¤ Diet : NG feeding 200 ml 2 hourly (if needed). ¤ Protection of the airway. ¤ Regular monitoring of the patient with respiratory function, VC (vital capacity) & ABG. Artificial ventilation if VC

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Acute Transverse Myelitis (Hospitalization & specialist intervention) ● Sudden onset of weakness of both lower limbs, with involvement of the bowel and bladder. All modalities sensations are lost in lower limb. (usually a definitive sensory level present which help determine site of lesion). ● On examination – upper motor type neuron lesion of the lower limb (jerks exaggerated, planter bilateral extensor). Motor : Muscle tone : Increased on lower limbs and normal in upper limbs. Muscle power : Normal in upper limbs and diminished in lower limbs ● MRI of the spinal cord – confirm the diagnosis. Treatment, ► Bed rest: Change posture 2 hourly. ► Diet : NG feeding 200ml 2 hourly (if needed). ► Inj. Methylprednisolone 1 gm daily for 3 days with PPI. ¤ Inf. 5% DNS 200 cc + Inj. methylprednisolone (1 gm), I/V @ 50 drops/minute stat and daily for 3 days ► Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / PPI (acm) / Losectil (skf), 1 vial IV stat and BD for 3 days ► Bowel and bladder involvement : ¤ Continuous catheterization. ¤ If constipation : Supp. Glycerin / Glysup 2.3 gm (sq), 4 sticks P/R stat & sos. ± ► Physiotherapy. ± ► Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g / Ceftron (bex) / Arixon (bex), 1 vial I/V stat & BD.

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: MENINGITIS is an infection and inflammation of the fluid and membranes (meninges) surrounding the brain and spinal cord. The inflammation from meningitis typically triggers symptoms such as headache, fever and a stiff neck. ENCEPHALITIS : Encephalitis is inflammation of the active tissues of the brain caused by an infection or an autoimmune response. The inflammation causes the brain to swell, which can lead to headache, stiff neck, sensitivity to light, mental confusion and seizures. ► Clinical features : • Fever. • Neck rigidity / positive Kernig’s sign. • Altered consciousness. • Headache. • Photophobia. • Meningococcal rash. ► Nervous system examination : • Neck rigidity : +, • Kernig's sign : +, • Brudzinski's sign : +, • Planter : Bilateral extensor (Unilateral in stroke). ► Investigation – Resuscitate and stabilize the patient. Initial tests : • CBC • RBS • S. Creatinine • S. Electrolytes • ALT • ECG • Blood for C/S • Throat swab for gram stain and C/S Lumber puncture & CSF study : protein increase, glucose decrease and cell count (neutrophil) increase. CSF, blood and urine should be sent for C/S. Management : 1. NG tube feeding 200 ml 2 hourly in sitting posture & maintain the posture for 10 minute. 2. Change the posture 2 hourly. 3. Continued catheterization. 4. Care of mouth, eye & bowel. ○ Miconazole 2% oral gel, apply TDS on oral cavity. ○ Eye drop Chloramphenicol 5mg/ml, [If patient can't close his eyes, 1 drop in each eye 3-6 times daily]. 5. Omeprazole injection or sachet 1 packet dissolve in ½ glass water through NG tube BD.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Meningitis / Encephalitis Adv. : ▪ Lumber puncture & CSF study ▪ CBC with ESR ▪ CRP ▪ RBS ▪ S. Creatinine ▪ Blood for C/S ▪ Urine R/M/E According to suspect : ▪ CXR PA view ▪ CT scan of brain ▪ S. Electrolytes ▪ ALT ▪ ECG Treatment, If altered consciousness : ✔ NG tube feeding 200 ml each 2 hourly from 8 am to 10 pm in sitting position & maintain posture for 10 min. ✔ Change posture 2 hourly. ✔ O 2 inhalation sos, if respiratory distress. ✔ Tepid sponging. ✔ Supp. PC / Renova (ops) / Ace (sq) 500 mg, 1 stick P/R sos, if temp > 101° F. ✔ Continuous catheterization. ✔ Care of eye, skin, oral cavity & bowel. ✔ Inj. NS / 5% DNS 1000 mL, I/V stat @ 10 d/m. ✔ Inj. Esomeprazole 40 mg / Esonix (incp) / Maxpro (ren), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ✔ Inj. Dexamethasone (5mg/1ml) / Roxadex (nuv) / Decason (ops) / Dexa (ren), 2 amp (10 mg for adults) I/V stat & QDS for 2-4 days (IV steroid should be given 20 min after giving IV ceftriaxone / antibiotics). Empirically add both, then change according to senior or C/S report (bacterial meningitis) : ✔ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Oricef (hc) / Rofecin (rad) / Ceftron (sq) 2g (two gram) , 1 vial I/V stat & BD (twice daily) . ✔ Inj. Vancomycin / Covan (ren) / Vancomin (ops) 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. Add only viral encephalitis c̄ seizure or alteration of consciousness : ✔ Inj. Acyclovir / Xovir (bacn) / Virux (sq) 500mg, 1 vial I/V stat & TDS/QDS for 2-3 wks. If raised ICP : Papilloedema, altered sensorium, VI nerve palsy, convulsion or decerebrate posture, add : ✔ Inf. Mannitol 20% / Osmosol (bex) / Osmotic (pop) / Manisol (ori), 300 ml I/V stat @30d/m then 150ml I/V TDS or QDS @30 d/m daily for 3 days. [1 gm/kg or 5ml/kg IV stat and 0.5gm/kg or 2.5ml/kg 6 hourly, catheterization before mannitol bottle infusion.] If convulsion : ✔ Tab. Carbamazepine 200mg / Tegretol CR (nova) / Anleptic CR (sq) / Zeptol CR (sun), 1+0+1 or, 1+1+1. Or, ✔ Tab. Phenytoin 100mg / D-Toin (drg) / Diphedan (ambee), 1+0+2 (increase 2 to 6 tab per day).

treatment.title: BACTERIAL / PYOGENIC MENINGITIS
treatment.description: ► Empirical antibiotics (Box given below). ● Inj. Ceftriaxone (2 gm) 1 vial IV stat & BD (change antibiotic according to C/S report) + Inj. Vancomycin (1gm) 1 vial IV stat and 12 hourly ± Ampicillin. ● ± Inj. Dexamethasone 10 mg (2 amp), 20 min before giving antibiotics stat & 6 hrly for 2-4 days [0.15mg/kg QDS for 2-4 days]. Box : Treatment of pyogenic meningitis of unknown cause. 1. Adult aged 18 – 50 years without typical meningococcal rash : • Ceftriaxone 2g i.v. 12-hourly for 10 – 14 days or, • Cefotaxime 2g i.v 6-hourly. 2. Patients in whom penicillin-resistant pneumococcal infection is suspected. As for (1) but add : • Vancomycin 1g i.v. 12-hourly or, • Rifampicin 600 mg i.v. 12-hourly. 3. Adults aged over 50 years and those in whom Listeria monocytogenes infection is suspected (brain-stem signs, immunosuppression, diabetic, alcoholic). As for (1) but add : • Ampicillin 2g i.v. 4-hourly or, • Co-trimoxazole 5mg/kg i.v. daily in two divided doses. 4. Patient with a clear history of anaphylaxis to β -lactams : • Chloramphenicol 25mg/kg i.v. 6-hourly plus • Vancomycin 1g i.v. 12-hourly. Adjunctive treatment : • Dexamethasone 0.15mg/kg 4 times daily for 2 – 4 days. If Streptococcus pneumoniae, or pneumococcus meningitis suspected, steroid is used to limit CNS complications, such as hearing loss. Dexamethasone must be given 10-20 min before 1 st dose of antibiotic. Practical empirical antibiotics to be start : Ceftriaxone 2g i.v. 12-hourly + Vancomycin 1g i.v. 12-hourly (15mg/Kg BD). ± Dexamethasone.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Symptoms : • Headache • Vomiting • Low-grade fever • Lassitude • Depression • Delirium • Behaviour changes [Usually intact sensorium & not urgently hospitalized.] Signs : • Meningism (may be absent). • Oculomotor palsies. • Papilloedema. • Depression of conscious level. • Focal hemisphere signs. Staging of severity : • Stage I (early) : Non-specific symptoms and signs without alteration of consciousness. • Stage II (intermediate) : altered consciousness without coma or delirium plus minor focal neurological signs. • Stage III (advanced) : stupor or coma, severe neurological deficits, seizures and abnormal movement. Investigations : • CBC • RBS • S. Creatinine • S. Electrolytes • ALT • TSH • Chest X-ray P/A view • CSF study • CT/MRI of brain

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Management : • As soon as the diagnosis is made or strongly suspected, Anti-TB regimen should be started as per National TB guideline that include pyrazinamide. • The use of glucocorticoids in addition to anti-tuberculous therapy has been controversial. Recent evidence suggests that it improves mortality, especially if given early, but not focal neurological damage. • Surgical ventricular drainage may be needed if obstructive hydrocephalus develops. • Skilled nursing is essential during the acute phase of the illness. • Adequate hydration and nutrition must be maintained. ► Anti-tuberculous therapy on discharge : ● Tab. Rimstar (4FDC), 3+0+0 – 2 months[Mention the date], Then, ● Tab. Rimstar (2FDC), 3+0+0 – 7 months [Mention the date] Number of tablets according : ○ 30 – 37 kg : 2 Tab ○ 38 – 54 kg : 3 Tab ○ 55 – 70 kg : 4 Tab ○ >70 kg : 5 Tab Oral steroid : ● Tab. Dexamethasone 0.5 mg, 2+2+2+2 (A/M) – 1 week, Then, ● Tab. Dexamethasone 0.5 mg, 2+2+2 (A/M) –– 1 week, Then, ● Tab. Dexamethasone 0.5 mg, 2+0+2 (A/M) –– 1 week, Then, ● Tab. Dexamethasone 0.5 mg, 2+0+0 (A/M) –– 1 week + ● Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (Before meal) – 1 month ● Tab. Pyridoxine 20 mg, 1+0+0 ––– 12 months Advice : 1. Take medicine regularly. 2. If during taking rifampicin (rimstar) tear, urine may become orange colour, not to worry. 3. If white part of eyes, palm, sole becomes yellow, immediately stop medicine and contact to specialist / health center. 4. Contact after every 1 month with reports of – CBC, SGPT, serum creatinine.

treatment.title: VIRAL MENINGO-ENCEPHALITIS
treatment.description: ● Patient present with fever, headache, focal neurological signs (aphasia and/or hemiplegia, visual field defects), seizures and alteration of consciousness. ● On examination – neck rigidity and Kernig's sign absent, planter bilateral extensor. ● Investigation – CSF study. Treatment : ○ Inj. Acyclovir 500 mg, 10 mg/kg IV stat and TDS [2-3 weeks]. ○ Inj. Dexamethasone 5mg/ml, 1 – 2 amp IV stat and 8 hourly ○ Tab. Phenytoin sodium 100 mg / Tab. Carbamazepine 200 mg, (1+0+2) or, (1+1+1) ○ If fever present, Tab. P/C, 1+1+1 or, 2+2+2 (A/M) & Suppository Paracetamol 500mg, 1 stick P/R – if needed. On discharge : ● Tab. Acyclovir 200 mg, 2+2+2+2+2 ---- 14 days [Ideally, injectable should be given for 2-3 weeks] ● Tab. Phenytoin sodium 100 mg, 0+0+2. Discussion : ► If having features of raised ICP e.g., papilloedema, altered sensorium, VI nerve palsy, convulsion or decerebrate posture, add : ● Inf. Mannitol 20%, 300 ml IV stat @30 drops/min, then, 150 ml running TDS for 5 days. [1 gm/kg IV stat and 0.5 gm/kg 6 hourly] [Ensure catheterization before administration] ► Prophylaxis for close contacts in case of meningococcal meningitis : Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg, 1 tab stat, Or, Tab. Ofloxacin 400 mg, 1 tab stat, Or, Tab. Rifampicin 300 mg, 2+0+2 --- 5 days.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : ¤ The usual presentation is with headache, sometimes accompanied by diplopia and visual disturbance (most commonly, transient obscurations of vision associated with changes in posture). ¤ Clinical examination reveals papilloedema. False localizing cranial nerve palsies (usually of the 6th nerve) may be present. ¤ Investigation – brain imaging (CT scan or MRI) is usually normal but required to exclude a structural or other cause (e.g. cerebral venous sinus thrombosis). The ventricles are typically normal in size or small (‘slit’ ventricles). The diagnosis may be confirmed by lumbar puncture, which shows raised normal CSF constituents at increased pressure (usually > 30 cm H 2 O CSF). Management : 1. Weight reducing diet. 2. Avoid any precipitating condition or medication. 3. Carbonic anhydrase inhibitor-acetazolamide or topiramate may help to lower intracranial pressure. 4. Repeated lumber puncture (LP). 5. Optic nerve fenestration or lumbo-peritoneal shunt if - Patient failing to respond. - Chronic papilloedema threatens vision.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Cerebral abscess (Refer for hospitalization & specialist) Cerebral abscess may present acutely with fever, headache and drowsiness. Seizures, raised intracranial pressure (headache worse in morning, early morning vomiting, bradycardia) and focal hemisphere signs occur alone or in combination. Distinction from a cerebral tumour may be impossible on clinical grounds. Investigation : CT or MRI confirm brain abscess. Antibiotic choice guideline : Frontal lobe Cefotaxime 2-3 g IV 4 times daily, Plus, Metronidazole 500 mg IV 3 times daily. Temporal lobe Ampicillin 2-3 gm IV 8 hourly, Plus, Metronidazole 500 mg IV 8 hourly. Cerebellum Ceftazidime 2 g IV 3 times daily, or, Gentamicin 5 mg/kg IV daily. Any site Flucloxacillin 2-3 gm IV 6 hourly, or, Cefuroxime 1.5 gm IV 8 hourly. Multiple abscesses Benzylpenicillin 1.8 - 2.8 gm IV 6 hourly if endocarditis or cyanotic heart disease. Otherwise Cefuroxime 1.5 gm 8 hourly, Plus, Metronidazole 500 mg IV 8 hourly. Treatment : 1. Antibiotic – empirical antibiotic choice. 2. Anticonvulsant- carbamazepine. 3. Paracetamol if fever. 4. Surgery : Burr-hole aspiration or excision-where presence of a capsule may lead to a persistent focus of infection.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : 1. Patient is usually female and between 15 to 50 years. 2. Fatigable muscle weakness. 3. Movement is initially strong but rapidly weakens as muscle use continues. Worsening of symptoms towards the end of the day or following exercise is characteristic. 4. There are no sensory signs or signs of involvement of the CNS. 5. The first symptoms are usually intermittent ptosis or diplopia but weakness of chewing, swallowing, speaking or limb movement also occurs. The patient is unable to undertake tasks above shoulder level, such as combing the hair, without frequent rests. Investigation : 1. Tensilon test (edrophonium test) – bed side test, not done usually now a days. 2. Nerve conduction velocity (NCV) – Repetitive stimulation during nerve conduction studies may show a characteristic decremental response. Management : 1. Pyridostigmine 30-120 mg 6 hourly – ○ Tab. Pyridostigmine 60 mg / Pyrostig (pop) / Dostimid (uni), 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 2. To control diarrhea & colic – ○ Tab. Propantheline 15 mg / Prokind (bacn), 1 + 1 + 1 (30 min before meal) 3. Immunological treatment – ○ Thymectomy. ○ Plasma exchange. ○ IVIg. ○ Corticosteroid (must be started in hospital). ○ Use of other immunosuppressive agent, e.g. azathioprine 2.5 mg/kg daily.

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Classification based on presentation : • Symptomatic patient with classical features. • Asymptomatic bacteriuria is the isolation of a significant bacterial count in an asymptomatic patient. Usually no treatment needed.
