But must advise antibiotic in pregnancy with asymptomatic UTI. • Re-infection is a recurrent UTI with the same or different organism following clearance of the original UTI. Classification based on presentation : • Uncomplicated UTI is a symptomatic bladder infection with a normal urinary tract. • Complicated UTI is a symptomatic infection in any part of the urinary tract with a functional and structural urinary abnormality. Or if any systemic features present. E.g., Male patient with UTI, Female patient with pregnancy, Catheter or stent in situ (within tract), Uncontrolled DM etc. Types : ● Upper UTI – acute pyelonephritis, involving the calyx or renal pelvis. ● Lower UTI – acute urethritis and cystitis, involving UB ± urethra. Causative agent : E.coli (80%), Staph. saprophyticus (in sexually active women), Klebsella, Proteus mirabilis (offensive urine) etc. Symptoms and sign : ► Lower UTI or Cystitis • Dysuria / Burning sensation during micturition • Frequency • Urgency • Nocturia • Suprapubic pain or tenderness • Offensive urine • Haematuria ► Upper UTI or Pyelonephritis • Fever with chill and rigor. No fever in lower UTI. • Night sweats • Loin or flank pain • Renal angle tenderness • Nausea, vomiting Urethral discharge (no fever) ↓ Dx : Urethritis. ↓ ↓ Watery / Mucoid urethral discharge Purulent urethral discharge ↓ ↓ Dx: Chlamydial (50%) Urethritis Dx: Gonorrhoeal Urethritis Investigation : Urine R/M/E : Mid stream sample shows pyuria (WBC or pus cell ≥ 5/HPF or, ≥ 10/mm 3 ), nitrate, sugar etc. Urine for C/S, to identify the offending organism and antibiotic sensitivity pattern. Indications of C/S of urine : 1. Persistent high fever despite of antibiotic may perinephric abscess (or other complications). 2. Flank/suprapubic pain (severe). 3. Change in voiding pattern. 4. Vomiting, confusion, sepsis. Antibiotic choices : • Cystitis : Nitrofurantoin / ciprofloxacin / cefixime for 7 days. • Pyelonephritis : Cefuroxime/ciprofloxacin/ceftriaxone for 14 days. In case of renal impairment, Cefuroxime preferred. Antibiotic duration – 3-5 days in women or 7-14 days in men & during pregnancy. Intravenous (IV) antibiotic should be given in following conditions (severe/complicated) : i) Complicated UTI (UTI with renal impairment, immunocompromised condition like DM, cancer patient, getting cytotoxic drugs, long term catheterized patient etc.). ii) UTI (upper) with high fever with poor general condition.

treatment.title: Acute prostatitis & Epididymo-orchitis
treatment.description: Acute prostatitis : Perineal or suprapubic pain, pain on ejaculation & prostatic tenderness on DRE. ► Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg, 1+0+1 --- 28 days Epididymo-orchitis : Testicles are infected & back (the epididymis) of the testicles becomes swollen and painful. ► Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg, 1+0+1 --- 14 days Or, Cap. Doxycycline 100 mg, 1+0+1 --- 14 days

treatment.title: Persistent or recurrent UTI
treatment.description: ► Persistent or recurrent UTI : Frequent UTI (three or more per year), may be due to genital prolapse, BPH etc. Treatment : If an underlying cause cannot be treated then low dose prophylactic antibiotic should be prescribed for a long time period. • First choice – Trimethoprim 100 mg at night. • Second choice – Nitrofurantoin 50 mg at night.

treatment.title: LUTS – Neurogenic
treatment.description: ► LUTS – Neurogenic : - Burning micturition. - No pus cells in urine. - No growth in C/S - Neurogenic – in this case no role of antibiotics. Neurogenic Lower urinary tract symptoms (LUTS) (LUTS) : ● Tab. Amitriptyline / Amit (gen) 10 mg, 0+0+1 for 3 months ● Tab. B1 + B6 + B12 / Neucocare (bex), 1+0+1 for 2 months ● Cap. Tamsulosin (0.4mg)/ Uromax (uni), 0+0+1 for 1 month ► If LUTS due to Herpes : ● Acyclovir 200 or 400 mg, 1+0+1 ---- continue

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: ACUTE PYELONEPHRITIS (Refer to nephrologist & hospitalization) Features : • Fever with chill and rigor & night sweats denotes upper UTI (severe) & presence of pus (perinephric abscess). • Loin or flank pain / renal angle tenderness. Inv. : USG of W/A or KUB, CBC with ESR, Urine RME and CS. Treatment : ✔ Inj. Cefuroxime 750mg or, 1.5gm / Furotil (hc) / Furocef (ren), 1 vial IV stat and TDS Or, ✔ Inj. Gentamicin 80mg/2ml / Gentin (ops) 80 mg, 1 amp IV stat and TDS Or, If patient is critically ill or failing to progress after conventional antibiotic or patient having urinary tract obstruction : ✔ Inj. Meropenem 1 gm / Meropen (ren) / Neopenem (hc) 1000mg, 1 vial IV stat and TDS + If MRSA is suspected : add – ✔ Inj. Linezolid 2mg/ml / Arlin (bex) / Linzolid (incp), 1 bottle (300 ml) IV stat and BD. Anti-pyretic : ⦸ Tab. Paracetamol 500 mg, 1+1+1+1 (A/M) --- If fever present. ⦸ Supp. Paracetamol 500 mg, 1 stick P/R ---- If needed.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: UTI management in hospital : C/C : 1. Abrupt onset of frequency of micturition and urgency. 2. Dysuria. 3. Suprapubic pain during and after voiding. 4. Intense desire to pass urine after micturition. 5. Cloudy/red urine. Adv. : ▪ CBC ▪ U/R/M/E ± ▪ Urine for C/S ± ▪ RBS ± ▪ USG of W/A Δ UTI ✔ Diet – Normal c̄ plenty of water. ✔ Inf. N/S / Saloride (bex) 1L, I/V stat @ 10 d/m (if severe vomiting). ✔ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Traxon (ops) / Oricef (hc) / Ceftron (sq) 1gm, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. Or, ✔ Inj. Cefuroxime 1.5g (or 750mg) / Furotil (hc) / Furocef (ren) / Cefotil (sq) / Kilbac (incp), 1 vial I/V stat & TDS. ✔ Inj. Timonium 5mg/2ml / Viset (hc) / Algin (ren) / Norvis (sq), 1 amp I/V stat and TDS. ✔ Inj. Tramadol 100mg/2ml / Anadol (sq), 1 amp I/M sos c̄ inj. ondansetron. ✔ Inj. Ondansetron 8mg/4ml / Zofra (skf), 1 amp I/V stat & BD/TDS (if vomiting). ✔ Inj. Esomeprazole 40mg / Nexum (sq) / Maxpro (ren), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ± ✔ Potassium Citrate + Citric Acid [(1500 mg + 250 mg)/5 ml] / Syp. Urikal (incp) / Citra-K (uni) / Uriset (sq) oral solution, 2 TSF (10 ml) + 1 glass water then, drink thrice (3 times) daily. ✔ Tab. Paracetamol / Napa (bex) / Ace (sq) / Renova (ops) 500m g, 1+1+1 or 1+1+1+1 or 2+2+2 (A/M) – if fever present. ✔ Supp. Paracetamol / Renova (ops) / Napa (bex) / Momodol (drg) / Ace (sq) 500 mg, 1 stick P/R sos, if temp ≥ 101 ° F. ✔ Please maintain temperature chart.

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment : Antibiotic is the mainstay of treatment. ► Tab. Cefuroxime 500 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ----- 7 – 14 days ► Tab. Tiemonium 50 mg/ Viset (hc)/ Algin (ren), 1+1+1 – 3-5 days ► Advice and prophylactic measures to be adopted by (specially female) with recurrent urinary infections : • Fluid intake of at least 2 L/day. • Regular complete emptying of bladder. • Maintain good personal hygiene. • Do not hold urine for long time. • Emptying of bladder before and after sexual intercourse. • Cranberry / sour fruit juice may be effective. • Avoid touching the orifice / vestibule with fecal contaminated hand during natural calls. Discussion : ► Citric acid 5% + Potassium cotrate 30 % oral solution may be used - To relieve discomfort in urinary tract infections. - To prevent kidney stone. - With uricosuric agent to prevent gout. - Acidosis caused by kidney diseases. It is Urinary Alkalinizing Agent & Prevention of repeated kidney stone formation : ¤ Urikal (incp) / Citra-K (uni) / CK (pop) / Urokit Plus (skf) oral solution, 2 TSF (10 ml) + 1 glass water then, drink thrice daily for 5 – 30 days. UTI in pregnancy : In 1st and 2nd trimester : [better to avoid nitrofurantoin at term] ► Tab. Nitrofurantoin 100 mg, 1+0+1 --- 7 – 14 days In 3rd trimester : ► Tab. Cefuroxime 500 mg, 1+0+1 ---- 7 – 14 days

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) (Call/Refer to nephrologist & hospitalization) Chronic kidney disease (CKD), previously termed chronic renal failure, refers to an irreversible deterioration in renal function that develops over a period of years. Initially it manifests only as a biochemical abnormality but eventually lead to uraemia as the disease progress. Common Causes : • Systemic Disease : DM, Hypertension (HTN) • Renal Disease : Glomerular Disease, Interstitial Nephritis, PKD, Reno-vascular Disease • SLE, Vasculitis. PRESENTING FEATURES : SYMPTOMS : • Loss of appetite • Nausea, Vomiting • Lethargy, Drowsiness • Pallor • Nocturia • Pruritus • Easy bruising SIGNS : • Anemia • Edema • Raised JVP • Pulsus paradoxus Aged person with risk factors (DM/HTN/Family history), present with loss of appetite & nausea-vomiting ± pedal / facial swelling → do routine investigations → ↑ S. Creatinine / BUN, ↓ Hb%, USG of KUB or W/A = decrease kidney size, lost cortico-medullary differentiation & suggestive of renal parenchymal diseases → CKD → Refer to Nephrologist. USG : Acute kidney injury Vs Chronic kidney disease Traits AKI CKD Echogenicity Hypoechoic Echogenic ( ↑ ) Kidney size Enlarged Small ( Cortical Thickness Normal Reduced ( Cortico-medullary differentiation Not lost Lost Two GFR values 3 months apart are required to assign stage. Stage of CKD eGFR Stage of CKD eGFR Stage 1 ≥ 90 Stage 4 (Severe) 1 5 – 29 Stage 2 (Mild) 60 – 89 Stage 5 (ESRD) Stage 3a 45 – 59 Stage 5 = Renal failure Stage 3b 30 – 44 GFR 2 - All GFR values are mL/min/1.73 m 2 . - Two GFR values 3 months apart are required to assign a stage. - In stage 4, artery venous (A-V) fistula should be done established by a vascular surgeon / urologist for hemodialysis (HD). - When death is likely without RRT (CKD stage 5), it is called end­ stage renal disease or failure (ESRD or ESRF). - End stage renal disease or failure (ESRD) is the stage when renal replacement therapy is compulsory either dialysis or renal transplantation, without which death is likely.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: MANAGEMENT OF CKD Diet & Lifestyle Modification : • ↓ Excess Protein consumption, Adequate Caloric intake. • Limiting PO 4 & K + intake. • Stop smoking. Rx of Hypertension : • BP Targets: 1g/day). • Drug of Choice: ACEi, ARB (if S. Creatinine & K + is static); CCBs, α -Blockers. Rx of Co-morbidities : • IHD • DM • Dyslipidemia Rx of Complications : • Renal Osteodystrophy : Vitamin D3. • ↑ PO 4 – Calcium Acetate, Sevelamer. • ↑ K + – Calcium Gluconate, Calcium Resonium. Iron Supplementation : • Target Hb% - 11g%. • Anemia Correction by EPO/ EPO Analogues/ Darbopoietin (Improves Exercise tolerance and fatigue). Renal Replacement Therapy : • It is the definitive Rx. • Hemodialysis, Peritoneal Dialysis, Renal Transplantation. Follow Up (F/U) : CBC, B. Urea/ S. Creatinine, S. Electrolytes, Urine RME, UTP Advice : 1. Fluid restriction (Intake : PDO+500ml). 2. Salt restriction. 3. Protein restriction (0.5 g/kg body weight/day). Severe protein restriction should be avoided. 4. Smoking should be stopped. 5. Social and psychological support.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) / Chronic Renal Failure (CRF) Diagnostic tools : 1. H/O – Anorexia, vomiting & generalized weakness. – H/O of DM, HTN, Interstitial / glomerular disease, systemic inflammatory diseases (SLE, vasculitis) etc. 2. O/G/E : - Anemia (++) / (+++). - BP : hypertension. - RBS : ↑ . 3. Investigations : 1. S. Creatinine : Raised. 2. USG of W/A c̄ special attention to kidney size ± Prostate : Bilateral small kidney ( ↓ CMD). 3. CBC c̄ Hb % – Anemic. 4. U/R/M/E – Proteinuria. 5. S. Electrolytes including Ca 2+ & PO 4 2- . Adv. : √ USG of W/A. √ S. Creatinine. √ CBC. √ U/R/M/E. √ ± RBS. √ ± S. Electrolytes. Δ CKD ¤ Diet : Normal c̄ fluid, fruit, salt & protein restriction. ¤ Fluid = PDO + 500mL. ¤ Please maintain input output chart. ¤ Tab. Domperidon 10 mg / Omidon (incp) / Don-A (acm), 1 + 1 + 1 (½ hr B/M). ¤ Tab. Calcium Carbonate 500 mg / Calbo (sq) / Ostogen (skf) 500 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M). ¤ Cap. Calcitriol 25 μ g / Dicaltrol (drg) / Raditrol (rad) / Calcitrol (sq), 1 + 0 + 0. ¤ Tab. Rosuvastatin / Rosutin (bex) / Rolip (ren) / RTV (del) 10mg, 0 + 0 + 1. Correction of anaemia : ¤ Blood transfusion (BT during dialysis). ¤ Cap. Ferrous Sulphate + Folic Acid + Zinc / Zif-CI (sq) / Ferozi-CI (drg), 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M). ¤ If anemia persists after iron therapy then inj. Erythropoietin Alfa (starting dose of 50 units/Kg, 3 times weekly intravenously or subcutaneously is recommended) / Inj. Epoetin (incp) [2200/-] / Initer (hc) [2600/-] / Recogen (aci) [2600/-] (5000IU/0.5ml), 1 PFS S/C A/U, 1 to 3 times weekly. Aim : Increase Hb 1gm/dl/month until target Hb (>10g/dl) is achieved. Plan for blood transfusion (BT) & plan to keep Hb% within 10-12 g/dL. If pedal or pulmonary edema : ¤ Inj. Frusemide 20 mg / Lasix (syn), 1 to 5 amp I/V stat & sos. ¤ Tab. Frusemide / Lasix (syn) / Fusid (sq) / Frusin (ops) 40 mg, 1 + 1 + 0. If HTN : ¤ Tab. Amlodipine / Amdocal (bex) / Amlopin (acm)/ CCB (ori)/ Camlodin (sq) 5mg, 1+0+0. ¤ Tab. Losartan / Losardil (drg) / Losart (acm) / Angilock (sq) 25 or 50 mg, 0+0+1. If metabolic acidosis present : add, ¤ Tab. Sodium Bicarbonate 600 mg / Alkalina (rad) / Sodicarb (pop), 1 + 1 + 1.

treatment.title: Discussion (Indoor Treatment)
treatment.description: Justification of drug therapy : ► Fluid must be restricted = Previous day output (PDO) + Insensible water loss (500 mL). Otherwise oedema will aggravate as kidney fails to excrete extra fluid. ► Salt restriction to stabilize electrolytes, 1 TSF is = 5 mg = 6 mmol. Patient will receive ½ TSF per whole day. ► Protein restriction (60 mg /day) should be done, if S. Creatinine > 2.6 mg /dl. - 1 piece of meat = 6.4 gm (match box size) protein. - 1 cap milk = 6.4 gm protein. - 1 egg albumin = 6.4 gm protein. ► If edema / decrease output use Furosemide (solo, not combined formulations). Not to use Furosemide + Spironolactone, as Spironolactone aggravates hyperkalemia in CKD. So better to avoid Frusin Plus (ops) / Edeloss (incp) / Fusid Plus (sq) / Frulac (ori) etc. Again coconut (dub) water or other fruits that have high K + , so fruits must be avoided. ► Vomiting & nausea are commonest symptoms, Domperidon 10 mg is used as anti-emetic. PPIs may be used in limit. ► CKD leads to hyperphosphatemia. For binding dietary Phosphate / PO 4 Tab. Calcium carbonate or acetate / Calbo 500 mg (1+1+0/1+1+1) is recommended. Active vit-D increases both calcium & phosphate level which can lead to secondary hyperthyroidism so combined calcium with vit-D should be avoided. Calcitriol is recommended after normalization of Ca 2+ & PO4 2- , both of them should be monitored on a regular basis during the course of treatment with calcitriol. ► Target BP is 1 g. If patient is hypertensive then add – Amlodipine with losartan. May also add – ¤ Tab. Prazosin 1 or 2 mg / Alphapress XR (ren) / Prazopress ER (uni) 2.5 mg, 1 to 8 tab daily, at once. ► Choice of anti HTN is – 1. Angiotensin-II receptor antagonists / blocker (ARB) (Tab. Losartan / Angilock 50 mg, 0+0+1) Or, ACE Inhibitor (ACEI). Don’t use if K + ↑ & oliguria. 2. If not control or cannot use ARB / ACEI. Calcium channel blocker (CCB) – Amlodipine / Diltiazem. 3. Alpha blocker – Tab. Prazosin / Alphapress 1mg (1 + 1 + 1). 4. Diuretics – Tab. Indapamide / Hypen-SR 1.5 mg (1+1+0). ► Antihypertensive in CKD, first choice should be ACEI, ARB or, non-dihydropyridines CCB (Amlodipine that also reduce proteinuria). After 2 weeks of prescribing ACEI / ARB, S. creatinine should be done, if creatinine level increases 25% than previous level then stop ACEI / ARB. ► Nephrotoxic Drug (should be avoided if increase S. Creatinine). ○ Ranitidine. ○ Cephalosporin except (Ceftriaxone). ○ Ciprofloxacin / Levofloxacin. ○ NSAIDs. ○ Gentamycin. ► If infection / fever or any source of infection present e.g., catheter for dialysis, then add non-nephrotoxic antibiotics – Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & OD (may use BD c̄ consultation) / C. Amoxicillin (1+1+1) / T. Azithromycin (0+0+1) / Tab. Pefloxacin 400 mg / Peflox (drg), (1+0+1).

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: ✔ Cap. Calcitriol 0.25 mcg, 1+0+0 – continue ✔ Tab. Calcium Carbonate 500 mg (A/M), 1+0+1 – continue ✔ Tab. Rosuvastatin 20 mg, 0+0+1 (B/M) – continue ✔ Tab. Domperidon 10 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 (½ hr B/M) – 60 days ✔ Tab. Amlodipine 5 mg, 0+0+1 – continue ✔ If diabetic : Insulin and/or Tab. Linagliptin 5 mg, 0+1+0. ✔ Tab. Loatidine 10 mg, 0+0+1 – 5 days (if purities). ✔ Tab. Frusemide 40 mg, 1+1+0 – 10 days (if edema). Advise : Dietary advice for CKD (see below) + Advice for DM & HTN + Regular follow-up with Hb%, Urine R/M/E, S. creatinine, ± S. electrolytes reports.

treatment.title: CKD c̄ Respiratory distress
treatment.description: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) c̄ Respiratory Distress & Metabolic Acidosis Diagnostic tools : 1. H/O – Respiratory distress in a Known CKD patient. 2. O/G/E : - Lung : Creps (of pulmonary edema) or, lung is clear. - Anemia (++) / (+++). - BP : hypertension. Adv. : ▸ CXR ▸ ABG Δ CKD c̄ Respiratory distress ¤ Protein, fluid & salt restriction. ¤ Fluid = PDO + 500mL. ¤ Nebulization with Salbutamol & Ipratropium solution / Windel Plus (incp) stat & 6 hourly. ¤ Inj. Frusemide 20 mg / Lasix, 5 amp I/V stat (if creps) & 2 to 5 amp I/V BD (8 amp & 4 pm). ¤ Inj. Sodium Bicarbonate / NaHCO3 (7.5 mg / 100 ml) / Inj. Sodib (jay), 8 vial I/V stat & 4 vial I/V BD. ¤ Tab. Domperidon 10 mg / Omidon (incp), 1 + 1 + 1 (½ hr B/M). ¤ Add – Calcium, Calcitriol, Iron & other ongoing drugs. Justification of drug therapy : ► Here, shortness of breath (SOB) is due to metabolic acidosis. Renal failure → Accumulation of organic acids & CO 2 → fall of HCO3 - . ► If dehydrated the use normal saline to resuscitate (even in diarrhea with AKI ± respiratory distress, not cholera saline). ► Avoid rapid correction of acidosis. That may cause fall of K + & Ca ++ levels. ► Also may have – H/O DM, HTN, Interstitial / glomerular disease, systemic inflammatory diseases (SLE, vasculitis) etc. & vomiting, anorexia & generalized weakness.

treatment.title: Dietary Suggestions
treatment.description: কিডনি রোগীদের খাদ্যাভাস সম্পর্কে উপদেশ : ১) প্রথম শ্রেণীর প্রোটিন থেকে (মাছ, মাংস, দুধ, ডিম প্রভৃতি) ৪০/৫০/৬০ গ্রামের বেশি খাবেন না (রোগীর ওজন ও অবস্থা অনুসারে) । ২) দ্বিতীয় শ্রেণীর প্রোটিন, চাল, আটা, ময়দা, সুজি ও সাবু খেতে পারবেন। ৩) কম পটাশিয়ামযুক্ত সবজি, যেমন- লাউ, মিষ্টি কুমড়া, ঝিঙ্গা, চিচিঙ্গা, মূলা, শশা, সীম (বিচি ছাড়া), ধুন্দুল খেতে পারবেন। ৪) বেশি পটাশিয়ামযুক্ত সবজি সিদ্ধ করে পানি ফেলে রান্না করতে হবে। যেমন- আলু, কাঁচা কলা, কলার মোচা, কাঁচা পেঁপে, গাজর, ডাটা শাক, সজিনা, নটে শাক, টমেটো, করলা ইত্যাদি। ৫) কম পটাশিয়ামযুক্ত ফল যেমন- আপেল, নাশপাতি, পেয়ারা, পাকা পেঁপে, জামরুল, বেল, আনারস খেতে পারবেন। দিনে যেকোন একটি ফলের ৫০ থেকে ১০০ গ্রাম রোগীর অবস্থা অনুসারে খেতে পারবে । ৬) বেশি পটাশিয়ামযুক্ত ফল যেমন- আম, জাম, লিচু, আনার/বেদানা, কলা, আমড়া, কামরাঙ্গা, আমলকি, ডাবের পানি, নারিকেল, কমলা, আঙ্গুর, কাঁঠাল, বড়ই, লেবু ইত্যাদি খাবেন না। ৭) ডাল, বিচি জাতীয় সবজি যেমন - সিমের বিচি, কাঁঠালের বিচি, করলার বিচি, পুঁই শাকের বিচি ইত্যাদি খাবেন না। কারন এগুলো বেশি পটাশিয়ামযুক্ত। ৮) কম পিউরিনযুক্ত খাবার যেমন- চিনি, গুড়, মধু, মিষ্টি (ডায়াবেটিকস থাকলে বাদ), দুধ জাতীয় খাবার, ডিমের সাদা অংশ, তেল, চাল, পাউরুটি, আটা, ময়দা, সুজি ও সাবু খেতে পারবেন। ৯) বেশি পিউরিনযুক্ত খাবার যেমন- গরুর মাংস, হাঁসের মাংস, কলিজা, মগজ, চিংড়ি মাছ, মাছের ডিম, সামুদ্রিক মাছ, শুটকি মাছ, ফুলকপি, বাঁধাকপি, পালংশাক, কচু ও কচু জাতীয় সবজি, পুঁইশাক, বেগুন খাবেন না। ১০) লবনাক্ত খাবার, যেমন - বেকিং পাউডার, বোতলজাত খাবার, চিপস, পপকর্ন, বাদাম, আচার, কোমল পানীয়, সস, শুকনা ফল, শুকনো সবজি, চানাচুর, মুড়ি-ভাঁজা, পাতে আলগা লবণ খাবেন না। ১১) পানি = আগের দিনের প্রসাবের পরিমাণর + ৫০০ মি.লি. বা ½ লিটার ২৪ ঘন্টায় সেইপরিমাণ পানি তরল করুন (রোগীর অবস্থা অনুসারে)। তরলের মধ্যে পানি, চা, কফি, যেকোনো তরলই একসাথে যোগ করতে হবে। ১২) কিডনি বিশেষজ্ঞ ও ডায়াটেশিয়ানের পরামর্শ অনুসারে ডাযেট চার্ট করবেন। ১৩) নিয়মিত ডায়ালাইসিস হলে খাবারের পরিমান বাড়াতে হবে, এতো নিয়মতান্ত্রিক না হলেও হবে। মূলকথা : রোগীর অবস্থা অনুসারে খাবারের পরিমান বাড়াতে বা কমাতে হবে। তবে প্রোটিন / আমিষ, পটাশিয়াম, সোডিয়াম ও পিউরিন যুক্ত খাবার নিয়ন্ত্রন করতে হবে।

treatment.title: Dialysis / RRT
treatment.description: ► Indication for Dialysis / Renal Replacement Therapy (RRT) : Acute kidney injury (AKI) with – 1. Fluid overload (refractory to diuretics) & pulmonary edema. 2. High potassium with ECG change / Hyperkalemia (K+ > 6.5 mmol/L)***. 3. Severe metabolic acidosis (H + >79nmol/L; pH 4. Rapidly climbing urea/creatinine (Urea > 30mmol/L or >18 mg/dL & S. creatinine > 6.8 mg/dL or 600 μ mol/L) *** 5. Symptomatic uraemia : when kidney failure leads to the clinical symptoms and signs, collectively referred to as uraemia. E.g., Uraemic encephalopathy, pericarditis, nausea, pruritus, bleeding diathesis : due to uremia induced platelet dysfunction. 6. Nonresponsive (to treatment) anuria or oliguria. 7. Intoxication / overdose : Star fruit (কামরাঙ্গা), methanol (adulterated alcohol), ethylene glycol, barbiturate, aspirin, lithium, anti-viral, anti-bacterial etc. ► Indication of dialysis in CRF (WHO criteria) : 1. Uraemic pericarditis. 2. Uraemic encephalopathy or neuropathy. 3. Pulmonary edema unresponsive to diuretics. 4. Severe hypertension if not control by medical management. 5, Severe hyperkalaemia not control by medical management. 6. Severe bleeding diathesis. 7. S. Creatinine > 12 mg/dl or BUN > 100mg/dl. ► Types of dialysis / RRT : 1. Intra-peritoneal dialysis (IPD). 2. Hemodialysis (HD). 3. Hemofiltration. 4. Hemodiafiltration. 5. Surgery : Renal transplantation.

treatment.title: Peritoneal Dialysis
treatment.description: IPD অনুমতিপত্র / কনসেন্ট ফর্ম আমি আমার রোগী ...................... এর অবস্থা গুরুতর এবং উন্নতি হবার সম্ভাবনা অনিশ্চিত তা জানি। আমি এটাও জানি পেরিটোনিয়াল ডায়ালাইসিস চলাকালীন সময়ে রোগীর ক্ষতি / মৃত্যু হতে পারে। তারপরও রোগীর অবস্থার উন্নতি হওয়ার আশায় আমি আমার বাবা / মা / সন্তান ................... এর পেরিটোনিয়াল ডায়ালাইসিস করাতে রাজি আছি। ইহাতে রোগীর কোন প্রকার অসুবিধা হইলে হাসপাতাল কর্তৃপক্ষ দ্বায়ী থাকবে না। স্বাক্ষর (রোগীর সাথে সম্পর্ক) সময় ও তারিখ Requirements of IPD : ╠ Inf. PDS (Peritoneal Dialysis Solution) – 60 bags. ╠ Ramsom’s PD set with catheter – (1) ╠ Insulin Syringe 40IU – (1) ╠ Inj. Heparin 5000IU/5ml / Inj. Diparin 5ml – (1) ╠ Inj. 2% Lidocaine / Jasocaine 50 ml – (1) ╠ JMS Micropore 2” – (1) ╠ 10 ml syringe – (1) ╠ Plastic pot 3 liter – (1) Add if not supplied in the ward : ╠ Sterile Gloves 7.5” – (2) ╠ Chlorhexidine / Germisol 200 ml – (1) ╠ Povidone Iodine 10 % / Viodin 10% solution – (1) ╠ BP blade no-15 – (1) Adv. (F/U) : ▪ S. Creatinine ▪ CBC Δ Pt on IPD With all aseptic precaution IPD was done with the written consent of patient. ¤ Inj. Heparin Sodium 25000 IU/5 ml / Heparon (bacn) / Heparin (Panpharma) / G-Heparin (gono), 10 units of 100 U insulin syringe mixed with 1L of Inf. PDS in each alternate cycle. If complaints pain then – ¤ Inj. 2% Lidocaine / Jasocaine (jay) 50 ml, 5 ml in each 1L IPD fluid in each cycle. ¤ No fruit, protein & fluid restriction during IPD. Transfuse blood if indicated. Use if needed – ¤ Inj. Diclofenac 75 mg / Diclofen (ops), 1 amp I/M stat & sos. ¤ Inj. Esomeprazole 40 mg / Esotid (ops), 1 amp I/V stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Diazepam 10 mg/2 ml / Sedil (sq), 1 amp I/M sos. Discussion : ► Common problem is blockage of IPD : Stop wheels → Remove connector → Aspirate with 10 cc syringe → Start wheels & avoid pressure on tubes. If failed then, Flush with 20-30 mL IPD fluid → start IPD fluid @ running. ► Composition of Inf. PDS : Na + : 130 mEq/L, Ca ++ : 3 mEq/L, Mg ++ : 1.5 mEq/L, Cl - : 100 mEq/L, CH3COO - : 35 mEq/L. ► Blood transfusion (BT) during IPD, if severely anemic. ► Adv. : S. Creatinine after completing IPD. ► Usually 60 bags of PDS should take 2 days / 48 hours to complete. Government may supply up to 20 / 40 bags of PDS. Period may decrease or may be done more frequently if needed. Procedure : - Inf. PDS two (both) bottles are infuse @running into peritoneal cavity, the excretory connector channel is kept close in case of inflation. - The excretory connector channel is kept close for further 1 hour after finishing of both PDS bottles. But in first 2 cycle (4 bags) no need to wait an hour. ► For End­ stage renal disease or failure (ESRD or ESRF), A-V fistula (usually on left hand) for hemodialysis (HD) is suggested.

treatment.title: A-V Fistula
treatment.description: ফিস্টুলা পরবর্তী উপদেশ - ১. ফিস্টুলার হাতে চাপ / প্রেসার দেওয়া যাবে না। ২. ফিস্টুলার হাতে ঘড়ি বা ব্রেসলেট পড়া যাবে না। ৩. ফিস্টুলার হাতে কোনো ইনজেকশন দেওয়া যাবে না। ৪. ফিস্টুলার হাত দিয়ে ভারী কাজ করা বা চাপ দিয়ে কাজ করা যাবে না। ৫. ফিস্টুলার হাত কুনই বরাবর ভাঁজ করা বা বুকের উপর তোলা যাবে না। ৬. নরম বল দিয়ে শিখানো হাতের ব্যায়াম করবেন। ৭. রাত্রে ঘুমানোর সময় ফিস্টুলার সাইডে একটি বালিশ দিয়ে ঘুমাবেন। ৮. সঠিক সময়ে উচ্চ রক্তচাপের ঔষুধ খাবেন। তবে মনে রাখবেন রক্তচাপ কমে গেলে যেমন নিয়মিত ১২০/৮০ মি.মি. (পারদ)-এর নিচে থাকলে, ফিস্টুলা বন্ধ হয়ে যেতে পারে। ► Infected / burst A-V fistula is a surgical emergency, must be treated with antibiotics (IV ceftriaxone) & BT if profound bleeding present. Burst fistula needs immediate vascular intervention / surgery.

treatment.title: Renal Biopsy
treatment.description: ► Indications of Renal Biopsy : • Nephrotic syndrome (NS) or Lupus Nephritis (LN) or glomerular proteinuria in adults (please see pedi-nephrology). • Nephrotic syndrome (NS) in children with – ○ Atypical presentation. ○ Steroid-resistant nephrotic syndrome (SRNS). ○ Before giving 3 rd line drugs. ○ Congenital NS. • Acute kidney injury that is not adequately explained. • CKD with normal-sized kidneys. • Isolated haematuria or proteinuria with renal characteristics or associated abnormalities. Histology : - Common in childhood → Minimal change. - Common in Adult → Membranous glomerulonephritis. - Common in Adult → Membranous glomerulonephritis. Investigations : 1. BT, CT, Platelet count. 2. HbsAg, Anti-HCV. 3. Total Urinary Protein (UTP) / PCR (Urine). 4. USG of KUB region c̄ Left kidney surface marking. (No need of USG, if USG guided biopsy). Pre Renal biopsy Order (Date & Time) : - NPO UFO (6 hours). - Prophylactic antibiotic. - Analgesic & anti-ulcerant. Requirements of Renal Biopsy : - Trucut biopsy needle - (2) - Specimen container / Plastic pot – (1) - Formalin 500 ml – (1) - Inj. 2% Lignocaine / Jasocaine 50 ml - (1) - BP blade 15 size - (1) - Hand Gloves 7.5” – (2) - 10 cc syringe – (1) - Povidone Iodine 10 % / Povisep solution – (1) - Chlorhexidine / Handirub - (1) অনুমতিপত্র / কনসেন্ট ফর্ম আমি আমার রোগী ...................... এর অবস্থা গুরুতর এবং উন্নতি হবার সম্ভাবনা অনিশ্চিত তা জানি। আমি এটাও জানি কিডনি (ডান/বাম ✓ ) থেকে কলা / টিস্যু নিয়ে পরীক্ষা করা ঝুঁকিপূর্ণ। তারপরও রোগীর রোগ নির্ণয় ও অবস্থার উন্নতি হওয়ার আশায় আমি আমার বাবা / মা / .............. এর কিডনি (ডান/বাম ✓ ) থেকে কলা / টিস্যু নিয়ে পরীক্ষা করাতে রাজি আছি। ইহাতে রোগীর কোন প্রকার অসুবিধা হইলে হাসপাতাল কর্তৃপক্ষ দ্বায়ী থাকবে না। স্বাক্ষর (রোগীর সাথে সম্পর্ক) সময় ও তারিখ Post Renal Biopsy Order (Time & Date) With all aseptic precaution renal biopsy was done with the written consent of patient & sent for histopathological examination to any histo-pathology lab (may be, Dhaka or, India). ¤ Bed rest for 24 hours. ¤ Tab. Frusemide 40 mg, 2 + 2 + 0. ¤ Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg or, Cefuroxime 500 mg, 1 + 0 + 1. ¤ Tab. Diazepam 5 mg / Easium, 1 tab sos. ¤ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 (B/M). ¤ Tab. Paracetamol 500 mg, 2 + 2 + 2 (A/M).

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: Acute Renal Failure (ARF) / Acute Kidney Injury (AKI)
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : ● H/O – Scanty micturition or complete cessation of micturition for 2 days. ● H/O hypovolaemia (Diarrhoea, HUS, hemorrhage / bleeding, snake bite, surgery etc.), infection, Cardiac / Liver diseases, following drugs, bladder outlet obstruction (BOO). ● Vomiting, anorexia & generalized weakness of short / abrupt onset. ● Drowsiness, fits & coma. O/G/E : - BP : Normal (by drug) / Hypotension (if hypovolemia) / Hypertensive (AGN). - Anemia (-) / (+). Drug History : following drugs may cause AKI / ARF – • NSAIDs (Aceclofenac, Diclofenac etc.). • ACE inhibitors & ARBs. • Ranitidine. • Aminoglycoside & Gentamycin. • Cephalosporin & Penicillin. Immediate stop these drugs. Investigation : • S. creatinine ↑. • S. electrolyte including HCO 3 (May, K + ↑ ). • Urine R/M/E – Normal. • USG of whole abdomen with special attention to kidney size & prostate – normal study. • RBS. • If suspect infection (fever) - o CBC c̄ PBF. o ICT for malaria. AKI Staging – KDIGO Classification : AKI stage Serum Creatinine criteria Urine output criteria 1 ↑ ≥ 1.5-2 fold from baseline, or, 6 consecutive hrs ↑ ≥ 26 μ mol/L within 48 hrs. 2 ↑ ≥ 2-3 fold from baseline 3 ↑ ≥ 3 fold from baseline, or, or, anuria for 12 hr. ↑ ≥ 354 μ mol/L, or, initiated on RRT (irrespective of stage at time of initiation).

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Acute Renal Failure (ARF) / Acute Kidney Injury (AKI)
treatment.description: Δ ARF following AWD / Drugs Adv. : ▸ 24 hours urine output : Initially low but increased in recovery phase. ▸ S. Creatinine ↑ . ▸ S. Electrolyte : Hyperkalemia / hypokalemia. ▸ Serum urea : ↑ . ▸ Serum phosphate : ↑ . ▸ Blood for C/S. ▸ Urine R/E. ▸ ECG. ▸ RBS. Tx, ¤ Protein, fluid & salt restriction. ¤ Fluid = PDO + 500mL. ¤ Maintain input-output (I/O) chart. ¤ Continuous catheterization. ¤ Stop any offending drug (if present). ¤ Rx of underlying cause. If hypovolemic : ( ↓ BP, ↑ pulse, ↓ urine volume, non-visible JVP, daily weight loss) – optimize hemodynamic status : ¤ Inf. N/S (if AWD/vomiting) or, H/S (if hemorrhage) 2L, I/V stat @running or @30d/m If fluid overload : ( ↑ BP, ↑ JVP, lung creps, gallop rhythm, oedema) : ¤ Inj. Frusemide (20mg/2ml) / Lasix (syn), 2 to 5 amp I/V stat and 2 amp I/V BD (8 am and 4 pm) Once euvolemic : ¤ Inf. N/S / Solo (sq) 1L or [PDO+500ml], I/V @20d/m, daily. ¤ Inj. Esomeprazole 40mg / Nexum (sq) / Maxpro (ren), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. If evidence of infection, ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Axon (aris) 1 gm, 1 vial I/V stat and OD. In case of urosepsis : omit ceftriaxone and add – ¤ Inj. Meropenem / Meropen (ren) 500 mg, 1 vial I/V stat and TDS. If acidosis : pH + >100nmol/L : ¤ Inj. Sodium Bicarbonate (7.5mg/25ml) / Inj. Sodib (jay), ½ vial (12.5 ml) slow I/V stat. If hyperkalemia : See electrolyte imbalance section. ¤ If within a day urine output not increased then start forced diuresis – ¤ Inj. N/S / Saloride (bex) 80 ml + 10 amp Inj. Frusemide 20 mg / Lasix (syn), I/V stat @ 4 dµ / min. ¤ If still after 100 ml drip U/O not increased –Then, IPD / Dialysis. Sign of recovery : Gradual return of urine output & steady improvement in plasma biochemistry.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Acute glomerulonephritis (AGN) / Nephr i tic syndrome • Most common glomerular cause of gross hematuria. • All ages, mean 7 years, school-age common, overcrowded living. • Characterized by sudden onset of hematuria (microscopic or macroscopic) with – ▪ Hypertension. ▪ Mild azotemia. ▪ Oliguria. ▪ Oedema. • Group A β hemolytic Streptococcus (GAS) : Responsible for acute post streptococcus GN (80-90% of AGN)-APSGN. ● Patient complaints of – swelling of face, leg & eyelid, oliguria, haematuria, fever may be present. History of skin lesion or sore throat 1-4 weeks before may be present. ● On examination – puffy face, high BP, leg edema present, bed side urine examination: proteinuria. ● Investigation – urine R/M/E-proteinuria (mild to moderate), hematuria, cellular (RBC, WBC) casts present. Management Treatment is supportive, with control of hypertension, edema, and dialysis as needed. Antibiotic treatment for streptococcal infection should be given to all patients and their co-habitants in post-streptococcal AGN.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Δ AGN Adv. : ▪ Urine R/E : RBC/RBC cast, Proteinuria. ▪ S. Creatinine ▪ Serum urea ▪ S. Electrolyte ▪ Serum C3 level : Low Tx, ✔ Diet – normal c̄ protein, salt, fruit restricted. ✔ Fluid restricted = Previous Day Output (PDO) + (400 ml × BSA). ✔ Maintain input output (I/O) chart. ✔ Tab. Cefuroxime 250 mg BD for 7-14 days; (In post streptococcal GN then phenoxy-methyl penicillin 250 mg 6 hourly for 7 days). ✔ To reduce edema : Tab. Frusemide (40mg daily or, 1-3 mg/kg/day BD). ✔ If itching, add – Desloratadine (0+0+1). ✔ To control hypertension : ○ Ramipril 1.25-2.5 mg/day or, ○ Captopril 25 mg (0.25-6mg/Kg/day in 2-4 dose) or, ○ Nefidipine 200 mg (0.25-0.5mg/Kg/day in 2-4 dose). ○ [If adult : Losartan ± Amlodipine] Note : The inflammatory glomerular injury may require corticosteroids and cytotoxic agents. Post infectious glomerulonephritis – corticosteroids have not been shown to improve outcome. Discharge criteria : 1. BP: Controlled. 2. Urine : Free of RBC.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Rapidly Progressive Glomerulonephritis (RPGN) (Call/Refer to nephrologist) Rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis (RPGN) is a disease of the kidney characterized clinically by : • Rapid decrease in GFR of at least 50% over a period of a few days to 3 months, usually in association with hypertension and oedema. • Pathologic finding : Extensive glomerular crescent formation. Rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis (RPGN) is the most common cause of AGN in older adults. Causes of RPGN : • Post infectious GN (PIGN) • Anti-GBM disease • Small Vessels vasculitis • IgA nephropathy • SLE Suspected RPGN – History : Prodrome : • Flu-like symptoms (malaise, fever, arthralgias, myalgias, anorexia), • Weight loss. Following the prodrome : • Abdominal pain. • Painful cutaneous nodules or ulcerations. • Migratory Poly-arthropathy. • When pulmonary or upper airway involvement is present : o Sinusitis symptoms. o Cough. o Hemoptysis. Examination : • Hypertension • Edema • (+ve) Urine heat coagulation test • Systemic Features: • Palpable purpura, F/O SLE, Crepitation in lungs etc. Investigations : • CBC • Urine R/E (albumin, RBC/Granular cast) • Red cell cast and dysmorphic RBC in urine • S. Creatinine, usually raised • S. Electrolytes, maybe normal • LFTs • ANA, cANCA, pANCA • Renal biopsy and histopathology Treatment Module : [If ANCA (+) & Anti GBM (+)] • High dose Methyl Prednisolone • Cyclophosphamide • Others : IVIG, Rutiximab, etc. Treatment : • Steroids : ✓ IV Methylprednisolone : 7 mg/kg/day (not to exceed 1g) for 3 days. Then, ✓ Oral Prednisone : 1 mg/kg/day (not to exceed 80 mg) for 3 weeks. Then, ✓ Oral Prednisone : 2 mg/kg every other day (not to exceed 120 mg) for 3 months. Then, ✓ Dose tapering by 25% every 4 weeks and discontinued. • IV/ Oral Cyclophosphamide : ✓ IV dose : 0.5 g/m 2 BSA (maximum 1 g/m 2 body surface area), Or, ✓ Oral dose : 2 mg/kg. Both doses adjusted according to a 2 weeks leukocyte nadir (lowest) count (goal 3000-4000/µL).

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Nephrotic Syndrome (NS) (Refer to pedi-nephrologist / nephrologist) Features : • Generalized Edema • Massive Proteinurea (>3.5g/day) • Bedside urine test +++ (protein) • Hypoalbuminemia ( • Hyperlipidemia ( ↑ Blood Total Cholesterol & Triglycerides) • Lipiduria (Lipid Casts in Urine) • BP usually normal. NS is triad of – 1. Proteinuria : >3g/24hr. 2. Hypoalbuminemia : usually 3. Oedema. O/E : Ascites : Present. ↓ Common Investigations : • CXR P/A View • USG of W/A • S. Creatinine, S. Urea, S. Electrolytes • HBSAg, Anti HCV • CBC with ESR, PBF • 24hrs UTP, ANA, C3, C4 ↓ Age • Steroids. • Other General Measures. Please see pedi-nephrology portion. & Age > 18 yrs : • Referral for Renal Biopsy. • Treatment based on history / specific under-lying causes (adult NS needs specialist referral). ↓ Refer to nephrologist or pediatric nephrologist. Principle of NS management : ✔ Diet (adult) – Normal with protein, salt, fruit restricted. ✔ Diet (child)– Normal diet with protein rich diet & salt restricted. ✔ Fluid restricted = Previous Day Output (PDO) + (400 ml × BSA). ✔ Maintain input output (IO) chart. ✔ If UTI then give antibiotic (before that do urine C/S). ✔ If UTI absent start specific treatment prednisolone / steroid (in children, ✔ Calcium supplementation if more than 3 months of steroid therapy. ✔ If upper GI discomfort add famotidine (PPI use should be limited). ✔ Frusemide preferably IV to reduce edema. ✔ Ramipril (ACEi) to reduce proteinuria. ✔ Atrovastatin (above 10 years of age) if hypercholesterolemia (Please see “Dyslipidemia” portion). ✔ Treatment of the underlying cause like DM, SLE etc. ► N.B. : Patients with frequent relapses and corticosteroid resistance may need cyclophosphamide or chlorambucil to induce subsequent remissions. ► Nephro-nephritic syndrome when massive proteinuria c̄ RBC cast / hematruria in U/R/E & M/E. For more about NS, please see - “pedi-nephrology” portion in GP Diary.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ NS Adv. : ▸ Urine R/E : Proteinuria (+++). ▸ 24 hours UTP : >3 gm. ▸ Fasting lipid profile : Hyper-cholesterolemia ▸ Serum albumin : Low ▸ RBS Tx, ✔ Diet : Normal with – salt & fluid restriction (1L/day). ✔ Maintain input output (I/O) chart. ✔ Tab. Frusemide 20 mg + Spironolactone 50 mg / Edeloss (incp), 1+1+0 (A/M) ✔ Cap. Esomeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (Before meal) ✔ Tab. Atorvastatin 10 mg, 0+0+1. ✔ Tab. Calcium + Vitamin D3, 0+1+0. ✔ Tab. Prednisolone 10 mg, 6+0+0 (A/M) [60mg/m 2 body surface area (BSA) for 6 weeks], Then, 4+0+0 (A/M) [40mg/m 2 body surface area for 6 weeks] – every alternative day for 6 weeks, then taper off 10 mg per 7 days or 1 week. Minimal change disease : Prednisolone. Membranous-nephropathy : Prednisolone + Cyclophosphamide. Follow up : • Oedema : • Urine output (U/O) : • Bedside heat coagulation test (HCT) : • Weight : Aim 0.5-1 Kg weight loss/day

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Discharge notes (NS) : ✔ Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan (incp) / Deltasone (ren) 10 mg, 6+0+0 (A/M) – 1 ½ month. Then, 4+0+0 (A/M) – 1 ½ month. Then, 3+0+0 (A/M) – 1 week. Then, 2+0+0 (A/M) – 1 week. Then, 1+0+0 (A/M) – 1 week. ✔ Cap. Esomeprazole 20mg / Maxima (acm) / Esoral (skf), 1+0+1 (before meal) – while taking prednisolone. ✔ Tab. Calcium + Vitamin D3 / Rocal-D (hc) / Calcin-D (ren), 0+1+0 (A/M) – 3 months. ✔ Tab. Frusemide 20mg+Spironolactone 50mg / Frulac-20 (ori), 1+1+0 (A/M)- Continue ✔ Tab. Atorvastatin/ Liptor (acm)/ ATV (del)/ Avas (ops) 10 mg, 0+0+1 – 3 months.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Lupus Nephritis (LN) (Call/Refer to nephrologist/rheumatologist) 1. H/O : – H/O SLE (may only dermatological manifestation) – Rash, fever, Joint pain, muscle pain. – Foamy Urine. – Peripheral ± peri-orbital edema. 2. O/G/E : - BP : ↑ . - Edema. - Other manifestation of SLE. 3. Inv : 1. Urinalysis or, U/R/M/E. 2. Antibodies to double-standard DNA. 3. Renal biopsy. Treatment : 6 cycle chemotherapy at every 1 month interval Cyclophosphamide / Neoclomide (hc) / Cyclotox (bacn) / Endoxan (syn) in 5% DA drip. Treatment on discharge : Model Prescription : Ø Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan (incp) / Precodil (ops) 20 mg, ৩ + ০ + ০ (আহারের পর) ...... ২ মাস, তারপরে, ২ ½ + ০ + ০ (আহারের পর) ...... ১৫ দিন, তারপরে, ২ + ০ + ০ (আহারের পর) ....... ১৫ দিন, তারপরে, ১ ½ + ০ + ০ (আহারের পর) ........ ১৫ দিন, তারপরে, ১ + ০ + ০ (আহারের পর) ....... ১৫ দিন, তারপরে, ½ + ০ + ০ (আহারের পর) ...... ৬ মাস Ø Tab. Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg / Reconil (incp) / Quinivir (sq), ১ + ০ + ১ (আহারের পর) ........ চলবে Ø Tab. Calcium Carbonate / Calbo (sq) / Ostogen (ops) 500 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (আহারের পর) ........ ২ মাস Ø Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Seclo (sq) / Proceptin (bex), ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার আধাঘন্টা পূর্বে) ...... ½ - ২ মাস Ø Tab. Domperidone 10 mg / Vave (aci) / Don A (acm) / Omidon (incp), ১ + ১ + ১ (খাবার আধাঘন্টা পূর্বে) ........ ১ মাস Ø Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Acupain (bacn) / Xidolac (bex), ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার পরে) ........ ব্যাথা থাকলে খাবেন (৩ দিন) উপদেশ : নিয়মিত ঔষধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে ১ মাস পর সব কাগজপাতি ও CBC c̄ ESR, RBS, S. Creatinine, SGPT, S. Bilirubin পরীক্ষার রিপোর্টসহ মেডিসিন / কিডনি বহি:বিভাগে দেখা করবেন।

treatment.title: Definitions
treatment.description: Hyponatremia ( ↓ Na + ) (Normally 135 – 145 mmol/L, Hyponaetremia if Normal sodium (Na + ) level in blood is 135 – 145 mmol/L. Hyponatraemia if Na + ● Mild : 130 – 135 mmol/L ● Moderate : 125 – 129 mmol/L ● Severe : Types : A. Hypovoluemic hyponatraemia : This type is the most encountered cases of hyponatraemia (vomiting / diarrhea due to any cause, diuretic intake, burn, etc.). B. Euvolaemic hyponatraemia (SIADH, hypothyroidism, primary polydipsia). C. Hypervolacmic hyponatraemia (CCF, Cirrhosis, Nephrotic syndrome, CKD). Types on duration of development : 1. Acute hyponatraemia – developed rapidly ( 2. Chromic hyponatraemia – developed slowly over 48 hours. Features : ● Asymptomatic in mild cases. ● Intractable vomiting due to any cause. ● Marked anorexia after any episode of vomiting and/or diarrhoea. ● Anorexia, vomiting after starting any diuretics. ● Severe cases: unconscious or drowsy after vomiting or diarrhoea. Mild and moderate hyponatraemia usually can be corrected orally with table salt intake. Severe hyponatraemia needs hospitalization.

treatment.title: Hypovoluemic hyponatraemia
treatment.description: Hypovoluemic hyponatraemia : ► If, Na+ ≥ 125 mmol/L → Oral correction. ● Orally, 1 TSF salt (NaCl) = 96 mmol/L ≈ 100 mmol/L. ► If, Na+ → IV correction. IV Inf. N/S, ● 1000 mL 0.9% Normal saline = 154 mmol/L ≈ 150 mmol/L. ● 1000 mL 3% NaCl = 513 mmol Na. ► Mild to moderate hyponatraemia : ● Tab. Sodium Chloride / Sodiclor (pop) / Naclitab (ops) / Adsalt (sq) 300 or, 600 mg, 2+2+2 (8 – 16 tablets per day, according to clinical condition). For correction of sodium we commonly use the following : ● 1 TSF NaCl (table salt) = 96 mmol Na. ● 1L 0.9% Inf. NS= 154 mmol Na. ● 1L 3% NaCl = 513 mmol Na. ► Severe hyponatraemia : ● Acute hyponatraemia (within 48 hours) – if there is signs of cerebral oedema, such as obtundation or convulsions etc. Planter reflex is bilaterally extensor in case of metabolic encephalopathy. Sodium levels should be restored rapidly to normal by infusion of hypertonic (3%) sodium chloride (NaCL 3%). Initial bolus of 150 mL over 20 minutes, then wait for 1 hour and check sodium level. 3% NaCl 150 ml can be repeated 2 times, depending on the neurological response and rise in plasma sodium. ● Chronic hyponatraemia – The rate of correction of the plasma Na concentration in chronic asymptomatic hypo-natraemia should not exceed 10 mmol/L/day. Rapid correction by 3% NaCl will cause detachment of myelin sheath / pontine demyelinolysis. ● The underlying cause should also be treated e.g., stop of diuretic, treatment of cause of vomiting etc.

treatment.title: Euvolaemic hyponatraemia
treatment.description: ► Euvolaemic hyponatraemia : ● Patients with euvolaemic hyponatraemia generally respond to fluid restriction in the range of 600-1000mL/24 hrs, accompanied where possible by withdrawal of the precipitating stimulus (such as drugs causing SIADH). ● In patients with persistent hyponatraemia due to prolonged SIADH, oral urea therapy (40 g/day) or oral vasopressin receptor antagonist can be used. Diagnosing SIADH : 1. Hyponatremia not on diuretics. 2. Euvolemic (no oedema). 3. Normal renal, adrenal and thyroid function. 4. Urine osmolarity >100mosml/L. 5. Urine Na + > 20mmol/L. 6. Reduced serum urate.

treatment.title: Hypervolaemic hyponatraemia
treatment.description: ► Hypervolaemic hyponatraemia : ● Treatment of the underlying condition. ● Cautious use of diuretics & strict fluid restriction. Potassium sparing diuretics (spironolactone) may be particularly useful in this context when there is significant secondary hyperaldosteronism,

treatment.title: Hypernaetremia (↑ Na+)
treatment.description: (Normally 135 – 145 mmol/L, Hypernaetremia if >145 mmol/L) Common cause of hypernatraemia (> 135 mmol/L) is inadequate preparation of one packet of ORS (dilute in less than 500 ml water). Refer if hypernatraemia c̄ renal impairment. Management : Treatment of hypernatraemia depends on both the rate of development and the underlying cause, if has developed rapidly, rapid correction may be attempted. Treatment of the underlying cause is the main focus. May wait if asymptomatic or in moderate to severe cases can be managed by infusing an appropriate volume of intravenous fluid (isotonic inf. 5% DA / dextrose or hypotonic inf. NS 0.45%) at an initial @ 15 drops/min [50-70 mL/hour]. ● Diet: Normal c̄ adequate water intake . ● Inf. 5% DA or Inf. 0.45% NaCl, I/V stat @ 15 drops/min [50-70 mL/hour]

treatment.title: Hypocalcemia (↓ Ca++)
treatment.description: (Normally 8.5 to 10.2 mg/dL or, 2.2 to 2.6 mmol/L) (Hypocalcemia if Features : ○ Tetany. ○ Carpopedal spasm, stridor and convulsion (in children). ○ Trousseau's sign : (+) & Chvostek's sign : (+). Treatment (Hypocalcemia) : ✔ Hospitalization. ✔ Diet: Normal, calcium containing food. ✔ Cardiac monitoring is recommended. ✔ Inj. Calcium gluconate 10% (1 gm/10 ml) or (0.5mg/5ml), (1-2 amp + 10-20 ml D/W) very slowly IV stat over 20 min stat, Then, ✔ Inj. Calcium gluconate 10% (1gm/10ml), [8 amp (8 gm) + inf. N/S 1L] IV @10drops/min ✔ Tab. Calcium carbonate + Vit-D / Ostocal D (skf) / Calbo-D (sq), (2 + 2 + 0) or, (2 + 2 + 2) (A/M) If associated with hypomagnesaemia : ✔ Inj. MgSO 4 (4 gm/100 ml) / Inf. Nalepsin (bex), I/V @ 20 drops/min stat and TDS If vitamin D deficiency : ✔ Tab. Cholecalciferol 1000 IU, 0+1+0. ✔ Correction of albumin deficiency (If present).

treatment.title: Hypercalcaemia (↑ Ca++)
treatment.description: (Normally 8.5 to 10.2 mg/dL or, 2.2 to 2.6 mmol/L) (Hypercalcemia if >2.5 mmol/l or, >10 mg/dl) Cause : bones, stones, moans, abdominal groans. Features : 1. Polyuria and polydipsia. 2. Renal colic. 3. Lethargy, anorexia, nausea. 4. Dyspepsia and peptic ulceration. 5. Constipation. 6. Depression, drowsiness and impaired cognition. 7. Features of underlying cause. 8. Drug history : Glucocorticoids, thiazide diuretics. Investigations : 1 st line : 1. Serum Ca: > 2.5mmol/l or >10mg/dl. 2. S. electrolytes 3. Phosphate 4. CBC: High ESR 5. CXR PA view : Ca lung 6. ECG: Prolongation of QT 7. RBS 8. X ray KUB : Renal stone 9. USG of KUB : Renal stone 2 nd line : 10. PTH 11. ACE : Sarcoidosis 12. Protein electrophoresis : Multiple myeloma 13. Bence jones protein in urine 14. Serum D3. Treatment (Hypercalcaemia) : ✔ Hospitalization. ✔ Diet: Avoid calcium containing food. If severe : Ca ≥ 3.5 mmol/L or ≥ 14 mg/dl : ✔ Inf. N/S 2-4 L/day, IV @20-40 drops/min. [Rehydration with normal saline] ✔ Inj. Zoledronic acid 4 mg/5ml / Zoclast (hc) / Zometa (nova) / Xoleron (bacn) + Inf. N/S 100 ml, IV @ 50 drops/min stat. If severe, symptomatic hypercalcaemia refractory to zoledronic acid : ✔ Inj. Denosumab 60 mg/ml, 1 amp S/C stat and repeat as per response. ✔ Bisphosphonates e.g., Disodium pamidronate 90 mg IV over 4 hours. Additional rapid therapy : a) Forced diuresis with saline & frusemide. b) Prednisolone 40 mg daily. c) Calcitonin. d) Haemodialysis. If mild : Ca ≥ 2.6 mmol/L or ≥ 10.5 mg/dl : ✔ Adequate hydration. ✔ Oral bisphosphonate [Syp. Phosphoprep (uni)]. ✔ Treat the underlying causes.

treatment.title: Hypomagnesemia (↓ Mg++)
treatment.description: Blood magnesium level is normally 1.7 to 2.2 mg/dL (0.85 to 1.10 mmol/L). Features : 1. Paresthesia, ataxia. 2. Tetany, seizure. 3. Diarrhoea / vomiting. H/O : Alcoholism. Past history : DM. Drug history : Loop or thiazide diuretics. Investigations : 1. Serum magnesium level 2. Serum calcium 3. Serum albumin 4. S. creatinine 5. ECG Model Prescription : Treatment : ✔ Diet : Normal Asymptomatic with hypomagnesemia & no vomiting : ✔ Tab. Magnesium Oxide 365 mg / Magnox (nuv) / Magnide (sq) / Magnito (eve), 1+1+1. Symptomatic or with K + /Ca + : If arrhythmia, tetany or convulsion present : ✔ Inf. Magnesium sulphate 4%, 60 ml, I/V @90 drops/min stat (or slow). [60ml over 5-10 min] and reassess. Mild symptom or after resolving of arrhythmia, tetany or convulsion : ✔ Inf. Magnesium sulphate 4%, 100 ml, I/V @25 microdrops/min. [100ml over 4 hours] daily – till Mg ++ >1.2 mg/dl. After becoming Mg ++ >1.2 mg/dl : ✔ Tab. Magnesium Oxide 365 mg / Magnox (nuv) / Magnide (sq) / Magnito (eve), 1+1+1.

treatment.title: Hypokalaemia (↓ K+)
treatment.description: (Normally 3.5 – 5 mmol/L, Sign : Proximal weakness, Paralytic ileus, Arrhythmia. Investigations : 1. Serum Electrolytes. 2. ECG : ST depression, U wave, Flattened T wave. 3. Urine potassium. 4. Plasma calcium and magnesium. Mild : If K + 2.5 – 3.5 mmol/L, then oral correction : ✔ Potassium containing fruits (banana, coconut water) & diet. ✔ Syrup. Potassium Chloride 500 mg/5 ml / KCL (ops) / KT (jay) / Electro K (acm), 2 TSF mixed ½ glass water TDS. Or, ✔ Tab. Potassium Chloride / Electro-K (acm), 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M). [It should be taken with or after meals with plenty of fluid (water/fruit juice), Stop after repeat S. electrolytes.] Severe : If K + (add above +) ✔ Inf. N/S 1 L + 2 amp Inj. Potassium Chloride (150mg/ml) / KCL (ops) / KT (jay), I/V stat @ 10 d/m with cardiac monitoring. ✔ Correction of hypomagnesemia (If present). ✔ Treat underlying cause. Concentration : ○ 1 amp KT = 20 mmol KCl = 1.5 gm/10 ml. ○ Maximum rate of infusion: 10 meq/L in hour. ○ Maximum KT in each liter of NS: 2 amp. ○ 1 TSF KT = 7.5 mmol K + . ○ To raise potassium level from 3 mmol/L to 3.5 mmol/L K + needed about 150 – 200 mmol & to raise potassium level from 2 mmol/L to 3 mmol/L K + needed about 200 – 400 mmol. Must Know : - Never to give KCl I/V directly, it causes cardiac arrhythmia, arrest & ultimately death. Warn the nurse to give in drip (very slowly). Again drip of K+ should not be infused rapidly, it causes phlebitis. - Never give 2 amp + 1 L N/S in less than 4 hours e.g., >40 d/m. - K + should not be infused with dextrose containing fluid (DA, DNS). Initial administration of K + with dextrose/glucose containing fluid to prevent further shift of K + into cells with glucose, that causes further reduction of serum potassium level. - Magnesium deficiency also needs to be corrected at the same time, particularly in refractory hypokalemia.

treatment.title: Hyperkalaemia (↑ K+)
treatment.description: (Normally 3.5 – 5 mmol/L, Hyperkalaemia if > 5 mmol/L) Sign : Progressive muscular weakness, Bradycardia, cardia arrhythmia, cardiac arrest. Drug history : NSAIDs, ACE inhibitors, potassium sparing drug, prolonged heparin therapy. Past history : kidney disease, burn. Investigations : 1. Electrolytes with bicarbonate 2. ECG : a. Tall peak T wave. b. Widening of QRS complex. c. Absent P wave 3. S. Creatinine. 4. RBS. Treatment (Hyperkalaemia) : Θ Immediate hospitalization. Θ Diet : Restricts fruits & avoid potassium containing food. First : Stabilize cell membrane potential : Θ Inj. 10% Calcium Gluconate 10 ml (if 5 ml then 2 amp) (1 amp + 10cc D/W) IV stat slowly over 15 min for 5 days. ± Θ Calcium Polystyrene Sulfonate / Calcium Resonium Sachets (sanofi) Or, Sodium Polystyrene Sulfonate 15gm / Normo-K (sq) / Level-K (incp), 1 sachet in 1 cup water after meal TDS/QDS. Second : Shift K + into cells (ICF) : Θ Inf. 25% Dextrose / Neutridex 100 ml + Inj. Short acting regular insulin (100 U) / Actrapid (novo) / Maxsulin R (incp) / Humulin R (incp) / Ansulin R (sq) / Gensulin R (bex) 5 IU, I/V stat @ 15 d/m. Θ Nebulization with Salbutamol (20 ml bottle) / Sultolin (sq) / Salmolin (acm) / Windel (incp) / Azmasol (bex), 2ml + 2ml N/S, stat and repeat once if necessary. [Cautious in IHD & avoid in tachy-arrhythmias] Third : If acidosis present : Θ Inj. Sodium Bicarbonate / NaHCO 3 (7.5 mg / 100 ml) / Sodi bi carb, 8 vial I/V slowly stat, then – 4 vial I/V slowly BD or 2 vial I/V slowly TDS. Fourth : Remove K + from body : Θ Inj. Frusemide 20 mg / Lasix (syn) / Fusid (sq) / Frusin (ops), 2 amp (4 ml) IV stat (& BD if needed). Or, Θ Inj. N/S 80 ml + 10 amp Inj. Frusemide 20 mg / Lasix, IV stat @ 4 dµ / min. Or, May Ion-exchange resin/Resonium suspension orally or per-rectally. Or, Dialysis / Intra-Peritoneal dialysis (IPD) may needed.

treatment.title: Terminology
treatment.description: An approach to diagnosis of anaemia from examination findings : Thalassemia → Anaemia Jaundice Hepato-splenomegaly Haemolytic anaemia → Anaemia No bleeding manifestation No lymp-hadenopathy No bony tenderness Hepato-splenomegaly Aplastic anaemia → Anaemia Bleeding manifestation No lymph-adenopathy No bony tenderness No hepato-splenomegally Acute leukaemia → Anaemia Bleeding manifestation Lymph-adenopathy Bony tenderness Hepato-splenomegaly Terminology in PBF & investigations : 1. RDW = Red cell distribution of width. A high red cell distribution width (RDW) means there are different red cell sizes. If low all are of same size. 2. Microcytic = ↓ cell size. 3. Anisocytosis = Different sizes of cells on smear = high RDW. 4. Poikilocytosis = Different shapes. 5. Bands early neutrophils (like a reticulocyte for neutrophils). 6. Polychromasía = Different colors, usually from reticulocytosis. 7. Hypochromic = ↓ Hb.

treatment.title: Anaemia
treatment.description: Anaemia is a condition in which the number of red blood cells or the haemoglobin concentration within them is lower than normal. It mainly affects women and children. Normal hemoglobin levels : Age Level (g/dL) Age Level (g/dL) Newborn 14 – 24 12 – 18 yr 12 – 16 At 1 month > 9 Adult women 12 – 16 1 – 6 months 10 – 17 In pregnancy > 11 6 mon – 2 yr 10 – 14 Adult men 14 – 18 ● Mild : Hemoglobin 10.0 g/dL to lower limit of normal. ● Moderate : Hemoglobin 8.0 to 10.0 g/dL. ● Severe : Hemoglobin 6.5 to 7.9 g/dL. ● Life-threatening : Hemoglobin less than 6.5 g/dL. Common Symptoms : - Pallor. - Tiredness. - Palpitation. - Headache. - Easy fatigability. - H/O GI Blood loss. - Excess menstrual loss. Common Signs : - Pallor in conjunctiva, tongue, palm, sole & nail. - Hyperdynamic Circulation : ○ Increased SBP, ○ Wide Pulse Pressure. - Tachycardia. - Delayed Capillary Refill (CRT). Common Investigations : - CBC, PBF, Red Cell Indices & Reticulocyte Counts. - Iron Profile : Ferritin, TIBC, Transferrin. - Hb Electrophoresis. - Coomb’s test/ANA etc. Check HB% & exclude suspected haemolytic anaemia. General Management : Packed Cell ± Iron infusion. Specialist referral : if poor response or recurrence.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Iron deficiency anaemia (IDA) is the most common form of anaemia. Iron deficiency anaemia develops when body stores of iron drop too low to support normal need blood cell production. It occurs when losses or physiological requirements exceed absorption. RBC in IDA, are microcytic (reduced in MCY) and hypochromic, low serum ferritin and/or low transferring saturation. Common Symptoms : • Fatigue • Leg cramps on climbing stairs • Craving for ice • Palpitation • Headache • Pallor Common Signs : • Impaired growth in infants and children • Anaemia • Angular stomatitis • Glory, smooth tongue • Koilonychia In most cases history of blood loss presents – menorrhagia, melaena & haematemesis, fresh per rectal or vaginal bleeding. Common Investigations : • CBC c̄ PBF : - Decreased Hb. - MCV – Reduced. - MCH/MCHC – Reduced. - RDW > 14% Peripheral Blood Film -Microcytosis, hypochromia. CBC c̄ PBF must be done before BT or 3 months after BT. • Iron Profile : - Ferritin : Reduced - Iron: Reduced - TIBC: Increased - Transferrin saturation: Decreased - Soluble transfrerrin receptor : Increased. Investigation to confirm iron deficiency anaemia – PBF – microcytic, hypochromic anaemia (MCV and MCHC reduced), S. Ferritin – low. Investigation to determine cause of iron deficiency anaemia – • Bone marrow aspiration for iron store. • To find out the aetiology : - Upper GI Endoscopy. - Colonoscopy with ileoscopy. - Serum tissue transglutaminase antibody. - Stool and urine exam for parasite. Exclude haemoglobinopathy (by Hb electrophoresis) in those from at-risk ethnic groups or with family history.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: General Management : 1. Diet- iron containing foods like banana, lal shak, kochu shak etc. 2. Oral Iron therapy : Ferrous sulphate (F/S) – 200mg 3 times daily for 3-6 months. If patient is intolerant to F/S 8 hourly dose then reduce dose to 12 hourly. Or, Ferrous gluconate – 300 mg 2 time daily 3-6 months. Side effect of oral iron : Nausea, abdominal discomfort, black stool, diarrhoea, constipation. 3. Parenteral Iron therapy : Indication – suitable formulation. i) Malabsorption --- Iron. ii) Chronic gut therapy --- Iron sucrose. iii) Chronic kidney disease --- Iron isomaltose. iv) Inability to tolerate oral iron – Iron carboxymaltose. 4. Blood transfusion (BT). Indicated it – i) If patient have angina. ii) Heart failure. iii) Evidence of cerebral anoxia. 5. Anthelmintic : Mebendazole 100 mg 12 hourly for 3 days. 6. Treatment of underlying aetiology. (like – treatment of menorrhagia, bleeding piles, PUD etc.) Model Prescription : ► Cap. Carbonyl Iron + Folic Acid + Zinc + Vit-B Complex + Vit-C / Cap. Zif Forte (sq) / EPL Plus (glob) / Prenat-XL (hc) / Zilvit (skf) / Xvit (bacn), 1 to 3 capsule per day (A/M) ––– 6 months Or, ► Cap. Ferric Maltol 30 mg / Filofer (acm) / Malifa (nav) / FML (ori), 0+1+0 or 1+0+1 (B/M) ––– 6 months Or, IV iron (given below). + ► Tab. Mebendazole 100 mg / Solas (ops) / Halben (ad-din), 1 + 0 + 1 ––– 3 days F/U after 3 months with CBC report & other as per suspect. IV iron total dose in mg = Body weight in Kg × (Target Hb – Actual Hb) × 2.4 + Storage iron. Storage iron : 15 mg/Kg, minimum 500 mg for calculation. E.g., patient of 46 kg weight, Hb is 8 g/dl. Calculation of IV iron requirement. Total iron deficit = = Weight (Kg) × (Target Hb – Actual Hb) × 2.4 + Storage iron = 46 × (13 – 8) × 2.4 + (15×46) = 46 × 12 + 690 = 552 + 690 = 1242 mg ✔ Inj. Ferric Carboxymaltose 500 mg/10 ml + Inf. NS 100 ml / Inj. Ferisen (hc) / Maltofer (incp) / Ferinject (uni) / Oxyfer (ibn) / Xefer (zis) / Hemoject (nuv) / Hemorise (acm) / Iroject (pop), I/V stat @ 30 drops/min and another dose 7 days later. Caution : There is danger of anaphylactoid reactions; hence facilities to manage these should be readily available. ► Follow up : - Hb should rise 1.0 gm/dL in every 7-10 days. - Reticulocyte count rises (reticulocytosis) within 7-10 days. - Clinical response by improvement of symptoms. Follow-up investigations : Once normal, monitor Hb. MCH, and MCV, - once every 3 months for 1 year, and then, - once every 12 months (annually). Give further iron supplements if Hb, MCH, or MCY fall below normal levels. Investigate further if unable to maintain Hb. ✔ Usually capsule formulations dissolve in ileum but iron absorption take place at stomach and duodenum, so iron capsule has little benefit. Iron should not be prescribed with PPI, because acidic media is necessary to absorb iron. REFER – ○ Unexplained IDA or failed treatment to maintain Hb ○ Iron deficiency anaemia with dysphagia, weight loss or suspected carcinoma, non responding rectal bleeding or melena.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: HEREDITARY HAEMOLYTIC ANAEMIA (THALASSAEMIA) (Referred to Hematologist) ● Generalized weakness. ● No fever. ● No bleeding manifestation. ● H/O repeated blood transfusion. ● Appearance : Mongoloid facies (Thalassaemic facies) : ○ frontal and parietal bossing, ○ depressed nasal bridge, ○ prominent zygomatic & maxillary prominence, ○ mal-aligned jaw and teeth. On examination – anaemia & jaundice present, no lymphadenopathy, no bony tenderness but hepatosplenomegaly present. Investigation – ▸ Hb% - reduced. ▸ PBF - microcytic hypochromic anaemia, target cell present. ▸ Serum Iron profile : Serum ferritin. ▸ X-ray skull : ○ Hair on end appearance. ○ Increased diploic space. ○ Thinning of cuticle. ▸ Hb electrophoresis (confirmatory for diagnosis) : Reduced HbA and raised HbF. Treatment : ● Blood transfusion: To keep Hb 10 gm/dl. ● Tab. Folic acid 5 mg / Folison, 0+0+1 --- continue ● Caution : Iron should not be supplemented in thalassaemia. When to start iron chelation : ○ Usually, after, 10-20 transfusions or, ○ When, S ferritin level >1000 ng/ml. ○ When liver iron conc. > 5000 µg/gram of dry weight. Iron Chelation Agent : ● Desferoxamine (Inj. Desferal 500 mg/vial) : Start with 25mg/kg, increase gradually, based on iron burden to 60 mg/kg, IV or SC, slowly over 8 hours/day for 5-7 days/week. ● Deferasirox (Tab Asunra 100mg, 400mg) : Start with 14mg/ kg/day, can be escalated to a max of 28 mg/kg/day, based on iron burden, orally, once daily. ● Deferiprone (Cap Kelfer 250 mg, 500 mg) : Start with 75mg/ kg/day, can be escalated to 99 mg/kg/day based on iron burden, given orally, 3 times daily. Iron chelators may be given alone or in combination. Combined chelation therapy is indicated for patients with high iron load. Counseling : Inform & blood test of partner before marriage. Discourage consanguineous marriage (within 1 ° cousins).

treatment.title: Dietary advice for thalassemia
treatment.description: XXX খাদ্য সামগ্রীর নাম থ্যালাসেমিয়া রোগীর খাওয়া নিষেধ : ¤ অধিক লৌহযুক্ত খাবার এড়িয়ে চলুন । ¤ মাছ : ইলিশ মাছ, কৈ মাছ, চিতল, টেংরা, ছোট মাছের শুটকি । ¤ মাংস : গরু ও খাসির মাংস, কলিজা । ¤ ডিমের কুসুম । ¤ শাক : কচু শাক, লাল শাক, পালং শাক, পুঁই শাক, সজিনা শাক/সাজনা ডাট (ছটি) ¤ সবজি : ফূল কপি, পুদিনা পাতা, ধনে পাতা, শিম, বরবটি, মটরশুটি, কাঁকরোল, কাঁচা পেঁপে, কাঁচা টমেটো । ¤ আনারস, বেদানা, শরিফা, খেজুর, তরমুজ । ¤ শিশুখাদ্য : কর্নফেক্স, খৈ, লৌহ মেশানো শিশুখাদ্য, যেমন : সেরিলাক, নিডো, কাউ এন্ড গেট ইত্যাদি। ¤ বিবিধ : ছোলা ও ছোলার ডাল, গুড়, বাদাম, কিসমিস, তিল, পান, জিরা, ধনে, সরিষা ইত্যাদি । ✔✔✔ খাদ্য সামগ্রীর নাম : থ্যালাসেমিয়া রোগীর খাওয়া যাবে : ¤ কম লৌহযুক্ত খাবার খাবেন । ¤ মাছ কাতলা মাছ, বোয়াল মাছ, মাগুড় মাছ, পুটি মাছ । ¤ মধু, দুধ, দুগ্ধজাত খাদ্য-দই, ছানা, রসগোল্লা ইত্যাদি । ¤ সবজি বাঁধা কপি, মিষ্টি আলু, করলা, শসা, ঝিঙ্গা, কাঁচাকলা, লাউ, চাল কুমড়া, মিষ্টি কুমড়া ঢেঁড়স, মুলা, শালগম । ¤ লিচু, কলা, পাকা পেঁপে, পাকা আম, কমলা লেবু, বেল, জামরুল, আমলকি, কাগজি লেবু, জামরুল, আতা, আঙ্গুর । ¤ বিবিধ মসুর ডাল, চাল, ময়দা, পাউরুটি ইত্যাদি । ╬ এছাড়াও নিচে নিয়ম সমূহ মেনে চলবেন : ● খাবার লোহার পাত্রে রান্না করবেন না । লোহার পাত্রে রান্না করা খাবার থেকে বিরত থাকুন। আয়রণ সিরাপ খাবেন না । ● তিন বেলা নাস্তা, দুপুরে ও রাতে খাবারের পর পর চিনি ছাড়া চা, কফি, দুধ বা টক দই খেতে হবে। ● খাবারের সাথে অথবা পরপর ফল খাওয়া যাবেনাা । ফল খেতে হলে তা দুই খাবারের মাঝে খেতে হবে। ● চিনি ও মিষ্টি জাতীয় খাবারও খাবার খাবারের সাথে খাওয়া যাবেনা। দুই খাবারের মাঝে খেতে হবে।

treatment.title: Blood transfusion reaction
treatment.description: a) Febrile reaction : Patient complaint of fever, chills & rigor (If isolated temperature ≥ 38°C (> 100°F) , or rise of 1-2°C from baseline). Patient otherwise normal. Management 1. Stop the (BT) transfusion temporarily. 2. Paracetamol tablet or suppository. 3. Restart the transfusion at a slower rate. 4. Observe the patient more frequently. b) Mild allergic reaction : Urticaria (wheal) : 1. Stop the transfusion. 2. Give Inj. Chlorpheniramine 10 mg/ml [Inj. Histacin (jay)] or Inj. Pheniramine 45.5 mg/2 ml [Inj. Amarin (ops) / Avil (syn)] IV slowly. 3. Restart the transfusion at a slower rate. 4. Observe frequently. c) Severe allergic reaction : 1. Bronchospasm (SOB), abdominal pain. 2. Angioedema, hypotension. Management 1. Discontinue the transfusion permanently. 2. Start O 2 inhalation. 3. Nebulize with salbutamol. 4. Give Inj. Chlorpheniramine 10 mg/ml [Inj. Histacin (jay)] or Inj. Pheniramine 45.5 mg/2 ml [Inj. Amarin (ops)/Avil (syn)] IV slowly. 5. If severe bronchospasm or hypotension then give Inj. Adrenaline / Adrin (gaco)/ Adrinor (incp) [ 1 mg/ml], ½ amp or, 0.5 mg IM stat. 6. Send clotted blood sample to the transfusion laboratory. 7. Take down blood unit & giving set & return intact or all other used/unused unit to blood bank. d) ABO incompatibility : 1. Start intravenous normal saline infusion. 2. Monitor urine output – maintain urine output >100mI/ hour. Give frusemide if urine output falls. 3. Treat DIC with appropriate blood products. 4. Inform consultant & blood transfusion department immediately. 5. Take down blood unit & giving set & return intact or all other used/unused unit to blood bank. Complications related to transfusion e.g., ○ Allergic reaction. ○ Acute haemolytic reaction. ○ TRALI (Transfusion Related Acute Lung Injury). ○ TACO (Transfusion Associated Circulatory Overload). ○ Transmission of infectious agents e.g. HBV, HIV.

treatment.title: Indoor (admitted for BT)
treatment.description: C / C : - Known case of Thalassemia. - Patient come for BT. O / E : - Jaundice (++) / (+++). - Anemia (++) / (+++). - Hepatomegaly (+/-) / Splenomegaly (+/-) - BP & Pulse. Δ Patient come for BT / Thalassemia. ¤ Diet : Normal / Full. ¤ Tab. Folic Acid 5 mg / Folison (jay), 0 + 1 + 0 Inv. : Hb%, Blood screening, cross matching & demand (Given).

treatment.title: Splenectomy
treatment.description: Splenectomized patient Splenectomy usually done in traumatic injury in spleen and in thalassemia, due to development of hypersplenism. Indications of splenectomy – ✔ Transfusion requirement is increased; > 200 ml of packed cells/Kg/year. ✔ Massive splenomegaly with – ○ Mechanical discomfort ○ Fear of rupture ○ Left upper quadrant pain ○ Early satiety Prerequisites of splenectomy : ✔ Age: > 5 years (time needed for maturation of immune system) ✔ Vaccination : Four weeks prior to surgery against – Strept pneumoniae, H. influenzae type b and N meningitides. ✔ Care after splenectomy : Oral Phenoxymethyl Penicillin (lifelong prophylaxis) : ○ Adults : 500 mg every 12 hours. ○ >2 years : 250 mg every 12 hours. ○ ○ Prompt treatment of any infection. ▸ Tab. Phenoxymethyl penicillin 500 mg, 1+0+1 ---- continue ▸ Pneumococcal vaccination : Inj. Pneumovax 23 (0.5 ml)-5 yearly ▸ Influenza vaccination : Inj. Influvax Tetra (0.5 ml) -- every yearly Special attentions in splenectomized patient : ● Pneumococcal re-immunization should be given every 5 yearly. ● Influenza re-immunization should be given yearly basis. ● Penicillin V 500 mg 12 hourly lifelong (adult). ● Should carry bracelet or card. ● If patient develop any fever then prompt action should be taken, blood, urine should be cultured, because these patient rapidly develop septicaemia. ● In sepsis, patients should be resuscitated and given IV antibiotics to cover pneumococcus Haemophilus and meningococcus, according to local resistance patterns. ● The risk of cerebral malaria is increased in the event of infection. ● Animal bites should be promptly treated with local disinfection and antibiotics, to prevent serious soft tissue infection and sepsis.

treatment.title: Vaccination before Splenectomy
treatment.description: Vaccination before Splenectomy (Before at-least 15 days or 2 weeks of surgery) ► These vaccines are important to prevent opportunistic post-splenectomy infection (OPSI). Prophylactic antibiotics on admission before surgery. ► For Pnemococcus : 0.5 ml IM/SC Injection : Pneumovax 23 (hc, 2470/-), Prenovax 23 (2500/-), less effective in S/S route. Or, 0.5 ml IM Injection Evimar 13 (incp, 4000/-), Prevenar 13 (pfizer, 7400/-). ► Specially for Hemophillus influenza : Inj. Influvax Tetra (incp, 950/-) / Vaxigrip Tetra (syn, 1340/-) 0.5 ml I/M stat. ± ► For Meningococcus : Inj. Ingovax ACWY (incp, 1000/-) / Mencevax ACWY (skp), 0.5 ml S/C stat.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : 1. Symptoms of anaemia. 2. Patient may complaint of yellow coloration of eye and urine. 3. Features of underlying cause of autoimmune hemolytic anaemia may predominant (SLE). Investigation – PBF – spherocytes and polychromasia on the blood film. The diagnosis is confirmed by the direct Coombs or antiglobulin test. Management : 1. 1 st line treatment is steroid – prednisolone 1 mg/kg/day orally, when Hb% normalized, reticulocytosis resolved; dose of steroid should be reduced over 10 weeks. 2. 2 nd line treatment – if the haemolysis fails to respond to glucocorticoids or can only be stabilized by large doses. These include immunomodulation/suppression and splenectomy. If splenectomy is not appropriate, alternative immunosuppressive therapy with rituximab (choice of drug), azathioprine, ciclosporin, mycophenolate or cyclophosphamide may be considered. 3. Blood transfusion (BT) – when developed heart failure or unabated fails in Hb%.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : 1. Symptoms of anaemia. 2. Patient may be strict vegetarian. 3. May have history of gastrectomy or ileal resection. Investigation – PBF – macrocytic anaemia (MCV-increased commonly > 120 fL), Serum vit-B12 – low or red cell folate level low. Management: ● Diet- normal. ● Inj. Hydroxy-cobalamine 1000 μ g (Inj. Vitamin-B12 1mg/ml) IM – total 6 doses 2-3 days apart. Then 3 monthly lifelong, if cause of megaloblastic anaemia cannot be cured. In the presence of neurological involvement, a dose of 1000 μ g on alternate days until there is no further improvement, followed by maintenance as above. ● Folic acid 5 mg daily for 3 weeks, then 5 mg weekly for lifelong; if cause of megaloblastic anaemia cannot be removed. Follow up : Hb% (should raise 1gm/dl every weekly) and Reticulocyte count, serum electrolytes after 1 week. If initial response is not maintained or PBF becomes dimorphic (both microcytic and macrocytic), add: ● Oral iron supplement – ferrous sulphate 200 mg 8 hrly, add, Tab. Iron-polymaltose + Folic acid + Zinc, 1+0+1 – 3-6 months. ● Blood transfusion – if severe angina or heart failure.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Sickle cell anemia (SCA) (Referred to Hematologist) Features : Peripheral smear from a patient with sickle cell disease shows sickle cells, polychromatophilic RBCs, target cells, and Howell-Jolly bodies. Management : 1. Folic acid supplementation. 2. Penicillin V 500 mg BD (to protect against pneumococcal infection which may be lethal in the presence of hyposplenism). 3. Hydroxycarbamide. 4. Vaccinate against pneumococcus, H. influenza & Hepatitis B. Treatment of vaso-occlusive crisis : 1. O 2 inhalation. 2. Rehydration with normal saline. 3. Antibiotic. 4. Analgesia with opioids. 5. Exchange transfusion : In life threatening crisis or to prepare the patient for surgery.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : 1. Patient complaint -red brown color urine particularly in morning. 2. Urine R/E- haemoglobinuria. Management : 1. Diet – normal. No restriction needed. 2. Blood transfusion if anaemia. 3. Folic acid supplementation. 4. Treatment of thrombosis. 5. Recently anticomplement C5 monoclonal Ab ecluzimab is used.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: ACUTE LEUKAEMIA (Referred to Hematologist/Pedi-hemato-oncologist) Features : Peak age of incidence: ALL : 1-5 years, AML over the age of 50 years. Acute onset of high grade Fever, generalized weakness and bleeding manifestation (petechiae, purpura, ecchymosis). Bone or joint pain. O/ E : ○ Appearance: Toxic, ill looking, ○ Anaemia: +/+/+++, ○ Bony tenderness : +, ○ Lymphadenopathy : +, ○ Hepato-splenomegaly present. Investigation – CBC with PBF – Hb%-reduced, ESR-high, WBC count-increased (1 × 10 9 – 500 × 10 9 /L): PBF – increase blast cell. Bone marrow examination (blast cell >20%) to confirm diagnosis. Management (Supportive) : 1. Correction of anaemia : Blood transfusion (before BT, PBF should be done). 2. Antibiotic to control infection. 3. Tab. Paracetamol (500 mg) 1+1+1 (A/M). 4. Control of bleeding : ○ Platelet transfusion. ○ Whole blood (if platelet transfusion not available). 5. Maintenance of nutrition. 6. Maintenance of fluid and electrolytes. For specific management patient should be referred to Haematologist.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Chronic myeloid leukaemia (CML) (Referred to Hematologist) Features : Patient present with the complaint of lump/discomfort in left side of the abdomen, weakness, loss of appetite. On examination – huge splenomegaly, usually no bony tenderness & ± lymphadenopathy. Investigation – a) CBC with PBF – ○ WBC count increased (10 thousands to 6 lacs), ○ PBF – predominant cells are neutrophils and myelocytes. Myeloblasts usually constitute less than 10% of all white cells. There is often an absolute increase in eosinophils and basophils, and nucleated red cells (reticulocytosis) are common. b) Bone marrow examination to confirm the diagnosis and phase of disease. c) S. uric acid – Usually increased due to increase cell breakdown. Before management identification of phase of CML is mandatory CML has 3 phases – ○ Chronic phase, ○ Accelerated phase and ○ Blast crisis phase. Progression of the disease from chronic phase is characterized by – i) increase in blast cell count, ii) reduction of platelet count, iii) increase in basophil count.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Multiple myeloma (MM) (Referred to Hematologist/Oncologist) Features : Patient usually old aged (60-70 years)-presented with generalized bodyache, headache (due to hyper viscosity). ○ Epistaxis / bleeding manifestation. ○ Heart failure nephrotic syndrome. ○ Back pain / spinal cord compression. ○ Features of hypercalcaemia. Investigation – for diagnosis any of the two criteria will require : i) Increase malignant plasma cells in the bone marrow. ii) Serum and/or urinary paraprotein present iii) Skeletal lytic lesions present (usually on skull x-ray). If asymptomatic with no evidence of end organ damage (e.g. to kidneys, bone marrow or bone) : Follow up only. If symptomatic : ● Tab. Naproxen 500mg, 1+1+1 (A/M). ● Cap. Thalidomide 50 mg [5mg/kg], 4+0+0 --- 4 days, Every 4 weekly until plateau phase of electrophoresis. ● Tab. Melphalan 2 mg [0.2mg/kg], 2+0+0 ---- 4 days, Every 4 weekly until plateau phase of electrophoresis. ● Tab. Prednisolone 20 mg, 2+0+0 (A/M) --- continue ● Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ---- continue ● Tab. Allopurinol 100 mg, 1+0+0 --- continue ● Tab. Alendronic Acid + Vitamin D3 (70 mg+2800 IU) / Ostel-D (sq) / Bonemass D (incp), 1 tab dissolved in 1 glass of water in morning in empty stomach and stay seated (up right) for ½ hour --- continue

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: HODGKIN'S LYMPHOMA / Hodgkin's disease (HD) (Referred to Hematologist/Oncologist) Signs and symptoms : ● Focal/generalized lymphadenopathy : Painless swelling of lymph nodes in neck, armpits or groin. ● May be pain in lymph nodes after drinking alcohol. ● Persistent fatigue. ● Fever with drenching night sweats. ● Weight loss. ● Severe itching. O/E : Lymphadenopathy : (+) Painless, Rubbery. Hepato-Splenomegaly : (+) Bony tenderness : (-) Investigation : 1. CBC. 2. FNAC/Biopsy of lymph node: Reed-Sternberg (RS) cell. 3. Serum LDH 4. CXR : To see mediastinal mass 5. CT chest, abdomen: For staging 6. Serum creatinine

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: NON-HODGKIN'S LYMPHOMA (Referred to Hematologist/Oncologist) Signs and symptoms of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma may include : ● Generalized lymphadenopathy : Swollen lymph nodes in neck, armpits or groin. ● Persistent fatigue. ● Fever. ● Night sweats. ● Unexplained weight loss. ● Compression syndromes may occur, including gut obstruction, ascites, superior vena cava obstruction and spinal cord compression. ● Abdominal pain or swelling. ● Chest pain, coughing or trouble breathing. O/E : Lymphadenopathy : (+) Painless, Rubbery. Hepato-Splenomegaly : (+) Bony tenderness : (-)

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Aplastic Anaemia Features : Patient present with the symptoms of pancytopenia. ○ Anaemia (weakness), ○ Leucopenia (infection-fever) and ○ Thrombocytopenia (bleeding manifestations – gum bleeding, epistaxis, menorrhagia etc.). On examination – ESR high, anaemia present, bruise, purpura, echymoses may be present but no bony tenderness, no lymphadenopathy, no hepatosplenomegaly. Investigation – PBF normocytic normochromic anaemia, bone marrow – hypocellular. Refer to hematologists or pediatric hemato-oncologists : all of blood cancer, including leukaemia, lymphoma, myeloma, myelodysplastic syndromes (MDS) and myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPN). Again aplastic anemia (pancytopenia) must be treat by hematologists from very beginning course of the disease.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Polycythaemia rubra vera Features : Patients over the age of 40 years and presents either as an incidental finding of a high haemoglobin, or with symptoms of hyperviscosity, such as lassitude, loss of concentration, headache, dizziness, blackouts, pruritus and epistaxis. Some patients present with manifestations of peripheral arterial disease or a cerebrovascular accident. On examination – patients are often plethoric face and the majority have a palpable spleen at diagnosis. Investigation – ○ Hb : adult female >16.5 gram/dl or, haematocrit > 0.48; ○ Hb : adult males 18.0 g/dl or, haematocrit > 0.52. Management : 1. Aspirin 75mg (0+1+0,A/M) daily (reduces the risk of thrombosis). 2. Hydroxycarbamide or interferon-alfa (suppression of marrow proliferation). Tab. Hydroxyurea 500 mg, 1+0+0 – continue. 3. Venesection: Remove 400-500ml blood every 5-7 days until PCV or Hct is below 45%. 4. Radioactive phosphorous for older patient. Prognosis : 1. Median survival after diagnosis : >10 years, some patients survive more than 20 years. 2. CVD or IHD in up to 60% of patients. 3. May convert to another myeloproliferative disorder, with about 15% developing acute leukaemia or myelofibrosis.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Deep Venous Thrombosis (DVT) Pain and swelling of leg gradual in onset, over hours or even days. Past history: Any immobilization, recent major surgery (e.g. pelvic, orthopedic), malignancy. On local examination : Asymmetrical swelling, Warm, red and painful tender leg. Investigations : Duplex ultrasound scan of lower limb, INR. Treatment : Tab. Rivaroxaban 20 mg / Rivarox (skf) / Kinexa (bex) / Rivaban (aci) 20 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) –– 21 days then, 0+1+0 (A/M) –– continue Duration of anticoagulation : 1. If temporary risk factor which can be removed : 3 months 2. If ongoing risk factor: Life long.

treatment.title: Rivaroxaban
treatment.description: Rivaroxaban dosing : Treatment of DVT & PE : 15 mg orally twice daily with food for the first 21 days for the initial treatment of acute DVT or PE. After the initial treatment period, 20 mg orally once daily with food for the remaining treatment. Prevention in the risk of recurrence of DVT and of PE : 20 mg once daily with food. Prophylaxis of DVT following Hip replacement surgery : 10 mg once daily for 35 days. Prophylaxis of DVT following knee replacement surgery : 10 mg once daily for 12 days.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Idiopathic Thrombocytopenic Purpura (ITP) Features : Patient usually female, presented with the complaint of bleeding manifestation like gum bleeding, purpura, easy bruising or sometimes epistaxis or menorrhagia (fever usually absent). On examination – anaemia and splenomegaly present. Investigation – PBF – usually normal except platelet count reduced, Bone marrow – increase in megakaryocytes. Treatment : Stable patient with Platelet count > 30 × 10 9 /L : No treatment. ↓ Patient with spontaneous bleeding : ● Tab. Prednisolone 1mg/kg/day at morning. Or, ● Inj. Dexamethasone 5mg/1ml, 2-amp IV QDS- 4 days [40mg/day for 4 days] ● Cap. Omeprazole 20mg, 1+0+1 (B/M). ↓ If severe hemostatic failure (significant mucosal bleeding or slow response to steroid): Above + IV Ig. ↓ Persistent or potentially life-threatening bleeding : Above + Platelet transfusion. ↓ If two relapses or primary refractory diseases : Second-line therapy include the thrombopoietin receptor agonists (TPO-RA) eltrombopag and romiplostim, splenectomy and immunosuppression. ● Splenectomy & ● Tab. Eltrombopag 25/50mg, 1+0+0. ↓ If significant bleeding persists despite splenectomy – low dose steroid, immunosuppressive therapy with rituximab, ciclosporin & tacrolimus.
