treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: HAEMOPHILIA Features : Male patient with spontaneous bleeding in muscle or joints following trivial (minor) injury. H/O prolonged bleeding following circumcision, tonsillectomy or minor surgery. Family history : Positive family history on maternal side. Joint examination: Calf muscle haematoma, knee haematoma. Knee jerks : May be absent. Investigations : 1. APTT: Prolonged 2. Factor VIII and IX : Reduced. Haemophilia A : ● Mild case (Factor VIII 0.05 – 0.4 U/mL) : A vasopressin receptor agonist (Desmopressin / DDAVP) 0.3 μ g/kg IV or, subcutaneously (S/C). Alternatively same effect can be achieved by intranasal administration of 300 μ g. ● Moderate to severe case (Factor VIII : In severe hemophilia A intravenous infusion of factor VIII concentration. ○ Inj. Aphanate (Factor VIII) 500 U, 50 unit/kg IV stat and 25U/kg TDS. Haemophilia B : Factor IX concentration intravenous infusion. Advices : Tab. Naproxen + Esomeprazole (500/20) mg, 1+0+1 or 1+1+1 (B/M) [If pain present] ○ Resting of the bleeding site by bed rest or splint. ○ Once bleeding has settled : mobilization and physiotherapy. Prognosis : 1. Over one-third survive : 5 years. 2. The outlook may improve further with new drugs and combinations of treatments. Cause of death : Infection & Renal failure.

treatment.title: F/U for thyroid hormones
treatment.description: Investigation (blood) in diagnosis & F/U for thyroid hormones : For diagnosis and F/U of – Investigation Comment Thyrotoxicosis : TSH, FT4, FT3 Both T3 & T4 reduces first after treatment, TSH takes time to increase. Hypothyroidism: TSH, FT4 (No FT3) No FT3, as it may be normal as it converted from T4. Secondary Hypothyroidism or, Hypopituitarism: FT4 (No TSH) No TSH, as it may low, normal or even slightly elevated due to inactive TSH isoform in blood.

treatment.title: Definitions & Features
treatment.description: • Hypothyroidism : Clinical syndrome resulting from thyroid hormone deficiency causing generalized slowing down of metabolic process. • Myxoedema : Severe form of hypothyroidism due to deposition of glycosaminoglycans. All hypothyroidism may not be myxoedematous. • Cretinism: Congenital hypothyroidism due to iodine deficiency. Symptoms : Short stature, mental retardation, deaf mutism. Signs : Puffy face, respiratory difficulty, cyanosis, jaundice, poor feeding, hoarse voice, marked retardation of bone maturation. Causes of Hypothyroidism A. Primary / Thyro-privic hypothyroidism : • Hashimoto's thyroiditis / Auto-immune hypothyroidism • Iodine deficiency/ Endemic goiter • RAI Therapy for Grave's disease • Thyroidectomy for thyrotoxicosis, thyroid cancer • Congenital • Drugs: Lithium, Amiodarone B. Secondary / Tropho-privic / Central hypothyroidism : Hypopituitarism due to primary adenoma, pituitary surgery. Symptoms of Hypothyroidism : • Weight Gain • Cold intolerance • Fatigue, somnolence • Constipation • Menorrhagia (in hyperthyroidism amenorrhea) • Infertility (recurrent abortion) • Aches and pains, muscle cramp • Sleep apnoea syndrome Signs of Hypothyroidism : • Face : • Puffy face with periorbital swelling and baggy eyelids. • Loss of lateral 1/3rd of eyebrows (Queen Anne's sign) • Macro-glossia • Skin : Dry, rough, cold and thick skin. • Hair : Dry and brittle. • Voice : Low pitched, slurred, hoarse and croaky (may be diagnosis over telephone). • Edema : Non pitting edema in legs, generalized swelling of whole body. • CVS : Bradycardia, low voltage ECG, HTN, Pericardial effusion, IHD, CCF. Neurological features of hypothyroidism : • Carpal tunnel syndrome • Delayed relaxation of ankle reflexes, best elicited by kneel down position on bed side (Hang up reflex). • Peripheral neuropathy • Dementia • Psychosis (Myxoedema madness) • Myxoedema coma Indications of thyroid function test for Hypothyroidism by different subspecialists : Internist : Weight gain, fatigue, anaemia, constipation, aches and pains, unexplained edema/pericardial effusion/pleural effusion. Endocrinologist : H/O thyroid surgery or RAI therapy, Goiter / abnormal thyroid examination, those with autoimmune disease. Cardiologist : Cardiac arrhythmia, unspecified HTN, patient taking Amiodarone, Hypercholesterolemia, low voltage ECG, CCF. Neurologist : Dementia, carpal tunnel syndrome, peripheral neuropathy, ataxia. Psychiatrist : Depression, psychosis, patient taking lithium. ENT surgeon : Deafness, hoarse voice. Dermatologist : Dry skin, alopecia, vitiligo. Gynaecologist : Menorrhagia, infertility, pregnancy loss. Geriatrician : Screening test or hypothermia.

treatment.title: Investigation
treatment.description: Hypothyroidism Patients complaints – rapid weight gain, cold intolerance, somnolence, swelling of whole body etc. On examination – bradycardia, skin-dry, rough; non-pitting edema, slow and delayed relaxation of the ankle jerk. Investigation – ● S. TSH and ● FT4 ± ● Thyroid autoantibodies In hypothyroidism, usually FT4 is low and TSH is high. Better to do test in fasting state & from nearest standard / well equipped clinical laboratory / diagnostic center / govt. Institute/ Nuclear Medicine & Allied Sciences (INMAS). Usually Free T3 is within normal limit (WNL). Because of compensatory response to falling T4. But T3 falls in late disease course. FT3 should not be done as a diagnostic tool of hypothyroidism. FT3 has only role in T3 thyrotoxicosis diagnosis. In suspected autoimmune hypothyroidism, thyroid auto-antibodies should be screened. Usually seen – 1. TPOAb or, thyroid anti-peroxidase (anti-microsomal). ± 2. TgAb or, Thyroglobulin Antibody. Conditions associated with elevated serum TPOAb levels includes – Hashimoto disease (90%-100%) & Graves’ disease (50%-80%). Thyroid auto-antibodies result is either positive or negative. If positive diagnosis is “Autoimmune hypothyroidism”. Other investigation in hypothyroidism : • Mainly, FT4 : ↓ , TSH : ↑ . • In secondary hypothyroidism : FT4: ↓ , TSH: ± ↓ . • In autoimmune hypothyroidism : thyroid autoantibodies : Antibody to thyroid peroxidase (90–100%), Thyroglobulin (80–90%) • CBC: Hb% (anemia). Features of anemia in Hypothyroidism : ¤ Normocytic normochromic (Due to thyroxine deficiency) ¤ Microcytic Hypochromic (Menorrhagia) ¤ Macrocytic (Associated pernicious anemia) • RBS. • ECG : Low voltage ECG, sinus bradycardia, ST-T wave abnormalities. • Chest X ray : Cardiomegaly due to HTN, HF, pericardial effusion also pleural effusion.

treatment.title: Treatment & Follow-up
treatment.description: Treatment : Levothyroxine / T4 / Thyroxine replacement for life long. In adult, replacement dose of T4, 1-2 μ g/kg/day 30-60 min before breakfast. May be taken any time in empty stomach. Levothyroxine (50µgm) daily (half hour before breakfast) for 3 weeks then 100 µgm daily. After 6 weeks do FT4 and TSH then adjust dose of levothyroxine (usually 100-150 µgm). Levothyroxine usually needs to continue for life long period. In adult approximately 1.6 mcg/kg levothyroxine is required to become euthyroid. So, levothyroxine can be started at a dose of 1.6 mcg/kg and follow up after 10 weeks. This should not be practiced in old age and IHD patients. Start at low dose in IHD patient. Increase dose of levothyroxine is required in the following condition : Pregnancy, childhood, Malabsorption, Drugs – Sertraline, Ferrous sulphate, Calcium carbonate, Anticonvulsant drugs, Aluminium hydroxide, Rifampicin, Chloroquine. Follow-up : Monitor FT4 and TSH every 4-6 weeks until dose of Levothyroxine is adjusted & stabilized, then FT4 and TSH monitored once every 6-12 months. Dramatic (thyroxine) drug response in hypothyroidism : ¤ Patient usually feel better within 2-3 weeks. ¤ Reduction in weight & periorbital puffiness occurs quickly. ¤ Restoration of skin & hair texture and resolution of any effusion take 3-6 months.

treatment.title: Hypothyroidism c̄ IHD
treatment.description: Hypothyroidism in Ischemic heart disease (IHD) The basic principle of management of hypothyroidism in IHD is to start levothyroxine at a lower dose than usual (25 µgm or lower), because it can aggravate or precipitate IHD. Levothyroxine (25 µgm) daily (half hour before breakfast) for 3 weeks (after 3 weeks, dose should be increased). It will be better to start a beta blocker before starting levothyroxine. Better refer to a cardiologist or endocrinologist.

treatment.title: Hypothyroidism in Pregnancy
treatment.description: Hypothyroidism in Pregnancy (Pg) ¤ Hypothyroid women treated with levothyroxine (LT4) who are planning pregnancy, Serum TSH should be evaluated preferably before conception and levothyroxine (LT4) dose adjusted to achieve S. TSH ¤ Levothyroxine replacement therapy dose requirements increase by 30-50% from early in pregnancy & by 20-30% increased dose in late pregnancy. Because placenta metabolize thyroxine & increase thyroxine binding globulin in pregnancy. FT4 & TSH should be assessed in every trimester. ¤ LT4, iron & calcium must not be taken at same time. Better to take LT4 at morning (before meal), calcium at noon (after meal) & iron at night (after meal). Drugs must be taken at least 4 hours apart. Complications of Hypothyroidism in pregnancy Mother : • Increase and recurrent Pregnancy loss • Pre-eclampsia • Preterm delivery • PPH • Increased maternal Mortality Fetus : • LBW • Still birth • Impaired intellectual and psychomotor development of fetus / Low IQ / Cretinism

treatment.title: Reference values
treatment.description: Reference values of thyroid function test : Investigation Normal values Target in Pg TSH 0.5 – 5 mIU/L below 2.5 mU/L FT4 or Free T4 0.8 – 1.8 ng/dL 1.0 – 1.5 ng/dl TT4 or Total T4 5 – 12 μ g/dL Other units : Serum TSH : 0.2 – 4.5 mU/L FT4 or Free T4 : 9 – 21 pmol/L FT3 or Free T3 150 – 500 pg/dL TT3 or Total T3 80 – 220 ng/dL TPO Antibody IU/ml Tg Antibody TBG 1.1 to 2.1 mg/dl

treatment.title: Levothyroxine (LT4)
treatment.description: Available Tab. Levothyroxine (LT4) : 12.5 mcg Thyronor (nuv) 25 mcg Thyrox (ren) / Thyrin (sq) / Thyronor (nuv) / Leroid (drg) 50 mcg or, 50 μ g Thyrox (ren) / Thyrin (sq) / Thyronor (nuv) / Thyrolar (acm) / Leroid (drg) / Thynor (skf) / Euthycin (incp) / Thormon (aris) / Leroxy (pop) / Synroid (bex) / Thyrotab (ibn) 75 mcg Thyrox (ren) / Thyronor (nuv) 100 mcg Thyrox (ren) / Thyronor (nuv) / Euthyrox (janata) Adult dose : Initial starting dose: 25-50 mcg/day, with gradual increments in dose at 6-8 week intervals, as needed. For patients older than 50 years or for patients under 50 years of age with underlying cardiac disease: 1.7 mcg/kg/day. Pediatrics dose : Age Recommended 0 – 3 months : 10-15 mcg/kg/day 3 – 6 months : 8-10 mcg/kg/day 6 – 12 months : 6-8 mcg/kg/day 1 – 5 years : 5-6 mcg/kg/day 6 – 12 years : 4-5 mcg/kg/day > 12 years but growth and puberty incomplete : 2-3 mcg/kg/day Growth and puberty complete : 1.7 mcg/kg/day. The dose should be adjusted based on clinical response and laboratory parameters.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Sub-Clinical Hypothyroidism (SCH) Subclinical hypothyroidism (SCH) is mostly asymptomatic with serum FT4 is in normal range or low normal and serum TSH (4.5 and 10 mIU/L or may also be higher) is mildly elevated. Indications of treatment of Subclinical hypothyroidism : • TSH >10 mIU/ml. • TSH 5-10 mIU/ml in association with goiter or, positive Anti-TPO (Anti-thyroid peroxidase) antibody or both. • Typical features of hypothyroidism. • Menstrual irregularity, infertility or recurrent pregnancy loss. Management of Subclinical hypothyroidism If treatment is indicated (if above conditions) : • Levothyroxine 25-50 μ gm/day • TSH measured at 4-6 weeks later and levothyroxine dose adjusted. • Target TSH 0.3-3 mIU/ml • Once TSH level stable, annual thyroid function test is done. Subclinical hypothyroidism is more common during pregnancy than overt hypothyroidism. Most people with subclinical hypothyroidism in pregnancy won’t require treatment postpartum (after pregnancy). If treatment not indicated : • Annual follow up both clinical and FT4 and TSH.

treatment.title: Advise with Levothyroxine (LT4)
treatment.description: Advise for patients on Levothyroxine (LT4) : • Visit your doctor as scheduled follow up or in any new problem. • Stick to a schedule. When you take your medication at a consistent time each day, it becomes a part of your routine. • If you miss a dose, take it as soon as possible. However, if it is almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose and go back to your regular dosing schedule. • Drugs e.g. Antacid, Calcium, Iron decrease Levothyroxine absorption, so should be taken four hours after. In pregnancy better to take LT4 at morning (before meal), calcium at noon (after meal) & iron at night (after meal). • Treatment target for TSH is less than 5 mU/l, but if a lady wants to conceive, her target is less than 2.5 (to be on safe side). Reference ranges of TSH in 1st trimester: 0.1-3.0 mIU/L, in 2nd trimester: 0.2-4.0 mIU/L and in 3rd trimester: 0.3-4.0 mIU/L. • So never to stop thyroxine drug if you become pregnant, even the dose needs to be increase by 25%. So must consult to your doctor. Miscarriage & fetal cretinism are fatal complications. • Tell your doctor if any rapid or irregular heart rate or any unusual symptoms such as excessive sweating, sleeplessness, changes in weight, menstrual irregularities. • Adverse effects of drug / LT4 overdose therapy : symptoms and signs of hyperthyroidism – Palpation, tremor, insomnia, restlessness, excessive warmth / sweating. As such please contact with your doctor immediately, you may need reduction of your ongoing LT4 drug dose.

treatment.title: CASE – 1
treatment.description: A 16 years old student came with fatigue, anemia & menorrhagia. Adv : USG of W/A (normal study) ↓ Adv : CBC c̄ PBF, S. TSH, FT4 • FT4 : 0.52 (Normal level 0.8 – 1.8) • TSH : 103 (Normal level 0.5 – 5) ↓ ⦸ Tab. Levothyroxine / Thyrox (ren) / Thyrin (sq) / Thynor (skf) / Thyronor (nuv) 50 μ g, 1+0+0 (30 min before meal) ---- 5 days, Then, 2+0+0 (30 min before meal) ---- continue ↓ Advice follow up after 6 weeks with – ● S. TSH, ● FT4 & ● Anti thyroid antibodies. In this case anti thyroid antibodies (TPOAb) is important, as here TSH is sky high. It is not done routinely as it is costly (3500/-). ↓ If Antithyroid antibodies +ve, then diagnosis is – Autoimmune hypothyroidism (Most likely Hashimoto's thyroiditis) This condition is an indication of lifelong LT4 drug. If antibodies are once positive, usually never become negative. So follow up investigation anti-thyroid antibodies should not be done frequently. ↓ Adjust dose of LT4 drug at F/U after 6 weeks to maintain, FT4 level within 0.8 – 1.8 (1-1.5 if pg) & TSH level within 0.5 – 5 ( ↓ Levothyroxine is adjusted & stabilized, then FT4 and TSH monitored once every 6-12 months. Refer to endocrinologist if not euthyroid.

treatment.title: CASE – 2
treatment.description: A 32 years old lady came with (primary) infertility. Access clinical/physical conditions & advice investigation of both husband (semen analysis) & wife (please see “infertility” portion, USG of W/A or TVS, S. TSH, FT4, U/R/M/E, as per need). ↓ • FT4 : 0.96 (Normal level 0.89-1.76) • TSH : 6.3 (Normal level 0.5-5) [Treat other condition, if any] ↓ Diagnosis : Subclinical hypothyroidism (SCH), ⦸ Tab. Thyroxine 25 mcg / Thyrox (ren) / Thyrin (sq) / Leroid (drg) / Thyronor (nuv) 25 mcg, [Low dose is required in SCH], 1 + 0 + 0 (30 minutes before breakfast) ----- continue ↓ Follow up after 6 weeks with FT4, TSH, Pregnant (risky) with TSH >2.5 mU/L should be evaluated for TPOAb / ant-thyroid antibodies. Target TSH is below 2.5 mIU/L & FT4 (1-1.5) ↓ Following delivery, thyroid function testing should be performed after 6 weeks (post-partum) and dose adjusted. Most people with subclinical hypothyroidism in pregnancy won’t require treatment postpartum (after pregnancy). ► Approach of subclinical hypothyroidism where LT4 therapy in pregnancy is recommended : ● TPOAb (+ve) positive women with TSH 4 to 10 mU/L ● TPOAb (-ve) negative women with a TSH > 10 mU/L

treatment.title: CASE – 3
treatment.description: A service holder on Tab. Thyrox 50 mcg 1+0+0 (B/M), came to F/U with complaints of sweating & insomnia. F/U investigation : TSH & FT4. ↓ • FT4 : 1.85 (Normally 0.89-1.76) • TSH : 0.22 (Normally 0.5-5) ↓ Thyrox over dose symptoms includes excessive sweating, sleeplessness, changes in weight (loss), menstrual irregularities. ↓ ► Tab. Thyrox 25 mcg ( ↓ dose), 1+0+0 ( ½ hr B/M) ----- continue, F/U after 6 weeks with S. TSH & FT4 report.

treatment.title: CASE – 4
treatment.description: A lady is on Tab. Thyrox 50 mcg, 2+0+0 (30 min B/M), came to F/U with feeling of cold & sleepiness & other mild symptoms. F/U investigation : TSH & FT4. ↓ • FT4 : 1.58 (Normally 0.89-1.76) • TSH : 19 (Normally 0.5-5) ↓ FT4 within normal limit but TSH ↑↑ ↓ Poorly compliant patients, Thyrox is taken in excess for a few days prior to F/U or clinic visit, resulting high/normal FT4, but TSH is high in such cases. ↓ ► Tab. Thyrox 50 mcg, 2+0 +0 (30 minutes before breakfast) --- continue previous dose regularly. ► Counseling for compliance (take drug in time). ► F/U after 6 weeks with S. TSH & FT4 report from a well equipped diagnostic or nearest nuclear medicine (INMAS).

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Myxoedema coma Medical emergency & refer to specialist / hospitalization/ ICU. Mortality rate of myxoedema coma is about 20%. Features for suspicion : Elderly patient with H/O hypothyroidism, thyroid surgery, radio iodine therapy, presents with marked hypothermia & altered consciousness, low temperature ± convulsion. Investigation : Take blood sample for S. FT4, S. TSH, S. Cortisol before starting treatment. As per need – RBS, CBC, S. Creatinine, S. Electrolytes. Treatment : Should be started before getting the lab reports. 1. NG tube feeding. 2. O 2 inhalation sos. 3. Slow rewarming-blanket. 4. Identify and treat precipitating illness. 5. Broad spectrum antibiotics. 6. Hydrocortisone 50-100 mg IV or IM 8 – 6 hourly. 7. Correct Hyponatraemia (water restriction), Hypoglycemia (IV glucose), Hypotension (cautious IV fluid, avoid fluid overload). 8. Definite Tx : Intravenous injection of 20 mcg liothyronine, followed by further injections of 20 mcg 3 times daily until there is sustained clinical improvement. If patient recover then after 48-72 hours, it is usually possible to switch patients to oral levothyroxine in a dose of 50 mcg daily. Or, Thyroxine 300-400 μ gm by NG tube as loading dose followed by 50-100 μ gm daily.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Myxedema coma Adv. : ▸ TSH, FT4, FT3 ▸ Cortisol level ▸ CBC with ESR ▸ RBS ▸ Blood for C/S, if infection is suspected. Tx, ✔ Bed rest. ✔ Change posture 2 hourly if unconscious. ✔ Rewarming with blanket. ✔ Diet: NG feeding, if unable to eat. ✔ O 2 inhalation [If cyanosed] stat and sos. ✔ Inj. Liothyronine 20 μ gm, IV stat and TDS. After 48-72 hours : ✔ Tab. Levothyroxine / Thyrox (ren) / Thyrin (sq) / Thyronor (nuv) 50 microgram (mcg), 1+0+0 (in early morning empty stomach) If infection is suspected : ✔ Inj. Co-amoxiclav 1.2gm / Tyclav (bex) / Moxaclav (sq), 1 vial IV stat and TDS. If secondary to hypothalamic or pituitary disease : ✔ Inj. Hydrocortisone 100mg/2ml / Intasone (incp), 1 vial IV stat and TDS. ✔ Inj. Omeprazole 40mg / Omenix (incp) / Ometid (ops), 1 vial IV stat and BD.

treatment.title: Hyperthyroidism and Thyrotoxicosis
treatment.description: Hyperthyroidism and Thyrotoxicosis • Hyperthyroidism : Hyperactivity of thyroid gland. Excess hormones comes from thyroid. • Thyrotoxicosis : Clinical syndrome or manifestation that results when tissues are exposed to high levels of circulating thyroid hormones. Thyrotoxicosis may be due to hyperthyroidism also extrathyroidal source of thyroid hormones eg. Struma ovarii or excessive ingestion of thyroid hormone. So, hyperthyroidism is a cause of thyrotoxicosis.

treatment.title: Features & Inv
treatment.description: Thyrotoxicosis It is increased thyroid hormones in blood with clinical symptoms Causes of Thyrotoxicosis : • Grave's disease (diffuse enlargement) • Toxic multinodular goiter (multiple nodules) • Solitary toxic adenoma (single adenoma) • Sub-acute thyroiditis (Throat pain) Features of malignant thyroid nodules : • Age 70 years • Male sex • H/O head or neck irradiation • Hoarseness, dysphagia or dyspnea •Thyroid Nodule, rapidly growing, firm or hard, irregular, fixed to underlying structures • Cervical lymphadenopathy • USG guided FNAC if size >1 cm (but suspect malignancy if nodules Patient complaints in thyrotoxicosis (hyperthyroidism) – ● Significant weight loss, ● Palpitation, ● Heat intolerance, ● Insomnia / Sleeplessness, ● Excessive sweating, ● Hyper-defecation (chronic diarrhea), ● Loss of appetite (in old age) ● Amenorrhea (Menorrhagia in hypothyroid) ● Irritability, emotional lability. On examination : ● Pulse – tachycardia (may be irregular if atrial fibrillation, AF), ● Tremor – present in out stretched hand, lid retraction, lid lag, exophthalmos and thyroid gland may be palpable. D/D : Biochemical evidence is must for exclude anxiety neurosis (AN), Postmenopausal syndrome, Iron deficiency anemia (IDA) etc. Transient sweating, palpitation of DM patient can be due to hypoglycemia. Grave's Disease consists of one or more of the followings - • Thyrotoxicosis • Diffuse goiter • Ophthalmopathy / Exophthalmos • Pretibial myxedema • Toxic multinodular goiter : Usually in older patients with longstanding euthyroid multinodular goiter. Physical examination reveal MNG, that may be quite large. Investigation – ✔ S. TSH, FT3, FT4 : FT3 & FT4 will be raised and S. TSH will be low. ✔ USG of thyroid gland : It is useful to detect nodule/tumor & its size. ✔ Thyroid scan (thyroid scintigraphy) & Radioisotope scan. Thyroid scan determines function & morphology of thyroid gland. Others – TSH receptor antibodies (TRAb), elevated in Graves’ disease and Radioactive iodine uptake (RAIU). In almost all thyrotoxicosis radioiodine uptake will be increased but in thyroiditis uptake will be reduced. Thyroid scan is preferred than RAIU test, as it gives more information & less radioactivity to patient.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Management : ● Tablet Carbimazole 40 – 60 mg daily (usually 45 mg/day), when patient become euthyroid (Normal FT4 & FT3, but TSH may not in normal limit) then 5-20 mg/day (usually 10 mg/day) for 9-18 months. If relapse occurs then surgery (subtotal thyroidectomy) or radio-iodine ablation. May also use in lower dose as per need. ● Beta blocker – propanolol 20 – 40 mg daily, gradually taper after patient becomes euthyroid to control symptoms due to sympathetic over activity. Beta-blockers should not be used for long-term treatment of thyrotoxicosis but are extremely useful in the short term, while patients are awaiting hospital consultation or following therapy. Verapamil & Diltiazem may be used as an alternative to β -blockers, e.g. in patients with asthma. Tab. Diltiazem 30 mg / Cardizem (drg) / Cardil (ibn) 30 mg, 1+1+1 ----- 6 weeks. May also use selective β -blockers in HTN or post MI patients. ● Subjective improvement occurs within 10-14 days. Patient biochemically euthyroid at 3-4 weeks. At this time carbimazole should be given at a dose of 5-20 mg daily for 12- 15 months. Tapper the dose & discontinue cabizol if TSH is normal at that time span. Treatment Mode Indication Prognosis Anti-thyroid drugs (Carbizol) Young patients ( More than 50% relapse within 2 years of stopping drug Radio-iodine therapy ● Patients >40 yrs age ● Recurrence following Antithyroid drugs Hypothyroidism >80% by 6 months Surgery / Sub-total thyroidectomy ● Large goiter ● Compressive symptoms ● Non compliant with Antithyroid drugs ● Hypothyroidism (25%) ● Hypo-parathyroidism (1%) Patient must be in euthyroid state before surgery/iodine therapy.

treatment.title: Model Prescription
treatment.description: Treatment : ► Anti-thyroid drugs : ⦸ Tab. Carbimazole 5 mg / Carbizol (sq) / Carbiroid (white horse) , 3 + 3 + 3 ---- 6 – 12 weeks ► β -blocker : (For palpitation) ⦸ Tab. Propranolol 10 mg or 40 mg / Propranol (ops) / Indever (aci) 10 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 ----- 6 weeks [T. Indever 40 mg, ½+½+½ or 1 + 1 + 1 can be given.] ± ► Anxiolytic & sedatives : ⦸ Tab. Clonazepam 0.5 mg / Disopan (incp) / Clonzy (phar) / Rivotril (rad) 0.5 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 2 – 6 weeks ► Advice to patient : • If sore throat or fever develops, should stop the drug and contact with physician. • CBC should be done immediately & absolute neutrophil count

treatment.title: Graves opthalmopathy
treatment.description: ► If Graves opthalmopathy : The majority of patients require no treatment other than reassurance. ● Stop smoking ● Use side shield attached spectacle. ● Methylcellulose eye drop & gel. Ophthalmic Solution Carboxy-methyl-cellulose 1% / Drylief (aris) / Aquafresh Liquigel (pop) / Tearfresh Liquigel (gen), Instill 1 drop in the affected eye(s) 4 times a day for 7 – 45 days / as per need. ● Refer non responsive cases & if visual deformity. May need prednisolone (short term 7-10 days, 1 mg/ kg body weight) and sometimes orbital radiotherapy. There is also an increasing trend to use alternative immunosuppressive therapies, such as rituximab and cyclosporin. Toxic multinodular goiter : Refer to ENT / surgery after being euthyroid.

treatment.title: Gestational Transient Thyrotoxicosis & Post-partum thyroiditis
treatment.description: Gestational Transient Thyrotoxicosis Usually associated with multiple pregnancies and hyperemesis gravidarum. Transient and usually does not require antithyroid drug treatment. Limited to first half of pregnancy due to higher serum hCG concentration which stimulate of thyroid gland. Treatment includes supportive therapy, management of dehydration ( Anti-emetic : Acliz Plus/Dicliz Plus, ORS). Antithyroid drug are not recommended in gestational transient thyrotoxicosis. ► Post-partum thyroiditis : TSH, FT3, FT4 should be tested 4-6 weeks post-partum in those with history of thyroid disease, goiter or other autoimmune disease including type 1 diabetes, in those known to have positive antithyroid peroxidase antibodies, or when there is clinical suspicion of thyroid dysfunction.

treatment.title: Thyrotoxicosis in pregnancy
treatment.description: Thyrotoxicosis in pregnancy (Refer to endocrinologist for starting carbizol in pg) Graves’ disease : The most common cause of sustained thyrotoxicosis in pregnancy. In this case treatment : Anti-thyroid drugs : Propylthiouracil should be used in the first trimester, with carbimazole substituted in the second and third trimesters. Surgery if poor compliance (usually done in 2 nd trimester). Less available anti-thyroid drugs but is indicated in pregnancy : Tab. Propyl-thio-uracil 50 mg / PTU (ren) / Thiocil (incp). But carbimazole is also safe in pregnancy. may be given throughout pregnancy to a thyrotoxic patient, but the smallest effective dose should be used. Carbimazole is widely using in pregnancy. Radio active iodine therapy is contra-indicated in pg.

treatment.title: Atrial fibrillation (AF) in thyrotoxicosis
treatment.description: Atrial fibrillation (AF) in thyrotoxicosis AF occurs in about 10% of patients with thyrotoxicosis. Chance of AF increases with age and subclinical thyrotoxicosis. Treatment of AF in thyrotoxic patient : 1. Beta-blocker : Characteristically, the ventricular rate is little influenced by digoxin but responds to the addition of a beta-blocker. 2. Anti-coagulants : Thromboembolic vascular complications are particularly common in thyrotoxic atrial fibrillation so that anticoagulation is required. Once thyroid hormone and TSH concentrations have been returned to normal, atrial fibrillation will spontaneously revert to sinus rhythm in about 50% of patients but cardioversion may be required in the remainder. Any arrhythmia (VT, SVT, AF) must treat by a specialist & needs hospitalization. Refer the cases of AF to endocrinologist / cardiologist.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Acute inflammatory disorder of thyroid gland most likely due to viral infection (mumps, coxsackievirus, adenovirus). This case should refer to specialists. Symptoms : • Throat pain, radiate to angle of jaw, ear and worse by swallowing. • Fever. • Symptoms of thyrotoxicosis : Palpitation, sweating. Signs : • Thyroid gland is moderately enlarged, tender. • Signs of thyrotoxicosis : tachycardia, tremor. Investigations : • ESR raised, sometime >100 mm in first hour. • FT4, FT3 : ↑ , TSH : ↓ (initial toxic phase). • RAI uptake : Low (initial toxic phase). Treatment : • NSAIDs / Anti-pyretic. • Short course of steroid (Prednisolone 20-40mg/day for 7-10 days). • Beta blocker. • No need of anti-thyroid drugs. • Prognosis : 10% develop Hypothyroidism in future.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Subacute Hyperthyroidism Serum TSH is low despite normal/upper end of normal range of serum FT4 and FT3. Indication of treatment : TSH • All individuals >65 years of age. • Post menopausal osteoporosis. • Cardiac risk factor : AF, Recurrent cardiac arrhythmia. • Patient with hyperthyroid symptoms. Treatment options include radioactive iodine therapy, antithyroid medications and surgery.

treatment.title: Factitious Thyrotoxicosis
treatment.description: Ingestion of thyroid hormonal drug preparations usually for weight loss. May be given by medicine store owners, homeopathic/natural cocktail drugs for weight loss & showing high energy, sleeplessness. May be with high caffeine methamphetamine or yaba derivatives. Clue for Diagnosis : • Clinical symptoms and biochemical features of thyrotoxicosis but goiter and eye sign absent. • High T4 : T3 Ratio (70 : 1), in conventional thyrotoxicosis – 30 : 1. • Low uptake in RAIU or Thyroid scan. Treatment : Counselling & stop thyroid hormone intake (may stop immediately).

treatment.title: Sick thyroid syndrome
treatment.description: In a patient with systemic illness who does not have any clinical evidence of thyroid disease. No drug treatment needed in other medical emergency conditions – AMI, Acute abdomen, Pneumonia, Acute CVD etc. Typically present with low serum TSH, low FT, and normal or low FT4. Thyroid function test should be repeated after (1-2 months) recovery.

treatment.title: Case – 1
treatment.description: A 36 years old house wife present with – weight loss, palpitation, sweating, frequent motion. ↓ Physical examination & O/E : Tachycardia, warm and sweaty hands, exophthalmos, diffuse goiter. Inv given. ↓ • FT4 : 4.12 (0.89-1.76) • FT3 : 9.2 (2.3-4.5) • TSH : 0.001 (0.5-5) • USG of thyroid: Diffuse enlargement. ↓ Diagnosis is Grave's disease. • Tab. Carbizol 5 mg, 3+3 +3 • Tab. Indever 40 mg, ½ + ½ + ½ • Follow up after 6 weeks, If in euthyroid state, Tab. Carbizol 5 mg 1-4 tab/daily. Advice : Consult if any problem (sore throat, fever etc).

treatment.title: Case -2
treatment.description: A 40 years new patient is on Tab. Carbizol 5 mg 3+3+3, came to with sore throat and fever. ↓ CBC : ● Hb%: 12.9 gm/dl (12-16 gm/dl) ● RBC count: 4.7 M/ml (4.5-6.5 M/ml) ● Total WBC count: 2700/cu mm (4000-11000/cu mm) ● Neutrophil : 40% (40-75% of TC) ● Lymphocytes: 55% (20-45% of TC) ● Total neutrophil count : 1080/ml (2500-7000/ml) ● Platelet count : 2.1 lac/cu mm (1.5-4.5 lac/cu mm) ↓ Diagnosis : Carbimazole induced agranulocytosis. Refer to endocrinologist. • Immediate cessation of anti-thyroid drug • Broad spectrum antibiotic. • Granulocyte colony stimulating factor : Inj. Filgrastim 30 mcg / Filastin (incp) S/C for 3 days • Anti-thyroid drug never should be tried. • Treated with Radio-active iodine ± surgery.

treatment.title: Case – 3
treatment.description: A 50 year man of diagnosed thyrotoxicosis on Tab. Carbizol, 5 mg 3+3+3, Misses F/U after 6 weeks & comes after 3 months despite advise was after 6 weeks, but he continues 9 tabs daily for these 3 months. ↓ • FT4: 1.12 (0.89-1.76) • FT3: 3.2 (2.3-4.5) • TSH : 20.1 (0.5-5) ↓ • Tapper down & continue. Tab. Carbimazole / Carbizol (sq) 5 mg, 1 + 0 + 1. • Follow up after 6 weeks.

treatment.title: Case-4
treatment.description: A 26 years student present with fever for 12 days, sore throat, Palpitation, sweating. On exam: Tachycardia, Tender thyroid. ↓ • ESR : 86 mm in 1st hour • FT4 : 4.12 (0.89-1.76) • FT3 : 9.2 (2.3-4.5) • TSH : 0.001 (0.5-5) ↓ • USG : Mild enlargement of thyroid. • Thyroid scan: Low uptake of isotope. ↓ Diagnosis : Subacute thyroiditis . (Subacute thyroiditis may be responsible for 15-20% of patients presenting with thyrotoxicosis and 10% of patients presenting with hypothyroidism.) ↓ • Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan 20 mg, 1+0+0 (A/M) (7 days) • Tab. Propanolol / Indever 40 mg, ½ + ½ + ½ (6 weeks). • Anti-pyretic or analgesics for 7 days. • F/U investigations after 3 months.

treatment.title: Case-5
treatment.description: A 65 years old man admitted in ICU with pneumonia / CCU with AMI (ant.), thyroid function test revealed - • FT4 : 1.12 (0.89-1.76) • FT3 : 2.1 (2.3-4.5) • TSH : 0.08 (0.5-5) ↓ Diagnosis : Sick thyroid syndrome. ↓ In acute medical emergency treat the life threatening conditions (AMI/CVD/Pneumonia). No anti-thyroid drug should be used. Advise thyroid function test to repeated after recovery (1 month).

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Thyroid storm / Thyrotoxic crisis (Medical emergency, must refer & hospitalize/ICU) (Mortality rate is 20-30%) Thyrotoxic crisis is most commonly precipitated by infection in a patient with previously unrecognized or inadequately treated thyrotoxicosis, shortly after thyroidectomy in an ill-prepared patient or within a few days of Radio-iodine therapy, when acute radiation damage may lead to a transient rise in serum thyroid hormone levels. Patient must be in euthyroid state before surgery / iodine therapy. The most prominent signs are – fever, agitation, delirium, tachycardia or atrial fibrillation and in the older patient, cardiac failure. Investigation – CBC (neutrophilic leucocytosis if any infection), TSH, T3, T4 (TSH will be low or undetectable, T3, T4 raised). Treatment of Thyroid Storm : • Rehydration with normal saline. • Fluid, O2, cooling blanket, paracetamol • Broad spectrum antibiotic – If any features of infection. • Propanolol 80-40 mg 6 hourly orally or 1-5 mg IV 6 hourly. • Carbimazole 40-60 mg/day orally / NG tube or rectally • Dexamethasone 2mg 6 hourly IV for 48 hours followed by tapering or, Sodium ipodate 500 mg orally ± amiodarone.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Thyrotoxic Crisis Adv. : ▸ TSH, FT4, FT3 ▸ CBC with ESR ▸ Blood for C/S, if infection is suspected ▸ ECG Tx, ✔ Diet : NG feeding, if vomiting. ✔ Change posture 2 hourly if unconscious. ✔ O ₂ inhalation stat and sos. ✔ Inf. Normalin (pop) or Hartsol (libra) 1L IV running – till normalization hydration status. ✔ Inj. Hydrocortisone 100mg/2ml / Cotson (ops), 1 vial IV stat and QDS ✔ Inj. Omeprazole 40mg / Seclo (sq), 1 vial IV stat and BD. ✔ Tab. Carbimazole / Carbizol (sq) / Carbiroid (white horse) 5mg, 3 + 3 + 3, or, ✔ Tab. Propylthiouracil 50 mg / Thiocil (incp) / PTU (ren), ½ + ½ + ½ or, 1 + 1 + 1. ✔ Lugol's iodine 5 drops with 1 glass of water stat (4 hours after carbimazole) and TDS. ✔ Tab. Propanolol / Propranol (ops) / Indever (aci) 40 mg, 2 + 2 + 2 + 2. If infection suspected : ✔ Inj. Co-amoxiclav 1.2gm / Fimoxyclav (syn) / Clacido (hc), 1 vial IV stat and TDS. Rx on Discharge : Same as hyperthyroid : • Tab. Carbimazole / Carbizol (sq) 5 mg, 3+3+3 – continue.

treatment.title: Adrenal / Supra-renal Hormones
treatment.description: Anatomical zone Hormones Effects Adrenal cortex is divided into three distinct layers : Outer - zona glomerulosa Mineralocorticoids (Aldosterone) Mainly ± BP Middle - zona fasciculata Glucocorticoids (Cortisol) Mainly ± Blood sugar Inner - zona Reticularis Sex hormones – androgens, estrogens Sex differentiation Adrenal medulla Catecholamines (epinephrine and norepinephrine) Sympathetic activity

treatment.title: Cushing syndrome
treatment.description: Cushing syndrome occurs due to excessive secretion of glucocorticosteroids (cortisol). This can be – ● ACTH dependent (pituitary adenoma -cushing disease, ectopic ACTH secretion, ACTH therapy) and ● ACTH non-dependent (iatrogenic- chronic glucocorticoid therapy, adrenal adenoma, adrenal carcinoma). In practice common cause of cushing syndrome is iatrogenic (excess intake of prednisolone, dexamethasone etc. Patient with arthritis & breathlessness take steroid tablets as per his/her willingness or in form of homeopathic / natural drug cocktail). Features of iatrogenic cushing syndrome : History of taking prednisolone, dexamethasone, unknown homeopathic/natural vitamin tablets/powder/liquids for long duration. ● Patient usually obese (moon face, abdominal obesity with striae, buffalo neck). ● Skin-thin, echymoses, bruise may be present. ● Pedal or scrotal edema without renal/cardiac impairment. ● Signs of infection may be present (fever, cough, dysuria) due to immuno-compression. ● Patient may have features of hypertension and DM. ● For confirmation-dexamethasone suppression test. (Usually not done to diagnose iatrogenic cushing syndrome). Treatment : 1. Gradual withdrawal of steroid. 2. Calcium supplementation (to prevent osteoporosis). 3. PPI, as steroid & Ca ++ are GI irritative. 4. Treatment of con-current infections. 5. Treatment of HTN, DM & hyperlipidemia (if any). 6. Taper the steroid : e.g., prednisolone, do reduction is 5mg/week until dose is 5mg/day. ► Steroid withdrawal protocol : decrease ( ½ tab) 5-10 mg in every 5-7 days. Available : Tab. Prednisolone 10 or 20 mg / Cortan (incp) / Predimax (acm) / Precodil (ops) / Deltasone (ren) / Deltapred (zis) [2 mg & 5 mg tablets are less / not available]. If complicated, refer to endocrinologist.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Addison's Disease (If complicated, refer to endocrinologist) Addison's disease occurs due to less / no secretion of glucocorticosteroids (cortisol). Adrenal insufficiency if less or no secretion of both glucocorticosteroids (cortisol – ↓ blood glucose) and mineralocorticoids (aldosterone – ↓ BP). Features : Usually clinical features of the cause of Addison's disease predominates (like features of tuberculosis, haemochromatosis (secondary to haemolytic anaemia) etc). Patient may complaint nausea, vomiting, vertigo and weight loss. On examination – blood pressure usually low, postural hypotension present, pigmentation present in the oral cavity, palmar creases, knuckles etc. S. electrolytes – hyponatraemia ( ↓ Na + ), blood glucose (RBS) – usually low. Suspect, if S. Cortisol at morning sample 8 to 9 AM – Low Confirm by short ACTH stimulation test. Follow up with – Pulse, BP, S. electrolytes, ± RBS.

treatment.title: Management & Advice
treatment.description: Management 1. Diet- normal 2. Hydrocortisone 15 mg at morning & 5 mg at 3 pm (lifelong). 3. Fludrocortisone 0.05-0.1 mg daily. 4. Treatment of the underlying cause e.g. antitubecular drugs, treatment of haemocromatosis etc. Hydrocortisone Prednisolone and Methylprednisolone Tab. Cotson (ops) 10 mg Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan (incp) / Precodil (ops)/Deltasone(ren) 10/20 mg Less available Tab. Methylprednisolone / Solupred (zis) / Methipred (gen) 4 / 8 / 16 mg Rapid onset of action Needs transformation Short acting Intermediate acting ↑↑↑ Blood sugar & ↑ BP ↑↑ Blood sugar & ↑↑ BP Advice : 1. If fever- double the dose of the steroid. 2. In severe vomiting – Injection hydrocortisone should be given (if patient cannot swallow tablet). 3. Minor surgery – Inj. hydrocortisone 100 mg IM with premedication. 4. Major surgery – Inj. hydrocortisone 100 mg IM 6 hourly until patient able to take oral tablet. 5. Patient should carry steroid card & bracelet. Patients should be encouraged to buy a bracelet and have it engraved with the diagnosis, current treatment and a reference number for a central database. Patients should be given a hydrocortisone emergency pack and trained in the self-administration of hydrocortisone 100 mg IM; they should be advised to take the pack on holidays/trips abroad.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Adrenal crisis & Adrenal insufficiency (If complicated, refer to endocrinologist) Features : ► Diagnosed case of Addison's disease. ► Sudden loss of consciousness/disorientation. ► Severe hypotension, hyponatraemia, hyperkalaemia and in some instances, hypoglycaemia and hypercalcaemia. ► The crisis is often precipitated by sudden withdrawal of steroids, stress, fever, insomnia, fasting, oral contraceptive pills. They also precipitates adrenal insufficiency. Adrenal insufficiency is suspected when all of a sudden ↓ BP (hypotension) & ↓ Blood glucose (hypoglycemia), even in whom non diabetic & no other cause. Hospitalization & specialist opinion is must, as it is a medical emergency. Management : 1. NPO TFO. 2. O2 inhalation sos. 3. Infusion normal saline to normalize blood pressure. 4. For hypoglycaemia – Infusion 10 % DA 1L IV @ 20 drops/min. 5. Inj. hydrocortisone 100 mg IV stat, and 100 mg 4 times daily for first 12-24 hours. Continue parenteral hydrocortisone (50-100 mg IM 4 times daily) until patient is well enough for reliable oral therapy. 6. Hyperkalaemia : should respond to volume replacement but occasionally requires specific therapy. 7. Treatment of the underlying cause e.g., antibiotic for infection.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Phaeochromocytoma (Rare, Refer to endocrinologist / surgeon) Pheochromocytoma is a rare tumor of the adrenal medulla, these neuroendocrine tumors typically release massive amounts of catecholamines. Classically presents with paroxysms of hypertension, panic attack and adrenergic symptoms including classic triad of episodic headache, sweating, and tachycardia. Management 1. Definitive treatment is surgery. 2. Preparation of patient before surgery minimum for 6 weeks. 3. Control of hypertension by α blocker phenoxybenzamine 10-20 mg orally 6-8 hourly. If tachycardia occurs add β -blocker – propanolol or labetalol. 4. During surgery – sodium nitroprusside & short acting α - antagonist phentolamine are used to control hypertension. 5. Post operative period hypotension should be managed by –fluid & noradrenaline infusion.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Prolactinoma (Refer to endocrinologist) Prolactinoma is the most common type (30%) of pituitary tumor (adenoma) that produces a hormone. A prolactinoma isn't life-threatening. But it can cause vision difficulties, infertility and other problems. Management 1. Dopamine agonist (bromocryptine 1st line drug) 2. Surgery (2nd line treatment).

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Hypopituitarism (Refer to endocrinologist) Management : 1. Glucocorticoid replacement (prednisolone). 2. Thyroxine 100-150 mcg daily (before this, steroid must be given). 3. Sex hormone in men of any age & in female 4. GH S/C injection in children & adolescent. 5. Treatment of the underlying cause.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Acromegaly (Refer to endocrinologist) Management 1. Surgery – 1st line treatment. 2. Radiotherapy- 2nd line treatment, if acromegaly persists after surgery. 3. Medical treatment – If acromegaly persists after surgery. a) Somatostatin analogue (cctreotide, lanreotide) slow releasing inj. every few weeks interval. b) Bromocryptine-if associated with prolactin excess. c) GH receptor antagonist Pegvisomant indicated in whom GH & IGF1 fail to suppress sufficiently by Somatostatin analogue.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Diabetes insipidus (Rare, refer to endocrinologist) Diabetes is of 2 types : Insipidus & Mellitus. Again diabetes mellitus is of 2 types – Type I & Type II. Diabetes insipidus (DI) : is the syndrome that results when there is a vasopressin deficiency (central diabetes insipidus) or when the kidneys fail to respond to the hormone (nephro-genic diabetes insipidus). Diabetes insipidus is caused by problems / lacks in vasopressin (AVP) / antidiuretic hormone (ADH). ADH is produced by the hypothalamus and stored in the pituitary gland until needed. Cranial DI : Desmopressin nasal spray 5 pgm in morning & 10 pgm at night. In sick patient IM injection. Nephrogenic DI : 1. Bendroflumethazide 5-10 mg/day. 2. Amiloride 5-10 mg/day. 3. Indomethacin 15 mg 8 hourly.

treatment.title: General Measures in Toxicology
treatment.description: All forms of acute poisoning has two aspects : (1) General management for all poisoning. (2) Specific management & antidotes. General Measures in Toxicology Decontamination : If patient presented in stable condition or If patient is resuscitated completely and the duration of poisoning is within one hour of incidence, then gastric decontamination should be tried if indicated. Then a syndromic approach should be followed to diagnose poisoning cases. ► If the poison is Swallowed : A sip of water to wash out their mouth. ► If the poison is Inhaled : Immediately get the person to fresh air. Avoid breathing fumes. ► If the poison enters the Eye : Flood the eye with saline or cold water from a running tap or jug. ► If the poison contacts the Skin : Remove contaminated clothing. Wash with soap and water and rinse well. ► If Injection / Bites and Stings – Do not excise and apply suction to bites/stings/constricting band. Airway, Breathing, Circulation and level of consciousness, should be assessed in all patients with acute poisoning. Continue resuscitation → If patient’s airway could not be secured, If GCS remain below 8 after resuscitation, If circulation not improved and breathing difficulty persist → Refer the patient to secondary or tertiary care center. Airway : • Awake and talking – likely to have intact airway. • If unconscious – immediately turn him / her to maintain recovery position. Management of airway : • Recovery position (see below). • Removal of dentures, debris. • Mouth gag. • Nasopharyngeal (OP) suction. Breathing : • Ineffective respiration (tachypnoea, grunting, wheezing, use of accessory muscles etc.) • Cyanosis. Management of breathing : • O 2 : 2 ‐ 3 L/min. • Positioning : upright/propped up. • Salbutamol nebulization and repeat if necessary. Circulation : • If SBP 100/min; patient is in shock. • Cool, pale, clammy, peripheral cyanosis altered conscious state. Management of shock : • O 2 : 2 ‐ 3 L/min. • Positioning : elevate foot. • In Shock : Inj. Normal Saline (0.9%) ‐ 20 ml/Kg bolus and repeat with same dose if necessary. • Reassess (CXR, ECG). • In cardiac arrest ‐ follow the standard procedure (BLS & ATLS). Level of Consciousness (LOC) : GCS score of less than 8, if – • Not obeying commands. • Not speaking. • Not eye opening. Management of LOC : • Recovery position. • O 2 inhalation. • Care of skin, eyes, oral cavity. • IV nutrition. Recovery Position : The head, neck and body should be in a straight line so that the tongue will not block the throat, and vomit or saliva can come out of the mouth. Turn the patient's face towards you, and tilt it back, with the jaw jutting forward. Take the patient's upper arm and place the hand under the face Place the patient's other arm across the chest. Now position the upper leg so that the bent knee rests on the ground and supports the patient's body and lower leg keep straight (as figure).

treatment.title: Gastric lavage
treatment.description: Gastric lavage indications • Gastric lavage should not be routine unless a patient has ingested a potentially life ‐ threatening amount of a poison. • Can be undertaken within 60 minutes of ingestion. • Contraindicated if the airway remains compromised. • Don’t use wide bored tube for stomach wash; rather use a nasogastric (NG) tube for aspiration with plain water. • A lavage is contraindicated following ingestion of strong caustics or corrosives, non­toxic agents and volatile hydrocarbons (e.g. kerosene, turpene etc.). The ‘golden hour’ concept of gut decontamination : (1) Get informed consent from the patient or a relative. (2) For drowsy patients, protect the airway in recovery position and raised foot end 20 cm. (3) Lubricate and pass down the NG tube orally after measuring the distance; a mouth gag can be used to prevent patient from biting on the tube. (4) Confirm the correct placement of the tube in stomach by aspirating fluid out or injecting air with the 50 mL syringe, while auscultating over the epigastrium. (5) Siphon off the gastric contents before lavage and save a specimen for toxicological analysis if necessary. (6) Small aliquots of lavage fluid should be repeatedly introduced by gravity ; the recommended volume of each aliquot is 200 mL for adults and 10 mL/kg for children. (7) The lavage can be stopped when a total of 3 L of lavage fluid is used and the return is clear. (8) Collect the first 50 ml of lavage fluid for toxicological analysis or medico legal purpose. Activated charcoal : May be considered if a patient has ingested a potentially toxic amount of a poison (known to be absorbed by charcoal) up to 1 hour of ingestion. Do in Poisoning : 1. Remove all contaminated cloths and wash the contaminated body with plain water particularly in OP poisoning. 2. Always maintain recovery position in unconscious patient with any poisoning. 3. Always follow National Poisoning Management Guideline in any confusion. Don't in Poisoning : 1. Do not give stomach wash / gastric lavage with wide bore tube. 2. No emesis and No forced dieresis (except salicylate and amphetamine) 3. No whole bowel irrigation exception iron, lead, zinc and packets of illicit drugs. 4. Never use Steroid or Antibiotic (except in aspiration pneumonia) 5. Do not give cathartics (laxative). 6. Never give anything by mouth to an unconscious patient.

treatment.title: Common poisoning
treatment.description: Common poisoning ▸ OPC poisoning ▸ Sedative poisoning or Drug overdose (diazepam, midazolam, amitriptyline, paracetamol etc.) or Mixed drug poisoning. ▸ Stupefying / Street / Unknown Poisoning. ▸ Corrosive poisoning (dettol, herpic, savlon etc.) N.B.: All the features of a particular poison may not be present in a particular case. Feature cluster Likely Poison Smell of OPC, bronchorrhea, bradycardia, hypotension, incontinence of urine & stool, miosis and hyper salivation. Organophosphorus, carbamate insecticides, some kind of mushroom, nerve agents. Coma, hypotension, hypothermia, shallow breathing, hypotonia, hyporeflexia, fast pulse, watering eyes, yawning, cramps, hallucinations, restlessness, diarrhea. Barbiturates, benzodiazepines (travel related) and alcohol combinations, severe TCA poisoning. Small pupils, slow breathing, unconsciousness, weak pulse low temperature, vomiting. Opioids Nausea, vomiting, tinnitus, deafness, sweating, hyperventilation, vasodilatation, metabolic acidosis. Salicylate (aspirin) Restlessness, agitation, mydriasis, anxiety, tremor, tachycardia, convulsion, arrhythmias, hyperthermia, hallucinations, fits, sweating, flushed skin, confusion. Sympathomimetic drugs, Amphetamine, Cocaine, Bronchodilator (Theophylline) dry, hot skin, fever, thirst, dry mouth, large pupils, fast pulse, difficulty in passing urine, hallucinations, fits, shallow breathing unconsciousness Atropine, Amitriptyline, Antihistamines, Datura stramonium, some kinds of Mushrooms Blindness (usually with other features). Quinine, Methanol Sample of used offending agents or preferably container of pesticide / agents as measure of identification is very important in clinical setting. All patients and their attendants should be repeatedly encouraged to bring the sample to the health facility for diagnosis and management.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: It is the most common poisoning found at different level of hospitals of Bangladesh. C / C : H/O of insecticide (কীটনাশক) intake __ hours ago. May have characteristic (kerosene) odour of OPC from mouth. O / E : • Hypotension (BP : ↓ ). • Bradycardia (HR: ↓ ). • Pin pointed pupil (PPP) / Miosis. • Lung : Bronchorrhea / Rhonchi / Creps. • Incontinence of Urine & Stool • ↑ Secretions : ↑ Salivation, ↑ Sweating. ► Sign & Symptoms (S/S) / Clinical Features (C/F) : A) Muscarinic Manifestation : - Increase bronchial secretion (Ronchi / Creps), Broncho-constriction, Dyspnonea, Cyanosis, Pulmonary oedema. - Bradycardia (Decrease HR) - Hypotension (Low BP) - Pupil : Miosis (Pin pointed pupil) - Sweating - Lacrimation - Urination - Defecation - Gastrointestinal discomfort - Emesis [Mnemonic, SLUDGE] B) Nicotinic manifestation : - muscular paralysis, - fasciculation, cramps, - areflexia, - weakness. C) CNS manifestation : - Convulsion, - Drowsiness, - Coma, - Slurred speech, - Confusion, - Tremor, etc. Most cases are asymptomatic. As most people intake a least amount of OPC or not a sip at all, only to seek attention & frighten the family members & force to agree with his/her demand. ► Patient with pure suicidal motive, may not give history of intake of agricultural poisons. Especially in a case of young female with – ● Severe respiratory distress (creps), ● Sweating, ● Pin pointed pupil, ● Pulse & HR – bradycardia, ● Severe BP – low. This type of incomplete given history case may be diagnosed as aspiration pneumonia and miss doses of atropine & gastric lavage, which is fatal, after OPC absorption. Bilateral lung creps + Pin pointed pupil (PPP) + ↓↓ BP & ↓ HR ↓ OPC Poisoning

treatment.title: SUSPECTED OPC POISONING
treatment.description: SUSPECTED OPC POISONING C/F : H/O possible OPC ingestion. O/E : No clear-cut symptom and sign of OPC poisoning. - BP normal. - Pupil : Normal in size & PLR brisk. - Lung : Clear / mild rhonchi. Management : Note pulse rate & give inj. Atropine 0.6 mg / ml, 1.2 mg or 2 amp IV stat. Again note pulse after 5-10 mins, If pulse increases by 20 – 25% (20 – 25 b/m) or more or pupils get dilated, it is possibly not OPC poisoning. A case of suspected or unknown poisoning ↑ HR by 20% & dilated pupil by this IV atropine confirms OPC poisoning. But usually unknown street poisons are sedative & stupefying in nature, not OPC.

treatment.title: Intensive I/V Atropine Therapy & Atropinization
treatment.description: First step of Rx : Intensive I/V Atropine Therapy If : - BP hypotensive. - Pupil : Pin pointed. - Lung : Severe rhonchi / Creps. Rx, ● Inj. Atropine 0.6 mg / ml, 2 – 5 amp I/V stat, then repeat after 5 minutes and double the dose every time, till signs of atropinization. Time Inj. 8:00 PM 5 amp 8:05 PM 10 amp 8:10 PM 20 amp 8:15 PM 40 amp so on ..…. if needed [ ► Atropine doubling dose should be within 15 min as it metabolizes quickly. No need to wait for 5 – 15 min. Duty doctor (DOD) should just stand beside the patient & DOD himself / herself will monitor intensive atropine therapy, even doubling IV dose within 2 min. High dose will make patient restless (not to panic, duration of action is only 30 – 60 min) but low dose will kill the patient. Repeat more & more ampules of atropine even within two (02) min if ↓ BP, Coarse lung creps, PPP to save the life. Rare but a patient may need 150 – 200 ampule of atropine to achieve atropinization.] ► Signs of Atropinization / Target end­ points for therapy : - Clear chest on auscultation with no wheeze. - Pupils no longer pin point or dilated. - Heart rate > 80 beats/min. - Blood pressure > 110 / 80 mmHg. - Raised temperature / Atropine fever. - Dry axilla. ► Observation : Every 15 – 30 minutes. If Atropinization is lost e.g., Recurrence of bronchospasm or bradycardia – 1. Bolus dose of atropine. 2. Increase the infusion rate. Calculation of “Maintenance dose” of Inj. Atropine : Once atropinized, calculate the total amount required and give 10 ‐ 20% of it per hour through infusion (NS) as maintenance. Example : If atropinization occurs after 35 ampoules. Then total amount required (5 + 10 + 20) ≈ 35 ampoules. 10 – 20% of 35 amp = 3.5 to 7 ampoules per hour ≈ 3 × 8 = 24 amp per liter of fluid, as here 3L fluid in 24 hours, so 1L of fluid to be infused within 8 hours. Then order should be, ● Inf. N/S 1L + Inf. 5% DNS 1L + Inf. 5% DA 1L + 24 amp Inj. Atropine in each liter of fluid, I/V stat @ 30 d/m. Then decrease the atropine dose 20% every 4 hourly, if sign of atropinization persists (stable BP & dryness). Follow-up of OPC : At this stage review the parameters at 30 mins interval for 3 hours, followed by hourly for 6 hours and 3 – 6 hourly for next 24 – 48 hours. If Atropinization is lost at any point, e.g.; bronchospasm, bradycardia, low SBP, sweating etc. Then start giving bolus dose again until they disappear and add 20% of bolus requirement to infusion per hour. Maintenance dose (for at least 2 days) is necessary to prevent intermediate syndrome.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ OPC Poisoning Tx, ● NPO-TFO. ● If external contamination, then, decontamination with water & remove contaminated clothing. ● Gastric lavage (If patient presents within 1 hour, not effective if done after 4 hours). ● O2 inhalation stat & sos. ● O-P suction sos. ● Change posture 2 hourly if unconscious. ● Continuous catheterization. ● Please monitor vital signs. Loading IV atropine : ✔ I/V Inj. Atropine till atropinization (here, suppose 35 amp). Maintenance IV atropine : [10-20% dose of atropinization per hour, then decrease the atropine dose 20% every 4 hourly, if sign of atropinization persists (stable BP & dryness)]. ✔ Inf. N/S 1L + Inf. 5% DNS 1L + Inf. 5% DA 1Lwith 24 amp Inj. Atropine in each liter of fluid, I/V stat @ 30 d/m. ✔ Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g / Traxon (ops) / Trizon (acm) / Oricef (hc) / Dicephin (drg) 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ✔ Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Losectil (skf) / Omep (ops) / Seclo (sq) / PPI (acm), 1 vial I/V stat & BD.

treatment.title: Antidote in OPC poisoning
treatment.description: Antidotes Dosing for OPC Poisoning : A. Atropine : 1. Inj. Atropine 2 amp (1.2 mg) IV stat followed by doubling of doses every 5 mins interval until full Atropinization occurs. 2. Check vital signs at 10 mins intervals. 3. Once atropinized, calculate the total amount required and give 10-20% of calculated dose/ hr with IV N/S as maintenance dose. 4. Check vital signs at 30 mins intervals for 3 hours, hourly for 6 hours 3-6 hourly for next 24-48 hours. 5. If Atropinizations is lost: (e.g.; bronchospasm, bradycardia etc.) start giving bolus dose again until they disappear and add 20% of bolus requirement to infusion/ hours. B. Pralidoxime : (Pralidoxime used only in cases of moderate – severe OPC poisoning cases) : 1. Inj. Pralidoxime chloride 1000mg/ml / Pradox (bacn) / Pralidox (incp) / Pralidot (globe) / PAM-A (mankind, india) : ● Adult : 1-2 g/IV stat over 15-30 mins. ● Child : 25-50 mg/kg IV over 15-30 mins. 2. If no improvement: Repeat after 1 hour. THEN, 8-12 hourly until improvement. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 115]

treatment.title: Atropine toxicity
treatment.description: Atropine toxicity : Note : Restlessness is the common phenomenon as atropine dilates pupil (photophobia), ↑ temperature / fever, dry mouth-tongue etc. Features : 1. Hot as hare. 2. Red as beet. 3. Dry as bone. 4. Blind as bat. 5. Mad as hen. Treatment : ● Reassurance the patient & attendance that this fever, dryness & restlessness due to atropine drip but this saline is necessary for further management / treatment. ● Temporary stop the atropine drip / infusion & check every 30 min if to see until features of toxicity have settled. ● Sips of water though patient is NPO for dry mouth. ● Tepid sponging. ● When they settle : start atropine at 70-80% of previous rate. ● Inj. Diazepam 10mg/5ml, 1 – 2 amp IV / IM stat. ● Or, if Patient is restless followings may be used (instead of Diazepam), ▸ Inj. Haloperidol 5mg/ Halopid (incp)/ Perol (ambee), ½ amp I/V stat & sos / 12 hourly. Or, 1 amp I/M stat & sos / 12 hourly. & ▸ Inj. Procyclidine 10 mg / Cyclid (incp) / Perkinil (sq), ½ amp I/V stat & sos / 12 hourly. Or, 1 amp I/M stat & sos / 12 hourly. ● If any sign of respiratory failure : Refer / Call to ICU for artificial ventilation.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: If patient develops pulmonary oedema : 1. High flow O ₂ inhalation stat. 2. Inj. Frusemide 20mg/2ml, 2-amp IV stat. ► Inj. Pralidoxime : (Pralidoxime used only in cases of moderate – severe OPC poisoning cases) : add according to senior. Now a days it is of little use. ✔ Inj. Pralidoxime 1g/ml / / Pradox (bacn) / Pralidox (incp) / Pralidot (globe) / PAM-A (mankind, india), Loading dose / Bolus [30mg/Kg] : 2 amp in 100 ml N/S I/V stat over 30 min. Then, Maintenance dose [8-10 mg/kg/hr] : 4 amp Inj. PAM + Inf. N/S or 5% DNS 500 ml, I/V stat @ 10 d/m through other channel. Or, Practically : ✔ Inj. Pralidoxime chloride 1000 mg/ml / Pradox (bacn) / Pralidox (incp) / Pralidot (globe) / PAM-A (mankind, india), 1 vial in 100 ml N/S IV slowly stat & TDS. ► Principles & Discussion of Stepwise Management : ● Removal of contaminated clothing & External decontamination with soap water / normal saline (N/S) (if contaminated). And also avoid self-contamination or contamination with other personnel. ● Gastric Lavage / suction / Stomach wash by 2% KMnO4 as early as possible within 1-2 hours (Usually done by EMO) must done. Practically gastric lavage done even after 4 hours as punishment therapy, but after 2 hours it has a little therapeutic value. ● Tab. Ultracarbon / Activated charcoal 2+2+2 (within 1 - 2 hours), helps to delay absorption. ► Starting of I/V Atropine : ● Continuous Catheterization. (From before first dose of atropine till maintenance dose of atropine with fluid for next 3-5 days. It is done to prevent urinary retention & neurogenic bladder.) ● Diet : NPO TFO (if patient is drowsy / unconscious) for 1 to 2 days. No need of NPO or to prolong NPO, if patient is stable. ● If respiratory distress : O2 inhalation stat & sos. Also nebulization with salbutamol & Ipratropium solution may be given. ● O-P (Oro-pharyngeal) / N-G (Naso-grastic) suction to remove bronchial secretion. ● Antibiotics is needed as to prevent suspected aspiration pneumonia, or as stomach wash injurious procedure. ● ‘Maintenance dose’ of Inj. Atropine is to prevent intermediate syndrome & to prevent remerging OPC signs. ● Stomach wash with potassium permanganate or tannic acid solution should be given. Or, insertion of NG tube the wash with normal saline may be tried. ● Psychiatrist evaluation for suicidal tendency (after recovery) is advocated. ► Intermediate syndrome (IMS) : About 20% of patients with OPC poisoning may develop weakness spreading rapidly from ocular to head and neck after 1-4 days after exposure and may last 2–3 weeks. There is no specific treatment but supportive care, including maintenance of airway and ventilation, should be provided if necessary. ► Polyneuropathy Organophosphate induced delayed polyneuropathy (OPIDN) : It is s a rare complication that usually occurs 2–3 weeks after acute exposure. Muscle cramps followed by numbness and paresthesia proceeding to flaccid paresthesia. Usually affecting lower limb first then upper limbs. Paralysis of the lower limbs is associated with foot drop and a high-stepping gait, progressing to paraplegia. Paralysis of the arms leads to wrist drop. There is no specific therapy for OPIDN. Regular physiotherapy may limit deformity caused by muscle-wasting. Recovery is often incomplete and may be limited to the hands and feet, although substantial functional recovery after 1–2 years may occur, especially in younger patients.

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Discharge for OPC poisoning Ø Cap. Cefixime / Emixef (incp) / Afix (aris) / Cef-3 (sq) / Triocim (bex) 200 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ......... ৭ দিন Ø Tab. Escitalopram 5mg / Esita (hc) / Nexcital (uni) / Oxapro (sq) / Nexito (sun) 5 mg, ১ + ০ + ০ ........ ২ মাস Ø Tab. Clonazepam / Rivotril (rad) / Pase 0.5 mg (ops), ০ + ০ + ১ ......... ১৫ দিন ± Ø Tab. Propantheline 15 mg/ Prokind (bacn)/ Thelin (incp), ১ + ১ + ১ ......... ১৫ – ৩০ দিন ± Ø Tab. Rabeprazole / Rabe (aris) / Acifix (bex) 20 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার আধাঘান্টা পূর্বে) ...... ১ মাস উপদেশ : নিয়মিত ঔষুধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে আত্মহত্যা প্রবণতার জন্য সব কাগজপাতিসহ মানসিক বহি:বিভাগে দেখা করবেন। OPC Poisoning in Children ◙ OPC poisoning is usually accidental in children so start with only 1 amp of atropine, if need more then 2, 4, 8 etc. ◙ Rx on discharge (children) : ☼ Syrup. Domperidone 5mg/5ml / Motigut (sq) / Deflux (bex) / Don-A (acm) / Omidon (incp) / Domiren (ren), [wt/12] TSF TDS [30 min B/M] [0.2-0.4 mg/Kg/Dose 4-8 hrly] Or, ☼ Syrup. Famotidine 40mg/5ml / Famomax (bex) / Famotack (sq) / Famogen (gen) / Famotid (drg) / Famodin (acm), [wt/20] TSF BD [30 min B/M] [1-5mg/kg/24 hr PO]

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: C / C : H/O of datura spp. intake __ hours ago. O / E : Tachycardia, Tachypnoea / Increased respiratory rate, Pupil : Dilated. Tx, Δ Datura Poisoning ● Stomach wash with KMnO 4 or potassium permanganate / Gastric lavage done in emergency (within 1 hr). ● O 2 inhalation stat (4-6L/min) & sos. ● NPO-TFO. ● Inf. N/S 1 L + 5% DNS 1L + Inf. 5% DA 1L, I/V stat @ 30 d/m. ● Inj. Ceftriaxone / Arixon (bex) 1 g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD ● Inj. Any PPI / Maxpro (ren) 40 mg, 1 vial / amp I/V stat & BD. ● Supp. P/C / Ace (sq) / Renova (ops) 500 mg, 1 stick P/R sos, if temp > 101°F. ● Tepid sponging. ● Continuous catheterization. ± ● Inj. Neostigmine/Neostig (pop) 0.5mg/ml, 1 or, 2 amp S/C stat.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ▸ Specific antidotes (Physostigmine or, Phostigmin/Neostigmine or, pilocarpine), if complaints – - Dryness of mouth. - Dysphagia. - Dilated pupil. - Diplopia / double vision. - Dry hot skin & face is flushed. - Delirium & hallucination. - Drowsiness. - Drunkenness. - Ludicrous movement. Then add – ± ● Inj. Neostigmine 0.5 mg/ml / Neostig (pop) / Neos-R (ren), 1 or, 2 amp S/C stat. Or, ● Inj. Physostigmine 0.5 mg, 1 or, 2 amp S/C stat. Single dose of any these antidote is enough to antagonize atropine like activity of datura. But can be repeat after 2 hours. ▸ If ↑ BP, then add – ● Inj. Furosemide / Lasix (if BP stable), 2 amp I/V stat. ▸ Antibiotic needed only if stomach wash & catheterization done. ▸ If delirium/restlessness diazepam/phenobarbitone may be used. ▸ If full stomach no forceful emesis should be tried. Repeated purgation & force diuresis are not recommended.

treatment.title: Poisoning by Drugs
treatment.description: Psychotropic Drugs : A chemical substance that changes brain function and results in alterations in : • Perception, • Mood, • Consciousness. These include : • Psychiatric medications : o Anxiolytics o Antidepressants o Mood Stabilizers o Anti-Psychotics • Recreational Substances : o Alcohol o Cocaine o LSD o Cannabis • Drugs for pain management : o Opiate Narcotics: Morphine, Codeine o Pregabalin o Anesthetics Management of Psychotropic Drugs Algorithm (Except Alcohol) : Suspected Poisoning with Psychotropic Drug / Substance. ↓ Clinical Assessment : History : • Drug; • Timing; • Dose. Examination : • Vital Signs (Pulse, BP, Respiration); • GCS; • Pupils; • Cyanosis. ↓ Identify Drug & suspect as per signs & symptoms. ↓ Barbiturates, BDZ and Alcohol Combinations, Severe TCA poisoning : • Coma • Shallow breathing • Hypotension • Tachyardia • Hypothermia • Yawning • Hypotonia, hypo ‐ reflexia • Watering eyes • Cramps • Hallucinations, restlessness • Diarrhea Or, Opioids Poisoning : • Small pupils/ Miosis • Unconsciousness • Hypothermia • Slow Breathing • Weak Pulse • Vomiting Or, Sympathomimetic drugs (Amphetamine, Cocaine) : • Restlessness, Agitation • Tremor • Confusion, Anxiety • Tachycardia, Arrhythmias • Hallucinations, Fits • Mydriasis • Convulsion • Hyperthermia • Sweating, Flushed Skin ↓ Initial Interventions : • Assessment of vital signs & cABCDE resuscitation. • Open IV access. • O 2 inhalation. • Consider single dose activated charcoal administration (if available) / Gastric Lavage (if arrival within 1 hr of ingestion). ↓ Initial investigations : • CBC with ESR, • RBS, • ECG, • SGPT, • S. Creatinine. Special conditions : o Coma : Electrolytes, ABG. o Dyspnoea : X-Ray chest P/A. o Unknown Poisoning with Critical Patient : Toxicological analysis of urine and/or blood. ↓ If drug Identified → Specific Antidote (if available). If drug is not identified, but patient stable → Observe 6 hours & discharge . If drug is not identified & patient is not stable → Refer to higher center.

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Sedative Poisoning Benzodiazepines (BDZs) are the most commonly used sedative agents in Bangladesh and hence commonly abused. Elderly individuals and very young persons are more susceptible to the toxic effect than people in other age groups. Common BDZ Drugs : Diazepam, Alprazolam, Clonazepam, Bromazepam, Midazolam. C / C : • H/O of sedative drugs intake __ hours ago. • H/O any co-ingested drugs. • Dizziness / Confusion / Drowsiness / blurred vision. • Anxiety / unresponsiveness / anxiety / agitation. O/E : • BP : Normal. • Lung : Clear • Patient : Well-alert. • Pupillary Light Reflex (PLR) : Brisk/sluggish. • Nystagmus ±. History : History should include the time, dose, and intent of the overdose. Determine if co-ingestants are present and the duration of benzodiazepine use. Symptoms include – Dizziness, Confusion, Drowsiness, Blurred vision, Unresponsiveness, Anxiety and Agitation. Examination : The physical examination should focus on the patient's vital signs and cardiorespiratory and neurologic function. The frequently found physical signs are – nystagmus, hallucinations, slurred speech, ataxia, coma, hypotonia, weakness, altered mental status, impairment of cognition, amnesia, paradoxical agitation, respiratory depression and hypotension. Management : • Supportive care: most patients improve with only supportive care. • Antidote : Only in severe CNS depression (non-responding) with severe respiratory depression or ineffective respiration. • Referral to a higher center : ○ Ineffective respiration/ respiratory depression. ○ Concomitant TCA overdose. ○ CNS depression/ coma persists after 24 hours. ○ If antidote is not available when needed. Benzodiazepine (BDZ) Poisoning management algorithm : Ingestion of BDZ → Hospitalization. ↓ cABCDE Management : • Quick Assesssment of GCS. • Check Vital Signs : Focus on Respiration & Temperature. • O 2 inhalation via Nasal Cannula (Maintain SpO 2 ≥ 93%). • Open IV Access. • Gastric Lavage [Use N/S or Fresh Water] NOT recommended unless : o Presenting within 1 hour, AND o Lethal dose ingestion. If, Severe Respiratory Depression → Refer to higher center for Invasive Ventilation Support. Or, If, No/Mild Respiratory Depression → Investigation : • RBS. • ECG (Especially if TCA ingestion is also present/suspected). • S. Electrolytes. • Urine for BDZ level (if possible). Watch for worsening the symptoms & signs : Respiratory Depression & CNS Depression ► Respiratory / Persistent CNS Depression : • Antidote of BDZ : Flumazenil. ► Hypotension / Shock : • IV fluids (NaCl / Lactated Ringer’s) & Vasoactive Agents (Dopamine, Dobutamine, Noradrenaline) ► Others : • Correct Electrolytes; • Correct Hypoglycemia. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 114]

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Benzodiazepine poisoning Or, Δ Sedatives Poisoning ● Gastric lavage within 1 hours. ● O 2 inhalation sos (if respiratory distress). ● Diet : Normal with plenty of tea, coffee (if pt is stable). Or, NPO (if pt is unconscious / in deep sedation for only 24 hours.) ✔ Inf. N/S 1 L + 5% DNS 1L + Inf. 5% DA 1L (if kept NPO), I/V stat @ 30 d/m. ✔ Inj. Furosemide / Lasix (syn) (if BP stable), 2 amp I/V stat & 1 amp I/V BD (8am & 4pm) ✔ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Axon (aris) / Triject (skf) 1g (if invasive stomach wash done), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ✔ Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / PPI (acm) / Ometid (ops), 1 vial I/V stat & BD (slowly).

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Antidote : Flumazenil : Indication : Respiratory depression (ineffective breathing) or Persistent CNS Depression (unconscious) : Patient regains consciousness within 15 to 30 seconds after injection of flumazenil. If the patient becomes unconscious again : Flumazenil can be given by IV infusion 100- 400 µg/hr adjusted according to level of arousal. Adult Dose : 0.1-0.2 mg IV per/min to a total dose of 1mg at one time or 3mg in 1h; infusion rates of 0.1 mg/min decrease disconcerting rapid arousal. Pediatric Dose : 0.002 – 0.02 mg/kg IV per/min. Contraindications of Flumazenil : • Known Hypersensitivity. • Serious concomitant TCA over dose. • BDZ being used to control potentially life-threatening condition (e.g., ↑ ICP, Status Epilepticus). • Chronic BDZ use. Flumazenil is BDZs receptor specific antagonist, it has no role in barbiturate (Barbit), ethanol or other sedative-hypnotic. It should not use where sedative-hypnotic poisoning except benzodiazepines (BDZs) or, mixed drug poisoning c̄ tri-cyclic antidepressants (TCA), Patient with seizure or, other cardiac pathology. ► Antidote (Flumazenil) is rarely needed. Benzodiazepines (BDZs) are safe drug with high therapeutic index (TI). Antidote of benzodiazepines (BDZs) : ● Inj. Flumazenil 0.5 mg/5ml / Anexate (roche) [3056/-], 0.2 mg I/V stat over 1 min, If no improvement then, 1 vial (0.5 mg) I/V stat, i f no improvement then, 1 vial (0.5 mg) I/V stat, up to 6-10 vial (3-5 mg). ► Tab. Ultracarbon / Activated charcoal 2+2+2 (within 1 - 2 hours), helps in delayed absorption. Activated charcoal effectively absorb almost all drugs & poison except metal (Fe, Na, K, Li) & alcohol. ● Tab. Ultracarbon (Activated charcoal), 50 gm with water stat [If present within 1 hour or symptomatic patient with unknown time of ingestion]. ► Actually benzodiazepines (BDZs) are of high therapeutic index (TI), so safe drugs. Attempting suicide with benzodiazepines (BDZs) is a silly task. A patient with normal kidney & liver functions need no medical help upto 10-20 tabs / 1-2 strip(s) ingestion. Gastric wash usually not needed upto 10 tabs / 1 strip ingestion even more but washing procedure is routinely done as punishment therapy, so give an antibiotic. But dose to toxic effect may vary. Think of immediate gastric wash & antidote (Flumazenil) is signs are alarming – - Depressed respiration. - PLR : Sluggish & mid dilated. - ± Disoriented, drowsy or in deep sedation.

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Discharge for sedative poisoning Ø Cap. Cefixime / Triocim (bex) / T-cef (drg) 200 mg, (if stomach wash given) ১ + ০ + ১ ..... ৭ দিন Ø Tab. Escitalopram 5mg / Esita (hc) / Nexcital (uni) / Oxapro (sq) / Nexito (sun) 10 mg, ½ + ০ + ০ .... ৬ দিন, তারপরে, ১ + ০ + ০ .... ২ মাস Ø Tab. Clonazepam / Disopan (incp) / Clonatril (hc) 0.5 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ .... ১৫ দিন ± Ø Tab. Rabeprazole / Rabe (aris) / Acifix (bex) 20 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার আধাঘান্টা পূর্বে) .... ১ মাস উপদেশ : নিয়মিত ঔষুধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে আত্মহত্যা প্রবণতার জন্য সব কাগজপাতিসহ মানসিক বহি:বিভাগে দেখা করবেন।

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: C / C : • H/O of overdose of TCA / Amitriptyline ___ hours ago. • Dry mouth, sedation, hallucination. • Urinary retention. • Jerky movements, drowsiness. • Hyperreflexia with extensor plantar, coma and seizures. • Improvement can be expected within 24 hour. • Indication hospital observation : Ingestion >5mg/kg. • Life threatening : 10 – 20mg/kg. O / E : - BP : Normal / Hypotension. - Pupil : Dilated. - Temperature: Hypothermia. - Lung : Clear / Depressed respiration. - ECG : Arrhythmia. - Hyper-reflexia – Extensor planter. - ± Coma, seizure.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ TCA Poisoning ● NPO-TFO except activated charcoal. ● Gastric lavage (up to 24 hrs if TCAs). ● Activated Charcoal / Cap. Laxicarbon (thn), 1 + 1 + 1 / Tab. Ultracarbon (merck), 2 + 2 + 2. ● Maintain airway, breathing and circulation. ● High flow O ₂ inhalation sos. ● Monitor vital sign. ● Inf. NS 1L + 5% DNS + H/S 1L, I/V stat @ 10 drops/min. ● Inj. Esomeprazole 40 mg / Sergel (hc), 1 vial I/V stat & BD (slowly). ● Continuous catheterization.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Investigation – ¤ Serum electrolytes with Bicarbonate : Hypokalemia and acidosis. ¤ ECG : QT prolongation. ► Gastric wash / elimination may be useful even after 4 hours (up to 24 hours of ingestion), because tricyclics (TCAs) shows slow gastric emptying & re-secretion from stomach. ► ECG monitoring is a must & discharge only if ECG normal for 24 hours. If arrhythmia in ECG (VT, SVT, AF etc.) then manage that according to cardiology portion (Tachy-arrhythmia). ► Arrhythmias are common if there is acidosis present, target is arterial blood pH 7.5 (slightly alkaline). ► If arrhythmias occur with no acidosis and fail to respond to treatment with amiodarone or phenytoin, bicarbonate (25-50 mL 8.4% IV) may still be useful with in 20 minute. ► If VT compromising cardiac output, lidocaine 50-100 mg IV should be given. ► Indications of sodium bicarbonate : 1. Wide QRS complex 2. Prolonged QT interval 3. Arrhythmia 4. Acidosis ► If acidosis then add – ● Inj. NaHCO3 / Sodibicarb 7.5% 100 ml, 1 – 2 vial (25 – 50 ml) I/V stat. ► If BP stable may also be used – Inj. Frusemide 20mg/2ml / Lasix, 2 amp I/V stat then, 1 amp BD (8 am & 4 pm). But force diuresis in any poisoning is discouraged. ► Usually Amitriptyline 300 mg / day, (12 tabs of 25 mg) or (30 tabs of 10 mg) need no treatment. But we start conservative treatment even if patient admitted with no sign. ► Incase of mixed drug poisoning avoid antibiotics (but may if catheterization & stomach wash). In case of β -blocker (atenolol, propranolol etc.) main risk is hypoglycemia, so RBS is a must. 5% DA or, 25% Dextrose, if hypoglycemic. ► If hypotension : Inf. NS 1 – 2 L running → if refractory to IV fluid: Inj. Norepinephrine. ► If seizure occurs : Self-limiting. If prolonged or recurrent – Inj. Diazepam 10mg/5ml 1 amp IV stat.

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Discharge for sedative poisoning Ø Cap. Cefixime / Cefim-3 (aci) / Fix-A (acm) 200 mg, (if stomach wash given) ১ + ০ + ১ ..... ৭ দিন Ø Tab. Escitalopram 5mg / Esita (hc) / Nexcital (uni) / Oxapro (sq) / Nexito (sun) 10 mg, ½ + ০ + ০ ..... ৬ দিন, তারপরে, ১ + ০ + ০ ..... ২ মাস Ø Tab. Clonazepam 0.5 mg / Leptic (acm) / Rivo (ori) / Xetril (bex) 0.5 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ..... ১৫ দিন ± Ø Tab. Rabeprazole / Rabeca (sq) / Xorel (ibn) 20 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার আধাঘান্টা পূর্বে) ..... ১ মাস উপদেশ : নিয়মিত ঔষুধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে আত্মহত্যা প্রবণতার জন্য সব কাগজপাতিসহ মানসিক বহি:বিভাগে দেখা করবেন।

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: C / C : H/O of ___ tab of P/C ___ hours ago. O / E : - BP : Normal / Hypotension. - Pupillary Light Reflex (PLR) : Sluggish / Brisk. - Lung : Clear / Respiration depressed. Fatal dose of P/C is 12 g at a time or 150mg/kg body weight. Phase I (0 – 24 hours) : Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, arrhythmia, bradycardia. ↓ Phase II (24 – 72 hours) : Right upper abdominal pain. ↓ Phase III (72 – 96 hours) : Bleeding manifestation, portal HTN, hepatic encephalopathy, oliguria. ↓ Phase IV (96 hours – 2 weeks) : Hepatic failure and death or compete resolution.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Paracetamol Poisoning ● NPO-TFO except medication. ● Gastric lavage (within 1 hour of ingestion). ● O2 inhalation stat & sos. ● Inf. N/S 1L + 5% DNS 1L + Inf. H/S 1L, I/V stat @ 30 d/m. ● Tab. Ultracarbon / Activated charcoal, 2 + 2 + 2 ● Inj. Esomeprazole 40 mg / Sergel (hc), 1 vial I/V stat & BD (slowly). ● Monitor vital signs.
