treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Investigations : Repeat all investigations daily. 1. SGPT, SGOT 2. PT 3. Serum bilirubin 4. Serum electrolytes 5. Serum creatinine 6. RBS 7. CBC ► Follow up : 1. Blood sugar. 2. Urine output. 3. Any bleeding manifestation. 4. Development of jaundice. ► Indications of NAC : 1. Single overdose >12g or 150mg/kg. 2. High risk patient taking >75mg/kg. 3. Presenting more than 8 hours after single overdose and symptomatic e.g., right hypochondriac pain, vomiting. 4. A person ingested >7.5-10 g/day over a short period of time. 5. Child ingested >90 mg/kg. ► Special antidote : N-acetylcysteine (NAC)or Methionine. Dose : [Ref - Management of Acute poisoning : A practical manual, rcmc.com.bd] • N­-acetylcysteine : I/V: Adult: 150 mg/kg IV in 200 ml of 5% dextrose over 15 minutes, followed by 50 mg/kg IV in 500 ml dextrose over 4 hours, followed by 100 mg/kg IV in 1000 ml 5% dextrose over 16 hours. • Children (less than 20 kg) : 150 mg/kg IV in 3 ml/kg of 5% dextrose over 15 minutes, followed by 50 mg/kg IV in 7 ml/kg of 5% dextrose over 4 hours, followed by 100 mg/kg IV in 14 ml/kg of 5% dextrose over 16 hours. If IV unavailable, • Oral : give 140 mg/kg as a loading dose, followed 4 hours later with 70 mg/kg given every four hours for an additional 17 doses. ► Incase of prolonged prothrombin time & increased liver enzyme (SGPT), antidote N-acetylcysteine (NAC) should be given. Toxicity depends on individual, usually if more than 20 tab of P/C (500 mg) or 15 tab of Extend (665 mg). ● N-acetylcysteine (NAC) / Tab. Mucomist DT (bex) / Viscotin (uni) / Alistin EF (ops) / Tylace (sq) 600 mg, 14 tab (140 mg/kg) orally (or, dissolved) stat, then 7 tab (70mg/kg) every 4 hours orally for 3 days or 17 doses or continue upto normalize investigations / hepatotoxicity. [Advice : Diluted solution of NAC should be freshly prepared and to be taken within 1 hour. If vomiting occurs within 1 hour of ingestion repeat that dose.] Or, ● Methionine: 2.5 gm stat and 2.5 gm 4 hrly. [Total 4 doses, 10 gm] ► Tepid sponging, if raised temperature, no P/C advocated. ► In case of established hepatotoxicity : ▸ Inf. 5% DA 500ml + Inj. N-acetylcysteine (50mg/kg), I/V stat @ 10 d/m – continue till PT and liver enzymes begin to return to normal. ► If hepatic failure develops : ▸ Inj. Mannitol 20% 300 IV running stat [If cerebral edema/encephalopathy present] ▸ Inj. Konakion 6-amp IV stat [If PT in elevated] ▸ NAC : Inf. 5% DA 0.5L + Inj. NAC (50mg/kg), I/V @15 d/m and repeat until recovery. ▸ Fresh frozen plasma : 10 ml/kg 6 hourly, If active bleeding develops. ▸ Inj. Omeprazole 40mg 1 vial IV stat & BD. ► Broad spectrum antibiotic, because sepsis is common in liver failure. ▸ Inj. Ceftriaxone 1gm 1 vial IV stat & BD. ► If renal failure develops – hemodialysis.

treatment.title: Unknown Poisoning / Stupefying / Street Poisoning
treatment.description: The unknown poisoning are cases which brought to the hospital where there is no clue about the poisoning substance. The physicians are occasionally confused with the diagnosis and management. Commonly travel related poisoning using public transport is found to be the unknown poisoning but there are also cases where the actual agents could not be evaluated through history or circumstances. The Key Features of unknown poisoning : • Victims mostly brought to the hospital by police or attendant in a drowsy state. • Most of the victims had lost their valuables including money. • Fortunately most of the patients are recovered and discharged within 24 hours after admission uneventfully without any specific treatment. Refer the patient to a higher center : • Patient remain sedated or unconscious more than 24 hours. • Patient having co-morbidity. • Patient having respiratory depression. • Patient developing/worsens vital sign. Management Protocol must be followed : • Rapid assessment of the vitals. Measure Pulse, BP, Respiration and Temperature immediately. • Assess the state of consciousness by Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS). • In all cases put the patient in to lateral position with the lower leg straight and the upper leg flexed; in this position the risk of aspiration is reduced. • Clear airway passage by removal of any obstructing object, vomit or dentures, and by backward pressure on the mandible. Patient who are awake and talking – Likely to have intact airway. In obtunded patients check for gag reflex. Nursing care of the mouth and pressure areas should be instituted. • Check for Breathing by observing efforts of ventilation, respiratory rate, cyanosis and use of accessory muscle of respiration. Institute oxygen if required. • Put an Intravenous (IV) cannula but administration of IV fluids is unnecessary unless the patient has been unconscious for more than 12 hours or is hypotensive. Normal saline (0.9% NaCl) will be the fluid of choice. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 119] Accidental exposure to inhalation of unknown substance & Ingestion of suspected unknown substance. ↓ Open area/ Fresh air, give high flow oxygen. Maintain airway, breathing and circulation ↓ Gut Decontamination, if present within 1 hour. (Gastric lavage with Normal saline or fresh water) ↓ Monitoring of vitals. Watch for worsening Sign/Symptoms. Routine test for organ involvement. ↓ Follow the Toxidrome Clinically : Cholinergic : Confusion, CNS depression, weakness, salivation, lacrimation, urinary and fecal incontinence, gastrointestinal cramping, emesis, diaphoresis, muscle fasciculations, pulmonary secretion. ↓ (Tx) Supportive care + Antagonize muscarinic symptoms with IV Atropine. Stop aging of enzyme blockade with IV PAM in severe cases. Prevent and terminate seizures with Diazepam (same as OPC poisoning management) Or, Sympathomimetic : Delusions, paranoia, tachycardia (or occasional bradycardia), hypertension, hyperpyrexia, diaphoresis, piloerection, mydriasis, hyperreflexia, seizures, hypotension, dysrhythmias. ↓ (Tx) Supportive care + Monitor airway, diagnose ICH, rhabdo IV Fluid for insensible loses and volume repletion, Benzodiazepine, BP management if severe, Never give Beta Blockers. Or, Anticholinergics : Delirium with mumbling speech, tachycardia, dry-flushed skin, dilated pupils, myoclonus, slightly elevated temperature, urinary retention, and decreased bowel sounds; seizures and dysrhythmias. ↓ (Tx) Supportive care + IV Fluid to replace insensible losses, Benzodiazepine, Physostigmine : Short acting, help in uncontrollable delirium, do not use if ingestion not known. Suspected TCAS : IV NAHCO 3 . Or, Opiates : Coma, respiratory depression, miosis, hypotension, bradycardia, hypothermia, Pulmonary edema, decreased bowel sounds, hyporeflexia, seizures (occasional), watch for needle mark. ↓ (Tx) Supportive care + Competitive opioid antagonist : Naloxone until spontaneous respirations. May have to redose as opiates may act longer. Nalmefene and naltrexone if available. Or, Sedative and Hypnotics : CNS depression, lethargy, respiratory depression, bradycardia, hypotension. Midpoint pupil, Nystagmus, Hallucinations, Slurred speech, Ataxia, Coma, Hypotonia. ↓ (Tx) Supportive care + Be wary of the benzo "antidote" Flumazinil, rarely indicate. If seizures develop either because of benzo withdrawal, a co-ingestant or metabolic derangements, have to use 2 nd line agents, barbiturates, for seizure control. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 120]

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discussion
treatment.description: C / C & O / E : H/O of unknown / travel poisoning __ hours ago. • Drowsy or deep sleep. • History of poisoning during traveling or ingestion or inhalation of food or other substances by unknown person. • Pupil constricted or dilated and light reaction normal or absent. • Circulation (pulse & BP) usually normal. • Respiration : usually normal. • Management or treatment is symptomatic and supportive. Usually patient recover after 24 hours. Δ Unknown / Street / Travel Poisoning Investigation – ¤ ECG. ¤ RBS. ¤ S. Creatinine. ¤ S. Electrolytes. ● NPO TFO. ● O2 inhalation sos (4-6L/min). ● O-P suction sos. ● Inf. N/S 1L + Inf. 5% DNS 1L + Inf. H/S 1L, I/V stat @ 30 d/m ● Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Omep (aris), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ● Continuous catheterization. ● Antibiotic if catheterization is done : Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftizone (bren) 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ● Please monitor vital signs. Discussion : ► Gastric lavage usually not indicated, until clearly knowing the type & time of poisoning. ► Always try to avoid costly drug & investigation as patient attendant are not available. Treatment is only conservative & treat the condition that appears, e.g., symptomatic treatment. Opium / Opiate Poisoning : ► If coma, respiratory distress, convulsion then think of antidote IV naloxone (Unavailable in market). ● Inj. Naloxone 0.4 mg / ml, 1 ml I/V stat, repeat another 1ml after 2 min until patient awakened (up to 25 ml, highest 50 ml). Long acting Methadone may also be used as antidote.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: Aluminium Phosphide / Kari / Rice / Gas Tablet Poisoning
treatment.description: Each 3g fumigation tablet liberates 1g toxic PHOSPHINE (PH 3 ). Mortality rate is 80-100%, even in a single tablet intake [Fatal dose : 150-500mg]. Synonym wheat pill (pond toxin), so counseling of patient’s near relatives is a must. C/C : • H/O ingestion of gas tablet/rice tablet/ bullet/Kari tablet. • Abdominal pain and vomiting. O/E : • Garlic like odor from breath : +. • Pulse : Rapid thready pulse. • BP : Hypotension. • RR : Tachypnoca.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Aluminium Phosphide / Kari / Rice / Gas Tablet Poisoning
treatment.description: Δ Aluminum phosphide / Gas tablet poisoning Investigations : • ECG : Arrhythmias, ST elevation or depression. • CBC : Leucopenia. • SGPT, SGOT : Raised. • Bicarbonate level : Acidosis. Tx, ● NPO-TFO except medication. ● Change posture 2 hourly if unconscious. ● Gastric lavage. ● Inf. N/S 1L + 5% DNS 1L + Inf. H/S 1L, I/V stat @ 30 d/m. ● Inj. Esomeprazole 40mg / Nexum (sq), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ● Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g / Ceftron (sq) 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ● Inj. Hydrocortisone 100 mg/ Cotson (ops), 1 vial I/V stat & QDS. ● Inj. Magnesium Sulfate 4 gm/100 ml / Nalepsin (bex), 1 bottle IV @60 d/m stat and ½ bottle I/V @30 d/m (drops/min) TDS. ● Inj. Frusemide / Fusid (sq) / Lasix (syn), 2 amp I/V stat & BD [If SBP >90 mmHg] If patient in cardiogenic shock : Add, ● Inj. Dopamine 200mg/5ml+ 500ml N/S, I/V @32 micro drops (Or 8 drops/min) stat and continue [4-6 mcg/kg/min].

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.disease_name: Aluminium Phosphide / Kari / Rice / Gas Tablet Poisoning
treatment.description: ► Gastric lavage is the must as early as possible : Wash stomach with vegetable oil e.g., soyabean oil (try at home to vomit out the gas tablet as early as possible) or 2% sodium bicarbonate solution or small amount of potassium permanganate (KMnO 4 ) (1:10,000 or 1:5,000). is mixed with 2-3 L water until water turns pink], then, 100 gm activated charcoal through NG tube. ► Induce vomiting with soyabean oil or soap water is effective home management. ► Perform gastric lavage. Use, if available, 1 : 5000 potassium permanganate (KMnO4) solution or 2% sodium bicarbonate solution. Normal wash without olive oil / cocking oil / charcoal / KMnO4 / NaHCO3 will produce toxic phosphine gas. ► Maintain fluid balance and give sodium bi carbonate to correct acidosis. ► Correct shock with adequate fluid. If hypotension does not respond to adequate IV fluids, raise the foot end of the bed. Give dopamine. ► If still no response, give hydrocortisone 400 mg IV 4 hourly or dexamethasone 4 mg IV 4 hourly. ► Give vitamin K1 (phytomenadione) 10 mg IV daily, if prothrombin time is prolonged.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: ► Ingestion of more than 1 g of copper sulphate results in manifestation of symptoms of toxicity, lethal is 10-20g. ► Tute poisoning has non-specific signs and symptoms such as dysgeusia, abdominal pain, vomiting, muscle cramps or spasms, diarrhoea, corrosive gastroenteropathy, ink-like urine, jaundice, anaemia and seizures. Dysgeusia is a condition in which a foul, salty, rancid, or metallic taste sensation persists in the mouth. Treatment is symptomatic & supportive. Δ CuSO4 (Tute) Poisoning ● Gastric lavage (within 1 hour). ● Diet – Normal or Liquid. ± ● Inf. 5% DNS 1L, I/V stat @ 10 d/m. ● Cap. Amoxicillin / Moxin (sq) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1. ● Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Omegut (pop), 1 + 0 + 1 (B/M) ● Tab. Anti-oxidant / Rex (sq), 1 + 0 + 1. ± ● Tab. Prochlorperazine 5 mg / Vergon, 1 tab stat & sos ● Add antidote, if obvious symptoms appear. Discussion : ◙ Antidote : • D­-Penicillamine (Oral) : Adult dose : 1000 to 1500 mg/day divided every six to 12h, before meals. For Pediatrics : Initially 10 mg/kg/day gradually increase to 30 mg/kg/day divided in two or three doses as tolerated. Doses up to 100 mg/kg/day in four divided doses can be given. • BAL / Dimercaprol : Dose : 3 to 5 mg/kg/dose deep I/M every four hours for two days, every four to six hours for an additional two days, then every four to 12h for seven days. [Ref - Management of Acute poisoning : A practical manual, rcmc.com.bd]

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : ● H/O ingestion of corrosive agent ___ hours back. ● Pain and difficulty during swallowing. Δ Corrosive Poisoning Adv. : ▸ Herpic/savlon or any corrosive agent : Endoscopy of upper GI ● NPO-TFO. ● Inf. 5% DNS 1L + Inf. 5% DA 1L, I/V stat @ 20 d/m. ● Inj. Esomeprazole 40 mg / Sergel (hc), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ● Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g / Ceftron (sq) 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ● Inj. Nalbuphine 20mg/2ml / Nalbun-2 (incp), 1 amp I/M stat & sos ● Inj. Prochlorperazine 12.5mg/1ml / Vergon (ops), 1 amp I/V stat & sos, just before Inj. Nalbuphine-2. [Or, Inj. Ondansetron 8mg/4ml / Emistat (hc), 1 amp IV stat & sos instead of vergon.] ● Inj. Diazepam 10 mg/2ml / Sedil (sq), 1 amp I/M stat & sos. ► For external burn and eye injury : 1. Wash immediately with water for 20 – 30 mins. 2. For skin : Silver sulphadiazine 1% (Cream Burna), Apply locally stat and 2 to 6 times daily for first 3-5 days. 3. For eye : Eye drop Chloramphenicol (Chloram E/D) 5mg/5ml, 1 drop in each eye stat and 4 to 6 times daily. Note the area involved & type of injury if homicidal in nature for medico-legal purpose. ► Investigations : 1. ECG 2. Serum electrolytes 3. RBS 4. Serum creatinine 5. SGPT ► Induced emesis, gastric aspiration and lavage are contraindicated. ► Main treatment is NPO, followed (within 12-24 hours) by upper GI endoscopy. If stricture of oesophagus then dilation of esophagus by ENT surgeons may needed. Even no dilution or neutralization should be done. However these are practiced traditionally (Please avoid oral drugs in larger amount of ingestion) – ● Liquid Paraffin, 2 TSF stat & TDS. ● Al(OH)3 + Mg(OH)2 + Simethicon / Entacyd Plus (sq) Suspension, 2 TSF stat & TDS. ● Tab. Sucralfet 500 mg / Gastralfet, 2 + 2 + 2 + 2. ► Another antibiotic – ● Inj. Amoxicillin / Moxacil (sq) 500mg, 1 vial I/V stat & TDS. ► If abdominal pain presents (instead of Nalbuphine 20mg/2ml), ● Inj. Tiemonium Methyl-Sulphate / Algin / Norvis / Tinium / Veralgin, 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. ► Steroids has no role in corrosive poisoning, rather harmful. ► Rx of complication : Surgery : For oesophageal stricture and gastric outlet obstruction.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : • History of ingestion of savlon. • Burning sensation in throat and upper abdomen. Management : ● No stomach wash advocated. ● Nothing per oral – till further order (NPO-TFO) except medication. ● Irrigate exposed eyes with sterile cold water or saline at least for 20 minutes and continue until the patient returns to normal. ● Inj. omeprazole (40mg) 1 vial i/v slowly over 10 minutes stat and 12 hourly or once daily. ● Antibiotics (oral or IV). ● IV infusion 5% DNS 1L + H/S 1L IV @ 20 d/m stat and daily. ► Savlon / Shampoo / Bleaching powders are also chemical / corrosive poisoning. If bleeding (haematomesis) present, transfuse fresh human blood.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Conventional rat killer is (2% Zinc Phosphide) super anticoagulant, but vague “Rat killer poison” may contains organo-phosphorus compound (OPC) or, Gas tablet / Aluminium Phosphide so must see BP, Lung & Pupil to exclude OPC. If gas tablet then negative counseling is must. Inv. : • Prothrombin Time (PT) & • International normalized ratio (INR). If PT is prolonged in rat killer poisoning : Add, ● Inj. Phytomenadione or Vitamin K1 10 mg / Konakion (rad) / K MM (incp) / Dinakion (drg) 10mg/ml, 1 amp slow IV stat and OD for 5 days. Or, 1 amp I/M stat & daily until normalization of PT. ● If active bleeding present – BT / Fresh frozen plasma.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Kerosene Poisoning / Household Non-Toxic Product Poisoning
treatment.description: ► Stomach wash is contra-indicated in kerosene poisoning. Usually children present with cosmetic or other household products poisoning. Stomach wash is not advocated in any corrosive & volatile agent poisoning. ► Hospitalize all patient irrespective of severity & ingested amount. C/C : H/O kerosene ingestion (mostly children) : • Smell of kerosene in breath, mouth and body. • Burning sensation of mouth and pharynx. • Fever. • Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, diarrhea. • Convulsion/coma. Δ Kerosene Poisoning Adv. : ▸ CXR P/A view. ▸ ± CBC. Tx, ● NPO-TFO except medication. ● Removal of contaminated clothes and wash with water (if applicable). ● O ₂ inhalation sos. ● [DNS+NS+HS 1L in case of adults]. If 2 – 5 yr : Inf. Babysol Junior (osl) / Liboot-S Junior (libra) 1L to 1.5L, I/V stat @ 10 d/m. If >5 yr : Inf. 5% DNS 1L to 2L, I/V stat @ 10 d/m. ● Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g/10ml or 500mg/5ml, [wt] ml I/V stat & once daily. ● Susp. Famotidine 40 mg/5 ml / Famotid (drg) / Famotack (sq) / Famomax (bex), ½ TSF BD. [If adult : Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg, 1 vial IV stat & OD or BD].

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.disease_name: Kerosene Poisoning / Household Non-Toxic Product Poisoning
treatment.description: ► Complication : CXR PA view (aspiration pneumonia) : • Grade 0 : Normal. • Grade 1 : Minimal unilateral perihilar infiltration. • Grade 2 : Bilateral infiltration. • Grade 3 : Confluent fluffy shadow on one or both sides. • Grade 4 : Extensive bilateral infiltration with consolidation and/or pleural effusion. Add if needed (with senior consultation) : ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone / Roxadex (nuv) / Dexa (ren) / Decason (ops), ½ or ⅓ amp I/V stat & BD. In case of creps / Pulmonary edema : ☼ Inj. Furosemide 20 mg/2 ml / Lasix (syn) / Fusid (sq) / Frucin (ops), [wt/10] ml I/V stat & BD. In case of convulsion : Age ☼ Inj. Phenobarbitone 1ml / Barbit (incp) 1 ml + 9 ml D/W then, [wt] ml I/V stat then, [wt/8] ml I/V BD. Age > 3 months : ☼ Inj. Diazepam 2ml / Sedil (sq), [wt/10] ml + DA D/W P/R stat & sos. Household Non-Toxic Product (Cosmetic/Mud) Poisoning : ▸ Diet : Normal. ▸ Al(OH)3 + Mg(OH)2 + Simethicon / Entacyd Plus Suspension, 2 TSF stat & TDS. ▸ Liquid paraffin 250 ml P/O stat. If no symptom develops within 24 hours : D c̄ A (discharge with advice) : ▸ Cap. Cefixime 400 mg 1+0+1 for 14 days or, Syp. Cefixime [wt/10] tsf BD for 14 days (for children). উপদেশ : • নিয়মিত ওষুধ খাবেন। • পরবর্তীতে কোন সমস্যা হলে মেডিসিন বহির্বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। If reappearance of fever on 3rd or 5th day : • Inj. Ceftriaxone 1gm 1 vial IV stat and BD – 5 days. • Inj. Metronidazole 500mg/100ml1 bottle IV stat and TDS – 5 days. If CNS manifestation e.g., irritability, Convulsion, coma etc.: • General management of unconscious patient. • Inj. Diazepam 10mg/5ml, 1 amp IV stat [1 amp for every 20kg] [0.5mg/kg/dose]. • Inj. Phenobarbitone 200mg/ml, 1 amp IV stat and ¼ th of loading dose BD [15-20mg/kg IV stat then 5-6mg/kg BD].

treatment.title: Alcohol Poisoning & Addiction
treatment.description: Taking a Brief Alcohol History including : A. Consumption : • Consumption in units of alcohol per day/week • Drinking pattern daily/continuous or episodic/binge drinking? • Drinking behaviour in last week and last 6 months. • When did patient last drink? B. Alcohol dependence : All indicative of dependence syndrome : • History of morning/relief drinking? • Change in tolerance? • Strong compulsion to drink? • Continued drinking despite problems? • Priority of drinking over other pursuits/activities? C. Alcohol-related problems : • Co-existing health conditions. (including co-existing drug and/or mental health problems)? • Risk of harm to Self ± Others? • Urgency for treatment? • Motivation and readiness to change? • History of withdrawal symptoms. (sweating, tremor, nausea / vomiting anxiety, insomnia, seizures, hallucinations, delirium tremens)? Examination : Alcohol may affect any Organ / Organ System Specific physical signs (liver injury) : • Spider naevi. • Palmer erythema • Dupuyten’s contracture • Gynaecomastia • Telangiectasia • Facial mooning • Parotid enlargement

treatment.title: Alcohol-related problems
treatment.description: Types : 1. Ethanol Poisoning / Alcohol Intoxication. (non toxic but overdose) 2. Adulterated Alcohol Poisoning or, Methanol / Ethylene Glycol (Toxic spirit) poisoning. 3. Chronic intoxication – Alcoholic liver disease or other organ failure / carcinoma.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Initial Investigations : • RBS • S. Electrolytes • S. Creatinine • LFTs • Blood Ethanol / Metahnol / Ethylene Glycol level (if available) Additional Screening For : • Hepatitis C • Hepatitis B • HIV Type of Alcohol identified? Alcoholic person with ethanol (non toxic) overdose. Ethanol from licensed dealer with no toxic adulteration. Supportive Care : • cABCDE : o Protect Airway. o Maintain Circulatory Status. o Monitor GCS. • Gastric Lavage (if very recent ingestion). • O 2 inhalation. • IV PPI. • Treat Hypoglycaemia. • Thiamine. • If acidosis : IV NaHCO 3 . Ethanol : • Mostly Supportive Care. • Hemodialysis (if severe). • Blood Glucose Monitoring. Antidote for Methanol / Ethylene Glycol : • Fomepizol (preferred) : 15mg/kg IV over 30 mins stat, THEN, 10mg/kg 12 hrly until methanol • Ethanol (5%-10%) : 600 mg/kg IV/ PO stat, THEN, 66-154 mg/kg/hr (Target serum level 100-150 mg/dl). Additional Drugs : • If Ethylene Glycol : o Thiamine: IV 100 mg 6 hrly. o Pyridoxine: IV 50mg 6 hrly. • If Methanol : o Folinic/Folic acid : 1 mg/kg (max 50 mg) 4 hrly. o Consult ophthalmologist if visual disturbance. • Discharge when stable. • Thiamine Supplement 100 mg/day. • Counselling.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Acute Adulterated Alcohol / Methanol Poisoning Patients present after 12 to 24 hours, following ingestion. Within first 12 – 24 hour patients may appear normal, and this is described as the latent period. Nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain subsequently followed by central nervous system (CNS) depression and hyperventilation as metabolic acidosis occurs. C / C : H/O of adulterated alcohol (wine) 24 hours ago. [Usually after a celebration event : Village fair or social local gatherings, 1 st January (English new year), 15 th April (Bangla new year), Dosomi (last day of Durga puja), etc.] O / E : BP & Pulse, Neurologic findings & Blindness in later stage. Δ Methanol Poisoning Adv. : ▸ S. electrolytes. ▸ S. creatinine. Tx, ● Diet – Normal ● Clearing of the airways (O-P suction sos). ● O 2 inhalation 4-6 L/min. ● Inf. 5% DNS 1L + 5% DA 1L, I/V stat @ 20 d/m. ● Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Seclo (sq) / Losectil (skf) / Omep (ops) / Omenix (incp), 1 vial I/V slowly stat & 12 hourly. ● 95% Ethanol (Whiskey) Loading dose : 100 ml, then diluted with water or fruit juice or carbonated drinks, then, Maintenance dose : 40 ml (then dilute also) per hour. ● Please monitor vital signs.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Usually patient came for treatment next day (24 hours) after ingestion with severe vomiting & semi-consciousness or other non specific symptoms. Stomach wash within 1 hour of ingestion is advocated but it is not effective in the next day. ► Antidote of methanol is ethanol. I/V regiment : Loading dose : 10% ethanol 7.5ml/Kg IV over 30-60 min. Maintenance dose : 10% ethanol 1.96ml/Kg/Hr IV. Oral regiment : Loading dose : 95% ethanol / give alcohol arrack (Gin or whisky or traditional party wine) 1.8 ml / Kg orally. Maintenance dose : 95% ethanol / alcohol 0.2ml/Kg/Hr orally dilute in water or fruit juice orally for 3 ‐ 4 days. ► Table : Ethanol Content of Different Alcoholic Beverages. BEVERAGE ALCOHOL BY VOLUME (ABV) BEVERAGE ALCOHOL BY VOLUME (ABV) Beer 4 – 6% Fortified Wine 15 – 22% Cider 4 – 6% Tequila 38 – 40% Wine 10 – 12% Rum 40% Mead 10 – 14% Vodka 40% Sake 16% Whiskey 40 – 50% ► Sedation (diazepam) can be given cautiously to prevent delirium and restlessness & if acidosis then sodi-bi-carb. ► Ophthalmologist consultation for visual disturbance (blindness). ► In severe cases patient should be intubated and mechanical respiration should be given. ► Another antidote : Fomepizole (unavailable).

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Wernicke-Korsakoff syndrome is an unusual type of memory disorder due to a lack of thiamin (vitamin B1) requiring immediate treatment. Chronic Hazardous Drinking ↓ o Ataxia o Ophthalmoplegia o Confusion ± o Hypoglycaemia ↓ Suspect (Dx) : Wernicke’s encephalopathy Features : ○ History of alcoholism, malabsorption, malnutrition, hyperemesis gravidarum. ○ Brain stem sign e.g. ataxia, nystigmus & extra-ocular muscle weakness particularly lateral rectus. ○ ACUTE CONFUSIONAL STATE (DELIRIUM), then dx : Wernicke encephalopathy (WE). Management : 1. Intravenous thiamine 2 vial 8 hourly for 48 hours, then oral thiamine 100 mg & hourly. 2. Treatment of the underlying cause.

treatment.title: Definitions & Management
treatment.description: Drowning Syndrome “Near-drowning” is the previous term of drowning syndrome typically used to describe almost dying from suffocating under water. Hypoxemia is the principal problem in the drowning or submersion injury, previously known as near drowning. Hypoxemia for more than 5 minutes causes neurological damage. Drowning may be fatal or non-fatal. Aspiration of water occurs in 90% of cases. The three effects of aspiration are : 1. Aspiration pneumonia, 2. Hypoxemia & 3. Hypothermia. Clinical feature : • Common age ~3 years. • Anxious appearance of victim. • Respiratory distress. • Altered vital signs e.g. tachycardia, low BP. • Cool extremities. • Loss of consciousness. • Acidotic breathing (metabolic acidosis). • Apnea, Asystole (cardiac arrest). Investigation : • CXR P/A view – Consolidations, foreign body, evidence of aspiration, pulmonary oedema. • Serum electrolytes: Hypo or hypernatremia, hyperkalemia. • Creatinine – altered. • CBC. • RBS : decreased • ECG – Cardiac arrhythmia. Treatment : A. At Community – Basic life support (BLS). B. At Hospital – 1. Resuscitation (ABC) : • Assess vital signs (e.g. pulse, BP, respiration, SpO 2 ). • Open airway & remove visible debris. • Initiate rescue breathing (e.g. mouth to mouth). • Start chest compression with bag and mask ventilation (CPR). Give oxygen by face mask 5-6 L/min. • IV fluid for hypotension –N/S, Ringers lactate, 20 ml/kg over 1 hour for volume expansion. 2. Keep child warm, wrap with blanket. 3. Prophylactic antibiotic if contaminated water or sewage 4. If CNS complication – Mannitol, anticonvulsants. 5. If pulmonary oedema- Diuretics. 6. If AKI –Fluid restriction. 7. Correction of dyselectrolytemia 8. Treatment of hypoglycemia. 9. Observed at least 6-12 hours before discharge.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Drowning Syndrome Tx, ☼ NPO – TFO. ☼ O 2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P (Oro-pharyngeal) suction : If frothy discharge from mouth or nose. ☼ Keep the baby warm : Removal of damp cloths, drying skin, wrapping with warm blanket and keep in warm environment. ☼ Inf. 5 % DA [100 × wt] ml, I/V stat @ (ml / 100) d/m (Minimum fluid). ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g/10ml or 500mg/5ml, [wt] ml I/V stat & once daily. ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone 5mg/ml / Roxadex, [wt/13] ml I/V stat & BD. In case of creps / Pulmonary edema, ☼ Inj. Furosemide / Lasix 2ml, [wt/10] ml I/V stat & BD. In case of convulsion ( ☼ Inj. Phenobarbitone 1ml, 1ml + 9 ml D/W then, [wt] ml I/V stat then, [wt/8] ml I/V BD. In case of convulsion ( >3 months of age), ☼ Inj. Diazepam 2ml / Sedil (sq), [wt/10] ml + double amount D/W P/R through rubber tube stat & sos. ► If features cerebral oedema present : IV mannitol. ► On discharge : ▸ Syrup. Cefixime 100mg/5ml [10 mg/kg/day] / Cef-3 (sq) / T-cef (drg) / Triocim (bex), [wt/20] TSF BD – 14 days.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : • H/O electrocution ____ hours back. • Unconsciousness/ semi-consciousness. • Burn & Pain. Δ Electrocution Adv. : ▸ ECG (Urgent): Look for VT. ▸ Serum electrolytes ▸ Serum creatinine If unconscious / semiconscious + absent pulse + non recordable BP → Urgent CPR. If cardiac status is normal : ● Diet : Normal ● Inf. Normal saline 1L IV @10 drops/min If pain present : ● Inj. Ketorolac 30mg/1ml / Acupain (bacn) / Torax (sq) / Todol (ops) / Zeropain (hc), 1 amp IM or IV stat and BD ● Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / PPI (acm) / Losetil (skf) / Seclo (sq) / Omenix (incp) / Omep (aris), 1 vial IV stat and BD If 3 ° burn present : ● Inj. Flucloxacillin 500 mg, 1 vial IV stat and QDS. ● Inj. Tetanus toxoid 40IU/0.5ml / TT vax (pop) / Vaxitet (incp), 1 amp IM stat on left arm. ● Inj. Tetanus antitoxin 3000IU/1ml (equine) / Vaxitet (incp) or, Tetanus Immunoglobulin 250 IU/ml (human) / Protet-IG (incp), 1 vial IM stat on right arm ● Silver Sulpbadiazine 1% topical / Cream Burna (sq) / Burnsil (bex) / Silcream (jay), Apply thick layer over burn area TDS ● Call to burn and plastic surgery department. ► Most dangerous complication of electrocution is ventricular fibrillation (VF) so pulse and BP record & ECG in must. If VF then Mx of VF (Please cardiology portion).

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Discharge note (Electrocution) : ✔ Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Rolac (ren), 1+0+1 (A/M) – If pain. ✔ Tab. Pantoprazole 20mg/ Pantobex (bex), 1+0+1 – PPI c̄ NSAID. ✔ Cap. Flucloxacillin / Phylopen DS (sq) / Flubex (bex) 500 mg / Flupen (drg) / Flustar (ren), 1+1+1+1 – 7 days if burn present. ✔ Silver Sulphadiazine 1% topical / Cream Burna (sq) / Burnsil (bex) / Silcream (jay), Apply over burnt area TDS – 7 days.

treatment.title: First aid method
treatment.description: Recommended first aid methods (after any snake bite) : 1. Reassurance : Reassurance has prime importance to stabilize the victims. They should be advised not to get panicked and be told that majority of snakes are non-venomous; moreover, venomous snakes sometimes fail to inject enough venom effectively during bites. 2. Immobilization : Make sure that bitten limb (hand or leg) is immobile and the victim must be kept still. Advise : • Bite in lower limb: “Sit down, do not walk”. • Bite in upper limb: “Do not move the limb”. Pressure immobilization method : We can give pressure by simple crepe bandage or by any long strips of clothes (like Gamcha or Urna). The bandage is wrapped firmly around the entire bitten limbs, starting distally around the fingers or toes, and moving proximally, to include a rigid splint. The bandage is bound as tightly as for a sprained ankle, but not so tightly that the peripheral pulse (Radial, posterior tibial, dorsalis pedis) is occluded or that a finger cannot easily be slipped between its layers. বিষাক্ত সাপ কামড়ালে করণীয় : ▸ আক্রান্ত ব্যাক্তিকে সাহস দিতে হবে যাতে ভয় না পায়। ▸ সাপটি দেখতে পেলে কোন প্রজাতির তা চেনার চেষ্টা করুন। ▸ ক্ষত স্থান পরিষ্কার পানি ও সাবান দিয়ে ধুয়ে ফেলুন। ▸ কামড়ের দাগের সংখ্যা দিয়ে বিষক্রিয়ার পরিমাণ বোঝা যায় না। ভাইপবর ও কোবরার কামড়ে প্রথমেই জায়গাটা ফুলে যাবে, বাথা হবে, বিষের ফোলা ক্রমশই বাড়বে। কিন্তু কালাচের কামড়ে কোনও ব্যথা বা ফোলা হয় না। ▸ কাকড়া বিছের কামড়ে ও হুলে তীব্র ব্যথা হয় কিন্ত ফোলা হয়ই না বললেই চলে। কাকড়া বিছের কামড়ে এভিএসের কোন ভূমিকা নাই। ▸ শরীরের যে অংশে সাপ কামড়েছে তা যথা সম্ভব স্থির রাখতে হবে। অপ্রয়োজনীয় নড়াচড়া বিষকে রক্ত প্রবাহে প্রবেশ করাতে সহায়তা করবে। প্রয়োজনে কাঠের একটি তক্তা বা বাঁশের কঞ্চি বা ছড়ার সাথে কাপড় দিয়ে ব্যান্ডেজ করে দিতে হবে। অধিকাংশ সাপের কামড়ই হাতে বা পায়ে হয়। এক্ষেত্রে হাত বা পায়ের হাড় ভাঙ্গলে যেমন করে নড়াচড়া বন্ধ করা হয়, সেভাবে স্পিন্ট (splint) দিয়ে বাধার মত করে নিতে হবে। রাস্তাঘাট বা মাঠে ময়দানে তো আর স্পিন্ট পাওয়া যায় না। সেক্ষেত্রে যে কোনও শক্ত কাঠি বা গাছের ডালও এই স্পিন্ট-এর কাজ করতে পারে। ▸ পায়ে বা হাতে কামড়ালে কাটা স্থানের ২ ইঞ্চি উপরে কাপড় দিয়ে চওড়া করে হাল্কা বাঁধন দিন। ▸ আক্রন্ত ব্যাক্তিকে অতিদ্রুত নিকটস্থ হাসপাতালে নিয়ে যেতে হবে। এক ঘন্টার মধ্যে চিকিৎসা শুরু করতে হবে। ▸ সাপে কাটা স্থানে কোন রকম কাটা-ছেঁড়া করা যাবেনা। ▸ কাটা স্থানে মুখ দিয়ে চুষে রক্ত বের করার চেষ্টা করবেন না। ▸ কাটা স্থানে কোন প্রকার কবিরাজি ঔষধ ইত্যাদি লাগাবেন না। [উ ইনস্টিটিউট অব হেলথ অ্যান্ড ফ্যামিলি ওয়েল ফেয়ার (২০১৬), স্বাস্থ্য ও পরিবার কল্যাণ দপ্তর, পশ্চিমবঙ্গ সরকার] Ideally, compression bandages should not be released until the patient is under medical care in hospital, resuscitation facilities are available and antivenom treatment has been started. 3. Wash the bitten area : gently with soap and water, or wipe with a wet cloth and cover with bandage. 4. Remove as early as possible all rings, bangles, anklets, cords or clothing, ‘Tabij’, ‘Dhaga’ etc., which may cause constriction if swelling occur. 5. Place the patient in the lateral recumbent position. 6. If non-breathing, insert an oral airway or perform rescue breathing if necessary. 7. If the snake is killed somehow, bring it to hospital. Be careful and do not handle snakes bare handedly as they might pretend to be dead. Do not waste time for catching or killing the snake. 8. Quick transport : Seek help to get the patient rapidly transferred to a hospital by motorbike or ambulance or other motorized vehicles. [Cobra may has fatal effect within 30-60 min & Russell's viper within 100 min.] [Ref – Learner’s Guide for Management of Snakebite, DGHS, 2020-21, Page 17 & 18]

treatment.title: Syndromic Approach
treatment.description: ◙ There are four common poisonous snake in Bangladesh, Indian, Nepal & Sri-Lanka region. This family so collectively called “Big Four Family”. Big Four Snake Family – A. Russell's viper. B. Cobra(s). C. Saw-scaled viper. D. Common krait. SYNDROMIC APPROACH : Syndrome 1 : Local envenoming (swelling etc.) with bleeding/clotting disturbances = Viperidae (all species) Syndrome 2 : Local envenoming (swelling etc.) with bleeding/clotting disturbances, shock or acute kidney. injury with/without neurotoxicity = Russell’s viper Syndrome 3 : Local envenoming (swelling, blisters etc.) with neurotoxicity = cobra or king cobra. Syndrome 4 : Neurotoxicity with minimal or no local envenoming. Bitten on land or while sleeping on the ground = krait Bitten in the sea, estuary and some freshwater lakes = sea snake Syndrome 5 : Neurotoxicity with dark brown urine and acute kidney injury (no bleeding/clotting disturbances) : Bitten on land or while sleeping indoors = krait (B. niger) Bitten in sea, estuary and some freshwater lakes = sea snake [Ref – Learner’s Guide for Management of Snakebite, DGHS, 2020-21, Page 14 & 15] ◙ Antivenom (PAV) only for following snake bite – 1. Cobra (Neck hood) [Gokhra], 2. Krait (Banded scales) [Keotey], 3. Russell’s viper [Chandrabora], 4. Saw scaled / carpet viper. ◙ 1 ml of Snake Anti-venom / Inj. Antivenom (incepta) or (Haffkine, India) neutralizes – 1. 0.60 mg of Indian Kobra (Naja naja) venom, 2. 0.45 mg of Common Krait (Bungarus caerules) venom, 3. 0.60 mg of Russell’s Viper (Vipera russelii) venom, 4. 0.45 mg of Saw scaled viper (Echis carinatus) venom. Snake Toxicity Russell’s viper Hemotoxic +++ Myotoxic ++ Green pit viper Hemotoxic + Indian Cobra Hemotoxic + Neurotoxic +++ Common Krait Neurotoxic +++ Myotoxic + Sea snake Neurotoxic ++ Myotoxic +++ Hemotoxic “+++” means : Local envenoming (Swelling of limbs) + Bleeding or clotting disturbance. [WBCT ( whole blood clotting test) > 20 min] Hemotoxic “+” means : Local envenoming (Swelling of site) + But NO systemic bleeding or clotting disturbance. [WBCT ( whole blood clotting test) Neurotoxicity “+++” means : Neurotoxic Features + No Local Envenoming + But NO bleeding or clotting disturbance. [WBCT ( whole blood clotting test) [ ≡ Viper (Family = Viperidae) term is related to hemotoxic snakes.] [ ≡ Krait (Genus = Bungarus) terms for band pattern snake & related to neurotoxicity.] [ ≡ Cobra (mostly family Elapidae & genus Naja) terms for hooded snake.] [ ≡ King cobra is the known longest poisonous snake & are neurotoxic, hemotoxic & cytotoxic. They can cause harm even without biting, by splitting poisonous saliva only. King cobra is not common in Bangladesh, but Binocellate Cobra is common & Monocellete Cobra is more common.] [ ≡ Russell's viper is more common in northern area (Rajshahi, Chapainobabgonj etc.) & Krait is common in southern area (Chattogram).]

treatment.title: Snake Identifications
treatment.description: ◙ চিকিৎসার প্রয়োজনে বাংলাদেশের বিষধর সাপকে দু'ভাগে ভাগ করা হয়। যথা : নিউরোটক্সিক আর হিমোটক্সিক। নিউরোটক্সিক সাপ আবার দু'রকম ফণাযুক্ত আর ফণাহীন। • ফণাযুক্ত নিউরোটক্সিক সাপ : গোখরো, কেউটে, শঙ্খচুড়। • ফণাহীন নিউরোটক্সিক সাপ : কালাচ ও শাঁখামুটি। • হিমোটক্সিক বা রক্তক্ষরণকারী সাপ : চন্দ্রবোড়া ও গেছোবোড়া। কোবরা / ফণাযুক্ত সাপ : এদের কামড়ের লক্ষণ হল প্রচণ্ড ব্যথা আর ক্রমবর্ধমান ফোলা। নিউরোটক্সিক সাপের কামড়ের রোগীর দুই চোখের পাতা পড়ে আসবে (ptosis) → কথা জড়িয়ে আসবে (nasal voice) → রোগী ক্রমশ ঝিমিয়ে পড়বে (lathergy) → রোগী ঝাপসা দেখবে (ophthalmoplegia) । কমন ক্রেট / কালাচ : দিনের বেলায় এদের প্রায় দেখাই যায়না। প্রায় সব ক্ষেত্রে এরা রাত্রে খোলা বিছানায় কামড়ায়। রাত্রে বিছানায় কামড়ের ২ থেকে ২০ ঘন্টা পর নার্ভে বিষের লক্ষণ দেখা যায়। কালাচের কামড়ে কোনও ব্যথা বা ফোলা হয় না, তাই অনেক সময় বুঝায় যায় না । চন্দ্রবোড়ার কামড়েও গোখরোর কামড়ের মতো ব্যথা আর ক্রমবর্ধমান ফোলা প্রাথমিক লক্ষণ। রক্ত জমাট বাধতে বাধা দেয়। কামড়ের পর প্রতি ১ মিনিট নষ্ট হলে কিডনি ফেইল বা খারাপ হয়ে যাওয়ার সম্ভাবনা ১% শতাংশ করে বাড়তে থাকে। সামুদ্রিক সাপ : চ্যাপ্টা লেজের সামুদ্রিক সাপ ভীষণ বিষধর (neurotoxins and myotoxins)। সমুদ্রে স্নান করতে নামলেই সাপে কামড়ায় না। এরা শান্ত প্রকৃতির ও সহজে কামবায় না। জেলেদের জালে ঐ সব সাপ উঠে আসে। মনে রাখতে হবে যে সমুদ্রের সাপ কামড়ালে কিন্তু প্রাণ বাঁচানো মুশকিল। পলিভ্যালেন্ট এ ভি এস সমুদ্রের সাপের কামড়ে কাজ করে না। সব সামগ্রিক সাপই ভীষণ বিষধর। এদের কামড়ে প্রচুর এভিএস দিতে হয়। এর আইষ মায়োটক্সিক, শরীরের মাংসপেশী দ্রুত নষ্ট হতে থাকে। কিডনি দ্রুত নষ্ট হয় । ব্র্যান্ডেড ক্রেট বা শঙ্খিনী : খুব শান্ত স্বভাবের সাপ। তাই এর কামড় সাধারণত পাওয়া যায় না। দাঁড়াশ : ফনাহীন, বিষহীন। প্রিয় খাবার : ইঁদুর । কুসংস্কার রয়েছে, যে এদের লেজে বিষ আছে। প্রকৃতপক্ষে কোন সাপের লেজেই বিষ থাকে না। হেলে বা হলহলে সাপ : এই সাপ বিষহীন ও দিবাচর। কামড়ায় না বললেই চলে। কমন ক্রেট / কালাচ কমন উলফ / চিতি ১। গায়ের রং কালো। ১। ধুসর বা বাদামী। ২। সরু সরু সাদা ব্যান্ড বা চুড়ি লেজের শেষ পর্যন্ত (একত্রে দুটি করে) ২। চওড়া ব্যান্ড, ঘাড় থেকে শুরু। ৩। ঘাড়ের কিছু দূর পর থেকে খুব সরু ব্যান্ড শুরু হয়ে লেজ পর্যন্ত বিস্তৃত। ৩ লেজে কোন ব্যান্ড থাকে না। ৪। চরম বিষাক্ত। ৪। নির্বিষ। গেছোবোড়া লাউডগা এরা মৃদু বিষযুক্ত সাপ। বিষহীন (মৃদু বিষযুক্ত) সবুজ সাপ। অন্যান্য সাপের মত। লম্বা নাক। মোটা শরীর। লিকলিকে লম্বা শরীর। গাছ, বন-জঙ্গল, চা বাগান, পার্বত্য অঞ্চল। গাছে গাছে ঘুরে বেরায় বলে চোখের উপর কামড়াতে পাড়ে। বাংলাদেশের পিট ভাইপার (গেছোবোড়া) গুলি মোটা নাকের নয়। যদিও বিষ আছে, এরা ঘাতক নয়। কিন্তু কেরালা ও তামিলনাড়ুর মোটা নাকের গেছোবোড়া (Hump Nose Pit Viper) খুব শক্তিশালী হিমোটক্সিক ও ঘাতক। ► নার্ভ বিষ ছড়িয়ে পড়ার লক্ষণ : • শিবনেত্র / ptosis - চোখের পাতা ঢুলে আসে। • ফণাধর সাপের কামড়ে ৩০ মিনিটের মধ্যেই শুরু হয়। • কালাচ কামড়ের ক্ষেত্রে ২ থেকে ২৪ ঘন্টাও লাগে। • নিওস্টিগমিন কালাচের কামড়ে কাজ করে না, কোবরার কামড়ে কাজ করে। • যদি পড়ে আসা চোখের পাতা “Atropine ও Neostigmine” ইনজেক্টশনে ঠিক হচ্চে দেখা যায়, তবে নিশ্চিত ভাবে বলা যায় যে এটি ফণাযুক্ত সাপের কামড়। উপদেশ : • আগামী ২৪ ঘন্টা ঘুমানো যাবে না। • চোখের পাতা পড়ে আসলে (Ptosis) । • নাকে কথা বললে (Nasal Voice) । • ঘাড় ভেঙ্গে আসলে (Broken Neck) । [উ ইনস্টিটিউট অব হেলথ অ্যান্ড ফ্যামিলি ওয়েল ফেয়ার (২০১৬), স্বাস্থ্য ও পরিবার কল্যাণ দপ্তর, পশ্চিমবঙ্গ সরকার]

treatment.title: Bite marks, Dry Bite & Snakelet
treatment.description: ◙ Bite marks : Fang marks (two puncture wound) may be seen in venomous snake bite. But fang marks never conclude that the snake is venomous or not. So practically bite is not so important to differentiate & initiate of treatment. Note : These are not exact two fang marks but are venomous snake bite. Venomous snake bite may be lacerated or incised wound. In venomous krait bite there is usually no visible wound at all. ◙ Dry Bite & Snakelet : অনেক সময় সাপ কামড় দিলেও বিষ ঢালে না, একে ড্রাই বাইট (dry bite) বলে। রাসেল’স ভাইপারের ৫০% কামড় এবং কোবরার প্রায় ৩০% ভাগ কামড় ড্রাই বাইট হয়ে থাকে। কিন্তু বাচ্চা সাপ বা ছোট সাপগুলো ড্রাই বাইট করতে পারে না। বাচ্চা সাপগুলো এগ্রেসিভ বা আক্রমণাত্মক হয়ে থাকে। তাই বিষধর সাপের বাচ্চা (snakelet / baby snake), বড় সাপের থেকেও বেশি ক্ষতি সাধন করতে পারে। ◙ Same dose for child in pediatrics ward, as injected amount of venom is not known. Snake itself can not discriminate the amount of venom injected to adult or child.

treatment.title: Snake Bite (under observation) or, Non-venomous
treatment.description: C / C : H/O of snake bite ___ minutes ago. H/O appearance of snake : Poisonous or not ?? O / E : - BP & Pulse. - Lung / Breath sound. - Local swelling ±. - Abdominal pain or non specific symptoms, (early toxic sign). - Ptosis ± (1 st neurologic sign). - Ophthamloplegia ±. - Nasal voice (2 nd neurologic sign) ±. - Dysphagia ±. - Broken neck sign ± (late neurologic sign). - Bleeding manifestation / hematuria ±. - Blood coagulation test : (In suspected viper bite) Take 3-5 ml ml fresh venous blood in test tube. After 20 min if not clot occur it non-hemotoxic poison / non-poisonous. If clot occurs it indicate (hemotoxic) poisonous. Δ Non-venomous Snake Bite Or, Δ Snake Bite (under observation) Adv. : ▸ 20 min WBCT (If viper snake suspected) Tx, ● Reassurance & supportive care. ● Diet – Normal. ● Wash the bite site with normal saline. ● Immobilization of bitten limb or body part. ● Cap. Amoxicillin 500 mg, Moxin (ops) / Moxacil (sq), 1+1+1 or, ● Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg / A-Flox (acm) / Flubex (bex) / Fluclox (aci), 1+1+1+1 ● Tab. Paracetamol / Napa (bex) / Ace (sq) / Renova (ops) / ATP (gen) 500mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) ● Inj. Tetanus Toxoid 40IU/0.5ml / Vaxitet (incp) / TT vax (pop), 1 amp I/M stat on other (left) arm. ● Inj. Tetanus antitoxin (Equine) 3000IU/ml / Vaxitet-IG (incp), 1 amp I/M stat on one (right) arm. ● Discharge after 24 hr of observation.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Identify the snake by brought specimen, description or by photograph. Identified as venomous, non-venomous, snake not identified, suspected snakebite, treat according. ► No sedative / anxiolytic (Diazepam) can be given. As sedative / anxiolytic will make patient drowsy & may show false broken neck + ptosis. So weather that drowsiness, broken neck & ptosis are due to diazepam or venom of snake, would difficult to diagnosis envenomation. ► Better to avoid aspirin & NSAIDs. Use paracetamol as analgesic, if needed. In case of suspected hemotoxins / not clotted blood coagulation test, must avoid I/M (intramuscular) injections. ► Observe 24 hours if no sign-symptom of venom appears, then consider as non-poisonous snake bite & discharge. ► Immobilize the beaten limb with a splint or sling : If bitten in lower limb, don’t walk, if upper limb, don’t move the limb. Pressure bandage by simple crepe bandage/ long strips of cloths; wrapped firmly (Not so tight to occlude peripheral pulses as one finger may be introduced through bandage) around bitten limb proximal to the bitten site. Tourniquet may be applied in case of venomous snake to prevent absorption & finally removed after 1 hour. Usually patient presents with highly tight tourniquet & counsel the attendants that these tight bandage will cause death of limb and gangrene, so remove the tie and observe the patient.

treatment.title: Antivenom (AV) therapy
treatment.description: In dications of Antivenom (AV) therapy : Systemic Envenoming : Systemic signs Clinical Laboratory Haemostatic Spontaneous systemic bleeding 20 WBCT, Prothrombin time, Thrombocytopenia ( 9 /litre or 100 000/cu mm) Neurotoxic signs Ptosis, external ophthalmoplegia, paralysis etc. Cardiovascular abnormalities Hypotension, shock, cardiac arrhythmia Abnormal ECG Acute kidney injury (renal failure) Oliguria / Anuria Rising serum creatinine / urea Haemoglobin-uria / Myoglobin-uria Dark brown urine, muscle aches and pains (feature of generalized rhabdomyolysis) Urine dipsticks, Hyperkalemia Low Hemoglobin / Haematocrit Local Envenoming : ● Local swelling involving more than half of the bitten limb (in the absence of a tourniquet) within 48 hours of the bite. ● Swelling after bites on the digits (toes and especially fingers). ● Rapid extension of swelling (for example, beyond the wrist or ankle within a few hours of bite on the hands or feet). ● Development of an enlarged tender lymph node draining the bitten limb. [Ref – Learner’s Guide for Management of Snakebite, DGHS, 2020-21, Page 20]

treatment.title: 20-minute whole blood clotting test
treatment.description: Procedure to do 20 WBCT : 1. Place a 3 to 5 ml of freshly sampled venous blood in a small dry glass tube. 2. Leave undisturbed for 20 minutes at ambient temperature, in erect position. 3. If the blood is still liquid (unclotted) and runs out, the patient has hypofibrinogenaemia and “incoagulable blood” as a result of venom induced consumption coagulopathy. 4. In perspective of Bangladesh, incoagulable blood is diagnostic of a viper bite and rules out an elapid bite. 5. Warning : If the tube used for the test is not made of ordinary glass, or if it has been used before and cleaned with detergent, its wall may not stimulate clotting of the blood sample in the usual way and test will be invalid. If there is any doubt repeat the test in duplicate, including a “Control” (blood from a healthy person). 6. Should repeat after 6 hour of admission or antivenom infusion. If still not clotted then repeat antivenom infusion. 7. It is not indicated in identified kobra or krait bite [Ref – Learner’s Guide for Management of Snakebite, DGHS, 2020-21, Page 16]

treatment.title: Systemic signs of envenoming
treatment.description: Systemic signs of envenoming : Neurotoxic sign : Ptosis (partial or complete) usually symmetrical and progressive, diplopia, external ophthalmoplegia, loss of gag reflex/palatal palsy (bulbar palsy), dysphonia/nasal voice, bilateral facial paralysis, inability to open the mouth or protrude the tongue, paralysis of chest muscles and diaphragm (shallow breathing), ‘broken neck sign’, weak grip, diminished tendon reflexes. Signs of haematological abnormalities : Persistent bleeding from bite site, venepuncture site and or inflicted wound if any, multiple bruises or large blood collection, haemorrhagic blisters, bleeding from gingival sulci, haemoptysis, haematemesis, haematuria, epistaxis, per vaginal bleeding. Signs of renal failure : Scanty or no urine output, dark coloured urine, hypotension, clinical uraemic syndrome (Nausea, vomiting, hiccups, fetor, drowsiness, coma, flapping tremor, muscle twitching, convulsions, pericardial friction rub, signs of fluid overload). Clinical uraemic syndrome : Nausea, vomiting, hiccups, fetor, drowsiness, coma, flapping tremor, muscle twitching, convulsions, pericardial friction rub, signs of fluid overload. Signs of myotoxicity : Muscle tenderness, weakness, respiratory failure, black urine, renal failure. Signs of cardiovascular toxicity : Hypotension, shock. Signs of local envenomation : Swelling, tenderness, bleeding, ulceration, necrosis, at the sight of bite with local lymph node enlargement. Psychological features : Snakebite is also known to cause significant psychological morbidity in the form of acute stress reaction and anxiety immediately and depression and post traumatic stress disorder as a sequel. [Ref – Learner’s Guide for Management of Snakebite, DGHS, 2020-21, Page 12 & 13]

treatment.title: Venomous Snake Bite
treatment.description: If sign of envenomation : Δ Venomous Snake Bite Adv. : ▸ 20 WBCT (only if Russell's viper or green viper) Tx, ● Diet – Normal or NPO if Krait bite signs. ● O 2 inhalation sos. ● Wash the bite site with normal saline. ● Immobilization of bitten limb or body part. ● Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg / Flux (ops) / Flupen (drg) / Phylopen DS (sq), 1 + 1 + 1 + 1. ● Tab. Paracetamol / Xpa (aris) / Tamen (skf) / Fast (acm) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 ± 1 (A/M). ● Inj. Adrenaline 1 mg/ml / Adrin (gaco) / Adrinor (incp), ¼ ampoule or 0.25 ml or 25 U of insulin syringe (100 U) sub-cutaneously (S/C) – stat (before giving inj. PAV) [Child dose 0.005mg/kg S/C] ● Inj. Polyvalent Anti-snake Venom (PAV) 10 vials, each vial dilute with 10 ml D/W or N/S then dilute in 100 ml N/S then, infuse total 200 ml I/V initially @ 5 d/min, if no reaction then @ 60 d/min. [Child dose same]

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: Never to late to give AV provided the indications are present : ╬ Only if features of systemic envenoming are present for bites of snakes mentioned in the above box. ╬ Do not give AV for local envenoming alone, except for Cobra and Russell’s viper bites when indicated. ╬ Commence AV immediately if indicated for the bites of Cobra, Krait, Russell’s viper. ╬ Currently available AV is not recommended for the bites of Green pit viper, Sea snake. ╬ Tx of Green pit bite : Care for local swelling, Supportive care for coagulopathy. ╬ Tx of Sea snake bite : Conservative treatment, Support for neurotoxicity, Supportive for AKI. ► Adult and children should receive same dose (10 vials) of AV. Before initiating AV, prophylactic subcutaneous adrenaline (dose - adult 0.25 ml of 0.1% solution and in children 0.005 mg/kg) should be given to the victim. In case of premedication (PAV), Inj. Adrenaline in an Insulin syringe (100 unit) upto mark 25 (for adult), then administer this subcutaneously & in case of treatment of anaphylaxis, 0.5 ml of Inj. Adrenaline (for adult) in a 3 ml syringe and administer intramascularly. Some patients may require additional vials / doses of antivenom (see below). ► When Inj. Adrenaline should not be given : ➢ Hypertension ➢ Cardiac diseases ➢ H/O Cerebrovascular disease ➢ Coagulopathy

treatment.title: Antivenom reaction & dosing
treatment.description: Antivenom reaction ► Antivenom reaction : Antivenom reaction is common but rarely fatal. Types : A. Early reaction : ● Anaphylactic reaction (Develop within 10-180 min). ● Pyrogenic reaction (Develop within 1 to 2 hour). B. Late reaction : ● Serum sickness (Occurs within 1-12 days). ► Pyrogenic reaction (Develop within 1 to 2 hour) : Management of pyrogenic reaction: ● Stop antivenom. ● Paracetamol suppository. ► Anaphylactic reaction (Develop within 10-180 min) : Clinical features : ▪ Urticaria, itching, fever. ▪ Palpitation, vomiting. ▪ Dry cough. ▪ Abdominal pain, diarrhea. ▪ Severe anaphylaxis : Hypotension, bronchospasm, angioedema. Management of anaphylactic reaction : ● Stop antivenom. ● Inj. Adrenaline IM ( ½ ampoule or 0.5 mg for adults and 0.01 mg/kg body weight for children). ● Inj. Chloropheniramine maleate adults 10 mg, children 0.2 mg/kg by I/V injection over a few minutes) ● Inj. Hydrocortisone (adults 100 mg (1 vial), Children 2 mg/kg body weight). Anaphylaxis unresponsive to I/M Inj. Adrenaline : ● I/V 0.9% Normal saline. ● I/V Inj. Adrenaline Infusion. ● I/V Inj. Dopamine Infusion. ► Serum sickness (occurs within 1-12 days) : Management of serum sickness: ● Oral Prednisolone adults 5 mg six hourly, Children 0.7 mg/kg/day and ● Chloropherniramine adults 2 mg six hourly, Children 0.25 mg/kg /day in divided doses. ╬ CAUTION : Have Inj. Adrenaline, Inj. Hydrocortisone, Inj. Anti-histamine available at bedside. Observe the patient carefully during the time of administration of antivenom and upto 3 hours for signs of anaphylaxis. Monitor pulse, blood pressure and respiration and observe for the appearance of an urticaria or rash. ╬ No role of Inj. Hydrocortisone & Inj. Prochlorperazine [vergon (ops)] or Chlorpheniramine [Histacin (jay)] before starting PAV infusion. Should be used only in anaphylactic reaction. ╬ The dose can be repeated every 5-10 minutes if the reaction does not persist or the symptom worsens. If sign of envenomation persists after 10 vial of antivenom : Must repeat after 1-2 hours (if case of Cobra & Krait) or, Must repeat after 6 hours (in case of Russell's Viper). ► Criteria for repeating the initial dose of antivenom : a) Persisting or deteriorating signs of systemic envenoming e.g. If no improvement or deterioration of neurotoxic features (Cobra or Krait) 1-2 hours after completion of antivenom. For known or suspected Krait bite do not repeat AV after total 2 to 3 doses even if there is persistent neurotoxicity. b) In viper bites, persistence or recurrence of blood incoagulability after 6 hours of antivenom treatment. Initially at the start of AV therapy & repeat in 6 hours : if the blood does not clot in 20 minutes, repeat AV infusion and perform 20WBCT every 6 hours interval. Continue the cycle till the blood clots. [Ref – Learner’s Guide for Management of Snakebite, DGHS, 2020-21, Page 22] In monsoon season (June-August every year), a patient from rural area or poor socio-economic (slept on the floor) condition may present at hospital at dawn (3 to 5 AM) with abdominal pain / discomfort & non-specific symptoms or nothing else. Clinical – Non-specific complaints with no abdominal rigidity & pain on touch. Inv. – X-ray Abdomen A/P view in erect posture & USG of W/A. D/D – Surgical acute abdomen. – Common Krait snake poisoning. Common Krait is a snake with small fangs, causes neurotoxic poisoning & leaves no fangs marks, no local or bite site swelling. Again they rush in human habitants at night & bite a sleeping person. So it is difficult to diagnosis a common krait bite.

treatment.title: Antivenom is not available
treatment.description: If antivenom is not available : Incase of neurotoxicity : ► In case of cobra bite & no PAV available. In resource limited setting, where respiratory support is not available, patients can be maintained on neostigmine and atropine four hourly. Neurotoxic feature / ascending paralysis presents – ► Inj. Atropine and Neostigmine (Atropine to be given before neostigmine) : ▸ Inj. Atropine 0.6 mg/ml / Atropine (che/jay), 1 amp I/V stat & 4 hourly. [1 amp or 1 ml or 0.6 mg for adults, (0.08 × wt) ml or 50 μ g/kg for children]. ▸ Inj. Neostigmine 500 mcg / ml / Neostig (pop) / Prostig (che), 4 amp S/C stat & 15 minutes after atropine injection and then 4 hourly. [50-100 μ g/kg or (0.2 × wt) ml for children S/C]. ▸ Continue until neurotoxic feature improve. ► Assisted ventilation via AMBU bag or mechanical ventilation & refer to higher center with ICU facility. In case of Haematological abnormality : • Strict bed rest to avoid even any minor trauma. • I/M injection must be avoided. • Fresh whole blood or FFP transfusion should be given. [ ≡ These are supportive treatment to allow the body to excrete the venom / toxin.]

treatment.title: Acute Kidney Injury (AKI)
treatment.description: Acute Kidney Injury / AKI (Acute Renal Failure) A major problem following Russell’s viper bites. Features of renal failure : • Scanty or no urine output, • Dark coloured urine. Clinical uremic syndrome : nausea, vomiting, hiccups, fetor, drowsiness, coma, flapping tremor, muscle twitching, convulsions, pericardial friction rub, signs of fluid overload. Prevention and treatment of AKI (after viper bite) : a. Early use of AV. b. Correct hypovolemia with intravenous fluid and optimization of blood pressure (by fluid and vasopressor). c. Ensure adequate urine output hourly (at least 0.5ml/kg/hr.) with an indwelling catheter and maintain accurate fluid balance charts. d. Continue adequate intravenous fluids. e. Use intravenous furosemide if needed. f. Correct metabolic acidosis with a slow intravenous infusion of 50 – 100 mmol of sodium bicarbonate, check and repeat ABG. g. If rhabdomyolysis is suspected, 200 ml of 20% mannitol may be infused intravenously over 20 minutes, but this must not be repeated as there is a danger of inducing dangerous fluid and electrolyte imbalance. h. Correct hyperkalemia : hyperkalaemia is a silent killer in the recovery phase of Russell’s viper bite. i. Dialysis : dialysis is indicated in oliguria or anuria with rising blood urea or creatinine, fluid overload leading to pulmonary oedema, metabolic acidosis in addition to hyperkalaemia. Avoid : K+ containing fruits like coconut water, banana and fruit juice, NSAIDs, heparin. Monitor : Urine R/M/E, ECG and CPK, serum transaminases, blood urea and serum creatinine daily. Serum electrolytes, especially potassium, should be estimated 6 hourly in case of sea snake bite. Urine should be examined for microscopic hematuria and active sediments. Myoglobinaemia : Severe myalgia is suggestive of myotoxicity (rhabdomyolysis) of venom. In severe myotoxicity myoglobin appears in urine giving it a red colour. In mild cases urine may look normal and should be examined in laboratory for myoglobinuria. Myoglobin could damage renal tubules leading to acute renal failure. [Ref – Learner’s Guide for Management of Snakebite, DGHS, 2020-21, Page 24 & 25.]

treatment.title: Haematological abnormality after viper bite
treatment.description: Haematological abnormality after viper bite Haematological symptoms : Spontaneous bleeding from gum, vomiting of blood, haemoptysis, haematuria, persistent bleeding from bite site, venipuncture site and inflicted wound if any. Signs of haematological abnormalities : Persistent bleeding from bite site, venipuncture site and or inflicted wound if any, multiple bruise or large haematoma, haemorrhagic blisters, bleeding from gingival sulci, haemoptysis, haematemesis, hematuria, epistaxis. Management : 1. Strict bed rest to avoid even minor trauma; 2. Transfusion of clotting factors and platelets; ideally, fresh frozen plasma (FFP) and cryoprecipitate with platelet concentrates / platelet aphaeresis or, if these are not available, fresh whole blood. 3. Intramuscular injections should be avoided. Detect coagulopathy in viper bites by performing the 20 WBCT 2 hourly during the first 6 hours and then 6 hourly. Coagulation profile should be checked 6 hourly (especially for viper bites). Platelet count should be estimated twice daily. CAUTION : Delay the release of tight bands, bandages and ligatures : if the patient has already applied these, they should not be released until the patient is under medical care in hospital, medical staff and resuscitation facilities are available and antivenom treatment (if indicated) has been started. [Ref – Learner’s Guide for Management of Snakebite, DGHS, 2020-21, Page 25]

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Tetrodotoxin (TTX) / Puffer fish Poisoning Some sea fish (puffer fish, porcupine fish, sun fish, trigger fish), blue ring octopuses, rough skinned newts, moon snails etc. contains neurotoxins called tetrodotoxin. Management : ► Stomach wash with in 1 hour with 2% sodibicarb (NaHCO3) & Tab. Ultracarbon / Activated charcoal (2+2+2) is useful. Purgation and forced diuresis to lessen absorbed poison is not recommended. ► Clearing of the airways & O 2 inhalation 4-6 L/minute. Artificial respiration with oxygen inhalation by mask in mild cases and direct ventilatory support and sedation in severe cases. ► If NPO, then fluid support. Infusion 5% DNS 1000cc + 5% DA 1000 cc IV @ 20 drops/ minute. ► Inj. omeprazole (40mg), 1 vial IV slowly over 10 minutes stat and once or twice daily. ► If symptoms are like food poisoning (vomiting + diarrhoea) then treat as a patient of diarrhea (Inf. C/S 1 – 3 L). ► Atropinization and inj. neostigmine has been used for come round from unconsciousness and restoring neurogenic power both sensory and motor. Tx : unconscious or paralysis then, ● Inj. Atropine 0.6mg/ml / Atropin, 2 amp I/V stat & 4 hourly. After 15 min, ● Inj. Neostigmine 500 mcg / ml / Neostig, 1 amp S/C stat & 4 hourly. Until neurotoxic features improve. ► There is no specific antidote, so only symptomatic treatment. Steroids may need as life saving measure although contraindication persists.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Bee / Wasp / Insect bite O / E : - Search for anaphylactic shock. (Anaphylactic shock → H ypotension + Stridor + Wheeze present). - BP & Pulse. - Bite marks / Local findings. - Neurologic findings. If simple or a few bee sting : ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Omegut (pop) / Seclo (sq) / Xeldrin (aci) / Proceptin (bex), 1 + 0 + 1 ( ½ hr B/M) ---- 3 – 5 days ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Etorac (incp) / Rolac (ren) / Torax (sq) / Xidoloc (bex) / Pair (drg), 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M) ---- 3 – 5 days Δ Bee or Wasp Sting or bite O/E : BP = Lung = Adv. : ▸ ECG. ▸ S. creatinine. Tx, ● Diet – Normal. ● Please monitor vital signs. ● Inj. Ketorolac 30mg/1ml / Rolac (ren), 1 amp I/M stat & BD (or TDS if severe) ● Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Omep (aris), 1 vial IV stat and BD. ● Inj. Hydrocortisone 100 mg / Cotson (ops), 1 vial IV stat & QDS. ● Inj. Pheniramine 45.5mg/2ml / Avil (syn), 1 amp IV stat & sos. ● Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg / Flupen (drg) / A-Flox (acm) / Flux (ops), 1+1+1+1 --- 7 days ± ● Tab. Fexofenadine 120 mg / Fenadin (ren) / Fexo (sq) / Fexofast (drg), 0+0+1 --- 7 days If pain isn't controlled yet : ● Inj. Nalbuphine 20mg/2ml / Nalbun-2 (incp), 1 amp IM stat & sos c̄ Inj Avil. Rx on Dc̄A (severe cases of sting) : ✔ Cap. Flucloxacillin / Phylopen DS 500mg, 1+1+1+1 – 7 days ✔ Tab. Fexofenadine / Telfast (syn) 120 mg, 0+0+1 – 7 days ✔ Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Todol (ops), 1+0+1 (A/M) – if pain present ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20mg / Opal (hc), 1+0+1 (B/M) – if taking ketorolac

treatment.title: Rabid animals
treatment.description: Reservoir / Source of Infection : Rabid canine animals include mostly Dog (95%); in very few cases other animals are Cat, Fox, Jackal, Mongoose, Monkey and other Wild-animals. ✔✔✔ Need vaccination incase of abrasion / bite – 1. Dog (কুকুর) 2. Cat (বিড়াল) 3. Fox (শিয়বল) 4. Monkey (বানর) 5. Bat (বাদুড়) 6. Mongoose (বেজি) XXX Need NO vaccination incase of abrasion / bite – 1. Human (মানুষ/বাচ্চা) 2. Rabbit (খরগোশ) 3. Squirrel (কাঠবিড়ালি) 4. Lizard (গুইসাপ) 5. Rat (ইঁদুর)

treatment.title: Types
treatment.description: Incubation Period : The incubation period varies from 5 days to several years (usually 2–3 months; rarely more than 1 year), Classic Rabies (furious form) Manifestations : Furious rabies accounts for approximately 80% of the total number of human cases; manifesting as signs of – • Delirium with alternating periods of calm i.e., Altered Behavior, Hallucinations, Incoordination. • Aerophobia. • Hydrophobia during attempts at drinking due to extremely painful laryngeal spasms. • Photophobia. • Excessive salivation, Aggression, Hyperactivity periods of agitation alternating with lucidity. • After a few days, the infection invariably leads to coma and death by cardio-respiratory arrest. Paralytic or Dumb Form (less common): An acute, ascending paralysis resembling Guillain-Barre syndrome predominates with initially relative sparing higher cortical functions. Paralytic (or dumb) rabies accounts for approximately 20% of the total number of human cases.

treatment.title: Pre-exposure prophylaxis
treatment.description: Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) is a course of rabies vaccination administered to protect a person from getting rabies before exposure to a rabid (or suspected rabid) animal/any other mode of exposure to the rabies virus. E.g., laboratory professionals and caregivers for infected dogs and humans. Pre-exposure : Inj. Anti-Rabies Vaccine (ARV) : ► Intradermal (I/D) : 0.1ml/ dose, 2 doses 2-sites in each day, on Day 0 and Day 7. ► Intramuscular (I/M) : 1 vial / 1 ml, 2 doses on Day 0 and Day 7 (1-site).

treatment.title: Post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP)
treatment.description: Table : Category of exposure/wound and recommended measures. Category I • Touching or feeding of animals. • Licks on intact skin. Wash the site with soap and water only. Category II • Minor scratches or abrasions without bleeding. • Wound management. • Anti-Rabies vaccine (ARV) as soon as possible on 1 st visit. Category III • Single or multiple transdermal bites or scratches with active bleeding. • Licks or broken skin, contamination of mucous membrane with saliva (i.e. licks). • Exposure to bats. • Wound management. • Administer Anti-Rabies vaccine (ARV) immediately. • Administer rabies immunoglobulin (RIG) and • Prophylactic antibiotics if required. • Anti-tetanus vaccination if required. [Ref – National Guideline for Animal Bite Management in Bangladesh 2021, 3rd Ed, CDC, DGHS, Page – 25]

treatment.title: Management of animal bite wound
treatment.description: Wound care : ► Should be immediate, is essential even if the person presents long after exposure. ► Wash thoroughly (immediate washing and flushing) with soap and running water for 15 minutes, followed by an iodine solution (aqueous solution). ► Keep wound open. ► Bleeding at any wound site indicates potentially severe exposure and must be infiltrated with either human or equine rabies immunoglobulin. ► Avoid using a bandage or stitch to the wound. Post Exposure Prophylaxis (PEP) by Cell Culture Vaccine (CCV) : ARV : 0.1 ml I/D injection at both right & left arm (Deltoid), 3 times (D0, D3, D7). Intradermal (ID) vaccine regimen : WHO recommended PEP vaccination schedules using the intradermal (ID) regimen : (D0, D3, D7, D14, D28) (2-2-2-0-0) Route : Intradermal (ID), Total dose: 0.6 ml. As per WHO 2018 guideline (intra-muscular) : (DO, D3, D7, D14, D28) (1-1-1-1-0) Route : Intramuscular regimen. Site : Deltoid or Anterolateral thigh ( ≤ 2 years). Dose : PVCV-0.5ml/inj or PCECV-1 ml/ inj. Caution: Reconstituated vaccine to be used within 8 hours. Discard after 8 hours of preparation of drugs. ► No problem with vaccination in case of the following condition – 1. Pregnancy. 2. Lactation. 3. Other illness. 4. Very young child. 5. Old age. Administration of Intradermal vaccine ( ≤ 15 ° angle). Administration of Intra-muscular vaccine (90° angle). Administration of RIG inside the border of the wound. Indication of RIG : ● All WHO-Category III exposures. ● Bites by all wild animals viz. by mongoose, jackal, fox etc. ● Category II exposures in immunocompromised / immunosuppressed individuals, including HIV infected people & AIDS patients.

treatment.title: RIG (ERIG/HRIG) & anaphylaxis
treatment.description: If RIG was not administered when the anti-Rabies vaccination started, it could be administered until the 7th (seventh) day after administering the first dose of the Anti-Rabies vaccine. Beyond the 7th (seventh) day, RIG is not indicated since an antibody response due to the anti-rabies vaccine is presumed to have occurred. As per WHO 2018 guideline : Equine RIG : 40 IU/kg body weight(maximum 3000 IU). Human RIG: 20 IU/kg body weight (maximum 1500 IU). Route : Local infiltration and IM. Site : Infiltration in & around the wound. Timing : Within 7 days of 1st vaccine dose (Day-0). Not to be used : After 7 days of 1st vaccine dose. Caution : Different site of AVR & RIG vaccine (IM/ID). Precaution : Skin test for ERIG (equine). Danger : Anaphylaxis. Rare if human RIG. [Ref – National Guideline for Animal Bite Management in Bangladesh 2021, 3rd Ed, CDC, DGHS, Page – 43] Tolerance and Side Effects : i. There may be transient tenderness/redness at the injection site. ii. Brief rise in body temperature. iii. Skin reactions are infrequent. iv. RIG must never be given intravenously since this could produce symptoms of shock, especially in patients with antibody deficiency syndromes. v. Serum sickness may occur in 1% to 6% of patients, usually 7 to 10 days after injection of ERIG. vi. Serum sickness is characterized by joint pain, proteinuria etc. It can be managed by non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAID) and H 2 blockers. As for all vaccinations, recipients should be kept under medical supervision for at least 15-20 minutes after vaccination. Management if anaphylactic reaction occurs : The patient should be hospitalized for 24-48 hours for observation and management by : i. Adrenaline : The dose is 0.5 ml of 0.1 percent solution (1 in 1000, 1mg/ml) for adults and 0.01 ml/kg body weight for children, injected intramuscularly (IM). ii. Inj Hydrocortisone : 100 mg stat. iii. Inj. Chlorpheniramine. iv. Inj. Ranitidine. v. Oxygen and others, as necessary management for anaphylaxis / shock. The patient is sensitive to ERIG, then HRIG should be used (if possible). A patient who had prior exposure to antisera (e.g., Anti-tetanus, serum, anti-diphtheria serum) should receive a subcutaneous dose of Inj. Adrenaline (the requirement will be half dose of that required for treatment for anaphylaxis). [Ref – National Guideline for Animal Bite Management in Bangladesh 2021, 3rd Ed, CDC, DGHS, Page – 29 & 30]

treatment.title: WHO গাইডলাইন
treatment.description: কুকুর / প্রাণির কামড়ে আক্রান্ত হলে করণীয় : বিশ্ব স্বাস্থ্য সংস্থার (WHO) গাইডলাইন অনুযায়ী যত দ্রুত সম্ভব চিকিৎসা নিতে হবে। ক্ষতের বিবরণ ক্ষতের ধরণ চিকিৎসা ক্যাটাগরি-১ অক্ষত চামড়ায় লেহন, প্রাণির সংস্পর্শ, কিন্তু কোন আঁচড় / ক্ষত নাই। সকল ক্যাটাগরিতে অতিদ্রুত কাপড় কাঁচার সাবান / ডিটারজেন্ট ও প্রবাহমান পানি দিয়ে ১৫ মিনিট ধরে ক্ষত স্থান ধৌত করতে হবে। কোন টিকা দিতে হবে না। ক্যাটাগরি-২ আঁচড় / ক্ষত আছে কিন্তু রক্তক্ষরণ নাই। Inj. Anti Rabies Vaccine (ARV) ক্যাটাগরি-৩ ক্ষত ও রক্তক্ষরণ আছে। Inj. ARV & Inj. RIG ► Inj. ARV : Dose of Inj. ARV : প্রতি ডোজে 0.1 মিলি (ইনসুলিন সিরিজ্ঞের 10 U) × দুই বাহুতে; চামড়ার মধ্যে (Intra-dermally), ডোজ- D0, D3 &D7 ► Inj. RIG : Dose of Inj. RIG ক্ষতস্থানে (Infiltration) (40 IU / kg Body Weight, maximum 3000 IU) , ডোজ- D0.

treatment.title: Re-Exposure
treatment.description: Re-Exposure (Rabies PEP of previously vaccinated persons) : According to WHO Guideline, If a rabid dog bites a previously immunized person within 3 months of completing an entire course of PEP or PrEP, no new course of PEP is required. For exposures occurring more than 3 months after a PEP or PrEP course, treated as Re - Exposure Prophylaxis for previously immunized individuals : 0.1 ml ARV Intradermal (1 Site) 2 times only (D0 & D3) Or, Entire vial IM deltoid (1 Site) 2 times only (D0 & D3) (Site may anterolateral thigh, if (1 vial = 1 ml or 0.5 ml depending on vaccine type) No IG need if previously vaccinated within 5 years. No RIG is indicated for any of the schedules if reliable vaccination history is available (with Card).

treatment.title: Antibiotics
treatment.description: Line of treatment with systemic antibiotics recommended for animal bites management : Adult : First-line : • Amoxicillin/clavulanate, 500/125 mg every 8 hours for 7-10 days according to severity. Alternatives : • Clindamycin, 300 mg 3 times per day, Plus, Ciprofloxacin, 500 mg twice per day, 7-10 days according to severity. • Doxycycline, 100 mg twice per day, 7-10 days according to the severity. • Cefuroxime Axetil 500 mg twice per day, Plus, metronidazole, 250 to 500 mg 4 times per day, 7-10 days according to the severity. Child : First-line : • Amoxicillin / clavulanate, 25 to 45 mg per kg divided every 8 hours, 7-10 days according to the severity. Alternative : • Clindamycin, 10 to 25 mg per kg divided every 6 to 8 hours, Plus, trimethoprim / sulfamethoxazole, 8 to 10 mg per kg (trimethoprim component) divided every 12 hours, 7-10 days according to the severity. Pregnant : • Amoxicillin (500 mg) 8-hourly with Flucloxacillin (500mg) x 6 hourly, 7-10 days according to the severity. Local antibiotics : 2% Mupirocin topical antibacterial skin preparations [Bactrocin (sq) / Bactrobex (bex)] ointment, 7-10 days according to the severity.

treatment.title: Rabies (ICU support)
treatment.description: Flow chart for management of rabies patient : Admit the patient immediately (ICU support). ↓ Transfer the patient to Rabies ward. ↓ Keep the patient in a calm, quiet, semi-dark room. ↓ Assess status of the patient with history and physical examination. ↓ Open an I/V channel by cannula with Infusion of 5% DNS (1000 ml & sos) ↓ Restrain the patient to bed if the patient is aggressive and violent ↓ Ensure control of seizure with Diazepam. Adults: 1-2 ampoule/IV. Children: 0.2-0.3 mg/kg/dose per rectal. ↓ Sedation with diazepam 10 mg 4-6 hourly. Chlorpromazine 50-100 mg if necessary. ↓ Do nasopharyngeal / oropharyngeal suction, if necessary. ↓ Oxygen inhalation- SOS. ↓ Introduce nasogastric tube and Foley's catheter. ↓ Ensure minimum handling of the patient. ↓ Change posture 2 hourly to avoid bed sore, if necessary. ↓ Maintain nutrition by I/V fluid or Nasogastric tube feeding or gastrostomy. ↓ Maintenance of input/output chart. ↓ Recording of vital signs (pulse, blood pressure, temperature and respiration). ↓ Broad-spectrum antibiotics to be given (Inj. Ceftriaxone) I/V • Adult : 2 gm 12hourly • Children : 100-200mg/kg/ day divided 12 hrly ↓ Ensure standard safety precautions with barrier nursing. Other Symptomatic management as necessary. [Ref – National Guideline for Animal Bite Management in Bangladesh 2021, 3rd Ed, CDC, DGHS, Page – 40]

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Tetanus (Refer for hospitalization) ● H/O injury e.g. RTA, cut injury, needle pricking etc. Symptoms first appear from 2 days to several weeks after injury which causes difficulty in opening the mouth and in masticating. ● Rigidity spreads to involve the muscles of the face, neck and trunk. ● The back is usually slightly arched ('opisthotonus') and there is a board-like abdominal wall. ● In the more severe cases, violent spasms lasting for a few seconds to 3-4 minutes occur spontaneously, or may be induced by stimuli such as movement or noise. Management : 1. Nurse in a quite-calm room. 2. Avoid unnecessary stimuli like noise, light. 3. O 2 inhalation stat 4-6 L/minute. 4. Change the posture 2 hourly. 5. NG tube feeding 200 ml 2 hourly in sitting posture and maintain the posture for 10-15 minutes. 6. Infusion 5% DNS 1000 cc IV daily 7. Inj. benzylpenicillin 600 mg IV 6 hourly or Inj. Metronidazole if allergic to penicillin. 8. Inj. Human Tetanus Immunoglobulin (TIG) 250 IU/ml / Inj. Tetagam-P (csl) / Protet-IG (incp) 12 to 24 amp IM stat. [Inj. Vaxitet-IG (incp) Tetanus Antitoxin (Equine) 15 – 30 amp] 9. Inj. Diazepam (10 mg) 1 amp IV stat & BD. Prevention of tetanus : ○ In all contaminated wounds, cleansing, dressing & appropriate antibiotic. All dirty contaminated wound, category III animal bite requires both TT & TIG. ○ For those already immunized, only a booster dose of toxoid is required. ○ Tetanus prophylaxis for dirty (soil/cow dung contaminated) wound or puncture wound by rusty nail or burn or deep/infected wound. Indicated in adults and children ≥ 7 years (as children are vaccinated against tetanus as per EPI schedule). TT : ► Inj. Tetanus toxoid 40 IU/0.5 ml / TT vax (pop) / Vaxitet (incp) (95 /-), 1 vial I/M stat on left arm or on buttock. TIG : ► Inj. Human Tetanus Immunoglobulin 250 IU/ml / Protet-IG (incp) (975/-) / Tetagam-P (ial) (1300/-), 1 vial (or 2 vial, if severe) I/M stat on right arm or on alternate buttock. Or, ► Inj. Tetanus Antitoxin [Equine] 3000 IU/ml, Inj. Vaxitet-IG (incp) (408/-), 1 vial (or 2 vial, if severe) I/M stat on right arm or on alternate buttock. IM Injection may be given on right arm instead of Human Tetanus Immunoglobulin 250 IU/ml / Protet-IG (incp). But Protet-IG is of human origin and vaxitet-IG is of horse origin. Hypersensitive reactions may occur after the injection of any serum of animal origin & must not be given in pregnancy. In case of extensive burns, it is advisable to administer a second injection of 250 IU human tetanus immunoglobulin after the exudative phase of the burn has subsided (about 36 hours after onset of the burn).

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : Fever high grade in the first day, intensity of the fever gradually decreases; associated with generalized bodyache, sore throat, rhinorrhea (runny nose) , appetite may be normal or slightly reduce. On examination -temperature is raised, patient otherwise normal. Investigation – diagnosis is clinical, but for exclusion of other infection investigation may be done usually if fever persists after 3 days. CBC-usually normal, CXR-normal, urine R/E-normal. Management 1. Paracetamol 500 mg 6 to 8 hourly to reduce temperature. 2. Antihistamine if rhinorrhoea. 3. If sore throat-povidone iodine mouth wash (10 ml mixed in half glass warm water, then gargling 4 times daily for 7 days). 4. Tepid sponging.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : Fever, malaise, cough, runny nose. Treatment : ► Tab. P/C 665 mg / Longpara (ibn) / Xpa XR (aris), 1+1+1+1 (A/M) –– till fever subsides. ► Tab. Fexofenadine / Fexofast (drg) / Fexo (sq) 120 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ––– 5 – 10 days If present within 48 hours of onset : ± ► Cap. Oseltamivir / Tamiflu (roche, 232/-) / Oseflu (bex, 150/-) / Flutavir (incp. 150/-) 75 mg, 1+0+1 – 5 days. ► If bacterial superinfection : Antibiotic accordingly. Advice : Bed rest during acute phase, Annual vaccination of elderly, children of 2-7 years, chronic cardiopulmonary disease, immunocompromised and health workers.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Main symptoms : 1. Fever 2. Cough 3. Dyspnoea 4. Loss of taste or smell 5. Hailing from endemic area. Other symptoms : 1. Myalgia 2. Fatigue 3. Anorexia 4. Headache 5. Ageusia 6. Diarrhoea 7. Vomiting 8. Rhinorrhoea 9. Sore throat 10. Red eye 11. Abdominal pain 12. Skin lesion (chilblain, nodule) etc. Classification of COVID diagnoses : Asymptomatic > Mild > Moderate > Severe > Critical. Trait Mild / ILI Moderate Severe SpO 2 ≥ 95 % 90 – 93 % RR Normal > 30 b/m Pneumonia No Yes Yes (Severe) ► Asymptomatic : No symptoms but test positive. ► Mild case : In¬fluenza Like Illness (ILI). ► Any mild case with radiological evidence (CXR / HRCT) is a moderate case / COVID pneumonia. ► Pregnant women with COVID-19 should be considered as a severe case if the saturation level is below 94%. ► Critical Case : Severe COVID-19 case meeting any of the following criteria : - Respiratory failure and requiring mechanical ventilation - Sepsis - Septic shock - ARDS - Any organ failure that requires ICU care ► Monitor oxygen saturation (SpO2) at two sets of time – ● At rest and ● Minimum exertion such as walking for 3-6 minutes. RAT = Rapid Antigen Test.

treatment.title: Asymptomatic / Mild Cases
treatment.description: Asymptomatic / Mild Cases : SpO2 ≥ 95% c̄ No Co-morbidity No treatment needed.
