Only give suggestions / advices. উপদেশ / নির্দেশাবলি : ১। উপসর্গ থাকলে এবং পূর্বে করোনা পরীক্ষা না করে থাকলে নিকটস্থ হাসপাতালে থেকে করোনা পরীক্ষা করবেন। ২। বাসাতে চৌদ্দ (১৪) দিন হোম আইসোলেশেনে বাসায় থাকবেন - ● পরিবারের অন্যান্য সদস্যদের থেকেও সামাজিক দূরত্ব বজায় রাখুন। ● সম্ভব হলে রোগী পৃথক ঘর ও বাথরুম ব্যবহার করবেন। ● রোগীর সাথে দেখা করার সময় রোগী ও সেবাদানকারী মুখে উভয়ই মাক্স পরবেন। ● নূন্যতম ২০ সেকেন্ড ধরে ঘন ঘন হাত ধুবেন বা সেনিটাইজ করবেন। ● হাচিঁ কাশি দেওয়ার সময় অবশ্যই টিস্যু বা কুনইয়ের ভাজ ব্যবহার করবেন। ৩। পালস অক্সিমিটারের মাধ্যমে প্রতিদিন একবার আঙ্গুলে লাগিয়ে অক্সিজেন মাত্রা (SpO 2 ) দেখবেন। ► যদি ৯৫% বা তার বেশি থাকে তবে বাসাতেই ২১ / ১৪ দিন হোম আইসোলেশেনে থাকবেন। এবং টেলিমেডিসিনের মাধ্যমে চিকিৎসা গ্রহণ করুন। ► যদি ৯৪% বা তার কম থাকে তবে পনেরো মিনিট পর পর দুইবার অক্সিজেন মাত্রা (SpO 2 ) দেখবেন। যদি পুনরায় প্রতিবার ৯৪% বা তার কম থাকে তবে অতিসত্ত ¡ র নিকটস্থ অক্সিজেন সমৃদ্ধ হাসপাতালে ভর্তি হবেন। ৪। নিয়মিত হাল্কা হাঁটাহাঁটি ও শ্বাসতন্ত্রের ব্যায়াম (যেমন বেলুন ফোলানোর ও পেট ফুলিয়ে শ্বাস নিয়ে পাঁচ সেকেন্ড ধরে রাখার চেষ্টা করবেন)। অথবা তিনটি বলযুক্ত স্পাইরোমিটার বা রেসপিরোমিটার দিয়ে নিয়মিত ব্রিদিং একসারসাইজ করবেন।

treatment.title: ব্রিদিং একসারসাইজ
treatment.description: ব্রিদিং একসারসাইজ ২) মাউথপিসে বা পাইপে শ্বাস টেনে বলগুলো তোলার চেষ্টা করবেন। যতটি বল পারবেন তুলে কিছুক্ষণ ধরে হবে। একটি বা দুটি বা তিনটি যতটি বল এবং যতক্ষণ পারবেন তুলে ধরে রাখতে হবে। ২) যন্ত্রটিকে উল্টিয়ে মাউথপিসে ফু দিয়ে হবে অর্থাৎ শ্বাস ছাড়তে হবে। এইক্ষেত্রে একটি বা দুটি বল (তিনটি বল উঠানো কঠিন) উঠিয়ে কিছুক্ষণ ধরে রাখার চেষ্টা করতে হবে। ৩) এইভাবে দিনে যতবার পারবেন এবং যতক্ষণ পারবেন, ব্যায়ামগুলো করবেন। ৪) হৃদরোগ বা অতিরিক্ত শ্বাসকষ্ট বা বুক ব্যাথা থাকলে রোগী অবশ্যই চিকিৎসকের পরামর্শ অনুযায়ী বিদিং এক্সারসাইজটি করবেন।

treatment.title: Home Treatment c̄ Discussion
treatment.description: Home Treatment for Mild / ILI Case Ø Tab. Paracetamol / Napa (bex) 500 mg, ১+১+১ বা ২+২+২ (জ্বর থাকলে খাবেন) ...... ৩ দিন (জ্বর না কমলে উষ্ম গরম পানি দিয়ে বুক পিঠ ও গলা বাদ দিয়ে বারবার গা মুছ করবেন।) Ø Cap. Esomeprazole / Tab. Othera (aci) 20 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (আহারের আধাঘন্টা পূর্বে) ...... ৭ দিন Ø Syp. Butamirate / Mirakof (sq) / Bukof (bacn), ৩ চামচ দিনে ৪ বার (কাঁশি থাকলে খাবেন) ...... ৫ দিন Alternate Antitussive – Ø Inh. Budesonide 200 mcg / Aeronid (bex), ২ চাপ দিনে ২ বার (কাঁশি থাকলে নিবেন) ....... ৭ দিন Ø Tab. Acetylcysteine 600 mg / Mucomist DT (bex) / Alistin DT (ops) / Viscotin (uni), ১+০+১ / ১+১+১ ....... ৭ দিন ► Steroid : There is no role of systemic steroid in mild cases. Rather it may be harmful if given during the viremic phase of the disease especially in the first 7 days. ► Antibiotic : there is no role of antibiotic in COVID-19 infection. ► Mild case with controlled comorbidities – such as DM, HTN, IHD, Asthma / COPD / ILD, CKD, CLD, Malignancy, Pregnancy, Obesity can be managed at home. However, patient should be carefully monitored at home with finger pulse oximetry and be watchful about danger signs, example- Breathing difficulties, chest pain, light headedness, disorientation, extreme weakness which results in even difficulties in walking and drop in oxygen saturation to ≤ 93% etc. ► Mild case with multiple uncontrolled comorbid conditions such as HTN, DM, IHD, CKD, CLD, COPD/Asthma/ILD and prothrombotic state such as high risk-pregnancy and active malignancy etc. should receive thrombo-prophylaxis along with symptomatic management and should be admitted. Add - Ø Inj. Enoxaparin / Cleven 40 mg / 60 mg, 1 PFS S/C stat & OD at different sites of body. Then, Ø Tab. Rivaroxaban / Rivarox / Kinexa 10 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ..... ৩০ দিন ► Inv. : No routine investigations are required for mild cases. Discontinuing of transmission-based precaution including isolation and return to workplace criteria : ● For asymptomatic patients : 10 days after sample collection. ● For symptomatic Mild patients : 14 days after symptom onset provided patient has no fever and decreased respiratory symptoms for 03 days. ● For hospitalized patients : 21 days after symptom onset provided patient has no fever and decreased respiratory symptoms for 03 days.

treatment.title: General Discussion
treatment.description: General Discussion Treatment on Admission Date : Time : Date ● Diet : Low Carbohydrate diet. Please follow the only one marked drugs ● O 2 inhalation stat. (according to need, target SpO 2 is ≥ 94-90%). ● Maintain awake prone position for 4-6 hr/day. Lateral & propped up position. ● Please monitor RBS, SpO 2 & other vitals daily. ● Tab. P/C / Napa 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 (A/M) & 1 tab sos if temp ≥ 99°F. ● Supp. Clofenac / Voltalin 50 mg, 1 stick P/R sos, if temp ≥ 101°F. + ¤ Inj. Enoxaparin / Cleven 40 mg / 60 mg, 1 PFS S/C stat & OD at different sites of body + Antibiotic : Give any one : There is no role of antibiotic in COVID-19 infection. ¤ Cap. Doxycycline / Doxicap 100 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ¤ Tab. Amoxicillin + Clavulanic Acid / Tab. Moxaclav 375 / 625 mg , 1 + 1 + 1 ¤ Cap. Cefixime / Triocim 200 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Aciphin / Exephin 1 g 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ¤ Tab. Moxifloxacin/Moxigen 400 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ¤ Inj. Moxifloxacin / Iventi / Moxibac 400 mg, 1 bottle I/V daily. ¤ Inj. Meropenem / Merocil / Neopenem 1 g, 1 vial I/V stat & TDS. Oral antibiotic in moderate case & IV in severe be given if bacterial infection is suspected. + (Any one) Steroid : Give any one (Discussed below) ¤ Tab. Dexamethasone / Sonexa 4 mg, 0 + 1 ½ + 0 (A/M) ¤ Inj. Dexamethasone 6 mg --- 1 amp I/V OD. ¤ Inj. Dexamethasone 5mg / ml, 1 amp I/V stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Methylprednisolone 250 mg / 500 mg mix with 100 ml Inf. N/S (Solo) I/V @ 30 d/m once daily (OD). + PPI is a must as steroid is used. ¤ Cap. Omeprazole/Othera 20 mg 1+0+1 (B/M) ¤ Inj. Esomeprazole / Omeprazole / Exium 40 mg, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ± Anti-viral (if indicated) : ¤ Inj. Remdesivir 100 mg, 2 vial (200 mg) + Inf. N/S 100 ml, I/V @ 25 d/m on day 1. Then, 1 vial (100 mg) + Inf. N/S 100 ml, I/V @ 25 d/m on day 2-5. ¤ Tab. Baricitinib / Baritor / Baricent / Barinez 2 mg, 0 + 2 + 0 + (Any one/two) Use any one Anti-tussive. If H/O Atopy / Allergy (Any one) ¤ Tab. Doxofylline / Unifylin 200 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ¤ Syp. Butamirate / Bukof / Mirakof, 3 TSF QDS. ¤ Tab. Acetylcysteine 600 mg / Mucomist DT / Tab. Alistin DT, 1 + 0 + 1 ¤ Inh. Budesonide / Aeronid 200 μ g, 2 puff BD. ¤ Inh. Symbion / Pulmicort-F 160/4.5 μ g, 1 puff BD. ¤ Tab. Montelukast / Telukast 10 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ¤ Tab. Fexofenadine / Fixal 120 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 + (Any one) Vitamins & minerals are not a must but may help in ↑ Appetite & boost immune response. Any one ¤ Tab. Zinc / Xinc B / Ceevit 250 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ¤ Tab. Multivitamin / Lysivin, 1 + 0 + 1 ¤ Tab. Multivitamin / Aristovit M / Bextrum Gold / Nutrum Gold / Bextrum Silver (> 50 yrs), 0+1+0 (A/M) ¤ Tab. Calcium + D 3 / Ovocal-D, 0+1+0 (A/M) ¤ Cap. Vit D 3 / Cholecalciferol / D-Balance / D-Rise / D-Gain 40000IU, 1 cap weekly (on Friday) for 7 weeks. ± Use according to need. If restless. ¤ Inj. Halopid 5 mg/ml, 1 amp I/M sos. ¤ Inj. Perkinil 10 mg/2ml, ½ amp I/M sos with Halopid In ICU : ¤ Inj. Midazolam 50 mg / 50 mL, 1 ml/hr I/V through syringe pump. ¤ Tab. Midazolam / Dormitrol 7.5 mg, 1 tab sos. ¤ Tab. Clonazepam / Pase 0.5 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ¤ Tab. Lorazepam 1 mg / Lozicum, 0 + 0 + 1 ► Antiviral : Remdesivir should be used only if all the following criteria meet – ● Suspected or laboratory confirmed COVID case. ● Adult and pediatric patients (>12 years old). ● Within 10 days of symptom onset. ● Hypoxic (SpO 2 ≤ 93%). ► Baricitinib : In hypoxic patients where steroid cannot be used, the JAK inhibitor Baricitinib may be added at doses of 4 mg once daily for 14 days. ­It should be given as add on to Remdesivir. There is no room for using Baricitinib alone. ► Moderate Case : Steroid should be given in patients having respiratory distress and becoming hypoxic SpO 2 ≤ 93%. Dosing : Dexamethasone (0.5 mg): 12 tablets daily for adults Or, Dexamethasone (4 mg /Tab. Sonexa 4 mg): 1 ½ tablets daily. ► Severe case : Steroid : ● Inj. Dexamethasone 6 mg once daily for 7 to 10 days. ► Dose of Steroid in Other COVID-19 Syndromes : ARDS due to COVID : Inj. Methylprednisolone 1-2 mg/kg q12 OR, Inj. Dexamethasone 20 mg IV daily for 5 days & then 10 mg IV daily for 5 days & then 5 mg IV daily for 5 days. Refractory Sepsis : Hydrocortisone 50 mg IV 6 hourly. Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS) : Inj. Methylprednisolone 1-2 mg/kg q12 OR Inj. Dexamethasone 10 mg q6. ► Must note the starting date (SF) of antibiotic, steroid, remdesivir, baricitinib to calculate the exact date of end of that drug therapy. ► Routine investigation (for Moderate / Severe case) : ● CBC, ● S. ferritin, ● CRP, ● S. creatinine, ● RBS, ● S. ALT, ● D-dimer, ● CXR P/A view. Other labs or imaging as per treating clinician’s decision. HRCT of chest should not be routinely advised. It can only help in diagnostic dilemma. ­There is no role HRCT scan of chest in the management of COVID-19. ► Laboratory features associated with severe COVID-19 : Investigation Normal Range In COVID (usually) • D-dimer > 1000 ng/mL • CRP > 100 mg/L • LDH 110 to 210 units/L > 245 units/L • Ferritin males 30-300 mcg/L; females 10-200 mcg/L; > 500 mcg/L • CPK 40 to 150 units/L >2× upper limit of normal • Absolute lymphocyte count Normal range for age ≥ 21 years : 1800 – 7700/microL ► Tocilizumab : It should not be routinely used to manage severe to critical COVID-19. It can only be used under consultant’s supervision only if following clinical and biochemical criteria are fulfilled. Indication : A) Clinical Criteria : Patients (Adult ≥ 18 years) who have been admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU) within the last 24 hours and who require invasive mechanical ventilation, noninvasive mechanical ventilation (NIV), or high flow nasal cannula (HFNC) oxygen (needing >30 L/min of oxygen and FiO2 > 0.4). B) Biochemical criteria : CRP > 75 mg/dl. Dosing : Consider only 1 dose at 8 mg/kg (max: 800 mg/dose). Can be repeated after 12-24 hours if no clinical improvement. Max 2 doses. Contraindication : 1. Significant immunosuppression, particularly in those with a H/O recent use of other biologic immuno-modulating drugs. 2. Alanine transaminase (ALT) >5 times the upper limit of normal. 3. High risk for gastrointestinal perforation. 4. An uncontrolled, serious bacterial, fungal, or non-SARS-CoV-2 viral infection. 5. Absolute neutrophil count 6. Platelet count Writing an order : ¤ Inj. Tocilizumab / Actemra (roche) 480 mg + mix with 100 mL Inf. N/S, I/V @ 25 d/m stat. [ ► Inj. Actemra (roche) is available as 400 mg & 80 mg. A 60 Kg adult needs 60×8 = 480 mg, = 1 vial of 400 mg + 1 vial of 80 mg, = 6 vial of 80 mg.] ► Convalescent Plasma therapy : Till date, there is no quality evidence to recommend the use of convalescent plasma in severe COVID 19 cases for treatment purpose. ► Ivermectin : Till date, there is no quality evidence suggesting any role of Ivermectin in treating COVID-19 in any grade of severity or preventing COVID-19. It can only be used in context of clinical trial. ► Aerosol-generating procedures (AGPs) : ○ Bronchoscopy ○ Open suctioning ○ Nebulizer treatment ○ Manual ventilation ○ Disconnecting a patient from a ventilator ○ T piece trial ○ Bronchoscopy ○ Noninvasive positive pressure ventilation (NIPPV) ○ Tracheostomy ○ Cardiopulmonary resuscitation ► Discharge Criteria : Resolution of fever without the use of anti-pyretics such as paracetamol and significant improvement in the respiratory symptoms (e.g., cough, shortness of breath) for at least 03 days. There is no need for retesting the patient to give discharge from hospital. For severe or critical patients – physician’s discretion may be used for giving discharge.

treatment.title: Oxygen Management
treatment.description: OXYGEN MANAGEMENT Sequence of oxygen delivery devices used in the management of COVID-19. The most effective treatment of COVID pneumonia (moderate to severe) is – 1. Oxygen. 2. Anticoagulant – LMWH. 3. Steroid. Goal of therapy : ● PaO 2 /FiO 2 ratio > 150 ● PaO 2 > 55 mm hg ● SpO 2 > 88% ● pH > 7.30 ● Plateau Pressure 2 O ● Driving Pressure (plateau pressure-PEEP) ► Target SpO 2 is 94% during initial resuscitation and 90% for stable patients. For pregnant patients and patients with other organ failure target SpO 2 is 94%. Target SpO 2 : 88 % COPD 90 % For stable patient 92 - 94 % During initial resuscitation 94% Pregnant & Organ failure patient Variable Oxygen Devices : 1) Nasal Cannula 2) Medium Concentration O 2 /Simple Face/Hudson Mask 3) Non Rebreathing Mask or High Concentration Mask Fixed oxygen Concentration Device or High Flow Devices 1) Oxygen Through Venturi Masks. 2) High Flow Nasal Cannula (HFNC). 3) CPAP Devices. 4) BiPAP Devices. Variable Oxygen Devices : 1) Nasal Cannula : Deliver oxygen with very low flow rate (1 to 6 litre up to 45% oxygen). FiO 2 increases 4-5% for each liter of flow above environmental O 2 conc (21%). 2) Medium Concentration O 2 / Simple Face / Hudson Mask : Can give 5-10 liters oxygen through this device (30-50% in adult). 3) Non Rebreathing Mask or High Concentration Mask : Can give 10-15 liters oxygen through this device up to 80% FiO 2 . Fixed oxygen Concentration Device Or, High Flow Devices 1) Oxygen Through Venturi Masks : These are more applicable for COPD patients. 2) High Flow Nasal Cannula (HFNC). 3) Non-Invasive Ventilation (NIV) : ● CPAP Devices. ● BiPAP Devices. Respiratory care pathway for COVID 19 patients with acute hypoxemic respiratory failure Oxygenation targets : ● SpO 2 between 92% to 96% with the lowest possible FiO 2 . ● Oxygenation targets for most critically ill patient is SpO 2 >90%. ● Target is lower for patients with acute hypercapnic respiratory failure from COPD and higher in pregnancy. ● Consider awake proning. Start supplemental oxygenation with low flow system via nasal cannula. If no improvement in oxygenation ↓ Give higher flow oxygen by – ● Simple face mask: up to 10 LPM and provide up to 60% FiO 2 . ● Venturi Mask: up to 15 LPM and provide up to 60% FiO 2 . ● Partial rebreather mask: up to 15 LPM and provide up to 70% FiO 2 . ● Non rebreather mask: up to 15 LPM and provide up to 95% FiO 2 . N.B. Nebulized therapy should be avoided, use MDI if needed If no improvement in oxygenation ↓ High flow nasal cannula (HFNC) ▪ HFNC Can provide upto 100% FiO 2 with 70L/min flow. ▪ HFNC is generally preferred over NIV, except for acute hypercapnea due to COPD exacerbation and acute cardiogenic pulmonary edema. If no improvement in oxygenation ↓ Non-invasive ventilation (NIV) [BiPAP] If no improvement in oxygenation ↓ Invasive ventilation / Intubation and mechanical ventilation.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment sheet proforma for Severe COVID Pneumonia (SpO2 ≤ 90%) or, Moderate Cases / Pneumonia (SpO2 ≤ 93%) Diagnostic tools : H/O : – Fever for 7 days (Note it is ≥ 10 days or not). – Respiratory distress / SOB (shortness of breath) for 5 days. – Cough for S/D. O/G/E : - SpO2 : ____% with O2 or, ____% without O2. - Pulse (P). - Respiratory rate (RR). - BP. - Anemia (-) / (+). - Jaundice (-) / (+). - Oedema (-) / (+). R/F : - DM - HTN - BA (H/O Atopy / Allergy) Clinical Diagnosis : ● Suspected / Positive (RT PCR / RAT) ● Mild / Moderate / Severe / Critical ● COVID (with / c ̅ _____) ● DM / HTN / CKD Δ COVID Pneumonia Adv. : ▸ Rapid antigen test (RAT) for covid Or, ▸ PCR for covid ▸ CXR P/A view ▸ CBC ▸ CRP ▸ RBS ▸ S. Creatinine ▸ S. D-dimer ▸ S. LDH ▸ S. Ferritin ▸ S. CPK Plan : ▸ HRCT of chest Tx, ¤ Diet : Normal/Diabetic/Low carbohydrate. ¤ O 2 inhalation stat (according to need). ¤ Awake proning 4-6 hours/day. ¤ Inj. Enoxaparin / Cleven (bex) / Cardinex (drg) 40 mg, 1 PFS S/C A/U stat & OD ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Arixon (bex) / Ceftron (sq) / Trizon (acm) 1g. 1 vial IV stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Remdesivir 100 mg or (5 mg/ml) / Pandovir (bacn) / Remivir (skf), 2 vial (200 mg) + Inf. N/S 100 ml, I/V @ 25 d/m on day-1. Then, 1 vial (100 mg) + Inf. N/S 100 ml, I/V @ 25 d/m on day 2-5. ¤ Tab. Baricitinib / Baritor (sq) / Barinez (bacn) / Baricent (incp) 2 mg, 0 + 2 + 0. ¤ Inj. Dexamethasone / Dexa (ren) / Roxadex (nuv) 6 mg, 1 amp I/V stat & OD. ¤ Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / PPI (acm) / Seclo (sq) / Losectil (skf), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ¤ Tab. Doxofylline / Unifylin (gen) / Doxiva (nip) / Docopa (aris) 200 mg, 1 + 0 + 1. ¤ Tab. Clonazepam / Disopan (incp) / Clonatril (hc) / pase (ops) 0.5 mg, 0 + 0 + 1. ¤ Tab. P/C / Renova (ops) / Reset (incp) 500 mg, 1+1+1 or, 1+1+1+1 or, 2+2+2 (A/M).

treatment.title: Explanation & Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Explanation : Anti-coagulation : ╠ Inj. Enoxaparin must be injected S/C at different sites of body. Only around umbilicus (A/U) can cause local hematoma though A/U is the preferred site as absorbs rapidly. ╠ Thromboprophylaxis should be given till discharge or for 7 days whichever is longer and followed by Tab. Rivaroxaban 10 mg once daily Or Tab Apixaban 2.5 mg twice daily for 1 month (especially patients at high risk for thromboembolism and based on clinician’s discretion). ╠ Thromboprophylaxis in moderate case : ● LMWH (Enoxaparin) at standard dose of 40 mg SC OD. ● For obese patients (BMI > 40) LMWH, 40 mg SC BD. ● If CrCl ╠ Anticoagulation in severe case : Initiate Thromboprophylaxis (LMWH) in standard doses of 1mg/kg/day and assess the bleeding risk. ╠ If LMWH / Enoxaparin cannot be given or contraindicated, use unfractionated heparin (UFH). Dose of unfractionated heparin : 5000unit SC BD. ╠ Antibiotic : There is no role of antibiotic in COVID-19 infection. Oral antibiotic (in moderate case) such as Amoxicillin Clavulanic acid or Doxycycline may be given if bacterial infection is suspected. Injectable may be given in hospitalized patient. ╠ Steroid should be given in patients having respiratory distress and becoming hypoxic SPO2 ≤ 93%. Dexamethasone has more potent anti-inflammatory property than any other steroid. Dosing : Dexamethasone (0.5 mg): 12 tablets daily for adults in (in moderate case) & Inj. Dexamethasone 6 mg once daily for 7 to 10 days (in severe case). ╠ There is no role of systemic steroid in mild cases (SPO2 ≥ 95%). Rather it may be harmful if given during the viremic phase of the disease especially in the first 7 days. ╠ Oral or injectable PPI (Omeprazole / Esomeprazole) must be given to patient with steroid therapy. ╠ Antiviral : Remdesivir should be used only if all the following criteria meet – ● Suspected or laboratory confirmed COVID case. ● Adult and pediatric patients (>12 years old). ● Within 10 days of symptom onset. ● Hypoxic (SpO2 ≤ 93%). ╠ Baricitinib : In hypoxic patients where steroid cannot be used, the JAK inhibitor Baricitinib may be added at doses of 4 mg once daily for 14 days. ¬It should be given as add on to Remdesivir. There is no room for using Baricitinib alone. It reduces steroid requirement in patients. ╠ Multivitamins & Minerals : should not be routinely given, but may give to improve appetite & as per clinical need. Treatment on Discharge Ø Cap. Cefixime / Triocim (bex) / Tocef (gen) 200 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ...... ৭ দিন Ø Tab. Rivaroxaban / Rivarox (skf) / RivaXa (sq) 10 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ...... ৩০ দিন Ø Tab. Paracetamol / Napa (bex) / ATP (gen) 500 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ (জ্বর থাকলে খাবেন) ...... ৩ দিন Ø Cap. Esomeprazole / Exium (rad) / Esogap (euro) 20 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (আহারের আধাঘন্টা পূর্বে) ...... ৭ দিন Ø Syp. Butamirate (7.5 mg/5 ml) / Mirakof (sq) / Bukof (bacn), ৩ চামচ দিনে ৪ বার (কাঁশি থাকলে খাবেন) ...... ৫ দিন Ø Tab. Doxophylline/ Doxorin (ops) / Flindof (sq) 200 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ....... ৩০ দিন Ø Tab. Baricitinib / Barinez (bacn) / Baritor (sq) 2 mg, ০ + ২ + ০ ....... শুরু থেকে মোট ১৪ দিন If steroid given for more than 2 weeks at high dose, it should be tapper in discharge – Ø Tab. Dexamethasone / Sonexa (aris) 4 mg, ০ + ২ + ০ (ভরাপেটে) ----- ৫ দিন, তারপরে, ০ + ১ + ০ (ভরাপেটে) ----- ৫ দিন May add according to need – Ø Tab. Montelukast / Lumona (skf) / Trilock (ops) 10 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ....... ৩০ দিন Ø Tab. Multivitamin / Lysivin (sq), ১ + ০ + ১ ........ ৩০ দিন উপদেশ / নির্দেশাবলি : ১। বাসাতে একুশ (২১) দিন হোম আইসোলেশেনে সম্পূর্ণ বিশ্রামে থাকবেন - ● পরিবারের অন্যান্য সদস্যদের থেকেও সামাজিক দূরত্ব বজায় রাখুন। ● সম্ভব হলে রোগী পৃথক ঘর ও বাথরুম ব্যবহার করবেন। ● রোগীর সাথে দেখা করার সময় রোগী ও সেবাদানকারী মুখে উভয়ই মাক্স পরবেন। ● নূন্যতম ২০ সেকেন্ড ধরে ঘন ঘন হাত ধুবেন বা সেনিটাইজ করবেন। ● হাঁচি কাশি দেওয়ার সময় অবশ্যই টিস্যু বা কুনইয়ের ভাজ ব্যবহার করবেন। ২। জ্বর না কমলে উষ্ম গরম পানি দিয়ে বুক পিঠ ও গলা বাদ দিয়ে বারবার গা মুছ করবেন। ৩। একুশ (২১) দিন পর কোনো উপসর্গ না থাকলে পুনরায় করোনা পরীক্ষা করার দরকার নাই। ৪। যাদের ডায়াবেটিকস বা উচ্চরক্তচাপ আছে বা নতুন করে সনাক্ত হয়েছে, তারা মেডিসিন বহি:বিভাগ বা ডায়াবেটিকস হাসপাতালে নিয়মিত চেকআপে আসবেন ও ঔষধের ডোজ প্রয়োজনমত পূন:নির্ধারন করে নিবেন। ৫। ডায়াবেটিকসের ঔষধ বা ইনসুলিন নেওয়ার পর অবশ্যই খাদ্য গ্রহন করবেন। কোনো অবস্থাতে ক্ষুধার্ত থাকবেন না। ৬। পালস অক্সিমিটারের অক্সিজেন মাত্রা (SpO 2 ) ৯৪% বা তার কম থাকে তবে অতিসত্ত্বর নিকটস্থ অক্সিজেন সরবরাহ আছে এমন হাসপাতালে ভর্তি হবেন। ৭। নিয়মিত হাল্কা হাঁটাহাঁটি ও শ্বাসতন্ত্রের ব্যায়াম (যেমন বেলুন ফোলানোর ও পেট ফুলিয়ে শ্বাস নিয়ে পাঁচ সেকেন্ড ধরে রাখার চেষ্টা করবেন)। অথবা তিনটি বলযুক্ত স্পাইরোমিটার বা রেসপিরোমিটার দিয়ে নিয়মিত ব্রিদিং একসারসাইজ করবেন।

treatment.title: Steroid Induced Hyperglycemia
treatment.description: Steroid Induced Hyperglycemia Dexamethasone is the steroid of choice in moderate & severe COVID. But in critical COVID clinician can choose – ► Dose of Steroid in Other COVID-19 Syndromes : ARDS due to COVID : ○ Inj. Methylprednisolone 1-2 mg/kg q12 OR, ○ Inj. Dexamethasone 20 mg IV daily for 5 days & then 10 mg IV daily for 5 days & then 5 mg IV daily for 5 days. Refractory Sepsis : Hydrocortisone 50 mg IV 6 hourly. Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS) : Inj. Methylprednisolone 1-2 mg/kg q12 OR Inj. Dexamethasone 10 mg q6. ► A diabetic & hypertensive person must take the drugs he /she is currently on treatment. Continue the anti-diabetic drug. Continue treatment with ACE inhibitors / ARBs if already on these drugs unless there is other reason for discontinuation (e.g., hypotension, acute kidney injury). Continue statins drugs in hospitalized patients with COVID-19 who are already taking them. ► Steroid Induced Hyperglycemia should be treated with insulin. May be aided by oral drugs. Regular CBG / RBS checkup is a must. Suggest patients to by a glucometer, if hospital does not provide. ● Dexamethasone is long acting steroid, it may be given BD or TDS according to need. Long acting insulin should be choosen. ¤ Inj. Insulin Glargine / Larsulin (sq) / Vibrenta (incp) / Lantus (syn) / Abasaglar (hc) 100 IU/mL, Dose : 0.2 – 0.4 U/Kg/Day S/C once daily. May also use – ¤ Tab. Linagliptin 5 mg / Linatab (incp), 1 + 0 + 0 ● Prednisolone & Methylprednisolone is intermediate acting so intermediate acting insulin is choice of drug. Insulin Pump Initiate insulin pump if RBS ≥ 16.7 mmol/L. Target RBS : ± 10 mmol/L. Preparation of insulin pump : 1 IU/mL. As, 100U in 100 ml N/S or, 40U in 40 ml N/S. Precaution : Check electrolytes, if K+ Dose : ¤ Inj. Insulin Regular / Actrapid HM (novo) / Ansulin R (sq) 1 IU/mL, [RBS/5] ml/hr I/V through insulin / syringe pump. ¤ Please check RBS hourly. If patient is on dexamethasone then add – Long acting insulin 10 U once daily S/C at night. Or, dose may increase up to 20 U/day.

treatment.title: Mucormycosis in COVID-19 Patients
treatment.description: Risk Factors for Mucormycosis : ● Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus. ● Diabetic Ketoacidosis. ● Prolonged neutropenia. ● High-dose corticosteroid or prolonged steroid treatment. ● Immunosuppressive therapy associated with transplantation. ● Patients with hematologic malignancy. (commonly leukaemia and lymphoma) recipients of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (HSCT) and less commonly, patients undergoing solid-organ transplantation. ● Elevated levels of free iron and paradoxically desferroxamine. ● Prolonged ICU stay. Additional Suggestive Risk Factors for COVID-19 patients : ● Prolonged Use of steroid. ● Other immunosuppressive drugs including IL- 6 inhibitors. ● COVID induced hyperglycaemia. ● Inappropriate and prolonged use of same mask. ● Humidified oxygen with poor filter system. ● Industrial oxygen. ● Use of iron and zinc. ● Indiscriminate use of antifungal (voriconazole) and antibiotics in severe cases. Types of Mucormycosis : i. Rhinocerebral (44-49%) ii. Pulmonary (10-11%) iii. Cutaneous (10-16%) iv. GI (2-11%) v. Disseminated, (6-12%) and vi. Miscellaneous (Renal etc.) vii. Chronic Mucormycosis. Diagnosis : When to suspect mucormycosis – 1. Sinusitis – nasal blockade or congestion, nasal discharge (blackish/bloody), local pain on the cheek bone not responding to medications. 2. Unilateral facial swelling. 3. Blackish discoloration over bridge of nose/palate that quickly progresses to become severe. 4. Chest pain, pleural effusion, haemoptysis, worsening of respiratory symptoms despite conventional therapy. Patient may give a history of having suffered from severe COVID in the past 2-4 weeks or having been hospitalized from COVID-19 to receive supplemental oxygen as well as immunosuppressive drugs (including steroids). Laboratory Diagnosis : 1. Biopsy with histopathologic examination is the most sensitive and specific modality for definitive diagnosis. Culture is positive in less than 40% cases. 2. Radiology : ● CT scan or MRI of Orbit in Rhinocerebral Mucormycosis. ● High Resolution CT of Chest in Pulmonary Mucormycosis. ● MRA/MRV or CTA/CTV- to detect cavernous sinus thrombosis or internal carotid artery thrombosis. 3. Baseline investigations and further inv. for follow up : ● CBC to assess neutropenia, ● Blood sugar ● S. creatinine ● S. electrolyte ● ABG ● Blood culture –Usually negative ● CSF study (in case of CNS Mucormycosis/Meningitis): shows Lymphocytic pleocytosis and Elevated Protein. S. creatinine and S. electrolytes should be done weekly or as needed to detect nephrotoxicity at the earliest possible time. Other investigations can be done as per clinician’s advice Treatment of mucormycosis involves a combination of : 1. Correction of underlying risk factors for infection : ● Control of diabetes and diabetic ketoacidosis. ● Reduce steroids (if patient is still on) with an aim to discontinue rapidly. ● Avoid immuno-modulating drugs. ● Correction of neutropenia if possible. 2. Surgery : Early complete surgical debridement of involved tissues wherever possible. 3. Initiation of systemic antifungal therapy : The drug of choice for Mucormycosis is – 1. Liposomal Amphotericin B (LAmB). 2. Amphotericin B Deoxycholate (AmB). may be used if LAmB is unavailable. ● Liposomal Amphotericin B (LAmB) : 5mg/kg Daily, administered as an infusion over 2 hours (start with 10mg/kg/ day (if CNS involvement). At least 1 litre of Saline should be given a day to reduce the risk of nephrotoxicity due to LAmB. ● Amphotericin B Deoxycholate (AmB) : 1mg/kg body wt daily or alternate days for upto 2.5 gram. ● Other drugs that may be used if available are : Posaconazole, Isavuconazole.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : Patients complaints of fever, cough, sore throat. Macular, petechial or erythema multiforme rashes may occur (ampicillin induced). On examination – Severe pharyngitis with tonsillar exudates, cervical, axillary and inguinal lymphadenopathy and splenomegaly. Management : 1. Diet – normal (no restriction). 2. Send throat swab for culture and sensitivity (to detect any secondary infection in pharynx and tonsil). 3. Antibiotic – Penicillin, avoid ampicillin or amoxicillin. May change according to C/S report. 4. Paracetamol for fever. 5. Povidone iodine mouth wash gargling relieves throat discomfort. 6. Aspirin gargling also helpful to reduce throat discomfort. 7. When pharyngeal oedema is severe, a short course of glucocorticoids e.g. prednisolone 30 mg daily for 5 days. 8. Contact sports should be avoided until splenomegaly has completely resolved because of the danger of splenic rupture. 9. Return to work governed by patient physical fitness.

treatment.title: Types of Dengue
treatment.description: Dengue Viral Infection [Ref – Pocket Guideline for Dengue Clinical Case Management 2022 (Revised), National Malaria Elimination & Aedes Transmitted Diseases Control Program Disease Control Division, DGHS, Mohakhali, Dhaka-1212] Clinical Presentation (Types) of Dengue : ● A. Dengue fever (DF). ● B. Dengue haemorrhagic fever (DHF). ● C. Dengue shock syndrome (DSS). ● D. Expanded dengue syndrome. Malaria / Dengue: • H/O Travel to malaria endemic zone in last 30 days? • History of dengue in the locality? Dengue fever (DF) can be managed at outdoor basis. But dengue hemorrhagic fever (DHF) and dengue shock syndrome (DSS) must be managed at hospital. A. Dengue fever (DF) : Acute febrile illness c̄ 2 or more of the following indicates DF – • Headache • Retro-orbital pain • Myalgia • Arthralgia / bone pain (break-bone fever) • Rash • GIT manifestations: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea • Haemorrhagic manifestations (mild, unusual hg) • Leukopenia (WBC 3 ) • Platelet count ≤ 150,000 cells/mm 3 • Rising hematocrit (Hct.) (5-10% from baseline) Diagnosis : Tourniquet test positive + WBC ≤ 5,000 cells/cu.mm. (Positive predictive value = 83%) Tourniquet Test : The tourniquet test is part of the new WHO case definition for Dengue. The test is a marker of capillary fragility and it can be used as a triage tool to differentiate patients with Dengue fever from other non-Dengue febrile illness. How to do a Tourniquet Test ● Take the patient's blood pressure and record it, for example, 100/70 mmHg. ● Inflate the cuff to a point midway between SBP and DBP and maintain for 5 minutes. (100+70)/2= 85 mm Hg. ● The pressure is released for at least one minute and the skin below the cuff is examine for petechiae. See image (below). ● A finding of 10 or more petechiae in one square inch area considered positive. In DHF, the test usually gives a definite positive result when there is 20 petechiae per 1 inch with a sensitivity of more than 90%. Positive tourniquet test with greater than 10 petechiae per one square inch. B. Dengue Haemorrhagic Fever (DHF) : All of following confirms DHF : ● High, continuous fever 2-7 days (like Dengue fever). ● Haemorrhagic manifestations; tourniquet test positive, petechiae, epistaxis, haematemesis etc. ● ± Liver enlargement ● ± Shock Laboratory : ● Platelet count 3 . ● Evidence of plasma leakage : rising Hct. >20% ± pleural effusion, ascites, hypoproteinaemia, hypoalbuminemia (serum albumin Evidence of Plasma Leakage Key differentiating point between DF & DHF : (Dengue and Dengue Haemorrhagic Feve) • Rise in Hct. : 20% (e.g. In children 35 → 42 and in adults 40 → 48) • Circulatory failure : Cold/cold clammy skin, CRFT > 2 Sec, tachycardia, weak pulse, narrow pulse pressure • Fluid accumulation – Ascites / Pleural effusions. • Albumin C. Dengue Shock Syndrome (DSS) : • Cool extremities, delayed capillary refill time (CRFT). • Lethargy or restlessness. (which may be a sign of reduced brain perfusion). • Tachypnoea or Kussmaul’s breathing. • Tachycardia, weak pulse. • Narrow pulse pressure: Pulse pressure ≤ 20 mmHg with increased diastolic pressure, e.g. 100/80 mmHg. • Hypotension by age, defined as systolic pressure D. Expanded Dengue Syndrome : • Unusual manifestations with severe organ involvement such as liver, kidneys, brain or heart associated with Dengue infection reported in DHF and also in DF who do not have evidence of plasma leakage. • These unusual manifestations may be associated with co-infections, co-morbidities or complications of prolonged shock.

treatment.title: Investigations
treatment.description: Investigations : 1 st to 5 th days of fever : 1. CBC with Platelet count (Platelet count and haematocrit need to be repeated in DHF patient daily). 2. Haematocrit. 3. SGOT and SGPT (Not mandatory but helpful, increased SGPT/ SGOT more than 5-fold will be commonly seen in DHF patients.) 4. NS1 antigen – can be positive on the 1 st day of fever. Unlikely to be positive after 5 days. After 7 th day of fever : • IgM and IgG Antibodies (Day 5-7 window period) [Anti-dengue antibody : Raised Ig titre with ELISA or positive in IgM antibody. Commonly positive after 5 day.] Follow up testing may be done on 1 st afebrile day, but should be done daily when DHF is suspected. Dengue case classification : Dengue Viral Infection : Symptomatic & Asymptomatic. New revised WHO Dengue case classification by severity Group A Dengue without warning signs Mild DF Group B Dengue with warning signs Moderate DF Group C Severe dengue Severe DF Criteria for dengue : Probable Dengue : 1. Live in/travel to dengue endemic area. 2. Fever (high grade) and 2 of the following criteria : • Nausea, vomiting • Rash • Aches and pains • Tourniquet test positive • Leucopenia • Any waring sings 3. Laboratory Confirmed dengue (important when no sign of plasma leakage). Warning Signs : • Severe abdominal pain or tenderness. • Persistent vomiting > 3 times/ day, not drinking. • Persistent diarrhoea > 3 times/ day. • Clinical fluid accumulation. • Less/on urine output for 4-6 hours. • Pale, cold and clammy hands and feet • Bleeding / Mucosal bleeding: epistaxis, black stool, haematemesis, excessive menstrual bleeding, dark coloured urine (haemoglobinuria). • Lethargy and/or restlessness, sudden behavioural change. • Giddiness. • Liver enlargement > 2cm. • Laboratory : Increase in hematocrit (Hct.) concurrent, with rapid decrease in platelet count. • No clinical improvement or worsening of the situation just before or during the transition to a febrile phase or as the disease progresses. Requiring strict observation and medical intervention. High-risk patients : 1. Infants and the elderly. 2. Obesity. 3. Pregnant women. 4. Peptic ulcer disease with history of bleeding. 5. Women who have menstruation or abnormal vaginal bleeding. 6. G6PD (glucose-6-phosphate deficiency). 7. Thalassemia and other haemoglobinopathies. 8. Congenital heart disease (CHD). 9. Chronic diseases such as DM, HTN, asthma, IHD, CKD, CLD. Criteria for severe dengue 1. Severe Plasma Leakage : leading to : • Shock (DSS) • Fluid accumulation with respiratory distress 2. Severe Bleeding : as evaluated by clinician 3. Severe Organ Involvement • Liver : AST or ALT ≥ 1000 • CNS: Impaired consciousness • Heart and other organs 4. Metabolic & electrolytes abnormalities

treatment.title: Group-A : Dengue Fever (DF)
treatment.description: Management Plan for Mild Dengue Clinical and Laboratory Criteria for Patients who can Be Treated at Home ● Able to tolerate orally well, good urine output and no history of bleeding. ● Absence of warning signs. ● Physical examination: - Haemodynamically stable - No tachypnoea or acidotic breathing. - No tender liver or abdominal tenderness - No bleeding manifestation - No sign of third space fluid accumulation - No alterations in mental state ● Investigation : Stable serial Hct. ● No other criteria for admission (i.e.- co-morbidities, pregnancy, social factors) Group-A : Dengue Fever (DF) Treatment : ● Tab. P/C / Napa (bex) / Ace (sq) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ----- if fever present. [ ≤ 3000mg/day for adults & 15 mg/Kg/dose, TDS/QDS for children] উপদেশ : ১। সম্পূর্ণ বিশ্রামে থাকবেন। ডিহাইড্রেশন বা পানিশূন্যতা পরিহারের জন্য পর্যাপ্ত পরিমাণ পানি খাবেন। খাবার স্যালাইন, ফলের রস বা জুস ইত্যাদি খেতে থাকবেন। ২। জ্বরের জন্য প্যারাসিটামল ছাড়া অন্য কোন ঔষধ যেমন – অ্যাসপিরিন, মেফেনামিক এসিড বা আইবুপ্রোফেন ইত্যাদি খাবেন না। চিকিৎসকের পরামর্শ ছাড়া কোন ওষুধ সেবন থেকে বিরত থাকবেন। ৩। জ্বর আসলে কুসুম গরম পানি দিয়ে বারবার গা মুছ (Tepid sponging) করাবেন। পারতোপক্ষে যতটা কম ওষুধ খাওয়া যায় ততই ভালো। ৪। নিম্নাক্ত লক্ষণ গুলো দেখা দিলে যথাসম্ভব দ্রুত নিকটস্থ হাসপাতালে ভর্তি হবেন এবং ডেঙ্গু রোগী হিসেবে চিকিৎসা শুরু করবেন (ওয়ার্নিং সাইন) : • অতিরিক্ত পেট ব্যথা করা। • দিনে তিনবারের বেশি বমি হওয়া। • দিনে তিনবারের পাতলা পায়খানা / ডায়রিয়া হওয়া। • মুখ বা মাড়ি দিয়ে রক্ত বের হওয়া। • দিনে ছয় ঘণ্টার মধ্যে কোন প্রস্রাব না হওয়া। • প্রেসার বা রক্তচাপ কমে যাওয়া। • অস্থির লাগা বা জ্ঞান হারিয়ে ফেলা। • অতিরিক্ত ক্লান্তি বা দুর্বলতা দেখা দিলে। • পেট ফুলে গেলে বা পেটে পানি জমলে বা শ্বাসকষ্ট হলে। • পরবর্তী রক্ত পরীক্ষায় পরবর্তী বা দ্বিতীয় রক্ত পরীক্ষার থেকেও হেমাটোক্রিট বিশ শতাংশের বেশি বৃদ্ধি এবং অণুচক্রিকা বা প্লাটিলেট সংখ্যা কমে গেলে। ► Adequate fluid intake (around 2500 ml or 8-10 glasses for average-sized adults or accordingly in children, around 50ml/kg)-e.g. milk, fruit juice (caution with diabetic patients), oral rehydration solution (ORS) or barley/rice water/coconut water. ► Take paracetamol (not more than 3 grams per day for adults; 10-15 mg/kg/dose, not more than 3 to 4 times in 24 hours in children). ► During febrile phase : Paracetamol : 15 mg / kg /dose and should be administered in frequency of not less than six hours. The maximum dose for adult is 4gm/day In children (1tsf-5ml-120mg), ► Criteria for discharging patients : 1. Absence of fever for at least 24 hours without the use of anti-fever therapy. 2. Return of appetite. 3. Visible clinical improvement. 4. Satisfactory urine output. 5. A minimum of 2-3 days have elapsed after recovery from shock. 6. No respiratory distress from pleural effusion and no ascites. 7. Platelet count of more than 50000/mm 3 .

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Group B : Dengue Haemorrhagic Fever (DHF) : (Hospitalize) ↓ Shock ( ↓ BP & PP) Group C : Dengue Shock Syndrome (DSS) Compensated Shock Decompensated Shock ● Pulse pressure ≤ 20 mmHg. [PP = SBP - DBP] ● Profound shock. ● Hypotension ( ● No BP & no pulse. ● Fainting. Δ DHF / DSS Adv. : ▸ Dengue NS1 antigen (within 5 days of fever) ▸ Anti-dengue IgM & IgG (after 7 th days of fever) ▸ CBC (c̄ Hct) c̄ Platelet count before staring IV fluids. ▸ RBS ± ▸ S. Albumin ± ▸ SGPT/SGOT ± ▸ S. Creatinine ± ▸ USG of W/A Δ DHF – ► Normal diet c̄ adequate fluid. ► Inf. 0.9% Normal Saline / N/S 1L, I/V @20 d/m daily for 48 hours. ► Tab. Paracetamol / Tamen (skf) 500 mg, 2+2+2 – if fever present ► Cap. Omeprazole 20mg / Ometid (ops), 1+0+1 (30 min B/M) Δ DSS – ► Bed rest. ► O 2 inhalation : 4-6L/min stat and sos. If pulse pressure ≤ 20 mmHg or ↓ BP : ► Inf. NS / 5% DNS 500 ml, I/V running over 1 hour. [500ml/min adult, 10ml/kg/hr in children] If patient improves after initial 500ml fluid : ► Packed cell volume / Fresh whole blood = 5 to 10 ml/kg (or, 1 unit in adults) in 1-2 hrs. Monitoring : Until the period of risk is over (48 hours). 1. Vital signs and peripheral perfusion : 1-4 hourly until out of critical phase. 2. Urine output : 4-6 hourly. 3. HCT : Before and after fluid replacement, then 6-12 hourly. Note : CBC (Hct) must be done before any fluid in fusion or blood transfusion (BT), otherwise result will be misleading. 4. Blood glucose (RBS).

treatment.title: IV Fluids in Dengue
treatment.description: IV Fluids in Dengue [Ref – Pocket Guideline for Dengue Clinical Case Management 2022 (Revised), Page – 17, 18] Two types of I/V fluids are used in Dengue : 1. Crystalloid (first choice). 2. Colloid. Indications of I/V fluid in Dengue : ● Patient cannot have adequate oral fluid intake or vomiting. ● When Hct continues to rise 10-20% despite oral rehydration. ● Impending shock / Shock. Types of Crystalloid uses in Dengue : Preferable : 0.9% NaCl (isotonic normal saline solution) (0.9% NS). For children 1) 5% dextrose in Ringer's lactate solution (5% DRL). 2) 5% dextrose in acetated Ringer's solution (5% DRA). 3) Hartman solution (HS). Indications of Colloid in Dengue : ● Patients with massive plasma leakage. ● Patients with shock with rising Hct. ● Those not responding to the max volume of crystalloid. ● If pulse pressure Types of Colloid uses in Dengue : 1) Dextran-based : Dextran 40, Dextran 70. 2) Starch based (6% HES) : Voluven, Plasmasol (6% hydroxyl ethyl ester with normal saline), Volulyte (6% hydroxyl ethyl ester with balanced electrolyte). 3) Gelatin. 4) Human Albumin. 5) Plasma. 6) Haemaccel. 7) Blood and blood components. Indications for Platelet Concentrate : It has been observed that there is very limited role of platelet transfusion. In most of the situation fresh whole blood transfusion is suffice. However it may be required in some special situation. The indication of which may be as follows : 1. Very Severe Thrombocytopania who need urgent surgery. 2. Clinical judgement of the treating physician. Indications for blood transfusion (BT) : 1. Overt bleeding (more than 10% or 6-8ml/kg). 2. Significant drop of Hct. 3. Hypotensive shock + low/normal Hct. 4. Persistent or worsening metabolic acidosis. 5. Refractory shock after fluid 40-60 ml/kg.

treatment.title: IV Fluid for Compensated Shock
treatment.description: IV Fluid Therapy for Compensated Shock : Do immediately : Hct. & Blood sugar + give oxygen. I/V 5% DNS / NS 10ml/kg/hr (in child) within 1-2 hrs & 500ml/hr in adults. ↓ If patient is improved or stable after initial bolus therapy : Reduce the rate of IV Fluid to – ○ In child: 10 > 7 > 5 > 3 > 1.5 ml/kg/hr. ○ In adult : 500 > 250 > 150 > 100 > 80 > 40 ml/hr. ○ Keep IV cannula for more than 24 hrs. ↓ If patient is still unstable after initial bolus therapy : Check the Hct after 1 st bolus. ↓ If Hct ( ↓ ) is decreased or, sign of obvious blood loss or, concealed haemorrhage suspected : Need blood grouping, Rh typing, screening, cross matching & demand and transfuse blood as soon as possible. Transfuse Packed Cell Volume (PCV) : 5 ml/kg/ hr, Or, Fresh Whole Blood : 10 ml/kg/hr (or 1 unit in adults) in 1-2 hrs. OR, If Hct ( ↑ ) in increased with vital signs deteriorated : Colloid (Dextran-40 in NS / Plasmasol / Voluven) 10 ml/kg/hr (500 ml/hr in adults as bolus). ↓ Not Improved : Packed Cell Volume (PCV) : 5 ml/kg/ hr, Or, Fresh Whole Blood : 10 ml/kg/hr (or 1 unit in adults) in 1-2 hrs. ↓ Not Improved : Check ABCS again and plan for Dialysis (Peritoneal / Haemodialysis) organ & ICU support. [Ref – Pocket Guideline for Dengue Clinical Case Management 2022 (Revised), Page – 14]

treatment.title: Profound Shock (Decompensated Shock)
treatment.description: Flow Diagram for Profound Shock (Decompensated Shock) – Profound Shock (No BP, No Palpable Pulse) ↓ Do immediately : Hct., Blood sugar. Give oxygen & check ABCs and correct. NS 10-20ml/kg IV bolus (in adults 500ml) in 15-30 min free flow (IV Fluid may be NS, DAR, DLR). Start Colloid – 10ml/kg IV followed by IV frusemide. ↓ If improved : Change IV Fluid to 5% DNS / NS : 10ml/kg/hr for 1-2 hr (in Child) & in adults 500 ml/hr (125 drop/min). then, follow & reduce the rate of IV Fluid compensated shock flow chart (given above). ↓ If not improved : Repeat 2nd Bolus of IV Fluid (IVF). Give NaHCO 3 1-2mEq/kg (or, 50-100ml in adults). ↓ If Hct ( ↓ ) is decreased or, sign of obvious blood loss or, concealed haemorrhage suspected : Need blood grouping, Rh typing, screening, cross matching & demand and transfuse blood as soon as possible. Transfuse Packed Cell Volume (PCV) : 5 ml/kg/ hr, Or, Fresh Whole Blood : 10 ml/kg/hr (or 1 unit in adults) in 1-2 hrs. OR, If Hct ( ↑ ) in increased with vital signs deteriorated : Colloid (Dextran-40 in NS / Plasmasol / Voluven) 10 ml/kg/hr (500 ml/hr in adults as bolus). ↓ Not Improved : Packed Cell Volume (PCV) : 5 ml/kg/ hr, Or, Fresh Whole Blood : 10 ml/kg/hr (or 1 unit in adults) in 1-2 hrs. ↓ Not Improved : Check ABCS again and plan for Dialysis (Peritoneal / Haemodialysis) organ & ICU support. [Ref – Pocket Guideline for Dengue Clinical Case Management 2022 (Revised), Page – 15]

treatment.title: Management of Fluid Overload
treatment.description: Flow Diagram for the Management of Fluid Overload Signs of Fluid Overload : Puffy eyelids, Distended abdomen, Dyspnea / Tachypnea, Positive Lung Signs : Crepitation, Wheezing, Rhonchi. ↓ • Give Oxygen. • Insert urinary catheter. • Check ABCS and correct. • Check Hct. • Non invasive CPAP. ↓ Reabsorption Phase Critical / Early Convalescence phase High + wide pulse pressure : >36 hrs after shock or, >60 hrs after onset of leakage ↓ BP + narrow pulse pressure : Shock or Signs of fluid overload ↓ ↓ Discontinue IV fluid or Keep Vein Open (KVO) infusion. Frusemide 1mg/kg/dose IV (in child) (40gm in adults). Dextran 40 rate 10ml/kg/hr (in child) (in adults 500ml/hr) Frusemide 1mg/kg/dose IV (in child) (in adults 40mg) given midway to Dextran. ↓ ↓ If improved : good urine output >1 ml/kg/hr (>50ml/hr in adults) Or, Vital signs improved with ↓ Hct. If not improved : ↓ Hct. >10 points (below baseline) & vital signs deteriorated. ↓ ↓ ● Stop IV fluid and follow up vital signs + amount of urine output. ● Repeat Frusemide – if signs / symptoms of fluid overload persist. Fresh Whole Blood:10 ml/kg/hr (or 1 unit in adults). ↓ Still not Improved : with no urine output, still dyspnea/tachypnea. ○ Check ABCS again, consider mechanical ventilation. ○ Pleural and/or Peritoneal tapping. ○ Plan for dialysis (Peritoneal / Haemodialysis). [Ref – Pocket Guideline for Dengue Clinical Case Management 2022 (Revised), Page – 16] Dos or Don’ts : ▸ Do not give aspirin or any NSAIDs for the treatment of any undiagnosed fever in monsoon season (June to October). ▸ Avoid giving blood transfusion unless indicated, reduction in hematocrit or severe bleeding. ▸ Avoid giving steroid as these will complicate the situation and there is no sound evidence based indication. ▸ Don’t use antibiotics as these don’t help. ▸ Avoid : Aspirin, Mefenemic acid, Ibuprofen or other NSAIDs, Steroids, Antibiotics. ▸ Don’t change the speed of fluid rapidly. ▸ Avoid giving I/V therapy before there is evidence of hemorrhage or bleeding. ▸ ORT with ORS or its equivalent is recommended for patients with moderate dehydration caused by vomiting & high temperature. ▸ Food should be given according to appetite. But fresh fruit juice should be given frequently. ▸ Cases of DHF should be observed every hourly. [Ref – Pocket Guideline for Dengue Clinical Case Management 2022 (Revised), Page – 22]

treatment.title: Expanded Dengue Syndrome
treatment.description: D. Expanded Dengue Syndrome (Dengue with Organopathy) : Severe Dengue can be complicated with Myocarditis, Encephalopathy / Encephalitis / Acute liver failure, AKI, ARDS and sometimes with multiorgan failure (MOF). ● These complicated patient should be managed by Medicine Specialist / Pediatricians for comprehensive care. ● In myocarditis with raised Troponin I and ECG changes (Bradycardia, Tachycardia, ST-T changes) injudicious use of antiplatelet, anticoagulant or intervention (e.g. pacemaker and others) should be avoided (20% fluid is to be reduced). ● In encephalitis, judicious use of steroids can be given. (Dexamethasone : 0.15 mg / kg 6-8 hourly for 3-5 days) ● In hepatic encephalopathy in dengue, the management should be done as per protocol of Hepatic Encephalopathy. ● For AKI, ARDS or multiorgan failure, appropriate supportive care should be provided. Hepatic Encephalopathy : - O 2 Therapy. - Fluid : 20% fluid reduction. - Colloid. - Frusemide in fluid overload. - Lactulose. - H 2 receptor blocker. - Rifaximin. - FFP. Monitoring (Hepatic Encephalopathy) : - Blood sugar. - Correction of electrolytes. - Bleeding episodes. - I/O (intake output) chart. - Any convulsion. Note : Expanded Dengue Syndrome cases should be managed - In tertiary care setting. - Here management of EDS is highlighted above. - For detailed management further reading is recommended. [Ref – Pocket Guideline for Dengue Clinical Case Management 2022 (Revised), Page – 20]

treatment.title: Pregnancy, child & Discharge Criteria
treatment.description: Pregnancy and Dengue Management : - Medical termination is to be avoided. - MR should not be allowed. - Hospital delivery advised. - Blood should be available. - Multi-disciplinary approach - Avoid elective C/S & induction of labour. - No episiotomy. - No instrumental delivery. - In Preterm labour advice delay in delivery. - Inj. steroid I/V to be given for Premature delivery. - Active management in third stage of labour. - Misoprostol / Tranexamic acid can be given I/V (1 gm in 10 ml solution administered slowly over 10 min). - All Dengue patients or suspected Dengue patients with pg must get admitted to hospital for close monitoring. In case of child : - Shock is to be managed by Normal Saline. - After shock managed, 5% DNS is to be used for maintenance. - Below 1 yr age : Baby saline (0.45% NaCl) is to be used. [Ref – Pocket Guideline for Dengue Clinical Case Management 2022 (Revised), Page – 21] When to stop intravenous fluid therapy : 1. Cessation of plasma leakage. 2. Stable BP, pulse and peripheral perfusion. 3. Hematocrit ↓ in the presence of a good pulse volume. 4. Apyrexia (without antipyretics) for more than 24-48 hrs. 5. Resolving bowel/abdominal symptoms. 6. Improving urine output. 7. Continuing intravenous fluid therapy beyond the 48 hrs of the critical phase will put the patient at risk of pulmonary oedema and other complications such as thrombophlebitis. Signs of recovery : 1. Stable pulse, blood pressure and breathing rate. 2. Normal temperature. 3. No evidence of external or internal bleeding. 4. Return of appetite. 5. No vomiting, no abdominal pain. 6. Good urinary output. 7. Stable hematocrit at baseline level. 8. Convalescent confluent petechiae rash or itching, especially on the extremities. Discharge Criteria : 1. No fever for at least 24 hr with no antipyretic drugs. 2. At least two days have lapsed after recovery from shock. 3. Good general condition with improving appetite. 4. Normal Hct at baseline value or around 38-40 % when baseline value is not known. 5. No distress from pleural effusions. 6. No ascites. 7. Platelet count has risen above 50,000/mm³. 8. No other complications.

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.disease_name: Pyrexia of unknown origin or Fever Under Evaluation (PUO / FUO)
treatment.description: If patient complaints only fever, no other symptoms or clinical findings else then diagnose it Fever under evaluation. If no causative agent & definitive diagnosis of fever can be find out then the diagnosis is Pyrexia of unknown origin or Fever Under Evaluation (PUO / FUO). Δ Fever under evaluation Adv. : According to suspect – ▸ CBC ▸ CXR ▸ RBS After 7 days of fever : ▸ Widal test. ▸ Weil-Felix test. ▸ MP & ICT for malaria / kala-azar. ▸ Sputum for AFB or GeneXpert. ▸ MT test (if child) Tx, ¤ Diet – Normal. ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftron (sq) 1 g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. [2g BD if suspected meningitis, encephalitis, skin & fascia necrosis, toxic shock syndrome, septic shock etc.] Or, Oral antibiotic : ¤ Cap. Cefixime / T-Cef (drg) / Denver (hc) / Fix-A (acm)/ 3-C (edruc) 200 or 400 mg, 1 + 0 + 1. ¤ Tab. Esomeprazole Mups / Exium Mups (rad) / Maxpro Mups (ren), 1+0+1. ¤ Tab. Paracetamol / Renova (ops) / Tamen (skf) / Napa (bex), 1+1+1+1 or, 2+2+2 (A/M). ¤ Supp. Paracetamol / Momodol (drg) / Renova (ops) / Fast (acm) / Napa (bex), 1 stick P/R stat when temp >101°F. Only if vomiting presents, add – ¤ Tab. Ondansetron 8 mg / Ondason (drg) / Emistat (hc) / Emeren (ren), 1 + 0 + 1. ¤ Medicine for other symptomatic treatment. ► If unexplained fever with no positive test then dx : Pyrexia of Unknown Origin (PUO). Rx, IV ceftriaxone & anti-pyretic.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: When to suspect enteric fever – ● High grade fever persisting more than 7 days, ● No organ specific features except GI features. E.g., without apparently no other symptoms caused by salmonella bacteria. Features : 1. Fever stepladder pattern-gradually increases for 4 to 5 days. 2. Headache, myalgia, relative bradycardia etc. 3. Enlarged spleen. Severe infection – high fever with chills and rigor (hepatic type), toxicity, prostration, abdominal pain, diarrhea, coated tongue, hepatosplenomegaly (20- 30%), rose (25%) spot on 7 th – 10 th day. It resolves by 4-6 week, usually with no complication. [In 2nd week : cough, abdominal distension, diarrhea, rare 3rd week : delirium, coma, death.] If fever for more than 7 days + normal WBC count or leucopenia + splenomegaly = Typhoid fever (confirms diagnosis). Investigation – ➢ Blood C/S in 1 st week. → ➢ Urine/ stool C/S in 2 nd week. ● CBC with ESR – Leucopenia, ● Blood for MP (in endemic zone), ● Urine R/M/E (exclude UTI), ● X-ray Chest P/A view (exclude inflammatory infiltration or consolidation), ● Blood for C/S, if feasible. If patient presented with fever for more than 7 days and no history of taking antibiotic, blood culture & sensitivity (C/S) must be advised. ● Widal test has little value in diagnosis, only rising titre is diagnostic. But is a popular tool in upazila & GP. Management : 1. Tab. Paracetamol & tepid sponging. 2. Antibiotic is must : Double dose & double duration. Empirical Treatment (any one) : ▪ Azithromycin : 500 mg twice daily for 7 days. ▪ Cefixime : 400 mg BD for 10-14 days. ▪ Ceftriaxone : 2 gm/day for 10-14 days (IV in hospital). ▪ Ciprofloxacin: 500 mg BD for 10-14 days (shows resistance) [For pediatric patients (any one) : ▪ Ceftriaxone : 80-100 mg/kg/day- 10 days (7-14 days). ▪ Cefixime : 20 mg/kg/day BD for 10-14 days. ▪ Cefuroxime: 30-40 mg/kg/day BD 10-14 days.] Refer to medicine specialists or hospitalize, if not responding. Multidrug Resistant Treatment : ▪ Meropenem : 1 gm IV 8 hourly for 10-14 days. In any infectious disease intravenous & oral antibiotic therapy depends on patient's condition. If patient is toxic, vomits, cannot take oral foods, poor general condition then intravenous antibiotic should be used. ▪ Dexamethasone : 3 mg/kg stat followed by 1 mg/kg every 6 hourly for 2 days in - Severe disease with shock, stupor, obtundation & prolonged symptoms.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : • Fever for more than 7 days. • Associated with headache, malaise, weakness, cough. O/G/E : Rash : Erythematous maculopapular rash appears on 5-7 th day. Skin condition: Eschar surrounded by cellulitis Lymphadenopathy: Usually posterior cervical lymph node. Investigation : 1. CBC : Thrombocytopenia. 2. Weil-Felix Test / Triple antigen : (1:80 normal, only raising titer is significant): OX-2 ≥ 1:160, OX-19 ≥ 1:160, OX-K ≥ 1:160. ► Tab. P/C 665 mg / Napa Extend (bex) / Ace XR (sq), 1+1+1+1 (A/M) – if fever present (5 days). ► Cap. Doxycycline 100 mg / Doxysina (ibn) / Doxy-A (acm) / Doxicap (aris) / Doxin (ops), 1+0+1 –– 7 – 14 days. Or, Cap. Tetracycline / Tetrax (sq) / A-Tetra (acm) / Tetrasina (ibn) 500 mg, 1+1+1+1 –– 7 – 14 days Advice : Take medicine regularly. Supp. P/C if high fever. If the fever is high, soak the cloth in water and wipe the body with it. Normal diet with plenty of fluid / water intake.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Suspect malaria if fever with chills and rigor, subsided with profuse sweating (may be with no particular pattern) & travelling history to endemic zone of malaria e.g. hill tracts. For practical management falciparum malaria can be categorized as – Uncomplicated & Severe (Complicated) falciparum malaria. Investigations : 1. CBC. 2. Thick and thin film form malarial parasite. 3. ICT for malaria. Others : 1. Serum bilirubin. 2. Serum electrolytes. 3. RBS. 4. Serum lactate. Features of Uncomplicated Malaria : ● Fever or history of fever within last 48 hours. And ● Absence of convincing evidence of other febrile illness. And ● High index of suspicion based on time, place and person. And ● Diagnosis is confirmed by presence of asexual form of P. falciparum or P. vivax in blood slide examination (BSE) or, Rapid diagnostic test (RDT) positive. [Drug history is as important as BSE since it may be false negative. RDT is preferred in urgency.] Management of Uncomplicated Malaria : ▸ Paracetamol & tepid sponging to reduce temperature. ▸ Anti-malarial drugs [1 st line drug = Co-Artem (ACT) (Artemether & Lumefantrine)] combination (total 24 tablets in 6 doses (3 days), 2nd dose 8 hours after 1 st dose, subsequent doses are 12 hours interval). ► Tab. Artemether 20 mg + Lumefantrine 120 mg / Co-Artem (ACT) / Tab. Lumertam (sq) / Artemet (incp) / Arexel (jay), 4 + 4 + 0 --- 1 st day (0 th & 8 th hour), 4 + 0 + 4 --- 2 nd day (24 th & 36 th hour), 4 + 0 + 4 --- 3 rd day (48 th & 60 th hour) Weight Number of ACT tabs Weight Number of ACT tabs ● Adult & >35 kg 4 ● 15 – 25 kg 2 ● 25 – 35 kg 3 ● 5 – 15 kg 1 PLUS, Single dose Primaquine : ► Tab. Primaquine 15 mg / P Phos (hudson) / Jasoprim (jay) / Kanaprim (glob), Single dose 3 tab stat after meal [0.75mg/kg]. ▸ Situations where Artemether & Lumefantrine cannot be given : a) Pregnancy in first trimester. b) Children In these situation Quinine for 7 days : ► Tab. Quinine Sulfate 300 mg / Jasoquin (jay) / Quin (hudson) / Kanaquine (glob), Adult dose (600mg TDS) : 2 + 2 + 2 --- 7 days. For children : 10 mg/kg TDS for 7 days. Weight Number of tabs 8 hrly Weight Number of tabs 8 hrly ● Adult & >40 kg 2 ● 10 – 19 kg ½ ● 30 – 39 kg 1½ ● 3 – 9 kg ¼ ● 20 – 29 kg 1 Here, Portion of Quinine Sulphate 300 mg tabs taken TDS.

treatment.title: Vivax & Uncomplicated Malaria
treatment.description: Vivax malaria : ► Tab. Chloroquine 250 mg / Avloquin (aci) / Quinolex (glob) / Malorin (albion) / Uniquin (aexim), 1st day : 4 tab [10mg/kg] 2nd day : 4 tab [10mg/kg] 3rd day : 2 tab [5 mg/kg] ► Tab. Primaquine 15 mg / P Phos (hudson) / Jasoprim (jay) / Kanaprim (glob), 0+1+0 (A/M) ---- 14 days in adult & 0.3mg/kg in children. Primaquine used for radical cure (prevention of relapse) of vivax malaria. Alternative regimen for uncomplicated malaria : Tetracycline and Doxycycline are contraindicated in children younger than 8 years old and in pregnant and lactating women. 1. "Quinine for 7 days + Tetracycline (250mg 6 hourly) 7 days (Q7+T7) or, 2. "Quinine for 7 days + Doxycycline (100mg 12 hourly) 7 days (Q7+D7) or, 3. "Quinine for 7 days + Clindamycin (300mg 8 hourly ) 7 days (Q7+C7)

treatment.title: Severe Malaria
treatment.description: Features : 1. Fever or history of fever within last 48 hours. And, 2. Diagnosis is confirmed by presence of asexual form of P. falciparum in blood slide examination (BSE) or rapid diagnostic test (RDT) positive. And, 3. One or more of the following features of severity : I. Change of behavior, confusion or drowsiness. II. Altered consciousness or coma. III. Generalized convulsion more than 2 episodes in 24 hours. IV. Difficulty in breathing due to acute pulmonary edema or ARDS or deep breathing (acidotic breathing). V. Circulatory collapse (shock). VI. Clinical jaundice. VII. Severe prostration (extreme generalized weakness so the patient cannot work or stand or seat without assistance, in small child failure to thrive). VIII. Severe vomiting leading to nothing per oral. IX. Bleeding tendency or abnormal spontaneous bleeding. X. Severe anaemia (Hb- XI. Oliguria or acute renal failure. (urine output XII. Laboratory features : a) Hyperparasitemia (>5% or 2.5 lac per microliter). b) Hypoglycaemia (RBS below 4mmol/L). c) Fluid and electrolyte disturbance (hyponatraemia). d) Haemoglobinuria. e) Renal impairment (S. creatinine >3 mg/dl). Criteria for referral to higher center : • Persistent unconsciousness >48 hrs. • Uncontrolled convulsions. • Respiratory Distress. • Persistent Hypotension despite fluid challenge. • Severe Anaemia (Hematocrit • Oliguria/ AKI. (Urine output • Black Urine. • Deep Jaundice. • Pregnancy.

treatment.title: Cerebral Malaria
treatment.description: Cerebral malaria is the most severe neurological complication of infection with Plasmodium falciparum malaria. It is a clinical syndrome characterized by coma and asexual forms of the parasite on peripheral blood smears. Management : If falciparum (+) ve : ↓ 1st Line : IV Artesunate, THEN Oral ACT. 2nd Line : IV/ IM Quinine, THEN Oral Quinine/ ACT. Antimalarial Therapy for Cerebral Malaria : 1st Line Initial Therapy : Artemisin Derivative : Injection Artesunate – Drug of choice. • Loading Dose : 2.4mg/kg IV at 0 hours, 12 hours and 24 hours. • Maintenance Dose : 2.4mg/kg IV daily until the patient can tolerate oral medication but not more than 5 days. OR, 2nd Line Therapy : Quinine : • Loading Dose : 20 mg salt/kg IV over 4 hours in 5% DA (5-10 ml/kg) depending on the patient’s overall fluid balance. • Maintenance dose : After 8-12 hours of loading dose: 10mg salt/kg IV over 4 hours in 5% DA (5-10 ml/kg) over 4 hours, 8 to 12 hourly until the patient can tolerate oral medication. In patients requiring more than 48 hours of parenteral therapy, reduce the quinine or quinine maintenance dose by a third to half (i.e. 5-7 mg salt/kg of body weight every 8-12 hourly for future 3 to 4 days. When patient will be able to take orally then switch from IV to oral therapy. Infusion 5% DA is preferred as there is a chance of hypoglycemia due malaria & anti-malarial drugs itself. In Hospital Care : • Care of unconscious patient : NG tube feeding 200 ml 2 hourly in sitting posture & maintain the posture for 10 min, continued catheterization, change the posture 2 hourly, care of mouth, eye & bowel. • Hypoglycemia (RBS → 25% DA I/V bolus : o Adult 40 ml & Children 2 ml/Kg diluted with equal volume of 0.225% NaCl. • Temperature >101°F : o Tepid sponging & Paracetamol (P/C). o Tab/Supp P/C : 15mg/Kg/dose – 6 to 8 hourly. • Convulsions → Diazepam : o Adult : IV 10 mg. o Children : 0.3 to 0.5 mg/kg P/R. o No prophylactic anticonvulsant. • Omeprazole IV or oral sachet 1 packet dissolve in ½ glass water through be BD to prevent stress ulcer. • Follow up the patient with urine output, blood sugar, GCS. If falciparum (-) ve : ↓ Check for differential diagnosis – • Neck rigidity? • Kernig’s sign? • Aural discharge/ rash? → Treat for meningitis with IV antibiotics. • Seizure with focal neurological signs? → Treat for encephalitis with IV antivirals. • ↑ Suspicion of Malaria? • Severe Symptoms? • Quick confirmation not possible? → Treat initially with : • IV Anti-Malarial • IV Antibiotics • IV Antivirals Repeat tests to confirm. Antibiotics should be started in appropriate doses to cover the possibility of meningitis (even if CSF is inconclusive) and other unrecognized infections.

treatment.title: Kala-Azar
treatment.description: Features : • H/O fever ≥ 2weeks. • Residing/traveling in endemic areas. • Splenomegaly. Additional signs of kala-azar (KA) : • Weight loss. • Anaemia. • Darkening of skin. Inv. : rK39 RDT Positive → Diagnosis : New Kala-azar (NKA). • If H/O treatment for KA. • No improvement of initial treatment within one month or reappearance of symptoms & sign of KA. • Confirmation : parasitologically by splenic smear or bone marrow examination or PCR. ↓ Diagnosis : Retreatment Kala-azar. ↓ (if failed) Diagnosis : Kala-azar Treatment Failure (KATF) or, Relapse KA. Post Kala-azar Dermal Leishmaniasis (PKDL) • Residing/travelling in the KA endemic areas. • H/O treatment for KA any time in the past. • Suggestive skin lesion without loss of sensation, which may be hypo-melanotic, macular, papular, nodular or mixed. ↓ Investigation : rk39 RDT / Splenic aspirate / Slit skin smear / PCR / Bone marrow – positive. ↓ (if failed) Diagnosis : Post Kala-azar Dermal Leishmaniasis (PKDL). Refer for national surveillance : Kala-azar is a disease under DGHS National Kala-azar Elimination Program (KEP). Rapid kit test of rk39 has been distributed KA endemic areas & treatment is given free of cost with a strict contact tracing. So if KA is diagnosed, must refer to upazilla health complex (UHC) or Civil Surgeon (CS) Office or 250 bedded Sadar hospital or Govt. medical college hospitals. Treating a KA patient in private GP is not acceptable.

treatment.title: New Kala-azar (NKA)
treatment.description: 1 st Line of Treatment for NKA : 1. Liposomal Amphotericin B (LAmB) : Drug of choice. 10 mg/kg IV infusion single dose with 5% dextrose. 2. Miltefosine : 1 st Alterative Choice. a. Age 2-11 years : 2.5 mg/kg twice daily with meal × 28 days. (Not exceeding 50 mg/day). b. Age ≥ 12 years & wt 50 mg daily in the morning with meal × 28 days. (Total 50 mg/day) c. Age ≥ 12 years & wt 25 – 50 kg : 50 mg twice daily with meal × 28 days. (Total 100 mg/day) d. Adult (> 50 kg) : 75 mg twice daily with meal × 28 days. (Total 150 mg/day) 3. Combination Treatment : 2 nd Alternative Choice : Please see below – Same as combination therapies of TREATMENT FOR RKA. ► The 2 nd line of treatment including Amphotericin B Deoxycholate and Sodium Stibogluconate (SSG) are no longer used in NKEP of Bangladesh since the 1 st line of treatment is found much more superior and effective. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 34]

treatment.title: Retreatment Kala-Azar (RKA)
treatment.description: Combination Therapy : 1 st Alternative Choice : LAmB + Paromomycin : Liposomal Amphotericin B (LAmB) 5mg/kg IV infusion on day 1. Plus, Paromomycin 15mg/kg IM from day 2 to day 11. If for any reason this combination cannot be given for intolerance, hypersensitivity or relative contraindication, subsequent choice should be decided. ↓ Combination Therapy : 2 nd Alternative Choice : Miltefosine + Paromomycin : Miltefosine- oral form, the dose would be same as that of NKA for 10 days. Plus, Paromomycin 15mg/kg IM for 10 days. OR, LAmB + Miltefosine : LAmB 5mg/kg IV infusion on 1 st day. Plus, Miltefosine oral tablet from 2 nd day - 8 th day × 7 days. ↓ When combination fails : Liposomal Amphotericin B (LAmB), Dose – 3-5mg/kg/day total 15mg/kg in every alternate day. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 35]

treatment.title: Post-Kala-azar Dermal Leishmaniasis (PKDL)
treatment.description: First Line of Treatment : a. Miltefosine : Adult dose : 100 mg daily in two divided doses for 12 weeks. Children : 2.5 mg/kg/day in two divided doses, not exceeding 50mg/day for 12 weeks. ↓ Second Line of Treatment : a. Liposomal Amphotericin B (LAmB) : 5mg/kg/day total 20mg/kg in 4 divided dose once in a week. Or, b. Sodium Stibogluconate (SSG) : 20-mg/kg/day in intramuscular route. Total 6 cycles and each cycle consists of 20 days of treatment and 10 days in between two cycles. Or, c. Amphotericin B deoxycholate : Dose : 4 courses of 20 injections IV over 5-6 months in every alternate day dose. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 35]

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features – Unilateral swelling of leg (non-pitting edema) in filariasis endemic areas. Acute filarial lymphangitis presents with fever, pain, tenderness and erythema along the course of inflamed lymphatic vessels. Inflammation of the spermatic cord, epididymis and testis is common. Patient with swollen feet, legs, scrotum, vulva, hands, breast, thick irregular skin, lymphangitis, lymph node enlargement, itching, occasionally fever, rash, warm- tender limbs, cough, breathlessness etc. Investigation – ● Duplex study of vessels / lower limb : Deep VeinThrombosis (+); Cellulitis (-). ● ICT/CFT for Filaria. ● Eosinophillia. ● Direct visualization of microfilaria in blood at night (10 pm – 2 am), lymphatic exudates, hydrocele fluid, chylous urine. ● Adult worm in lymph nodes. Filariasis Elimination Program : DEC (Di-ethyl-carbamazine) + ALB (Albendazole). Age group DEC 100 mg ALB 400 mg Number of tablets 2 – 8 yr 1 1 2 > 8 – 12 yr 2 1 3 > 12 yr 3 1 4 Once & this cycle can be repeated 3 months interval for 6 cycles. Successive Period = 5 years. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 36] Another (12-days) regimen : DEC 6 mg /kg daily in 3 divided doses for 12 days. Should be started at low dose and gradually increase the dose. In tropical pulmonary eosinophillia same dose of DEC for 14 days. But one day treatment is generally as effective as the 12-day regimen. [Ref – Mass drug administration (MDA) : • Everybody in the community (endemic or relevant), except pregnant women, children below two years of age and persons who are very sick from other illness. • Administration of single dose of antifilarial drugs to the entire community (mass drug administration), yearly once for 5-6 years. • Protection from mosquito bites & enforce national vector (mosquito) control program. Supportive care : Hygiene, regular washing, elevation of limbs, crepe bandage, appropriate shoes, exercise, hydration, pain killer (NSAID), antibiotic, antifungal, antihistamine etc. Special Situations : 1. ADLA (Acute Dermato-Lymphangio Adenitis) : Paracetamol, Amoxycillin/Erythromycin, IV or IM Penicillin. Ceftriaxone IV/IM form can also be used in severe form of ADL. ▸ Soaking the affected limb in water of room temperature or rapping the limb with soaked towel. ▸ No weight lifting, waking. ▸ Take rest. ▸ No DEC by mouth during the attack of ADLA. 2. Hydrocele : Surgery (Hydrocelectomy – excision and eversion). 3. Lymphoedema of scrotum or penis : using scrotal sac, antibiotic, antifungal, reconstructive surgery. 4. Lymph Scrotum : Application of sterile bandage, repair of leaking, antibiotic, antifungal. 5. Chyluria : Low fat & high protein diet, plenty of water, avoid hard work & lifting, take rest. Refer for national surveillance : Filariasis is a disease under DGHS National surveillance. So if diagnosis is confirmed, must refer to upazilla health complex (UHC) or Civil Surgeon (CS) Office or 250 bedded Sadar hospital or Govt. medical college hospitals. May treat this patient conservatively or surgically in private or GP, but inform national health authority (DG Health) is a must.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : May have history of prolonged immersion in contaminated water (sweeper). 1. Fever. 2. Conjunctival congestion. 3. The patient may have a transient macular erythematous rash, but the characteristic skin changes are purpura and large areas of bruising. In severe cases there may be epistaxis, haematemesis and melaena, or bleeding into the pleural, pericardial or subarachnoid spaces. Jaundice present and liver enlarged, renal impairment present (Icteric leptospirosis or Weil's disease). Definitive diagnosis of leptospirosis depends upon isolation of the organism – blood for (C/S) culture and sensitivity. Management 1. Diet – normal, salt, fruit and protein restricted diet if AKI. 2. Blood transfusion- if bleeding manifestation. 3. Treatment of renal failure. 4. If uveitis then oral and local steroid. 5. Cap. Doxycycline 100 mg, 1+0+1 or, inj. ceftriaxone 1 gm daily for 1 week. (In any infectious disease intravenous & oral antibiotic therapy depends on patient's condition. If patient is toxic, vomits, cannot take oral foods, poor general condition then IV antibiotic should be used).

treatment.title: Analgesic Pharmacology
treatment.description: Analgesic Pharmacology Name & Available strength Prescribing note [Maximum dose] Generic / Brands Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) : Mefenamic acid 250 mg 500 mg 50 mg/5 ml (Syp) 500 mg TDS [Max 1500mg/day, for HPR 250 (paci) HPR-DS 500 Dysmen (ren) Fenaton (drg) Flamic (glob) Tolfenamic acid 200 mg 200 mg OD / SOS [Max 600 mg/day] Tufnil (skf) Tolfem (sq) Arain (ops) Mygan (incp) Tenoxicam 20 mg 20 mg OD [Max 40 mg/day] Raditil (rad), Oxicam (aci), Oxiflam (ibn), Xten (sq), Xicotil (aris), Tenorix (ori) Nabumetone 500 mg 750 mg 1000 mg 500 mg OD [Longest half-life (t ½ ), suitable for choric pain & once daily dosing.] [Max 1500 mg/day] Nabumet (skf), Alkanon (ren), Buflex (bex), Nabumax (pop), Nameton (acm), Nabu (aci), Elivier (incp) Aceclofenac 100 mg 200 mg 100 mg BD or SR 200 mg OD [Max 200 mg/day] Flexi (sq) Reservix (incp) Mervan (aris) Ternilla (hc) Zolfin (bex) Paino (skf) Diclofenac Tab. 50 mg (also 75 or 100 mg) Supp. 12.5/25/50 mg IM inj. 75 mg/3 ml 1% Gel 50 mg BD or 100 mg SR OD [Most GI-toxic, nephro-toxic but most potent] [Max 200 mg/day] Clofenac (sq) Voltalin (nuv) Diclofen (ops) A-Fenac (acm) Genac (glob) Ibuprofen 200 mg 300 mg 400 mg 100 mg/5 ml (Syp) 5% Gel Pediatric dose : 5-10 mg/kg/dose (6 hourly) Adult dose: 400 mg (6 hourly) [Max 3200 mg/day] Flamex (aci), Advel (ops), Profen (acm), Inflam (syn) [Safe in hepatic impairment & child] Dex-ibuprofen 300 mg 400 mg (also 200 mg) 100 mg/5 ml (Syp) 300 mg BD [Max 900 mg/day] Purifen (incp) Xflam (sq) Dexifen (bex) Ketoprofen 100 mg 200 mg (also 50 mg) IM inj. 100 mg/2 ml 100 mg BD or 50 mg QDS [Max 300 mg/day] Tab. Profenid-E (syn), Cap.
