treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: Opioid analgesics : Morphine, Nulbuphine, Pethidine, Tramadol. Vergon should be given with opioid analgesics. Result of chronic NSAID therapy : 1. Increased risk of cardiovascular disease. 2. NSAID-induced gastric ulcer. 3. Fluid retention and renal impairment. 4. Interstitial nephritis and anaphylaxis. Judicious use of NSAIDs : ● All NSAIDs reduce prostaglandin (PG) synthesis. Steroid also does this, in slightly different mechanism. Generally, they are nephrotoxic and GI toxic. In case of long time users, LFT & RFT must be done at every 6 – 8 weeks interval. ● Use NSAIDs at the lowest effective dose for the shortest possible time. Never prescribe more than one NSAIDs at a time. ● Allow minimum 14 days to assess efficacy of any particular NSAIDs or dose after that dose can be increased/changed. ● If unsatisfactory result (pain not reduce or side effects occurs) then change to another NSAIDs is necessary, as effect of NSAIDs is individualized. ● Self-education for limiting its use : Self-adjustment of NSAID dose is beneficial. A person should stop or reduce NSAID dose by himself when his pain comes down and becomes tolerable. ● NSAIDs are of risk of gastrointestinal upset, bleeding and perforation. NICE guidelines advise that a PPI should be co-prescribed with all NSAIDs, including COX-2-selective (e.g. etoricoxib) NSAIDs. ● Avoid NSAIDs in the elderly and in those with important comorbidity, including IHD, stroke, heart failure, CKD, CLD, acute hepatitis and hypertension. Current use of anticoagulants (warfarin) is a contraindication to NSAIDs use. ● Coxibs are contraindicated in people with ischaemic heart disease, cerebrovascular disease, peripheral arterial disease, mild, moderate or severe heart failure. ● Sulindac is the least nephrotoxic of all NSAIDs. Ibuprofen is the least GI toxic drug among all NSAIDs, safe for children. Naproxen is less nephrotoxic & GI toxic, cardio friendly suitable for elderly patients.

treatment.title: Analgesic ladder
treatment.description: Analgesic ladder : Paracetamol ↓ Paracetamol + NSAIDs (+ PPI) ↓ Paracetamol + NSAIDS + Pain Modulators ↓ Paracetamol + NSAIDS + Pain Modulators + Opioids ↓ Paracetamol + NSAIDs + Pain Modulators + Opioids + Steroid Pain Modulators : Anti-epileptic & Anti-depressant. Anti-epileptic : 1. Pregabalin, 2. Gabapentine. Anti-depressant : 1. TCA : Amitriptyline. 2. SSRI. Fluoxetine and Escetalopram. 3. SNRI : Duloxetine.

treatment.title: Low back pain (LBP)
treatment.description: Most common symptoms in adult. Only a small number of patients with back pain have a clear diagnosis, most LBP are of unknown aetiology. Self-limited is usual and resolve without specific therapy. Causes of Low Back Pain (LBP) : Mechanical : • Idiopathic (nonspecific) (most common) • Lumbar spondylosis • Disc herniation • Spondylolisthesis • Spinal stenosis • Fractures (mostly osteoporotic) Inflammatory : • Spondyloarthritis. Destructive : • Neoplastic • Infectious • Traumatic Metabolic : • Osteoporotic compression fractures • Paget's disease Referred pain : from major viscera, retroperitoneal structures, urogenital system, aorta, or hip Acute low back pain is defined as low back pain lasting less than 3 months. Inflammatory pain : Usually associated with morning stiffness, increased in morning or late night. Improves with exercise, aggravates by taking rest. Non Specific LBP : Improves with rest, aggravates by work or movement or exercise. Increased in evening. Distinguish nociceptive pain from radicular pain : Nociceptive pain is localized & Radicular Pain is band-like and it radiates down the leg, usually no local tenderness. * Non Specific LBP is usually cramping or spasmodic type. * Radicular pain is sharp, shooting, and electric. * Nociceptive pain is usually dull aching. Pain on forward flexion is consistent with nerve root irritation from a disk herniation. Pain on extension suggests spinal stenosis and/or spondylolisthesis. Tenderness over the vertebral bodies should prompt further investigation for vertebral metastasis or compression fracture.

treatment.title: Mechanical low back pain (LBP)
treatment.description: Features of (simple) Mechanical low back pain (LBP) Or, Non-specific LBP : ● Pain limited to back or upper leg. Simple LBP does not radiate beyond the knee (which would imply nerve root irritation). ● Pain varies with physical activity (relieve after taking rest). ● Onset often sudden and precipitated by lifting or bending. ● Recurrent episodes. ● Do not obey nerve root distribution. ● SLR : (-). ● No systemic feature. ● No morning stiffness. ● May have H/O trauma, weight lifting or fall, obese or over weight patient. Investigations : A. Acute mechanical back pain : No inv required if clinically confirmed. B. With persistent pain (> 6 weeks) or red flags : MRI of spine. C. Plain X-rays L/S – Lumbar curvature may be lost in simple LBP. In patients suspected of having Vertebral compression fractures, OA Degenerative disc disease. To exclude D/D : ESR, CRP normal (no inflammation), For safety of treatment : S. creatinine, SGPT. Management : 1. Reassurance, explanation (90% will recover by 6 weeks). 2. Weight loss in over weight patient. 3. Analgesic – Paracetamol next NSAID+PPI. 4. Muscle relaxants – T. Baclofen / Flexilax (sq) 5 or 10 mg, 1+1+1. 5. Same advice as given below (PLID). Red flags of low back pain : (Refer to rheumatologist / internist / orthopedic surgeon / physical medicine specialist) : 1. Age : Presentation 55 years. 2. Character : Constant, progressive pain unrelieved by rest. 3. H/O Carcinoma, TB, HIV, CKD, osteoporosis etc. 4. Constitutional symptoms & systemic upset, sweats, weight loss. 5. Major trauma. 6. Severe / symmetrical spinal deformity. 7. Painful spinal deformity / Thoracic pain. 8. Multiple levels of root signs. 9. Saddle anesthesia. 10. Progressive neurological sign/muscle wasting.

treatment.title: Exercise Therapy
treatment.description: জীবনশৈলী পরিবর্তন, ওষুধ, পাশাপাশি কিছু ব্যায়াম এর জন্য ফিজিক্যাল মেডিসিন ও রিহ্যাবিলিটেশন বিভাগ / বিশেষজ্ঞ-এর যোগাযোগ করবেন : কোমর ব্যথা কমাতে ব্যায়াম : ১. সমতল হালকা নরম বিছানায় চিত হয়ে শুয়ে দুই হাত শরীরের দুই পাশে রেখে দুই পা সোজা করে শুতে হবে। এরপর হাঁটু ভাঁজ না করে এক পা ওপরের দিকে তুলুন যত দূর সম্ভব। ১০ সেকেন্ড পা তুলে রাখতে হবে বা ১০ গোনা পর্যন্ত পা তুলে রাখতে পারেন। একইভাবে অপর পা ওপরে তুলুন এবং একই সময় নিন। ২.এবার একইভাবে হাঁটু ভাঁজ না করে একসঙ্গে দুই পা তুলতে হবে এবং একই সময় নিন। ৩. এবার এক হাঁটু ভাঁজ করে দুই হাত দিয়ে জড়িয়ে ধরে হাঁটুকে বুকে লাগানোর চেষ্টা করতে হবে। এভাবে ১০ সেকেন্ড পার করতে হবে। একইভাবে অপর হাঁটু বুকে লাগাতে হবে এবং একই সময় পার করুন। ৪. এবার একসঙ্গে দুই হাঁটু ভাঁজ করে দুহাতে জড়িয়ে বুকে লাগাতে হবে। ৫. সর্বশেষ ধাপটি হলো দুই পা সোজা করে পায়ের পাতার দিকে সটান করে ১০ সেকেন্ড রাখতে হবে। প্রতিটি ধাপ ১০ সেকেন্ড দীর্ঘায়িত হবে বা ১০ গোনা পর্যন্ত করতে হবে। এই ধাপগুলো অনুসরণ করে দু-তিনবার সকাল-রাতে করতে হবে — যা কোমরের মাংসপেশির প্রদাহ কমায় ও শক্তিশালী করে তোলে। ফলে কোমরে ব্যথা কমে আসে। সহজে ধারাবাহিক ধাপ : ১. পা উঠিয়ে ১০ সেকেন্ড ধরে রাখুন। ২. দুই পা ধরে বুকের কাছে নিন । ৩. কুঁজো হয়ে পা ধরার চেষ্টা করুন। ৪. পা ভাঁজ করে কোমর উঠিয়ে ১০ সেকেন্ড পর্যন্ত গুনুন। ৫. উপুর হয়ে শুয়ে পা ভাঁজ করে উঠান ৬. উপুর হয়ে শুয়ে সম্পূর্ণ পা ১০ সেকেন্ড ধরে রাখুন। ৭. কাঁধ উঠিয়ে ১০ সেকেন্ড ধরে রাখুন । ৮. চেয়ার ধরে মেরুদন্ড সোজা রেখে উঠবস করুন। কোমড় ব্যথা সাধারনত জটিল এবং ঝুকিপূর্ণ, তাই এসকল ব্যথার ক্ষেত্রে এক বা একাধিক বিশেষজ্ঞ চিকিৎসকের (অর্থোপেডিক, ফিজিক্যাল মেডিসিন, নিউরোলজি ইত্যাদি) ও ফিজিওথেরাপিস্টের পরামর্শ অনুযায়ী চিকিৎসা করা প্রয়োজন। কিছু কিছু কোমড় ব্যথা সামনের দিক ঝুকে কাজ করলে রোগের মাত্রা এবং ক্ষতি বৃদ্ধি পায়, তাই চিকিৎসকগন সামনে ঝুকে কাজ করতে নিষেধ করেন।

treatment.title: Straight Leg Raise (SLR)
treatment.disease_name: Prolapsed Lumbar Intervertebral Disc (PLID) or, Lumbar Disc Herniation
treatment.description: The Straight Leg Raise (SLR) test is commonly used to identify disc pathology or nerve root irritation, as it mechanically stresses lumbosacral nerve roots. Technique : With the patient lying down without pillow on their back on an examination table or floor, the examiner or asked to lift the patient's leg, keeping the knee is straight. Interpretation : If the patient experiences sciatic pain, and more specifically pain radiating down the leg (radiculopathy), when the straight leg is at an angle of between 30 and 70 degrees, then the test is positive and a herniated disk is a possible cause of the pain. A negative test (>70 ° ) suggests a likely different cause for back pain.

treatment.title: Features & Prognosis
treatment.disease_name: Prolapsed Lumbar Intervertebral Disc (PLID) or, Lumbar Disc Herniation
treatment.description: Prolapsed Lumbar Intervertebral Disc (PLID) or, Lumbar Disc Herniation ● ± LBP (low back pain) ± Leg pain. ● Patient present with sudden/gradual/repeated episodes of unilateral back pain radiates to lower limb (below knee); there may be tingling, numbness and paraesthesia of the affected limb. ● Constant aching pain is felt in the lumbar region and may radiate to the buttock, thigh, calf and foot. ● Pain is exacerbated by coughing or straining but may be relieved by lying flat. ● On examination – patient cannot stand straight (scoliosis present), SLR positive and lateral bending of the affected side cause intense pain. ● Investigation – MRI of L/S confirm the diagnosis with/without vertebral screening. Prognosis : ► If no/minimal relief : Refer to neurosurgeon : If no response to conservative treatment or progressive neurological deficits. Surgery : - No response to conservative treatment. - Progressive neurological deficits develop. - Central disc herniation with bilateral symptoms & signs & disturbance of sphincter function require urgent surgical decompression. ► Significant relief (70 – 80%) : • Restart daily activities. • Physiotherapy and exercises. • Don’t lift heavy weight, bend forward, squat, jumping, skipping, jogging. • Follow up of the patient with partial relief and clinic-radiological evidence with conservative treatment, refer if deterioration of symptoms.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Prolapsed Lumbar Intervertebral Disc (PLID) or, Lumbar Disc Herniation
treatment.description: Treatment : Analgesics (Pain Modulators) : ► Tab. Naproxen / Naprosyn (syn) 500mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) – 7-21 days ► Cap. Omeprazole / PPI (acm) 20mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) – 7-21 days ► Tab. Pregabalin / Gabarol CR (aci) / Neurolin CR (sq) / Nervalin CR (bex) 82.5 or, 165 mg, 0+0+ ½ – 7 days, then, 0+0+1 – 1-3 months ○ Early mobilization: bed rest does not help recovery. ○ Back strengthening exercises. ○ Avoid physical maneuvers likely to strain the lumbar spine. Vitamin D3 and B12 is necessary : ► Tab. Vit-B1+B6+B12 / Neurocare (bex) / Neuro-B (sq) / Neurobest (ren) / TPC (acm), 1 + 0 + 1 ---- 1 – 3 months ► Tab. Calcium Carbonate 500 mg + Vit-D3 200 IU / Ostocal (skf) / Calbo-D (sq)/Rocal-D (hc)/Calcin-D (ren), 1+1+0 ---- 1 – 3 months ± ► Tab. Baclofen / Beklo (ops) / Flexibac (bacn) / Flexilax (sq) / Bacmax (bacn), 10 mg or 5 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) ---- 1 – 3 months ± ► Oral steroid in tapering dose. উপদেশ : (১) দুই - চার (০২ – ০৪) সপ্তাহ সম্পূর্ণ বিশ্রাম করবেন। (২) পিড়া, টুল, নিচু চেয়ারে মাজা ভাঁজ করে বসবেন না। (৩) নিচে বসে কোন কাজ, যেমন - রান্নার কুটাবাছা, কাপড় ধোঁয়া ইত্যাদি করবেন না। পায়খানায় উচু কমোড / চেয়ার ব্যবহার করবেন। (৪) নিচে কোনো কাজ করতে হলে, মাজা কাত করে নিচু হবেন না বরং হাঁটু ভাঁজ করে বসে কাজটি করে, আবার হাঁটুতে চাপ দিয়োই উঠে দাঁড়াবেন। মাজাতে কোনোভাবেই চাপ দেবেন না। (৫) ভারী কাজ করবেন না। বসে থাকার বা শুয়ে থাকার সময় শিরদাঁড়া যত সম্ভব সোজা করে রাখবেন। (৬) উচুঁ-নিচু বা গদিযুক্ত বিছানায় শোবেন না। আঁকা-বাঁকা হয়ে শোবেন না। (৭) বেশী দিন বেদনানাশক / ব্যাথার বড়ির সেবন পরিহার করুন। NSAIDs, Steroid জাতীয় ওষুধ (Cortan / Deltasone / Decason) কখনোই চিকিৎসকের পরামর্শ ছাড়া খাবেন না ।

treatment.title: Diet, Catheter, Drain & T-Tube
treatment.description: Diet options : ● Normal or Full. ● Normal with plenty of water / coconut water / ORS / lemon juice intake. ● Normal with fluid, salt & fruits restriction. ● Semi-solid. ● Diabetic. ● Liquid. ● Protein-rich. ● NPO TFO (Nothing Per Oral till Further Order). ● NPO TFO except medication. Catheter removal : All catheters should be removed in the 1 st POD except in – 1. Surgery Kidney or ureter : 3rd or 4th POD after ensuring that there is no hematuria. 2. Surgery of urinary bladder or prostate : After 2 or 3 weeks. DRAIN TUBE MANAGEMENT Changing the gloves : Ensure that there is no air inside the gloves before insertion, Collection measurement : ● 1 finger of gloves : Approximately 10 ml. ● 5 fingers of gloves : 50 ml. ● Full Gloves : Approximately 100 ml. MANAGEMENT OF T-TUBE Measure the amount of bile daily in a sterile container connected with long vertical limb. Observe for : a. Jaundice : Increasing or not. b. Temperature : Increasing or not. c. Dressing is soaked with bile or not. d. Tenderness in the hypochondrium. Clamping of T-tube : On 7 th day : Clamp the t-tube. 7 th POD : Clamping for 2 hours 8 th POD : Clamping for 4 hours 9 th POD : Clamping for 8 hours 10 th POD : Clamping for 24 hours On 11 th day : T-tube cholangiogram should be done. If T-tube cholangiogram is normal then remove the t-tube on 12 th or 13 th day.

treatment.title: OT note & SOAP F/U
treatment.description: OPERATION NOTE (OT NOTE) Particulars of patient: Preoperative diagnosis: Date and time of operation: Elective or emergency procedure: Names of operating surgeon: Names of assistant surgeon: Name of anesthetist: Mode of anaesthesia: Operative procedure: Incision name: Operative diagnosis: Findings: Additional procedures performed and why: Any tissue removed or altered: Any prosthesis/implanted materials used: Details of closure technique: Detailed postoperative care instructions: Signature & Date. FOLLOW UP (“SOAP” protocol) Any follow up should be mainatin “SOAP” criteria – 1. S ubjective complication : - What patient compliants ? - Syptoms relieved or not ? 2. O bjective findings : ○ Pulse, ○ BP, ○ HR, ○ Heart, ○ Lung, ○ Temperature, ○ Urine Output (U/O), ○ Dehydration or fluid overload, ○ Drain tube collection (DTC), ○ Per-vaginal bleedig (PVB), ○ Bowel sound / Flatus etc. 3. A ssessment : Stable/improving/not improving/deteriorating. 4. P lan : Further investigation/ongoing treatment/all oral/add any drug/omit any drug/call any department/fresh order/discharge.

treatment.title: Gut Preparation
treatment.description: Preparation for Stomach Surgery : GUT PREPARATION For Gastrectomy / Gastro-jejunostomy : 3-day preparation (Diet Plan) : Day 3 before surgery : Low residual diet (Bread, Milk, Daal, liquid diet). Day 2 before surgery : Liquid Diet (Only glucose/ saline). Day 1 before surgery : NPO TFO. Inf. NS (2L) + 5% DNS (1L) (According to body weight) + Inj. KT 1 amp in each litre fluid. Gastric lavage : 1. Gastric lavage ideally done before each feed for 3 days before operation. 2. A 16 or 18 Fr. Ryles tube is inserted into the stomach and the gastric juice is aspirated. Normal saline is allowed to run through the Ryles tube and then aspirated back. 3. Give 200 ml normal saline through NG tube, then wait for 10-15 minute then aspirate. This is repeated till the return is clear. Preparation of Gut Surgery : 1. Aqualax 3 sachet mixed with 3L water & drink from (3pm to 6 pm) on the day before operation (250 ml every 15 mins). 2. Take ORS, Glucose water if needed. 3. In elderly patient if BP falls add Inf. Normal saline IV @ 25d/min. 4. Give Supp. Glysup 2.3gm 4 stick PR stat at 3pm on the day before OT. Enema : 1. Day before Surgery: In the Evening (6 pm). 2. On the day of Operation: In the Morning (6 am). For Perineal / anal surgery / Fistulotomy / Hemorrhoidectomy : Supp. Glysup 2.3 gm 4 stick PR Stat at 8:00pm be the day before operation. Gut Preparation for IVU Or X-Ray KUB Or USG of W/A : 1. Low residual diet (Bread, Milk, liquid diet). 2. Tab. Ultracarbon 2+2+2. 3. Syp. Lactulose 4 TSF BD.

treatment.title: ABCDE trauma care
treatment.description: Principle of emergency management 1. Primary Survey And Resuscitation : ATLS management : ABCDE of trauma care : A = Airway with cervical spine protection. B = Breathing and ventilation. C = Circulation with haemorrhage control. D = Disability (neurological status) limitation. E = Exposure : for assess other injuries. F = Fluid resuscitation. 2. Emergency Surgical toileting & suturing.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment following stitch / casualty / RTA / Physical Assault [Must refer to govt hospital if any assault for medico-legal documentation.] Diagnostic tools : ● H/O Physical Assault / RTA / Fall from height (FFH). ● Injury note : No visible abrasion, bruise or cut injury seen. Or, 1. Sharp cut injury over scalp measuring 3×2×muscle cm. 2. Another sharp cut injury over mid of flexor surface of right forearm measuring 3×2×bone cm. 3. Multiple abrasion on the front of the trunk, largest one, measuring 10×7.5 cm. In case of adult : ✔ Cap. Cefixime 200 mg or 400 mg / Cef-3 (sq) / Triocim (bex), 1 + 0 + 1 ---- 7 – 14 days ✔ Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg / Flux (ops) / Phylopen DS (sq), 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ---- 7 – 14 days ✔ Tab. Vitamin C 250 mg / Ceevit (sq)/ Vasco (ops)/ Ascobex(bex), 1+0+1 or, 1+1+1 ---- 10 days [Radivit-C 1000mg (rad)] ✔ Ointment Mupirocin 2%/ Bactrocin (sq) / Trego (incp) / Mupiderm (hc), apply locally TDS --- 10 – 20 days If pain present, ✔ Tab. Ketorolac 10mg /Todol (ops)/Xidolac (bex)/Acupain (bacn), 1+1+1 or 1+0+1 (after meal) ---- 5 – 14 days ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Losectil (skf) / Xeldrin (aci) / Xelopes (bacn), 1+0+1 (30 minutes before meal) --- 7 – 15 days Or, Avoid NSAIDs in asthmatic patient : ✔ Cap. Tramadol 50mg / Anadol (sq) / Utramal Retard (uni) / TRD-Contin (mundi), 1 + 1 + 1 (After meal) ---- 5 days ✔ Tab. Ondansetron 8 mg / Emistat (hc) / Anset (ops) / Emeren (ren) / Zofra (skf), 1+0+1 or, 1+1+1 ---- 5 days In case of child : ✔ Syrup. Cefixime 100mg/5ml / Fix-A (acm) / Denver (hc) / 3-C (edruc) / Cefim-3 (aci), 1 TSF for every 20 kg BD ---- 7 – 14 days [1-4 years: 1 TSF OD; 5-10years: 1 TSF BD] ✔ Syrup. Flucloxacillin 125mg/5ml / Fluclox (aci) / Phylopen (sq) / Flucloxin (sq), 1 TSF for every 8 kg QDS ---- 7 – 14 days [50-100mg/Kg/day 6 hourly] ✔ Tab. Vitamin C 250 mg / Ceevit (sq) / C-Rich (gen) / Vasco (ops) / Citrux-C (nipro) 250 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 --- 10 – 20 days ✔ Ointment Mupirocin 2%/ Bactrocin (sq) / Trego (incp) / Mupiderm (hc), apply locally TDS ---- 10 – 20 days ✔ Syrup. Paracetamol 120mg/5ml / Napa (bex) / Renova (ops), 1 TSF for every 8 Kg TDS/QDS – if pain --- 5 – 10 days ✔ Tab. Rabeprazole 10 mg / Finix (ops) / Rasonix JR (incp) / Xorel (ibn) / Paricel (aci) 10 mg, 1+0+1 ---- 7 – 14 days

treatment.title: Tetanus & antibiotics prophylaxis
treatment.description: Tetanus & antibiotics prophylaxis for skin cut injury or laceration or any trauma : ► Primary Treatment : always ensure good wound management like appropriate surgical debridement, toileting / wound cleaning and stitch / closure. ► Tetanus prophylaxis for dirty (soil/cow dung contaminated) wound or puncture wound by rusty nail or burn or deep/infected wound. Indicated in adults and children ≥ 7 years (as children are vaccinated against tetanus as per EPI schedule). ► Inj. Tetanus toxoid 40 IU/0.5 ml / TT vax (pop) / Vaxitet (incp) [95 BDT(/-)], 1 vial I/M stat on left arm. ► Inj. Human Tetanus Immunoglobulin 250 IU/ml / Protet-IG (incp) [975/-] / Tetagam-P (ial) [1300/-] 1 vial (or 2 vial, if severe) I/M stat on right arm. Tetanus Antitoxin [Equine] 3000 IU/ml Vaxitet-IG (incp) [408/-] IM Injection may be given on right arm instead of Human Tetanus Immunoglobulin 250 IU/ml / Protet-IG (incp). But Protet-IG is of human origin and vaxitet-IG is of horse origin. Hypersensitive reactions may occur after the injection of any serum of animal origin & must not be given in pregnancy. In case of extensive bums, it is advisable to administer a second injection of 250 IU human tetanus immunoglobulin after the exudative phase of the burn has subsided (about 36 hours after onset of the burn). ► TIG & TT is must in a simple case of minor bleeding RTA or rusty treenail, wire nail prick. TIG is of higher cost but should be administered. If patient refuse to take, note “Patient refuse to take TIG injection” on the admission ticket / form. ► TIG & TT must be given at different sites, otherwise antigens of TT & antibodies of TIG will neutralize each other. ► Must investigate radiologically, if any joint / bone is involved according to rule of two. Such as – X-ray left femur including both knee & hip joints B/V (100% Digital). ► BT demand is must if bleeding is significant. Antibiotics : ► Flucloxacillin or Cephradine (4 times daily) acts best on skin & soft tissue but Cefadroxil or Cefixime (2 times daily) may also be used. ► Give Cefixime 200 or 400 mg, 12 hourly for 7 days if immunocompromised (Like children, Old age, Diabetic patient, patient taking steroid or other immunocompromising drug etc.) ► Give Cefuroxime 500 mg 12 hourly for 7 instead of cefixime in case of fascia, muscle, tendon injury. ► Give Metronidazole 400 mg TDS or Ciprofloxacin 500 mg BD for 5 days if trauma involving oral cavity, intra-abdominal structures & perineum (Because of anaerobe. In traumatic breach, anaerobe can reach to heart and develop endocarditis).

treatment.title: Suture Off
treatment.description: APPROPRIATE SUTURE MATERIALS Scalp : Silk 2-0/ Prolene 2-0 cutting body. Extremity/ abdomen: Prolene 2-0 cutting body. Back: Prolene 1-0 cutting body. Face: Prolene 3-0/4-0 cutting body. Guidelines for Day of Suture Removal by Area Body Regions Removal Eyelid 3 – 4 Eyebrow 3 – 5 Nose 3 – 5 Lip 3 – 4 Face (other) 3 – 4 Scalp 6 – 8 Chest, abdomen 8 – 10 Ear 10 – 14 Back 12 – 14 Extremities 12 – 14 Hand 10 – 14 Foot, sole 12 – 14 Joints (over elbow and knee) 14 [As a whole : 5 days for face & scalp & 7 days for rest of the body.] Requirements : ╬ Inj. Jesocaine 2% ---- 1 ╬ 10 cc syringe ----- 2 ╬ Surgical gloves 7” ----- 2 ╬ Betadin / Apodine solution ---- 1 ╬ Mer Silk 2-0 C/B ----- 1 (for skin) If muscular layer involved, ╬ Vicryl 2-0 C/B ---- 1 Suture need for – 1. Scalp = Mer Silk / Silkus 2-0 C/B. 2. Face = Polypropylene / Prolene / Prolus 4-0 C/B. = if deep add : Polyglactin / Vicryl / Solus 3-0 R/B. 3. Limbs = Polypropylene / Prolene / Prolus 2-0/ 3-0 C/B. = if deep add : Polyglactin / Vicryl / Solus 3-0 R/B.

treatment.title: Trauma over abdomen (Indoor Treatment)
treatment.description: Trauma over abdomen Following Physical Assault / Stab Injury / RTA / Fall from height (FFH) Diagnostic tools : 1. C/C : Abdominal pain ± distension following H/O Physical Assault / RTA / Fall from height (FFH)__ hours. 2. O/E : - Pulse & BP. - Features of shock. - Lung. - Abdomen : soft / rigid, bowel sound +/-, - Percussion : liver dullness. - Anemia & Jaundice. 3. Injury note : Multiple abrasion & bruise on the front of the abdomen / trunk. Or, no visible abrasion, bruise or cut injury seen. Δ Trauma over chest & abdomen Adv. √ Plain X-ray abdomen A/P view with both dome of diaphragm in erect posture. (100% Digital). √ CXR P/A view (if suspected trauma over chest). √ USG of W/A. √ Blood grouping, Rh typing & Demand. Tx, ¤ NPO TFO. ¤ NG suction 2 hourly ¤ O2 inhalation sos. ¤ Inf. H/S 3 L, IV stat @ 30 d/m. ¤ Inj. Ciprofloxacin 200mg/100 ml, 1 bottle / bag IV stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Metronidazole (500 mg/100 ml), 1 bottle / bag IV stat & TDS. ¤ Inj. Nalbuphine (20 mg/2 ml) / Nalbun-2, 1 amp IM stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Prochlorperazine 12.5 mg(1 ml)/Vergon, 1 amp IM stat & BD c̄ Nalbuphine. ¤ Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Prevas 40 mg, 1 vial IV stat & BD. ¤ Continuous catheterization. ¤ Please monitor vital signs. Discussion : - Other investigations should be done according to complaints, such as – X-ray Pelvis B/V or affected joint B/V. - Blood grouping, Rh typing, screening, cross matching & demand are must as ruptured spleen / ruptured abdominal organ causes internal bleeding & shock, even without any visible bleeding. - If patient is in shock, increase the inf. H/S 3L to 5 L & give 1 / 2 bottle I/V stat @ running. - If the gut are exposed through abdomen, use Gypsonate / Supratulle / Paraffin gauze over gut then a loose bandage round the abdomen at admission. - May add : ¤ Inj. Amikacin 500 mg / Kacin, 1 amp IV stat & BD. Ceftriaxone 1g BD + Metronidazole 100 ml TDS + Amikacin 500mg BD combination may also be used. - Exclude pneumothorax by CXR P/A view & clinically, if trauma over chest with respiratory distress.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Close Fractures ( ≠ ) Diagnostic tools : 1. C/C : - Blunt trauma over (site) / H/O fall from height (FFH) or road traffic accident (RTA) / Physical Assault (PA). - Swelling / pain / deformity of the (site) for S/D. 2. O/E : - Site : Tender & swollen ± reddening. - Raised local temperature. - Pulse & BP. 3. Adv. : X-ray of affected site including two adjacent joints both view. Urgent X-ray is mandatory, according to rule of “2” – 1. Two views : AP & Lateral / Both view. 2. Two joints : above & below ≠ site. 3. Two occasions : Immediate & 1/2 weeks later. 4. Two limbs : In children. But following patients will not advise X-ray at the time of patient receive, rather X-ray will be advised after adequate resuscitation. 1. # Femur (Open / Close). 2. # Pelvis (Open / Close). 3. # Tibia (Open / Close). 4. # Hypovolemic shock due to bleeding (Open / Close). 4. Injury note : There is a close fracture of shaft of right (rt) femur, X-ray plate no. 3251 (DMCH, 5/8/2024). A sharp cut injury over flexor surface of left (lt) forearm, measuring 3×2×muscle cm. Multiple abrasion over front of the chest, largest one measuring 7.5×2. Immediate (ATLS) / Primary survey / ABCDE of trauma care – A : Airway with cervical spine protection. B : Breathing and ventilation. C : Circulation with haemorrhage control. D : Disability : neurological status. E : Exposure : undress the patient and assess for other injuries.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discussion
treatment.description: Δ Close ≠ Shaft of Rt Humerus following RTA. Adv. : ▸ Xray Rt humerus including both elbow & shoulder joint B/V (100% Digital). Tx, ¤ Diet : Normal. ¤ Inj. Diclofenac / Diclofen 75 mg, 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. ¤ Cap. Omeprazole / Maxpro 20 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 (B/M) ¤ Tab. Diazepam / Easium 5 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ¤ Elevation of affected limb. ✔ In femur & pelvis close fracture, hemolysis & internal hemorrhage is common. Immediate blood requisition amount : 1. Fracture of shaft of femur : 2-3 units. 2. Fracture pelvis : 3-4 unit. 3. Tibia fracture : 1-2 unit. So, 2 units for shaft of femur & 3 unit for pelvis must be given. ✔ Principle is not to open a close fracture & not to close immediately an open fracture. But incase seniors want to open reduction under GA, then follow – ¤ NPO for 6 hours (prior to OT). ¤ Inf. H/S 1L, I/V stat @ 20 d/m. ✔ If localized swelling add anti-inflammatory & anti-edema – ¤ Tab. Bromelain 50mg + Trypsin 1 mg / Kontab, 2+2+2 or, 1+1+1 (Before meal) for 5 – 7 days. [Kontab is not authorized by govt. drug authority.] ✔ Before using NSAIDs exclude bronchial asthma (BA), PUD & other medical illness, use centrally acting analgesics like tramadol, nalbuphine, other opioids etc. ✔ Ketorolac of another choice of analgesic – ¤ Inj. Ketorolac / Torax 30 mg, 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. ✔ Nalbuphine with Vergon, in case of severe pain – ¤ Inj. Nalbuphine 20mg / Nalbun-2, 1 amp I/M stat & sos. ¤ Inj. Prochlorperazine 12.5mg / Vergon, 1 amp I/M stat & sos c̄ Nalbuphine. ✔ GA fitness for adults – 1. CBC, ESR, Hb%. 2. RBS. 3. S. Creatinine. 4. Urine R/M/E. 5. CXR P/A view. 6. ECG (if abnormal then, Echo). ✔ GA fitness for children – 1. CBC, ESR, Hb%. 2. CT, BT (for male child). 3. CXR P/A view.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discussion
treatment.description: ∆ Open Fracture ≠ Δ Open ≠ Shaft if Lt Humerus following RTA Adv. : ▸ X-ray Left (Lt) humerus including both elbow & shoulder joint B/V (100% Digital). Tx, ¤ Diet : Normal. ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftron (sq) 1gm, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ¤ Cap. Flucloxacillin / Flupen (drg) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ¤ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Seclo (sq), 1 + 0 + 1 (B/M) ¤ Inj. Diclofenac / Clofenac 75 mg, 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. ¤ Inj. TT 40IU / Vaxitet, 1 amp I/M stat. ¤ Inj. Tetanus Antitoxin / Vaxitet-IG, 1 amp I/M stat on another arm. ¤ Elevation of affected limb. Discussion : ✔ Surgical toileting & debridement is must. ╠ 10 cc syringe – (1) ╠ Hand gloves 7” – (3) ╠ Inf. N/S 1L – (3) ╠ Povidone Iodine 10% / Povisep solution – (1) ╠ H 2 O 2 / Joly – (1) ╠ Inj. Lidocaine / Jasocain – (1) ╠ Acetic Acid 6% / Pran Vinegar – (1) [if greenish pus / Pseudomonas is suspected.] ╠ Vicryl for muscles & silk / prolene for skin. [Never try primary closure in an open ≠ ] First wash visible dirt by N/S or soap & tap water. Removal of hair (in case of hairy area) is must. ✔ Requirements for plasters – ╠ POP (Indian) – (3-6, according to need). ╠ Soft roll (1-3, according to need). ╠ Roll bandage (Supplied by government). ✔ Effect of calcium & Vit-D3 / Cholecalciferol in fractures is still under study, but seniors use it. Ø Tab. Coral Calcium 500 mg+ Vit-D3 200 IU / Coralex (acm) / Becoral D (bex) / CoralCal-D (rad) / Reef D (hc) / Coralcin-D (ren), ০+১+০ বা ১+১+০ (আহারের পরে) ...... ১ - ২ মাস ± Ø Cap. Vit-D3 (Cholecalciferol) / D-rise 20,000IU, প্রতি সপ্তাহে একবার খাবেন ...... ১ - ২ মাস ✔ Principle of Fracture Care “4R” – 1. Resuscitation : - Control of bleeding : Immediately ligate bleeding vessels with polyglactin sutures / vicryl. - Correction of hypovolemic shock, if BP falls. IV Hartmann or volume dilators (Plasmasol / Albumin) & BT is immediate tasks. 2. Reduction : Close / Open. Aim is adequate apposition & normal anatomic alignment of the bone fragment as far as possible. 3. Retention / Immobilization : Atleast a cast on admission. 4. Rehabilitation : Adequate rest & mild exercise before fully involved life.

treatment.title: Low Back Pain (LBP)
treatment.description: ∆ Low Back Pain (LBP) : ∆ Low Back Pain (LBP) following RTA / Mechanical musculo-skeletal pain. Adv. : ▸ Xray Lumbo-sacral spine B/V. (100% Digital). [according to clinical suspect] Tx, ¤ Diet : Normal. ¤ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Opton (bex), 1 + 0 + 1 (B/M) ¤ Inj. Ketorolac / Zeropain (hc) 30 mg, 1 amp I/M stat & BD / TDS. ¤ Tab. Tizanidine / Sirdalud (san) 2 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 ¤ Diclofenac / Clofenac (sq) 1% Gel, Apply locally BD / TDS. ± ¤ Tab. Diazepam / Sedil (bex) 5 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ✔ Alternate choice of muscle relaxants – ¤ Tab. Tolperisone / Myolax 50 mg, 1 + 1 + 1. ¤ Tab. Beclofen / Flexibac 10 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 / 1+1+1. ✔ For chronic pain – ¤ Tab. Amytriptyline/Tryptin 10/25mg, 0+0+1 (H/S or, at night) ✔ Pregabalin / Gabapentin for twinging sensation.

treatment.title: Practical / OT Notes
treatment.description: 1. Shoulder joint dislocation = Adv. X-ray Rt shoulder B/V. = Close reduction under GA (Kocher method) & immobilization. Clinical signs : 1. Flattening of contour of the shoulder. 2. Hamilton’s ruler test : Tip of acromion and the lateral epicondyle of humerous can be touched by a straight ruler. 3. Dugo’s test : Patient can’t touch the opposite shoulder by affected sided hand. 2. ≠ shaft of humerus = U-cast. 3. Supracondylar ≠ of humerous / Epiphyseal separation (Child) = Long arm back slab (LABS). 4. ≠ shaft of radius ± ulna = i. Upper ⅓ = Close reduction + Pronation + LABS. ii. Middle ⅓ = Close reduction + Mid-Prone + LABS. iii. Colles’ ≠ / Lower ⅓ = Close reduction + Supination + Ulnar deviation + Glass holding manner + LABS. 5. ≠ Clavicle / Scapula = Elbow bag or, Triangular sling. Requirements for triangular sling = ১. মার্কিন কাপড় ১ গজ। ২. সেফটি পিন ১ ডজন। 6. ≠ Neck ± shaft of femur = i. Surface traction. ii. Skeletal traction. iii. Hip spika (Child). 7. ≠ Suprachondylar / Tibia / Fibula = Long leg back slab (LLBS). 8. Patellar ≠ = Cylinder plaster ± K-wire fixation. 9. Avulsed distal phalange (hand/foot) = Needle fixation. 10. Cut extensor tendons of forearms → Repair of tendons (modified Kessler suture by Polypropylene / Prolene 2/0) → Repair of paratenon → Repair of extensor retinaculum → Short arm anterior slab with cock up position. 11. Cut flexor tendons of forearms → Repair of tendons (modified Kessler suture by Polypropylene / Prolene 2/0) → Repair of paratenon → Repair of flexor retinaculum → Short arm back slab. 12. Cut tendo-achilles → Repair of tendons (modified Kessler suture by Polypropylene / Prolene 2) → Repair of paratenon → Long leg anterior slab (LLAS).

treatment.title: Consideration in Regional Anesthesia
treatment.description: ► Inj. Lidocaine + Adrenaline / Jasocaine A Or, Inj. Lidocaine / Jasocaine = - Adrenaline causes vasoconstriction & prolongs time of anesthesia. But should use in trunk & upper extremities, should not use in phalanges / peripheral parts. - Use 10 cc / 20 cc syringe to draw lignocaine from vial & never re-enter needle into same vial, in case of re-entry of needle discard the vial / bottle, as it has already contaminated with that patient’s blood. - For re-entry a sterilized needle may kept in situ in the vial’s sponge zone. Thus contents of the bottle will not be contaminated. - Never to use more than 20mL lidocaine, if you need more then mixed 20mL lidocaine with normal saline (N/S). Keep in mind lidocaine is cardio-toxic. - Lidocaine should not be injected into vessels, it causes convulsion & even death. So, a back suction from syringe during infiltration is needed weather fresh blood comes into syringe or not. If blood comes into syringe, never to inject. Intra dermal (I/D)/ sub-cutaneous (S/C) are the choice of drug administration. Exception : All reduction should be done under GA/SAB. But in case of hematoma block at fracture site (Colle’s ≠ ), a back suction to see the blood, confirms the needle is within the hematoma. - Perform digital block & wrist block in case of larger site / wire fixation, not use lidocaine locally.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Acute Osteomyelitis : C/C : - Local pain & swelling (at the end of) long bone with local swelling. Fever 102°-103°F. Investigation : 1. TC, DC = Neutrophilic leukocytosis. 2. X-ray of the part = Elevation of periosteum + Soft tissue swelling. Chronic Osteomyelitis : C/C : - Recurrent pain (especially at night), swelling & fever. Investigation : 1. TC, DC = Neutrophilic leukocytosis. 2. X-ray of the part = Elevation of periosteum + Soft tissue swelling.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Acute Osteomyelitis Adv. : ▸ Pus/blood for C/S ▸ CBC with ESR ▸ CRP ▸ Plain X-ray ▸ MRI ▸ Tissue culture ▸ Bone scan Tx, ¤ Diet : Normal. ¤ Absolute bed rest. ¤ Immobilization of the limb by splintage. ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Triject (incp) 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & 12 hourly. ¤ Inj. Flucloxacillin / Flux (ops) 500 mg, 1 vial I/V stat & 6 hourly. ¤ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Xelopes (bacn), 1 + 0 + 1 (½ hr B/M) ¤ Inj. Ketorolac 30 mg / Toradolin (rad), 1 amp I/M stat & 8 hourly ¤ Supp. Diclofenac / Clofenac (sq) 50 mg, 1 stick P/R sos. ¤ Incision & drainage under GA and pus sent for culture & sensitivity (C/S). ► In chronic osteomyelitis : use mild oral NSAIDs & surgical options – 1. Sequestrectomy. 2. Evacuation & curettage for Brodie’s abscess. 3. Amputation. Discharge note : ► Cap. Cefixime 200mg, 1+0+1 –– 1 month ► Cap. Flucloxacillin 500mg ,1+1+1+1 –– 1 month ► Tab. Ketorolac 10mg, 1+1+1- A/M- If pain present –– 5-15 days ► Cap. Omeprazole 20mg, 1+0+1-30 min before meal – 5-15 days উপদেশ : নিয়মিত ঔষধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে কোন সমস্যা হলে অর্থো-সার্জারি বহির্বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন।

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Management : 1. Primary Survey And Resuscitation : ATLS management. 2. Emergency Surgical toileting & suturing. 3. Hospitalization & neuro-surgical intervention. Questionnaire if H/O head injury : ● History of unconsciousness at any time since injury? ● Amnesia for the incident or subsequent events? ● Severe and persistent headache, nausea, vomiting? ● Bleeding from nose/ear? ● Seizures or presence of black eye? ● Suspected fracture of skull and hematoma of scalp? Glasgow Coma Score (GCS) : Head injury clinical classification. Traits Score Eyes open Spontaneously 4 To verbal command 3 To painful stimulus 2 Do not open 1 Verbal Normal oriented conversation 5 Confused 4 Inappropriate/words only 3 Sounds only 2 No sounds 1 Intubated patient T Motor Obeys commands 6 Localises to pain 5 Withdrawal/flexion 4 Abnormal flexion 3 Extension 2 No motor response 1 Total Score 15/15 (Dead patient) Lowest Score 3/15 National Institute for Health and Clinical Excellence (NICE) guidelines for computed tomography (CT) in head injury : 1. GCS 2. GCS 13 or 14 at 2 hours. 3. Focal neurological de ﬁ cit. 4. Suspected open, depressed or basal skull fracture. 5. More than one episode of vomiting. 6. Any patient with a mild head injury over the age of 65 years or with a coagulopathy, for instance warfarin use, should be scanned urgently. 7. Dangerous mechanism or injury or anteg-rade amnesia >30 minute warrants CT within 8 hours.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Head injury Adv. : ▸ CT scan of brain. Injury note : A lacerated wound over right frontal region measuring 7.5cm×1cm×bone. Tx, ¤ NPO-UFO. ¤ Complete bed rest with head raised 30°-45° above level of heart. ¤ O-P suction & O2 inhahation sos. ¤ Inf. N/S / Solo (sq) / N/S-acme 1L, IV stat @ 30 d/m. ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Oricef (hc) 1g, 1 vial IV stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Omeprazole / PPI (acm) 40 mg, 1 vial I/V stat & BD ¤ Inj. Ketorolac / Toradolin (30 mg), 1 amp I/M stat & BD ¤ Inj. Dexamethasone 5 mg / Decason, 1 amp I/V stat & TDS ¤ Inj. Phenobarbitine 200 mg / Barbit, 1 amp I/M stat & BD

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Never use 5% DA as it is hypotonic when metabolized and aggravates hypovolaemia. It increases risk of cerebral edema. ► If dirty laceration / cut injury present, add – ¤ Inj. TIG / Vexitet IG (incp), 1 amp I/M stat on right arm. ¤ Inj. TT / Vexitet (incp) / TT vax (pop), 1 amp I/M stat on left arm. Add antibiotic (with ceftriaxone) – ¤ Inj. Flucloxacillin / Flux (ops) 500 mg, 1 vial IV stat & QDS. ► If patient is unconscious – ¤ NG tube feeding. 200 ml for 5 times from 8 am to 10 pm. ¤ Continuous catheterization. ¤ Change posture 2 hourly. ¤ Pneumatic bed / Foam (Swan) bed. ► Incase of vomiting – ¤ Inj. Ondansetron 8 mg / Emistat (hc), 1 amp I/V stat & TDS or sos. ► Incase of bleeding per ear, nose or hematuria – ¤ Inj. Tranexamic Acid / Traxyl (nuv) / Xamic (ren) 500 mg, 1 amp I/V stat & TDS. ► Incase of restless patient – ¤ Inj. Haloperidol 5 mg/1ml (Route : IM only) / Halopid (incp) / Perigen (gen) / Perol (ambee), 1 amp I/M stat & sos. ¤ Inj. Procyclidine 10 mg/2ml (Route : IV or IM) / Perkinil (sq)/ Cyclid (incp)/ Extranil (gen)/ Kdrin (ops), 1 or ½ amp I/M stat & sos c̄ Haloperidol. ► Incase of active (during) convulsion – ¤ Inj. Phenobarbitine 200 mg / Barbit (incp), 1 amp I/V stat & BD. Or, ¤ Inj. Levetiracetam 500 lamp I/V stat and BD. Indication of Phenobarbitone / Levetiracetam in head injury : 1. Acute subdural, epidural, or intracerebral hematoma (SDH, EDH or ICH) 2. Open-depressed skull fracture. 3. Brain contusion on CT. 4. Seizure within the first24 hours after injury 5. Glasgow Coma Scale score 6. Penetrating brain injury. 7. History of significant alcohol abuse. ► Clinical signs of intracranial hypertension (IC-HTN) & clinical signs of herniation : 1. Pupillary dilatation (unilateral or bilateral). 2. Asymmetric pupillary reaction to light. 3. Decerebrate or decorticate posturing (usually contralateral to blown pupil) 4. Progressive deterioration of the neurologic exam not attributable to extra-cranial factors. IC-HTN may produce a bulging fontanelle in an infant. "Blown pupil" means fixed & dilated pupil (sun set eyes). ► Indications of mannitol : 1. Evidence of intracranial hypertension. 2. Evidence of mass effect (focal deficit, e.g., hemiparesis). 3. Sudden deterioration prior to CT (including pupillary dilatation) 4. After CT, if a lesion that is associated with increased ICP is identified. 5. After CT, if going to operating room. 6. To assess "salvageability": in patient with no evidence of brainstem function, look for return of Brainstem reflexes. ► If Raised intra-cranial pressure (ICP) : ¤ Ensure catheterization & add Inj. Mannitol 20% IV. ¤ Inj. Mannitol 20% IV 300 ml, I/V running stat, then 150 ml running TDS for 3 days. ● Inj. Mannitol 20% / Osmosol (bex) / Osmotic (pop) / Manisol (ori) / Mannitol (osl) / Acme's Mannitol (acm), 500 ml (155/-). ● Dose : 0.25 – 1 g IV every 3 to 4 hours. Children dose: 1-2 gm/kg body weight (Mannitol 20% means 100 ml contain 20mg)]. ► Discharge criteria in minor and mild head injury : 1. GCS 15/15 with no focal de ﬁ cits. 2. Normal CT brain if indicated. 3. Patient not under the in ﬂ uence of alcohol or drugs. 4. Patient accompanied by a responsible adult. 5. Verbal and written head injury advice to seek medical attention if any of them appears : • Persistent/worsening headache despite analgesia. • Persistent vomiting. • Drowsiness. • Visual disturbance. • Limb weakness or numbness.

treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : 1. H/O : - H/O RTA / Physical assault ___hours ago. - Numbness & weakness of lower limbs / paraplegia. - ± Urinary / bowel incontinence / Involuntary escape. Or, Hanging : - H/O Suicidal attempt. - accidental scarf wrapping over neck in auto-vehicles. 2. O/G/E : - BP, ±Dehydration, ± Features of shock. - Glasgow Coma Score (GCS). 3. Inv. : X-ray ± MRI of affected region. Δ Hanging Adv. : ▸ MRI of Brain c̄ spinal screening. Injury note : “note the injuries” Minor & Conscious patient : ¤ Diet : Full. ¤ O2 inhalation sos. ¤ Tab. Ciprofloxacin / Ciprocin (sq) / Cipro (apollo) 500 mg, 1 + 0 + 1. ¤ Tab. Vit-B1+ B6+ B12 / Neuro B (sq) / Myelin (ops), 1 + 1 + 1. ± ¤ Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Toradolin (rad) / Toramax (pop), 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M). ¤ Tab. Baclofen / Beklo (ops) / Flexibac (bacn) 5 or 10 mg, 1 + 1 + 1. ¤ Absolute bed rest. ¤ Change posture 2 hourly. ¤ Continuous catheterization. ¤ Cervical / Collar Cuff, if injured neck / Cervical spine. ¤ Trilon / Lumbosacral Brace, if injured lumber-scaral spine. ► Add according to seniors / obvious sing of spinal injury / radiculopathy – ¤ Inj. Methylprednisolone / Solupred (zis) 1g + Inf. N/S 100mL, I/V stat @ 30 d/m for three (03) consecutive day. ► Incase of restless patient – ¤ Inj. Haloperidol 5 mg/1ml / Halopid, 1 amp I/M stat & sos. ¤ Inj. Procyclidine 10 mg/2ml / Perkinil, 1 amp I/M stat & sos. ► For accidental scarf wrapping over neck in autovehicles : no need to urinary catherization, if patient is well alert. For abrasion add – ¤ Povidone Iodine 5% / Betadine ointment, Apply locally thrice daily. Or, ¤ Mupirocin 2% / Bactrocin ointment, Apply locally thrice daily.

treatment.title: Decerebrate & Decorticate posturing
treatment.description: DECORTICATE POSTURING Decorticate posturing is a pose that human body may hold automatically because of damage to or disruptions in brain & medulla, Such as – stroke, trauma, RTA, hanging, hypoxia, drowning etc. DECEREBRATE RIGIDITY : Decorticate posturing is also called decorticate response, flexor posturing, or, colloquially, "mummy baby". Decerebrate posture results from damage to the upper brain stem. In this posture, the arms are adducted and extended, with the wrists pronated and the fingers flexed. The legs are stiffly extended, with plantar flexion of the feet. ● Upper and lower limb extended. ● Lesion below midbrain. ● Temperature regulation absent. ● Not better outcome. Opisthotonos in tetanus (a severe muscle spasm of the neck and back) may appear the same as decerebrate posture, but that is of neuro-muscular abnormality. DECORTICATE RIGIDITY : Decorticate posture results from damage to one or both corticospinal tracts. In this posture, the arms are adducted and flexed, with the wrists and fingers flexed on the chest. The legs are stiffly extended and internally rotated, with plantar flexion of the feet. ● Upper limb flexed and lower limb extended. ● Lesion above midbrain. ● Temperature regulation present. ● Better outcome.

treatment.title: Spinal TB / Pott’s Spine
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : H/O : - Spinal deformities / Kyphosis. - Paraplegia. - Back pain & spinal tenderness. - Stiffness & weakness. Adv. : - X-ray of the spine. - CT scan of Lumbo-sacral (L/S) spine (with screening of rest of the spine). Osteoarticular TB and spinal TB (Pott’s disease) : Treatment of osteoarticular TB and spinal TB for treatment is 12 months with 2 (HRZE)/10 (HR). Pott's disease is a severe form of TB that should be treated on a priority basis because of the risk of neurological sequelae due to the chronic compression of the spinal nerves. -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- Ø Weight 50 Kg. Ø Tab. Rimstar 4-FDC, ৩ + ০ + ০ (খাবার ৩০ মিনিট পূর্বে) ...... ১০ মাস (১১ সেপ্টেম্বর, ২০২৪ থেকে ১০ জুলাই,২০২৫), তারপরে, Ø 2-FDC, ৩ + ০ + ০ Or, Tab. Rifampicin 450 mg + Isoniazid 300 mg / Rimactazid 450 / Rifagen, ১ + ০ + ০ (খাবার ৩০ মিনিট পূর্বে) ……. ০২ মাস (১১ জুলাই, ২০২৫ থেকে ১০ সেপ্টেম্বর,২০২৫) Ø Tab. Levofloxacin / Evo (bex) / Leo (acm) 750 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ (or 3 tab of 250 mg) …… ৬ – ১২ মাস Ø Tab. Vit-B6 (Pyridoxine) 25 mg / Sixvit (bacn) / Pyrovit (phar), ০ + ১ + ০ ....... ১২ মাস ± Ø Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan 20 mg, ২ + ০ + ০ (খাবার পরে) ...... ১ মাস, তারপরে, ১ ½ + ০ + ০ (খাবার পরে) ...... ৭ দিন, তারপরে, ১ + ০ + ০ (খাবার পরে) ...... ৭ দিন, তারপরে, ½ + ০ + ০ (খাবার পরে) ...... ৭ দিন, তারপরে, ¼ + ০ + ০ (খাবার পরে) ...... ৭ দিন ± Ø Cap. Esomeprazole 20 mg / Esoprex (bacn) / Sergel (hc), ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার আধাঘন্টা পূর্বে) ...... ১ – ২ মাস উপদেশ : সম্পূর্ণ বিশ্রাম থাকবেন ও দুই (০২) মাস পর দেখা করাবেন করবেন। নিকটস্থ যক্ষা কেন্দ্র বা সরকারী হাসপাতাল থেকে ঔষধ সংগ্রহ করবেন। পরবর্তীতে এই সমস্যার জন্য পূর্বের সব কাগজপাতিসহ নিউরো-সাজার্রি বহি:বিভাগে দেখা করবেন। ► EP TB patients who do not improve at the end of 6 months must be investigated for drug resistant TB and should be referred to specialist physicians or tertiary care centres. ► If rifampicin resistance is detected, an MDR-TB regimen should be prescribed according to recent drug resistant TB treatment guidelines. ► Anti-tuberculosis tablets are according to weight : FDC tablets are composed as follows : • 4 FDC : isoniazid 75 mg + rifampicin 150 mg + pyrazinamide 400 mg + ethambutol 275 mg. • 2 FDC : isoniazid 75 mg + rifampicin 150 mg. Pre-treatment weight (Kg) Intensive phase daily during first 2 months Continuation phase daily during next 4 months Number of 4 FDC tablets Number of 2 FDC tablets 30 - 37 2 2 38 - 54 3 3 55 - 70 4 4 > 70 5 5 ► It is also recommended that all patients with TB meningitis and TB pericarditis, an initial adjuvant corticosteroid therapy with dexamethasone or prednisolone, tapered over 6-8 weeks, should be used. The drug most frequently used is prednisolone, in a dosage of 0.5 – 1 mg/kg daily, increased up to 2 mg/kg daily in the case of severely ill patients, for 4 weeks. The dose should then be gradually tapered down @ 2.5 – 5 mg every week over 4 to 8 weeks (1-2 months in total duration).
