treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Spinal Headache / Post Dural Puncture Headache (PDPH)
treatment.description: H/O – Headache following any operation under SAB / Lumber puncture. CSF leakage / decreased CSF is the cause of this headache. Δ PDPH following LP If continuous CSF leakage – Epidural blood patch / seal. Tx, ¤ Diet : Full. ¤ Inf. N/S / Solo (sq) 1 L, I/V stat @ 20 d/m ¤ Tab. Paracetamol / Reset (incp) 500 mg, 2 + 2 + 2 (A/M) If vertigo add – ¤ Tab. Prochlorperazine 5mg/ Stemetil (syn), 1 + 1 + 1 ¤ Tab. Procyclidine 5 mg / Perkinil (sq), ½ + 0 + ½ Non-pharmacological : ¤ Lie flat without pillow. ¤ Take plenty of water.

treatment.title: Primary & Secondary Lesions
treatment.description: Terms used to describe skin lesion / Cutaneous sign : Primary Lesions / Lesion types : 1. Macule (Spot / colour change) : a circumscribed flat area of skin of ≤ 1cm diameter that appears different from the skin surrounding it, usually because of different colour. e.g. freckle. 2. Patch : the same as a macule but larger. (up to 15-20 cm) 3. Papule : a discrete elevation of skin of ≤ 1cm diameter. (Size up to 0.5cm) 4. Nodule : similar to a papule but having significant depth (into dermis or even subcutaneous layer) and usually > 1 cm in diameter. 5. Tumours. 6. Plaque : a raised area of skin with a flat top, >1 cm in diameter. e.g. psoriasis. 7. Vesicle / Blister : a small ( ≤ 1 cm) fluid-filled blister. e.g. pemphigoid. 8. Bulla / Bullae : and a larger (> 1 cm in diameter) fluid-filled blister. e.g. pemphigoid. 9. Pustule / pimples : a visible accumulation of pus in a blister. 10. Cyst : Vesico-pustule / Papulo-pastule. 11. Weal / Hives : an evanescent discrete area of dermal oedema, often centrally white due to masking of local blood supply by fluid; weals can be papules, macules, patches and plaques, and are the hallmark of urticarial. e.g. a nettle sting. 12. Abscess : a localised collection of pus in a cavity. 13. Burrow : a linear or curvilinear papule, caused by a burrowing scabies mite. 14. Comedone : a plug of keratin and sebum in a dilated pilosebaceous orifice. 15. Papilloma : A projecting nipple like mass. e.g. skin tag. 16. Petechiae are pinhead-sized, flat macules of extravascular blood in the dermis; 17. Purpura are larger and may be palpable; 18. Ecchymosis (‘bruise’) is the term used to describe bleeding that involves deeper structures. 19. Telangiectasia : visible dilatation of small cutaneous blood vessels. e.g. psoriasis. Secondary lesions / Other terms : 1. Atrophy : an area of thin, translucent skin caused by loss of epidermis, dermis or subcutaneous fat, e.g. secondary to excess topical corticosteroids. 2. Crust / Scabs : dried exudate of blood or serous fluid. e.g. eczema. 3. Erosion : an area of skin denuded by complete or partial loss of the epidermis. 4. Excoriation / Scratch mark / Abrasion : a linear ulcer or erosion resulting from scratching. 5. Fissure / Cracks / Cleft : a slit-shaped deep ulcer, e.g. in irritant dermatitis of the hands. 6. Microscopic fissure / Chap / Chapping. 7. Scale / Exfoliation : a flake arising from the stratum corneum. Any condition with a thickened stratum corneum, i.e. hyperkeratosis, can cause scaling. An immature thickened and cohesive stratum corneum, e.g. as in psoriasis or Bowen’s disease, gives characteristic optical and physical properties that cause a different scale from that due to a thickened, but otherwise normal, stratum corneum. 8. Scar / Cicatrices : replacement of normal structures by fibrous tissue at the site of an injury. 9. Sinus : a cavity or channel that permits the escape of pus or fluid. 10. Stria : a linear, atrophic, pink, purple or white band caused by connective tissue changes. e.g. Cushing syndrome. 11. Ulcer : an area from which the epidermis and at least the upper part of the dermis have been lost. [Ref – Modified from, Davidson’s Medicine 24 th , Page 1211 & Andrews’ Diseases of the Skin, 12th , Page 11-14]

treatment.title: Pharmacology & Consideration in Dermatology
treatment.description: Pharmacology & Consideration in Dermatology Mild potent : Hydrocortisone Acetate Topicort (sq) / Zocort (aci) / Cortider (skf) Desonide Desotop (aci) Moderately potent : Hydrocortisone Butyrate Eczacort (zis) Clobetasone Butyrate Ezex (sq) / Clobecort (ops) Triamcinolone Acetonide Trialon (drg) / Trimcort (che) / Aristocort (aris) Betamethasone Valerate Betaderm (incp) / Bet-A (acm) / Bantovet (dbl) Highly potent : Betamethasone Dipropionate Betameson (sq) / Betason (ops) Mometasone Furoate Soneta (aris) / Mometa (pop) / Momeson (incp) Super Potent : Clobetasol Propionate Exovate (bex) / Dermasol (sq) / Clovate (aci) / Xenovate (uni) / Topiclo (skf) / Nyclobate (incp) Halobetasol Propionate Halobet (sq) / Ulticort (incp) / Halocort (aci) / Haloxa (ops) Halcinonide Dermalog (jay) / Zemalog (gaco) General Principles of use of topical steroid : ▸ Potent and super potent steroid should not be used for prolong period (maximum 2 – 3 weeks). ▸ Super potent steroid should be avoid in case of children & over thin skin of adult person like eyelid, face, genitalia, mucous membrane, already atrophied skin. ▸ For prolong use of steroid (eg. Eczema / LSC), potent/super potent steroid is followed by moderate to mild potent steroids. ▸ For chronic inflammatory conditions like AD, Psoriasis, LP, steroid substitute should be used after considerable time period to prevent steroid induced side effects (Topical tacrolimus ointment or pimecrolimus cream). ▸ For widespread lesions – use a moderate or mild potent steroid. ▸ Lesions over scalp – use moderately potent or mild agents or lotion base. ▸ For oral lesion – moderately potent steroid in an orabase / oral paste. ▸ For secondarily infected lesions, combination with topical antibiotics is prudent. ▸ Corticosteroids can be applied under occlusion to augment their potency. Wrape polyethelene around the lesion, after steroid application. ▸ Intermittent use of potent topical steroids has not been associated with side effects and can be employed in steroid responsive dermatoses. (weekend therapy/ intermittent therapy). ▸ Avoid abrupt discontinuation of potent/super potent topical steroids, rather taper to less potent steroid & then stop to prevent flare of the dermatoses. ▸ Should not use super potent steroid when mild or mid topical steroid brings clearance of the lesions. ▸ Additives preparation may be used to augment the potency of topical steroids like propylene glycol, urea, and salicylic acid. Clobetasol Propionate + Salicylic Acid. ▸ For wide spread lesions and hairy parts of the body lotion, spray, foam formulations should be used. Topical Ratinoid : Tacrolimus : 0.03% & 0.1% Strength 0.03% used in child Sun screen : For outdoor sun Protection Factor (SPF) should be > 50 & within room (indoor) SPF SunMask Cream (sq) / Depiwhite.M SPF 50+ Lotion (uni). Anti-staphylococcal antibiotics of skin & soft tissue infections : Penicillins : Flucloxacillin, Cloxacillin, Amoxicillin + clavulanic. First generation cephalosporin : Cephapirin, Cefadroxil. Tetracyclines : Doxycycline, Minocycline. Macrolides : Clarithromycin. Lincosamide : Clindamycin (with anaerobic coverage). Oxazolidinone : Linezolid (with MRSA & VRSA coverage). Antibiotics with anaerobic coverage : clindamycin, penicillin with beta-lactamase inhibitor combination (amoxiclav), second-generation cephalosporins, carbapenems, and quinolones. Metronidazole has broad-spectrum anaerobic activity. When to refer to a dermatolgist : 1. When diagnosis is not established / doubtful. 2. Widespread lesions affecting job / social life & psychology adversely. 3. Dermatological emergencies : Toxic epidermal necrolysis and Stevens-Johnson syndrome (SJS), Eczema herpeticum, wide spread Erythroderma and Pemphigus vulgaris etc. 4. Failed topical treatment after 1 – 3 months. Normal cellular trunover of skin is 1 – 3 months. So any topical agent exerts it effect within 2 months. No patient should treat by an GP more than 3 months, if no improvement. 5. Co-existing / underlying connective tissue disorders must be referred to a rheumatologist. 6. Prescribung hazardous drugs : cytotoxic drugs etc. Regular follow up of liver function tests are monitored.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Severe abdominal pain for ___ hours. 2. Nausea, vomiting for___ hours. 3. Constipation for___ hours. 4. Abdominal distension for___ hours. Drug history : Any NSAIDs. Past history : Any HTN, DM, pervious surgery. O/G/E : • Pulse : • BP : • Temperature : • Anemia : • Dehydration : Per abdomen examination : • Local tenderness / muscle guard : • Board like abdominal rigidity : • Upper border of liver dullness : • McBurney's point tenderness : • Rebound tenderness : • Murphy's sign : • Any Lump : • Inguinal hernia : • Testicular pathology : Δ Acute Abdomen Adv. : ▸ Plain X-ray abdomen erect posture A/P view including both dome of diaphragm ▸ USG of W/A ▸ CBC, RBS, ▸ S. Creatinine, ▸ S. electrolyte ▸ Urine RME Tx, ¤ NPO-TFO. ¤ NG suction 2 hourly. ¤ Inf. H/S (1 L) + 5%DNS (1 L) + 5%DA (1 L), I/V@30 d/m stat & daily. ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Trizon (acm) 1 gm, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Metronidazole 500mg/100ml / Flamyd (incp) / Filmet (bex) / Metro (osl), 1 bottle I/V stat and TDS. ¤ Inj. Omeprazole 40mg / Omenix (incp), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Tramadol 100mg/2ml / Utramal (uni), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. ¤ Inj. Ondansetron 8mg/4ml / Ofran (sq), 1 amp I/V stat and TDS. ¤ Continuous catheterization. ► If constipation : • If bowel sound present – ¤ Supp. Glycerin / Glysup (sq) 2.3 gm 4 sticks P/R stat. • If still no bowel movement – ¤ Enema solution : Monobasic & Dibasic Sodium Phosphate (19 gm+7 gm)/118 ml, 1 bottle P/R stat. Adults and children >12 yr : one bottle per 24 hours up to 3 days. Children between 2 and 11 years : use ½ (59 ml) bottle of enema. DO NOT USE, if children under 2 years, if dehydrated present, H/O kidney diseases. ► Alternate pain killer : In case of lower abdominal pain (LAP) [appendix, inguinal hernia, urinary tract etc.], NSAIDs can be used – ¤ Inj. Ketorolac (30 mg/ml) / Pair (drg) / Etolac (ibn) 30 mg, 1 amp I/M (or I/V) stat & BD or TDS. If pain isn't relieved: Inj. Nalbuphine 20mg/1ml 1 amp I/M stat & sos with Inj. Prochlorperazine or Ondansetron. ¤ Inj. Nalbuphine–2 ml (20 mg) / Nalbun-2 (incp), 1 amp IM stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Prochlorperazine 12.5 mg (1 ml) / Vergon (ops), 1 amp IM stat & BD c̄ Nalbuphine. ± Anti-spasmotic : ¤ Inj. Tiemonium 5 mg/2 ml / Viset (hc) / Norvis (sq), 1 amp IM or IV stat & BD. ► Alternate antibiotic : ¤ Inj. Ciprofloxacin 100 ml(200 mg) /Ciprox, 1 bottle / bag IV stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Metronidazole 100 ml (500 mg), 1 bottle / bag IV stat & TDS. ± ¤ Inj. Amikacin 500 mg / Kacin, 1 amp IV stat & BD.

treatment.title: Clinical notes
treatment.description: 1. Obliteration liver dullness on percussion + broad like rigidity : Perforation > Confirmed by X ray abdomen > resuscitation ± surgery/laparotomy. 2. Cramping pain, vomiting, constipation+ abdominal distension + bowel sound may be absent : Intestinal obstruction or Subacute obstruction > Confirmed by X ray abdomen. 3. Pain in right iliac fossa + McBurney's point tenderness+ rebound tenderness : Acute Appendicitis > CBC, HR USG, Urine R/M/E > resuscitation ± surgery/appendectomy. 4. Pain in the right hypochondrium + Murphy's sign positive : Acute Cholecystitis > USG of HBS (or preferably W/A). 5. Severe cramping pain not relief by usual analgesics + loin to groin pain : Renal colic > Confirmed by X ray KUB, USG of KUB. 6. Severe epigastric pain which radiates to the back+ Pain not relief by usual analgesics + May associated with shock, oliguria, discoloration in flank / umbilicus : Acute Pancreatitis > Serum lipase, USG of HBS (preferably USG of W/A). 7. High fever with chill and rigor + Abdominal pain + Renal angle tender : Pyelonephritis. 8. If above this are excluded in severe upper abdominal pain it more possibility to be PUD. 9. Dysuria + suprapubic tenderness + Urgency, frequency : Cystitis / UTI. 10. Lower abdominal pain + Vaginal discharge – itchy (+/-), foul-smelling + Dyspareunia + H/O recent abortion / STI : PID. 11. H/O amenorrhea+ Lower abdominal pain and tender + P/V bleeding + Shock / Anaemia : Rupture ectopic pregnancy > Beta HCG, USG of W/A. 12. Lower severe lower abdominal pain+ HO Adnexal cyst : Torsion adnexal cyst > Color Doppler USG of W/A.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.disease_name: Perforation of GCHV (gas containing hollow viscus)
treatment.description: See "Acute Abdomen" for indoor treatment. Diagnostic tools : 1. H/O – (Generalized / Upper) Abdominal pain for ___ hours. 2. O/A/E : - Restricted movement of abdomen with respiration. - Muscle guard / rigidity all over the abdomen. - Obliterated liver dullness. - Absent / diminished bowel sound. 3. O/G/E : - P : tachycardia. - BP : hypotension. - Dehydration present. - Features of shock. - Anemia & Jaundice. Investigations : Plain X-ray abdomen erect posture AP view including both dome of diaphragm, shows pneumo-peritoneum : crescent shaped gas shadow under the right dome of diaphragm. Note : ► Diet causes gastric acid secretion & more leakage, so NPO & bed rest is must. ► NG suction with wide bore NG / BMI feeding tube is needed to relief from abdominal distension, pain & to remove GI secretions. Suction may be given half / 1 / 2 hourly according to need. ► Fluid absorption is hampered so hypovolaemia occurs. Initially ringer’s lactate / Hartmann’s solution is indicated & then according to electrolyte deficits. Normal saline is infused to a diabetic person. Cautious about 5% DA as it is hypotonic when metabolized and may aggravate hypovolaemia. ► Most (1 st ) commonest site of perforation is anterior wall of the first part of duodenum. And 2 nd common is gastric ulcer perforation. ► In the posterior wall of duodenum perforation bleeding occurs due to erosion of large vessel e.g. gastro duodenal artery. ► As we don’t know the exact site of perforation, it is better to give 1 / 2 unit(s) of blood demand to be given. And BT demand is a must in anemic patient. ► In perforation peritoneal cavity is invaded by gut flora and leads to bacterial peritonitis. It occurs / developed in three stages : Stage I Stage of chemical peritonitis. Stage II Stage of Illusion or stage of peritoneal reaction. Stage III Stage of bacterial peritonitis / abdominal distension. So antibiotic is must for treatment. Combination of Ceftriaxone / Cefotaxime, Amikacin/Ciprofloxacin, Metronidazole is the best. ► Never use NSAIDs as is aggravates perforated peptic ulcers. Centrally acting analgesics / Opioids like – Nalbuphine, Tramadol, Pethidine, Morphine should be used. ► Nalbuphine or other opioids commonly causes nausea & vomiting so Prochlorperazine should be injected with it. ► Anti-ulcerant / anti secretory drugs should be added. PPI has more efficacy, suppress both basal & meal stimulated acid secretion with longer duration of action & doesn’t develop tolerance. H 2 blocker has lesser efficacy, suppress only basal acid secretion with shorter duration of action & develops tolerance. So PPI should be the drug of choice. ► Continuous catheterization for monitoring urine output. If less than 0.5 ml/kg/hr then inject force diuretic like furosemide or mannitol to prevent acute tubular necrosis (ATN), as hypovolaemia causes pre renal failure. ► If presents with fever then suspect typhoid ulcer perforation. - Treatment : Surgery : Emergency exploratory laparotomy with repair of perforation & through peritoneal toileting. ► Products prescribe to buy at admission : A. For IV channel : 1. IV cannula 20G --- (2) 2. Micropore 1" (JMS) --- (1) [Usually IV cannula is supplied in the wards, so ask the SSN (nurse) on duty to know the supplied drugs or appliances. IV cannula 18G can also be used. It is wider than 20G. Never give 1 piece of IV cannula to buy as first one may be failed. So give it should be given 2 to 3 in number.] B. For IV fluids infusion : 1. Inf. H/S 1L ----- (3) 2. Saline Set ----- (1) [Generally all types of IV fluids & saline sets are supplied in all the wards of govt medical college.] C. For NG suction : 1. BMI feeding tube 16 Fr ---- (1) 2. 50 cc Syringe ----- (1) 3. Micropore 1" (JMS) ---- (1) 4. Hand glove (Ansell / Comfit) 7" --- (1) 5. Jasocaine Jelly ----- (1) [Generally hand gloves & liquid paraffin are supplied in all the wards, jasocaine (2%) jelly may not be given if liquid paraffin is available. Double NG or Ryle’s tubes of different sizes (18 Fr / 16 Fr / 14 Fr) can be prescribed to buy at a time. So that you can use narrower (14 Fr) NG tubes if wider (18 / 16 Fr) one fails. Usually micropore is bought earlier with IV channels & infusions, so it can be avoid, if presents. Hand gloves are sized 7" (for male) & 6.5" (for female). In sequence of larger sizes are 7.5" > 7" > 6.5" > 6".]

treatment.title: Discharge note
treatment.disease_name: Perforation of GCHV (gas containing hollow viscus)
treatment.description: After 3 rd POD (appearance of bowel sound and abdomen is soft) & D c̄ A : ✔ Triple therapy : Amoxicillin 1g + Clarithromycin 500mg + Lansoprazole 30 mg / Pylotrip Kit (sq) / Helicon Kit (gen) / Triokit (ops), 1 strip at morning and 1 strip at night – 14 days Or, ✔ Tab. Esomeprazole 20 mg + Amoxicillin 1000 mg + Clarithromycin 500 mg / Maxpro HP (ren) / Esotid HP (ops), 1 strip at morning and 1 strip at night – 14 days. ✔ After 14 days : Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Seclo (sq) / Losectil (skf) / Cosec (drg), 1+0+1 (30 min before meal) – 2 months. ✔ Cap. Cefixime / Triocim (bex) 400 mg, 1+0+1 – 7 days. ✔ Tab. Metronidazole 400mg, 1+1+1–total 5 days from beginning. ✔ Cap. Tramadol / Anadol SR (sq) / TRD-Contin (mundi) / Utramal Retard (uni) 100 mg, 1+0+1- A/M – If pain present. ± ✔ Povidone Iodine 5% / Betadine (mundi) / Viodin (sq) / Povin (ops), Ointment, apply locally BD – 7 days. ± ✔ Tab. Vitamin C 250 mg / Vasco (ops), 1+0+1 – 10 days. উপদেশ : (১) সাত (০৭) দিন পর নিকটস্থ স্বাস্থ্য কমল্পেক্সে সেলাই কাটাবেন। (২) ধুমপান, অ্যালকোহল, ফাস্ট ফুড, ঝাল মসলা জাতীয় খাবার, Aspirin, NSAIDs থেকে বিরত থাকুন। (৩) পরবর্তীতে এই সমস্যার জন্য পূর্বের সব কাগজপাতিসহ সার্জারি বহি:বিভাগে দেখা করবেন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Suspect if – 1. Abdominal pain. 2. Nausea, vomiting. 3. Constipation. 4. Abdominal distension. History and clinical examination : • No bowel sounds on auscultation. • No passage of flatus or faeces. • Abdominal distension • Distension increases pain from the abdominal wound. • Vomiting may occur. Clinical signs : On abdominal examination : - Abdominal distension. - Visible peristalsis. - There may be tenderness all over abdomen. - If rebound tenderness is present, it indicates underlying strangulation. - Bowel sound may be increased in initial phase (exaggerated), once strangulation sets in abdomen may be silent (sluggish). - P/R examination may reveal ballooning of rectum or empty rectum. Finger may be smeared with blood or mucus. In case of pseudo-obstruction fecal scybala (pellets) may be palpable. 3. O/G/E : - P : tachycardia. - BP : hypotension. - Dehydration present. - Anemia & Jaundice. Inv. – • USG of W/A (whole abdomen) • CBC • S. Electrolytes • S. creatinine • Blood grouping & Rh typing • S. Amylase • Upper GI endoscopy/Short colonoscopy (if indicated). Causes of intestinal obstruction : Intraluminal – Faecal impaction, Foreign bodies, Bezoars, Gallstones. Intramural – Stricture, Malignancy, Intussusception, Volvulus. External – Bands/Adhesions, Obstructed Hernia. Hospitalization & conservative management for 24 – 48 hours. If not improved then Specialist consultation/Referral & exploratory laparotomy. Medical / conservative correction : • Nil by mouth (NPO) • Antibiotics • NG suction • Analgesic • I/V fluid • Anti-ulcerant • Catheterization • Anti-emetic • Specialist consultation • Electrolyte correction

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Vomiting due to Gastric Outlet Obstruction (GOO) [Gastric Carcinoma / PUD] Diagnostic tools : 1. H/O - Projectile vomiting for ___ days. - Weight loss & constipation. - Occasionally abdominal pain. 2. O/A/E : - Visible epigastric peristalsis. - Fullness in the left hypochondrium. - Succussion splash. 3. O/G/E : - P : tachycardia. - BP : hypotension. - Dehydration present. - Anemia & Jaundice. Adv. : - Endoscopy of UGIT & biopsy. - Barium meal X-ray of the stomach & duodenum. Note : - For correction of electrolyte imbalance (metabolic alkalosis) NaCl solution with KCl should be given but ad Syp. KT or Inj. KT only after doing S. electrolytes. - Blood / PCV may be transfused for correction of anemia. - Treatment : Bilateral truncal vagotomy with gastrojejunostomy. - Preparation of stomach is recommended 3-5 days before surgery. - Centrally acting analgesic / opioid Tramadol may be used to relief pain. If no pain, no analgesics needed. - Ondansetron / Emiston (ibn) may also be used as anti-emetic instead of Prochlorperazine / Vergon (ops) / Stemetil (sq). - The fluid and electrolyte deficits in any GI obstructions are caused by – Vomiting, Reduced oral intake, Defective absorption, Sequestration of fluid in the lumen of gut. - Volumes of different GI secretions : Saliva 1–1.5 liters Gastric juice 1.5–2.5 liters Bile 1 liter Pancreatic juice 1.5 liters Small intestinal juice 3 liters - Suppository Glycerol / Supp. Glysup (sq), 4 stick P/R stat & sos or Fleet enema, 1 bottle P/R stat & sos may be given only if bowel sound presents or flatus has passed after admission. Better not no use laxatives at admission without evaluation of strangulation or complete blockage, in those case laxatives are contraindicated. - Conservative treatment & resuscitation is the first to observe any improvement or relief (as in subacute intestinal obstruction, SAIO). Exploratory laparotomy is the last choice. - Other centrally acting opioids like Tramadol or anti-spasmotics like Hyoscine Butyl Bromide (Butapan) or Tiemonium Methylsulphate [Viset (hc) / Visral (ops)] may also be used.

treatment.title: D/D, features & complication
treatment.description: D/D : Pain in Right Iliac Fossa : • UTI • Right ureteric stone • Salphingo-oophoritis • Ectopic Pregnancy • Torsion Testis • Carcinoma Testis Must exclude rupture ectopic pregnancy in reproductive females. - Acute appendicitis first attack may treat conservatively. But appendicectomy is the treatment of recurrent appendicitis. - Diagnosis is clinical mostly : The Alvarado (MANTRELS) score : Traits Score Symptoms Migratory RIF pain 1 Anorexia 1 Nausea and vomiting 1 Signs Tenderness (RIF) 2 Rebound tenderness 1 Elevated temperature 1 Laboratory Leukocytosis 2 Shift to left 1 Total 10 Interpretation : Score (1 – 4) = Conservative treatment. Score (5 – 7) = Admit, observe & rescore. Score (8 – 10) = Definitely operate immediately. Clinical features : • Pain started around the umbilicus then shifting to the right iliac fossa and fixed there. • Nausea rather than vomiting. • Mild rise of temperature. • Tenderness & rebound tenderness in right iliac fossa. • Positive Rovsing’s Sign. Investigations : • Complete blood count: neutrophilic leucocytosis (TC 11,000 18,000 per cmm, more than 18,000 per cmm likely other diagnosis or complicated). • Urine RME: a few pus cells and no RBC are usual (more pus cell or presence of RBC is likely urological diagnosis). • X-ray KUB : no radio opaque shadow along the kidney, ureter or urinary bladder region. • USG of lower abdomen: Kidney, ureter and urinary bladder region normal. In case of female patient: ovary, fallopian tube or uterus is normal. A small collection in right iliac fossa or edematous appendix may be diagnostic. • RBS and serum creatinine level if possible or required Complication : • Appendicular lump. • Appendicular abscess. • Burst appendix. • Generalized peritonitis. If complications : Generalized peritonitis, appendicular lump or abscess, previous H/O high fever or high fever, moderate or huge pelvic collection, H/O missed period in case of female (15-45 years of age), pregnancy or diagnostic dilemma-refer the patient to the hospital according to referral protocol with above mentioned treatment.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Definitive management : Appendicectomy : Surgical removal of vermiform appendix. If patient is improved with conservative management : If recurrent appendicitis Interval appendicectomy after 6 weeks. If no improvement / Increase of pain, pulse, temperature, size of lump and softening is lump / abscess : appendicectomy with thorough peritoneal toileting (Drain tube must be applied) Post-operative treatment : Nil by mouth for 12-24 hours. IV fluid, antibiotics, analgesics and anti-ulcerant as mentioned before. Switch to oral therapy as early as possible. Patient may be discharge on 2 nd or 3 rd POD after check dressing. Treatment of non-complicated or 1 st attack of acute appendicitis : ✔ Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg / Ciprocin (sq) / Neofloxin (bex), 1 + 0 + 1 --- 7 – 14 days ✔ Tab. Metronidazole 400 mg / Metryl (ops) / Amodis (sq), 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 --- 7 – 14 days If pain present, (must avoid NSAIDs in perforation & PUD) ✔ Tab. Ketorolac 10mg / Todol (ops) / Xidolac (bex) / Acupain (bacn), 1+1+1 or 1+0+1 (after meal) ---- 5 – 14 days ✔ Tab. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (30 minutes before meal) --- 7 – 15 days Or, Avoid NSAIDs in asthmatic patient : ✔ Cap. Tramadol 50mg / Anadol (sq) / Utramal Retard (uni) / TRD-Contin (mundi), 1 + 1 + 1 (After meal) --- 5 days ✔ Tab. Ondansetron 8 mg / Emistat (hc) / Anset (ops) / Emeren (ren) / Zofra (skf), 1+0+1 or, 1+1+1 ---- 5 days

treatment.title: Appendicular Lump
treatment.description: Most common D/D – Intestinal TB and Ca caecum. Treatment : ► If patient is improved with conservative management → Interval Appendicectomy after 6 weeks. ► If no improvement / Increase of pain, pulse, temperature, size of lump and softening is lump → Appendicectomy with thorough peritoneal toileting (drain tube must be applied).

treatment.title: Appendicular Abscess
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : 1. C/C : - H/O acute appendicitis few days back. - Continued spiking pyrexia ē rigors & sweating. - Increasing local pain. - Increasing size of mass in spite of treatment. 2. O/A/E : - Very tender mass, rigidity, oedema & reddening of overlying skin. - Restricted movement of abdomen with respiration. - No obliteration of liver dullness. - Bowel sound : Present. 3. O/G/E : - Pulse & BP. - Temp : raised. - Anemia & Jaundice.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Emergency conservative management for any condition of GB / Gall Bladder & Pancreatic followed by elective surgical mx : Δ Acute Cholecystitis or, Δ Acute Pancreatitis Adv. : ▸ USG of W/A ± ▸ Urinary amylase. ► NPO-TFO. ► Inf. HS (1 L) + 5% DNS (1 L) + 5% DA (1 L), I/V @ 30 drops/min stat & daily ► Inj. Cefuroxime 750 mg / Turbocef (bex), 1 vial IV stat and TDS. ► Inj. Ondansetron 8mg/4ml / Emistat (hc), 1 amp I/V stat and TDS. ► Inj. Tiemonium methyl sulphate 5mg/2ml / Algin (ren) / Norvis (sq) / Tinium (acm), 2 amp I/V stat, then, 1 amp I/V TDS ► Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Seclo (sq), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. If pain persists : ► Inj. Tramadol 100mg/2ml / Anadol (sq), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. Other investigations as per need : CBC, RBS, S. Amylase, S. Creatinine, S. Electrolytes (if H/O vomiting), S. Bilirubin (total, direct & indirect), S. Albumin, A:G ratio, BT, CT, etc.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : Colicky pain upper abdominal (right quadrant) pain for (long time), sometime referred to right scapula. Fever, nausea & vomiting for short duration. • Right upper quadrant or epigastric pain • Fever • Tenderness in the right upper quadrant • Positive (+ve) Murphy’s sign. • Mild jaundice / icterus may be present (10%). O/G/E : - Pulse & BP. - Temp : raised. - Anemia & Jaundice. Investigation : • CBC • LFTs • USG of HBS or W/A D/D of right upper quadrant or epigastric pain : • Acute Cholecystitis • Appendicitis • Perforated peptic ulcer • Acute pancreatitis • Acute pyelonephritis • Myocardial infarction • Pneumonia-right lower lobe Definitive management : Cholecystectomy after 6 weeks. Conservative Management : Usually needs hospitalization, NPO-TFO & IV medication (as above). Uncomplicated cases : Resolved with conservative treatment. Discharge and advice for hospital admission after 6 weeks for cholecystectomy. Complicated cases : (Specialist consultation / Referral) • Empyema of the gall bladder • Mucocele • Perforation • Biliary obstruction • Acute cholangitis • Acute pancreatitis • Gallstone ileus Liver function tests (LFTs) may be advised – 1. Serum bilirubin. 2. SGPT. 3. Alkaline phosphatase. 4. Serum albumin. 5. Prothrombin time. 6. A:G ratio.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Emergency conservative management for any condition of GB / Gall Bladder & Pancreatic followed by elective surgical mx : Δ Acute Cholecystitis or, Δ Acute Pancreatitis Adv. : ▸ USG of W/A ± ▸ Urinary amylase. Tx, ► NPO-TFO. ► Inf. HS (1 L) + 5% DNS (1 L) + 5% DA (1 L), I/V @ 30 drops/min stat & daily ► Inj. Cefuroxime 750 mg / Turbocef (bex), 1 vial IV stat and TDS. ► Inj. Ondansetron 8mg/4ml / Emistat (hc), 1 amp I/V stat and TDS. ► Inj. Tiemonium methyl sulphate 5mg/2ml / Algin (ren) / Norvis (sq) / Tinium (acm), 2 amp I/V stat, then, 1 amp I/V TDS ► Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Seclo (sq), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. If pain persists : ► Inj. Tramadol 100mg/2ml / Anadol (sq), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. Other investigations as per need : CBC, RBS, S. Amylase, S. Creatinine, S. Electrolytes (if H/O vomiting), S. Bilirubin (total, direct & indirect), S. Albumin, A:G ratio, BT, CT, etc.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Features : Asymptomatic (75%). Recurrent attack of colicky pain in upper and right upper abdomen, sometimes radiating to tip of shoulder or inferior angle of scapula, associated with nausea, vomiting, and exacerbated by heavy meal. • Vague intermittent (upper) abdominal pain/discomfort. • Nausea/flatulence and intolerance to fatty food. O/E : Nothing significant. Inv : USG of W/A. Treatment : ● Tab. Tiemonium 50 mg / Visceralgine (Nuv) / Algin (ren) / Norvis (sq) / Viset (hc), 1 + 1 + 1 ---- 3-7 days, ● Referred to surgeon or tertiary level hospital for elective / routine cholecystectomy. - Make an investigation profile & send patient attendants to anesthesiologists prior to operation. Follow the suggestion of anesthesiologist if referred as unfit. - Patient come for schedule OT, may receive Tab. Albendazole 400 mg & Inj.TT & TIG.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Jaundice ± Fever : ● Intermittent (if due to stone), Progressive (if due to Ca of head of pancreas) / Fluctuating (periampullary Ca). ● Generalized itching, more in trunks and extremities. ● Dark colored urine, Pale offensive bulky stool which float on water (In the pan) Pain : In right hypochondrium. ● Colicky in nature, ● Radiating to inferior angle of scapula or tip of right shoulder, ● Aggravated by fatty foods and relieved by medication, ● Associated with nausea and occasional vomiting. Dx : Obstructive jaundice due to Choledocholithiasis / Periampullary Ca / Ca of head of pancreas. ► Diet : Carbohydrate rich diet. ► Tab. Cefuroxime 500 mg, 1+0+1. ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1- B/M. ► Tab. Tiemonium 50 mg, 1+1+1- B/M. ► Inj. Vitamin K l 2mg/0.2 ml, 1 Amp IM stat and daily – 5 days. If PT is still prolonged after Vitamin K1 administration : administer fresh frozen plasma. Advice : • USG of W/A. • ALP. • MRCP. • Serum albumin. • SGPT. • PT with INR. • Serum bilirubin : Direct and indirect. Routine : • CBC. • ECG. • RBS. • Echocardiography (>40 yrs). • CXR PA view. • Urine RE. • S. Creatinine. Surgery : ● Choledocholithiasis : Cholecystectomy with choledocholithotomy with T-tube insertion. ● Periampullary carcinoma and Ca of head of pancreas : Whipple's procedure.

treatment.title: Acute Pancreatitis
treatment.description: Acute Pancreatitis (Please see Gastroenterology portion) Sudden severe upper abdominal pain radiating to back relieve by leaning forward. Also nausea and vomiting, ± fever. May have H/O alcoholism / Gall stone. O/A/E : Severe agonizing pain, may radiates to back. Relieved by sitting or leaning forward (Muslim prayer position). Abdomen : Soft, tender, bowel sound present. O/G/E : - P : tachycardia. - BP : may be hypotension. - May have features of shock. - Anemia & Jaundice.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Cellulitis (?) / Abscess Adv. : ▸ RBS ¤ Diet : Normal. ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Triject (skf) 1000 mg, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Flucloxacillin / Flux (ops) 500 mg, 1 vial IV stat & QDS / 6 hourly. ¤ Inj. Ketorolac 30 mg / Toradolin (rad), 1 amp I/M stat & sos (or BD). ¤ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / PPI (acm), 1 + 0 + 1 (B/M) ¤ Tab. P/C / Napa (bex) 500 mg, 2 + 2 + 2 (if temp > 100°F) ¤ Elevate the affected part (to reduce edema).

treatment.title: Dry Gangrene
treatment.description: Gangrene Gangrene two types. They are – 1. Dry gangrene : due to obstruction of blood flow. 2. Wet gangrene : due to injury and infection. Dry gangrene Dry Gangrene commonly seen in : • Vascular disease • Diabetes • Burn • Arterial trauma caused by accident, wound or surgery. • Autoimmune Disease. Features of Dry Gangrene : • Shriveled skin – changes from blue to black-eventually comes off. • Cold, numb skin. • Pain.

treatment.title: Wet Gangrene
treatment.description: Wet gangrene Wet Gangrene are of various types : • Internal gangrene. e.g. appendix, colon etc. • Gas gangrene : inside muscle or organs. • Fournier’s gangrene : Scrotal/genital area. • Meleny’s gangrene : skin to deeper tissue. Features of Wet Gangrene : • Swelling, pain and fever. • Red, brown, purple, blue, greenish black or black skin. • Blister or sore with bad smelling discharge. • A crackling noise on pressing the affected area. • Thin, shiny or hairless skin. • A line between healthy and damaged skin. Treatment - • Surgery- Debridement, amputation. • Medical treatment- Antibiotic, Anticoagulants, Vasodilator. • Supportive care. • Rehabilitation (in case of amputation). Specialist consultation / Referral : In severe cases, candidate of limb amputation & all types of wet gangrene.

treatment.title: Bed Sore / Pressure Ulcer
treatment.description: BED SORE (Pressure Ulcer) The Four Stages of Pressure Injuries : • Stage 1 : Non-blanchable erythema of intact skin. • Stage 2 : Partial-thickness skin loss with exposed dermis. • Stage 3 : Full-thickness skin loss. • Stage 4 : Full-thickness skin and tissue loss. Management : ● Pneumatic bed / foam & change posture 2 hourly, if paralyzed patient. ● Keep the dependent parts of the body clean & dry. ● Topical ± Systemic antibiotic. ● Apply moist to absorbent dressing, e.g. hydrogel, foam etc. cleanse the wound. ● Surgical debridement & regular dressing.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Pyo-dermas / Cellulitis / Diabetic Foot / Breast Abscess / Wound Infection
treatment.description: Wound infection : ╬ Fever > 4 days, ╬ Erythema and induration, Erythema > 5 cm, ╬ Temperature > 38°C, ╬ WBC > 12000/cumm. Definitive management : Incision & drainage followed by regular dressing. 1. Wound debridement 2. Regular dressing 3. Foot bath 4. Secondary closure 5. Skin graft/ flap Treatment : Broad spectrum injectable or oral antibiotic. Diabetic foot may need treatment of longer duration. Antibiotic for Gram +ve & -ve : (any one) ✔ Cap. Cefixime / Cefim-3 (aci) 400 mg, 1+0+1 --- 7 to 60 days Or, ✔ Tab. Cefuroxime + Clavulanic Acid / Xiclav (zis) / Roxiclav (euro) / Clavusef (ops) 500 mg, 1+0+1 ---- 7 to 60 days + Antibiotic for resistant Gram +ve : (any one) ✔ Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg / Flubex (bex) / Fluclox (aci) / Flustar (ren) 500 mg, 1+1+1+1 ---- 7 to 60 days Or, ✔ Tab. Linezolid 400 or 600 mg / Arlin (bex) / Linzolid (incp) / Lizid (rad) / Anulid (hc) 600 mg, 1+0+1 ---- 7 to 60 days + Analgesic c̄ PPI : ✔ Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Toradolin (rad) / Toramax (pop), (1+0+1) or, (1+1+1) (if pain, after meal) ---- 5 days ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Ometid (ops) / Opal (hc), 1 + 0 + 1 (30 minutes before meal) ---- 5 days ± Immune booster vitamins : ✔ Tab. Ascorbic acid / Ceevit (sq) 250mg, 1+0+1 ---- 14 to 60 days ✔ Tab. Vit-B Complex + Zinc / Xinc B (skf), 1+0+1 ---- 14 to 60 days + Advice : - Foot bath with (lukewarm water + 10% Povidone Iodine + Salt) three to five times daily should be advised. - If abscess is formed, Immediate incision of drainage of pus c̄ debridement of dead tissue. - Regular dressing & secondary closure ± skin grafting ± flap when tissue is healthy. Antibiotic options : A. Wound around trunk, head, neck, extremity : Start Penicillin or Cephalosporin group of antibiotic. B. Wound of perineum or operation of GI tract or female genital tract : Start 2nd gen Cephalosporin, Sulbactum added Penicillin, Nitroimidazole or Aminoglycosides.

treatment.title: Advice for diabetic foot
treatment.disease_name: Pyo-dermas / Cellulitis / Diabetic Foot / Breast Abscess / Wound Infection
treatment.description: Advice for diabetic foot prevention : Inspect feet daily. Check for cuts, blisters, redness, swelling, or nail problems. Use a magnifying hand mirror to look at the bottom of feet. Consult a doctor if noticed anything. Dry feet completely and apply lotion to the top and bottom—but not between your toes, which could lead to infection. Never go barefoot. Always wear shoes and socks or slippers, even inside, to avoid injury. Check that there aren't any pebbles or other objects inside your shoes and that the lining is smooth. A glucometer should be used to monitor regular RBS / CBG (capillary blood glucose) check-up & adjust insulin accordingly. Avoid duplicate and non standard glucometers. CBG / RBS reading of any glucometer is 15% higher than laboratory RBS of the same individual in the same time, because in laboratory RBS is measured by serum & hold blood.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : 1. C/C : - Throbbing pain on the affected part. - Affected area is swollen, hot / reddened, indurated & tender. 2. O/A/E : - Red, swollen & tender are ± Discharging sinus. - Aspiration with syringe : Confirmatory. - Fluctuation test +ve. 3. O/G/E : - Pulse & BP. - Temp : raised. - Treatment : Incision & drainage. Conservative treatment is same as cellulitis / Diabetic foot. - Antibiotics according culture & sensitivity (C/S) report. - Indiscriminate long term use of antibiotics before draining abscess may form antibioma. But in hospital we will use injectable antibiotics on admission & emergency incision & drainage is done. This short term antibiotic will not produce antibioma, if drained timely. - Regular dressing may be required. - In case of little boils / inflammatory lump with no discharging sinus hot compression is a must. Hot compression will help to drain the pus & form opening of pus cavity.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Benign Lipomatous Neoplasm or Lipomas are the most common soft-tissue tumor. These are slowly growing, benign fatty tumors form soft, lobulated masses enclosed by a thin, fibrous capsule. Clinical features • Lipomas are small mobile soft swelling in undersurface of skin and subcutaneous tissue. • Usually not painful. • Remain same size for long period. • Sometimes very large. Physical Examination • Typical appearance of lipoma is soft, well demarcated, smooth surfaced, lobulated swelling with slip sign. • Sometimes shows pseudo-flactuation. Management : • Small lipoma needs observation. • If lipoma is large, symptomatic then needs excision ± biopsy. Treatment after surgery : ➢ Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg, 1+1+1+1 for 7 days ➢ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 for 7 days ➢ Tab. Diclofen 50 mg, 1+0+1 for 5 days ➢ Stitch off after 7 – 10 days.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: ► Depth of burn : Assessment of depth of burn wound : • Epidermal burn (1st degree) : involves only the epidermis. • Superficial dermal (superficial 2nd degree) : epidermis & some of the dermis. • Deep dermal (deep 2nd degree) : epidermis & a larger part of dermis. • Full thickness (3rd degree) : involves epidermis & dermis, also may penetrate the underlying structures. ► Superficial burn : Blister present, Red / pink color & painful. ► Deep burn : White / charred black colored & painful. Scald usually superficial, electrical / chemical usually deep & flame burns are mixed. Admission Criteria : • Burns greater than 10% of total body surface Area (TBSA) in pediatric ( 60 years) • >15% TBSA in adults (partial thickness) • Full thickness burns >5% TBSA • Burns involving special areas: face, hands, feet, genitalia, perineum & major joints • Electrical burns • Chemical burns • Inhalation burns • Circumferential burns of the limbs, trunk or neck • Burns in people with pre-existing medical or psychological disorders that could complicate management, prolong recovery or increase mortality • Burns associated with trauma • Burns with pregnancy • Burns in patients who will require emotional, rehabilitative intervention Criteria of burn patients to be called "CRITICAL" : • >15% in a child. • >30% in adult with partial thickness or full thickness burn. • All inhalation burn. • All chemical burn. • Patients presenting late with inadequate resuscitation and / or in a state of sepsis. • All electric burn. • Burns with severe co-morbid disease.

treatment.title: Minor Burn
treatment.description: Minor Burn Dressing : 1 ° Burn (epidermis only) or involving little body surface . ↓ Do not burst the blisters, apply Burna cream (sq). ↓ Apply medication is a paraffin gauze dressing over burn area. Such as : Supra Toli or Gypsonet. ↓ Apply rolled bandage & prescribe drug treatment. Better to refer & avoid burn of medico-legal cases in GP. Refer to burn & plastic surgeon or hospitalize : Even if minor burn of face or sensitive area, electric burn, 3 ° full thickness burn, high chance of disfigurement. Treatment of minor burns (in adult) : ✔ Cap. Cefixime 200 mg or 400 mg / Cef-3 (sq) / Triocim (bex), 1 + 0 + 1 ----- 7 – 14 days ✔ Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg / Flux (ops) / Phylopen DS (sq), 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ---- 7 – 14 days ✔ Cream Silver Sulfadiazine 1% / Burna (sq) / Burnsil (bex) / Silcream (jay), The burn areas should be covered all times, applied twice daily or whenever necessary, thickness should be of approximately 1.5 mm ---- 3 – 5 days (short time), then, ✔ Any other antibiotic ointment (after stopping Burna cream & no / little raw surface) : Nebanol ointment, apply TDS for 7 – 14 days ✔ Tab. Vitamin C 250 mg / Ceevit (sq) / Vasco (ops) / Ascobex(bex), 1+0+1 or, 1+1+1 ---- 10 days [Radivit-C 1000mg (rad)] ✔ Tab. Ketorolac 10mg /Todol (ops)/Xidolac (bex)/Acupain (bacn), 1+1+1 or 1+0+1 (after meal, if pain) ---- 5 – 14 days ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Losectil (skf) / Xeldrin (aci) / Xelopes (bacn), 1+0+1 ---- (30 minutes before meal) --- 7 – 15 days ✔ Tab. Fexofenadine 120 or 180 mg / Axodin (bex) / Fenadin (ren) / Fexo (sq) / Fexofast (drg) / Telfast (syn), 0+0+1 (f itching) --- 5–7 days Treatment of minor burns (in children) : ✔ Syrup. Cefixime 100mg/5ml / Fix-A (acm) / Denver (hc) / 3-C (edruc) / Cefim-3 (aci), 1 TSF for every 20 kg BD ---- 7 – 14 days [1-4 years: 1 TSF OD; 5-10years: 1 TSF BD] ✔ Syrup. Flucloxacillin 125mg/5ml / Fluclox (aci) / Phylopen (sq) / Flucloxin (sq), 1 TSF for every 8 kg QDS ----- 7 – 14 days [50-100mg/Kg/day 6 hourly] ✔ Cream Silver Sulfadiazine 1% / Burna (sq) / Burnsil (bex) / Silcream (jay), The burn areas should be covered all times, applied twice daily or whenever necessary, thickness should be of approximately 1.5 mm ---- 3 – 5 days (short time), then, ✔ Any other antibiotic ointment (after stopping Burna cream & no / little raw surface) : Nebanol ointment, apply TDS for 7 – 14 days Or, Ointment Mupirocin 2%/ Bactrocin (sq) / Trego (incp) / Mupiderm (hc), apply locally TDS ---- 10 – 20 days ✔ Tab. Vitamin C 250 mg / Ceevit (sq) / C-Rich (gen) / Vasco (ops) / Citrux-C (nipro) 250 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ---- 10 – 20 days ✔ Sus. Ibuprofen 100 mg/ 5ml / Flamex (aci) / Profen (acm) / Advel (ops) / Inflam (not available - syn), 1 TSF for every 10 Kg TDS (max dose) – if pain --- 5 – 10 days ✔ Tab. Rabeprazole 10 mg / Finix (ops) / Rasonix JR (incp) / Xorel (ibn) / Paricel (aci) 10 mg, 1+0+1 ---- 7 – 14 days

treatment.title: Major Burns
treatment.description: ► Primary Survey And Resuscitation : ATLS management : ABCDE of trauma care : A = Airway with cervical spine protection. B = Breathing and ventilation. C = Circulation with haemorrhage control. D = Disability (neurological status) limitation. E = Exposure : undress the patient and assess for other injuries. F = Fluid resuscitation. Airway : • Check airway, maintain patency. • Remove foreign body. • Stabilize neck for suspected spine injury. Breathing : • Administer 100% O 2 . • Expose chest & observe expansion. Circulation : • Insert 2 I/V cannula or a central venous catheter preferably through unburned skin. • Keep patient warm. • Draw blood for essential studies : CBC, S. electrolyte & urea, S. creatinine, Liver function test, Blood grouping & Rh typing, S albumin, Arterial blood gas analysis. Disability : • Establish level of consciousness. Exposure : • Remove all clothing & dwelling. • Keep patient warm. Fluid resuscitation : (>10% in children, >15% in adults need fluid) • Estimate burn area. • Commence IV fluid (Hartmann's saline). • Parkland formula (4 × % of TBSA burn × weight in kg burn). Pain relief : • Morphine / Nalbuphine / Tramadol in small incremental dose according to pain score. Nutrition : • Insert nasogastric tube within 10-14 hours for larger burns (>20% TBSA in adult, >13% in children). Antibiotics : • For first week - Gram positive coverage (empirical), if already infected, both gram positive & negative. Tetanus Prophylaxis : • Both TT & TIG, if not immunized. • Anti-ulcer & anti-histamine drugs. • Vitamins : ▸ Vitamin A (50000 IU), twice daily for 5 days ▸ Vitamin C (500 mg), three times daily for 1 month ▸ Vitamin E (400 mg), three times daily for 1 month ▸ Zinc sulphate (20mg), two times daily, 10 days Activity : • Head and of bed raised at 20 ° . ► Initial wound care : • Adequate analgesia. • Adequate explanation of procedure. • Maintain warm environment. • Wash and dry within 30 minutes. • Remove loose devitalized tissue, exudate, old dressing. • Blisters are opened and all dead skin removed across the joints. • Reassess the depth & extent of burn. • Dress with silver sulfadiazine cream and cover with fresh sterile gauze & cotton or apply hydrocolloid dressing & cover with fresh gauze. • Reassess 24/48 hours after for any change. • Plan the strategy for further management. • Consider Escharotomy for circumferential full thickness or deep dermal burns of the limbs, chest, abdomen, or neck. ► Emergency Dressing : 1. Before dressing one amp of Nalbuphine (Nalbun-2) (I/M) + Prochlorperazine (Vergon) or ¾ amp of Pethidine (I/M) (G-Pethidine) should be given. 2. Need not to burst the blister. But remove local turbid (pus) fluid collection with the help of sterile artery forceps (if any). 3. Apply thick layer of silcream (Silver sulphadiazine 1%) + gauze. Paraffin soaked gauze / Gypsonate / Supratulle may be used here. Silcream is usually supplied in the ward. 4. Next apply cotton layer. Cotton will keep the dressing dry. Wet dressing remains contact with contaminated bed sheets / exterior & will act as a media for bacterial growth. 5. Next apply roll bandage ± Plaster. For upper / lower limb use plaster / slab for immobilization. Elevation of the affected limb is a must ti prevent oedema. 6. For face : Use povidone iodine 5% / viodin ointment as silcream (Silver sulphadiazine 1%) will produce scar. Dressing of Burn : 4 layers of dressing – 1. Silver Sulfadiazine 1% : 3-5mm thickness. 2. Sterile Gauze: To prevent adhesion of cotton with the wound. 3. Cotton layer: For soaking. 4. Roll Bandage: For fixing. ► Management of the burn wound from second day onwards : • Give maintenance fluid. • Nutritional management : ▸ Early enteral feeding is encouraged on it to prevents paralytic ileus. ▸ Reduce rise of catabolism. ▸ Maintenance integrity. ▸ Reduce rink of bacterial translocation. • Pharmacological management : ▸ Mild: Paracetamol, Ibuprofen. ▸ Moderate: Codeine, Tramadol. ▸ Strong: Morphine, Diamorphine. • Non pharmacological management : ▸ Positioning (comfortable positioning reduces pain). ▸ Elevation (elevation of limb & head reduces pain & edema). ▸ Dressing (open wounds are more painful than dressed wound). ▸ Communication (reassurance & explanation reduce anxiety). ► Assess % of burn & hospitalize : as a rough rule, the patient's hand is approximately 1% of the body surface area. Diagnosis of Percentage (%) of burn is done by Lund & Brower Chart or, by rule of nine. Percentage (%) surface area (Rule of 9) : 1. Head and neck 9 2. Entire right arm 9 3. Entire left arm 9 4. Front of the trunk = 2 × 9 18 5. Back of the trunk = 2 × 9 18 6. Entire left leg (back & front) 18 7. Entire right leg (back & front) 18 8. Perineum 1 Indication of hospitalization : 1. For adult : If burnt area is >15%. 2. For pediatric patient: If burnt area is >10%. ► Fluid resuscitation : Fluid is must in child with burn more than 10% BSA or, adult with more than 15% burn. ► Diagnosis of Percentage (%) of burn is done by Lund & Brower Chart or, by rule of nine.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ 1° / 2° / 3° Burn (approximate % of burn). ¤ Diet : Normal c̄ High protein + ORS + Plenty fluid. ¤ Inf. H/S 2 L (must calculate amount), I/V stat @ ___ d/m. ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Dicephin (drg) 1g, 1 vial IV stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Flucloxacillin / Flupen (drg) 500 mg, 1 vial IV stat & QDS. ¤ Inj. Nalbuphine 20 mg / Raltrox-2 (ops), 1 amp I/V stat & sos ¤ Inj. Prochlorperazine 12.5 mg (1 ml) / Vergon (ops), 1 amp I/V stat & TDS c̄ Nalbuphine. ¤ Inj. Ketorolac / Torax (sq) 60 mg, 1 vial I/V stat & TDS. ¤ Inj. Omeprazole / PPI (acm) 40 mg, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ¤ Inj. TIG / Vaxitet IG (incp), 1 amp I/M stat on right arm. ¤ Inj. TT / Vaxitet (incp) / TT vax (pop), 1 amp I/M stat on left arm. ± ¤ Continuous catheterization. ► Fresh Order after correction of fluid deficit : Advice : 1. Hb% 2. Serum albumin ► TT may not be given as children under 7 years, as they gets EPI schedule vaccination. TIG may be given. ► Injectable Pethidine 75 mg / Tramadol 100 mg / Ketorolac 30 mg may also be used as analgesics. ► Pethidine is the best analgesics for children. If it is supplied in the ward, it must be used. Or, Sent patient’s attendance to buy Inj. Pethidine 100 mg with a slip containing sign & seal of DOD. Dose must be adjusted according to weight. Fluid Calculation in case of Burn : Required fluid in ml = 4 × Weight in kg × Burn BSA (%). ● Half of this volume is given in first 8 hours & Second half is given in subsequent 16 hours. ● At first, Crystalloid fluid most commonly Ringer's Lactate or Hartmann's solution is given. ● For example, a 70 kg patient with a 40% burn would, using this formula, require a figure of 4 × 70 × 40 = 11,200 mL. Half of this (5600 mL) is given in the first 8 hour and the other half in the next 16 hour. ● This does not include the daily maintenance fluid requirement (3 liters in an adult). [BSA : body surface area]. ● In children, maintenance fluid must be given. This is normally Dextrose Saline given as follows – 100 ml/kg for first 10 kg + 50 ml/kg for next 10 kg + 25 ml/kg for rest of the body weight. ● If patient come delay, adjust the fluid according to urine outcome. Notes – - Mild burn / scald on a face may need excision & reconstruction surgery. Refer to a plastic surgeon, even a mild case of facial burn. - Nalbuphine inj. or, Diclofenac 50 mg supp / 75 mg inj. major to minor burn is advocated before dressing, then normal saline wash then silcream dressing is must. Mosquito net is suggested in case of open burn wound. - Must avoid pressure on flap / skin graft after surgery. Avoid dressing even avoid untie roll bandage within 03 days of skin graft surgery. Positioning of patient must be suggested as per site of wound / surgery. - Povidone Iodine 5% / Betadine ointment or, Mupirocin 2% / Bactrocin ointment should be advised in discharge.

treatment.title: Discharge note
treatment.description: Discharge note (BURN) : ► Cap. Cefixime / Triocim (bex) / Denver (hc) 400 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 –––– 7 days ► Cap. Flucloxacillin / Fluclox (aci) / Flubex (bex) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ––– 7 days ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Toradolin (rad) / Zeropain (hc), 1+1+1 or 1+0+1 (A/M) – if pain present ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Seclo (sq) / Cosec (drg), 1 + 0 + 1 (30 min before meal) – 7 days ► Tab. Vitamin C 250 mg / Ceevit (sq) / Vasco (ops), 1 + 1 + 1 or 1 + 0 + 1 –– 10 – 15 days উপদেশ : নিয়মিত ওষুধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে কোন সমস্যা হলে সার্জারি বহির্বিভাগ বা বার্ন-প্লাস্টিক বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। For Surgical Scars : ✔ Betamethasone 0.1% + Neomycin 0.5% / Betnovate-N cream, ক্রিম আঙ্গুলের সাহায্যে ক্ষতস্থানে দিনে দুইবার (ভোর ও সন্ধ্যায়) লাগিয়ে ম্যাসাজ করবেন। ক্রিম লাগিয়ে রোদে যাবেন না। ........ ২ মাস / প্রয়োজনমত Or, ✔ Multi-agents (Methylpolysiloxane, Paraffin, etc.) / Cicatrix cream (50 ml, Price 3,000 BDT). ক্রিম আঙ্গুলের সাহায্যে ক্ষতস্থানে দিনে দুইবার (ভোর ও সন্ধ্যায়) লাগিয়ে ম্যাসাজ করবেন। ক্রিম লাগিয়ে রোদে যাবেন না। ....... ২ মাস / প্রয়োজনমত

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discussion
treatment.description: Emergency conservative management for any inguinal or scrotal condition followed by emergency definitive surgical management : Δ Dx Adv. : ▸ CBC ▸ RBS ± ▸ Blood grouping & Rh typing. DRE : Diagnosis is made by only clinical. Tx, ► Diet : Normal. Antibiotic(s) : [Cipro + Metro or, levo] ► Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg, 1+0+1. + ► Tab. Metronidazole 400 mg, 1+1+1. Or, ► Tab. Levofloxacin 500 mg, 0+0+1. Analgesics : ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M, if pain) ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ► Ointment Anustat (bex) / Anorel (pop) / Retocain (drg) / Erian (sq), Apply locally BD. ± ► Ointment Nitroglycerin 0.4% / Rectocare (sq) / Nitrovas (pop), Apply locally BD [stay in lying position after each application to avoid headache]. Laxative(s) : ► Cap. Lubiprostone / Lubilax (bacn) / Gutfix (sq) 24 mcg, 1+0+1. ± ► Cap. Tranexamic acid / Traxyl (nuv) / Tracid (acm) 500 mg, 1+1+1 (if bleeding). ± ► Diosmin + Hesperidin, if hemorrhoid. ± ► Hip bath. ► Definite surgical treatment : • Hemorrhoid/Piles → Longlow/stapled haemorrhoidectomy. • Perianal abscess is incision & drainage. • Rectal polyp → polypectomy, • Carcinoma rectum → APR (Abdominal perineal resection), • Fistula-in-ano → Fistulotomy, Fistulectomy. • Acute anal fissure → Conservative management. • Chronic anal fissure → Fissurectomy, Sphincterotomy.

treatment.title: Causes
treatment.description: Hematochezia / Per rectal (P/R) bleeding H/O constipation / Painful defecation. D/D : (1) Haemorrhoid / Piles (2) Rectal prolapsed. (3) Anal fissure / Fistula in ano. (4) Rectal prolapse. (5) Ano-rectal abscess / Peri-anal abscess. (6) Anorectal injury. (7) Carcinoma rectum.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: Hemorrhoids / Piles / Varicose veins of the anal canal
treatment.description: Haemorrhoids are defined as an abnormal swelling or enlargement of the anal vascular cushions. Presents with – ● Painless bleeding (bright red) that can be mild to severe (even squatting or coughing may ooze grasp of blood). ● Mucous discharge, prolapsed, occasionally pain due to proctitis ● Chronic cases develop anemia due to continuous blood loss. Internal hemorrhoids are the most common type. They occur inside the rectum and usually aren't painful. External hemorrhoids are the least common type. They occur at the opening of the anus and can be very painful. Four degrees of haemorrhoids : Degree Features Treatment First (1°) Bleed only, no prolapse Conservative Second (2°) Prolapse, but reduce spontaneously RBL Inj. Sclerosant Third (3°) Prolapse and can be manually reduced Haemorrhoidectomy (THD & PPH) Fourth (4°) Permanently prolapsed Haemorrhoidectomy RBL : Rubber Band Ligation THD : Transanal Haemorrhoaid Dearterialization PPH : Procedure for Prolapse and Haemorrhoaid Recurrent symptoms → Exclude malignancy with appropriate rectal and colonic imaging / sigmoidoscopy / short colonoscopy. Clinical : DRE / Proctoscopy.

treatment.disease_name: Hemorrhoids / Piles / Varicose veins of the anal canal
treatment.description: Δ Dx Adv. : ▸ CBC ▸ RBS ± ▸ Blood grouping & Rh typing. DRE : Diagnosis is made by only clinical. Tx, ► Diet : Normal. Antibiotic(s) : [Cipro + Metro or, levo] ► Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg, 1+0+1. + ► Tab. Metronidazole 400 mg, 1+1+1. Or, ► Tab. Levofloxacin 500 mg, 0+0+1. Analgesics : ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M, if pain) ► Cap. Tranexamic acid / Traxyl (nuv) / Tracid (acm) 500 mg, 1+1+1 (if bleeding). ± ► Diosmin + Hesperidin, if hemorrhoid. ± ► Hip bath.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Features of anal fissure : 1. Severe pain on defecation that is sharp/cutting/tearing in nature and aggravated by constipation. 2. Bleeding is usually small (drop by drop) and occurs as a streak by the side stools. 3. A foul-smelling discharge is present in chronic cases. 4. Pruritus may be also present. Primary anal fissures are most commonly located at the posterior midline (90%) and less commonly at the anterior midline. Skin tag are often seen. In anal fissure, no diagnostic procedures are required. A digital rectal examination (DRE) is painful and should be avoided or deferred. Chronic anal fissure : ● Sentinel skin tag ● Hypertrophic anal papilla ● Hypertrophic anal sphincter Definitive surgical management : 1. Manual dilatation of the anal sphincters - Lord's procedure. 2. Lateral anal sphincterotomy. 3. Dorsal fissurectomy and sphincterotomy.

treatment.description: Δ Dx Adv. : ▸ CBC ▸ RBS ± ▸ Blood grouping & Rh typing. DRE : Diagnosis is made by only clinical. Tx, ► Diet : Normal. Antibiotic(s) : [Cipro + Metro or, levo] ► Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg, 1+0+1. + ► Tab. Metronidazole 400 mg, 1+1+1. Or, ► Tab. Levofloxacin 500 mg, 0+0+1. Analgesics : ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M, if pain) ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ► Ointment Anustat (bex) / Anorel (pop) / Retocain (drg) / Erian (sq), Apply locally BD. ± ► Ointment Nitroglycerin 0.4% / Rectocare (sq) / Nitrovas (pop), Apply locally BD [stay in lying position after each application to avoid headache]. treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: An anal fistula — also called fistula-in-ano — is a tunnel that develops between the inside of the anus and the outside skin around the anus. Most anal fistulas are the result of an infection that starts in an anal gland. ○ Persistent or intermittent discharge that may be blood stained. ○ Pain and itching around anus. ○ May H/O perianal abscess that has been drained. On local examination : ✓ Usually, an opening within 3-4 cm of the anal orifice with granulation tissue. ✓ DRE : May reveal the internal opening. Low level fistula : Opens into the anal canal below the anorectal ring. High level fistula : Opens into the canal at or above the anorectal ring. Advice : Fistulography, Hb%, RBS. Definitive surgical management : Fisulotomy, Fistulectomy, Seton suture placement.

treatment.title: Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: DRUG MANAGEMENT OF COLO-RECTAL SURGERY ► Antibiotic : ¤ Tab. Levofloxacin 500 mg, 0+0+1 --- 7 – 14 days, Or, ¤ Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg, 1+0+1 --- 7 – 14 days ± ¤ Tab. Metronidazole 400 mg, 1+1+1 --- 5 – 7 days + ► Laxative / Stool softener – (Any one or two) ¤ Isubgul powder 2tsp with water 2 times a day, or, ¤ Cap. Lubiprostone 24 μ g / Lubilax (bacn) / Gutfix (sq) / Lubistone (incp) / Lubigut (zis) 24 mcg, 1+0+1 --- 15 days, or, ¤ Tab. Sodium Picosulfate 10mg / Ezylife (phar) / Ezyfeel (euro) / Abdolax (incp) / Sopilax (sq), 0+0+1 ---- 15 days, or, ¤ Syp. Lactulose 3.35 gm/5 ml, 2 or 3 tsf TDS – 15-30 days, or, ¤ In pg: Syp. Magnesium hydroxide – 2 tsf bd/tds for 5-7 days. ✔ Lactulose 3.35 gm/5 ml Oral Solution / Avolac / Dlac (drg) / Lactu (bio) / Osmolax (sq) / Sinalac (ibn) / Regulose (gen) / Serelose (bex), দুই থেকে তিন চামচ দিনে তিনবার ---- (৫ দিন – ১ মাস) (কোষ্ঠকাঠিন্য / কষা ভাব দূর বা পায়খানা নরম না হওয়া পর্যন্ত) ± ✔ Powder Ispaghula Husk [Psyllium] 3.5 gm/5.4 gm / Powder Fiberlax (incp) / Radigel-N (rad) / Gutgel (ops) / Laxadil-EP (drg), Take 1-2 spoonful with the provided spoon, Put this husk into an empty glass, Fill glass with water (150 ml or 2/3 of the glass) stir & drink immediately --- (regular at night) as per need + ¤ Hipbath / হিপবাথ : এক গামলা কুসুম গরম পানিতে ২ চামুচ লবণ এবং ২ চামুচ পভিসেপ বা ভায়োডিন ১০% স্যলুশন মিশিয়ে ১০-৩০ মিনিট গামলায় বসে থাকবনে, দৈনিক ৩ বার এবং প্রত্যকেবার মলত্যাগের পর। ------- প্রয়োজনমত বা ১ মাস + ► Anto-rectal ointment : ✔ Ointment Cinchocaine + Hydrocortisone + Neomycin + Esculin / Anustat (bex) / Anorel (pop) / Retocain (drg) / Erian (sq), A small quantity of the ointment should be applied with finger (nozzle for deep application) in the painful pruritic area in the morning and evening and after each stool for 15 – 30 days. ± ► Anti-hemorroid drug – It is must in hemorrhoid / piles. DS Tablet are Diosmin 900 mg + Hesperidin 100 mg. ¤ Tab. Diosmin 450 mg + Hesperidin 50 mg / Normanal (ren) / Daflon (servier) / Hemorif (sq) / Sangril (hc), 2 + 2 + 2 for first 3 to 7 days then, 2 + 0 + 2 for next 3 to 7 days then, 1 + 0 + 1 for 14 to 60 days. ± If pain (analgesic & anti-ulcerant) : ✔ Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Toradolin (rad) / Rolac (ren) / Toramax (pop) / Zeropain (hc), 1 +0 + 1 or, 1 + 1 + 1 (after meal) ---- 5 days ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Xeldrin (aci) / Xelopes (bacn) / Cosec (drg) / Ometac (nav), 1+0+1 (30 minutes before meal) --- 5 – 10 days ± Anti-fibrinolytic drugs : ¤ Cap. Tranexamic acid 500 mg / Traxyl (nuv) / Xamic (ren) / Frabex (sq)/Tracid (acm)/Tramic (paci), 1+1+1 (If bleeding) - 5 days উপদেশ : (১) নিয়মিত ঔষধ খাবেন ও ব্যবহার করবেন। এন্টিবায়োটিকের ডোজ পূর্ণ করবেন, ব্যবস্থাপত্রে উল্লেখিত সময় পর্যন্ত খাবেন (২) প্রচুর পানি ও শাক-সবজি খাবেন। পায়খানা নরম রাখবেন ও কোষ্ঠকাঠিন্য মুক্ত থাকবেন। দুধ, বেলে শরবত, পেঁপে বা এমন খাবার যাতে পায়খানা নরম হয় সেসব খাবার খাবেন। অতিরিক্ত চা কফি পরিহার করুন। যেসব খাবার বা ওষুধ খেলে কোষ্ঠকাঠিন্য হয়, যেমন : আয়রন ট্যাবলেট, ক্যালসিয়াম ইত্যাদি বিনা কারণে বা চিকিৎসকের পরামর্শ ছাড়া খাবেন না। (৩) পরবর্তীতে এই সমস্যবরর জন্য পূর্বের সব কাগজপাতিসহ দেখা করবেন।
