treatment.title: Ileostomy / Colostomy Closure
treatment.description: Inv. : Distal loopogram. Rx on admission : Oral antibiotic : Tab. Ciprofloxacin / Ciprocin 500 mg (1+0+1) & other symptomatic drugs.

treatment.title: Evacuation procedure & Catheter Trial
treatment.description: Retention of Urine Types : • Acute • Chronic Clinical presentation of acute urinary retention : • Inability to pass urine and severe lower abdominal discomfort. • Bladder is usually palpable and tender. Management of acute urinary retention: Urgent catheterization : either per urethra or suprapubically. Clinical presentation of chronic retention of urine : • Patients often have no pain. • Low pressure retention may present with overflow incontinence. • High pressure retention often presents with renal failure. Management of chronic urinary retention: • Catheterization : Standard treatment. • Monitor diuresis. Treatment during discharge : • Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg, 1+1+1 for 7 days. • Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (30 min before meal) for 7 days. • Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg, 1+0+1 (if pain after meal) for 7 days. Diagnose the cause of acute urinary retention & treat according to cause. Investigations : - USG of W/A c̄ PVR (post void residual), then – - Plain X-ray of KUB, then – - IVU. Emergency management : 1. Attempt Catheterization with Bardia bi channel foley’s catheter 16Fr for male (12Fr for female). If fails – 2. Attempt Catheterization with Bardia bi channel foley’s catheter 12Fr. If fails – 3. Attempt Catheterization with All Silicone bi channel foley’s catheter 8Fr. If fails – 4. Attempt Catheterization with All Silicone bi channel foley’s catheter 6Fr. If fails – 5. Supra pubic puncture on admission. [Or, Supra pubic cystostomy (SPC) in emergency OT (if available).] - See Procedure portion of this book to know how to do catheterization (Standard). Here, easy & practical method is given : 1. Wear gloves & introduce almost the whole tube of jasocaine jelly into urethra. 2. Introduce catheter into urethra almost all the length. 3. Aspirate the straight channel with a empty 10cc syringe for confirmation of urine drainage. 4. If urine come into the empty syringe then inflate the balloon with distilled water through oblique channel. Amount of D/W or tap water is 10cc for bardia bi channel catheter & amount is written in the oblique channel of All Silicone catheter. 5. Attach a urobag (protos) with straight channel. 6. Fix the catheter with micropore in the inner thigh. Same for All Silicone catheter – Just have to pull the silicone wire out, after insertion of whole catheter into the urethra. Special note – Sometimes jasocaine jelly split over the gloves & the catheter. Then the tube slips & tough to insert. Then use artery forceps for insertion. - Never try catheterization in known case of stricture urethra. It is a contra-indication. - If catheterization procedures fails then supra pubic puncture (SPP) / aspiration : 1. Take a urobag & saline set. Chamber end of the saline set is fixed with catheter end of the urobag. 2. Antiseptic wash with Chlorhexidine / Povidone Iodine. 3. Insert the needle of the 20G or 22G IV cannula into the abdominal wall just 2 cm above the pubic symphysis or on the bulged portion. 4. Remove the needle & fix the cannula end of the saline set with the IV cannula. 5. Fix the cannula with abdominal wall by micropore. Catheter Trial : If catheter in situ remains more than 7 days, we should not remove the catheter all of a sudden, rather we should give catheter trial. Urinary bladder remains empty for a long time (7 or more days) & a little amount of urine will stimulate UB & micturition desire will occur. Procedure : Tie the tube of urobag till micturition desire appears. When patient desires to urinate tie off the urobag tube & re tie the tube after 10 mins. Thus follow the tie & re-tie method for 4 to 6 times. Then remove the catheter. Removal of a catheter : Take an empty 10 cc syringe & aspirate all the D/W through oblique channel. Preferably twice, in the second time nothing (or only air) will aspirate. Then pull off the catheter.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: Benign Enlargement of Prostate (BEP) / Hyperplasia of Prostate (BHP)
treatment.description: LUTS (Lower Urinary tract symptoms) can be described as : Obstructive symptoms / Voiding : • hesitancy (worsened if the bladder is very full); • poor flow (unimproved by straining); • intermittent stream – stops and starts; • dribbling (including after micturition); • sensation of poor bladder emptying; • episodes of near retention; Irritative symptoms / Storage : • frequency; • nocturia; • urgency; • urge incontinence; • nocturnal incontinence (enuresis). Features of bladder outflow obstruction : • Acute retention of urine • Chronic retention • Overflow incontinence • Impaired bladder emptying DRE : Evidence of prostatic enlargement with a smooth prostate gland. Abdominal examination : Evidence of bladder enlargement in patients with urinary retention. Investigation : 1. USG of W/A c̄ PVR. 2. Urine R/M/E or U/R/M/E. 3. PSA (if Ca Prostate suspected). Post-void residual volume (PVR) normally less than 20 – 50 mL. D/D – Carcinoma Prostate.

treatment.title: Outdoor Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Benign Enlargement of Prostate (BEP) / Hyperplasia of Prostate (BHP)
treatment.description: Management • Conservative : Drugs- Cap. Tamsulosin plus Dutasteride, daily for 6 months • Surgery : Trans-urethral resection of the prostate (TURP). Conservative treatment : lf PVR 12 ml/s. If prostate size (volume) 40 gm : (Moderate BPH symptoms PVoL ► Cap. Tamsulosin 0.4 mg / Uromax (uni) / Maxrin (sq) / Uropass (acm) / Urocap (drg) / Uroflo (bex), 0+0+1 (after meal) --- continue [May be increased upto 0.8 mg or 800mcg after 2-4 weeks if response is inadequate.] Or, if prostate size (volume) ≥ 40 gm (Moderate & progressive BPH symptoms PVoL ≥ 30cc; PSA ≥ 1.5 ng/ml) : ► Cap. Tamsulosin 0.4 mg + Dutasteride 0.5 mg / Uromax-D (uni) / Maxrin D (sq) / Tamisol D (hc) / Combomax (drg) / Uroflo Plus (bex), 0 + 0 + 1 (after meal) ---- continue If irritative symptoms present : Add – ► Tab. Solifenacin Succinate 5 or 10 mg / Utrobin (uni) / Solider (sq), 1+0+0 ---- continue [¤ The most common adverse reactions of drugs used in “BPH/ Urinary retention/ Urinary incontinence” were retro-grade ejaculation, impotence, decreased libido, breast disorders (including breast enlargement and tenderness), ejaculation disorders and dizziness. So avoid in young adult male.] Advice : 1. Reduction of evening fluid intake. 2. Reduction of caffeine intake. 3. Bladder retraining and prevention of constipation.

treatment.title: Acute retention of urine
treatment.disease_name: Benign Enlargement of Prostate (BEP) / Hyperplasia of Prostate (BHP)
treatment.description: Hospitalization. Emergency catheterization by – Foleys rubber bi channel catheter 16 Fr ↓ Foleys rubber bi channel catheter 14 Fr or 12 Fr ↓ Silicon bi channel catheter 10 Fr or 08 Fr ↓ Supra-pubic puncture (SPP) with IV cannula (18 G / 20 G) ↓ Supra-pubic cystostomy or supra-pubic catheter (SPC)

treatment.title: Surgical Treatment & TURP
treatment.disease_name: Benign Enlargement of Prostate (BEP) / Hyperplasia of Prostate (BHP)
treatment.description: Surgical Treatment : Trans-urethral resection of prostate (TURP) Or, Open prostatectomy (for larger BEP). Indications of TURP : 1. Refractory urinary retention 2. Chronic retention with renal insufficiency---- increased serum creatinine increased blood urea level, residual urine volume 200 ml or more, hydroureteronephrosis in IVU uremic manifestation. 3. BOO with complication • Recurrent UTI • Recurrent gross heamaturia • Large bladder diverticula • Stone formation 4. Combination of severe symptoms and a low flow rate Treatment during discharge on 5th POD (TURP) : • Cap. Cefixime 400 mg, 1+0+1 for 7 days. • Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 for 7 days. • Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg, 1+0+1 for 7 days. • Laxatives - Syp. Lactulose, 3 tsf 8-12 hourly for 7 days.

treatment.title: Urinary incontinence
treatment.description: Urinary incontinence Urinary incontinence is defined as any involuntary leakage of urine. Risk factors for incontinence : Childbirth, hysterectomy, obesity, recurrent UTI, dementia or poor mobility, an acute illness or hospitalization, especially in older people, smoking, caffeine and constipation. Common types are : - Stress incontinence - Urge incontinence - Overflow incontinence - Continual incontinence.

treatment.title: Stress Incontinence
treatment.description: STRESS INCONTINENCE : This occurs because passive bladder pressure exceeds the urethral pressure, due either to poor pelvic floor support or a weak urethral sphincter. Stress incontinence is very common in women and seen most frequently following childbirth. Symptom – patient complains of incontinence during coughing, sneezing or exertion.

treatment.title: Urge Incontinence (Overactive Bladder Syndrome)
treatment.description: URGE INCONTINENCE (OVERACTIVE BLADDER SYNDROME): This usually occurs because of detrusor over-activity, which produces an increased bladder pressure that overcomes the urethral sphincter. The incidence of urge incontinence increases with age. Usually resulting from UTI, DM, spina bifida or multiple sclerosis. Symptoms – sudden, intense urge to urinate followed by an involuntary loss of urine. Management ► Weight reduction if obese. ► Pelvic floor muscle exercise (Kegel exercises) (please see below). ► STRESS INCONTINENCE : No approved medications. The antidepressant duloxetine can be used. Dose is 60-80 mg/day, should be started at low dose 20 mg BD then increase dose according to the response. ¤ Tab. Duloxetine / Dulox (acm) / Duloxen (incp) 30 mg, 0+0+1 --- 7 days, then 1+0+1 --- 3 months or continue ► URGE INCONTINENCE (OVERACTIVE BLADDER) : Anticholinergic drug like tolterodine (start 1 mg/day then gradually increase according to response up to 2 mg 2 times daily), solifenacin (start 5 mg/day then gradually increase according to response up to 10 mg/day) can be helpful. Mirabegron is a new drug to treat stress incontinence. Effective in irritative symptoms – ¤ Tab. Solifenacin Succinate 5 mg or 10 mg / Utrobin (uni) / Solider (sq), 1+0+0 ---- 1 month or continue Or, ¤ Tolterodine Tartrate 2 mg / Tab. Ucol (sq) / Cap. Detrusin LA, 1 + 0 + 0 ---- 1 month or continue

treatment.title: Overflow Incontinence
treatment.description: This occurs when the bladder becomes chronically over-distended. It is most commonly seen in men with benign prostatic enlargement. Symptoms - patient complains of incontinence of urine without any urge of micturition. This may happen during the day or even wetting the bed at night. Examination- Rectal examination is needed to assess the prostate in men and to exclude faecal impaction as a cause of incontinence Management ► Alpha blockers (tamsulosin, alfuzocin, terazosin) is helpful. In men with overflow incontinence, these medications relax bladder neck muscles and muscle fibers in the prostate and make it easier to empty the bladder. Drug treatment as ‘BEP’ portion. ► Surgery- if not responding to drugs.

treatment.title: Continual Incontinence
treatment.description: This is suggestive of a fistula, usually between the bladder and vagina (vesicovaginal), or the ureter and vagina (ureterovaginal). Prolonged obstructed labour is common cause of vesicovaginal fistulae. Also may be following gynaecological surgery, patients with gynaecological malignancy or post radiotherapy. Genital examination should be done to identify phimosis or paraphimosis in men, and vaginal mucosal atrophy, cystoceles or rectoceles in women. Usually treatment is surgical.

treatment.title: Pelvic floor muscle / Kegel Exercises
treatment.description: Pelvic floor muscle exercises (Kegel exercises) – improves both stress and urge incontinence (see venereology portion of GP Diary for details) : 1. Find the right muscles. To identify pelvic floor muscles, stop urination in midstream. Watch carefully which muscles is required to do this. Exercise of those muscle should be done. 2. Perfect your technique. Tighten (contract) the muscles use to stop urinating and hold for five seconds, and then relax for five seconds. 3. Maintain your focus. Work up to holding the contractions for 10 seconds at a time. 4. Repeat three times a day-do this 3 times/day (10 to 15 repetitions each time).

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Renal & Ureteric stone (also called renal calculi, nephrolithiasis or urolithiasis) A kidney stone usually asymptomatic, until it moves around within the kidney or passes into one of the ureters. The most common location for a stone to obstruct is near the ureteropelvic junction (UPJ) because in this region the diameter of the ureter is very narrow. ± Nausea & vomiting. Symptoms : Pain (Ureteric colic) : ● Site : Loin or groin and lower abdomen (right side). ● Character : Intermittent, agonizing ● Severe, sharp pain in the side and back, below the ribs. ● Pain comes in waves and fluctuates in intensity (colicky pain). ● Radiation : Typically referred to the groin, external genitalia and the anterior surface of the thigh. If the stone enters the bladder, the pain can be referred to the tip of the penis. ● Patient can locate the pain with his/her tip of a finger. ● Pain or burning sensation while urinating. ● ± Nausea & Vomiting, ± Hematuria. Investigation : USG of W/A & X-ray KUB. Some stones are not seen in USG but in x-ray & vice versa. There are four main types of ureteral stones, including : Calcium stones, Uric acid stones. Struvite stones, Cystine stones. Definitive treatment : A. Conservative only : If Or, if asymptomatic with – - Single stone, - Unilateral. - Below renal pelvis. - No hydronephrosis. B. Surgical treatment : 1. Repeated attacks of pain and the stone is not moving. 2. Stone is increasing in size. 3. Complete obstruction of the kidney flow. 4. Urine is infected (complicated & recurrent UTI). 5. Stone is too large to pass. 6. Stone is obstructing solitary kidney or bilateral obstruction. Conservative treatment : if stone size is/are less than 5 mm, usually no treatment (or conservative rx) needed. May wait & treat conservatively up to 8-10 mm with no pain & non complicated cases under urologist supervision.

treatment.title: Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Medical expulsion therapy : ► Cap. Silodosin 4 mg or, 8 mg / Rapasin (uni) / Rapiflo (sq) / Flowrap (hc) / Siloflo (aris), 0+0+1 ---- 1 month ► Furosemide 20 mg + Spironolactone 50 mg / Frulac (ori) / Diretic (drg) / Fusid Plus (sq) / Edeloss (incp), 1+0+0 ---- 10 days ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Toradolin (rad) / Toramax (pop) / Zeropain (hc) / Rolac (ren), 1+0+1 (A/M) ---- 10 days ► MUPS Tab. Esomeprazole 20 mg / Maxpro MUPS (ren) / Remmo (bex) / Othera (aci) / Exium MUPS, 1+0+1 ---- 10 days ± ► Tab. Levofloxacin 500 mg / Levoxin (incp) / Evo (bex) / Trevox (sq) / 3-F (edruc) / Ovel (aris), 0+0+1 ---- 10 days Advice : Water intake 2-2.5 L/day, Regular jogging & jumping, Careful watch to urine stem for calculi (if possible). ○ Potassium Citrate + Citric Acid (1500 mg+250 mg)/5 ml oral solution may be given to prevent renal stones to some extent in recurrent cases. It alkalizes urine & relieves dysuria. ± ► Syp. Citra-K (uni) / Urikal (incp) / CK (pop) / Uriset (sq), 2 TSF (10 ml) + 1 glass (250 ml) water for adults & ½ glass (125 ml) for children, 3 times daily. ○ Advice / Prophylactic measures to be adopted by women with recurrent urinary infections : • Fluid intake of at least 2 L/day. • Regular complete emptying of bladder. • Good personal hygiene. • Emptying of bladder before and after sexual intercourse. • Cranberry / sour fruit juice may be effective. Surgical treatment, if symptomatic & no improvement – Procedure for stone removal : ¤ Extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy (ESWL) / Stone crash. ¤ Percutaneous nephrolithotomy (PCNL) / mini per PCNL. ¤ Ureteroscopy (URS) / Ureteroscopy. ¤ Cystoscopic removal or destruction. ¤ Endoscopic removal or destruction. ¤ Open removal or destruction (not now a days).

treatment.title: Counselling (KUB stone)
treatment.description: High chances of recurrence, so counselling is a must : যাদের একবার কিডনিতে পাথর ধরা পড়েছে, গবেষণা বলছে তাদের কিডনিতে পাথর অপারেশন করার পরেও বা প্রাকৃতিকভাবে বের হওয়ার পরেও আগামী পাঁচ বছরের মধ্যে শতকরা ৩৫ ভাগ মানুষের আবার পাথর হতে পারে এবং দশ বছরের মধ্যে প্রায় শতকরা ৫০ ভাগ মানুষের পুনরায় কিডনিতে পাথর হতে পারে। সেই জন্য কতগুলো নিয়ম মেনে চললে এটার প্রতিকার সম্ভব। তবে অনেক ক্ষেত্রে পুঙ্খানুপুঙ্খভাবে নিয়ম মেনে চলার পরও পাথর হতে পারে, নিয়মগুলো তারপরও কিছু নিয়ম মেনে চলা ভালো। ১। যথেষ্ট পরিমাণ পানি পান করবেন যাতে, প্রতিদিন মূত্রের পরিমাণ আনুমানিক দুই লিটার এর মত হয়। ২। পানির বদলে অন্য কোন কোমল পানীয় বা জুস পরিহার করবেন। সর্বদা বিশুদ্ধ পানি পান করার চেষ্টা করবেন। ৩। পাতে লবণ খাওয়া যাবেনা। কচুর লতি, পালং শাক, পুঁই শাক, গরু - খাসির মাংস খাওয়া পরিহার করুন। পূর্বে দুধ বা দুগ্ধ জাতীয় খাবার বারণ করতে বললেও বর্তমানে এ জাতীয় খাবারে কোনো নিষেধাজ্ঞা নাই।

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: RUPTURE URETHRA ○ H/O RTA / crush injury / fall followed by inability to pass urine. ○ Bleeding from external urethral meatus. Inv. : Retrograde urethrogram. Treatment : ► Catheterization (16 or 18 Fr), If failed : Suprapubic puncture or cystostomy, followed by urethroplasty. If not done blood clot within UB & chance of urethral stricture. ► Referred to urology department / urologist for definite treatment : delayed anastomotic urethroplasty. ► Inj. Cefuroxime 750 mg / Furotil (hc) / Furocef (ren) 750 mg, 1 vial I/V stat & TDS. ► Inj. Nalbuphine 20 mg/2ml / Nalbun-2 (incp) / Raltrox-2 (ops), 1 amp I/M stat & BD (sos) ► Inj. Ondansetron 8 mg / Anset (ops) / Zofra (skf) / Emiston (ibn), 1 amp I/V stat & TDS. ► Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / PPI (acm) / Omep (aris) / Omecap (che), 1 vial I/V stat & BD.

treatment.title: Hematuria
treatment.description: 1. C/C : - Presence of blood in the urine for ___ hours / days. - Pain & burning sensation during micturition (may be absent in painless hematuria). - Lower abdominal pain & discomfort (may be absent). 2. O/E : - Pulse & BP. - Renal angle tenderness. - Abdomen. - Actually microscopic hematuria is a case of nephrology / medicine & macroscopic / gross / frank hematuria is a case of urology / surgery. It may be due to trauma, stone or neoplasm. So, further evaluation & diagnosis is must. Then treat according to cause. - If huge & ongoing loss of blood, then add – ¤ Inf. H/S 3L, I/V stat @ 40 d/m. ¤ 2 unit BT demand given. ¤ Continuous catheterization. - Continuous bladder irrigation : Catheterization with Bardia Tri channel Foley’s catheter 24 Fr (or, 22 Fr). Attach normal saline (N/S) bottle one after one with the oblique irrigation channel till the bleeding / red coloured urine stops to drain into urobag. Actually the aim of emergency irrigation is to prevent blood clot in the UB. - Giving flash to a catheter : Often blood clots within catheter tube & blocks irrigation / urine drainage. Then forcefully aspiration with tommy syringe is done. Or, instilled 30 cc D/W & aspirate.

treatment.title: Inguinal Hernia
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : – Inguinal or inguino-scrotal lump & asymptomatic → Hernia. Or, – Inguino-scrotal swelling → Hydrocele. Examination of the lump & definite surgery. Dx : (1) Right / Left / Both sided (2) Reducible / Irreducible (3) Incomplete / Complete (4) Indirect / Direct (5) Inguinal / Femoral Hernia.

treatment.title: Obstructed Inguinal Hernia
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : 1. C/C : - Sudden sever inguinal pain. (If pain more than 6 hours, suspect strangulated hernia.) - Vomiting for same duration (S/D). 2. O/A/E : - Very tender mass & may reddening of overlying skin. - Restricted movement of abdomen with respiration. - No obliteration of liver dullness. - Examination of hernia. 3. O/G/E : - Pulse & BP. - Temp : raised. - Anemia & Jaundice. Refer to general surgeon / urologist for definitive management : 1. Direct inguinal hernia : Hernioplasty. 2. Indirect inguinal hernia : Herniotomy with hernioplasty.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Inguino-scrotal Lump / Swelling Emergency conservative management for any inguinal or scrotal condition followed by emergency definitive surgical management : Δ Inguino-scrotal Swelling Adv. : ▸ USG of W/A Tx, ► Bed rest. ► NPO-TFO. ► Inf. N/S 1 L+ 5% DNS 1 L, I/V stat @ 20 drops/min. ► Inj. Ketorolac 30 mg/1ml / Rolac (ren), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. ► Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Ometid (ops), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ► Inj. Nalbuphine 20 mg/1ml/ Nalbun-DS (incp), 1 amp I/M sos. ► Inj. Ondansetron 8 mg/4ml / Emistat (hc), 1 amp I/V sos c̄ Nalbuphine. ± ► Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftron (sq) 1g, 1 amp I/V BD.

treatment.title: Hydrocoele
treatment.description: Refer to general surgeon / urologist for definitive management : 1. In infants : Wait till the age of two years to allow spontaneous resolution. 2. Beyond the age of two years : Herniotomy by the inguinal approach. 3. In adults : Hydrocoele Repair (Jaboulay or Lord's Procedures).

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Inguino-scrotal Lump / Swelling Emergency conservative management for any inguinal or scrotal condition followed by emergency definitive surgical management : Δ Inguino-scrotal Swelling Adv. : ▸ USG of W/A Tx, ► Bed rest. ► NPO-TFO. ► Inf. N/S 1 L+ 5% DNS 1 L, I/V stat @ 20 drops/min. ► Inj. Ketorolac 30 mg/1ml / Rolac (ren), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. ► Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Ometid (ops), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ► Inj. Nalbuphine 20 mg/1ml/ Nalbun-DS (incp), 1 amp I/M sos. ► Inj. Ondansetron 8 mg/4ml / Emistat (hc), 1 amp I/V sos c̄ Nalbuphine. ± ► Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftron (sq) 1g, 1 amp I/V BD.

treatment.title: Epididymo-Orchitis
treatment.description: Pain and swelling of scrotal region, relieved by rest and elevation of testis. ► Choice in older men : Cap. Ciprofloxacin / Ciprocin 500 mg (sq), 1+0+1 --- 7 days Or, ► Choice in young men with H/O sexual exposure : Cap. Doxycycline / Doxicap 100 mg (ren), 1+0+1 ---- 7 days ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Toradolin (rad) / Toramax (pop), (1+0+1) or, (1+1+1) (if pain, after meal) ---- 5 days ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Ometid (ops) / Opal (hc), 1 + 0 + 1 (30 minutes before meal) ---- 5 days

treatment.title: Torsion of Testis
treatment.description: ● Most common between ages of 10-25 years though it may occur at any age. Sudden onset of pain in the affected testis and lower abdomen. ● Patients with testicular torsion typically present with abrupt scrotal pain, whereas those with epididymo-orchitis have a more gradual onset of pain. Emergency hospitalization & management : see above. Definitive surgery : Exploration of scrotum. ● If viable : Untwisting of cord and orchidopexy. ● If not viable : Orchidectomy.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Inguino-scrotal Lump / Swelling Emergency conservative management for any inguinal or scrotal condition followed by emergency definitive surgical management : Δ Inguino-scrotal Swelling Adv. : ▸ USG of W/A Tx, ► Bed rest. ► NPO-TFO. ► Inf. N/S 1 L+ 5% DNS 1 L, I/V stat @ 20 drops/min. ► Inj. Ketorolac 30 mg/1ml / Rolac (ren), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS. ► Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / Ometid (ops), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ► Inj. Nalbuphine 20 mg/1ml/ Nalbun-DS (incp), 1 amp I/M sos. ► Inj. Ondansetron 8 mg/4ml / Emistat (hc), 1 amp I/V sos c̄ Nalbuphine. ± ► Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftron (sq) 1g, 1 amp I/V BD.

treatment.title: Acute & Chronic Ischaemia
treatment.description: Peripheral vascular diseases consist of arterial and venous disorders (acute and chronic). Peripheral arterial occlusive disease, peripheral occlusive vascular disease is a common disorder caused by acute or chronic interruption of blood supply to the limbs, usually due to atherosclerosis. AETIOLOGY : - atherosclerosis and thrombosis - embolism (80% cardiac in origin, microthrombi cause ‘blue toe syndrome’) - vascular trauma - vasculitis (e.g. Burger's disease) The presence of PAD is one of the best predictors of future death, stroke or MI. Predictor : ● Male > female before 65 years. ● Increased risk with increased age. ● Affects 10 % of population >65 years in Western world. Fontaine classification : - Stage I : Asymptomatic. - Stage II : Intermittent claudication. - Stage III : Rest pain / night pain. - Stage IV. Necrosis / gangrene. Chronic ischaemia : - Intermittent claudication in calf (femoral disease), thigh (iliac disease) or buttock (aorto-ilaic disease). - Cold peripheries and prolonged capillary refill time. - Rest pain, especially at night. - Venous guttering. - Absent pulses. - Arterial ulcers, especially over pressure points (heels, toes). - Knee contractures. - Leriche’s syndrome (intermittent claudication, impotence, absent femoral pulses) indicates aortic occlusion. Acute ischaemia (7P’s) : - Pain - Pallor - Pulseless - Paraesthesia and paralysis — indicate life threatening ischaemia that requires immediate treatment, - Perishing cold - Pistol shot onset - Mottling Muscle rigidity

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: MANAGEMENT : Non- disabling claudication (chronic ischemia of mild to moderate intensity) management - ● Stop smoking & strict sugar control (if applicable). ● Exercise programme (exercise until claudication occurs, rest until pain subsides, repeat cycles for 45 - 60 minutes) ● Antiplatelet agents : aspirin or clopidogrel. ● Statin : regardless of baseline cholesterol levels. ● Tab. Cilostazol 50 or 100 mg / Cilosta (sq) / Pancil (drg), 100 mg 2 times daily – improves claudication distance (contraindicated in patient with CCF). ● Tab. Pentoxifylline 400 mg / Oxifyl CR (sq) / Trental (syn), 2 to 3 times daily : Improves blood flow by increasing RBC deformability and blood viscosity. Disabling claudication / critical ischaemia Mx : - Immediate refer to vascular surgeon - May need : Balloon angioplasty = intravascular stent Bypass surgery or Amputation. In critical ischaemia whether limb saving surgery or life-saving amputation depending on viability of limbs. Acute ischaemia : - Need immediate referral to vascular surgeon. - Heparin anticoagulation. - Surgical or radiological (aspiration) embolectomy. - Thrombolytic therapy. In acute ischaemia case — revascularization (pharmacological or surgical means) must be within golden hour (6 hours).

treatment.title: Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Ø Tab. Pentoxifylline / Oxifyl CR (sq) 400 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার পরে) ...... চলবে Ø Tab. Cilostazol 100 mg / Cilosta (sq), ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার পূর্বে) ....... চলবে ± Ø Tab. Atorvastatin / Atova (bex) 10 or 20 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ....... চলবে ± Ø Tab. Aspirin + Clopidogrel (75+75) mg / Clopid-As (drg), ০ + ১ + ০ (খাবার পরে) ..... চলবে ± Ø Cap. Cefixime / Afix 200 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ........ ৭ দিন Ø Cap. Esomeprazole 20 mg / Sergel (hc), ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার ৩০ মিনিট পূর্বে) .... ১৫ দিন Ø Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Rolac, ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার পরে) ..... ব্যথা থাকলে খাবেন উপদেশ - প্রেসার / ডায়াবেটিকসের ঔষুধ আগের মতই চলবে। ধুমপান বা কোনো নেশা জাতীয় দ্রব্য সেবন সম্পূর্ণ নিষিদ্ধ। পরবর্তী চিকিৎসার জন্য NICVD, শেরে বাংলা নগর, ঢাকাতে বা ভাস্কুলার সার্জনের সাথে যোগাযোগ করবেন। ► Vestibular sedatives like Cinnarizine can be used in high dose – Ø Tab. Cinnarizine / Cinaron 15 mg (3+3+3), Or, 75 mg (1+0+1) / (1+1+1).

treatment.description: 1. I/V cannula 18 G 3 2. Micropore 2” (JMS / Nichiban) 1 3. Saline Set 2 4. Inf. Hartmann’s Solution / Inf. H/S 1000ml 4 5. Inf. Normal Solution / Inf. N/S 1000ml 4 6. Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftron / Axon / Winner 1g 1 7. Inj. Ketorolac / Toradolin / Todol / Torax 30 mg 1 8. Surgical gloves / Ansell Gammex / Comfit (7” + 6.5”) 4 + 2 9. NG tube 14 Fr + 16 Fr 1+1 10. 50 cc syringe 1 11. 2% Jesocaine Jelly 1 12. Two way Foley’s Catheter (Bardia) 16 Fr 1 13. Urobag / Protos 1 14. 10 cc D/W (Distilled Water) 1 15. 10 cc syringe 1 Endotracheal (ET) tube 7 + 7.5 1 + 1 Inj. Suxa / Neosuxa 5 Inj. Ephidrin 2 Inj. Dopamine 1 Inj. G-Ketamine 1 Inj. Cotson 100mg 1 Inj. Roxadex / Dexa 2 Viodin / Povisep 100 ml 1 Shaving blade (Gillette) 2 BP blade 22 1 Drain tube 16 + 18 Fr 1 + 1 Prolene 1 C/B needle 1 Vicryl 2-0 C/B needle 2 Vicryl 3-0 C/B needle 2 Mer Silk 1-0 C/B needle 1 Vicryl / Solus 1/0 T/C c̄ R/B 2 Note : JMS is a company of Singapore. JMI is a Bangladeshi company. Both are efficient, especially JMS. - Ansell / Comfit gloves are costly, no need to suggest those gloves for only catheterization or NG intubation. - Urinary catheters are supplied in the ward (TruCath) & others where balloon inflating side channels are sealed & requires syringe with needle for inflation. These are sometimes blocked & removal of catheter may be difficult. Bardia (original) catheters are best.

Inj. TPS 1 25. Viodin / Povisep 100 ml 1 26. Shaving blade (Gillette) 2 27. BP blade 22 1 28. Drain tube 16 + 18 Fr 1 + 1 29. Prolene 1 C/B needle 1 30. Vicryl 2-0 C/B needle 2 31. treatment.title: Antiseptics / De-sloughing agent / Surgical toileting
treatment.disease_name: Antiseptics / De-sloughing agent / Drugs for surgical toileting
treatment.description: Antiseptics / De-sloughing agent / Drugs for surgical toileting – 1. Povidone Iodine 10% / Viodin (sq) solution = Over intact skin, abrasion, deep cut, muscles etc., almost anywhere. 2. Chlorhexidine 0.5% / Hexisol / Alcohol / Spirit = Only over intact skin & disinfecting in-animated objects. Never on wound or tissue. 3. Cetrimide 0.5% + Chlorhexidine 0.1% / Savlon = immersion of sharp surgical instrument overnight. It is used for only household activities. Never on intact or lacerated skin or tissue. 4. Hydrogen per-oxide / H 2 O 2 / Jolly = Gangrenous, dirty, maggot wound, not on healthy tissue. 5. 0.9% NaCl / Inf. Normal saline (NS) = most safe & effective disinfectant for surgical toileting (anywhere) including intra-peritoneal. Principle of surgical toileting with sterile 0.9% NaCl is to wash thoroughly with gallons of water [1 UK gallon ≈ 4.6L]. 6. Bleaching powder / EUSOL / Edinburg University Solution of Lime : Ulcers & slough, never over gut. 7. 6-10% Acetic Acid / Pran Vinegar : Greenish pus & suspected Pseudomonas infection.

treatment.title: Delayed Gut Function after Major Abdominal Surgery
treatment.disease_name: Delayed Gut Function after Major Abdominal Surgery
treatment.description: Diagnosis : - No flatus passed. - Stethoscope : No abdominal / bowel sound. Immediate Inv. : Serum electrolytes. If, ↓ K + , then add – Θ Inf. N/S 1L + 2 amp Inf. Potassium Chloride 1.5g/10ml / Inj. KT, I/V very slowly stat @15d/m with cardiac monitoring.

treatment.title: Risk bond
treatment.description: Consent of patient party for surgery (High risk bond) অঙ্গিকারনামা আমি নিম্নে স্বাক্ষরকারী, এই মর্মে প্র্যতয়ন করছি যে, আমার রোগীর রোগ সম্পর্কে সংশিষ্ট চিকিৎসকগণ আমাকে পুরোপুরি বুঝিয়েছেন। আমি তা সম্পর্কে সম্পূর্ণরূপে বুঝেছি। আমার রোগীর অবস্থা আংশকাজনক, অপারেশন পরবর্তী সময়ে, এমনকি অপারেশন টেবিলেও রোগীর মৃত্যু হতে পারে। এ সম্পর্কে আমি সম্পূর্ণরূপে অবগত। অবশ বা অজ্ঞানজনিত কারণে বা অপারেশন চলাকালীন বা অপারেশন পরবর্তী সময়ে সৃষ্ট জটিলতায় সংশিষ্ট চিকিৎসকগণের সিদ্ধান্তের সাথে আমি একমত। সবকিছু জেনে বুঝে জরুরী ভিত্তিতে অপারেশনের অনুমতি দিলাম। এতে কোন প্রকার সমস্যার জন্য সংশিষ্ট চিকিৎসকগণ কোনোভাবেই দ্বায়ী থাকবেন না। ওয়ার্ড নং : কখগ মেডিকেল কলেজ হাসপাতাল, কখগ। স্বাক্ষর : তারিখ : রোগীর সাথে সম্পর্ক :

treatment.title: Ocular Pharmacology
treatment.description: The conjunctival sac (30 μ L) can contain only 1 drop (50 μ L) of any drug. More than 1 drop will eventually split out of eyes, if instilled. So E/D is always prescribed 1 drop in one eye at once. Antibiotic eye drops (E/D) are of topical use. More frequent use of E/D increases efficacy. Oral moxifloxacin is used once daily but E/D moxifloxacin is used 3 – 6 times a day for more duration ( 7–20 days ) than oral. Eye ointment is usually used once at night before sleep, as it causes blurred vision in day time. Immediate referral to eye specialist or ophthalmologist if vision is compromised or showing systemic symptoms or any simple condition not improving within 3 months.

treatment.title: Features & Inv
treatment.description: CATARACT (Senile Immature Cataract / SIC) / (Senile Mature Cataract / SMC) / Age related cataract / Sub-capsular cataract (SPC) C/F : Blurring or Dimness of vision : Detailed evaluation of anterior and posterior segment with measurement of visual acuity to confirm cataract. ● Visual acuity reduced to hand movement & chalky white pupillary area with hazy media. → Mature cataract. → Advice the patient for cataract surgery : SICS / Phaco + PCIOL under Local anesthesia after following investigations. ● Visual acuity reduced to counting finger & grayish white pupillary area with visible fundus. → Immature cataract → Do the refraction to get the best corrected visual acuity (BCVA). Diagnostic tools : 1. C/C : - Gradual dimness of vision of Rt / Lf eye for long duration. 2. R/F : - DM (-) / (+). - HTN (-) / (+). - BA (Bronchial Asthma) (-) / (+). 3. Investigations for cataract surgery : 1. RBS or FBS & 2HABF 2. Complete Blood Count 3. ECG 4. Chest X ray 5. Echocardiography (if needed) 4. Ocular Examination / Biometry of eye : 1. SPT (Patent / Block), If blocked then, 2. Regurgitation test (-) / (+). 3. Pupillary Light Reflex (PLR) : Rt – Brisk / Absent. Lt – Brisk / Absent. 4. IOP / Intra ocular pressure. 5. Visual acuity. 6. Lens : Pearly white / Greyish / Milky. 7. Biometry (K1, K2, AXL, IOL, IOP of both R&L Eye). 8. Fundus.

Omeprazole 20 mg / Cosec (drg) / Losectil (skf) / PPI (acm) / Prevas (gen) / 20mg 1+0+1 (B/M) ––– 5 – 10 days ± ☼ Tab. Betamethasone 0.1%+Neomycin 0.5%/ E/Oint Neobet-E, apply on affected eye, before sleep daily for 15 days.

treatment.title: Painless Red Eye
treatment.description: Acute Red Eye (Hyperaemia) → Dx Scheme Red Eye with painless/mild pain → May treat by a GP. Red Eye with moderate/severe pain → Refer to an ophthalmologist. Painless Red Eye : (Or, No / Mild pain Red Eye) - Diffuse red eye - No/Mild pain - Normal vision - Little burning sensation Viral Conjunctivitis : ○ Bilateral painless red eye. ○ Discharge : Watery. ○ Itching : No / Mild. ○ Photophobia : No. ○ Contact H/O others with conjunctivitis. ○ H/O URTI (Upper Respiratory Tract Infection). Allergic / Atopic Conjunctivitis : ○ Bilateral painless red eye. ○ Usually one eye is more severe than other. ○ Discharge : Watery. ○ Itching : Intense itching. ○ Photophobia : No. Dry Eye / Contact lens irritation : ○ May lead to Keratitis / Corneal Ulcer / Abrasion. ○ Bilateral painless red eye. ○ Usually one eye is more severe than other. ○ Discharge : Watery/Serous (intermittent). ○ Itching : No. ○ Photophobia : No. Kerato-conjunctivitis (Vernal/Allergic) : ○ Discharge : Watery. ○ Itching : Present. ○ Photophobia : No. Bacterial Conjunctivitis : ○ Unilateral painless red eye (may be bilateral in later). ○ Discharge : Purulent. ○ Itching : No/Little. ○ Photophobia : No. ○ Early morning Purulent Discharge & Matted Eyelash. Gonococcal / Chlamydial Conjunctivitis : ○ Discharge : Hyper-purulent. ○ Itching : No/Little. ○ Photophobia : No. ○ ± H/O sexual exposure. ○ Acute onset ( → Gonococcal. ○ Late onset purulent discharge → Chlamydial.

treatment.title: Focal Redness
treatment.description: Localized / Focal Redness with no / little pain : Subconjunctival Haemorrhage ○ Unilateral localized redness (may be diffuse also) ○ No/little pain & No itching ○ No discharge ○ H/O Cough/Straining ○ H/O taking anti-coagulants ○ H/O trauma/assault

treatment.title: Painful Red Eye
treatment.description: Painful Red Eye Redness + Sharp Eye Pain (Foreign Body Sensation) → • Keratitis • Corneal ulcer/abrasion Redness + Dull Aching Pain → • Episcleritis / Scleritis / Uveitis • Acute Angle Closure Glaucoma Episcleritis ○ Unilateral localized redness. ○ Mild painful & burning sensation. ○ No itching. ○ H/O Rheumatoid Arthritis → Scleritis / Episcleritis Blephartitis : ○ Tender swollen eye lid. ○ Mild painful & burning sensation. ○ No itching. Pterygium ○ It is a raised, fleshy, triangular-shaped growth on conjunctive. ○ Usually unilateral. ○ No itching. ○ Mild painful & burning sensation (If inflamed pterygium). Acute Angle Closure Glaucoma (AACG) ● Sudden onset of severe unilateral eye pain (or headache) associated with blurred vision, ● Rainbow-colored halos around bright lights. [রোগী কোন আলো দেখলে তখন আলোর চারপাশে কালো বৃত্তাকার বলয় দেখতে পায়।] ● Nausea/Vomiting/Abdominal pain. ● Unilateral Red Eye + Dilated Pupil → AACG ● Redness + Constricted Pupil → Iritis Orbital Cellulitis ○ Irreducible proptosis. ○ Diplopia & pain with eye movement.

treatment.title: Bacterial Conjunctivitis
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Redness of eye (unilateral, sequentially bilateral). 2. Discharge : usually purulent, thin or thick muco-purulent. 3. Discomfort, photophobia, burning. Very sensitive to light. 4. Swollen eyelids. On awaking lids adhere together. 5. Fluctuating or decreased vision. Model Prescription : ☼ Moxifloxacin 0.5 % Eye drop / E/D Iventi (sq) / Optimox (aris) / Odycin (bex) / Moxibac (pop), ১ ফোটা আক্রান্ত চোখে দিনে ৪ বার থেকে ৬ বার -- ১০ থেকে ১৪ দিন Or, Ciprofloxacin 0.3% Eye drop / E/D Ciprocin (sq) / Cipro-A (acme) / Neofloxin (bex), ১ বা ২ ফোটা আক্রান্ত চোখে দিনে ৪ থেকে ৬ বার --- ১০ থেকে ১৪ দিন ☼ Tobramycin eye ointment / T-Mycin (aris) / Tomycin (ibn), রাতে শোবার পূর্বে আক্রান্ত চোখে একবার লাগাবেন ---- ১০ দিন ☼ Tab. Paracetamol / Napa (bex) / Renova (ops) 500 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ (ভরাপেটে – ব্যথা হলে) --- ৩ থেকে ৫ দিন ☼ Tab. Rupatadine 10 mg / Rupa (aris) / Rupadin (bacn) / Rupin (zis) / Duvent (bex) / Dipa (drg), ০ + ০ + ১ ----- ১০ দিন উপদেশ : ১) সূর্যালোক থেকে বেঁচে থাকতে কালো চশমা ব্যবহার করবেন। ২) চোখে পানি লাগাবেন না। তবে এলার্জিক কঞ্জান্টিভাইটিস এর ক্ষেত্রে পানি দিয়ে চোখ ধুয়ে ওষুধ ব্যবহার করতে পারবেন। ৩) চোখ কচলাবেন না। ৪) চোখে ঠান্ডা পানির কম্প্রেশন দিতে পারবেন। ৫) সমস্যা না কমলে সাত (০৭) দিন পর দেখা করবেন।

treatment.title: Allergic Conjunctivitis
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Redness & intense itching of eyes and urge to rub eyes. 2. Discharge : Usually watery, stringy mucus discharge. 3. Seasonal and annual variation. Model Prescription : ☼ Olopatadine 0.2% eye drop / E/D Alacot DS / Patadin DS (pop) / Olpadin DS (aris) / Patalon DS (ibn), চোখে ১ ফোটা করে দিনে ২ বার ---- ৩০ দিন থেকে ৩ মাস Or, Ketotifen 0.025% Eye Drop / Alarid (sq) / Tofen (bex) / Fenat (drg) E/D, চোখে ১ ফোটা করে দিনে ৩ বার ---- ৩০ দিন ☼ Tab. Cetirizine 10 mg / Acitrin (aci) / Cetrin (drg) / Atrizin (bex) / Alatrol (sq) / Citin (ops), ০ + ০ + ১ ------ ৭ থেকে ১৪ দিন

treatment.title: Viral Conjunctivitis
treatment.description: C/F : ● Severe itching causing frequent rubbing, making eyes redden. ● Burning sensation in eye. ● Eye become swelled (area around the edge of cornea where the cornea meets sclera). ● Photophobia. ● Eye look muddy. ● Family history of atopy. O/E : ● Conjunctiva – follicle and papillae in peripheral conjunctiva. ● Lens – Transparent. ● Cornea – white dot at limbus. Model Prescription : ☼ Fluorometholone + Tetrahydrozoline (0.1% + 0.025%) Ophthalmic Solution / AFM-T (aris) / Metalone Plus (incp) / Eylon (ibn), 1 drop in affected or both eye 4 times (6 hourly) daily for 15 days, then, 1 drop 2 times (12 hourly) daily for next 15 days. ± ☼ Olopatadine 0.2% eye drop / E/D Alacot DS / Patadin DS (pop) / Olpadin DS (aris) / Patalon DS (ibn) / Olodin DS (rem), 1 drop 2 times daily ---- 2 months, Then, 1 drop 4 times daily ---- 1 month ☼ Tab. Fexofenadine 120 mg / Alanil (acm) / Axodin (bex) / Dinafex (skf) / Fexofast (drg) / Telfast (syn), 0+0+1 ---- 15 days In resistant cases : tear production / anti-dry eye drug : ☼ Cyclosporine 0.05% eye drop / Cyporin (aris) / Sporium (incp), 1 drop in affected eye 8 hourly ---- 3 months

treatment.title: Keratitis & Corneal Ulcer
treatment.description: Breach of continuity of the corneal epithelium super-added with organismal invasion. Keratitis : Inflammation of the cornea. Symptoms: Pain, redness, watering, photophobia, white spot on the cornea, decreased vision with short duration Diagnosed by detailed history and evaluation of anterior segment with slit lamp. Clinical Types & Symptomatic Assessment: 1. Bacterial Infection-Redness, watering & discharge. 2. Viral Infection- Redness, watering & severe photophobia 3. Fungal Infection- Redness, watering, discharge & H/O agricultural trauma (especially in harvesting season) Clinical signs : 1. Lid edema 2. Circumciliary congestion 3. Corneal epithelial defect with infiltrate 4. Dendrite or punctate shaped lesions (in viral keratitis) 5. Dry, cheesy, grayish white infiltrate with feathery margin (in fungal keratitis) 6. Satellite lesions (in fungal keratitis) 7. Anterior chamber reaction present 8. Hypopyon (yellowish white colour & water level present – in bacterial corneal ulcer; thick, cheesy and no water level- in fungal corneal ulcer) Clinical Tests : Clue for diagnosis : 1. Corneal sensitivity diminished – (in favour of viral corneal ulcer). 2. Corneal smear with 10% KOH (+) ve – (in favour of fungal corneal ulcer) 3. More symptoms, less signs – (in bacterial corneal ulcer) (patient looks very ill) 4. Less symptoms, more signs – (in fungal corneal ulcer) (patient not worried, not anxious)

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: A. Bacterial Corneal Ulcer 1. Intensive broad-spectrum topical antibiotic therapy : Dose depends on severity of ulcer • Monotherapy with a Fluoroquinolone. (Moxifloxacin / Levofloxacin / Gatifloxacin / Besifloxacin) • Dual therapy Combination of two antibiotics. For Gram-negative organisms – Gentamicin, Tobramycin etc & For Gram-positive organisms – Ciprofloxacin, Cephalosporin. • Topical fortified (concentrated) drops, e.g., - Fortified Gentamicin / Tobramycin 15 mg/ml or, - Fortified Cefuroxime / Cefazolin / Ceftazidime 50 mg/ml are most effective for moderate to severe cases. • Sub-conjunctival injections may be also given for moderate to severe cases. It may be mixed with injection Atropine. Gentamicin (20 – 40 mg) is usually preferred. Injections are given every 24 hour for about 5 days. 2. Atropine (1%) E/D – 1 drop 2-3 times daily 3. Systemic Analgesic like Diclofenac/Naproxen (NSAIDs) with anti-ulcerant (PPIs). 4. Anti-glaucoma medication- if necessary. B. Fungal Corneal Ulcer 1. Local anti-mycotics : Natamycin (eg. N Mycin/ Natoph) 5% one hourly, then tapered according to response. 2. Systemic anti-mycotics: e.g., Tab Ketoconazole / Fluconazole 200 mg b.i.d (12 hrly) (in normal liver function). 3. Atropine (1%) E/D one drop 8 hourly. 4. Analgesics and anti-glaucoma medication, if needed. C. Viral Keratitis or Corneal Ulcer 1. Topical antiviral : Acyclovir 3% (Clovir) or Ganciclovir 15 % (Xoviral) eye gel- 5 times a day for 4 weeks 2. Artificial tear (e.g., Sinafresh Liquigel) : 6 times daily for 8 weeks 3. Mild Steroid eye drops (e.g, Flurometholone E/D) (e.g., AFM) if no superadded infection- 4 times daily for 4 weeks 4. Use of photo-sunglass. 5. Antibiotic : If superadded infection- Moxifloxacin E/D (e.g, Moxigen) - 4 times daily for 2/3 weeks (before steroid use). 6. Vitamin C : Tab. Vit-C- 12 hourly for 2 weeks. 7. Systemic antiviral : Tab. Acyclovir 800 mg five times daily or Valcyclovir 1 gm for 5-7 days should be given in case of Herpes Zoster virus involvement 8. Systemic steroids to reduce severity of post herpetic neuralgia, optic neuritis, cranial nerve palsy in case of Herpes Zoster Ophthalmicus. Note : For all types of corneal ulcer, if medical treatment is non-responding & impending to perforation, surgery may be needed (in referral hospital) like – • Tarsorrhaphy (temporary or permanent) • Cauterization • Conjunctival hooding • Corneal debridement • Tissue adhesion by cyanoacrylate glue with bandage contact lens (BCL) • Keratoplasty (therapeutic)

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/F : ● Redness in eye. ● Pain (difficult to open eyelid). ● Watering with something feeling in eye. O/E : ● Conjunctiva – congested. ● Comea – keratitic lesion. Model Prescription : ☼ Ganciclovir 0.15% Oph Gel / Gansovir (nipa) / Xoviral (aris) / Herpigel (pop) / Zirgan (ibn) eye gel, 5 times a day for 21 days. ☼ Chloramphenicol 0.5% / A-phenicol (acm) / Opsophenicol (osl) / Cloram (ibn) eye drop, 1 drop in both eyes 4 times a day for 7 days. ☼ Polyethylene Glycol + Propylene Glycol (0.4%+0.3%) / PolyTear (unido) / Systear (aris) / Fresh tear (skf) / filtear (incp) eye drop, 1 drop 6 times daily for 1 month. ☼ Tab. Desloratadine 5 mg Momento (bex) / Deslor (ori) / Neocilor (incp) / Sedno (sq) / Desatrol (nav), 0+0+1 for 15 days. If non responsive : refer to ophthalmologist.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/F : ● Eyes become red. ● Foreign Body (burning) sensation of affected eye. ● Blurred vision. ● H/O Bell's palsy and patient can't close the affected eye. O/E : ● Eyelid – incomplete eye closure of affected eye. ● Cornea – exposure keratitis. ● Conjunctiva – congested. Model Prescription : ☼ Levofloxacin 0.5% or 1.5% (TS) / Levobac (pop) / Levoxin (incp) / Ovel (aris) / Levosina (ibn) E/D, 1 drop 4 times a day for 1 month. ☼ Ophthalmic Ointment Ciprofloxacin 0.3% / Bactin (ibn) / Cero (gaco) / Ciprox (ops), apply at bed time — continue. ☼ Artificial tear eye drop : Hypromellose 0.5% / Tear (gaco) / Lubric (incp) / Hypro (nipa) / Tearin (bio) E/D, 1 drop in right eye hourly — continue. ± ☼ Povidone Iodine 5% / Eyerin (osl) / Protear (aris) / Aquatear (bio) / Teargen (gen) E/D, 1 drop 4 – 8 times a day — continue. Refer / consultation from ophthalmologist.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/F : ● H/O agricultural trauma. ● Pain, redness, whitish discharge from affected eye. O/E : ● Vision — decreased. ● Conjunctiva — congested. ● Cornea — ulcerated area in the center of cornea. Model Prescription : ☼ Ophthalmic Suspension Natamycin 5% / N-Mycin (aris) / Natapro (pop) / Natoph (ibn) / Natagen (gen) / Natadrop (rem), 1 drop 6-8 times daily (every 2 hourly) ........ 21 days ☼ Ophthalmic Ointment Clotrimazole 1% / Fungizol (rem) / Aristen (aris), Apply 3 – 6 times a day ........ 21 days ☼ E/D Moxifloxacin 0.5%/ Moxivin (hc)/Optimox (aris)/Iventi (sq) / Odycin (bex) / Moxif (bio), 1 drop every 4 hours ...... 10 – 21 days ☼ E/D Atropine 1% / Atrogen (gen) / Ocupin (osl), 1 drop 3 times daily ........ 7 days ☼ Tab. Paracetamol 1000 mg / Renova Max (ops) / Napa One (bex) / Ace Power (sq) / Fast One (acm), 1+1+1 ± 1 ....... 5 days, if pain

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : ● Ocular pain. ● Light sensitivity. ● Redness with watering. ● Gradually vision. P/H : Contact lens user (most commonly happened by contact lens users and by Gram -ve organism) O/E : ● Corneal thinning with ulcer. ● Stromal edema. ● Endothelial inflammatory plaque. ● Conjunctiva – congested. Model Prescription : ☼ Moxifloxacin 0.5 % Eye drop / E/D Iventi (sq) / Floromox (ibn) / E/D Optimox (aris) / Odycin (bex) / Moxibac (pop), 1 drop in affected eye 4 times daily for 10 – 21 days. ☼ Ciprofloxacin 0.3% Ophthalmic Ointment / Bactin (ibn) / E/Oint Aprocin (aris) / Cero (gaco) / Ciflox (rem), Apply to the affected eye at time at night for 10 – 21 days. ☼ E/D Atropine 1% / Atrogen (gen) / Ocupin (osl), 1 drop 3 times daily for 7 days Advice : ● Do not apply water to eyes. ● Wear black glasses. ● Avoid eye makeup & artificial eyelash. ● Avoid contact lens. Refer to Ophthalmologist.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: It is a scratch to the surface of cornea. Usually with history of trauma by finger or mild injury or contact lens. C/C : ● Feeling of something in cornea. ● Watering. ● Pain. ● Redness. Model Prescription : ☼ E/Gel Hypromellose 0.3% or 0.5 % / Hyprogel (pop) / Genagel (gen) / Tear (gaco), 1 drop 6 times in both eyes for 7 – 14 days. ☼ Moxifloxacin 0.5 % Eye drop / E/D Iventi (sq) / Floromox (ibn) / E/D Optimox (aris) / Odycin (bex) / Moxibac (pop), 1 drop in affected eye 4 times daily for 7 – 14 days. ☼ Eye Ointment Chloramphenicol 1% / Aristophen (aris) / E/Oint. Opsophenicol (osl) / Cloram (ibn), Apply to the affected eye once at night for 7 – 14 days. ☼ Tab. Vit-C 250 mg / Ceevit (sq) / Chewce (nav) / Citrux-C (nip), 1 + 1 + 1 (after meal) for 5 – 14 days.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: Corneal Ulcer of RE / LE ± Hypopyon
treatment.description: Corneal Ulcer of RE / LE ± Hypopyon [Ward Management] Diagnostic tools : 1. C/C : - Watering of Rt / Lt eye for short duration. - Pain in Rt / Lt eye for short duration. - Photophobia of Rt / Lt eye for short duration. - ± Pus in anterior chamber (hypopyon). - H/O agricultural trauma present / absent. 2. R/F : - DM (-) / (+). - HTN (-) / (+). - BA (Bronchial Asthma) (-) / (+). - BP. 3. Ocular Examination (O/E) : 1. Eye lid – Oedematous. 2. Eye lash – Matted. 3. Conjunctiva – Ciliary congestion. 4. Cornea – Ulcerated area with slough. 5. Anterior chamber (AC) – Hypopyon (+) / (-). 6. Iris – Normal / Muddy. 4. Adv. : 1. Slit lamp Exam. 2. Flourescein dye test.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Corneal Ulcer of RE / LE ± Hypopyon
treatment.description: Δ Corneal ulcer Adv. : ▸ RBS ▸ Corneal scrapping and conjunctival swab for C/S. Tx, ☼ Diet : Normal. ☼ Atropine E/D, 1 drop in affected eye 8 hrly. ☼ Bromofenac sodium eye drop, 1 drop in affected eye 8 hourly. ☼ Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500mg, 1+0+1. ☼ Tab. Diazepam 5mg, 0+0+1. ☼ Tab. Ketorolac 10mg, 1+1+1 (A/M). ☼ Cap. Omeprazole 20mg, 1+0+1 (B/M). ☼ Pad & bandage in affected eye. If hypopyon presents, add – ☼ Tab. Acetazolamide 250 mg, 1+1+0 or, 1+1+1+1 (after meal) ☼ Tab. Potassium chloride (KCl) 600mg, 1+0+1 or, 1+1+1.

treatment.title: Outdoor Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Corneal Ulcer of RE / LE ± Hypopyon
treatment.description: ○ If hospitalized : Treated conservatively or surgery. ○ If surgery, Operation (OT) Note (Date & Time) : • Indication (I) : CU R/E or L/E. • Name of the operation (N/O) : Tarsorraphy R/E or L/E. • Name of Surgeon (N/A) : Dr. A. • Assistant Surgeon (N/AS) : Dr. B. • Anesthesia (A) : L/A. Discharge c̄ advice (D c̄ A) : [○ All E/D must be tapered according to response. ○ Use all types of topical ocular drugs for 21 to 60 days. ○ Use drugs on affected eye only.] ► Moxifloxacin 0.5% Eye Drop, ১ ফোটা করে দিনে ১২ বার (ডান/বাম চোখে) – ১৪ দিন ► Natamycin 5% Eye Drop, ১ ফোটা করে দিনে ১২ বার (ডান/বাম চোখে) – ১৪ দিন ► Atropine 1% Eye drop, ১ ফোটা করে দিনে ৩ বার (ডান/বাম চোখে) – ১৪ দিন ► Ciprofloxacin Eye Ointment, রাতে ১-২ বার (ডান/বাম চোখে) –– ১৪ দিন ► Clotrimazole 1% Eye Ointment, দিনে ৫ বার (ডান/বাম চোখে) – ১৪ দিন ► Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500mg ১+০+১ – ৭ দিন ► Tab. Ketorolac 10mg ১+০+১ (ভরা পেটে; ব্যাথা হলে) – ৭ দিন ► Cap. Omeprazole 20mg ১+০+১ (খাবার আগে) – ৭ দিন উপদেশ : ১। নিয়মিত ওষুধ খাবেন। ১৫ দিন পর দেখা করবেন।। ২। ১ মাস কালো চশমা ব্যবহার করেন। ৩। ১৫ দিন আক্রান্ত চোখে পানি লাগাবেন না।

treatment.title: Eye Lid Injury
treatment.description: C/C – H/O trauma. Definitive management : Repair of eyelid vicryl 6-0. Treatment : ¤ Diet : Normal / Full. ¤ Pad & bandage in affected eye. ¤ Tab. Ciprofloxacin / Neofloxin (bex) 500 mg, 1+0+1 (for 7 days). ¤ Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Zeropain (hc), 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M) (for 5 days). ¤ Tab. Rabeprazole 20 mg / Acifix (bex), 1 + 0 + 1 (for 5 days). ¤ E/D Levofloxacin 0.5% / Levobac (pop) / Ovel (aris) / Levosina (ibn), apply in affected eye 4 – 6 times daily for 10 days. ► Requirements of repair of eye lid – ╠ 6-0 C/B Vicryl – (1) / (2) ╠ Surgical Gloves 7” – (2) ╠ Aristophen / Cloram E/Oint – (1) ╠ Viodin 10% solution – (1). ╠ Eye drape (Tinjin China) – (1). ╠ Syringe 5 cc - (2). ╠ Inj. Jesocaine / Ultracaine - (1). ► Discharge contains oral & local antibiotics, anti-ulcerants & NSAIDs / Paracetamol.

treatment.title: OT lists
treatment.disease_name: Chemical (Alkaline or Acidic) Burns / Ocular Injury
treatment.description: C/C - H/O trauma & disfigurement. ► Requirements of repair of Penetrating eyeball – ╠ 10-0 Monofilament Nylon (Aurolab) – (1) / (2) ╠ Eye drape (Tinjin China). ╠ 15° Lance Tip [Microsurgical knife webal edge] – (1). ╠ Hypromellose 2% / Lubric Gel – (1). ╠ Viodin 10% solution. ╠ Surgical Gloves 7” – (2) ╠ Aristophen / Cloram E/Oint – (1) ╠ Atropine / Atrogen E/Drop – (1) ╠ Inf. H/S 500 ml – (1) / (2). ╠ Saline set – (1). ╠ Syringe 5 cc - (2). ╠ Inj. Jesocaine / Ultracaine - (1).

treatment.title: Treatment (Ocular Injury)
treatment.disease_name: Chemical (Alkaline or Acidic) Burns / Ocular Injury
treatment.description: If Adult : Δ Ocular Injury ¤ Diet : Normal. ¤ Pad & bandage in affected eye. ¤ Tab. Ciprofloxacin / Neofloxin (bex) 500 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ¤ Tab. Esomeprazole / Sergel (hc), 1 + 0 + 1 (30 m mg in B/M) ¤ Tab. Diclofenac / Intafenac (incp) 50 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M) ¤ Inj. Diclofenac 75 mg / 3 ml / Diclofen (ops), 1 amp I/M stat & sos. ¤ Atropine 1% / Atrogen (gen) Eye drop, 1 drop in affected eye 8 hourly. ¤ E/oint. Chloramphenicol 1% / Cloram (ibn), Apply in affected eye 8 hourly. If Child : Δ Ocular Injury ¤ Pad & bandage in affected eye. ¤ Diet : Normal. NPO from night. (As GA may be given in next day.) ¤ Syp. Ciprofloxacin 250 mg/5 ml / Ciprocin (sq) / Cipro-A (acm) / Aprocin (aris), [wt/20] TSF P/O 12 hourly. ¤ Syp. Ibuprofen 100 mg/5 ml / Profen (acm) / Flamex (aci) / Inflam (syn) / Advel (ops), [wt/15] TSF P/O 8 or 12 hourly. ¤ Syp. Famotidine 40mg/5ml / Famomax (bex) / Famotack (sq) / Famogen (gen), [wt/20] TSF P/O 12 hourly. ¤ Tab. Diclofenac / Intafenac 50 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 or, ½ + 0 + ½ (A/M). ¤ Inj. Diclofenac / Intafenac 75 mg / 3 ml, ½ or, 1 amp I/M stat & sos. ¤ Atropine 1% / Atrogen Eye drop, 1 drop in affected eye 8 hourly. ¤ E/oint Chloramphenicol 1% / Cloram, Apply in affected eye 8 hourly.

treatment.title: Treatment (Chemical eye injury)
treatment.disease_name: Chemical (Alkaline or Acidic) Burns / Ocular Injury
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Congestion and chemosis of conjunctiva. 2. Corneal epithelial damage, total loss of corneal epithelium, corneal haziness or totally opaque cornea. 3. Limbal ischaemia, anterior uveitis. 4. Cataract and rise in intraocular pressure (IOP). 5. Lid injury, symblepharon. Δ Chemical injury to eye Tx, ¤ Irrigate eyes with normal saline or tap water for 30 min. ¤ Pad & bandage in affected eye. Mydriatic and Cycloplegic agents : ¤ E/D Homatropine 2% / Matropin (aris) / Homatropine (reman), 1 drop in affected eye 3 times a day. Or, ¤ E/D Atropine 1% / Atrogen (gen) / Ocupin (osl) / Aristopin (aris), 1 drop in affected eye 8 hourly. Antibiotic : ¤ E/D Gentamicin 0.4% / Gentabac (pop) / Genacyn (sq) / Ciprofloxacin 0.3% / Ciprocin (sq) / Ciprox (ops), 1 drop in affected eye 4 – 6 times a day. Analgesic c̄ PPI : ¤ Tab. Ibuprofen 400 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) ¤ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) Steroid eye drop : if inflammation and congestion present : ¤ E/D Dexamethasone 0.1% / Sonexa (aris) / Orbidex (pop) / Dexagen (gen), 1 drop on affected eye, 6 hourly daily. ► Retract the eyelids and irrigate the conjunctival sac with NS or Ringer's lactate or water using intravenous tubing connected to the irrigating solution for 30 minutes. Remove retained solid particles after double eversion of eyelid after anaesthetizing conjunctiva using forceps. If double eversion is not possible, use a moistened cotton tipped applicator. ► Treatment : Surgical repairment options for any ocular injury or chemical burn : 1. Debridement. 2. Tenoplasty. 3. Limbal stem cell transplantation. 4. Keratoplasty 5. Keratopresthesis. ► Patient (Any Serious) Referral : For complicated case / severely injured eyeball, immediate refer with an eye pad (suggestion of senior should be acknowledged). পরবর্তী চিকিৎসার জন্য যোগাযোগ করবেন - জাতীয় চক্ষু বিজ্ঞান ইনস্টিটিউট (NIO), আগারগাঁও, ঢাকা।

treatment.title: Foreign Body (FB) on Eye
treatment.description: C/C – H/O trauma / visible FB. Itching & Redness. ¤ Oxybuprocaine 0.4% / Novocain Eye drop, 1 drop in (R/E or, L/E) affected eye. ↓ ¤ Wash with Normal saline. ↓ Remove FB with hypodermic / baby needle / swab stick / gently with moistened and twisted cotton wool or a clean piece of cloth. ↓ ¤ Chloramphenicol 0.5% / Cloram Eye drop, 1 drop in (R/E or, L/E) affected eye 6 hourly. ↓ ¤ Pad & bandage in affected eye. ↓ Follow up after 24 hours.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: It is a sight-threatening ocular emergency. Common types : 1. Acute narrow angle glaucoma 2. Lens induced (phacomorphic) glaucoma (due to hypermature cataract) seen in elderly patients. Symptoms 1. Sudden severe ocular pain 2. Severe headache 3. Marked fall of vision 4. H/O gradually progressive loss of vision 5. Photophobia, redness 6. Nausea, vomiting (simulating acute abdomen) 7. Excessive watering Signs 1. Vision – Reduced to PL (perception of light) or, PR (projection of rays) 2. Eyeball-Tender 3. Eyelids – Swollen with narrowing palpebral apertures 4. Conjunctiva-Congested & chemosed 5. Cornea – Steamy/cloudy & insensitive 6. Anterior chamber – Very shallow 7. Pupil – Mid dilated, vertically oval & not reacting to light 8. Iris – Pattern is lost & discolored 9. IOP (Intra ocular pressure) highly raised 10. Fundus – Cannot be visualized due to hazy media

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment of Acute Glaucoma ● The patient should be positioned supine. ● Drug treatments are : 1. Pilocarpine (2% or 4%) E/D – frequently (5-10 min interval) upto pupil gets constricted, then 4 times daily. 2. Timolol E/D (0.5 %) twice daily – continue (if there is no contraindication) or / with Brimonidine, 1 drop bd daily. 3. Tab. Acetazolamide (250mg) – 2 tabs stat, then 1 tab thrice daily with tab potassium bicarbonate (600mg) supplement – upto control of intraocular pressure. 4. Intravenous infusion – Mannitol 20%, 1-2 gm/kg body weight, e.g., 300-500 ml over 30-45 minutes, (kidney function test needed). 5. Steroid – antibiotic combined (e.g., Floromox D) E/D – 4 hourly upto control of inflammation. 6. Strong systemic analgesics (even injection pethidine) and anti-emetic to relieve pain and vomiting. ● Referred to secondary / tertiary hospital (after eye becomes quiet) for operative treatment (cataract ± glaucoma surgery).

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : 1. C/C : - Red eye / congestion with marked diminished vision. - Severe headache ± nausea, vomiting. - Lacrimation & photophobia. 2. R/F : - DM (-) / (+). - HTN (-) / (+). - BA (Bronchial Asthma) (-) / (+). 3. Ocular Examination (O/E) : 1. Pupil – dilated, vertically oval. 2. PLR – Non-reactive to light / fixed. 3. IOP – Markedly raised. 4. Adv. : 1. Gonioscopy. 2. Slit lamp Exam. [ ▸ Individually dimness of vision (as in cataract), red eye (as in conjunctivitis), headache (as in migraine) are not medical emergency, but combined of this symptoms is an ocular emergency, so this should be treated immediately with medicine & later definite surgery trabeculectomy with peripheral iridectomy.]

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Acute Congestive Glaucoma (ACG) Tx, ¤ Diet : Normal / Full. ¤ Complete bed rest. ¤ E/D Pilocarpine 2% / Piloma (ops) / Pilo (reman) / Optacarpine (pop), 1 drop in both eyes 4 hourly. ¤ E/D Dexamethasone 0.1% / Sonexa (aris), 1 drop on both eyes 4 hourly. ¤ E/D Timolol 0.5% / Timopress (incp) / Ocupres (pop), 1 drop on both eyes 12 hourly. ¤ Tab. Acetazolamide 250 mg / Acemox (acm), 1 + 1 + 0 (after meal) Or, 1+1+1+1. ¤ Tab. KCl 600mg / Electro K (acm), 1 + 1 + 1. ¤ Cap. Esomeprazole 20mg / Cosec (drg), 1 + 0 + 1 (30 min B/M) ¤ Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Rolac (ren), 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M). ▸ May need, Intensive Pilocarpine therapy : ¤ Eye drop Pilocarpine 2% / % / Piloma (ops) / Pilo (reman) / Optacarpine (pop), 1 drop every min for 5 min, Then, 1 drop every 5 min for 15 min, Then, 1 drop every 15 min for 30 min, Then, 1 drop every 30 min for 2 hrs, Then, 1 drop 12 hrly.

treatment.title: Chalazion vs Stye
treatment.description: Differentiate Chalazion & Stye : Points / Traits Stye / External Hordeolum Chalazion / Internal Hordeolum / Hailstone / Meibomian cyst / Tarsal cyst Definition Acute suppurative inflammation of hair follicle of eyelashes including gland of Zeis & Moll is called Stye. Chronic inflammatory lipogranuloma of mebomian gland is called Chalazion. Causative Agent Mainly infective. Staphylococcus aureus Lipid / Sebum. (No organism primarily) Onset Acute & sudden Chronic & insidious Pain Present Absent or may be. Site of swelling Lid margin Away from lid margin Sign of inflammation Present Absent Treatment Systemic antibiotics, systemic analgesics & Epilation Conservative & Surgery (Incision & curettage) Complication Rarely, orbital cellulitis. Infection, burst, calcification etc.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: STYE & CHALAZION C/F of stye : Redness, pain, swelling of eyelid margin. O/E : ○ Eyelid-swelled. ○ Localized tenderness ○ Pus point around eyelash. Treatment outline of stye : ○ Use a warm compress. ○ Clean your eyelid with mild soap and water ○ Antibiotic, Pain killer & Anti-histamine ○ Avoid using makeup and wearing contact lenses ○ Use antibiotic ointments ○ Massage the area to promote drainage Chalazion : C/C : 1. Localized swelling on the eyelid. 2. Non-tender. 3. Firm in consistency. Incase of infected chalazion : Tender swelling of (middle of) eyelid. Surgery : Incision and curettage (If nodule persist). Treatment of chalazion : ○ Most chalazion treated with warm compresses to the eyelid to increase the circulation of blood to the inflamed area, encourage eyelid drainage, and promote healing. ○ In addition, antibiotic drop or ointment to use immediately after the compresses. ○ Incision and curettage (If cosmetically ugly or large). Refer to ophthalmologist, if not improved.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Tx : STYE & INFECTED CHALAZION Eye Drop : ☼ Moxifloxacin 0.5 % Eye drop / E/D Iventi (sq) / Floromox (ibn) / Optimox (aris) / Odycin (bex) / Moxibac (pop), 1 drop in affected eye 4 times daily for 10 days. Or, ☼ Tobramycin 0.3 %eye drop / T-Mycin (aris) / Tomycin (ibn), 1 drop in affected eye 6 hourly –– 10 days + Eye Oint at night : ☼ Ciprofloxacin 0.3% Ophthalmic Ointment / Bactin (ibn) / E/Oint Aprocin (aris) / Cero (gaco) / Ciflox (rem), Apply to the affected eye at time at night for 10 days. Or, ☼ Tobramycin eye oint, apply in affected eye before sleep – 15 days. + Oral antibiotic : ☼ Cap. Flucloxacillin / A-flox (acm) / Flucloxin (skf) / Flupen (drg) / Flustar (ren) 500 mg / Phylopen DS (sq) 500 mg, 1+1+1+1 –– 7 days Or, ☼ Tab. Cefuroxime Axetil + Clavulanic Acid 500+125 mg / Fuclav (drg) / Turboclav (bex) / Famiclav (acm) 500 mg, 1+0+1 –– 7 days Analgesics (if pain) : ☼ Tab. Paracetamol 665 mg / Napa Extend (bex) / Renova XR (ops) / Xpa XR (aris), 1+1+1 (A/M) –– 5 days, if pain present. Anti-histamine (if itching) : ☼ Tab. Fexofenadine 120 mg / Fenadin (ren) / Fexo (sq) / Fixal (ops) / Ritch (aci) / Fastel (rad), 0+0+1 –– 7 – 15 days Advice : 1. Apply slight heat in affected part. Hot compression. 2. Usually resolves spontaneously but may need surgery. ▸ In case presence of pus – Epilation & evacuation (Stye) or, Vertical incision & curettage (Chalazion) is the definite treatment. Conservative treatment is to wait for spontaneous resolution. Wash with baby shampoo may be suggested in case of stye & eye lid massaging for tiny chalazion.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : Eyes become red, swelled ± itchy. May be extremely itchy. Scale on the eyelashes. Eyes may be red and swelled. Treatment of Blepharitis : ● Warm Compresses : Wet a clean washcloth with warm water. ● Eyelid Scrubs : Soak a clean washcloth, cotton swab (Q-tip) applicator. ● Antibiotics : Apply an antibiotic ointment & eye drops. ● Skin and Eyelid Hygiene. Model Prescription : ☼ Eye ointment Dexamethasone + Tobramycin 0.1%+0.3% / T-Mycin Plus (aris) / Dexagen T (gen) / Tomycin-D (ibn) E/oint, 2 times a day (at base of eyelids) --- 14 days Or, 1 time at night for 14 days ☼ Ophthalmic Solution Carboxymethylcellulose Sodium 1% / Drylief (aris) / Sinafresh liquigel (ibn) / Aquafresh Liquigel (pop) / Tearfresh Liquigel (gen) Eye drop, 1 drop 6 times daily in both eyes for 1 – 2 months ☼ Clean the eyelids with baby shampoo (twice weekly). ☼ Tab. P/C / Ace XR (sq) / Longpara (ibn) 665 mg, 1+1+1 – (if pain) ☼ Tab. Fexofenadine / Dinafex (skf) / Fexomin (ibn) / Vifas (hc) / Delfex (del) / Alanil (acm) 120 mg, 0+0+1 --- 10 days

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Dry Eye Disorder, also called kerato-conjunctivitis Sicca : When occurs, eyes become dry as tears (Reduced production/abnormal tear) are not adequately lubricating eyes. Dry eyes may lead to inflammation and damage of eye surface. C/C : ● Stringing and burning sensation in eyes. ● Redness . ● Feeling of dryness. ● Very sensitive to light. ● Stingy mucus in and around eyes. Model Prescription : ☼ Ophthalmic Solution Carboxymethylcellulose Sodium 1% / Drylief (aris) / Sinafresh liquigel (ibn) / Aquafresh Liquigel (pop) / Tearfresh Liquigel (gen) / Everfresh (gaco) Eye drop, 1 drop 6 times daily in both eyes for 3 months ☼ Ophthalmic Ointment White soft paraffin + Liquid paraffin + Wool alcohol (57.3%+42.5%+0.2%) / Night Fresh (gen) / Parafresh (ibn), Apply at night before sleeping for 3 months. ☼ E/Gel Hypromellose 0.3% or 0.5 % / Hyprogel (pop) / Genagel (gen) / Tear (gaco), 1 drop 4 times in both eyes for 3 months. ± ☼ Povidone Iodine 5% / Eyerin (osl) / Protear (aris) / Aquatear (bio) / Teargen (gen) E/D, 1 drop 4 – 8 times a day — 7 – 14 days Advice : ○ Wear glasses when going out in the sun. ○ Go in the strong sun by wearing sunglasses. ○ Do not stare for long periods of time (at a book, computer). ○ Close your eyelids at regular intervals. ○ Keep the computer below the eyes. ○ Drink plenty of water, stop smoking.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Pterygium is a pinkish (fleshy), triangular tissue overgrowth of the conjunctiva (invades into cornea), which is the thin clear membrane on the surface of the eye. Cause is unknown (but chronic exposure to UV light may cause to happen). Model Prescription : ☼ Carboxymethylcellulose Sodium 1% / Drylief (aris) / Sinafresh liquigel (ibn) / Aquafresh Liquigel (pop) / Tearfresh Liquigel (gen) Eye drop, 1 drop 6 times daily in both eyes for 2 months. ☼ Refer to Ophthalmologist.

treatment.title: Dacryocystitis
treatment.description: Watering from one eye (epiphora of affected side, painless, aged) is chronic dacryocystitis if no other symptoms for exploring D/D. Pain, redness of medial canthus with epiphora is acute dacryocystitis. O/E : ● Vision (VA) – Normal. ● Cornea – Normal. ● Eye – watery. ● Regurgitation of Pus after pressing over the area. ● Sac patency test — Block. Model Prescription : ☼ Moxifloxacin 0.5 % Eye drop / E/D Iventi (sq) / Moxquin (incp) / Optimox (aris) / Odycin (bex) / Eyemox (acm), 1 drop in affected eye 4 times daily for 30 days. ☼ After 1 months, if not improved : Refer to ophthalmologist for syringing or DCR.

treatment.title: Acute Dacrocystitis
treatment.description: Patient with prolong watering in one or both eyes. If watering with signs of acute dacryocystitis / acute on chronic dacryocystitis, treat conservatively with – 1. Cap. Flucloxacillin (500mg), 1 cap 6 hourly for 7-10 days. 2. Tab. Ketorolac (10 mg), 1 tab twice daily for 3-5 days. 3. Cap. Omeprazole (20 mg), 1 cap twice daily for 5 days. 4. Moxifloxacin eye drop, 1 drop 6 hourly for one month. 5. Ciprofloxacin eye ointment, at bedtime for one month. ► If resolved → Refer → DCR with or without intubation after 4-6 wks. Investigations for DCR operation : 1. FBS & 2HABF / RBS 2. Complete Blood Count, BT, CT 3. Serum Creatinine 4. Urine R/E 5. Chest X ray 6. X ray PNS OM view 7. ECG Pre-operative order (CDC) (Date & Time) ¤ Diet : Normal / Full. ¤ Please take informed (written consent). ¤ Please send this patient to OT, when call. Signature & Date Operation Note (Date & Time) Indication : CDC R/S or L/S. Name / operation : DCR R/S or L/S (with or without intubation). Name of Surgeon : Dr. A. Assistant Surgeon : Dr. B. Anesthesia : L/A. Rx on admission or Post-operative order (Date & Time) or Dc̄A ☼ Diet : Normal. ☼ E/D Moxifloxacin, 1 drop in left/right eye 6 hourly – 14 days ☼ Cap. Flucloxacillin 500mg 1+1+1+1 – 7 days ☼ Tab. Ketorolac 10mg 1+0+1 (A/M) – 7 days ☼ Cap. Omeprazole 20mg 1+0+1 (B/M) – 7 days ☼ Tab. Fexofenadine 120mg 0+0+1-7 days ☼ N/D Xylometazoline 0.01%, 2-3 drops 2-3 times daily – 14 days ± ☼ Cap. Tranexamic acid 500mg, 1+0+1 – 5 days উপদেশ : সাত (০৭) দিন পর সুতা কাটিয়ে যাবেন। ডি. সি. আর টিউব (DCR R/S or L/S with intubation) থাকলে আড়াই তিন (০৩) মাস পর ডি. সি. আর টিউব কাটিয়ে যাবেন। কোন সমস্যা হলে চক্ষু বহি:বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন।

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: 1. Marked unilateral axial irreducible proptosis. 2. Restricted and painful ocular motility. 3. Lid oedema, chemosis of conjunctiva. 4. Fever, headache, nausea, vomiting, malaise, prostration. Mx : Refer immediately to an ophthalmologist.

treatment.title: Scleritis & Episcleritis
treatment.description: Episcleritis is a self-limiting condition. It is inflammation of episclera, visible irritation and reddening (red or pink) of episcleral blood vessels. There are two types of episcleritis, nodular and simple : ● Nodular episcleritis lesions have raised surface. ● Diffuse or simple episcleritis lesions are flat. Patient symptoms include redness, mild ocular discomfort or no pain, and normal visual acuity. Model Prescription : ► Topical corticosteroids eye drops given several times per day. ○ Ophthalmic Solution Dexamethasone 0.1% / Dexacort (osl) / Sonexa (aris) / Orbidex (pop) / Dexagen (gen) E/D, 1 drop into the conjunctival sac 6 – 8 times a day 7 – 14 days. ► Topical lubricant eye drops such as artificial tears. ○ Hypromellose 0.5% / Tear (gaco) / Lubric (incp) / Hypro (nipa) / Tearin (bio) E/D, 1 drop into the conjunctival sac 3 – 5 times per day for 7 – 14 days. ► Cold compressions 3 to 4 times per day. ► NSAID + PPI : Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory medications given by mouth are prescribed in more severe cases.

treatment.title: Uveitis
treatment.description: Uveitis (Refer to ophthalmologist) Uveitis (anterior uveitis includes iridocyclitis and iritis), is a rare condition that causes inflammation of uvea, the middle layer of the eye. It's important to get it treated early to avoid long-term complications such as vision loss and blindness. Symptoms can include : ● having a red eye with pain. ● being very sensitive to bright light. ● having blurry vision. ● irregular shape of pupil. ● lacrimation. ● seeing “floaters” (specks or moving clouds in your vision) all of a sudden (haloes around light).

treatment.title: Refractory Error (RE)
treatment.description: Refractory Error (RE) Myopia / Short-sightedness : Unable to see distant (blackboard) objects clearly, impaired visual acuity ± Young age. Dx : Simple Myopia B/E (both eye) [Upto -6D]. Or, Pathological Myopia B/E [more than -6D, between -15 to -20D]. Tx : 1. Spectacles : Concave spherical lens. 2. LASER in-situ keratomileusis (LASIK), Criteria – - Age ≥ 21 years. - Stable RE / Power of spectacles ≥ 2 years. - Absence of other ocular / corneal disorders. - Non-satisfactory result with spectacles.

treatment.title: Refractory error c̄ headache
treatment.description: Refractory error c̄ headache – ● Tab. Flunarizine 5 or 10 mg / Norium (skf) / Fluver (aci) / Minium (ops) / Flurizin (sq) / Fluzin (acm), 0+0+1 for 3–6 months. Or, ● Tab. Amitriptyline 12.5 mg + Chlordiazepoxide 5 mg / Amilin Plus (ops) / Reelife (skf) / Limbix (acm) / Emijoy (bex) / Tryptin Plus (sq), 1 + 1 + 1 for 3 – 6 months

treatment.title: Hypermetropia, Presbyopia & Astigmatism
treatment.description: Hypermetropia / Long-sightedness : Unable to see near (newspaper) objects clearly, impaired visual acuity. Dx : Hypermetropia B/E (both eye). Tx : Spectacles : Convex spherical lens. Presbyopia / চালশে : Old age + Unable to see near (newspaper) objects clearly, impaired visual acuity due to aging process. Tx : Spectacles : Convex spherical lens. Start spherical +1.00D & may increase upto +3.50D, according to patient & ocular examinations. Astigmatism : Unable to see ± near ± distant objects clearly, impaired visual acuity. Unequal refraction of light in different meridians ± abnormal corneal curvature. Tx : 1. Spectacles : Cylindrical lens. 2. Surgery.

treatment.title: Symptomatic Treatment
treatment.description: Symptomatic Treatment (Find out the cause & treat the cause of dry eye) ► Dry eye – Temporary relief of burning and irritation due to dryness of the eye. ☼ E/D Polyethylene Glycol 0.4% + Propylene Glycol 0.3% / Freshtear (skf) / Systear (aris) / Autotear (pop) / PolyTear (unido), Instill 1 drop 4 times daily in the affected eye(s) or as needed. Or, ☼ E/Drop. Hypromellose 0.5% / Lubric (incp) / Eyefresh (osl) / Tear (gaco) / Tearsol (reman), ১ ফোটা দুই চোখে দিনে ২ - ৫ বার ........ প্রয়োজনমত ► Entropion – Inward turning of a single / multiple eyelash causing rubbing & FB sensation. Treatment – Epilation (removal of a single or few ingrown eye lash). ► Itchy, watery, red and swollen eyes and/or eyelids – ☼ Olopatadine 0.2% eye drop / Alacot DS / Patadin DS (pop) / Olpadin DS (aris) / Patalon DS (ibn) E/D, চোখে ১ ফোটা করে দিনে ২ বার --- ১৫ দিন থেকে ৩ মাস ► Watery eye(s) – [Topical antibiotic followed by sac patency test.] ☼ Moxifloxacin 0.5 % Eye drop / Iventi (sq) / Optimox (aris) / Odycin (bex) / Moxibac (pop) E/D, ১ ফোটা আক্রান্ত চোখে দিনে ৪ থেকে ৬ বার ---- ১০ থেকে ২০ দিন

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – ● Pain or discomfort in the throat. ● Difficulty in swallowing. ● ± Fever & cough. ● ± Hoarse or broken voice. ► Tab. Cefuroxime 500 mg + Clavulanic acid 125 mg / Kefuclav (skf) / Furoclav (ren) / Fuclav (drg) 500+125 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ---- ৭ দিন ► Tab. Paracetamol / Fast (acm)/ Napa (bex) / ATP (gen) 500 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ + ১ ---- ৫ দিন (ভরাপেটে, ব্যাথা বা জ্বর থাকলে খাবেন) ► ± Tab. Rabeprazole 20mg / Acifix (bex) / Finix (ops) / Rabeca (sq), ১ + ০ + ১ ---- ৫ দিন ► Povidone Iodine 1 % Mouth wash / Viodin 1% (sq) / Arodin 1% (aris) / Betadine 1% (mundi) / Povisep 1% (jay) Mouth wash, আধা গ্লাস কুসুম গরম পানিতে ২-৩ চামচ (১০-১৫ মিলি) এই মাউথ ওয়াসটি মিশিয়ে দিনে ৩-৪ বার গড়গড়া করবেন ----- ৭ দিন ► Tab. Fexofenadine / Fexo (sq) / Axodin (bex) 120 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ --- ৭ দিন উপদেশ : পর্যাপ্ত পরিমাণ পানি খাবেন। সাতদিনে গলা ব্যাথার উন্নতি না পেলে প্রেশক্রিপশনসহ দেখা করবেন। Referral principle : Refer to ENT specialist / higher centers / hospitals if unexplained sore throat more than 1 month.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – • Fever & malaise. • Pain in the throat aggravated during swallowing. • Discomfort in the throat. • Difficulty in deglutition • H/O recurrent attack of sore throat. • Unpleasant teste and bad smell in the mouth. Oral Cavity / Local examination (O/E) : ○ Enlarged or fibrosed tonsils, ○ Anterior pillar – congested & redness, ○ Inspissated pus in tonsillar crypts, ○ Jugulodigastric lymph nodes (LN) – palpable (enlarged), non-tender. Diagnosis is mainly clinical. Investigations for GA or surgery : • CBC • BT, CT, Blood grouping • HBs Ag, S. Creatinine • Urine R/M/E • Chest X-ray P/A view • X ray Nasopharynx (lateral view) – in children • ECG – if patient is > 40yrs ► Tab. Cefuroxime 500 mg + Clavulanic acid 125 mg/ Tab. Cefotil Plus (sq) / Rofuclav (rad) 500+125 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ---- ৭ দিন ► Tab. Paracetamol / Renova (ops) / Reset (incp) 500 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ + ১ ----- ৫ দিন (ভরাপেটে, ব্যাথা বা জ্বর থাকলে খাবেন) ► ± Tab. Rabeprazole 20 mg / Paricel (aci) / Rabe (aris), ১ + ০ + ১ ----- ৫ দিন ► Povidone Iodine 1 % Mouth wash / Viodin 1% (sq) / Arodin 1% (aris) / Betadine 1% (mundi) / Povisep 1% (jay) Mouth wash, আধা গ্লাস কুসুম গরম পানিতে ২-৩ চামচ (১০-১৫ মিলি) এই মাউথ ওয়াসটি মিশিয়ে দিনে ৩-৪ বার গড়গড়া করবেন ------ ৭ দিন ► Tab. Fexofenadine / Fenadin (ren) / Fexofast (drg) 120 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ----- ৭ দিন উপদেশ : পর্যাপ্ত পরিমাণ পানি খাবেন। সাতদিনে গলা ব্যাথার উন্নতি না পেলে প্রেশক্রিপশনসহ দেখা করবেন। Refer to ENT specialist, if any complication. Surgical Treatment – Tonsillectomy under G/A.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: D/D ● Peri-tonsillar abscess / Quinsy. ● Acute retro-pharyngeal abscess. ● Para-pharyngeal abscess. ● Sun-mandibular abscess / Ludwig’s angina. Δ Neck space abscess Adv. : X-ray Nasopharynx (L/A) view. Tx, ► Incision & drainage is the definite treatment. Then – ¤ Diet : Liquid to solid. ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Dicephin (drg) 1 g, 1 vial IV stat & BD ¤ Inj. Metronidazole 100 ml / Flagyl (syn), 1 bottle IV stat & TDS ¤ Inj. Esomeprazole 40 mg / Nexum (sq), 1 vial IV stat & BD ¤ Inj. Ketorolac / Todol (ops) 30 or 60 mg, 1 amp I/M stat & BD In case of laryngeal oedema, add – ¤ Inj. Hydrocortisone 100 mg / Cotson (ops), 1 vial IV stat & TDS ► Maintenance of airway is of first priority. Intubation or tracheostomy. Keep NPO, if GA is preferred for surgery. ► Combined Ceftriaxone + Metronidazole or, Co-amoxiclav + Metronidazole is preferred in any pus forming condition of neck or head / mouth.

treatment.title: Hoarseness of voice
treatment.description: A. Local cause : 1. URTI (most common). 2. Laryngitis : Hoarseness + malaise + fever and/or pain on using voice. 3. Trauma : shouting, coughing, vomiting, instrumentation. (Treatment is rest) 4. Carcinoma of larynx: The first sign is usually hoarseness, followed by stridor, dysphagia and pain. 5. Carcinoma (Ca) lung : Weight loss + hoarseness persisting > 3wk, particularly smokers > 50y. 6. Hypothyroidism. 7. Acromegaly. B. Neurological problems : 1. Laryngeal nerve palsy 2. MND 3. Myasthenia gravis 4. Multiple sclerosis C. Muscular dystrophy. D. Psychological / functional.

treatment.title: Tracheostomized patient
treatment.description: ► H/O FB inhalation & noisy breathing. ► Perform tracheostomy, if severe breathlessness & O2 saturation rapidly decreases. Requirements – ╠ Rosaline Tracheostomy Tube 7.5 mm (with balloon) – 2 ╠ NaCl 0.9% / Norsol Drop ––– 1 ╠ Inf. Normal Saline & a Dropper ––– 1 ╠ BP blade 15 –––– 2 ╠ Comfit gloves 7” + 6.5” ––– 2 + 2 ╠ 3-0 C/B Silk ––––– 1 ► Order for / Care of a tracheostomized patient – 1. O-P suction sos. 2. Keep a moist gauze piece over tube. 3. Pour 5-7 drops of Norsol / N/S drop hourly. 4. Inflate & deflate balloon for 5 min hourly. 5. Change tube after 72 hours & wash with lukewarm water. Advice in Bengali – (১) রোগীকে একা রাখা যাবে না। (২) রোগী কথা বলতে পারবে না, এতে অস্থির হওয়া যাবে না। অস্থির হলে রোগীরই ক্ষতি হবে। (৩) গলার শ্বাসনল ( Tracheostomy Tube ) এর ভিতর প্রতি ঘন্টা পর পর ৫ - ৭ ফোটা নরমাল স্যালাইন ( Norsol Solution ) দিতে হবে। (৪) শ্বাসনলের সাথে সংযুক্ত বেলুনটির বাতাস প্রতি ঘন্টা পর পর ৫ মিনিটের জন্য বের করে দিতে হবে। ৫ মিনিট পর আবার পূর্বের ন্যায় বেলুনটি ফুলিয়ে দিতে হবে। (৫) এক টুকরো ভেজা গজ দিয়ে সর্বদা শ্বামনলটির মুখ ঢেকে রাখতে হবে। (৬) তিন দিন বা ৭২ ঘন্টা পর শ্বামনলটি পরিবর্তন করতে হবে। রোগীর গলার শ্বাসনল পরিবর্তন হলে সাথে সাথে গরম পানিতে ধুয়ে পরিষ্কার করতে হবে। (৭) শ্বাসনালীর কফ ও শ্লেষ্মা জমে শুকিয়ে গেলে ধীরে ধীরে শ্বাসনলের ভিতর ফাঁকা জায়গা কমে যায়। এতে রোগীর শ্বাসকষ্ট হতে পারে। শ্বাসকষ্ট হলে লক্ষ্য করুন যে শ্বাসনল দিয়ে ঠিকমত বাতাস যাওয়া আসা করছে কিনা। শ্বাসনল দিয়ে ঠিকমত বাতাস যাওয়া আসা না করলে সাকশন মেশিনের চিকন পাইপ দিয়ে শ্বাসনলের ভিতরের ময়লা পরিষ্কার করতে হবে। পরিষ্কার করা সম্ভব না হলে শ্বামনলটি পরিবর্তন করতে হবে। ► IV steroid to prevent laryngeal oedema. ► GA given then post-operative order : - NPO for 6 hours. - IV fluid (according to weight). - IV antibiotic. - IV analgesic / Supp. - IV anti-ulcerant.

treatment.title: OT lists, Indoodr Tx & Discharge
