treatment.description: Investigations : 1. TSH, FT4, FT3. 2. High resolution USG of thyroid gland. 3. Thyroid scan. Routine investigations (for PACU) : 1. CBC 2. Blood grouping and Rh typing 3. RBS 4. S. Creatinine 5. CXR P/A view 6. ECG 7. Urine R/M/E 8. HBs Ag Drug list for thyroidectomy : Vicryl 2-0 R/B – 2 ET tube – 1 Vicryl 1-0 R/B – 2 Inj. Ceftriaxone 500mg/1gm – 1 Prolene 3-0 C/B – 1 Inj. Omeprazole 40mg – 1 Romo Vac – 1 Inj. Ketorolac 30mg/1ml – 1 Plastic pot (medium size) – 1 Inj. Ondansetron 8mg/4ml – 1 40% formalin OT note (Date & Time) N/O surgery : Thyroidectomy Indication: N/O surgeon: N/O assistant: N/O anaesthesia: N/O anesthesiologist : Tx, ¤ NPO for 6 hours then sips of water followed by soft, liquid diet (ice cream). ¤ Inf. H/S 1L, I/V stat @ 20 d/m. ¤ Inf. Ceftriaxone / Cectron (sq) 1 g, 1 vial I/V stat & 12 hourly. ¤ Inf. Ketorolac 30 mg / Zeropain (hc), 1 amp I/M stat & 8 hourly. ¤ Inf. Omeprazole 40 mg / Ometid (ops), 1 vial I/V stat. ¤ Supp. Diclofenac / Clofenac (sq) 50 mg, 1 stick P/R SOS ¤ Please monitor the vital signs. After 6 hours & on discharge : -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- ● Diet : Sips of water then liquid to normal. ● Cap. Cefixime 400mg 1+0+1-total 14 – days. ● Tab. Fexofenadine 120mg 0+0+1- total – 14 days. ● Tab. Ketorolac 10mg 1+1+1 (A/M) – 5 days (If pain present). ● Cap. Omeprazole 20mg 1+0+1 (B/M) – if taking ketorolac. উপদেশ : ১। ৭ দিন পর হিস্টোপ্যাথলজি (মাংস পরীক্ষার) রিপোর্ট সহ দেখা করবেন। ২। ১০ দিন পরে সেলাই কেটে নিবেন। ৩। ১ মাস পর FT4, FT3, S. TSH রিপোর্ট সহ দেখা করবেন। ৪। পরবর্তীতে কোন সমস্যা হলে নাক, কান, গলা বহির্বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: ● Present as inspiratory stridor from 2nd week of life, remits by 6 months to 18 months. ● Suprasternal & intercostal recession present. ● Fast breathing, cough, subcostal recession, added sound in lung are absent. These features develop when pneumonia, asthma supervene, which these infants are prone to. Investigation : ● Chest x-ray ● Laryngoscopy excludes uncommon differentials like collapse, congenital agenesis, eventration, diaphragmatic hernia, choanal atresia, laryngeal web. Treatment : Parental anxiety require reassurance.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : ● Otalgia / Earache / pain in the ear. ● Itching, ± deafness, ± discharge. O/E : Otoscopy : debris seen, erythema, ±furunculosis etc. A GP can perform aural toileting ± suction & removal of visible debris by clean cotton mixed with antifungal ± steroid & Jobson Horne Probe . Again, Jobson Horne Probe can be used to remove few foreign bodies in ear or nose. Jobson Horne Probe, Foreign body hook, Crocodile forceps & Thudichum’s nasal speculum are essential ENT tools for GP chambers. ► Ear Drop Ofloxacin + Clotrimazole + Beclomethasone + Lidocaine (0.3%+1%+0.025%+2%) / E/D Candistin (uni) / Audicare (aris) / Candiflox (ops) / Otoxin (incp), ৩-৪ ফোটা আক্রান্ত কানে (ডান/বাম) দিনে ৩-৪ বার ...... ৭ – ১৪ দিন, নির্দেশনা – কাত হয়ে শুয়ে আক্রান্ত কানে (ডান/বাম) দিয়ে কানে আঙ্গুল দিয়ে হালকা চাপ দিয়ে ২-৫ মিনিট ধরে রাখবেন। ► Cap. Flucloxacillin / Phylopen DS (sq) / Flubex (bex) 500 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ + ১ ….… ৭ দিন ► Tab. Levocetrizine 5 mg / Alcet (sq) / Seasonix (incp), ০ + ০ + ১ ….… ৭ – ১৪ দিন ব্যাথা থাকলে খাবনে, ► Cap. Esomeprazole 20mg / Nexum (sq) / Sergel (hc), ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার ত্রিশ মিনিট পূর্বে) ...... ৫ দিন ► Tab. Diclofenac / Diclofen (ops) / Clofenac (sq) 50 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার পরে) ........ ৫ দিন উপদেশ : ○ কানে পানি ঢুকাবেন না। গোসলের সময় সরিষার বা নারিকেলের তেলে ভিজানো তুলা দিয়ে আক্রান্ত কান বন্ধ রাখবেন। সাঁতার কাটা যাবে না। ○ হাঁচি কাশির সময় মুখ খোলা রাখবেন। ○ দশ দিনে মধ্যে অসুখের উন্নতি না পেলে প্রেশক্রিপশনসহ দেখা করবেন। Another alternate E/D : ► Ear drop Clotrimazole 1% / Clarizol 1%, ৩ ফোটা আক্রান্ত কানে (ডান/বাম) দিনে ৩-৪ বার ........ ৭ দিন, নির্দেশনা – কাত হয়ে শুয়ে আক্রান্ত কানে (ডান/বাম) দিয়ে কানে আঙ্গুল দিয়ে হালকা চাপ দিয়ে ৫ মিনিট ধরে রাখবেন। ± Miconazole 2% + Hydrocortisone 1% / Unigal HC cream, পরিষ্কার তুলা কোনো কাঠি বা পরিষ্কার কটনবাডের সাথে লাগিয়ে আক্রান্ত ডান/বাম কান পরিষ্কার করবেন, দিনে ৩/৪ বার ........ ৩ দিন

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – ● Sneezing, itching, watery nasal discharge. ● Feeling of nasal obstruction. ● May have seasonal variation. ● H/O bronchial asthma (BA) or allergic conjunctivitis. ► Nasal Spray Fluticasone 50 mcg/spray / Lutisone (incp) / Flonaspray (sq) / Flutispray (nav) / Fluvent (aci), ২ চাপ করে উভয় নাসারন্ধে দিনে ২ বার ---- ১ মাস ► Tab. Montelukast / Montene (sq) / Monas (acm) / M-Kast (drg) / Lumona (skf) / Aeron (hc) / Reversair (aci) 10 mg, ০ + ০ + ১ ----- ১ মাস ► Tab. Cetirizine 10 mg / Atrizin (bex) / Alatrol (sq) / Citin (ops), ০ + ০ + ১ ---- ৭ দিন Or, ► Tab. Levocetrizine 5 mg / Alcet (hc) / Netis (ops) / Rinotin (bio), ০ + ০ + ১ ----- ৭ দিন উপদেশ : ○ যেসব জিনিসের সংস্পর্শে বা খেলে হাঁচি কাশি বা নাক দিয়ে পানি পড়া শুরু হয়, সেসব বস্তু থেকে দূরে থাকবেন ও খাওয়া থেকে বিরত থাকবেন। ○ হাঁচি কাশির সময় মুখ খোলা রাখবেন। ○ ….. দিন পর প্রেশক্রিপশনসহ দেখা করবেন। Surgical toileting (incision & drainage) may need if non healing & recurrent. ● Other local steroid may used : ○ Nasal Spray Azelastine + Fluticasone (137 mcg+50 mcg) / Flutispray Plus / Flonasin Nasal Spray, ১ চাপ করে উভয় নাসারন্ধে দিনে ২ বার ---- ১ মাস ● If no response with steroid nasal spray. Then use short term oral / systemic steroid, ○ Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan / Deltasone 20mg, 1 + 0 + 0 (A/M) ------ 7 days

treatment.title: Medical treatment options
treatment.description: Medical treatment options in dentistry : ● Antibiotic (with extended Gram +ve coverage). ● ± Anti-amebic drug. ● Analgesic with PPI. ● Multi-vitamin & multi-minerals (vit-C, vit-B complex). ● Mouthwash. Antibiotics Alternatives – ► Cap. Cefixime / Triocim (bex) / Afix (aris) / Denver (hc) 200 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ----- 5 – 14 days Or, ► Cap. Cephradine / Lebac (sq) / Rocef (hc) / Plycef (ren) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ----- 5 – 14 days Or, ► Cap. Amoxycillin 500 mg + Clavulanic acid 250 mg / Moxaclav (sq) / Tyclav (bex) / Fimoxyclav (syn) / Augment (skf), 1 + 1 + 1 ----- 5 – 14 days Or, ► Cap. Clindamycin / Clindacin (incp) / Clindax (ops) / Climycin (sq) 300 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ---- 5 – 14 days ± Anti-amoebic – ► Tab. Ornidazole / Robic (sq) / Ornid (drg) / Ornil (ops) 500 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ----- 3 – 7 days Or, ► Tab. Metronidazole / Flamyd (incp) / Filmet (bex) 400 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 ---- 3 – 7 days Or, ► Tab. Ciprofloxacin / Neofloxin (bex) / Bactin (ibn) 500 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ----- 5 – 14 days Analgesics with PPIs : PPI : ► Cap. Omeprazole / Esomeprazole / Sergel (hc) 20 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 (30 min B/M) ----- 5 – 10 days NSAIDs : ► Tab. Etoricoxib / Tory (sq) / Rito (ops) 60 mg or 90 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ---- 3 – 5 days [Must avoid in cardiac patients (IHD)] Or, ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg / Pair (drg) / Todol (ops) / Xidolac (bex), 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M – after meal) ------ 3 – 5 days Or, ► Tab. Ibuprofen / Flamex (aci) / Advel (ops) / Profen (acm) 200 mg or 400 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 (A/M – after meal) ----- 3 – 5 days Or, ► Tab. Diclofenac / Diclofen (ops) / Clofenac (sq) / Voltalin Forte (nova) 50 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M – after meal) ------ 3 – 5 days Mouth anti-septic wash preparation : ► Povidone Iodine 1% / Viodin Mouth wash, ১ - ৩ চামচ আধা গ্লাস পানিতে মিশিয়ে গড়গড়া করবেন, দিনে ৩ / ৪ বার ----- ৭ দিন – ১ মাস অথবা, ► 3% H 2 O 2 / Hydrogen Peroxide mouth wash / Jolly Solution, ১ – ৩ চামচ আধা গ্লাস পানিতে মিশিয়ে গড়গড়া করবেন, দিনে ৩ – ৪ বার --------- ১৪ দিন অথবা, ► Listacare blue mint (gen) / Orocare cool mint (sq) / Orocare original (sq) Mouth Wash (250 ml) দিনে ২ – ৩ বার ৩০ সেকেন্ড ধরে কুলকুচি করে ফেলে দিবেন ------ ৭ দিন – ১ মাস (Listacare is Eucalypytol + Menthol + Methyl Salicylate + Thymol. It is indicated in gingivitis, oral hygiene, Bad breath, Teeth cleaner & brighter. Not indicated in pediatrics ward / below 12 years.) অথবা, ► NaCl / Normal saline / Salt mouth wash, ৩ - ৫ চামচ লবণ এক গ্লাস পানিতে মিশিয়ে গড়গড়া করবেন, দিনে ৩ বার ......... ১০ দিন অথবা, ► Mouthwash Chlorhexidine Gluconate 0.2% / Oralon (aci) / Oral-C (uni) Mouthwash, Thoroughly rinse the mouth for about one minute with 5 – 10 ml twice daily ------ 7 – 30 days Multivitamins : ► Tab. Calcium + Vit-D 3 + Vit-C / Cavic-C Plus (incp) / Cora-C (aci) / Algecal C Plus (ops), ১ ট্যাবলেট আধা গ্লাস পানিতে মিশিয়ে ভরাপেটে খাবেন ---- ১০ দিন - ১ মাস Sensitive tooth : ► Fluoride rich toothpaste / Sensodyne Rapid Relief / Mediplus DS Toothpaste, দিনে ও রাতে ১ বার করে এবং মিষ্টিজাতীয় খাদ্য গ্রহনের পর দাঁত মাজবেন। অথবা, ► Sodium Fluoride 0.022% / Listacare whitening plus mouthwash, দিনে ও রাতে ১ বার করে এবং মিষ্টিজাতীয় খাদ্য গ্রহনের পর কুলকুচি করবেন। উপদেশ : দিনে দুই বার (সকালে ও রাতে) দাঁত ব্রাশ করবেন বা মাজবেন । তবে অবশ্যই রাতে ঘুমাতে যাওয়ার আগে ব্রাশ করতেই হবে। খাওয়ার পর সবসময় ভালো করে কুলি করা উচিত্। প্রতি ৩ মাস অন্তর টুথব্রাশ বদলাবেন। দাঁতের ক্ষয় রোধ করতে মিষ্টি, অম্ল জাতীয় খাবার কম খান। ধূমপান, জর্দা, গুল প্রভৃতি থেকে দূরে থাকুন।

treatment.title: Shedding & eruption
treatment.description: Timing of shedding & eruption : Starts teeth eruption from 6 months of age to 24 months. Completes deciduous shedding & teeth eruption 6-7 yrs to 12-13 yrs. Completes eruption of 3 rd & 4 th molar teeth 17-26 yrs.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Visible dirt / caries. 2. ± Pain, ± Cavity seen. Adv. : X-ray IOPA view. Treatment – 1. Healthy oral & dental hygiene. - Brushing twice daily. - Mouth wash gurgle. - Avoid sweets, chocolates & soft drinks. - Brushing after taking them. - Scaling & Polishing. 2. Cavitation confined to dentine = Filling / Restoration (with amalgam, composite, glass ionomer or other suitable filling materials). Medical : No treatment needed, but if pain – NSAID with PPI.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Inflammation of pulp, commonest cause of dental pain. C/C – 1. Radiating toothache. 2. Caries & Cavitation seen. Adv. : X-ray IOPA view. Treatment – 1. Root Canal Treatment (RCT) [Removal of pulp only.] 2. Apicoectomy (non responsive to RCT). 3. Extraction (If gross destruction of tooth). 4. Pulpotomy followed by pulp capping (for deciduous teeth/child). Medical Treatment – 1. One antibiotic especially for Gram positive bacteria. 2. Another antibiotic for anaerobic bacteria /Anti-amoebic. 3. Anti-ulcerant (PPI). 4. Analgesic (NSAID).

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Visit needed for RCT is at least 3 days, even more as per patient’s condition. Observation of the condition is the cause of multiple visits. As the case is infected there is a chance of pus formation, if no complication arise RCT final task may be done in day-3. Day 1 : Step 1 & Step 2. Day 2 : Step 3 & Step 4. Day 3 : Step 5. Pain usually subsides after Day-1 visit, but subsequent two visits are needed for observation, to prevent cystic lesion, crowning to prevent fragility of the teeth & blockage of further toothache. There is another special regiment “Single visit RCT”, requires only one visit.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Soft tissue inflammation around teeth (especially partially erupted tooth, commonly 3 rd molar / wisdom teeth / আক্কেল দাঁত). C/C – 1. Difficulty in mastication. 2. Severe pain. 3. Decrease mouth opening. 4. ± Fever. 5. ± Bad smell & taste sensation. 6. ± Pain in the eye & ear. Adv. : X-ray IOPA view ± X-ray OPG view. Treatment – 1. Conservative medical treatment. 2. Operculectomy / Excision of mucosal flap / Flapectomy. 3. Wisdom teeth extraction (Mal-directed & complicated). 4. Grinding or extraction of the opposing tooth. Medical Treatment – 1. One antibiotic especially for Gram positive bacteria. 2. ± Another antibiotic for anaerobic bacteria / Anti-amoebic. 3. Anti-ulcerant (PPI). 4. Analgesic (NSAID). 5. ± Mouthwash.

treatment.title: Dento-Alveolar Abscess
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Severe radiating toothache. 2. Localized swelling & redness on gingiva. 3. Mobile tooth. Adv. : X-ray IOPA view. Treatment – 1. Incision & drainage. 2. Apicoectomy ± Treatment of pulpitis. Medical Treatment – 1. One antibiotic especially for Gram positive bacteria. 2. ± Another antibiotic for anaerobic bacteria / Anti-amoebic. 3. Anti-ulcerant (PPI). 4. Analgesic (NSAID). 5. ± Mouthwash.

treatment.title: Periapical Abscess
treatment.description: Clinical Features : 1. Severe throbbing pain. 2. Tooth is tender to touch, is extruded, mobile. 3. May be associated with localized or diffuse swelling. Adv. : X-ray IOPA view ± X-ray OPG view. Treatment – 1. Conservative medical treatment same as Pulpitis. 2. RCT, if possible. 3. Teeth extraction (ultimately). 2 / 3 days before any surgical procedure : Start prophylactic antibiotics : Ø Cap. Cephradine / Lebac (sq) / Rocef (rad) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ....... 7 days Ø Tab. Ornidazole / Robic (sq) / Ornid (drg) 500 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ....... 5 days

treatment.title: Lateral Peridontal Abscess
treatment.description: Clinical Features : ¤ Severe throbbing pain. ¤ Tooth is tender to touch, is extruded, mobile. ¤ May be associated with localized or diffuse swelling. ¤ Sometimes associated with bad taste.

treatment.title: Apicoectomy / Root amputation / Root resection
treatment.description: Cut off apical portion of root & curettage of periapical necrotic, granulomatous, inflammatory or cystic lesion.

treatment.title: Dry Socket
treatment.description: Dry Socket One of hazards after extraction / avulsion of tooth. C/C – 1. Increased severe radiating pain after 2/3 days extraction / avulsion. 2. Whitish affected area. 3. Bad smell & taste sensation. 4. ± Pain in the eye & ear. Treatment – Socket debridement under local anesthesia / Inj. Lignocain 2% / Jasocaine. Medical treatment : Mouthwash.

treatment.title: Infected Socket
treatment.description: Infected Socket One of hazards after extraction / avulsion of tooth. C/C – 1. Increased severe radiating pain. 2. Discharge / debris. Treatment – Socket debridement & surgical toileting under Local anesthesia / Inj. Lignocain 2% / Jasocaine. Medical Treatment – 1. One antibiotic especially for Gram positive bacteria. 2. Another antibiotic for anaerobic bacteria / Anti-amoebic. 3. Anti-ulcerant (PPI). 4. Analgesic (NSAID). 5. ± Mouthwash.

treatment.title: Oral / Apthous Ulcers
treatment.description: Oral / Apthous Ulcers See, Gastroenterology portion. Treatment of causes, if associated with – 1. Sharp teeth. 2. Ill fitted denture. 3. Avoid check bite. 4. Avoid brush trauma.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Red swollen gum. 2. Bleeding from gum during brushing. 3. Increased salivation. 4. ± Bad taste & breath. Acute gingivitis presents with enlargements may be localized or generalized, very painful, deep red in color, soft, friable with shiny surface. Chronic type may be localized or generalized, often painless and slowly progressive. Treatment – Incision & drainage : if localized collection of pus. Otherwise conservative / medical treatment. 1. Healthy oral & dental hygiene. - Brushing twice daily. - Mouth wash gurgle. - Avoid sweets, chocolates & soft drinks. - Brushing after taking them. 2. Scaling procedure. Adv. 1. CBC, ESR, Hb%. 2. TC, DC. Exclude clinically – Hematological diseases like leukemia, aplastic anemia, Collagen synthesis diseases like SLE & Viral hemorrhagic fever like dengue, chikungunya. Medical Treatment – 1. One antibiotic especially for Gram positive bacteria. 2. Another antibiotic for anaerobic bacteria / Anti-amoebic. 3. Multi-vitamin & multi-minerals. 4. ± Analgesic (NSAID) & anti-ulcerant (PPI). 5. ± Mouthwash.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Soft tissue inflammation around teeth. 2. Bleeding & discharge. 3. Loosen teeth. Clinical Features : 1. Swollen gums, bleeding from gums either spontaneously or on eating something hard, difficulty in chewing food. 2. Dull pain in the gums, pus discharge from gum on pressing. 3. Loosening of teeth, recession of gums. 4. There is slowly progressive destruction of periodontium, loss of periodontal attachment and presence of periodontal pocket. Treatment : (Refer to a dentist) Medical treatment same as gingivitis. Surgical treatment as per patient’s condition. Advise : Maintain oral hygiene. Brushing twice daily once after breakfast and once after dinner with super soft tooth brush for at least 3 minutes.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Acute Necrotizing Ulcerative Gingivitis (ANUG) / Trench Mouth
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Sudden onset of ulceration on gum. 2. Pain & bleeding from gum. 3. Pseudo-membrane on gum. 4. Non-contagious bacterial infection associated with stress, systemic upset or drug eruption. Treatment – Irrigation with 3% H 2 O 2 & normal saline. Then, conservative / medical treatment. 1. Healthy oral & dental hygiene. - Brushing twice daily. - Mouth wash gurgle. - Avoid sweets, chocolates & soft drinks. - Brushing after taking them. 2. Preserve foil of drugs in future. Adv. : 1. CBC, ESR, Hb%. 2. TC, DC. Exclude clinically – Dermatological emergency – Steven Jonson Syndrome (SJS), Hematological diseases like leukemia, aplastic anemia, Collagen synthesis diseases like SLE & Viral hemorrhagic fever like dengue, chikungunya. Medical Treatment – 1. One antibiotic especially for Gram positive bacteria. 2. Another antibiotic for anaerobic bacteria / Anti-amoebic. 3. Multi-vitamin & multi-minerals (Vit-C, Vit-B complex, calcium). 4. ± Analgesic (NSAID) & anti-ulcerant (PPI). 5. ± Mouthwash.

treatment.title: Features & Reduction
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Sudden stiff TMJ with partial opening of mouth. 2. Decreased mouth opening. Reduction : Place four fingers of both hands on angles of mandible & thumb into oral cavity over molar teeth. Grasp the mandible the gently push downwards, backwards & upwards. Immobilize mandible by a band. Temporo-mandibular Joint (TMJ) Arthralgia / Pain NSAID & PPI. Find out the cause & treat according to it.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Fracture ( ≠ ) / (#) Lower Jaw / Mandible Hospitalization & refer to specialist C/C – 1. H/O trauma. 2. Pain ± bleeding. 3. Difficulty in mouth opening. Adv. : X-ray OPG view ± CT scan of maxillofacial region. Surgical / Definite Treatment – 1. Close : Inter-maxillary fixation (IMF), Arch bar wiring. 2. Open : ORIF / Mini-plate screw fixation. Medical Treatment (Presence of laceration / injury) – 1. One antibiotic especially for Gram positive bacteria. 2. Another antibiotic for anaerobic bacteria / Anti-amoebic. 3. Multi-vitamin & multi-minerals. 4. ± Analgesic (NSAID) & anti-ulcerant (PPI). 5. ± Mouthwash.

treatment.title: Le Fort classification
treatment.description: Le Fort classifications of mid-face fractures Three types (All involve the pterygoid plates.) : -Le Fort I: Low maxillary. -Le Fort II: Pyramidal. -Le Fort III: Craniofacial dysjunction. Rx on admission same as – Fracture of lower jaw / Mandible. Treatment – 4R ’s (Principle of any fractures) – ► Resuscitation, ► Reduction, ► Retention & ► Rehabilitation.

treatment.title: Gum injury & actively bleeding
treatment.description: Gum injury & actively bleeding ↓ Pressure pack by cotton. ↓ Cold / Ice application. ↓ Inj. Tranexamic acid 500mg / Xamic soaked cotton pack pressure. ↓ If pinpoint bleeding – Electrocautery. If actively bleeding – Ligate bleeding vessels with Vicryl 3/0 C/B or, Slik 3/0 C/B or, Catgut 3/0 R/B if deformed mucosa. ↓ Stable patient, then discharge.

treatment.title: Edentulousness / Missing Tooth
treatment.description: C/C – 1. H/O trauma long ago. 2. Cosmetically ugly. 3. Difficulty in chewing & pronunciation. 4. Old age & shedding of teeth. Treatment – 1. Removable orthodontics appliances / Prosthesis : - Complete removable denture, Bridge implant etc. 2. Fixed orthodontics appliances / Prosthesis : - Fixed implants, Braces, etc. ▸ Find out cause & treat it. ▸ Never to discourage orthodontics appliances implants, even in last stage of life, it is needed for psychological support & one can’t recite correct pronunciation of The Holy Book(s), e.g. Al-Quran. ▸ Refer the patient to Prosthodontics Department.

treatment.title: Crowded Teeth / Mal-occlusion
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Congenital. 2. Cosmetically ugly. 3. Difficulty in chewing & pronunciation. Treatment – Single protruded tooth → RCT + Crowning. → Tooth extraction + Bridge implant. → Porcelain / Ceramic Crowning. ▸ Refer the patient to Orthodontics Department.

treatment.title: Spacing / Gap of tooth
treatment.description: Treatment : Brace ± Crown & veneers ± Re-construction surgery according to denture conditions.

treatment.title: Single blackened tooth
treatment.description: Tx options : → RCT + Crowning. → Porcelain / Ceramic Crowning. → Tooth extraction + Bridge implant. ▸ Refer the patient to Orthodontics Department.

treatment.title: Unequal (Front) Tooth
treatment.description: Treatment – Tooth Slicing (Orthodontics Department).

treatment.title: Avulsed Tooth with full root
treatment.description: Clinical Features : History of fall, sports injury, assault or accident. Central incisors and developing teeth frequently avulsed are more. Patient presents with a bleeding clot in the socket and a raw wound. Treatment modalities : May be traumatic or other dental / medical conditions. ↓ Preserve the tooth in saliva / oral cavity, artificial tear / Saline / Hypromellose 0.5% / Lubric Eye Drop / Cow milk / Coconut water. Interim storage is best, place back the tooth in the socket immediately. ↓ Contact to Dentist / Conservative / OMS unit as early as possible, as early as possible. ↓ Re-implant & fixation surgery. Best result is observed if tooth is re implanted within 5-10 minutes. Fixation of implanted tooth with periodontal wiring, arch bar wiring or composite resin; fixation period 6 to 8 weeks; Root canal treatment done after replantation only (to avoid desiccation of periodontal ligament). Medical Treatment – 1. One antibiotic especially for Gram positive bacteria. 2. Another antibiotic for anaerobic bacteria / Anti-amoebic. 3. Multi-vitamin & multi-minerals (Vit-C, Vit-B complex, calcium). 4. ± Analgesic (NSAID) & anti-ulcerant (PPI).

treatment.title: Cracked Tooth Syndrome
treatment.description: Partially avulsed tooth. May be traumatic or assault. Treatment – According to condition. - Porcelain / Ceramic Crowning. - Extraction of root & bridge implant. - RCT & Crowning.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: AWD (Acute Watery Diarrhoea) c̄ Grade of Dehydration
treatment.description: C / C c̄ Duration : - Passage of loose watery stool for ___ hours. - ± Vomiting for ___ hours/days. - ± Fever for ___ hours/days → AGE. O / E : • ***Dehydration (+ / ++ / +++ / -). - Skin pinch : Goes back rapidly / Slowly / Very slowly. - Eye ball : Normal / Sunken / Very sunken. - Tongue : Moist / Dry / Very dry. • Urine output : H/O anuria. • Temperature (+ / ++ / +++ / -). No dehydration : Not enough sign to classify as some or severe dehydration. Some dehydration : Presence of 2 or more of following signs. 1. Restless, irritable. 2. Sunken Eyes. 3. Drinks Eagerly, thirsty. 4. Skin Pinch goes back slowly. Severe dehydration : Presence of 2 or more of following signs. 1. Lethargic/unconscious. 2. Sunken eyes. 3. Unable to drink or drinks poorly. 4. Skin pinch goes back very slowly ( ≥ 2 secs). • Features of hypovolemic shock : - Cold clammy periphery. - Poor perfusion. - Weak thread pulse. - Tachycardia. - Tachypnea / apnea. • Lung / Heart : NAD. • Respiratory rate (R/R) : ____breaths / min (b/m). • Heart rate (H/R) : ____ beats / min (b/m). Δ AWD c̄ No sign of Dehydration : May treat as OPD patient [Please see pediatrics in “GP diary”.] ☼ Diet : Full / EBF (if ≤ 6 months). ☼ ORS or OSR-N, _____ TSF / purgation or motion. ¤ 10 TSF, if ≥ 1 years. ¤ 20 TSF, if ≥ 2 years. ¤ 30 TSF, if ≥ 3 years. ¤ 40 TSF, if ≥ 4 years. ¤ If ≥ 5 years, as much as child can intake. [Here, approximately Age×10 TSF ORS-N.] ± ☼ Syp. Zinc 10mg/5ml / Xinc (skf) / Ziton (drg) / Nid (ops) / Pep (ori) / Zesup (sq) / Zedex (bex) [avoid if vomiting present], ¤ If ≤ 6 months, ½ TSF BD for 14 days. ¤ If ≥ 6 months, 1 TSF BD for 14 days. ± ☼ Probiotics : Mini bottle : Enterogermina (syn) / Oral powder or Sachet : Acteria (rad) / Probio (sq) / Proteria KIDS (ops). [Mixed with 2 TSF of water, milk or juice orally, once daily for 5-7 days]. ¤ If ≤ 2 years, 1+0+0 for 5 days. ¤ If ≥ 2 years, 1+0+1 for 5 days. ► If the stool contains blood associated with abdominal pain & tenesmus. The diagnosis will be Δ Dysentery, then add antibiotics like – ☼ If dysentery : Syp. Nitazoxanide100 mg/5 ml / Zox (sq) / Nitazox (incp) / Toza (skf), ¤ If ≤ 3 years, ½ +0+ ½ (TSF) for 3 days. ¤ If ≥ 3 years, 1+0+1 (TSF) for 3 days. Or, Syp. Azithromycin 200 mg/5ml / Zimax (sq) / Zithrin (ren) / Acos (rad) / Romycin (ibn) / Azimex (drg), [wt/20] TSF OD for 3 – 5 days. Or, Syp. Ciprofloxacin 250 mg/5 ml / Ciprocin (sq) / Neofloxin (bex) / Cipro-A (acm), [wt/25] TSF BD for 5 – 7 days. ► Treatment plan of AWD : 1. Diet : Normal / Continue EBF. 2. Fluid – according to gradation of dehydration. 3 . Zinc (Syrp/Tab) supplement (enhance re-epithelialization). Must remember – 1. No antibiotics (except dysentery). 2. No anti-emetics (Ondansetron, Prochlorperazine, etc.) 3. No anti-spasmotics (Tiemonium, Hyoscine etc.) 4. No formula feeding. But, we can use the above drugs according to our clinical experiences. But must not use Domperidone or PPIs like Omeprazole in diarrhoea, as it increases gut motility & increases frequency of motions. ► Mineral water bottle 500 ml ( ½ L) must be given to buy for dissolving 1 packet of ORS or ORS-N in exact 500 ml water. If mixed with less amount of water then it will be hypertonic & cause more dehydration. 1 Glass = 200 to 250 ml. So 2 to 2.5 glasses of water may be needed to make 500 ml. So must give 500 ml mineral drinking water bottle to buy for accurate measurement purpose. ► ORS-N is better for rotaviral AWD or otherwise ORS. ► Diet should be normal & full (EBF if age ► In AWD vomiting occurs due to electrolytes imbalanced so no anti emetic drugs needed, only fluid replacement is sufficient. ► Syrup or Tablet Zinc Sulphate Monohydrate should not be given if vomiting or bloating & it should be given after meal. Zinc causes gastric irritation. ► Probiotics help in growth of normal flora & absorption. It may be avoided. It is not practiced by many seniors. Commonly child diarrhoea is Rota viral, so antibiotics are not of use. ► In child fluid turnover is more about 18% & it is tough to feed the baby ORS-N. So, we may use IV fluid (cholera saline) 50ml/Kg or less in AWD even if no sign of dehydration. IV cannula & fluid infusion will be tough if hypovolemic shock is established & veins are collapsed. But according to IMCI IV fluid in no sign of dehydration is not indicated. It is impossible to cool down near relatives of a hospitalized child with AWD without any infusion. Along with this we can infuse 50ml/Kg Inf. C/S in – 1. High purging rate. 2. Oral ulceration. 3. Lactose intolerance. 4. Severe vomiting. 5. No or little oral intake. ► Check urine output, if absent for 24 hours then do S. Creatinine & S. Electrolytes. Manage them accordingly. ► Indication of antibiotic : 1. Suspected cholera 2. Febrile patient 3. Throat infection ► If diarrhea persists more than 2 week : Persistent diarrhea. ¤ Advice : Stool RE and C/S. ¤ Rehydration according hydration status. ¤ Syrup. Nitazoxanide 100mg/5ml / Zox (sq) / Nitazox (incp) • • >3 years : 2 TSF BD – 3 days ► Choice of antibiotic : Shigella: ¤ Syrup. Levofloxacin (125mg/5ml) / Levoxin (incp) / Evo (bex) / Levox (ops), [wt/12] TSF BD or 1 ml for every 5 kg Stat and BD for 5 days. • Children • Children >5 years: 10 mg/kg every 24 hours. E. Coli : EPEC, ETEC, EIPC : ¤ Susp. Azithromycin 200mg/5ml 1TSF for every 20 kg OD – 5 days. E. histolytica, C. difficile, Giardia : Metronidazole 20 – 40mg/kg/day TDS. ¤ Susp. Metronidazole 200mg/5ml, [wt/20] ml TDS or 1ml for every 5 kg stat and TDS for 5 days. ► If perianal excoriation present : Indicates lactose intolerance. ¤ Cream. (Clotrimazole 1% +Hydrocortisone 1%) / Neosten HC (bex) / Clotison (bio) / H-Trimazole (ops), Apply locally BD / TDS –– 5-10 days ¤ Give lactose free milk : Lactase 3333.33 IU/ml Pediatric Drops / Pediatric Drops Ezymilk (incp) / Chewable Tab Laczym (aris), ২-৩ চামচ বুকের দুধের সাথে ৪ ফোটা এই ওষুধটি মিশিয়ে খাওয়াবেন, প্রতিবার বুকের দুধ খাওয়ানো শুরু করা আগে খাওয়াবেন। --- ৫ থেকে ৭ দিন [Please see “GP Diary” for rotaviral diarrhoea”.]

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.disease_name: AWD (Acute Watery Diarrhoea) c̄ Grade of Dehydration
treatment.description: AWD c̄ Some Sign of Dehydration Δ AWD c̄ SSD ☼ Diet : Full / EBF. ☼ ORS [or, ORS-N] ¤ ¤ > 2 years: 20-40 TSF after each purging. ¤ > 5 years: 1-2 glass/cup/as required. ☼ Inf. C/S [50 × wt] to [75 × wt] ml, I/V stat @ [total amount in ml / 100] d/m. ☼ Syp. Zinc / Xinc (skf) / Ziton (drg) / Nid (ops), [wt/10] TSF stat & BD (A/M) for 14 days [avoid if vomiting presents] ¤ ½ TSF stat and BD ¤ > 6 months: 1 TSF stat and BD ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone (1000 mg/10 ml) or (500mg/5ml)/ Ceftron (sq) / Oricef (hc), [wt] ml I/V stat & OD (once daily) or, [wt/2] ml IV stat & BD. ± If fever present : ☼ Syrp. Paracetamol (P/C) 120 mg/5ml / Napa (bex) / Renova (ops) (15mg/kg/dose QDS), 1 TSF for each 8 kg body weight stat and QDS Or, ± ☼ Probiotics / Enterogermina (syn) Mini Bottle / TS6 Probiotic Powder / 3-nine sachet / Acteria (rad) / Probio (sq) / Proteria KIDS (ops) ¤ If ≤ 2 years, 1+0+0 for 5 days. ¤ If ≥ 2 years, 1+0+1 for 5 days. Powder : mixed with 2 TSF of water, milk or juice orally, once daily for 5 – 7 days. Discussion : Reassess hydration status after every 4 hours and treat according to hydration status. Note : Drug list given for one day : If Weight less than 10 kg, ╬ IV cannula 24 G -- (2) ╬ JMS Micro-burette --- (1) ╬ JMS Micropore 1" --- (1) ╬ Inf. C/S 500ml (if not available in ward) --- (2) ╬ Mineral water bottle 500 ml --- (1) ╬ Syp. Xinc (skf) / Zesup (sq) / Pep (ori) -- (1) ╬ Acteria (rad) / Probio (sq) / Proteria KIDS -- (1) If Weight more than 10 kg, ╬ IV cannula 22 G ---- (2) ╬ JMS Micropore 1" ---- (1) ╬ Inf. C/S 500ml (if not available in ward) --- (2) ╬ Mineral water bottle 500 ml --- (1) ╬ Syp. Nid (ops) / Ziton (drg) / Zedex (bex) --- (1) ╬ Powder Acteria (rad) / Proteria KIDS (ops) -- (1) I/V cannula are usually supplied in the ward. If, Weight ≥ 10 kg then 22 G (blue). 20 G (Pink) & 18 G (Green) are for adults. Always give 2 to 3 amount of IV cannula to buy, never a single piece as it may be miss prick. Micropore is a must to fix the cannula & is not supplied to the ward. Avoid microburette set for Inf. C/S in rapid correction.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.disease_name: AWD (Acute Watery Diarrhoea) c̄ Grade of Dehydration
treatment.description: ► In AWD c̄ Some Dehydration there is no indication of I/V fluids. Ideally 75 ml/Kg ORS – N should be orally given to take within 4 hours & follow up. But babies with some dehydration may deteriorate suddenly to sever dehydration & hypovolumic shock. Again it is a tough task to follow up the case in the over crowed pediatrics ward. So it will be safe if we use 75ml/kg Inf. Cholera saline (C/S) for 5-6 hours. The amount & rate of infusion is not strictly maintained. ► Correct fluid for dehydration & hypovolumia : 1. Cholera saline if diarrhoea / increase frequency of defecation. 2. Normal saline if vomiting. 3. Hartmann saline if visible / concealed bleeding (blood transfusion is the best or starch (plasmasol) may also be used). ► Alternative anti-biotic : Or, Inj. Ciprofloxacin (200mg/100ml) / Cipro-A (acm) / Neofloxin (bex) / Ciprox (ops) / Bactin (ibn) / Civox (pop) / Beuflox (incp), [5 × wt] ml IV stat & BD Or, Susp. Azithromycin (200mg/5ml) / Zimax (sq) / Zithrin (ren) / Acos (rad)/ Azimex (drg), [wt/20] TSF & OD (10 mg/Kg) for 5 days. ► Alternative anti-amoebic : ¤ Syrup. Metronidazole (200 mg/5 ml) / Filmet (bex) / Amodis (sq) / Metryl (ops) / Flamyd (incp) / Flagyl (syn), [wt/25] TSF TDS for 5 days. Or, Syrup. Nitazoxanide 100mg/5ml / Zox (sq) / Nitazox (incp) / Alinix (hc) / Nitasol (ibn) / Nitoxin (aris) / Toza (skf) ¤ ¤ > 3 years : 2 TSF BD –– 3 days ► Anti-spasmodics : (any one) ☼ Inj. Tiemonium 5mg/2ml / Algin (ren) / Norvis (sq) / Visonium (drg) / Visceralgine (nuv), [0.05 × wt] ml I/V stat & TDS Or, ☼ Inj. Hyoscine Butyl-Bromide 20mg/1ml / Butapan (syn), ½ amp ( ≥ 6 yrs) I/V stat & TDS. Or, ☼ Oral Syp. Tiemonium (10 mg/5 ml) / Algin (ren) / Visral (ops) / Viset (hc) / Onium (ori), [wt/5] ml TSF TDS for 3 – 5 days. ► Never use NSAIDs to relieve pain. Use anti-spasmotic (Hyosmide or Tiemonium methyl sulphate) or centrally acting analgesics (Tramadol, Nalbuphine). ► Anti-ulcerants are used in older child. H2 blockers (Famotidine) are better (Omeprazole) than PPI in pediatrics.

treatment.title: Δ AWD c̄ Severe Dehydration
treatment.disease_name: AWD (Acute Watery Diarrhoea) c̄ Grade of Dehydration
treatment.description: Fluid : ☼ Inf. C/S [100 × wt] ml, I/V stat @ [total amount in ml / 100] d/m. Give ⅓ portion of total fluid running and reassess hydration status then reassess after every 4 hours and treat (Inf. C/S) according to hydration status. AVOID MICROBURETTE SET. ► Probiotics oral powder : Acteria (rad) / Probio (sq) / Proteria KIDS (ops) / 3-nine sachet / TS6 probiotic + powder / Lactobacillus / Enterogermina mini bottle : should mixed with 1/2 cup of water, milk or juice orally, once daily for 5-7 days. But in younger baby it can be mixed with ¾ – 2 TSF water / milk to reduce the amount of fluid consumption.

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.disease_name: AWD (Acute Watery Diarrhoea) c̄ Grade of Dehydration
treatment.description: Treatment on discharge : Δ AWD c̄ No / Some / Severe Dehydration. (After 1 to 2 days of admission when adequate resuscitation & hydration. Must after decrease frequency of loose motion.) -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- ● খাবার স্যালাইন (ORS or ORS-N) : এক প্যাকেট খাবার স্যালাইন (ORS) ৫০০ মিলি পানির সাথে মিশিয়ে খাওয়াবেন। ২ বৎসরের নিচের বাচ্চাদের প্রতিবার পাতলা পায়খানার পর ১০-২০ চামচ স্যালাইন ধীরে ধীরে সময় নিয়ে খাওয়াবেন। আর ২ বৎসরের উপরের বাচ্চাদের ২০-৪০ চামচ। ± ● Syrup. Nitazoxanide 100mg/5ml / Zox (sq) / Nitazox (incp) / Alinix (hc) / Nitasol (ibn) / Nitoxin (aris) / Toza (skf), ¤ ¤ > 3 years : 2 TSF BD –– 3 days Or, Susp. Azithromycin (200mg/5ml) / Zimax (sq) / Zithrin (ren) / Acos (rad)/ Azimex (drg), [wt/20] TSF & once daily (10 mg/Kg) ± ● Probiotic : Acteria (rad) / Probio (sq) / Proteria KIDS (ops) : ১ প্যাকেট পাউডার হাফ কাপ পানি বা দুধ বা জুসের সাথে মিশিয়ে খাওয়াবেন। ২ বৎসরের কম হলে দিনে একবার। ২ বৎসরের উপরের হলে দিনে দুইবার। ...... ৫ – ৭ দিন ● Syp. Zinc (10 mg/5 ml) / Xinc (skf) / Zesup (sq) / Nid (ops) / Ziton (drg) / Zis-DS (acm) / Pep (ori) / Welzinc (hc), [wt/10] চামচ × দিনে ২ বার ..... ১৪ দিন Or, Use ondansetron if vomiting present & avoid zinc : ● Syp. Ondansetron (4 mg/5 ml) / Emistat (hc) / Zofra (skf) / Anset (ops) / Ofran (sq) / Emiset (alco) / Periset (acm) / Ondason (drg) / Emiston (ibn), [wt/25] চামচ × দিনে ৩ বার ..... ৩ – ৫ দিন উপদেশ (ডায়রিয়ায় সকলের জন্য প্রযোজ্য) : ১। ২৪ ঘন্টায় বাচ্চা ৬ বারের কম প্রসাব করলে, চিকিৎসকে জানাবেন। বড়দের তুলনায় বাচ্চাদের খুব সহজেই পানিশূন্যতা (ডিহাইড্রেশন) দেখা যায়। তাই নেতিয়ে পড়লে পড়লে বা অস্বাভাবিক মনে হলেই, অবশ্যই অতিসত্ত্বর নিকটস্থ হাসপাতালে শিশুকে ভর্তি করাবেন। ২। খাবার স্যালাইন অবশ্যই আধা লিটার (৫০০ মিলি) পানিতে এক প্যাকেট মিশিয়ে শিশুকে খাওয়াতে হবে। প্যাকেটে উল্লিখত পরিমাণের থেকে কম বা বেশি পানিতে খাবার স্যালাইন মিশিয়ে শিশুকে খাওয়ালে, তা শিশুর পানিশূন্যতা আরো বাড়িয়ে দেয়। তাই অবশ্যই আধা লিটার পানিতে এক প্যাকেট স্যালাইন মিশিয়ে শিশুকে খাওয়াতে হবে। তবে রাইস স্যালাইন / Rice Saline প্যাকেটে উল্লিখিত পরিমাণ ২৫০ বা ৫০০ মিলির সাথে মিশানো যায়। স্যালাইন গুলানোর আট (০৮) ঘন্টা পর সে স্যালাইন আর খাওয়ানো উচিত না। ৩। ঘন ঘন খাবার স্যালাইন খাওয়াবেন। কাঁশি হলেও খাবার স্যালাইন খাওয়ানো বন্ধ করা যাবে না। গরম পানিতে স্যালাইন গুলানো উচিত না। ৪। ভাতের মাড়, কাঁচা কলা ভর্তা, চিড়া, দই, ডাবের পানি, আনার বা বেদানার রস ডায়রিয়ায় উপকারী পানীয় বা খাবার। ডাবের পানি খাবার স্যালাইনের গুণসম্পন্ন। ৫। স্বাভাবিক খাবার ও বুকের দুধ চলবে। কোনো অবস্থাতেই বন্ধ রাখা যাবে না। তবে বাইরের ভাজাপোড়া, চিপস, চকলেট, শাকসবজি, অতিরিক্ত কার্বোহাইড্রেট/শর্করা খাবার থেকে বিরত রাখা উচিত। ► Note : Domperidone, PPIs, Multivitamins are contraindicated in AWD / Diarrhoea. But in case of abdominal distension or discomfort we can use – ᴏ Tab. Antacid (chewable), ½ +0+ ½ (3 days) . ► Convulsion develops in a patient with AWD : If there is convulsion in a patient of AWD then think of following and manage accordingly : 1. Electrolyte imbalance (so perform S. electrolytes urgently) 2. Hypoglycaemia : Corrected by 10% glucose [2ml/kg} IV stat slowly (give blindly). 3. Meningo-encephalitis : By Shigella (Shigella encephalopathy due to Shigella toxin).

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Upper abdominal pain. 2. Fever ± Vomiting. 3. Acute watery diarrhea (AWD). 4. H/O Haematemasis ± Malena. O/E – 1. Abdomen – soft but tender. 2. Duodenal point tenderness present. 3. longer history & related to meal. Treatment same as AWD. ► NPO is must in surgical acute abdomen before proper diagnosis. If kept NPO, infusion fluid according to age & weight must be given. But never DA. Because Dextrose in aqua (DA) is hypotonic when metabolized. Hartmann solution may be used but H/S is rarely used in medicine / Pedi-medicine ward.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Recurrent upper abdominal pain / poorly localized abdominal pain / nocturnal pain waking the child having relation with food and relieved by taking antacid. Feeding difficulty, crying episode, hematemesis, melena. Triple therapy : Antibiotic (Any two of the followings) : ● Syrup. Amoxicillin (125mg/5ml), 1 TSF for every 5 kg BD. [50mg/kg/day BD – 14 days] ● Syrup. Clarithromycin (125mg/5ml), 1 TSF every 16 kg BD. [15mg/kg/day BD – 14 days] ● Syrup. Metronidazole (200mg/5ml), 1 TSF for every 20 kg TDS. [20mg/kg/day BD or TDS – 14 days] Anti-ulcerant (Any one of the followings) : ● Tab. Rabeprazole 10 or 20 mg, 1 mg/kg/day BD – 1 to 2 months. ● Other PPI 1 mg/kg/day BD. ± ● Syrup. Sucralfate (1g/5ml), [40-80 mg/kg/day BD-14 days] Please see GP DIARY for more diseases of pediatrics.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Spondylitis is a group of inflammatory conditions that affect the joints in spine. Spondylosis isn't due to inflammation but rather the wear and tear of spine. Refers to the degenerative changes found on the lumbar spine. It describes the overgrowth (osteophytes) found mostly on the anterior and lateral edges of the superior and inferior end of the vertebral. ● Age usually over 40 years. ● Present with complaint of low back pain with no known precipitating event. X-ray L/S spine : - Show bone spurs on vertebral bodies in the spine, - Thickening of facet joints, - Narrowing of the intervertebral disc spaces. Spondylolisthesis : Spondylolisthesis is a forward translation of a vertebral body with respect to the vertebra below. Spondylolisthesis occurs most commonly in the lower lumbar spine. ● Usually occurs in children and older adults ● Achy low back pain (LBP). ● The onset of pain is usually insidious but may be related to a specific traumatic episode. ● The pain is usually worse with spinal extension and relieved by rest. ● Spondylolisthesis may be present, yet asymptomatic. Cold / Heat Compression : If any injury or immediate after event cold or ice massage for first 48 hours or 2 days. Then Moist Heat compression. Moist Heat compression for chronic pain : wet a towel in hot water and put a hot pack on area of pain / the waist, 10 minutes do 3 hours.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: It caused by pressure on the median nerve in the carpal tunnel of the wrist. Half of all cases of CTS occur in patients 40-60 years old, predominantly in females (70%). Symptom : ● Tingling and numbness, usually the thumb, index, middle and ring (radial three) fingers are affected, but not the little finger. May feel like an electric shock & wake the patient from sleep. ● The sensation also can travel from the wrist up the arm. ● Many people "shake out" their hands to try to relieve their symptoms. The numb feeling may become constant over time. ● Weakness of the thumb's pinching muscles, which also are controlled by the median nerve. Clinical tests : ● Bending the wrist, tapping on the nerve or simply pressing on the nerve can trigger symptoms in many people. ● Nerve conduction study (NCS). Treatment : ● Wrist splinting. ● Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs). ● Corticosteroids. ● Surgery (if medical treatment failed). Advice : ● Take short, frequent breaks from repetitive activities involving the use of the hands. ● Lose weight if you are overweight or obese. ● Rotate the wrists and stretch the palms and fingers. ● Wear a snug, not tight, wrist splint at night. ● Do not sleep on the hands.

Normal 0 false false false EN-US X-NONE X-NONE Patient (usually male young adult, 20 – 40 years of age), complaint of LBP (Low back pain) for more than 3 months improved by exercise and not relieved by rest, more marked after taking rest, significant morning stiffness, peripheral large joint (knee, ankle and elbow) may also be involved. Musculo-skeletal (MSK) examination : 1. Restricted spine movement (mainly lumber). 2. Obliteration lumber lordosis. 3. Schober test. 4. Chest expansion reduced. Investigations : 1. X-ray of pelvis (A/P) reveal bilateral sacroilitis. ► X-rays of the sacroiliac joint : ○ Irregularity and loss of cortical margins. ○ Widening of the joint space. ○ Subsequently sclerosis, joint space narrowing and fusion. ► X-rays of Lateral thoracolumbar spine : ○ Anterior 'squaring' of vertebrae. ○ Bridging syndesmophytes. ○ Ossification of the anterior longitudinal ligament and facet joint fusion. 3. HLA-B-27 : Positive in more than 90% patients. 4. MRI : Sacroiliitis. 5. CBC with ESR and CRP : raise. 6. Autoantibodies : Such as RF, ACPA and ANA, are negative.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Pharmacological : 1. NSAID – once daily or slow release taken at bedtime. 2. DMARD – for severe and / or persistent peripheral musculo-skeletal features of peripheral spondyloarthritis (SpA), both sulfasalazine and methotrexate are reasonable therapy choices. 3. Anti TNF therapy for disease inadequately controlled with above measure. 4. Oral corticosteroid – in acute uveitis. 5. Local glucocorticoid injections can be useful for persistent plantar fasciitis, other enthesopathies and peripheral arthritis. Drug treatment : ► Tab. Indomethacin 75 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) ---- 1 month ► Cap. Esomeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ---- 1 month ► If acute uveitis present : Tab. Prednisolone 10 mg, 2+0+0 (A/M). ► If persistent planter fasciitis, other enthesitis or peripheral arthritis : Inj. Triamcinolone 40mg/ml, 1 vial intra-articular stat. If severe and/or peripheral musculoskeletal features : ► Tab. Methotrexate 10 mg, 1 ½ tab every Friday. ► Tab. Folic acid 5 mg, 1 tab every Saturday. Or, ► Tab. Sulfasalazine 500 mg, 0+0+1 ---- 7 days, Then, 1+0+1 ---- 7 days, Then, 1+0+2 ----- 7 days, Then, 2+0+2 ----- continue ► Who are inadequately controlled on standard therapy : Anti TNF or anti-IL 17A therapy. Non-pharmacological : 1. Explanation, Education & Physiotherapy. 2. Daily back extension exercise including – a morning ‘warm up’ routine. 3. Avoid prolonged period of inactivity work like – driving, computer work, avoidance of poor posture. 4. Regular physical exercise with daily back extension exercise. Swimming is the best exercise. উপদেশ : ১ । একটানা বেশিক্ষণ চেয়ারে বসে থাকবেন না, মাঝে মাঝে সাঁতার কাটবেন । ২ । মাঝে মাঝে বিছানাতে উপুড় হয়ে শুয়ে থাকবেন । ৩ । পিঁড়া, টুল ইত্যাদিতে (কোমড় ভাঁজ করে) বসবেন না । ৪ । নিচে বসে মাছ, তরকারী, কাপড় ধোয়া যাবে না । ভারি জিনিস তুলবেন না । ৫ । উঁচু-নিচু বিছানায় শুবেন না । আঁকা বাঁকা হয়ে ঘুমাবেন না । ৬ । পায়খানায় উঁচু কমোড ব্যবহার করবেন । ৭ । খুব বেশী ব্যাথার ঔষুধ খাবেন না । ব্যাথার ঔষুধ বেশী খেলে কিডনী/হৃৎপিন্ডে সমস্যা হতে পারে । ৮ । Cortan/Deltasone / Decason জাতীয় ঔষুধ কখনোই চিকিৎসকের পরামর্শ ছাড়া খাবেন না । ৯ । নিচের ভ্যাকসিনগুলি দিয়ে নেবেন- Influenza vaccine, Pneumococcal Vaccine. Hepatitis B vaccine. In case of sulfasalazine : Follow up after 1 month with CBC and SGPT report. Surgery : Severe hip, knee or shoulder arthritis with secondary OA may require arthroplasty. Spinal osteotomy to correct stoop and make eyeline / posture ‘more normal’, can make a significant difference to patients with severe ankylosed kyphotic spines. Poor prognostic factors : 1. ESR > 30 mm in 1st hour. 2. Onset 3. Early hip involvement. 4. Poor response to NSAIDs.

treatment.title: Types & Features
treatment.description: Spondyloarthritis (SpA) Axial spondylarthritis Ankylosing spondyloarthritis Imaging : Sacroilitis on MRI only Bilateral / Unilateral sacroiliitis on x-ray History : Back pain > 3 months that has four of the following characteristics : 1. Improved by exercise. 2. Not relieved to by rest. 3. Insidious onset. 4. Night pain. 5. Age of onset Low back pain > 3 months improved by exercise and not relived by rest. Good response of back pain to NSAID. Family history of Spondyloarthritis. Limitation of lumbar spine movement in sagittal and frontal planes. History of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Clinical examination : Arthritis Enthesitis Dactylitis Psoriasis Chest expansion reduced Refer – as early as possible to rheumatologist or physiatrist. D/D : ╬ Abrupt severe LBP, radiating to lower limb with no morning stiffness & no systemic features → Inv : MRI of L/S spine with screening of whole spine. → Dx : PLID. ╬ Chronic LBP with morning stiffness > ½ hour, improves with exercise, aggravates by taking rest → Spondyloerthritis. Investigation : CBC, CRP, S. Creatinine, SGPT, HLA 27, X-ray pelvis AP ferguson view. Features : ● LBP (more at late night, early morning stiffness improved by activity, pain not improved by taking rest, good response to NSAIDs). ● Asymmetric oligoarthritis. (predominantly large joints e.g. knee/shoulder/ankle). ● H/O painful red eye, swelling of a whole digit. ● O/E : Swollen & tender. elbows, knees, ankle.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment : ±► Tab. Sulfasalazine 500 mg / Salazine (ops) / Reumazin (aris), 0+0+1 – 7 days, then, 1+0+1 – 7 days, then, 1+0+2 – 7 days, then, 2+0+2 – continue (Discontinue the medicine if any rash or itching occurs on the body). Analgesics : ► Cap. Indomethacin / Indomet (ops) / Reumacap (aris) SR 75 mg, Or, Tab. Naproxen / Naprox 500 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) ---- 14 days ► Cap. Esomeprazole / Maxpro 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ----- 2 months ± ► Tab. Baclofen / Flexibac (bacn) 5 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) --- 2 months Advice : • F/U after 1 month with – CBC, CRP, S. Creatinine, S. ALT. • Get the following vaccinations (before starting the medication) : ○ Inj. Prevenar-13 — 1 ampoule once in a lifetime. ○ Inj. Influvax Tetra — 1 ampoule administered once a year. ○ Inj. Hepavax-B — 1 ampoule in 0, 1 st and 6 th month. • Exercise regularly. See a rheumatologist / physical medicine specialist / physiotherapist regularly. • Aerobic exercise. • Stretching exercises. • Muscle strengthening : ○ Isometric Back Strengthening. ○ Trunk strengthening & coordination exercise.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Anlylosing Spondilitis (AS) (Refer this patient to internist/rheumatologist) Patient (usually male young adult, 20 – 40 years of age), complaint of LBP (Low back pain) for more than 3 months improved by exercise and not relieved by rest, more marked after taking rest, significant morning stiffness, peripheral large joint (knee, ankle and elbow) may also be involved. treatment.title: Cause
treatment.description: Monoarthritis is joint pain or inflammation that affects one joint. It may be acute or chronic. Acute arthritis usually lasts from hours to two weeks. Chronic arthritis is an inflammation of over two weeks. Chronic monoarthritis : ○ Osteoarthritis (OA). ○ TB arthritis. ○ Hemophilic arthritis. ○ Monoarticular presentation of oligoarthritis. Acute monoarthritis : ○ Septic arthritis. ○ Reactive arthritis. ○ Gout. ○ Pseudogout. ○ Trauma. ○ Hemarthrosis –Trauma, coagulation disorder. ○ Monoarticular presentation of oligoarthritis.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Clinically diagnosis is 100% accurate, if all the features : ● Patients are usually over weight and old aged (especially female). ● Pain in knee (especially right knee) / hip / neck / DIP / PIP joint. ● Insidious onset (long history with Intermittent nature). ● Pain mainly related to movement and weight bearing. ● Pain relieve after taking rest. ● Morning stiffness for less than 15 minutes. Core symptoms : ○ Pain (mild to moderate) in both knee joints for 6 months. ○ Mechanical in type, relieved by rest. ○ Morning stiffness for 10 – 20 minutes. Investigation – ○ X-ray of the affected joint-features of osteoarthrosis. ○ X-ray of L/S-bilateral osteophytes may be present in vertebrae. ○ ESR, CRP – normal (no inflammation). On joint examination : a. Bony swelling around the joint margin. b. Deformity, usually without instability. c. Muscle weakness and wasting. d. Mild or absent synovitis. e. Joint line or peri-articular tenderness. f. Crepitation. g. Restricted movement. X ray of involved joint : a. Joint space narrowing. b. Osteophyte. c. Joint deformity. d. Subchondral sclerosis. e. Subchondral cyst.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Management : 1. Full explanation of the condition e.g., Established structured changes are permanent & Pain & function can be improved. “OA is not a curable disease of old aged, but lifestyle & drugs are helpful to prevent joint movement & pain.” 2. Exercise – strengthening & aerobic exercise. 3. Hot or cold compression. 4. Reduction of adverse mechanical factors e.g., - Weight loss if obese or overweight. - Shock absorbing foot wear. - Use of walking stick for painful knee or hip OA. 5. Drug : - Paracetamol - Topical NSAID - Oral NSAID 6. In knee and 1 st CMC joint : Intra-articular injection of corticosteroid to relieve pain of knee & thumb base OA. ● Injection depomethyl-prednisolone, 80 mg for large joint and 40 mg for small joint, Or, ● Inj. Triamcinolone 40mg/ml, 1 vial intra-articular stat and every 3 months up to 1 year. 7. Surgery – Osteotomy & joint replacement for knee & hip joint OA. Indicated in : (i) OA in whom pain, stiffness & reduced function impact significantly on their quality of life. (ii) Refractory OA (refractory to non-surgical & adjunctive treatment). OA Prescription example : + For pain : (any one or both) : ✔ T. P/C 665 mg / Napa Extended, 1+1+1 (A/M) ---- 7 – 30 days ✔ Diclofenac 1% Gel / Fastfit (gen) / Voligel Max (bex) / Voligel (bex) / Clofenac (sq) / Voltagel (skf) / Moov (astra) Gel, Apply locally 2-4 times daily ---- 1 month ↓ ✔ Tab. Paracetamol 325 mg + Tramadol 37.5 mg / NapaDol (bex) / Acetram (sq) / Utracet (uni), 1+1+1+1 or 2+2+2+2 (max) --- 5 days ↓ If pain persists : (PPI must if taking NSAID) ✔ Tab. Naproxen 500 mg/ Napryn (hc), 1+0+1 (A/M) --- 5 – 14 days ✔ Cap. Esomeprazole 20mg / Exium (rad), 1+0+1 (B/M) - 5–14 days ± Stimulation of cartilage formation : ✔ Tab. Glucosamine 250 mg + Chondroitin 200 mg / Arth-A (acm) / Joinix Plus (incp) / Cartilex (aci), 1+1+1 or, 2+2+2 ----- 1 – 3 month Or, Tab. Glucosamine 750 mg + Chondroitin 600 mg / Contilex TS (sq) / Arthrosin TS (uni) / Carticare TS, 1+0+1 ----- 1 – 3 month ± Vitamin & Calcium (not must, but may be beneficial) : ✔ Tab. Vit-B1+B6+B12 / Neurocare (bex) / Neuro-B (sq) / Neurobest (ren) / TPC (acm), 1 + 0 + 1 ----- 1 – 3 months ✔ Tab. Calcium Carbonate 500 mg + Vit-D3 200 IU / Ostocal (skf) / Calbo-D (sq)/Rocal-D (hc)/Calcin-D (ren), 1+1+0 ---- 1 – 3 months উপদেশ : হাঁটু ব্যথার কারণ বের করে তার চিকিৎসার পাশাপাশি জীবনাচরণ পরিবর্তন ও কিছু ব্যায়াম খুবই উপকারী। হাঁটু গেড়ে বসা, হাঁটু ভাঁজ করে কোনো কাজ করা বা ভারী বস্তু ওঠানোর মতো কাজ করবেন না। ▸ হাঁটু ভাজ করে কোন কাজ করবেন না। ▸ হাঁটুতে চাপ পড়ে এ ধরনের কাজ ১ মাস করবেন না। ▸ টয়লেটে কমোড ব্যাবহার করুন। ▸ প্রয়োজনে ঝাড়ুর সাথে লম্বা হাতল লাগিয়ে ঝাড়ু দেওয়া বা ঘর মুছতে পারেন। হাটু বাকা করে কাপড় ধোয়া, থালাবাসন করা নিষেধ। প্রয়োজনে মোড়া বা টুলে বসে এবং মোড়ার সামনে উঁচু টেবিল বানিয়ে কাপড় কাঁচা, থালাবাসন পরিষ্কার করার ব্যাবস্থা করা যাবে। ▸ সিড়ি দিয়ে উঠা এবং নামা নিষেধ। ▸ দিনের বেলা Knee cap/band ব্যবহার করবেন। ▸ শরীরের ওজন কমান। এতে হাঁটুর ওপর ভার কমবে। ▸ অতিরিক্ত ক্যালরিযুক্ত ভাজাপোড়া, তেল, চর্বি ও চিনিযুক্ত খাবার এড়িয়ে চলুন। ওমেগা ৩ ফ্যাটি অ্যাসিড (সামুদ্রিক মাছ, তিসির তেল, বাদাম-আখরোট), ভিটামিন সি–যুক্ত খাবার (পেয়ারা, লেবু, আনারস, টমেটো), ভিটামিন ই–যুক্ত খাবার (সূর্যমুখীর তেল, পালং শাক, ব্রকলি) ও ভিটামিন ডি–যুক্ত খাবার (ডিম, সার্ডিন, স্যামন) বেশি করে খান।

treatment.title: হাঁটুর ব্যায়াম
treatment.description: জীবনশৈলী পরিবর্তন, ওষুধ, পাশাপাশি কিছু ব্যায়াম এর জন্য ফিজিক্যাল মেডিসিন ও রিহ্যাবিলিটেশন বিভাগ / বিশেষজ্ঞ-এর যোগাযোগ করবেন : হাঁটুর ব্যায়াম করুন : প্রথম পদ্ধতি : *** সমান বিছানায় চিত হয়ে শুয়ে পড়ুন। প্রথমে ডান পায়ের হাঁটুর নিচে একটা কুশন বা বালিশ রাখুন। এবার বালিশের ওপরে পুরো পা দিয়ে জোরে চাপ প্রয়োগ করুন বা পা সোজা টানটান করুন। ১০ সেকেন্ডের মতো ধরে রাখুন। এভাবে বাঁ পাশের পায়ের ব্যায়ামটিও করুন। দ্বিতীয় পদ্ধতি : প্রথম পদ্ধতির ন্যায় বিছানার ওপর চিত হয়ে একই ভঙ্গিতে থেকে প্রথমে ডান পা সাধ্যমতো ওপরের দিকে তুলুন। কিছুক্ষণ ধরে রাখুন। এবার একই ভঙ্গিতে বাঁ পা ওপরের দিকে তুলে কিছুক্ষণ ধরে রাখুন। এই ব্যায়ামটি কয়েকবার করুন। তৃতীয় পদ্ধতি : সোজা হয়ে দাঁড়ান। হাঁটু ও কোমর কিছুটা ভাঁজ করে চেয়ারে বসার ভঙ্গিতে নিচু হোন, আবার উঁচু হোন, তবে চেয়ারে বসবেন না। অর্থাৎ চেয়ারে বসলে যে ভঙ্গিতে থাকবেন সে ভঙ্গিতেই উঁচু-নিচু করুন; শুধু মনে করবেন, চেয়ারটা নেই। এভাবে কয়েকবার করুন। চতুর্থ পদ্ধতি : বিছানার ওপর অথবা মাটিতে বসে এক সঙ্গে দুই হাঁটু ভাঁজ করুন। এবার হাঁটু দুটিকে দুই হাতে বুকে জড়িয়ে ধরুন। এবার পা সামনের দিকে প্রসারিত করুন। আবার হাঁটু ভাঁজ করে বুকে জড়িয়ে ধরুন। এভাবে বেশ কয়েকবার করুন, দেখবেন কোমর ও হাঁটুর ব্যথা কমে গেছে। পঞ্চম পদ্ধতি : দুই পা সোজা করে পায়ের পাতার দিকে সটান করে ১০ সেকেন্ড ধরে রাখুন। এভাবে কয়েকবার করুন এবং প্রতিটি ধাপ হবে ১০ সেকেন্ড দীর্ঘ। এই ব্যায়ামের ফলে কোমরের মাংসপেশির প্রদাহ কমে, কোমরের পেশি শক্তিশালী করে। ফলে কোমরের ব্যথা কমে যায়।

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Features : Fever with acute or subacute mono-arthritis, e.g., sudden onset of joint pain with swelling (usually large joint – knee or hip joint), with overlying redness of the skin. Common in immunocompromised (DM, Drugs, HIV), Elderly (>80), prosthetic joints, skin infection, recent joint surgery, IV drug abuser, IA injection, ESRD, advanced hepatic disease, sickle cell disease, underlying malignancy, hypogammaglobinaemia, hemophilia. On examination – Swollen, hot and red, with pain at rest and on movement, joint is extremely tender and joint effusion present. Erythematous, hot tender joints, loose pack position. Patients may look toxic. Urgent investigation – Joint fluid study : Synovial fluid for Gram staining & culture; blood for CBC & culture, consider sputum, urine culture if suspected. ○ Raised ESR, CRP ○ SF Study : - Cloudy, TC>50,000, Neutrophilic. - Gm stain & C/S may be +ve. - Staph aureus is the most common organism. Management ✔ Hospitalization. ✔ Intravenous antibiotic for 2 weeks (then oral for at-least 4 weeks). Septic arthritis is the infective cause of joint pain (severe). So antibiotics is must, guidelines regarding choice of empirical antibiotics, then change according to C/S report : ► Inj. Vancomycin 1 gm IV every 12 hours, Plus, Inj. Ceftriaxone 1 gm IV 12 hourly. Or, ► Initially IV Flucloxacillin (2 g, 4 times daily). ✔ Inj. Flucloxacillin 500 mg / Fluclox ((aci) / Flux (ops), 4 vial IV stat and QDS for 2 weeks ► If penicillin-allergic : clindamycin (450-600 mg 4 times daily in younger patients), intravenous vancomycin (1 g twice daily if age > 65 years). ► If high risk of Gram-negative sepsis (recurrent urinary tract infection) : intravenous gentamycin (5 mg/kg once daily) or vancomycin (750-1000 mg twice daily). 2. Serial needle aspiration to dryness 2-3 times daily. 3. NSAID to relieve pain (c̄ PPI). ✔ Inj. Ketorolac 30mg/1ml / Toradolin (rad) / Rolac (ren), 1 amp IM stat and TDS/BD ✔ Inj. Omeprazole 40mg / Maxima (acm) / Maxpro (ren), 1 vial IV stat & BD 4. Physiotherapy from the first day : regular passive movement progressing to active movement. 5. Daily aspiration of synovial fluid till dryness. Change the antibiotic according to C/S report. Continue antibiotics for 4-6 months (injectable antibiotics for 2 weeks followed by oral antibiotics). 6. Advice after 14 days meet with the below report : CBC, CRP, S. Creatinine, S. ALT.

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Features : Usually young male, fever and weight loss. ● The onset is typically acute, with an inflammatory oligo-arthritis that is asymmetrical and targets lower limb joints, typically the ankles, mid-tarsal joints, metatarsophalangeal joints or knees. ● Achilles tendinitis or plantar fasciitis may also be present. ● May have, past history of dysentery/bloody diarrhea or UTI, H/O sexual exposure. ● Extra-articular features may be present – ▸ Circinate balanitis, ▸ Keratoderma blennorrhagica, ▸ Nail dystrophy with subungual hyperkeratosis, ▸ Oral ulcer, ▸ Conjunctivitis / Uveitis, ▸ Pustular psoriasis. [ ▸ Circinate balanitis is an ulcerative mucosal lesion over the glans or shaft of penis that is demarcated by a serpiginous erythematous border. The lesion is usually painless and sterile unless a superimposed infection occurs. Keratoderma blennorrhagica is seen on the soles or palms of affected individuals.] Investigation – ESR and CRP are raised, X-ray of pelvis (A/P) reveal unilateral sacroilitis, urine C/S and high vaginal swab C/S may reveal urethritis and causative organism. Management : 1. Rest. 2. NSAID (c̄ PPI). ✔ Tab. Indomethacin 75 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) ---- 14 days ✔ Cap. Esomeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ---- 14 days If not responsive : add – ✔ Tab. Naproxen 500 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) ---- 14 days If not responding to two different NSAIDs with full dose and duration (14 days) : ✔ Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan (incp) 5 mg, 4+0+0 --- 7 days (A/M), Then, 3½ +0+0 --- 3 days (A/M), Then, 3+0+0 --- 3 days (A/M), Then, 2 ½ +0+0 --- 3 days (A/M), Then, 2+0+0 --- 3 days (A/M), Then, 1½ +0+0 --- 3 days (A/M), Then, 1½ +0+0 --- 3 days (A/M) 3. If chlamydial urethritis is diagnosed, it should be treated empirically with a short course of doxycycline or azithromycin. ✔ Cap. Doxycycline 100 mg, 1+0+1 ------ 5 – 14 days 4. Intra-articular or systemic glucocorticoids may be required in patients with severe mono-articular synovitis or poly-articular disease, respectively. 5. If there is anterior uveitis- topical, oral, sub-conjunctival steroid. If severe mono-articular synovitis : ✔ Inj. Triamcinoloine40mg/ml, 1 vial intra-articular stat. Or, if severe poly-articular disease : ✔ Tab. Prednisolone 10 mg, 2+0+0 (A/M). 6. DMARD (sulfasalazine or methotrexate) should be given if - Persistent marked symptom, - Recurrent arthritis, - Severe keratoderma blennorrhagica. 7. Advice after 28 days follow the below tests – CBC, CRP, Creatinine, SGPT. 8. Do the exercises regularly.

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Diagnosed case of psoriasis, with – ● Medium to large joint oligo-arthritis (knee, ankle, elbow); ● Small joint arthritis with DIP joint involvement; ● LBP. O/E : • Swollen & tender. elbows, knees, ankle. • Scaly skin lesion over extensor surface of elbow, knee, back of ear, over hairline, inframammary area. Investigation – ESR and CRP usually increase. – RF, ANA, anti CCP negative. Management 1. Avoid splint & prolong rest. 2. NSAID. 3. Intra-articular injection of steroid to control synovitis. 4. DMARD – MTX is the choice. Others may also be, Sulfasalazine, Ciclosporin and Leflunomide. 5. Anti TNF therapy in whom DMARDs fail. ► Tab. Methotrax 10 mg, 1 tab weekly (e.g. Thursday) – continue. ► Tab. Folison 5 mg, 1 tab weekly (e.g. Friday) – continue. ► Cap. Rheumacap SR 75 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) – 14 days. ► Tab Maxpro 20 mg, 1+0+1 (½ hr B/M) – 14 days. Advice : F/U with report after 1 month – CBC, CRP, S. Creatinine, S. ALT. Vaccinations should be done (schedule given before).

treatment.title: Acute gouty arthritis
treatment.description: Features : In almost all cases, first attacks a meta-tarso-pharyngeal joint. Other common sites are, in order of decreasing frequency, the ankle, mid-foot, knee, small joints of hands, wrist and elbow. The axial skeleton and large proximal joints are rarely involved and never as the first site. ● Painful swollen joint (big toe, feet, and ankles most commonly). ● Red skin which may peel ± fever. ● Can be poly-articular – especially in elderly. ● May mimic septic arthritis. Drug history : Aspirin, thiazide diuretics, ACEi, pyrazinamide. Investigation – ▸ Serum uric acid – Usually raised. S. Uric Acid may be normal in acute attack. Advice for S. Uric Acid when acute attack subsided. The diagnosis of gout can be confirmed by the identification of urate crystals in the aspirate from a joint, bursa or tophus. ▸ Joint fluid study. ▸ X-rays of affected joint : Usually normal in acute gout well demarcated erosions chronic or tophaceous gout. ▸ CBC with ESR. ▸ Serum creatinine. ▸ Serum electrolytes. ▸ RBS. ▸ Lipid profile. ▸ USG of the joint. Acute Gout attack Management : 1. Application of ice pack. 2. NSAID. 3. Oral colchicine given in doses of 0.5 mg 2 or 3 times daily is the treatment of first choice. 4. Oral prednisolone (15-20 mg daily) or intramuscular methyl-prednisolone (80-120 mg daily) for 2-3 days are highly effective and are a good choice in elderly patients where there is an increased risk of toxicity with colchicine and NSAID. 5. Joint aspiration & intra-articular injection of steroid. ✔ Tab. Colchicine 0.6 mg / Colimax (sq) / Kolchin (incp), 1+0+1 – 2 weeks [2-4 times daily] [SE: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea] Or, In older patient : ✔ Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan (incp) 10mg, 2+0+0 (A/M) – 2-5 days ✔ Tab. Paracetamol / Xcel (aci) 500 mg, 2+2+2 ----- if fever present + ✔ Cap. Indomethacin / Reumacap SR (aris) 75 mg / Or, Tab. Naproxen / Napro-A (acm) 500 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) – 15 days + ✔ Tab. Pantoprazole / Pantonix (incp) 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) – 15 days

treatment.title: Chronic Gout
treatment.description: Repeated attack of pain and swelling in 1 st MTP joint. Each episode persists for about 3-5 days, more at night subsided spontaneously, often requiring NSAID. O/E : Erythematous, swollen 1 st MTP joint. Tophi present. Treatment (Chronic gouty arthritis) : ✔ Tab. Allopurinol / Esloric (sq) 100 mg, 0+0+1 --- 4 weeks, then, 0+0+2 --- 4 weeks, then, 1+0+2 --- continue and check CBC, Creatinine, SGPT after 4 weeks. Rapid increase the dose of allopurinol every 4 weeks until uric acid level 6mg/dl or below. ✔ Tab. Colchicine 0.6 mg / Colimax (sq), 0+0+1 --- 6 months. ✔ Tab. Pantoprazole/Pantonix (incp) 20mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) – 15 days. Advice : 1. Rest and elevate joint-apply ice packs. 2. Reduce weight. Correction of predisposing factors. 3. Avoid alcohol and purine-rich foods (e.g., offal, red meat (cow/goat meat, liver, kidney etc.), sea food, yeast extracts, pulses, and mussels). Eat vegetables, vitamin C fruits. 4. Avoid thiazide diuretics and aspirin. Diuretics should be stopped if possible. [ উপদেশ : অ্যালকোহল (বিয়ার), কলিজা, টারকি মুরগী, সামুদ্রিক মাছ খাবেন না । গরু, খাসি, মুরগী ও হাঁসের মাংস পারতপক্ষে খাবেন না। ডাল, মটরশুঁটি, মাশরুম, ফুলকপি কম খাবেন ।] Indications for urate-lowering drugs : 1. Recurrent attacks of acute gout (>1 attack within 12 months). 2. Evidence of bone or joint damage. 3. Tophi or erosions. 4. Renal impairment. 5. Nephrolithiasis. ▸ During initiation of urate lowering drug acute attack may occur but drug should not be stopped. Acute attack during initiation of urate lowering therapy; oral colchicine or NSAID can be added. If indication present (urate-lowering drugs) : ✔ Tab. Allopurinol/Esloric (sq)/Purinol (drg)/ Alurol (incp) 100mg, 1+0+0 --- 4 weeks [ ½ +0+0 – in older patient or in renal impairment.] [Increase the dose by 100 mg in every 4 weeks (50 mg in older people) until target serum urea level is achieved then continue. Maximum dose is 900 mg/day] ↓ If inadequate response to allopurinol, allopurinol is contraindicated or cause adverse effect or patient has renal impairment : ✔ Tab. Febuxostat 40 or 80 mg / Febus (aci) / Febustat (incp) / Barif (sq), 0+0+1 ---- continue, [40-80 mg/day, increasing to 120 mg daily in adequate response]. Follow up : S. uric acid should be measured 3-4 weeks interval, aim for low normal range.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Asymptomatic until a fracture occurs (common in postmenopausal women & men >50 years old). Osteoporosis does not cause symptoms until a fracture occurs, patient may have low trauma fracture, vertebral compression, osteopenia in X-ray etc. C/C : • Low back pain for 6 months. • Mechanical in type & relieved by rest. • Height loss. • Occasional steroid use. O/E : • Generalized bone pain. • Localized tenderness in back. • Cushingoid face & generalized swelling/anasarca (steroid use). On local examination : 1. Signs of fracture : Pain, local tenderness and deformity. 2. In hip fracture : In affected side : Unable to weight-bear, Shortened and externally rotated limb. 3. In vertebral fractures : Acute severe back pain : Which may radiate to the anterior chest or abdominal wall. Height loss and kyphosis in absence of pain or with chronic back pain.

treatment.title: Bone mineral density (BMD) / DEXA
treatment.description: Investigations : DEXA (BMD) – T score > -2.5. X ray of spine : Osteopenia or osteoporosis with or without wedge shaped vertebral fracture. Fragility fracture : A fracture that occurs as the result of a fall from standing height or less. These are typical of osteoporosis. Indications dual energy X-ray absorptiometry (DEXA) : • Low trauma fracture (#) in age > 50 years* • Clinical features of osteoporosis (height loss, kyphosis) • Osteopenia on plain X-ray. • Corticosteroid therapy (prolong). (>7.5mg prednisolone daily for >3 months). • Family history of hip fracture. • Low body weight (BMI • Early menopause ( • Diseases associated with osteoporosis. • Increased # risk on risk factor analysis (FRAX or Q Fracture) • Assessing response of osteoporosis to treatment. Bone mineral density T-score criteria for osteoporosis and low bone mass : Diagnosis T-score Normal +1.0 to -1.0 Low bone mass (osteopenia) -1.0 to - 2.5 Osteoporosis and fragility fracture ≤ -2.5 or lower

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Non-pharmacological treatment : 1. Cessation of smoking. 2. Moderation of alcohol intake. 3. Dietary calcium intake. 4. Exercise. Drug treatment indication : 1. BMD : T score below -2.5 or below -1.5 in corticosteroid induced osteoporosis. 2. Vertebral fracture (spontenous) irrespective of BMD (except traumatic vertebral fracture). Drugs commonly used : 1. Alendronate 70 mg/week or risedronate 35 mg/week. Orally on empty stomach & no food should be taken and should not lie in bed up to 30-45 minutes of administration. 2. Calcium & vitamin-D/ Calcitriol. 3. Parathyroid hormone (PTH) – teriparatide. Superior to alendronate. It is expensive, reserve for – a) Severe osteoporosis. BMD T score -3.5 to - 4.0 or below. b) Failure to respond adequately to other treatment. Duration 24 months, after that bisphosphonate should be used to maintain BMD. Teriparatide should not be used simultaneously with bisphosphonate (Zoledronic acid). 4. Calcitonin – has analgesic properties. Sometimes used short to medium time in patient with acute vertebral fracture. Dose S/C or IM 100-200U daily or intranasal spray 200 micro units daily. 5. Hormone replacement therapy (HRT) – primarily indicated for prevention of osteoporosis in women with an early menopause & for treatment of women with osteoporosis in their early fifties who have troublesome menopausal symptoms. Treatment : ⦸ Tab. Alendronic Acid 70 mg + Vitamin D3 2800 IU / Ostel-D (sq) / Bonemass D (incp), 1 tab in every Friday – taken in empty, stomach with plain water and remain upright for 30-45 minutes – 5-6 years. Or, ✔ Inj. Zoledronic Acid [5 mg/100 ml, for osteoporosis] / Zolenic (incp) / Zoltero (bex) / Zandra (hc), I/V @30 drops/min (over 15 min) – once yearly – 3 years. Or, ⦸ Tab. Risedronate 150 mg / Salost (uni) / Risonet (ops), 1 tab in every Friday – taken in empty stomach with plain water and remain upright for 30-45 minutes – 5-6 years Advice : 1. Smoking cessation. 2. Moderation of alcohol intake. 3. Adequate dietary calcium intake. 4. Exercise. 5. For Zoledronic Acid : Maintain oral hygiene and don't extract the tooth. Follow up : After 6 months – 2 years with BMD and X-ray L/S spine. NTX (N-Telopeptide) respond quickly than BMD. Surgery 1. Hip replacement – total or partial, indicated for intracapsular fracture of femoral neck. 2. Kyphoplasty – indicated for acute vertebral compression fracture where there is a significant degree collapse & severe pain. 3. Vertebroplasty – indicated for painful vertebral fracture which fails to settle by medical treatment.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Rickets is a childhood disease caused by vitamin D deficiency, leading to soft, weak bones that can deform. Osteomalacia is the adult form of rickets, also due to vitamin D deficiency, causing softened bones and increased fracture risk. Management : Respond quickly to treatment with ergocalciferol 250-100 μ gm daily. After 3-4 months treatment can generally be stopped or the dose of vit-D reduce to maintenance level of 10-20 μ gm cholecalciferol daily except in patient with malabsorption in whom higher dose may be required. Follow up 1. Clinical improvement. 2. Elevation in 25(OH)D 3 . 3. Reduce in PTH.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Definition : It is a chronic auto immune inflammatory destructive & deforming polyarthritis, characterized by bilateral symmetrical involvement of small and large joints with system and extra-articular feature with exacerbation and remission. Common Symptoms : • Bilateral, symmetrical involvement, small, joints of hand are almost always involved. Pain and swelling of the joints. • Prolonged morning stiffness (>30 min) or stiffness after inactivity. • Symmetric swelling (arthritis); soft tissue swelling (arthritis) of 3 or more joint areas observed by a physician for at least 6 weeks; • Usually swelling (arthritis) of the proximal interphalangeal, metacarpophalangeal, or wrist joints for at least 6 weeks; • Rheumatoid nodules; • The presence of rheumatoid factor; and • Radiographic erosions and/or periarticular osteopenia in hand and/or wrist joints. • Fever, weight loss, fatigue. General Signs : • Swelling and deformity of multiple joints in a symmetric fashion and often involving the hands. • May have erythema. • May have extra articular features. • Anemia and lymphadenopathy are common. • Tenderness of joints on passive movement. C/C : • Pain and swelling in multiple joints, specially small joints of both hands. • Morning stiffness for 60 minutes, pain improved by activity. O/E : • Swollen & tender MCP, PIP joints of both hands. • MCP squeeze test positive. Common Investigation : • S. creatinine • Urine R/M/E • CRP • FBC, ESR • SGPT • RA, Anti-ccp • RBS, HbA1C • X-ray chest P/A view (CXR) • X-ray hand • MT

treatment.title: RA Scoring
treatment.description: RA Scoring Joints affected : 1 large joint 0 2 – 10 large joints 1 1 – 3 small joints 2 4 – 10 small joints 3 >10 joints (at least 1 small joint) 5 Serology : Negative RF and ACPA 0 Low positive RF or ACPA 2 High positive RF or ACPA 3 Duration of symptoms : 0 >6 weeks 1 Acute phase reactants : Normal CRP and ESR 0 Abnormal CRP or ESR 1 Patients with a score ≥ 6 are considered to have definite RA. Hand examination : 1. Swelling and tenderness of wrist, MIC, PIP sparing DIP. 2. Deformities : ● Ulnar deviation of the fingers. ● 'Swan neck' deformity ● Boutonnière or 'button hole' deformity. ● Z deformity of the thumb. ● Dorsal subluxation of the ulna at the distal radio-ulnar joint. In foot : 1. Valgus deformity: In the hindfoot. 2. Flat foot. 3. Popliteal (Baker's) cysts.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Treatment : (Need specialist referral) : Diagnosis of RA ↓ Start csDMARD + Glucocorticoid (GC) • Short-term glucocorticoid (GC) ( ≤ 3 months), intra-articular GC injections for rapid relief of highly inflamed joints. • Corticosteroids – As a bridge therapy before the onset of full action of DMARD. Usual dose : 30 mg/day, decrease over 12 weeks. + csDMARD Start MTX, if eligible for methotrexate (MTX). - MTX (Methotrexate) is the drug of choice. - Started at a lower, dose of 10-15 mg/week then gradually increased up to 25 mg/week. - MTX dose is pillowed by tab Folic acid 5 mg on the next day. Contraindication to MTX : Leflunomide (if high disease activity) or, Sulfasalazine (if moderate-low disease activity / child bearing potential). • NSAID c̄ PPI : used as necessary ↓ Is improved at 3 months and attained treatment target at 6 months? If, Yes → Continue. If, NO, ↓ Presence of poor prognostic factors (High titre RF/ACPA, moderate to high disease activity, high tender/swollen joint counts, high acute phase reactants Absence of poor prognostic factors ↓ ↓ Add a JAK inhibitor. Change/add another csDMARD (Leflunomide, sulfasalazine) as monotherapy or combination. Is improved at 3 months and attained treatment target at 6 months? ↓ If, Yes → Continue → Dose taper / in sustained remission. If, NO, ↓ Change to another JAK inhibitor or switch over to another DMARD (switching can be done to a same or different class) ↓ Is improved at 3 months and attained treatment target at 6 months? ↓ If, Yes → Continue → Dose taper / in sustained remission. If, NO → Change to another JAK inhibitor or switch over to another DMARD (switching can be done to a same or different class). [Ref – RA guideline from BRS (Bangladesh rheumatology society) (Flowchart), Cureus, Page – 8] csDMARD – conventional synthetic DMARD; JAK inhibitor – Janus kinase inhibitor; bDMARD – biological disease- modifying drugs. JAK inhibitor : Tofacitinib is 5 mg twice a day. Tofacitinib can be used up to 15 mg daily because 20 mg increases the risk of thromboembolic events. Tofacitinib should not be started if hemoglobin (Hb) is Baricinitib showed clinically significant improvement compared to placebo and adalimumab (RA-BEAM study). Baricitinib was more efficacious as a first-line tsDMARD. This supports the BRS (Bangladesh rheumatology society) recommendation to use JAK inhibitors before bDMARDs. The SELECT-BEYOND research found that upadacitinib was beneficial and showed rapid improvement in bDMARD refractory RA patients (three or more bDMARDs). Other biological agent – abatacept, adalimumab, anakinra, certolizumab etc. Most effective with a conventional DMARD, for optimum duration. Life long Follow up : • Monitoring of disease (progression) actively. • Escalation of DMARD for non-responders. • Side effects of drugs. • Complication of disease. • Disease activity index : CDAI / DAS28-ESR /CRP — low / moderate / high.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: MODEL Treatment : ► Tab. Prednisolone 10 mg / Cortan (incp) / Deltasone (ren) / Precodil (ops) / Deltapred (zis) / Predimax (acm) 10 mg, 3 + 0 + 0 (A/M) ---- 2 weeks, then, 2 ½ + 0 + 0 (A/M) ----- 2 weeks, then, 2 + 0 + 0 (A/M) ---- 2 weeks, then, 1 ½ + 0 + 0 (A/M) ---- 2 weeks, then, 1 + 0 + 0 (A/M) --- 2 weeks, then, ½ + 0 + 0 (A/M) --- 2 weeks ► Tab. Esomeprazole 20 mg MUPS / Remmo (bex) / Othera (aci) / Exium MUPS (rad) / Esoral MUPS (skf), 1 + 0 + 1 ---- 12 weeks ► Tab. Methotrexate 10 mg / Methotrax (del) / Methox (pop) / Meth (drg) / Trexall (hc), 1 ½ tab every Friday ---- continue [Can be increased 5 mg in every 2-3 weeks up to 25mg/week] ► Tab. Folic acid 5 mg, 1 tab every Saturday ----- continue If pain is severe : ► Cap. Indomethacin 75 mg / Reumacap SR (aris) / Indomet SR (ops) / Imet SR (paci), 1+0+1 (A/M) ----- 14 days ► Advice : Follow up after 1 month with – CBC, CRP, S. Creatinine, S. ALT. Exercise regularly. Try to conceive after 1 month of stopping methotrax. ► Get the following vaccinations (before starting the medicine) : • Inj. Prevenar-13 – 1 ampoule once in a lifetime. • Inj. Influvax Tetra – 1 ampoule administered once a year. • Inj. Hepavax-B – 1 ampoule in 0, 1 st and 6 th month. ► If not responding: Refer this patient to internist / rheumatologist for triple therapy composed of methotrexate, sulfasalazine and hydroxychloroquine. Early referral to a rheumatologist is essential for diagnosis and the timely introduction of effective therapy.

treatment.title: RA in pregnancy
treatment.description: ► Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA) in pregnancy : Many patients go into remission during pregnancy. 1. Stop MTX, at least 3 months and leflunomide at least 24 months before trying to conceive. 2. Analgesic in pregnancy : Paracetamol (oral analgesic of choice): Maximum 4g/day, NSAIDS and selective COX-2 inhibitors: From implantation to 20 weeks of gestation. 3. Glucocorticoids : May be used to control disease flares. 4. DMARDs : May be used : Sulfasalazine, Hydroxychloroquine, Azathioprine. Must be avoided : Methotrexate, Leflunomide, Cyclophosphamide, Mycophenolate, Gold. 5. Biologic therapies : Relatively safe during pregnancy. The main theoretical risk is immunosuppression in the neonate. Except for certolizumab, which does cross the placenta in negligible amounts. 6. Breast-feeding : Must avoid Methotrexate, Leflunomide, Cyclophosphamide.

treatment.title: RA in young lady
treatment.description: Treatment of RA in young lady : ► Tab. Sulfasalazine 500 mg Salazine (ops) / Reumazin (aris), 0+0+1 – 7 days, then, 1+0+1 – 7 days, then, 1+0+2 – 7 days, then, 2+0+2 – continue. (Discontinue the medicine if any rash or itching occurs on the body) ► Tab. Predinisolone / Cortan (incp) 5 mg, 1½+0+0 (A/M) – 15 days, then, 1+0+0 (A/M) – 15 days, then, 1+0+0 – 1 month. Analgesics :(Avoid in Pg, use P/C) : ► Cap. Indomethacin / Indomet (ops) / Reumacap (aris) SR 75 mg, Or, Tab. Naproxen / Naprox 500 mg, 1+0+1 ---- 14 days (A/M) ► Cap. Esomeprazole / Maxpro 20 mg, 1+0+1 ---- 2 months (B/M) “Rheumatic Fever” – please see cardiology portion of this book.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) (Referred to rheumatologist/internist/dermatologist) Dx : SLE if any 4 of 11 features (of followings) : 1. Malar rash : Fixed erythema, flat or raised, sparing the nasolabial folds. 2. Discoid rash : Erythematous raised patches with adherent keratotic scarring and follicular plugging. 3. Photosensitivity : Rash due to unusual reaction to sunlight. 4. Oral ulcers : Oral or nasopharyngeal ulceration, which may be painless. 5. Arthritis : Non-erosive, involving two or more peripheral joints. 6. Serositis : Pleuritis (history of pleuritic pain or rub, or pleural effusion) or, pericarditis (rub, electrocardiogram evidence or effusion). 7. Renal disorder : Persistent proteinuria >0.5 g/24 hrs or cellular casts (red cell, granular or tubular). 8. Neurological disorder : Seizures or psychosis, in the absence of provoking drugs or metabolic derangement. 9. Haematological disorder : Haemolytic anaemia or leucopenia ( 10. Immunological findings : Anti-DNA antibodies in abnormal titer or presence of antibody to SM antigen or positive antiphospholipid antibodies. 11. ANA : Abnormal titer of ANA by immunofluorescence.
