Common presentation – ▸ Usually female, 20 – 30 years. ▸ Poly-arthralgia (joint pain) with morning stiffness, ▸ Facial rash / malar rash, discoid rash, photosensitivity, ▸ Oral ulcer, alopecia etc. On examination – ulcer or erosion may be present in hard palate, alopecia and tenderness in small joints of the hands. Investigation – CBC-high ESR, leucopenia and lymphopenia are typical of active SLE, along with anaemia, haemolytic anaemia and thrombocytopenia. ANA-positive. To see the renal involvement-urine R/M/E, S. creatinine. Drug history : Sulphonamides, penicillin, OCP.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: General management : 1. Educate the patient. 2. Avoid sun & UV light exposure, employ sun block. 3. Avoid smoking, if applicable. 4. Control hypertension & dyslipidaemia, if any. Mild to moderate disease (restricted to skin & joints) : 1. NSAID c̄ PPI. 2. Hydroxy-chloroquine 200-400 mg daily. 3. Frequently, however, glucocorticoids are also necessary (prednisolone 5-20 mg/day), often in combination with immunosuppressants such as methotrexate, azathioprine or mycophenolate mofetil (MMF). In mild to moderate SLE : ✔ Tab. Indomethacin 75 mg / Reumacap SR (aris) / Indomet SR (ops) / Imet SR (paci), 1+0+1 (A/M) --- if joint pain ✔ Cap. Esomeprazole 20 mg / Nexum (sq) / Alton (gen) / Opton (bex) / Pronex (drg) / Esotid (ops), 1+0+1 ( ½ hr B/M) --- continue ✔ Tab. Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg / Reconil (incp) / Quinivir (sq) / Reumaflex (del) / HCQ (uni) / Avloquin-H (aci) 200 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) --- Continue ✔ Tab. Prednisolone 10 mg / Precodil (ops) / Deltapred (zis) / Deltasone (ren), 2+0+0 (A/M) [5-20mg/day] ---- continue ± Trialon oral paste, Apply twice a day on mouth sores for 14 days. ✔ Use sunscreen with SPF 25-30 / SunMask Cream (sq) / Depiwhite Day SPF 20 (uni) / Neutrogena (USA) / Bioderma. Apply on the exposed area of the body to sunlight (such as face, neck & body) at least 30 minutes before exposure and whenever necessary. Massage on to skin until absorbed. To ensure maximum protection, repeat the application every two hours after continuous exposure to the sun and after swimming, physical exercise. Vaccination : • Inj. Prevenar 13 — 1 PFS I/M once in a lifetime. • Inj. Influvax Tetra — 1 Vial I/M administered once a year. • Inj. Hepavax-B — 1 Vial I/M in 0, 1 st & and 6 th month.

treatment.title: Steroid & immune suppression
treatment.description: Severe and life threatening disease (affecting kidneys, CNS or CVS): requires high dose steroid plus immune suppression. (Referred to internist / rheumatologist) 1. A commonly used regimen is pulse methylprednisolone (10 mg/kg IV) plus cyclophosphamide (15 mg/kg IV), repeated at 2-3 weekly intervals for six cycles. Alternatively in renal involvement cyclophosphamide can be replaced with mycophenolate mofetil (MMF) with fewer side effects. 2. Following control- prednisolone 40-60 mg daily, gradually reducing to 10-15 mg/day or less by 3 months. Azathioprine (2-2.5 mg/kg/day), methotrexate (10-25 mg/week) or MMF (2-3 g/day) should also be prescribed. 3. Co-trimoxazole 960 mg thrice weekly to prevent P. carinii infection due to cyclophosphamide. 4. Mesna is given with bolus cyclophosphamide to reduce risk of haemorrhagic cystitis. 5. Lupus nephritis with antiphospholipid syndrome who have had previous thrombosis require lifelong warfarin.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Systemic sclerosis (Referred to rheumatologist/internist/dermatologist) Diagnosis is usually clinical : It is a group of rare diseases, in which patient present with tightening of skin, joint pain and blackening of the fingers (Raynaud's phenomenon). On examination – skin tightening present and gangrene may be present in fingers. Investigation – anti topoisomerase antibody and anticentromere antibody may be positive. Management : A) Raynaud's syndrome with digital ulcer – 1. Avoid cold exposure. 2. Use of thermal insulating gloves / socks and maintenance of a high core temperature will help. 3. If symptoms are persistent, calcium channel blockers, losartan, fluoxetine and sildenafil have efficacy. 4. Antibiotic (longer duration than usual). 5. Intermittent infusion of epoprostenol may benefit severe digital ischaemia. B) Esophageal reflux – PPI and metochlopramide or domperidone. C) Antibiotic for bacterial overgrowth. D) ACEi for HTN should be given even in renal impairment. E) Joint involvement – analgesic and/or NSAIDs. If synovitis is present and RA co-exists (overlap condition), low-dose methotrexate can be of value. F) Pulmonary hypertension – endothelin-1 antagonist ' bosentan’. G) Glucocorticoids and pulse intravenous cyclophosphamide are the mainstays of treatment in patients who have progressive interstitial lung disease. Advice : 1. Do not handle anything cold (ice cream, ice, opening the fridge / taking something out of the fridge etc.). Avoid cold exposure. 2. Always wear socks on hands and feet in winter. 3. Use hot water for washing / bathing hands and feet in winter. 4. Do not use raw soap, detergent on hands. 5. Do not go to bed immediately after eating. Sleep after walking for 10-15 minutes. Avoid GERD aggravating factors. 6. Apply olive oil / vaseline on the skin.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Polymyositis & Dermatomyositis (Referred to rheumatologist/internist/dermatologist) Polymyositis and dermatomyositis are autoimmune diseases that cause inflammation of the muscles, most commonly the upper arms and thighs, resulting in weakness. Polymyositis affects mainly the muscles while dermatomyositis can cause skin lesions in addition to muscle inflammation. Management : 1. Oral glucocorticoids (prednisolone 1 mg/kg daily) are the mainstay of initial treatment of PM and DM (if there is a good response steroid should be reduced by approximately 25% per month to maintain dose of 5 – 7.5 mg). 2. Intravenous methylprednisolone 1 gm daily for 3 days in patient with respiratory or pharyngeal weakness. 3. Most patients require additional treatment with azathioprine & MTX. 4. Intravenous immunoglobulin may be effective in refractory cases.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Polymyalgia Rheumatica (PMR) (Referred to rheumatologist/internist) Features : Usually older adults over the age of 50 years, complaints of upper arms (shoulder), neck, lower back (hip), and thighs aching and stiffness, which worse at morning and gradually improve as the day progress, doesn't cause joint swelling. Investigation – ESR and CRP increase. Management : 1. Prednisolone 20-30 mg daily gradually reduce the dose 10-15 mg by about 8 weeks ultimately 5-7.5 mg for 12-14 weeks. 2. Immunosuppressive like MTX, azathioprine when steroid require > 7.5 mg/day.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Bechet’s Syndrome (Referred to rheumatologist/internist/dermatologist) Features : 1. Oral ulcer -deep & multiple. 2. Genital ulcer. 3. Skin lesion-erythema nodosum or acneform lesion. 4. Migratory thrombophlebitis. 5. Anterior or posterior uveitis. 6. Neurological sign – brain stem involvement, pyramidal tract signs etc. Management : 1. Oral ulcer – oral/topical steroid preparation. 2. Colchicine for erythema nodosum & arthralgia. 3. Thalidomide 100-300 mg daily for 28 days & effective for resistant oral & genital ulcer. 4. Corticosteroid & immunosuppressive for systemic disease.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Plantar fasciitis Heel & sole pain mainly may be due to – ▸ Plantar fasciitis : Swelling of the thick band of tissue on the bottom of your foot. ▸ Plantar bursitis : Inflammation of the bursa at the back of the heel. ▸ Calcaneal spur / Bone / Heel spurs : A bony outgrowth from the calcaneal tuberosity / heel bone. Symptoms : Plantar fasciitis typically causes a stabbing pain in the bottom of your foot near the heel. The pain is usually the worst with the first few steps after awakening, although it also can be triggered by long periods of standing or when you get up from sitting. It is more common in runners and in people who are overweight. Treatment : ● Treatment only required if causing discomfort. ● Topical or oral NSAID (nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs). ● PPI. ● Vit-B (Cyanocobalamin/B12) ± calcium ± vit –D. ● Physical therapy : rolling tennis ball under feet. ● Supportive shoes. ● Surgery (if medical treatment failed). উপদেশ : ১। পায়ের নীচে আব কলায় বল বা বোতল রেখে ব্যায়াম : প্রথমে মেঝেতে একটি বল বা বোতল রাখুন। বলের উপর একটি পা রেখে বারবার বিভিন্ন দিকে ঘোরান। কমপক্ষে ৫ মিনিট ধরে একটি পা করার পর অপর পা একইভাবে এই পদ্ধতি অবলন্বন করুন। ২। সার্বক্ষণিকভাবে নরম শোল বা ফোমযুক্ত স্যান্ডেল বা জুতা ব্যবহার করবেন। ‘ডক্টর সু’ নামে পাওয়া যায়। খালি পায়ে থাকবেন না। শক্ত জুতা পড়বেন না। সব সময় নরম স্যান্ডেল বা জুতা পড়ে থাকবেন। রাতে ঘুমানোর সময় স্প্রিন্ট ব্যবহার করতে পারেন ।

treatment.title: Calcaneal Spur
treatment.description: A calcaneal spur (also known as a heel spur) is a bony outgrowth from the calcaneal tuberosity (heel bone). Inv. : X-ray right or left foot B/V. Treatment & advice same as ‘plantar fasciitis’ (Please see above).

treatment.title: পায়ের পাতা বা গোড়ালি ব্যায়াম
treatment.description: পায়ের পাতা বা গোড়ালি ব্যায়াম : এক. পায়ের পাতা কোনো শক্ত কিছুর (যেমন পিঁড়ি) ওপর আলতো করে রাখুন। এবার পাতা দিয়ে শক্ত জিনিসটার ওপর ধীরে ধীরে চাপ দিন। গোড়ালির হাড় বাঁকাবেন না। তবে গোড়ালির ওপরের পেশি শক্ত হবে। এভাবে ১৫ সেকেন্ড চাপ দিন, ১০ সেকেন্ড শিথিল করুন। দুই. পায়ের পাতা এবার শক্ত জিনিসটার নিচে রাখুন। পাতা দিয়ে জিনিসটার ওপর মানে নিজের দিকে চাপ দিতে থাকুন। ১৫ সেকেন্ড চাপ দিন, ১০ সেকেন্ড শিথিল করুন। তিন. মেঝে বা শক্ত কিছুতে সোজা হয়ে পা লম্বা করে বসুন। এবার পায়ের পাতা ধীরে ধীরে নিচের দিকে বাঁকান যতটা সম্ভব। হাঁটু ভাঁজ হবে না। ১৫ সেকেন্ড ধরে রাখুন। কয়েকবার করুন। চার. একইভাবে বসে থেকে এবার পায়ের পাতা নিজের দিকে বাঁকান। যতটা সম্ভব। হাঁটু ভাঁজ না করে। ১৫ সেকেন্ড ধরে রাখুন। কয়েকবার করুন।

treatment.title: Causes
treatment.description: Neck Pain One of three individuals can recall at least one incidence of neck pain in their life time. Causes of Neck Pain : ► Mechanical • Cervical sprain & strain • Whiplash injury • Disc prolapse • Cervical spondylosis • Postural ► Inflammatory • Spondylitis • Juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) • Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) • Polymyalgia rheumatic ► Destructive • Neoplastic • Infectious • Traumatic ► Metabolic • Osteoporosis • Osteomalacia • Paget's disease ► Referred pain from • Pharynx • Cervical lymph nodes • Teeth • Angina pectoris Aortic aneurysm • Pancoast tumour • Diaphragm

treatment.title: Cervical Sprain or Strain
treatment.description: The most common presentation is non-radiating neck pain. If pain radiates, it is typically diffuse and not in a dermatomal distribution. Usually a history of an event that caused the symptoms. Range of Motion (ROM) restricted & painful. X-ray C/S B/V (cervical spine both view) : ○ Useful in trauma to rule out fracture. ○ Flexion/extension views can evaluate instability. Tx : please see below.

treatment.title: Cervical radiculopathy
treatment.description: Cervical radiculopathy is commonly due to compression of a cervical nerve root that causes pain or dysfunction. C/F : ● Presents as neck pain radiating to the arm / upper extremity. ● Patients may report pain and paresthesia or sensory loss in a specific (dermatomal) distribution. ● Less commonly, patients may report weakness in an involved extremity.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Drug Management : ○ NSAID + PPI ○ Pregabalin ○ Vitamin B1, B6 & B12 ○ Muscle relaxant Activities of Daily Living (ADL) advice : ○ Sitting Posture Modification ○ Carrying Posture Modification ○ Sleeping Posture Modification ○ Others Posture Modification ○ Reduce Stress. Physical Therapy : ○ Moist heat compression ○ If Acute traumatic or painful – Ice compression for first 2 days. Exercise : ○ Pain free range of movement (ROM) exercise. ○ Isometric neck strengthening. ○ Stretching exercise of shoulder & upper arm. ○ Postural exercises. Refer : If severe pain or pain not improved within 7 days.

treatment.title: ঘাড় ব্যথার ব্যায়াম
treatment.description: ঘাড় ব্যথার ব্যায়াম : ১. প্রথমে ঘাড় সোজা রেখে আরাম করে বসুন। দুই কাঁধ উঁচু করে রাখুন ১০ সেকেন্ড। এরপর স্বাভাবিক অবস্থায় ফিরে আসুন। প্রতিবেলায় পাঁচবার করে তিন-চার বেলা ব্যায়ামটি করতে পারেন। এই ব্যায়াম করার সময় শ্বাস-প্রশ্বাসের ব্যায়াম করলে আরও কার্যকর হবে। এ ক্ষেত্রে ঘাড় সোজা রেখে আরাম করে বসার সময় লম্বা করে শ্বাস নিতে হবে। কাঁধ উঁচু করার সময় শ্বাস ধরে রাখতে হবে। স্বাভাবিক অবস্থানে ফিরে আসার সময় আস্তে করে শ্বাস ছাড়ুন। ঘাড়ের সঙ্গে হাতে ব্যথা থাকলেও এই ব্যায়াম বেশ কাজে দেবে। ২. মাথা সোজা রাখুন। কপালে হাত রেখে মাথা দিয়ে হাতে চাপ দিতে চেষ্টা করুন। একইভাবে মাথার ডান দিকে, বাঁ দিকে ও পেছনে হাত রেখে মাথার সাহায্যে চাপ দেন। এভাবে প্রতিবেলায় কয়েকবার করে এই ব্যায়াম করতে পারেন। ৩. যাঁদের ঘাড়ে ব্যথার পাশাপাশি ঘাড় নাড়াতেও অসুবিধা হয়, তাঁরা প্রথম শিথিলায়ন ব্যায়াম করতে পারেন। পরে ধীরে ধীরে বিভিন্নভাবে ঘাড় নাড়িয়ে ব্যায়াম করতে পারেন। সামনের দিকে যতটা সম্ভব, ঘাড় বাঁকিয়ে ফেলুন। এরপর যতটা সম্ভব, পেছনের দিকে ঘাড় বাঁকা করুন। একইভাবে ডান দিকে ও বাঁ দিকে ঘাড় বাঁকা করতে চেষ্টা করুন। যতটা সম্ভব, তত দূর পর্যন্ত ঘাড় নাড়ানো হলে ধীরে ধীরে সীমা বা দূরত্ব বাড়তে থাকবে। এই ব্যায়ামও কয়েকবার করে দু-তিন বেলা করতে পারেন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: A condition of painful loss of shoulder motion with normal X-rays. Associated with diabetes mellitus (DM), hyperthyroidism, ischemic heart disease (IHD), inflammatory arthritis, and cervical spondylosis. C/C : • Decreased range of motion of the shoulder, which interferes with activities of daily life. • Pain (more commonly at night) is more common with overhead activities and in the early stages of adhesive capsulitis. O/E : • Active range of motion (AROM) & passive range of motion (PROM) : Restricted • Shoulder external rotation and abduction may be particularly limited and painful. • Tenderness to palpation of the shoulder. Investigation : Diagnosis is clinical. X-ray shoulder is normal. Association : RBS, FT4, TSH, CBC, CRP. Drug Management : NSAIDs + PPI ± Topical NSAID Gel. Intra-articular steroid injections (IASI) in non responsive cases. Activities of Daily Living (ADL) advice : • Limit rest/avoid prolonged rest; • Encourage shoulder movement; • Avoid vigorous or ballistic activities; • Avoid sleeping on involved side; • No buddy stretching. • Avoid prolonged sling (use only for short periods); • Avoid vigorous stretching and manipulation initially. Physical Therapy ○ If Acute traumatic or painful – Ice compression for first 2 days. ○ Then – moist heat compression. Exercise : • Pendulums (clockwise/counter-clockwise). • Overhead stretches. • Crossed adduction. • External rotation performed in supine using pipe/stick. • Internal rotation performed in standing. • Horizontal adduction performed in standing. • Pulley for elevation performed in sitting/standing. Refer – If severe pain or pain not improved within 7 Days.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: • Patients usually present with shoulder pain, weakness, and loss of range of motion (ROM) resulting in impaired shoulder function. • Referred pain or paresthesias into the region of the deltoid insertion and upper lateral arm. • Pain may occur with internal and external rotation and may affect daily self-care activities. • The patient can be awoken by pain in the shoulder, which impairs sleep. O/E : ○ PROM > AROM. ○ Tenderness over the acromion or over the greater tuberosity. ○ Painful Arc positive. ○ Jobs test positive.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: This is the most common tendinopathy, with pain arising from the origin of extensor carpi radialis brevis (ECRB) at the lateral epicondyle. Risk factors : Any activity that involves twisting of the wrist can lead to this condition. This condition may also be due to constant computer keyboard and mouse use. C/C : • Pain in the area just distal to the lateral epicondyle. • Radiating proximally or distally. • Patients may also complain of pain with wrist or hand movement, such as gripping a doorknob, carrying a briefcase, or shaking hands. • Patients occasionally report swelling as well.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Medial epicondylitis implies an inflammatory lesion with degeneration at the origin of the flexor muscles (the medial epicondyle of the humerus). In medial epicondylitis, the tendon of the flexor muscle group is affected (flexor carpI radialis, flexor carpi ulnaris, flexor digitorum superficialis, and palmaris longus). Risk Factors : • Repetitive stress. • Poor throwing mechanics. • Excessive throwing. • Repetitive wrist flexion, as in the trailing arm in a golf swing. C/C : • Pain in the area just distal to the medial epicondyle, radiating proximally or distally. • Patients may also complain of pain with wrist or hand movement, such as – gripping a doorknob, carrying a briefcase or shaking hands. • Patients occasionally report swelling as well. Tx : NSAIDs + PPI, Topical NSAID Gel. Supportive Device: band/ counter force brace.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: It is a painful condition affecting the tendons on the thumb side of the wrist. Pain on turning wrist, grasp anything or make a fist. Most common in female of 30-50 years. Symptoms of de Quervain tenosynovitis include : ○ Pain near the base of the thumb. ○ Swelling near the base of the thumb. ○ Difficulty moving the thumb and wrist when doing something that involves grasping or pinching. ○ A "sticking" or "stop-and-go" sensation in the thumb when moving it. Clinical test : Finkelstein test : Bend your thumb across the palm of your hand and bend your fingers down over your thumb. Then you bend your wrist toward your little finger. If this causes pain on the thumb side of your wrist, you likely have de Quervain tenosynovitis. Treatment : ● Topical or oral NSAID (nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs). ● PPI. ● Vit-B (Cyanocobalamin) ± calcium ± vit –D. ● Surgery (if medical treatment failed). Advice : ○ Applying ice to the affected area. ○ Avoiding repetitive thumb movements as much as possible. ○ Avoiding pinching with the thumb when moving the wrist from side to side. ○ Immobilizing the thumb and wrist, keeping them straight with a splint or brace to help rest the tendons.

treatment.title: Treatment principle
treatment.description: Antibiotic : Alternative antibiotics (empirically for Gram +ve bacteria) : ○ Co-amoxiclav or, Amoxicillin & Clavulanic Acid / Tyclav (bex) / Augment (skf) 665 mg (1+1+1 --- 7 days). ● Nasal decongestant : ○ N/D Oxymetazoline 0.05 % (adult, child > 6 yr) / 0.025% (child of 2-6yrs) / Afrin (aris) / Rynex (incp) / Oxynex (ops) / Xylocon (sq), 2 – 3 drops in each nostril twice daily for 3 – 5 days. Or, ○ N/D Xylometazoline 0.1% (adult & >6 yr) or 0.05% (½ - 6 years) / Antazol (sq) / Rhinozol (acm) / Xylomet (ops), 2 or 3 drops in each nostril two to three times daily for 3 – 7 days. Gurgling agent : Used for local irritation relief. ○ Listacare Blue Mint Mouthwash, twice daily for 10 days. Other home remedy gurgling agent : ○ আধা গ্লাস কুসুম গরম পানিতে ২-৩ চামচ লবণ বা আদা বা লবণ-আদা মিশিয়ে দিনে ৩-৪ বার গড়গড়া করবেন ------ ৭ দিন

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: Foreign body (FB) aspiration / FB in Trachea or bronchus
treatment.description: 3 stages : ● Initial event : Cough, choking, gagging. ● Asymptomatic interval: seen by physician, may be asymptomatic. ● Complication: obstruction, erosion, infection causes fever, cough, hemoptysis, pneumonia, atelectasis. Inv.: Immediate chest X-ray – atelectasis, FB. If indicated CT scan of chest. Treatment : Bronchoscopic removal.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : ● Unilateral severe pain. ● Odynophagia. ● Hot potato voice (muffled voice). ● Foul breathing. ● Deviator of tongue. Inv.: X-ray Nasopharynx (L/A) view. Counselling : Young children have a lot of throats infectious, and most will 'grow out’ of the problem without the need for surgery.

treatment.title: Indications
treatment.description: Indication of tonsillectomy – ● Recurrent Tonsillitis / Throat infection : Repeated attacks of sore throat- 4-5 times/year- for >2 consecutive years. - 7 + episodes in last 1 year or, - 5 + episodes each year for 2 years or, - 3 + episodes each year for 3 years or, - > 2 weeks/year lost school or work. ● Chronic Tonsillitis (> 3 months). ● Recurrent peri-tonsillar abscess / quinsy. (4 to 6 weeks after 2nd attack). ● Hypertrophy of adenoids/tonsils : Airway obstruction, Difficulty in deglutition, Interference with speech. E.g., Huge enlargement of tonsil producing sleep apnoea syndrome, interference to swallowing & respiration. ● Suspicion of malignancy (unilaterally enlarge tonsil). Ulcer over tonsil suspected for malignancy. In short, Paradise criteria for tonsillectomy : - 7 episodes in previous year. - 5 episodes in each previous 2 years. - 3 episodes in each previous 3 years. - Peritonsillar abscess. - Recurrent with school loss, much antibiotic requirement. - Asymmetrical tonsil (malignancy suspicion). Indication of Adenoidectomy : - Nasal obstruction with mouth breathing. - Loud snoring suggesting sleep disordered breathing. - Upper airway obstruction causing craniofacial / occlusive developmental abnormalities Tonsillectomy and adenoidectomy : Above indications of tonsillectomy and adenoidectomy+ Failure to thrive, speech abnormalities, cor-pulmonale, school miss, very frequent requirement of antibiotic. Contraindications of tonsillectomy : ● Active infection / exacerbations (e.g., Acute Tonsillitis). ● Bleeding & clotting disorders. ● Cervical spine pathology. ● Endemic of Poliomyelitis. ● Failure to control systemic disease like Hypertension (HTN), DM, Bronchial Asthma (BA).

treatment.title: Approach to pregnant lady in Outdoor (OPD)
treatment.disease_name: Approach to pregnant lady in Outdoor (OPD)
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : - Amenorrhoea for ___ weeks / ___ months. - H/O TT immunization done or not*. - Pulse. - Blood pressure*. - LMP*. - EDD by calendar method*. [Calculation of EDD = 1 st date of last menstrual period (LMP) + 9 calendar month & 7 days.]

treatment.title: কিশোরী / মহিলাদের টিটি (টিটেনাস টক্সয়েড) ভ্যাক্সিন
treatment.disease_name: Approach to pregnant lady in Outdoor (OPD)
treatment.description: • রোগের নাম : ধনুষ্টংকার. • টিকার নাম : টিটি (টিটেনাস টক্সয়েড / Td). • টিকার ডোজ : ০.৫ এম এল. • টিকাদানের স্থান : বাহুর উপরের অংশে. • টিকার প্রয়োগ পথ : মাংশপেশী (I/M). Td vaccination schedule for women of 15-18 years Dose Starting dose / Dose interval Duration of protection Td-1 15 years completed No protection Td-2 At least 4 weeks after Td-1 3 years Td-3 At least 6 months after Td-2 5 years Td-4 At least 1 year after Td-3 10 years Td-5 At least 1 year after Td-4 Child bearing age There is only minimum interval but no maximum interval in TT or Td immunization. বি.দ্র. : দুই টিটি’র ডোজের মধ্যে অবশ্যই ন্যূনতম বিরতি দিতে হবে। তবে কোনো সর্বোচ্চ বিরতির কোনো বাঁধা সময়কাল নাই। সরকারী হাসপাতাল ও মার্তৃ-শিশু কেন্দ্রে বা কিছু স্বাস্থ্য এন.জি.ও.-তে কিশোরী / মহিলাদের বিনামূল্যে টিটি টিকা দেওয়া হয়। তাই টিকা কার্ড অনুযায়ী নির্দিষ্ট তারিখে বা তারিখের পরে যথাসম্ভব শীঘ্রই টিকা নিয়ে নেওয়া উত্তম।

treatment.title: Td vaccination problem solving
treatment.disease_name: Approach to pregnant lady in Outdoor (OPD)
treatment.description: Problem solving quiz : A lady came to a GP at her 2 nd pregnancy. During her first pregnancy she received 2 dose of TT & conceived after 2 years. She does not received TT vaccination before her first pregnancy. Now would you suggest her further 2 dose of TT in 2 nd pregnancy? As per general rule, two dose of TT is advised during pregnancy (pg) at 16-20 weeks of Pg & after 4 weeks at 20-24 weeks of pg, in a lady with no history of TT immunization. In this scenario, Dose Starting dose / Dose interval In this scenario Duration of protection Td-1 - In 1 st pg (16-20 W) No protection Td-2 After 4 weeks In 1 st pg (20-24 W) Next 3 years Td-3 After 6 months In 2 nd pg (16-20 W) Next 5 years Td-4 After 1 year Next 10 years Td-5 After 1 year Next 25 – 30 years If she comes to a GP with an EDD before 3 years of 2 nd dose of Td, she needs no new dose of vaccination. Preferably only one dose of Td (it is Td-3) is given as it exceeds 6 months after Td-2. This new Td-3 dose will protect further 5 years.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: H/O amenorrhea & willing to conceive. Adv. : Urine for pg test / Beta hCG, USG of LA. ► Tab. Folic acid 5 mg+Zinc 20 mg/ Folneed (incp)/ Folic-Z (ops)/ Zifolet (sq) / ZnF (aris) / ESP (ori), 0+0+1 (after meal) ----- continue [ ☼ Pregnancy (pg) strip test is a good assumptive test. One line denotes non-pregnant, two tines (even fade 2 nd line) confirms beta hCG, most probably pregnant. USG of LA – gestational sac is earliest sign of pg, further USG of PP – showing (yolk sac) fetal pole & heart rate is confirmatory of true pregnancy. USG a must at 4-6 th week of gestation to exclude blighted ovum or molar pg. Early pregnancy structures (Chronologically) Sono-embryology Trans abdominal (TAS) Trans vaginal (TVS) Gestational sac 5.5 Weeks 5 Weeks Yolk sac 6 Weeks 5.5 Weeks Embryo (fetal pole) 6.5 Weeks 6 Weeks Cardiac activity 7 Weeks 6.5 Weeks

treatment.title: Blighted ovum / Anembryonic gestation
treatment.description: ☼ Blighted ovum / Anembryonic gestation : lf gestational sac size is >25 mm but inside of gestational sac there is no yolk or embryo. ☼ WHO ANC guideline, an ultrasound scan before 24 weeks of gestation is recommended for all pregnant women to estimate gestational age, detect fetal anomalies and multiple pregnancies, enhance the maternal pregnancy experience. Necessity of USG will also depend on patient’s condition. ☼ Anomaly scan or ultrasound for foetal anomalies can be done any time between 16 – 20 weeks plus six days. ☼ Allylestrenol is a synthetic progestogen used to treat miscarriages and preterm labor in women. It is not recommended for use in female under the age of 16 years. Indications are – 1. Premature labor 2. Miscarriage 3. Habitual abortion 4. Intrauterine growth retardation 5. Failure of implantation In case of a missed dose, it should be taken as soon as the patient remembers & she should continue the regular dosing schedule. A double dose is not recommended. Contra-indications : 1. Breast Cancer H/O breasts like lumps 2. Severe liver disease 3. Undiagnosed vaginal bleeding 4. Toxaemia of Pregnancy 5. Crisis, Seizures, Migraines

treatment.title: Antenatal Visit
treatment.description: Must record at 1 st visits : - Time of amenorrhea. - H/O TT immunization done or not. - Weight & height. - H/O of cardiac / renal / liver / hormonal dysfunction. - H/O recurrent abortion / obstetric events. - Identify risk factors & refer to specialist. - Pulse & Blood pressure. - LMP [1 st date of last menstruation, e.g., bleeding starting (calendar) date of last menstrual period]. - Estimated date of delivery (EDD) by calendar method. [Calculation of EDD = LMP date + 9 calendar month & 7 days] Investigation at 1 st visit : 1. Blood grouping & Rh typing 2. CBC, Hb%. 3. RBS (or FBS/2hr PPBS) / Random blood sugar with corresponding urinary sugar (RBS c̄ CUS). 4. HBsAg 5. VDRL 6. USG of L/A (Lower Abdomen) [Ultra sonogram (USG) of Pregnancy Profile (PP)] 7. Urine routine & microscopic examination (U/R/M/E) ± 8. Thyroid screening : S. TSH Investigation in subsequent visits : 1. Urine R/M/E (urine for albumin and sugar) 2. Hb% 3. RBS 4. USG of PP (Pregnancy Profile) Advice for pg lady (patient education) : 1. Eat fresh fruits and vegetables. Identify if there are important taboos about foods which are nutritionally important for good health. Advise the woman against these taboos. Avoid travel / long journey, avoid heavy exercise. 2. Advise the woman on how to prepare for delivery, when to go, what to bring and where to go in emergency. 3. Explain the danger signals when she should report to the health center immediately, day or night, without waiting such as a) Vaginal bleeding b) Convulsion c) Severe headache with blurred vision d) Severe abdominal pain e) Fast or difficult breathing f) Escape of fluid from vagina or change in frequency or intensity of foetal movements g) Persistent vomiting or decreased urine output. 4. Explain about black staining of stool due to oral iron, therefore, not to worry about it. 5. Iron (on night) and calcium supplements should be taken at different times of the day or at least 2 hours apart. 6. Discuss birth spacing (at-least 3 years) after delivery and counsel on the importance of family planning. Give advice on correct and consistent use of condoms for dual protection from sexually transmitted infections or HIV and pregnancy. 7. Avoid coital act (except at 2 nd trimester). Sexual intercourse should be avoided when abortion/preterm labour threaten and during the last 4 weeks of pregnancy. 8. Bowel: Constipation, fissures and haemorrhoids are common during pregnancy and should be treated with fluids, bulk laxatives and stool softeners. 9. Smoking, alcoholism and drugs of abuse are contraindicated. Caffeine is best avoided. 10. Daily foetal movement charting (DFMC): After 28 weeks, the woman should keep foetal movement count. 11. Follow up after 2-3 months or at least 4 times in pregnancy period. Follow up visits (WHO) : At least 4 visits ¤ 1 st : Within 16 weeks (4 months) ¤ 2 nd : Between 24-28 weeks ¤ 3 rd : At 32 weeks ¤ 4 th : At 36 weeks

treatment.title: 1st trimester
treatment.description: 1 st trimester : ► Tab. Folic acid 5 mg+ Zinc 20 mg/ Folneed (incp)/ Folic-Z (ops)/ Zifolet (sq) / ZnF (aris) / ESP (ori), 0+0+1 (after meal) ---- continue ► Tab. Pantoprazole 20 mg / Pantobex (bex) / Pantonix (incp) / Pantid (ops) / Pansec (drg), 1+0+1 (30 min before meal) --- continue ± PREGNANCY WITH PV BLEEDING : If high risk pregnancy (repeated abortion), add as per need – ► Cap. Progesterone 100 or 200 mg / Microgest (ren) / Mygest (drg) / Utrogestan (zas) / Utrogest (zis), 100 mg or 200 mg 2 to 3 soft gel capsules daily ----- continue Or, ► Tab. Allylestrenol 5 mg / Gestrenol (ren) / Geston (sq) / Allygest (nuv), 1 + 1 + 1 ---- continue Advice : If bleeding becomes severe or passage of fleshy mass, repeat the USG of PP and get hospitalized. [ ☼ Usually a pregnant mother suffers from severe GIT upset, nausea-vomiting-constipation, so a physician may avoid iron & calcium in her first trimester (till 12 weeks / 3 months), and starts in her 2 nd trimester & on. But guidelines of “The Institute of Public Health (IPH)” [Mohakhali, Bangladesh] suggest to start folic acid, iron & calcium from the planning of conception to (at-least) 3 months after delivery. Iron at night after meal & calcium morning + noon dose after meal is advised, some study suggests a weak relation between night intake of calcium with renal calculi. If nausea-vomiting-constipation is prominent, a GP may use folic acid, zinc, omitting calcium (Ca) & Iron (Fe). ☼ Folic acid in 1 st trimester prevents neural tube defects in newborn. 1 st trimester commonly associated with nausea vomiting so anti-emetics (meclizine) must be given. Avoid iron & calcium if it does aggravate nausea, vomiting & constipation. ☼ If the tests revealed reduce levels of hormones, treatment of threatened abortion with progesterone injections might also be undertaken to bring them to the required amount. ¤ Injection Hydroxyprogesterone Caproate 250mg/1ml or 500mg/2ml / HPC (pop) / Hydrogest DS (ren) / Caprogen DS (nuv), a dose of 500 mg or 250 mg I/M once weekly for 4-8 weeks. ☼ Time of delivery : • Usually planned C/S between 38 – 39 weeks of pregnancy if not otherwise indicated. • When C/S is indicated prior to 37 weeks’ - corticosteroids to the mother should be considered.]

treatment.title: 2nd & 3rd trimester
treatment.description: 2nd & 3rd trimester (up to full term pregnancy / FTP) : ► Tab. Carbonyl Iron + Folic Acid + Zinc + Vit-B Complex + Vit-C / Zif Forte (sq) / EPL Plus (glob) / ForMum (rad) / Prenat-XL (hc) / Momneed (acm) / Zilvit (skf) / Xvit (bacn), 0+0+1 (A/M) – continue ► Tab. Calcium Carbonate 500 mg [Elemental source] + Vit-D3 200 IU / Calbo-D (sq) / Rocal-D (hc) / Calcin-D (ren) / Ostocal D (skf), 1 + 1 + 0 (A/M, after meal) ----- continue ► Tab. Pantoprazole 20 mg / Pantobex (bex) / Pantonix (incp) / Pantid (ops) / Pansec (drg) 20 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 (30 min before meal) ----- continue as per need ± ► If high risk pregnancy (repeated abortion), add – Tab. Allylestrenol 5 mg / Gestrenol (ren) / Geston (sq) / Allygest (nuv) / Allynol (pop), 1 + 1 + 1 ---- continue

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : H/O – Amenorrhoea for ___ weeks (Usually in 1st trimester). – Vomiting for several times. – Dehydration may be present. O/E : - BP & Pulse. - Dehydration. - Anemia. - Jaundice. - Edema. Δ ____th gravida c̄ ____weeks pregnancy c̄ hyperemesis gravidarum. Adv. : ▸ S. Electrolytes (if dehydration presents). ¤ NPO-TFO. ¤ Inf. Hartsol 2L + 5% DNS 1L ± Inj. Vitamin B 50 forte 1 amp in each liter fluid, I/V @30 drops/min. ¤ Inj. Ondansetron 8mg/4ml / Emistat (hc) / Ofran (sq) / Anset (ops) / Zofra (skf), 1 amp IV stat & sos or TDS. Or, ¤ Inj. Palonosetron 0.075 mg/1.5 ml or 0.25 mg/5 ml / Paloxi (bacn) / Paloron (zis) / Paloset (sq) / Palzen (hc) / Vomiren (ren), 1.5 ml I/V stat & sos. ¤ Inj. Pantoprazole 40 mg / Pantid (ops) / Pantonix (incp), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. Or, ¤ Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg / PPI (acm) / Seclo (sq) / Cosec (drg), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ☼ Alternate anti-emetic : ¤ Inj. Prochlorperazine 12.5mg/ml / vergon (ops), 1 amp IV stat & TDS Or, ¤ Inj. Metoclopramide 5mg/5ml / Meclid (jay) / Motilon (syn), 1 amp IV stat & TDS

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Most (more than 80% affected) common in 2 nd & 3 rd months of pg (1 st trimester). Symptoms usually improve by 14 th – 16 th weeks. Nausea and vomiting (NV) in pregnancy, often known as morning sickness, is very common in early pregnancy. Though it is termed as morning but can occur any time of day and made worse by odour associated with preparation / sight of food. Management of nausea and vomiting in Pg : 1. Reassure the mother that it is a normal part of pregnancy. It will be resolved spontaneously after 1 st trimester. 2. Adjust lifestyle & advice frequent small meals – avoid greasy / spicy foods; eat foods which patient like most. 4. Maintain fluid intake – small sips / amounts frequently. 5. If severe / disabling consider anti-emetics e.g. meclizine hydrochloride, metoclopramide, cyclizine, ondansetron, palonosetron; any of those can be used. Suppositories are an effective method of administration if oral route is not tolerated. 6. If dehydrated or weight loss more than 2 Kg (hyperemesis gravidarum) admit / hospitalization for rehydration. Drug treatment : ► Tab. Pyridoxine + Doxylamine (10+10 or, (20+20) mg / Dicliz Plus (aris) / Vertina-D (sq) / Vertina-DX (sq) / Pyridox (incp), 1 + 0 + 1 (before meal) ---- 10 – 30 days [Or, if needed, 1 + 1 + 1 ( ½ hr before meal) --- 10 – 30 days] Or, Tab. Meclizine + Pyridoxine (25+50 mg) / Acliz plus (aris) / Vertina plus (sq) / Emenil plus (incp)/ Avert plus (bio) / Mecliz plus (nipa), 1 + 0 + 1 (before meal) ----- 10 – 30 days [Or, if needed, 1 + 1 + 1 ( ½ hr before meal) ---- 10 – 30 days] ± ► Tab. Pantoprazole 20 mg / Pansec (drg) / Pantobex (bex), 1+0+1 ( ½ hr before meal), if hyper-acidity ---- 10 – 30 days ± ► ORS, (1 sachet ORS + 500 mL drinking water), after each vomiting, as much patient can intake comfortably. Hyperemesis gravidarum (HG) is a pregnancy complication that is characterized by severe nausea, vomiting, weight loss, and possibly dehydration. Sign of dehydration : dry tongue, skin pinch, low blood pressure or a fast pulse etc. Exclusion of other causes e.g.; UTI, Overactive Thyroid, Twin / Multiple pregnancy, Trophoblastic / Molar pregnancy. Investigations : ➢ CBC and urine R/M/E; ➢ Thyroid test – FT4, TSH. ➢ Liver function test – serum bilirubin, SGPT; ➢ Renal function test – Blood urea, S. Creatinine, S. Electrolytes. ➢ An ultrasound of whole abdomen – to exclude trophoblastic diseases, multiple pregnancy and other problem e.g., cholelithiasis. ► Severe or complicated cases must be referred to an obstetrician.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.disease_name: Epigastric discomfort or Heartburn during pregnancy
treatment.description: 1. Affects 70% of women in the third trimester. 2. Reassure that it is not harmful. 3. Advise low fat, bland food, small amount and frequent meals. 4. Don't go to bed within 2 hours of eating. If symptoms worse at night then avoid eating late at night and consider raising the head end of the bed (1-2 bricks under the bed). Or may use 2 pillows during sleep. 5. Avoid gastric irritants, e.g. caffeine. 6. Antacid preparations e.g. milk of magnesia is helpful if lifestyle modifications are ineffective. Magnesium Hydroxide is an anti-acid / antacid with laxative action. ► Sus. Magnesium Hydroxide / Magmil (paci), MOM (ops) / Syp./Sus. Milk of magnesia (acme/ori), 1 – 3 teaspoonful (5 to 15 ml) up to 4 times daily with water for ½ – 2 months. ± ► Any PPI : Tab. Rabeprazole 20 mg / Acifix (bex) / Rabeca (sq) / Finix (ops) / Paricel (aci) / Xorel (ibn), 1+0+1 ---- 1 – 3 months (40mg, if not controlled by 20 mg, Pantoprazole is the best alternate PPI option)

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Affects up to 40% of pregnant women. Due to increase progesteron level during pregnancy which relax the smooth muscle of the intestine. Treatment & Advice : 1. Adequate vegetable & water intake. Increase fluid and fiber intake. Exercise is helpful (30 minutes walking). 2. Reduce or stop iron supplementation. After delivery may switch to elemental Iron [as carbonyl iron], rest iron formulations cause more constipation. Calcium citrate (Pg cat – C, so no used) causes no constipation, rest calcium formulations do. 3. Regular drinking of (cow/goat) milk is useful as laxative & natural source of calcium. Again vegetables are rich in iron with high fiber causing no constipation. 4. If necessary use a bulk forming laxative, e.g. ispaghula husk. Ispaghula Husk [Psyllium] is bulk-forming mild laxatives that is safe in pregnancy & pedi patient. Lactulose can be used (pregnancy category – B). Avoid bowel stimulants (bisacodyl) as they increase uterine activity. 5. Stool softeners e.g. milk of magnesia may help. Treatment : ► Powder Ispaghula Husk [Psyllium] 3.5 gm/5.4 gm / Powder Fiberlax (incp) / Radigel-N (rad) / Gutgel (ops) / Laxadil-EP (drg), Take 1-2 spoonful with the provided spoon, Put this husk into an empty glass, Fill glass with water (150 ml or 2/3 of the glass) stir & drink immediately --- (regular at night) as per need ► Sus. Magnesium Hydroxide / Magmil (paci), MOM (ops) / Syp./Sus. Milk of magnesia (acme/ori), 1 – 3 teaspoonful (5 to 15 ml) up to 4 times daily with water for ½ – 2 months. ± ► Cap. Dexlansoprazole 30 or, 60 mg / Canalia (aris) / DDR (acm) / DLP (gen) / Dexlan (ibn) / Lanso D (sq) / Xanra (osl) / Onedex (one), 1+0+1 (B/M) ---- 1 – 2 months, (60 mg, if not controlled by 30 mg, Pantoprazole is the best alternate PPI option)

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Hemorrhoids are swollen veins in rectum that can cause itching, burning, pain, or bleeding. Common in pregnancy, especially in the 3rd trimester. Risks factors in pregnancy : • Increased abdominal Pressure causes venous engorgement. • Growing baby also puts pressure on the large veins behind uterus. • Constipation very common feature in pregnancy. Management : 1. Ease Pressure : Avoid prolonged standing or sitting. 2. Avoid constipation : High fiber foods : whole-grain breads and cereals, fruits, vegetables and beans. Taking plenty of water. A glass of prune juice may also help. 3. Soothe Irritation : Hip bath – several times a day to relieve pain. Apply an icepack or cold compress for 10 minutes up to four times a day to bring down swelling. Use unscented, non-alcohol wet wipes after defecation. 4. Medication in hemorrhoid in pregnancy : General drugs : ● Pain reliever – paracetamol 500 or 665 mg tds for 5-7 days. ● Stool softener – ¤ Isubgul powder 2tsp with water 2 times a day or, ¤ Syp. Magnesium hydroxide -2 tsf bd / tds for 5-7 days. Specific drugs : ● Topical medication : (Local anesthetics gel – Lidocaine & Hemorrhoid cream – Cinchocaine) ¤ Ointment Cinchocaine + Hydrocortisone + Neomycin + Esculin / Anustat (bex) / Anorel (pop) / Retocain (drg) / Erian (sq) A small quantity of the ointment should be applied with finger (nozzle for deep application) in the painful pruritic area in the morning and evening and after each stool for 15 – 30 days. ● Anti-hemorroid drug – (if persistent symptom) : ¤ Tab. Diosmin 450 mg + Hesperidin 50 mg / Normanal (ren) / Daflon (servier) / Hemorif (sq) / Sangril (hc), 2 + 2 + 2 for 7 days then, 2 + 0 + 2 for 7 days then, 1 + 0 + 1 for 14 days. [DS Tablet are Diosmin 900 mg + Hesperidin 100 mg]

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Anemia in pg is physiological & dilution anemia. But if suspected may advise CBC with PBF. HB% at ANC visits & continuation of oral iron up to 3 months after delivery are sufficient for anemia management. Δ ____th gravida ____week pregnancy c̄ Mild / Moderate / Severe anaemia Advice : 1. CBC c̄ PBF. 2. USG of PP (pregnancy profile). 3. Hb electrophoresis. If Hb >8gm/dl : continue or increase dose of oral iron preparation. May add with ongoing multi-mineral & multi-vitamin : ✔ Cap. Ferric Maltol 30 mg / Filofer (acm) / Malifa (nav) / FML (ori) / Adiron (gen) / Ferromax (aci), 1+0+1 ----- 3 to 12 months. If Hb 6 – 8 gm/dl : ✔ Inj. Iron Sucrose 100 mg/5 ml (or 200mg/10ml) / Xenofer (bacn) / Anefer (ops) / Kidifer (gen), 10 ml (or 2 amp) in Inf. N/S @10-15 d/m (100-200mg Iron) once to thrice a week depending on the hemoglobin level. If Hb Blood transfusion (BT).

treatment.title: Cough ± Fever
treatment.disease_name: Cough, Fever, Headache, Fatigue, Insomnia, Sweating, Helminth during pregnancy
treatment.description: ► Herbal cough syrup [Adhatoda Vasica]/ Adovas (sq)/Devas (drg) / Honeybas (acm) / Adolef (ops), 2 TSF 3 times daily –– 5-14 days ± ► Tab. Montelukast 10 mg / Lumona (skf) / Montela (del) / Reversair (aci) / M-lucas (pop) / Odmon (ren), 0+0+1 –– 5-14 days ► Tab. Paracetamol / Napa (bex) / ATP (gen) 5 mg, 1+1+1 – 3 days ► Tab.Azithromycin / Zimax (sq) / Adiz(euro) 500 mg, 0+0+1 – 5 days

treatment.title: Headache during pregnancy
treatment.disease_name: Cough, Fever, Headache, Fatigue, Insomnia, Sweating, Helminth during pregnancy
treatment.description: Usually headache during pregnancy is tension headache as migraine tends to decrease in pregnancy. ● Check BP and urine for protein to exclude pre-eclampsia. ● Treat with rest and analgesic (paracetamol). ► Tab. Paracetamol / Napa (bex) 500mg, 1+1+1+1 (A/M) ----- if headache present

treatment.title: Fatigue during pregnancy
treatment.disease_name: Cough, Fever, Headache, Fatigue, Insomnia, Sweating, Helminth during pregnancy
treatment.description: It is the almost universal symptom of early pregnancy. Reaches peak at 12 – 15 weeks. In late pregnancy due to increase physical effort needed to do everyday tasks and sleep deprivation. ● Exclude anaemia, DM and hypothyroidism, if nothing found then just reassure, advice rest and adjustment of lifestyle.

treatment.title: Insomnia during pregnancy
treatment.disease_name: Cough, Fever, Headache, Fatigue, Insomnia, Sweating, Helminth during pregnancy
treatment.description: 1. Avoid sedative drug treatment. 2. Reassure & Relaxation techniques and mild physical exercise prior to sleep can help. 3. Non-pharmacological measures may help. - Avoid day time nap or sleep. - Avoid tea & coffee (after early evening). - Do tolerable physical exercises. - Avoid TV & mobile screen in bed time (after late evening). - Bedroom should be used only for sleep not for eating, watching screen & recreations.

treatment.title: Sweating and feeling hot
treatment.disease_name: Cough, Fever, Headache, Fatigue, Insomnia, Sweating, Helminth during pregnancy
treatment.description: Measure temperature to exclude fever. If apyrexial, reassure as normal symptom of pregnancy. If pyrexial / fever then identify the source and treat the infection.

treatment.title: Helminth infestation during pregnancy
treatment.disease_name: Cough, Fever, Headache, Fatigue, Insomnia, Sweating, Helminth during pregnancy
treatment.description: ✔ Routine deworming is not recommended during pregnancy. Anthelminthic (albendazole and mebendazole) can be used in documented helminthiasis during pregnancy or where prevalence of anemia is high during pregnancy. ✔ Anti-helminthic should be avoided in 1 st trimester, can be used in 2 nd and 3 rd trimester. ✔ In documented helminth infestation treatment given with albendazole 400 mg (T. Alben DS, single dose & may be repeat after 2 week) stat or mebendazole (T. Solas) 100 mg/dose total 6 doses (twice daily for 3 days).

treatment.title: Management
treatment.disease_name: Low Back Pain (LBP) during pregnancy / Leg cramps in pregnancy
treatment.description: Affects 60% of pregnant women, usually from the 2nd trimester onwards and worse in the evenings, may interfere with sleep / activities. This is due to weight gain & change in spine curvature during pregnancy. Management : Avoid bad posture, bending, weight lifting etc. Take adequate rest, but also encouraged to have light exercise (unless contraindicated e.g. pre-eclampsia), avoid NSAIDs & treat with simple analgesia, e.g. paracetamol. ► Tab. Paracetamol / Napa (bex) / Renova (ops) / ATP (gen) / Fast (acm) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 (A/M, if pain) ----- 5 – 30 days ► Tab. Calcium Carbonate 500 mg [Elemental source] + Vit-D3 200 IU / Calbo-D (sq) / Rocal-D (hc) / Calcin-D (ren) / Ostocal D (skf), 1 + 1 + 0 (A/M, after meal) ----- continue ► Tab. Vitamin B1 100 mg + B6 200 mg + B12 200 mcg / Neurocare (bex) / TPC (acm) / Neurobest (aris) / B126 (pop) / Povital (aci) / Neucos-B (rad), 1 + 0 + 1 ----- 1 – 3 months

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: ¤ Abnormalities of sensation (e.g. tingling, pins and needles) of hands / feet. Symptoms usually resolve after delivery. ¤ Reassure & Pyridoxine (vit-B6) may help (25 mg/day). ► Tab. Vitamin B1 100 mg + B6 200 mg + B12 200 mcg / Neuro-B (sq) / 3 Bion (Jen) / Supra-B (drg) / B126 (pop) / Povital (aci) / Neucos-B (rad) / Vitabion (incp) , 1 + 0 + 1 ----- 1 – 3 months

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Edema is common and usually benign (physiologic) during late pregnancy. Fluid retention affects commonly at lower limbs / ankles, hands / fingers, face. If edema involves only one leg, particularly when redness, warmth, and tenderness are present, DVT and cellulitis should be considered. If severe / sudden increase in edema, exclude pre-eclampsia (check BP, dipstick urine for protein). Advice : Physiologic edema is reduced by lying in the left lateral decubitus position, elevating the lower extremities, using compression stockings, and wearing loose clothing that does not restrict blood flow. Increased protein containing diet. Simply taking rest & elevation of limb by pillow below leg during rest & sleep can reduce oedema. Use of diuretic to treat edema in pregnancy : Usually not recommended, because it reduces amount of amniotic fluid.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Varicose veins are a common, usually harmless part of pregnancy for some women. They happen when the uterus applies pressure to the large vein (the inferior vena cava) that carries blood back to the heart from your feet and legs. 1. Cause aching legs, fatigue, itch and ankle/foot swelling. 2. Early morning swollen ankles indicate pathological edema, exclude pre-eclampsia (check BP, dipstick urine for proteinuria). 3. Elevate legs when sitting, provide support stockings and encourage walking / discourage standing still. 4. If develop complications e.g. thrombophlebitis then sholud be treated with antibiotic, ice packs, elevation, support stockings and analgesia. Elevation of limb during rest & elastic crepe bandage is must. Complicated cases must be referred to obstetrician & vascular surgeon.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: ● Bed rest at left / right lateral position. ● Plenty of water, liquid diet orally ● IV Fluid liter day — if admitted. ☼ Pregcare Oral Powder (purnava) 1 sachet+1 glass of water 2 times daily for 4 weeks ☼ Oral rehydration therapy : ORS. ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone 5mg/ml / Dexa (ren) / Roxadex (nuv), 2½ amp IM stat and repeat after 12 hours Advice : Follow up USG of PP after 1 – 4 weeks. ► Refer & hospitalization if oligohydramnios (USG : AFI

treatment.title: Definitions
treatment.description: Hypertensive (HTN) disorders during pregnancy Pre-existing hypertension Hypertension prior to pregnancy or occurring before 20 weeks of gestation. Gestational hypertension (G-HTN) ● BP ≥ 140/90 mmHg after 20 wks. Blood pressure ≥ 140/90 mmHg on two separate occasions, at least 6 hours apart, after 20 weeks of gestation. Pre-eclampsia (PET) ● BP ≥ 140/90 mmHg after 20 wks. ● Proteinuria, ● ± Maternal organ or uteroplacental dysfunction. Eclampsia ● BP ≥ 140/90 mmHg after 20 wks. ● Proteinuria, ● Maternal organ or uteroplacental dysfunction. ● Convulsion / seizures. ‘Pregnancy-induced hypertension (PIH)’ includes — (i) Gestational hypertension, (ii) Pre-eclampsia, and (iii) Eclampsia.

treatment.title: Gestational Hypertension
treatment.description: Definition : Gestational hypertension is defined as having a blood pressure greater than 140/90 mmHg on two occasions at least 6 hours apart after 20 weeks' gestation without the presence of protein in the urine. Treatment : Supportive Measures : control of edema (salt restriction in diet, leg rising during rest or sleep), treatment of associated conditions such as diabetes, renal disease, thrombophilia etc. Control of blood pressure : by antihypertensive. Labetalol : Starting oral dose – 100 mg twice daily, Maintenance dose – 200-400 mg twice daily, Severely high blood pressure –up to 1.2 to 2.4 g daily. Methyledopa : 250 mg bid up to 2000 mg/day Nifedipine – 10-20 mg tds daily [Calcium channel blocker (if no other choice)] [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 140] Brand names : ► Tab. Sardopa (incp) / Dopegyt (ambee), 250 or 500 mg, two or three times daily. Or, ► Tab. Labeta (bex) / Labegest (incp) / Betagest (ops) / Labecard (pop), 100 or 200 mg, 1 to 2 tab daily. Or, ► Tab. Nifedipine 10 or 20 mg / Nidipine SR (sq) – (20 mg) 1 tab bd / Nifin (acm) & Nifecap (drg) – (10 mg) 1 tab tds daily. Or, ► Tab. Amdocal (bex)/ Amlopin (acm)/ Camlodin (sq) / Amlocard (drg) / Amlotab (incp) 5 mg (1 to 2 tab) or 10 mg 1 tab daily.

treatment.title: PRE-ECLAMPSIA
treatment.description: Pre-eclampsia is toxemia of pregnancy (PET). Better to refer to obstetrician, not to treat by an GP. Criteria for diagnosis – 1. BP > 140/90 or, >+30/+15 from baseline BP at least 6 hours interval or, or BP ≥ 160/110 mm of Hg in single reading. 2. Proteinuria ≥ 0.3gm/24 hours. Urine dipstick is a useful screening tool, if ≥ 1+ protein then probably significant. 3. Nondependent oedema after 20 weeks of pregnancy even after 12 hours of rest or rapid gain of weight >0.5kg/week. Risk factors for pre-eclampsia : Moderate risk : 1. First pregnancy. 2. Age ≥ 40 years or 3. Pregnancy interval > 10 years. 4. BMI ≥ 35kg/m 2 at first visit. 5. Family history of pre-eclampsia. 6. Multiple gestation or pregnancy. High risk : 1. Hypertensive disease during previous pregnancy 2. Chronic kidney disease 3. Placental abnormalities: Hyperplacentosis, placental ischemia. 4. Thrombophilias (anti-phospholipid syndrome, protein C/S deficiency, factor V Leiden deficiency, SLE) 5. Past history of diabetes mellitus, hypertension. Type 1 or type 2 DM, chronic hypertension. If ≥ 1 high risk factor or ≥ 2 moderate risk factors, advise aspirin 75 mg from 12 weeks to birth and refer for specialist obstetric care. Interval for routine BP check-up : Pre-eclampsia is asymptomatic until its terminal phase and onset may be rapid. Frequent BP screening is essential. Check urine for protein at the time of each time blood pressure measurement. 1. If no risk factors for pre-eclampsia then routine antenatal care. 2. If one moderate risk factor for pre-eclampsia, recheck BP at least every 3 weeks from 24-32 weeks of gestation and at least every 2 weeks after 32 weeks of gestation. 3. If ≥ 1 high risk factor or ≥ 2 moderate risk factors, monitor as directed by the specialist. Investigations : • Blood Grouping (if not done previously) • S. Electrolytes • Blood urea, S. creatinine. • CBC • SGPT, S. Bilirubin • USG of pregnancy profile with BPP and colour Doppler study (if needed). • Coagulation profile : BT, CT, PT with INR, APTT, Fibrinogen, FDP, d-dimer • Urine RME Control of Blood Pressure in pre-eclampia : If Diastolic BP ≥ 110 mmHg : IV drug is needed & refer to specialist. If Diastolic BP • α -Methyl-dopa (500 mg – 2 gm)/day : Or, • Calcium Channel Blocker (CCB) 5 mg twice daily. Or, • Labetalol 200 mg once or twice daily. Following antihypertensive drugs are safe to use during pregnancy: 1. Methyldopa up to 2000 mg/day 2. Labetalol initially 100 mg BD can increase to 2.4 gm/day 3. Nifedipine 10-20 mg BD maximum 90 mg/day 4. Amlodipine 5-10 mg/day Use of aspirin in pregnancy 1. To prevent preeclampsia and eclampsia (should be initiated between 12 weeks and continued daily until delivery). 2. Low dose aspirin prophylaxis should be considered if more than one of several moderate risk factors for pre-eclampsia. 3. Also indicated in antiphospholipid syndrome ► Intravenous dose if diastolic BP ≥ 110 mmHg : ¤ Labetalol : Intravenous dose – 20 mg injected over 2 minutes. Additional injections – 40 or 80 mg every 10 minutes up to 300 mg. Infusion – 500 mg in 250ml N S @ 8-10 d/min. ¤ Hydralazine : I/V bolus 5-10mg every 20 minutes up to maximum dose of 30 mg. Infusion – 20 mg in 200ml N S @ 8-10 d/min.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: H/O : • A pregnant woman has elevated blood pressure. • A pregnant woman or a woman who recently delivered complains of severe headache or blurred vision. O / E : - Blood pressure : ≥ 140/90 mmHg (after the 20th week in a previously normotensive and non-proteinuric woman). - Pulse. - Anemia (-) / (+). - Edema : Pitting (+) / (++) / (+++) or, may (-). Categorization : 1. Gestational hypertension : > 140/90 mmHg without proteinuria. 2. Mild pre-eclampsia : • BP ≥ 140/90 mm of Hg but • Proteinuria (urinary albumin) trace to 1+. • Minimal elevation of liver enzymes. 3. Severe pre-eclampsia : • BP ≥ 160/110 mm of Hg or higher on 2 occasions at least 6 hours apart. • Proteinuria 3+ or more on 2 occasions 4 hours apart. • DBP > 90 mmHg + severe headache / blurring of vision / epigastric pain / vomiting + Proteinuria 2+ or more. ___ Gravida ___ weeks pg c̄ Antepartum / Postpartum Gestational hypertension / Pre-eclampsia (Mild) Δ PET (Mild) ¤ Diet : Normal. ¤ Bed rest. ¤ Tab. Methyldopa / Dopegyt (ambee) / Sardopa (incp) 250 mg, (500 – 2000 mg/day), 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 ± ¤ Tab. Nifedipine / Nifin (acm) 10 mg / Nidipine SR (sq) 20 mg, (1 + 1 + 1) or, 1 tab S/L stat (if BP is not reduced) ± ¤ Tab. Labetalol / Betagest (ops) / Labeta (bex) 200 mg (200 – 2000 mg/day), 1 + 0 + 1 or 0 + 0 + 1 ¤ Tab. Rabeprazole 20 mg / Finix (ops) / Acifix (bex) / Xorel (ibn), 1 + 0 + 1 ¤ Tab. Calcium, 1+1+0. ¤ Tab. Ferrous Sulphate (F/S), 0+0+1 ± ¤ Tab. Vitamin-B complex (B/C), 1+0+1. ► Advice : 1. CBC. 2. Blood grouping and Rh typing. 3. Serum Creatinine, S. urea, 4. S. Electrolytes. 5. S. Bilirubin. 6. SGPT. 7. Urine for protein (albumin). 8. Bedside clotting test. ► Monitoring : Every ½ hour • BP & Pulse : • Urine output : • Respiratory rate : • Patellar reflex : ► In Severe Pre-eclampsia (PET) c̄ no convulsion : ¤ Inf. Magnesium Sulphate (4 gm/100 ml) / Nalepsin (bex) / G-Mag Sulph (gono), Loading dose : 1 bottle I/V stat over 10-15 min. Maintenance dose : not needed. ¤ Continuous catheterization. ¤ Or, Ampoule : Inj. MgSO 4 2.47g/5ml / G-Mag Sulph (gono) / Magsum (ren) / Eclamag (incp), Loading dose : 8ml MgSO 4 + 12ml DW, I/V stat over 10-15 min. Maintenance dose : not needed. ¤ Continuous catheterization. ► Checklist for maintenance dose of Magnesium Sulfate – 1. Urine output > 30 ml per hour. 2. Respiratory rate > 16 breath/min. 3. Patellar reflex present. ► IV anti-hypertensive : Labetalol : If DBP > 110 mmHg, use IV Labetalol anti-HTN instead of oral. Intravenous dose – 20 mg injected over 2 minutes. Additional injections – 40 or 80 mg every 10 minutes up to 300 mg. Infusion – 500 mg in 250ml N S @ 8-10 d/min. ¤ Inj. Labetalol (50mg/10ml = 1 amp) / Labeta (bex) / Labegest (incp) / Betagest (ops) / Labecard (pop), 4 ml I/V very slowly (diluted c̄ 6 ml D/W) every 15 minutes until DBP 90 mm of Hg. Or, In infusion : 5 ampule / 250 mg in 250 ml Normal Saline, I/V @ 20mg/hr or 5 to 10 d/m, check BP at 20 min interval, if DBP ≤ 90 mmHg, then stop drip & stat oral anti-HTN. ► Antibiotic : only if indicated : ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Dicephin (drg) 1 g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ► If lung crepitation presents, then add – ¤ Inj. Furosemide 20 mg/2ml / Lasix (syn) / Fusid (sq), 2 amp I/V stat & sos.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnosis of Severe Pre-eclampsia / Eclampsia : • BP > 140/90 mm Hg. • Proteinuria ≥ 2+. • Severe headache. • Blurring of vision / Visual disturbance. • Epigastric or right hypochondriac pain. • Pulmonary oedema or cyanosis. • H/O convulsion (single or more). • Unconsciousness/ ↓ Level of consciousness. • Oliguria / ↓ Urinary output ( ___ Gravida ___ weeks pg c̄ Antepartum / Postpartum Eclampsia / Severe Pre-eclampsia Adv. : • CBC c̄ platelet count. • Blood Grouping. • S. Electrolytes. • S. creatinine. • Blood urea. • S. Bilirubin • SGPT. • Urine R/M/E. • Heat coagulation test (HCT) [bed side]. • Coagulation profile : BT, CT, PT with INR, APTT, Fibrinogen, FDP, d-dimer. • USG of pregnancy profile with BPP. • Colour Doppler study (if needed). Δ Antepartum / Postpartum Eclampsia ¤ ABCDE Resuscitation. ¤ Lie her on floor (to avoid injury). ¤ NPO-TFO. ¤ Eclamptic / Left lateral position. ¤ O 2 inhalation sos. ¤ O-P suction sos. ¤ Inf. NS or Hartsol 1L, I/V @40 drops/min. ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Exephin (incp) / Cefron (sq) 1 gm, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Metronidazole 500mg/100ml / Flagyl (syn) / Filmet (bex), (if post-partum), 1 bottle I/V stat and TDS. ¤ Inj. Pantoprazole 40 mg / Pantolok (ibn) / Pantobex (incp), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Frusemide 20mg/2ml / Lasix (syn) / Fusid (sq), 1-2-amp IV stat and & SOS. ¤ Inf. MgSO4 (4 gm/100 ml) 4% / Nalepsin (bex) / G-Mag Sulph (gono), 1 bottle I/V stat @ 60 d/m, then, 1 bottle I/V @ 6 drop/min for 24 hours. [Continue MgSO 4 up to 24 hours after last convulsion or delivery (whichever is longer)] If Diastolic BP ≥ 110 mmHg : ¤ Inj. Labetalol (50mg/10 ml) / Labeta (bex) / Labegest (incp) / Betagest (ops) / Labecard (pop), 4 ml slow I/V stat and 8-10 ml every 15 min until DBP 90 mmHg (Maximum dose 300 mg). [Or, If Diastolic BP >110 mmHg : Oral Labetalol 200 mg once or twice daily ± α -Methyl Dopa / Sardopa (incp) (500 mg to 2000 gm)/day.] If premature baby : ¤ Inj. Dexamethasone 5mg/ml / Roxadex (nuv) / Decason (ops) / Dexa (ren), 2 ½ amp I/V stat & once after 12 hours. [e.g., 11 AM & 11 PM] ¤ Maintain strict fluid balance & intake output chart. ¤ Continuous catheterization.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: ► General Management : • Shout for help (if unconscious or convulsing). • ABCDE Resuscitation. • If she is breathing: give : O 2 inhalation- 4-6L/min. • If apneic : Use Bag & Mask Ventilation. • IV access → IV fluid : Normal saline/ Hartman's solution. • Continuous catheterization. • Maintain strict fluid balance & intake output chart. • Maintenance of nutrition : Ryle’s tube or oral feeding. • Antibiotics : Inj. Ampicillin 500 mg IM/IV 6 hrly / Amoxicillin IV 8 hrly. • Monitor, Pulse, BP, respiration, reflexes at FHR, urine output / auscultated lung bases 30 minute intervals. • Care of the eye, skin and maintain oral hygiene. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 128] ► Specific management of Eclampsia : • A. Prevention / Control of Convulsion : By MgSO 4 . • B. Control of Blood Pressure : By anti-HTN. • C. Obstetric Management : after stabilizing the mother. A. Prevention / Control of Convulsion – MgSO 4 Therapy : Pre-Requisites : • Respiratory rate >16 breaths/min. • Urine output >30ml/hour. • Knee Jerk Present. Loading Dose : • I/V & I/M protocol – 3 g deep IM in each buttock and 4g in 12 ml D/W I/V over 10-15 min. • Only I/M protocol – 5 g in each buttock. Maintenance dose : • 2.5 gm I/M 4 hours after loading dose. • Continue 4 hourly in alternate buttock up to 24 hours after last convulsion or delivery (whichever is longer). Or, Inf. MgSO 4 / Nalepsin [1 bottle = 4gm/100ml], 1 vial I/V @ 60-70 drop/min, then 1 vial I/V @ 6 drop/min for 24 hours. MgSO 4 Toxicity : C/F – ○ RR ○ Urine output ○ Absent knee jerk. Management of MgSO 4 Toxicity : • Omit next dose of MgSO 4 . • Give inj. Calcium gluconate 1 gm (10ml) or, [500mg/5ml (2 amp)] slow I/V over 10 min. • Assisted ventilation (if needed). Control of Blood Pressure : If Diastolic BP Oral drug is prescribed. • α -Methyl Dopa (500mg-2gm)/day. OR, • Calcium Channel Blocker 5mg twice daily. OR, • Labetalol 200 mg once or twice daily. If Diastolic BP >110 mmHg : • Labetalol (50 mg/10ml = 1 amp) : 4 ml ( ½ -amp) I/V slowly every 15 minutes until DBP 90 mm of Hg. OR, 5-amp (250 mg) in 250 ml Normal Saline, IV @ 20mg/hr. OR, • Hydralazine (20mg/amp) : (unavailable in market) : 5 mg dissolved in 10 ml of DW, slow IV (1ml every 15 minutes) until diastolic BP is 90 mm Hg. OR, 1 amp in 200 ml Normal Saline, IV @ 8-10 drops/min. Obstetric Management : Conduction of delivery within 6-8 hrs of convulsion. Vaginal delivery if : • Favorable Cervix. • ARM or Oxytocin induction. • Avoid prolonged 2 nd stage. • Forceps can be used. Upto 3 rd Gravida after 28 weeks, Induction done by : Tab. Misoprostol 200 mcg / Cytomis, ¼ th tab P/V in posterior fornix. If labour does not start → emergency LSCS. LSCS if : • Unfavorable cervix (BISHOP score ≤ 3). • Mal-presentation. • Fetal distress. • No progress of labor. • Uncontrolled fits. • Critical condition, grasping respiration. • If patient not in Labour. Choice of Anesthesia : • Spinal Anesthesia. • Indication of G/A : o Repeated fits. o Thrombocyte count 3 . o Pulmonary edema with cardiorespiratory instability. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 129]

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Tips for anti-convulsants : ● If convulsion recurs after 30 mins of loading dose. give 2.5 gm inj. MgSO4 slowly over 5 mins. ● If skilled provider to use IV/IM protocol is not available, may give 5 gm IM in each buttock (only IM protocol). IV protocol is non-embarrassing, but IM ampules of MgSO4 is supplied in government hospital & little skill needed. ¤ Inj. Magnesium Sulphate (2.47g/5ml)/G-Mag Suph (gono). Loading Dose : (8 ml + 12 ml D/W) IV slowly stat over 10-15 min, Then, 6 ml I/M on each (both) buttock. Then (documented MD), Maintenance Dose : 1-amp (5 ml) deep I/M 4 hourly in alternative buttock (continue for 24 hrs after last convulsion or delivery). Date & Time Right buttock Left buttock Signature (DOD) 11 AM ✓ Dr. ABC 3 PM ✓ Dr. EFG 7 PM ✓ Dr. EFG 11 PM ✓ Dr. IJK ………. ● If MgSO4 not available / contraindicated – Inj. Diazepam 10 mg slowly over 2 mins and 40 mg in 500 ml normal saline in IV drip. ¤ Inj. Diazepam (10 mg/2 ml) / Sedil (sq) / Easium (ops) / G-Diazepam (gono) / Seduxen (ambee), Loading dose : 1 amp (diluted) I/V very slowly (over 2 min), If convulsion recurs : repeat loading dose. Then, Maintenance dose : 4 amp (40 mg) Inj. Diazepam + Inf. N/S, I/V stat @ 5 – 8 d/m. Rate adjusted so that patient remains sedated but arousable. Do not use >100mg/24 hrs. ● Maintain IV fluids – normal saline over 6 hrs initially and closely monitor to avoid fluid overload. ● Bed side clotting test : take 5 ml blood in a plain glass test tube, keep it upright, turn it after 5 minutes to see if clot forms. If fails to clot within 10 minutes -test positive (coagulation failure). ► Pre-eclampsia / Eclampsia with any complications : (Consult with senior/specialist & refer to tertiary centers) • CVD (Cerebro vascular disease). • Acute pulmonary edema (severe respiratory distress). • Acute renal failure (severe oliguria /anuria). • DIC (bleeding from multiple sites including venipuncture site). • HELLP syndrome (Hepatitis, ↑ live enzymes & low platelet count). • Hyperpyrexia. (Temperature >104° F.). • Abruptio Placentae. ► Catheterization if Magnesium Sulphate therapy is ongoing & monitor urine output (U/O), if U/o is ¤ Inf. N/S / Acme’s N/S 1L, I/V stat @ 30 d/m. Again urinary catheterization is important in eclamptic patient as the develops renal failure. Use diazepam if convulsion is not controlled. ► Add, Inj. Vitamin K1 10 mg / Konakion 10 mg, if HELLP syndrome, 1 amp I/M for 3 days. ► After stability of patient confirmation; Termination of pregnancy may be advocated as early as possible. ► Postpartum eclampsia : Fits occur for the first time in puerperium, usually within 48 hours of delivery. Fits occurring beyond 48 hours but less than 4 weeks after delivery is accepted as late postpartum eclampsia. ► Onset of fits : — Antepartum (50%). — Intrapartum (30%). — Postpartum (20%).

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Occurs in pregnancy or within 48 hours after delivery. Associated with severe pre-eclampsia. It is triad of – 1. H emolysis 2. E levated L iver enzymes 3. L ow P latelets count Signs : 1. Right upper quadrant pain 2. Hypertension 3. Nausea and vomiting 4. Oedema Pre-eclampsia / Eclampsia with any complications : Must hospitalization & consult with or refer to specialist.

treatment.title: Hypothyroidism in pregnancy
treatment.description: ¤ During pregnancy increase thyroxine binding globulin and placenta metabolize thyroxine. So, dose of levothyroxine replacement therapy increase by 30-50% from early in pregnancy. As for example, if a patient is getting levothyroxine 50 mcg 2 tablets in pre-pregnancy period then during pregnancy she needs to take levothyroxine 50 mcg 3 tablets. ¤ FT4 & TSH should be measured in every trimester and adjust dose of levothyroxine. ¤ Newborn must screen for hypothyroidism / cretinism & treat.

treatment.title: Thyrotoxicosis in pregnancy
treatment.description: ¤ Gestational thyrotoxicosis is usually associated with multiple pregnancies and hyperemesis gravidarum. This may transient and usually does not require anti-thyroid drug treatment. ¤ Graves’ disease – it is the most common cause of sustained thyrotoxicosis in pregnancy. ¤ Anti-thyroid drugs – propylthiouracil should be used in the first trimester, with carbimazole substituted in the second and third trimesters.

treatment.title: Asthma and pregnancy
treatment.description: 1. Asthma during pregnancy follows the rule of one third - one third become worse, one third remains same and one third improve. 2. All asthma medicine has been shown to be absolutely safe for both the mother and baby. Inhaled route is preferred than oral route. 3. Although it is evident that prednisolone is safe during 1st trimester, it’s use should be limited as rescue therapy for 7-14 days. 4. During labour, induction is usually done with prostaglandin E2 and oxytocin. It is better to avoid injection ergometrin. 5. Asthma medicine may enter the breast milk but the concentration is extremely small and do not have any adverse effect on the baby.

treatment.title: UTI in pregnancy
treatment.description: 1. If suspect UTI during pregnancy then advice urine R/M/E and urine for C/S. 2. Screening for UTI in first antenatal checkup, as most of the UTI remain asymptomatic. 3. 2-5% of pregnant women have asymptomatic bacteriuria, defined as pure growth of >10 organisms/mL, 1 in 3 will develop symptomatic infection (acute cystitis, pyelonephritis) if left untreated. 4. Treat UTI and asymptomatic bacteriuria for at least 1 week with suitable antibiotic. Choice of empirical antibiotic are – cefuroxime, nitrofurantoin, amoxicillin etc. Change according to C/S report. 5. Check urine R/M/E following treatment to ensure infection has cleared. C/C : 1. Amenorrhea for ____ wks. 2. Burning sensation of micturition for ____ 3. Lower abdominal pain for S/D (same duration). Δ ___ th gravida ___ week pg c̄ UTI Adv. : ▸ CBC ▸ USG of PP (pregnancy profile) ▸ Urine RME ▸ Urine for C/S (culture & sensitivity) ☼ Diet – Normal c̄ plenty of water. ☼ Maintain personal hygiene. ☼ Tab. Tab. Cefuroxime Axetil + Clavulanic Acid (500 mg+125 mg) / Cefotil Plus (sq) / Turboclav (bex) / Clavusef (ops) / Furoclav (ren) / Furotil Plus (hc), 1+0+1 for 7 – 14 days. Or, Cap. Nitrofurantoin 100 mg / Umactin BD (uni) / Nintoin SR (incp) / NFT (hc) / Tab. Ofuran SR (paci), 1+0+1 for 7 days. Or, if UTI c̄ high fever & willing to hospitalize – Inj. Ceftriaxone / Oricef (hc) / Trizon (acm) / Axosin (ibn), 1000 mg, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ☼ Tab. Paracetamol 500mg / Ace (sq), 1+1+1. ☼ Tab. Tiemonium 50mg / Viset (hc), 1+1+1. ☼ Cap. Omeprazole 20mg / Losectil (skf) / Omenix (incp) / Ometid (ops), 1+1+1 (B/M)

treatment.title: Causes
treatment.disease_name: Vaginal Bleeding after 28 Weeks of Pregnancy
treatment.description: VAGINAL BLEEDING AFTER 28 WEEKS OF PREGNANCY 1. Placenta Previa. 2. Abruptio Placenta. 3. Vaginal / Perineal Trauma. 4. Cervix : Polyp / cancer (H/O of post coital bleeding).

treatment.title: PLACENTA PREVIA
treatment.description: PLACENTA PREVIA Placenta is near or covers the cervical opening. C/C : ● Painless, causeless, recurrent per vaginal bleeding. ● No H/O trauma. Definitive management : A. Severe Active bleeding : C/S (irrespective of gestational age). B. Bleeding light/stopped : 1. Premature alive fetus : Steroid for lung maturation & expectant management till maturity / recurrence of heavy bleeding. 2. Term or dead fetus : (a) Type-I & Type-II anterior & bleeding is light : Vaginal delivery, (b) Type-III and IV : C/S.

treatment.title: ARUPTIO PLACENTA
treatment.description: ARUPTIO PLACENTA Placenta detaches prematurely from the uterus. C/C : ● Intermittent/constant abdominal pain. ● Severe vaginal bleeding & shock. ● No H/O trauma. Definitive management : A. Heavy bleeding : Termination of pregnancy. 1. CS: If delivery is not imminent. 2. NVD: If delivery is imminent (Expedite by VE/Forceps). B. Light/moderate bleeding : 1. C/S : If foetus alive / distressed, term/near term, delivery not imminent, unsatisfactory progress of labour. 2. Vaginal delivery : Foetus dead or too premature when maternal condition is stable cooperatively, etc. Per-vaginal examination (P/V/E) is not recommended in any active per-vaginal bleeding (P/V/B) conditions. Conservative treatment is same in any case vaginal bleeding after 28 weeks of pregnancy. Δ Placenta Previa / Abruptio Placenta Adv. : ▸ USG of lower abdomen. ▸ Blood grouping and Rh typing ▸ Coagulation profile ▸ Counseling for 2 – 4 units of blood ready. ☼ Bed rest. ☼ NPO-TFO. If patient is in shock, manage the shock first – ☼ Open IV channel by two wide bore cannula. ☼ Inf. H/S (1L), I/V running stat. ☼ Inf. Hydroxyethyl Starch 6% + Sodium Chloride 0.9% / Plasmasol (ori) / Volulyte () / Expansol (incp) / Hi-Vol (pop) 500ml, I/V stat @ 30 drops/min. ☼ Continuous catheterization. ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Trizon (acm) / Axon (aris) 1gm, 1 vial I/V state & BD. ☼ Inj. Nalbuphine (20mg/2ml) / Nabulex (gen) / Nalbun-2 (incp) / Newbain (sq), 1 amp I/M stat & sos (or TDS). c̄- ☼ Inj. Ondansetron (8mg/4ml) / Anset (ops), 1 amp IV stat & sos (or TDS). ☼ Inj. Tiemonium (50mg/1ml) / Algin (ren) / Timozin (incp), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS/QDS. ☼ Inj. Esomeprazole 40mg / Maxpro (ren) / Maxima (acm), 1 vial I/V stat & BD.

treatment.title: Leukorrhoea In Pregnancy
treatment.description: Usually vaginal discharge increase in pregnancy. P/V discharge is non-irritating & non-foul smelling, e.g., normal secretion. But if smelly, itchy, sore or associated with dysuria. Treat accordingly. Advice : 1. Assure that its normal physiology. 2. Wash private parts with lukewarm water. 3. Maintain personal hygiene. 4. Use clean & cotton undergarments. 5. Wiping after a bowel movement is to do so from front to back. Hand movement front (avoiding touch to vaginal vestibule) to back. It helps to avoid urinary tract infections by fecal contamination.

treatment.title: Vaginal candidiasis during pregnancy
treatment.description: Vaginal candidiasis is a common vaginal infection in pregnancy causing itching and soreness. There is no evidence that this infection harms the baby. Antifungal creams are effective, topical imidazole (clotrimazole, miconazole) or terbinafine appears to be more effective than nystatin for treating symptomatic vaginal candidiasis in pregnancy.

treatment.title: Striae gravidarum / Abdominal Striae / Stretch marks
treatment.disease_name: Striae gravidarum / Abdominal Striae / Stretch marks
treatment.description: Extremely common in pregnancy, affecting up to 90% of women in the second and third trimesters. It may be itchy. SG can cause itching, burning, and discomfort and typically present on the breasts, abdomen, hips, and thighs. SG first presents as flat, pink to red bands (striae rubra or immature striae) that become raised, longer, wider, and violet-red. Over a period of months to years, the marks fade and become hypopigmented (striae alba or mature striae), appearing parallel to skin tension lines as scar-like, wrinkled, white, and atrophic marks. ► Moisturizer, or emollient : Olive / coconut oil / Bio oil are useful. ► Cream Tretinoin 0.025% / Cosmotrin (bex) / Retin-A (syn) / Trinon (ren), This cream should be applied sparingly to the whole affected area once or twice daily for 3 – 7 months. There is an inadequate evidence of the safety of topically applied Tretinoin in human pregnancy. ► Anti-Stretch Mark Creams may be used : Mederma Stretch Marks Cream/ Lotion (high price).

treatment.title: Pemphigoid gestationis
treatment.disease_name: Striae gravidarum / Abdominal Striae / Stretch marks
treatment.description: Pemphigoid gestationis (PG) (previously called herpes gestationis) – rare. It is a bullous autoimmune, sub-epidermal dermatosis clinically and pathogenetically similar to bullous pemphigoid. Starts in mid-pregnancy and appears as a generalized, intensely itchy rash. May recur in subsequent pregnancies / with combine hormonal contraceptive. Refer for specialist management.

treatment.title: Impetigo herpetiformis
treatment.disease_name: Striae gravidarum / Abdominal Striae / Stretch marks
treatment.description: Impetigo herpetiformis – rare. Clinically and histologically, it is consistent with pustular psoriasis. Starts in the third trimester, mild itch. If patient become systemically unwell then refer. Remits after delivery but may recur in later pregnancies.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Pruritic urticarial papules and plaques of pregnancy (PUPPP)
treatment.description: It occurs in first / multiple pregnancies or if excessive weight increase in pregnancy. Appears at after 35 weeks of gestation. Clears spontaneously within 6 weeks after delivery. Recurrence in subsequent pregnancies is rare. PUPPP rash is a patch of itchy, hive-like bumps that form in the stretch marks on your belly and spread to other parts of your body when you're pregnant. Intensely itchy rash usually confined to lower abdomen / buttocks. Treatment : Treat with topical steroids ± calamine ± anti-histamine. ► Cream Hydrocortisone Acetate / Topicort (sq) / Zocort (aci) / Cortider (skf), Apply twice daily ---- 7 – 21 days (may use potent steroid one as per need & situation, refer if oral steroid needed) ► Tab. Levo-cetrizine 5 mg / Alcet (hc) / Seasonix (incp) / Sinacet (ibn), 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 7 – 21 days ► If severe itching, Calamine Lotion (amico) / Itchnil (skf) / Itchsol (incp), Apply in the body BD ---- till itching subsides.

treatment.title: Scabies during pregnancy
treatment.description: Please see “Dermatology” portion. Treatment – topical permethrin 5% can be used (category-B). Monosulfiram 25% is a good alternative.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : 1. H/O : – Amenorrhoea for ___weeks / ___ months. – Lower abdominal pain / LAP for ___ hours. – Leaking membrane / LM for ___ hours. 2. O / E : - Pulse. - Blood pressure. - Anemia. - Jaundice. - Oedema (May be physiological that disappears at morning when woke up & mention only if pathological). 3. O / H (Obstetrical History) : - Married for (M/F) x years. - Gravida : 1st / 2nd / 3rd & so on. - Para : ___ (NVD + C/S) + ___ (Abortion + MR). - Age of last child (ALC) x yrs. - Must mark at first page if its previous caesarean section as previous C/S (P/C/S). 4. G / H (Gynecological History) : - LMP. - EDD by calendar method. - EDD by USG. - Menstrual cycle (MC) - Menstrual period (MP) : Regular / Average / Irregular. - Menstrual flow (MF) [Calculation of EDD = 1 st day of last menstrual period (LMP) + 9 calendar month + 7 days] 5. R / F (Risk Factors) : - HTN + or – - DM + or – - BA + or – Gravida : Total number of conception including the present one. Para/Parity: Total number of deliveries after 28th gestational wks. Note – Previous twin pg means single conception & parity one. Para = After 28 weeks (NVD / CS) + Before 28 weeks (MR / D&C). Diagnosis (Dx) : __ th Gravida c̄ ___ weeks Pg / FTP c̄ LAP / LM / Previous C/S / Breech Presentation / (Mild/Moderate/Severe) oligohydramnios. Terminology : ○ 1st conception = Primi gravida. ○ FTP = Full Term Pregnancy. ○ LP = Labour pain. ○ LAP = Lower abdominal pain. ○ PROM = Premature rupture of membrane. ○ LM = Leaking membrane. Δ FTP c̄ LAP If not done earlier, all the inv at 1 st visit should be advised. Adv. : ▸ USG of PP. ▸ Blood grouping & Rh typing. ▸ Hb %. ▸ NVD or C/S OT drug list given. ¤ Diet : Liquid. ± ¤ Inf. H/S-acme 1L, I/V stat @ 10 d/m. ± ¤ Cap. Cephradine / Lebac 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ¤ Inj. Tiemonium / Viset (hc) / Tinium (acm) or, Inj. Hyoscine Butyl Bromide / Butapan (jay), 2 amp I/M stat & sos (if severe LAP). ¤ Tab. Tiemonium / Viset (hc) / Tinium (acm) or Hyoscine Butyl Bromide / Butapan, 1 + 1 + 1 ¤ Tab. Pantoprazole / Omeprazole 20 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 (B/M) ¤ Tab. F/S (Ferrous Sulphate) / Tab. Hemofiz, 1 + 0 + 1 (A/M) ¤ Tab. B/C (Vit B Complex) / Cap. B 50 forte, 1 + 0 + 1 ¤ 1 – 2 unit of Blood demand given. (if mother is anemic or opt for C/S)

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: Note : - Diet may full & normal in uncomplicated cases but the admitted patient may need emergency / elective caesarean section on admission day, so liquid diet is safe line. - Antibiotics : are not recommended but may use antibiotic as repeated per vaginal (PV) examinations done, again the patient may need episiotomy or surgical intervention. In case of leaking membrane (LM) injectable antibiotics should be given as it has higher chance of developing chorioamnionitis following PV examinations. Usually supplied drugs in the ward ceftriaxone may be given : ¤ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftron (sq) 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. - Pain : Though intermittent pain is associated with term pregnancy but severe pain may cause neurogenic shock so antispasmotic drugs should be given, in labour these antispasmotics don’t interfere with uterine contractions but relief pain to some extent.
