In severe lower abdominal pain (LAP) injectable antispasmotics should be given. Tablet in mild or moderate pain & Injectable in severe pain. - Anti-ulcerants : Anny PPI is safe. Pantoprazole is supposed to be more safe. Stress, iron tablet, nausea are associated with gastric irritation & ulceration. - Ferrous / iron tablets may be given to any pregnant & lactating mother. It should not be given at first trimester of pregnancy because nausea vomiting is common in this period and iron itself causes nausea & vomiting. It must be given in an anemic mother. Usually ferrous sulphate (F/S) tablet supplied in the ward. Ferrous ascorbate & fumarate is available in market. Oral iron treatment should be continued for 3-6 months for correction of anemia & replenish iron stores. Iron / Ferrous ion is best absorbed on acidic media / empty stomach but cause severe constipation & black / terry stool if taken before meal, so clinically it is better to prescribe after meal. - Multivitamin / vitamin B complex may be used. If patient complaint of PV bleeding or patient is anemic then transfuse blood. - If patient has previous caesarean section (P/C/S) or Breech Presentation or (Mild/Moderate/Severe) oligohydramnios or other risk factor, must to be marked boldly at the first page of the admission form & inform seniors. ► OXYTOCIN REGIMEN : Inf. Hartsol (libra) or N/S (osl/acme) 1000 ml + Inj. Oxytocin 20 IU (Or Inj. Hartsol or N/S 500ml + Inj. Oxytocin 10 IU), I/V @ 16 drops/min stat and increase after every 30 minutes by 16 drops/min (e.g. 16, 32, 48, 64) till 3-5 uterine contractions/10 minutes, each lasting >40 sec. Once desired contraction is achieved, maintain the ongoing rate. Note : Maximum rate 64 drops/min. Stop infusion if foetal heart rate 160/min or 25 contractions in 10 minutes. IM/IV Inj. Oxytocin 10 IU/ml : Linda-S DS (nuv) / Oxyton DS (ren) / Inducin (incp) / Syntocin (techno) / Pitocin DS (che). IM/IV Inj. Oxytocin 5 IU/ml : Linda-S (nuv) / Ocin (ops) / Oxyton (ren) / Syntocin (techno) / Pitocin (che).

treatment.title: Outdoor Treatment & Advise after NVD or C/S
treatment.description: Drug Treatment & Advise after NVD or C/S -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- Ø Cap. Cefixime / Fix-A (acm) / Cef-3 (sq) / T-cef (drg) / Triocim (bex) 200 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ .......... ৭ দিন ± Ø Cap. Flucloxacillin / Flubex (bex) / Flux (ops) / Flustar (ren) / Flupen (drg) 500 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ + ১ .......... ৭ দিন Ø (Any PPI) / Tab. Remmo (bex) / Othera (aci) / Maxpro MUPS (ren) / Maxima MUPS (acm), ১ + ০ + ১ ......... ১৫ দিন Ø Tab. Ketorolac 10mg/ Rolac (ren)/ Toradolin (rad)/ Torax (sq) / Xidolac (bex), ১ + ০ + ১ (খাবার পরে) ..... ব্যথা থাকলে খাবেন (৫ দিন) Ø Cap. Vitamin A / Ratinol Forte (drg) / Ovit-A (ops) 50000 IU, ৪ টি ক্যাপসুল একসাথে খাবেন (একবারই)। Ø Tab. Multivitamin & Multiminerals / Filwel Gold (sq) / Atoz Premium (rad) / Bextram Gold (bex) / Momvit (bex), ০ + ১ + ০ or, ১ + ০ + ১ ........ ৩ – ৬ মাস Ø Iron / Cap. Zif Forte (sq) / Prenat XL (hc) / EPL Plus (glob), ০ + ০ + ১ (খাবার পরে) ......... ৩ – ৬ মাস Ø Calcium / Becoral D (bex) / CoralCal-D (rad) / Ostocal D (skf), ১ + ১ + ০ (খাবার পরে) .......... ৩ – ৬ মাস উপদেশ : ১. নরমাল ডেলিভারীর পর দেড় মাস ও সিজারের তিন মাস পর্যন্ত ভারী কাজ করবেন না। ২. এক মাস / ছয় সপ্তাহ যৌন সহবাস থেকে বিরত থাকুন। ৩. জন্ম নিয়ন্ত্রন পদ্ধতি গ্রহন করবেন। ডেলিভারীর দেড় মাস / ছয় সপ্তাহ পর থেকে কনডম (ব্যারিয়ার ম্যাথড) বা মিনিপিল (POP) [Tab. Desogestrel 0.075 mg / Bredicon (ren) / POP-D (incp), ০+০+১] ব্যবহার করবেন। ৪. প্রযোজ্যক্ষেত্রে অপারেশনের (সিজারের) সাত(০৭)দিন পর সেলাই কাটাবেন। ৫. পরবর্তীতে কমপক্ষে তিন বছর পরে বাচ্চা নিবেন। মায়ের স্বাস্থ্যর ক্ষয়পূরণ ও নবজাতকের বিকাশের জন্য এই বিরতিটি জরুরী। ৬. বাচ্চাকে প্রথম ছয় মাস শুধুমাত্র বুকের দুধ খাওয়াবেন। ৭. সাত মাস থেকে দুই বছর পর্যন্ত বুকের দুধের পাশাপাশি অন্যান্য পুষ্টিকর খাবারও খাওয়াবেন। ৮. বাচ্চাকে, Inj. Phytomenadione 2 mg/0.2 ml / K-one MM (sq) / K MM (incp) / Babykion (che), ১ অ্যাম্পুল করে বাচ্চার জন্মের ১ম, ৪র্থ ও ২৮ তম দিনে (৪ ঘন্টা, ৪ দিন ও ৪ সপ্তাহ বয়সে) ড্রপারের সাহায্যে মুখে খাওয়াবেন। ৯. বাচ্চার জন্মের ৪২ দিনের মধ্যে টিকা প্রদান করা শুরু করতে হবে। দেড় মাস বা ৪২ দিন বয়সের মধ্যে সরকারী টিকাকার্ড করে ইপিআই সিডিউল অনুসারে সময়মত বাচ্চাকে টিকা প্রদান করতে হবে। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন। ► Follow up after 7 days & at 6 weeks is recommended. ▸ Progestogen-only-pill (POP), or a minipill is an effective contraceptive but does not suppress milk production whereas rest OCP dose. ▸ As per need antibiotic options – Ciprofloxacin 500mg- bd for 7 days ± Metronidazole (400mg) - tds for 5 days, Flucloxacillin should be used only in selected cases. ▸ Another antibiotic combinations : Ø Tab. Metronidazole / Metryl (ops) / Filmet (bex) / Flamyd (incp) 400 mg, ১ + ১ + ১ ....... ৩ দিন Ø Tab. Cefuroxime / Cefotil (sq) / Sefur (ops) / Kilbac (incp) 500 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ....... ৭ দিন Or, Tab. Cefuroxime + Clavulanic acid (500 mg+125 mg) / Cefotil Plus (sq) / Turboclav (bex) / Fuclav (drg) / Famiclav (acm) 500 mg can be used instead of Tab. Cefuroxime. ▸ Check & dressing on 1st POD (post-operative day). ▸ Check dressing on 3rd POD & of healthy wound Dc̄A (discharge with advise). ▸ If inadequate milk production – Ø Tab. Domperidone 10 mg / Domin (ops) / Motigut (sq) / Deflux (bex) / Don-A (acm), ২ + ২ + ২ (খাবার ৩০ মিনিট র্পূবে) – ১-২ মাস ▸ If dead fetus / IUD / To suppress breast milk – Ø Tab. Bromocriptine 2.5 mg / Bromolac (sq) / Bromodel (ops) / Criptine (ren), ½ + ০ + ½ ........ ২ দিন, তারপরে, ১ + ০ + ১ ........ ১২ দিন ▸ If retracted nipple – syringing / manual pumping (Please see “breast problem” portion below).

treatment.title: HEADACHE, NECK PAIN AND VERTIGO FOLLOWING C/S
treatment.description: Treatment : ¤ Take tea or coffee. ¤ Inf. H/S 1L, I/V @ 25 drops/m stat. ¤ Tab. Flunarizine / Norium (skf) 10mg, 1+0+1. ¤ Tab. P/C / Napa (bex) 1g, 1+1+1.

treatment.title: DRY COUGH FOLLOWING C/S
treatment.description: Treatment : ☼ Syrup Butamirate Citrate (7.5mg/5ml) / Mirakof (sq) / Drycofix (dbl) / Bukof (bacn), Or, ☼ Syrup Glycerol + Liquid Sugar (0.75 ml+1.93 ml)/5 ml / Honycol (ren) / Hunny (skf), Or, ☼ Syrup Herbal Vasakarista / Adovas (sq) / Honeybas (acm) / Devas (drg), 2 – 3 TSF TDS for 15 – 60 days. উপদেশ : বেশিক্ষণ শুয়ে থাকবেন না। হাঁটাহাঁটি করবেন। রোগীর পিঠে আস্তে আস্তে বাড়ি দিবেন। কাঁশি আসলে কফ বের করে দেওয়ার চেষ্টা করবেন। If ronchi present : ☼ Tab. Montelukast / Provair (uni) / Aeron (hc) 10 mg, 0+0+1. ☼ Nebulization with Salbutamol +Ipratropium / Windel Plus (incp) stat & 6 hourly.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : ○ Watery vaginal discharge after 28 weeks. ○ Fluid coming from the cervix / Fluid coming from cervix during coughing for ___ days. ○ Ask for : Lower abdominal pain, fever, foul smelling discharge, foetal movement. PROM : Preterm (28-37 weeks) / term (>37 weeks). Assessment (per speculum examination to confirm PROM & P/V exam for labour progress – if patient is in labour) and exclude risk factors – amnionitis, placental abruption, fetal distress, cord prolapse. Δ ___th Gravida c̄ ___ weeks Pg c̄ PROM Adv. : ▸ CBC c̄ CRP. ▸ USG of PP with biophysical profile & colour Doppler (if needed) ▸ Urine R/M/E & C/S. ▸ Blood grouping and Rh typing. ▸ BT, CT. ▸ Random blood sugar ▸ High vaginal swab (HVS) for C/S. ☼ Diet : Normal. ☼ Bed rest in left lateral position. ☼ Use Sterile vulval pad. ☼ O ₂ inhalation (If fetal movement is reduced). ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Rofecin (rad) / Oricef (hc) / Ceftron (sq) 1 gm, 1 vial I/V stat and BD. In Use steroid betamethasone 2 dose or dexamethasone 4 doses. Inj. Dexamethasone (5mg/1ml) / Dexa (ren), 1 ½ amp (1.5 ml) I/M stat & BD for 4 doses. Or, ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone (6mg/ml) / Roxadex (nuv), 1 amp I/M stat and 12 hrly for 4 doses. ☼ Cap. Carbonyl Iron 50 mg + Folic acid 0.50 mg + Zinc 61.80 mg / Zif CI (sq) / Ferix-V (ren) / Prenat-CI (hc) / Zeefol-CI (skf) / Ferozi-CI (drg), 0+0+1. ☼ Tab. Calcium 500 mg / Ostocal (skf) / Calbo (sq) / A-Cal (acm) / Caldil (drg) / Rocal (hc) / Aristocal (bex), 1+1+0 (with meal). If acidity occurs – ☼ Tab. Pantoprazole 20mg / Pantobex (bex) / Pantonix (incp), 1+0+1 ( ½ hour before meal).

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Pregnancy Expectant management : • Sterile vulvul pad. • Broad spectrum antibiotics. • Tocolytic agent-Magsulf / Beta-agonist • Dexamethasone 6mg – I/M 12 hrly for 4 doses (if Expectant management should continue for fetal lung maturity. Serial evaluation to consider delivery at 34 weeks if possible. ► Pregnancy ≥ 34 weeks : ● Risk factors – absent : Wait for spontaneous onset of labour for 24-48 hours. ● Risk factors – present : Immediate delivery & transfer the patient with “fetus in utero” to a center equipped with NICU if fetal distress occurs. ► PROM WITH CHORIOAMNIONITIS : 1. Fever. 2. Tachycardia. 3. Uterine tenderness. 4. Foul smelling per vaginal discharge. - Diet is full & normal in uncomplicated cases but the admitted patient may need emergency / elective caesarean section on admission day, so liquid diet is safe line. - Rest : Complete bed rest is advised. Urinary catheterization should be done to avoid straining & physical activity. - Antibiotics : are not suggested in all cases but we suggest an antibiotic (any cephalosporin) in all cases as we do repeated per vaginal (PV) examination. Again the patient may need episiotomy or surgical intervention, so prophylactic antibiotics are suggested. In case of leaking membrane (LM) injectable antibiotics should be given. Because it has higher chance of developing chorioamnionitis following PV examinations. - Pain : Though intermittent pain is associated with term pregnancy but severe pain may cause neurogenic shock so antispasmodic drugs should be given, in labour these antispasmodics don’t interfere with uterine contractions but relief pain to some extent. In severe lower abdominal pain (LAP) injectable antispasmotics should be given. Tiemonium Methylsulphate [Viset (hc) / Visral (ops) / Norvis (sq) / Tninium (acm)] can also be used here. Tablet in mild or moderate pain & Injectable in severe pain. ► Glucocorticoid therapy : Maternal administration of gluco-corticoids is advocated where the pregnancy is less than 34 weeks. This helps in fetal lung maturation so that the incidence of RDS, IVH and NEC are minimized. This is beneficial when the delivery is delayed beyond 48 hours of the first dose. Betamethasone is the steroid of choice, but dexamethasone is supplied in the ward. [Idiopathic Respiratory Distress Syndrome / Hyaline Membrane Disease / Respiratory Distress Syndrome (RDS), Intraventricular Hemorrhage (IVH) / Germinal Matrix Hemorrhage (GMH), Necrotizing Entercolitis (NEC)]. - Rexene / Plastic sheet should be given to buy to protect bed wetting. - NVD : If vaginal delivery is feasible. - CS : 1. Foetal distress. 2. Chorioamnionitis develops. 3. Failed induction. 4. Unsatisfactory progress of labour. 5. Malpresentation. 6. Previous C/S.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: • Excessive vaginal bleeding >500ml. OR, • Prolonged moderate bleeding. OR, • Any bleeding which deteriorates maternal condition after childbirth. Causes or D/Ds : 1. Retained placenta (Placenta not delivered within 30 min after delivery). 2. Retained products of conception. 3. Atonic uterus. 4. Cervical / vaginal tear. 5. DIC. 6. Inversion of uterus. A. General Management : • Shout for help. • ABCDE Resuscitation. • Massage fundus to expel blood clot. • Give 10 IU Oxytocin I/M. • IV access with 2 wide bore cannula & infuse Normal Saline / Hartmann's Solution with 20 IU oxytocin in 1L. • Inj. Ergometrine 0.2 mg I/M + Tab. Misoprostol 800 – 1000 µgm P/R. • If patient is in shock : o Infuse 1L IV Fluid in 20 min. o Then, 1L IV Fluid in 30 min. o Then, Titrate rate of infusion according to response. (pulse settle down 100 mmHg). o Urinary Catheterization. o Assess Vital signs, Monitor blood loss & Urine output until stable. o Send blood for : Hb%, Blood grouping. o Arrange blood for transfusion (if needed). [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 138] B. Specific Management – Post partum bundle approach : Flow Chart : Determine the cause of PPH and manage accordingly (Palpate uterus, examine placenta, examine birth canal with good light and adequate exposure). If placenta is not delivered : Retained Placenta : Placenta not delivered within 30 min after delivery → Controlled cord traction (CCT). If placenta is delivered → Continue observation. Retained Product of Conception : Portion of placenta / membrane missing → Remove placental / membrane fragment by exploration and curettage of uterus. ↓ If bleeding controlled : • Continue IV oxytocin for 4 – 6 hours : 20 Units oxytocin in 1L N/S @20 drops/min. • Transfuse blood (If necessary). • Antibiotics. • Iron (Fe Sulphate / Fumarate 150 mg + folic acid 400 mcg) OD for 3 months. If bleeding is not controlled : Specialist Consultation & Higher Center Referral. If placenta is delivered : Specialist Consultation OR, Higher Center Referral. Atonic Uterus : Uterus fails to contract after delivery. • Rule out DIC. • Bedside Coagulation Test. • Aortic Compression. • Bimanual Compression. • Intrauterine balloon tamponade (using condom catheter). DIC : Bleeding from multiple sites including venepuncture site. • Bed side clotting test. • No clot after 20 min/clot breaks easily. • Give fresh whole blood. Trauma : Tear of Cervix / Vagina / Perineum → Repair Injury. Vulval Hematoma : Bleeding from episiotomy wound → Open, Drain & Repair. Inversion of Uterus : Uterus inside out through vagina, severe pain & shock. • Inj. Pethidine I/M 1 mg/kg. • Urgent Repositioning of uterus under GA. • Antibiotics.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : H/O : – P/V/B within 24 hrs (after 28 wks pg) NVD / CS [Primary PPH] or, after 24 hours (within 42 days) [Secondary PPH]. – ± Lower abdominal pain (LAP). O / E : - Pulse : ↑ / Feeble / Unrecorable. - Blood pressure : ↓ . - Anemia (+). Adv. : 1. USG of lower abdomen c̄ special attention to uterus. 2. Hb%. 3. BT, CT. (Coagulation profile to exclude DIC). ▸ Counsel patient’s near relatives to keep ready at least 2 units of blood & may need more. Monitoring : Until stabilization of – ○ Pulse : ○ BP : ○ PV bleeding : ○ Urine output : ○ Anaemia : ○ Dehydration : ○ Abdomen : Soft or hard Δ Primary PPH due to retained placenta or atonic uterus If patient is in shock, manage the shock first – ☼ Bed rest. ☼ NPO-TFO. ☼ Massage fundus to expel blood clot. ☼ Open IV channel by two wide bore cannula. ☼ Inj. Oxytocin / Ocin (ops) / Linda-S (nuv) / Pitocin (che) 5 IU/ml, 2-amp I/M stat. ☼ Inf. N/S or Hartsol 1L + 4 amp (total 20 IU) Inj. Oxytocin 5 IU/ml, I/V @ running (within 20 min). Then, ☼ Inf. N/S or Hartsol 1L + 4 amp (total 20 IU) Inj. Oxytocin 5 IU/ml, I/V @ running (within 30 min) Then, ☼ Titrate rate of infusion according to response (pulse settle down 100 mmHg) Or, Continue IV Oxytocin for 4-6 hours : Inf. N/S or Hartsol 1L + 4 amp (total 20 IU) Inj. Oxytocin 5 IU/ml, I/V @20 d/m. ☼ Transfusion of blood. ± ☼ Volume dilator : Inf. 6% Polystarch + 0.9% NS / Plasmasol (ori) / Plasmex (bacn), I/V stat @ 30 d/m through another channel. ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Oricef (hc) / Rofecin (rad) / Axosin (ibn) 1gm, 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Metronidazole 500mg/100ml / Filmet (bex) / Metro (osl), 1 bottle I/V stat and TDS. ☼ Inj. Omeprazole 40mg / Omenix (incp) / Opal (hc) / Seclo (sq), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ☼ Continuous catheterization. ☼ Monitor vital signs regularly. ☼ Initiate and encourage breast feeding.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: Specific management for retained placenta : 1. Controlled cord traction (CCT). 2. Manual removal of placenta under GA/deep sedation : If controlled cord traction fails : remove placental fragment / membrane by exploration of uterus using hand, sponge holding forceps, large curette. Δ Primary PPH due to atonic uterus : Medical Mx (as above). Δ Primary PPH due to DIC : Medical management (see above). Plus – C/C : Bleeding from multiple sites including venepuncture site. • Bed side clotting test. • No clot after 20 min or clot breaks easily. • Coagulation profile (BT, CT) to exclude DIC. • Give fresh whole blood. Δ Primary PPH due to cervical / vaginal tear or trauma : Medical management as above, but avoid oxytocin in drip if uterus is hard, firm & contracted on palpation. If bleeding not controlled : Please add (P/A) according to need : ¤ Inj. Ergometrine 200 mcg/ml / Metherspan (ops), 2 amp I/M stat [then, 1 amp I/M TDS (if needed)]. ¤ Tab. Misoprostol / Cytomis (incp) / Isovent (sq) / Indula (ren) / Misotol (zis) 200µgm, 4 tab P/R stat. ¤ Inj. Tranexamic acid 500 mg / Traxyl (nuv) / Xamic (ren) / Tracid (acm) / Trexam (hc) / Frabex (sq), 1 amp I/V stat & TDS. ¤ Inj. Hydrocortisone 100mg/2ml / Cotson (ops) / Cortef (aris) / Cortinex (zis) / Intasone (incp), 2 vial I/V stat then, 1 vial I/V TDS/QDS. ► If lower abdominal pain, add – ¤ Inj. Hyoscine Butyl Bromide / Butapan (jay) Or, ¤ Inj. Tiemonium Methylsulphate / Viset (hc) / Norvis (sq) / Visonium (drg) / Tinium (acm) / Visral (ops) / Algin (ren), 2 amp I/V (or I/M) stat & 1 amp I/V (or I/M) TDS. ► Using too much ecbolic agents like Oxytocin 10IU / Piton DS, Ergometrine / Metherspan 125 mg, Tab. Misoprostol / Cytomis 200 mcg may be hazardous, so consult with senior which to use & which to not. Oxytocin contracts body of uterus, whereas Ergometrine / Metherspan contracts both body & cervix. Ergometrine / Metherspan is must not use in hypertensive patients & is indicated in fall of BP. ► Instead of oxytocin drip, we may use – ¤ Inj. Carboplatin (100mg/mL) / Duratocin (bacn) / Arbecin (ren) / Carbeshot (nuv), 1 amp I/V stat. Carbetocin is to be used as a single dose only IV slowly over 1 minute. ► 3rd Stage Hg / Retained Placenta : 1. Clamp the cord outside vagina with a thread & tie with thigh. 2. Placenta separated / Grasp of blood → Control Cord Traction (CCT) & fundal messaging. 3. Placenta non-separated → Manual removal under GA. 4. If bleeding not controlled → Hysterectomy (Final Tx). ► Secondary PPH : after 24 hours & within 14 days (from delivery), Treat as primary PPH. But after 14 days & within 42 days treat with oral drugs, as they are less hazardous. Tab. Norethisterone / Normens 5mg or Tab. Levonorgestrel / Nordette 28 (1+1+1) may be advocated in case of scanty & interrupted bleeding. These hormonal drugs prescribed for 10 days & after 2-3 days withdrawal bleeding occurs, so supervision of seniors is must. ► Rexene / Plastic sheet should be given to buy to protect bed wetting. ► Discharge with antibiotics, Iron, Multivitamin & suggestions same as caesarean section (C/S or, LUCS).

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Intrauterine growth restriction (IUGR) (Refer to an obstetrician) It is when a baby in the womb (a fetus) does not grow as expected. ► Tab. Allylestrenol 5 mg / Gestrenol (ren) / Geston (sq) / Allygest (nuv) / Allynol (pop), 1 + 1 + 1 ---- continue Advice : (mother) To increase amount & frequency of feeding & increase weight. Take proper rest & relax.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : H/O : – 9 months of amenorrhoea. – H/O no fetal movements. O / E : - Pulse. - Blood pressure (BP). - Anemia (-) / (+). - Oedema (-) / (+). - TT immunization (-) / (+). Δ ___th gravida ___week pg c̄ IUD Advice : ▸ USG of PP : No fetal cardiac activity. ▸ Coagulation profile (BT, CT, Platelet count). ¤ Diet : Normal. ¤ Inj. Ciprofloxacin 200mg/100ml, 1 vial I/V stat and QDS. ¤ Inj. Metronidazole 500mg/100ml, 1 bottle I/V stat & TDS. ¤ Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg, 1 vial I/V stat and BD. ¤ Counsel to keep 1 unit of fresh blood ready. With consultation : ¤ Tab. Misoprostol 200 mcg, ¼ tab P/V 4-6 hourly. ► Treatment is termination of pregnancy without caesarean section or any invasive procedure. Oxytocin drip / Misoprostol (Cytomis) tablets or, artificial rupture of membrane (ARM) is done according to seniors. ► According to textbooks wait till spontaneous expulsion of fetus, but it is very much painful for any woman to wait & carry a dead baby within her womb. Again it increases chance of infections. So early induced expulsion (medical/surgical) should be done. ► To suppress breast milk add – Ø Tab. Bromocriptine 2.5 mg / Bromolac (ren) / Bromodel (ops) / Criptine (sq), ½ + ০ + ½ ...... ২ দিন, তারপরে, ১ + ০ + ১ ...... ১২ দিন

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Immuno-prophylaxis for Rh negative mother Must refer a Rh-ve mother (husband is Rh+ve) to gynecologist for hospital delivery. Immunoprophylaxis for Rh negative mothers using anti-D immunoglobulin (300 mcg) (anti-D Ig) is given IM into the deltoid muscle as soon as possible after the sensitizing event-preferably within 72 hours though there is evidence of benefit up to 9 days. Women already sensitized should not be given anti-D Ig. Specific treatment : after delivery (NVD or C/S) : If the baby is Rh negative (-ve) : No Anti-D is needed. If the baby is Rh positive (+ve) : Add, ► Inj. Human Anti-D Immunoglobulins 300 mcg/2 ml / ¤ Inj. Rhophylac (csl) / RhoGAM (zas), 1 amp deep I/M within 72 hours of delivery (once) [Given to mother, not the baby. Do not inject intravenously. Pre-filled syringe price 4,700 BDT]

treatment.title: Puerperal Pyrexia
treatment.description: Fever with features of SIRS (Systemic Inflammatory Response Syndrome) within 6 weeks after delivery : excluding 1 st 24 hours but within 1 st 10 days of child birth. Fever with any 2 of the following criteria : • Respiratory Rate > 20/min • Heart rate > 90/min • Temp > 100.4°F or • WBC count > 12,000/mm 3 or 3 Causes of Puerperal Pyrexia with features : Puerperal Sepsis • Lower Abd. Pain • Foul Smelling Lochial Discharge • Tender Uterus Generalized Peritonitis • Abd. Pain • Vomiting • Abd. Tenderness & rigidity • Abd. Distension • Absent Bowel Sound Wound Infection • Discharge from wound site • Pain at/around wound site UTI • Dysuria • ↑ Urinary Frequency & Urgency • Tender Supra Pubic Region • Tender Renal Angle Pelvic Abscess • Lower Abd. Pain • Abd. Distension • Vomiting • Diarrhea • Tender Uterus • Swelling in Adnexa / POD Breast Abscess • Breast Pain • Firm, Enlarged, Tender Breast • Fluctuant Swelling in Breast RTI • Productive Cough • SOB • Chest Pain • Creeps / Ronchi in Lung [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 142] Investigations : • CBC, Blood grouping & Rh typing • Blood sugar (2 hours post-prandial) • S. Creatinine, S. Electrolytes, • Blood culture, HVS, wound swab for C/S • Urine R/E, C/S • USG whole abdomen General Management of Puerperal Pyrexia • Reassurance & Counselling. • I/V fluid, maintenance of nutrition. • Anti-spasmodic, Analgesic: NSAIDS. • Anti-pyretic and cold sponging. • Broad spectrum injectable antibiotic. • Catheterization if needed & strict fluid balance, I/O chart. • Monitoring : pulse, BP, temp, respiration. Antibiotic Choice : 1. Ceftriaxone + Metronidazole or, 2. Ciprofloxacin + Metronidazole or, 3. Amoxycillin + Metronidazole + Gentamycin or, 4. According to C/S Surgical Intervention : If fever doesn’t subside after 3 days reevaluate the causes of puerperal Sepsis (Pelvic Abscess / Generalized Peritonitis / Retained product) – Evacuation of uterus & pouch of Douglas (POD) - Colpotomy or laparotomy with peritoneal toileting or TAH with or without BSO. Breast Abscess / Mastitis – drainage of abscess followed by antibiotic and daily dressing but continue breast feeding. Wound debridement, analgesic, vitamins, transfusion of blood (BT) if needed.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Inadequate Secretion of Breast Milk / Lactation Failure
treatment.description: INADEQUATE SECRETION OF BREAST MILK / LACTATION FAILURE First ask how many times the baby had urinated in a day. If 6 times or more then breast milk secretion is adequate. If less 6 times (at 7 days old or later) then mother should be treated (usually milk production increases if mother had proper rest & stress free state, proper attachment & positioning). Please see “Basic Pedi” portion. ● Non-pharmacological approaches : ¤ Teach attachment & positioning of breast feeding, such as correct breast feeding advice, support. (Please see basic pedi portion) ¤ Regular breast feeding (1-2 hrly) because sucking stimulates lactation reflex. Increase frequent of breast feeding or sucking. ¤ Intake plenty of fluid intake & healthy foods - black cumin (কালোজিরা), milk, fruit juice, ● Pharmacological approaches : Domperidone and metoclopramide use widely to stimulate prolactin and enhance milk supply. ¤ Tab. Domperidone 10 mg / Domin (ops) / Deflux (bex), 2 + 2 + 2 or, 1 + 1 + 1 ----- 1 – 3 months ± ¤ Tab. Calcium Carbonate 500 mg [Elemental source] + Vitamin D3 200 IU / Calbo-D (sq) / Rocal-D (hc) / Calcin-D (ren) / Ostoca D (skf), 1 + 1 + 0 (A/M) ----- 1 – 3 months ± ¤ If still not improved : Powder Lactohil (Purnava limited), 2 TSF medicine in 1 glass of water TDS ---- 1 – 3 months, Or, Cap. Lactogin (thp), 1 + 1 + 1 ----- 1 – 3 months

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: SUPPRESSION OF BREAST MILK If baby is dead / IUD / necessary to suppress milk secretion. Drugs to stop lactation : 1. Cabergoline is currently most effective option. Dose is 0.5 mg half tablet 12 hourly for 2 days. 2. Bromocriptine 2.5 mg twice daily for 2 weeks. To minimize side effects is best achieved by gradual introduction e.g. 2.5 mg for 3 days, then 2.5 mg BD. Tx, ¤ Tab. Bromocriptine 2.5 mg / Bromolac (sq) / Criptine (ren) / Bromodel (ops), ½ + 0 + ½ --- - 2 days, then, 1 + 0 + 1 --- 10 days. Or, ¤ Tab. Cabergolin 0.5mg / Cabergol (pop) / Cabolin (ren) / Ferticab (nuv), on the first day after delivery 1-2 tab (1 mg) once & repeat after one week, if necessary.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: BREAST ENGORGEMENT ¤ Pain and feeling of tenseness or heaviness in both the breasts. ¤ Painful breast feeding. ¤ Generalized malaise or even transient rise of temperature. ¤ Usual onset : 3rd or 4th day postpartum. ● For pain & malaise : Tab. Paracetamol / Napa (bex) 500 mg, 2 + 2 + 2 (A/M) ----- 3 – 5 days ● Support the breasts : With a binder or brassiere. ● Manual expression : If any remaining milk after each feed. Baby should be put to the breast regularly at frequent intervals. Automatic breast pump is very much effective. [Chinese breast pumps are of only around 1500/-].

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: MASTALGIA IN PUERPERIUM Lump in right/left breast with severe pain, engorgement & redness. MASTITIS ► Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg / Flux (ops) / A-Flox (acm) / Flubex (bex) / Fluclox (aci) / Phylopen DS (sq) / Flustar (ren) 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ---- 14 days ► Tab. Ibuprofen 200 mg or 400 mg / Flamex (aci) / Inflam (syn) / Profen (acm) 400 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) ----- 5 – 14 days (if pain present) ► Cap. Esomeprazole 20 mg / Alton (gen) / Esoprex (bacn) / Esoral (skf) / Esotid (ops) / Exium (rad) / Maxima (acm) / Maxpro (ren), 1 + 0 + 1 ( ½ hr B/M) ------ 14 days Advice : ¤ Continue breast feeding or express milk. ¤ Never stop breast feeding to baby, even in breast abscess & mastitis. ¤ Proper positioning & attachment for breast feeding. ¤ Refer to a surgeon, if breast abscess.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Cracked nipple / Nipple fissures Symptoms often appear during the second or third week after baby is born. Localized soreness, pain around the areola & cracked nipple is – ¤ Raw, chafed and dry. ¤ Irritated, itchy, sore or painful. Treatment : ► Mupirocin Ointment / Bactrocin (sq) / Bactrobex (bex), Apply locally 2 – 3 times daily ------ 7 – 14 days (Apply topical antibiotic alternatively if both nipples are cracked. Not both nipple at the same time. Avoid breast feeding from that nipple for 4 hours of application of ointment) Advice : ¤ Apply freshly expressed breast milk directly in the affected nipple. Kept moist using a few drops of expressed milk or vaseline. ¤ Never stop breast feeding to baby even in breast abscess & mastitis. ¤ Hot compression. ¤ Proper attachment & positioning of breast feeding. May add – ► Miconazole oral gel 2% / Micoral (aci) / Gelora (sq) / A-Migel (acm) / Mycon (aris), Apply locally 3 times daily ---- 14 days Or, ► Ointment Zinc Oxide 40% / De-Rash (sq) / Q-Rash (bex) / Happynap (drg), Apply locally 2 – 3 times daily ----- 14 days

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Flat or Inverted Nipple Having inverted nipples at puberty is totally normal and nothing to worry about. Most women with inverted nipples will still be able to breastfeed. Nipples are no longer inverted after breastfeeding for some time. Treatment for complicated inverted nipples is surgery. Advice : ¤ Gently place your thumb and forefinger around your nipple and make a rolling motion & press to pop up the nipples. ¤ Pulling the nipple outward with a syringe or suction cup device. ¤ Hard plastic breast shells worn inside bra, to put pressure around the nipple, forcing it forward.

treatment.title: Drugs Safely Used in Pg
treatment.description: Drugs Safely Used in Pg Category-A & Category-B Symptomatic Treatment Notes : - Avoid all of the anti-hypertensive drugs during pg, except Methyldopa, Nifedipine. - Avoid every lipid lower drugs (statins). - Avoid benzodiazepine & barbiturates. - Avoid anti-cancer drugs. Vitamins & minerals : Anti-vertigo : Folic acid Zinc Meclizine / Meclizine + Pyridoxine (25+50 mg) Calcium Iron Pyridoxine + Doxylamine (10+10) mg or, (20+20) mg Cinnarizine + Dimenhydrinate (Category-C) Cinnarizine 15 mg / Cinnarizine 20 mg + Dimenhydrinate 40 mg Prochlorperazine/Vergon (cat C) Anti-emetic drugs : Analgesic & Anti-pyretic : Ondansetron, Palonosetron Paracetamol / Acetaminophen. Opioids Anti-diarrhoeal opioid : Tramadol, Morphine, Pethidine Loperamide Anti-rheumatic drugs : Hydroxychloroquine, sulfasalazine, azathioprine, steroid, ciclosporin, tacrolimus and anti TNF are safe. Steroid (cat-C) – Hydrocortisone / Cotson, Dexamethasone. Anti-spasmotic : Muscle relaxants : Tiemonium Cyclobenzaprine Anti-histamine : Bronchodialator : Levocetrizine Cetirizine Chlorpheniramine Diphenhydramine Fexofenadine Loratadine Des-loratidine (cat-C, avoid). Salbutamol, Levo-salbutamol, Theophylline, Steroid Inhaler Montelukast Ipratropium + Salbutamol / Windel Plus (cat-C) Butamirate / Mirakof (sq) PPI / Anti-ulcerants : Laxative : Antacid : Magnesium hydroxide H2 blocker : Famotidine Pantoprazole Esomeprazole Lactulose Ispagula husk Magnesium Hydroxide All PPIs are almost safe. Sedatives: Non-benzodiazepines : No BDZ is safe Zolpidem and diphenhydramine Antiepileptic drugs : Anti-bleeding : Carbamazepine, Lamotrigine, Levetiracetam Tranexamic Acid (cat-B), Aminocaproic acid (cat-C) Antidepressants : Tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs): Citalopram, fluoxetine, sertraline Amitriptyline or nortriptyline Avoid anti-psychotics (Cat-C) NSAIDs : It should be used with caution in first trimester and must be avoided in last trimester because of their potential to cause premature closure of the fetal ductus arteriosus and persistent pulmonary hypertension. High doses of NSAIDs in the third trimester may also reduce perfusion of the fetal kidneys and decrease fetal urine output. Category C pain-killer – Ketorolac, Diclofenac, Tramadol etc. Note : Topical NSAIDs are safe but avoid use in large surface area. Antibiotic use during pregnancy : Following antibiotics can be used during pregnancy (category-B) : 1. All betalactam antibiotic (penicillin, cephalosporin) 2. Among carbapenems — meropenem. 3. Betalactamase inhibitor e.g. clavulanic acid, tazobactam. 4. Macrolids e.g. azithromycin, erythromycin 5. Metronidazole, Nitrofurantoin. Antibiotics : Penicillin Amoxicillin Flucloxacillin Cephalosporin Cephradine Cefixime Cefuroxime Ceftriaxone Carbapenem Merpenem Macrolides Azithromycin Erythromycin Clarithromycin Nitrofurantoin Nintoin SR (incp), Ofuran SR (paci), Umactin (uni), NFT (hc) Cap. 100 mg, 1+0+1 ---7 days Nitazoxanide Zox (sq), Nitazox (incp), Nitoxin (aris) Tab. 500 mg, 1+0+1 --- 3 days Metronidazole Against anerobic organism. (Cat-B) Anti-tubercular Category-1 anti-TB drugs are safe. Anti-malarial Quinine sulphate / Chloroquine Anti-helminth Albendazole, Mebendazole (But must not use in 1 st trimester) Oral antifungal Terbinafine, Amphotericin-B. Topical antifungal All are safe except potassium iodide Anti-diabetics : Insulin 1 st line of treatment in GDM Metformin Better avoid, but continue if PCOS. Anti-hypertensives : Methyldopa 500mg/ day increase up to 2000 mg/day Labetalol 100 mg BD can increase to 2.4 gm/day Nifedipine (Cat-C) 10-20 mg BD maximum 90 mg/day Hydralazine 5-10 mg/day

treatment.title: Definitions
treatment.description: The average length of a menstrual cycle is 28 days. However, a cycle can range in length from 21 days to about 35 days and still be normal. Periods begin at age of 11 to 16 years and continue until the menopause (usually at 45 to 55 years). Bleeding can last from 1-8 days (average 5 days) and is generally heaviest in the first 2 days. Blood loss in each period is between 20-60 mL (>80 mL is abnormal and may lead to anaemia). Oligomenorrhoea : Infrequent periods (more than 35 days between periods). Management same as amenorrhea. Hypomenorrhoea is extremely light (small amount) menstrual blood flow. Menorrhagia (heavy periods) : defined as cyclical bleeding lasting for long time with excess loss of blood ≥ 80ml/month. Dysmenorrhoea : It is abdominal pain with menstruation.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Primary amenorrhoea : No menstruation by age 16 years when growth and sexual development is normal. According to cause: e.g., PCOS, cryptomenorrhea, uterine developmental anomaly, etc. Primary amenorrhoea should be refer to gynecologist. Investigations : 1. FSH, LH, testosterone on 3 rd day of menstruation. 2. Serum prolactin 3. TSH 4. USG of W/A with special attention to pelvic organ. Secondary amenorrhoea : Absence of menstruation for more than 3 months in a previously menstruating woman. Causes of secondary amenorrhoea : Always consider the possibility of pregnancy; if not then consider other causes. 1. Physiological – excess exercise, anxiety, stress etc. 2. Weight loss/anorexia. 3. Chronic anovulation including PCOS. 4. Hyperprolactinemia 5. Thyroid dysfunction (mainly hyperthyroidism). 6. Cushing syndrome. 7. Pituitary tumor, empty sella, Sheehan’s syndrome. 8. Use of contraceptive particularly injectable contraceptive. 9. Drug history – contraceptives e.g. injectable progestogens. Other drugs include : heroin, methadone, metoclopramide. 10. Post chemo and radiotherapy or gynaecological surgery. Patient with secondary amenorrhoea may have H/O – a) Weight change – weight loss may cause amenorrhoea. b) Life crisis or upset – e.g. exams, bereavement. c) Heavy exercise – gymnasts & athletes may amenorrhoeic. d) Hirsutism – suggests PCOS or androgen-secreting tumour. e) Galactorrhoea – present in 30% cases of prolactinoma(s). f) Sweats and/or hot flushes (suggests menopause). g) Cyclical pain – suggest outflow obstruction. h) Family history of premature menopause or late menarche. Investigations : 1. Pg Test / Urinary Beta hCG. 2. USG of W/A or L/A. 3. S. FSH, LH : On 3 rd day of menstruation. 4. If LH high, hirsutism, virilism : S. Testosterone : On 3 rd day of menstruation. 5. S. Prolactin. 6. Thyroid function test-TSH, FT3, FT4 Management of secondary amenorrhoea : 1. According to the cause. 2. If amenorrhoea is due to contraceptive then – If OCP / hormonal methods stopped – periods usually return within 3 months. Or if injectable progestogens stopped – periods usually return within a year. 3. If no cause is found, ► Tab. Norethisterone 5 mg / Normens (ren) / Remens (pop), 1+1+1 or 2+2+2 ---- 7 days, then stop for next 7 days, expecting withdrawal bleeding. Then start same drug (1+1+1) for 21 days, then 7 days off for 3 cycles & monthly follow up the patient.

treatment.title: Irregular ± Excessive Menstruation
treatment.disease_name: Irregular ± Excessive Menstruation & Post Ponding Menstruation
treatment.description: Drug treatment : ► Tab. Norethisterone 5 mg / Normens (ren) / Menoral (sq) / Ethinor (skf) / Remens (pop), 1 + 1 + 1 ----- 21 days then stop for 7 days. Bleeding will occur in these 7 days. Repeat the cycle for 3 months. Advice : ○ Reassurance and counselling. ○ Follow up of patient. ○ Change of contraceptive procedure.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : Lower abdominal pain (LAP) & cramp during menstruation. Investigation : USG of W/A. Treatment : ► Tab. Mefenamic Acid 500 mg / HPR-DC (pac) / Dysmen (ren) / Fenaton 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 (A/M) ---- First 3 days of menstruation ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Opal (hc) / OP-20 (glob) / Proceptin (bex) / Inpro (bio), 1 + 0 + 1 (B/M) --- First 3 days of menstruation Or, ► Tab. Naproxen 500 mg + Esomeprazole 20 mg / Solivo (hc) / Neso (aris) / Dinovo (bex) / Naprosyn Plus (rad) / Naproxen Plus (zen) / Napsec (drg) / Napro-A Plus (acm), 1+0+1 --- 3-5 days (B/M) ± ► Tab. Tiemonium Methylsulphate 50 mg / Tinium (acm) / Norvis (sq) / Algin (ren) / Visceralgine (nuv) / Visral (ops) / Timozin (incp), (1 + 1 + 1) or, (2 + 2 + 2) ---- 3 – 5 days Advice : 1. Hot compression to lower abdomen. 2. Drink plenty of water. 3. Maintain menstrual calendar.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: It means excessive menstrual flow. Frequently associated with pain during menstruation, weakness, palpitation. PUBERTY MENORRHAGIA : Patient is in pubertal age with heavy menstrual bleeding or prolonged menstrual bleeding. Investigation : 1. USG of W/A with special attention to pelvic organ. 2. CBC, Blood grouping & Rh typing. 3. BT, CT 4. S. TSH Treatment : ► Cap. Tranexamic acid 500 mg / Traxyl (nuv) / Xamic (ren) / Tracid (acm) / Frabex (sq), 1 + 1 + 1 ------- 5 days ► Cap. Iron + Folic acid + Vit-B complex + Vit-C / Alneed (incp) / Prenat Plus (hc) / Natavit TR (jay), 0 + 1 + 0 (A/M) ----- 3months If dysmenorrhea (painful mens) : ► Tab. Mefenamic Acid 500 mg / HPR-DC (pac) / Dysmen (ren) / Fenaton 500 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 (A/M) ---- First 3 days of menstruation ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Opal (hc) / OP-20 (glob) / Proceptin (bex) / Inpro (bio), 1 + 0 + 1 (B/M) --- First 3 days of menstruation ±► Tab. Tiemonium Methylsulphate 50 mg / Algin (ren) / Viset (hc) / Norvis (sq) / Visceralgine (nuv) / Visral (ops), 1+1+1 --- 5 days Advice : 1. Reassurance. 2. Maintain menstrual calendar. 3. Avoid any hormone containing drug as much as possible. If hormone containing drug is necessary, then start with progesterone only drugs.

treatment.title: Post Ponding Menstruation
treatment.disease_name: Irregular ± Excessive Menstruation & Post Ponding Menstruation
treatment.description: Patient wants to post pond her menstruation for certain period of time (such as for attending omra or hajj). ► Tab. Norethisterone 5mg / Normens (ren) / Menoral (sq) / Ethinor (skf) / Feminor (acm) / Remens (pop) / Norestin (nuv), 1 + 1 + 1 ----- start from 3 days before estimated date of menstruation and continue till desired time span. Bleeding occur when discontinue. Or, ► Tab. Drospirenone + Ethinylestradiol / Novelon Lite (ren) / Elisa (nuv) / Rosen Gold (incp), 0+0+1 ----- continue without iron pill till desired period

treatment.title: Hypomenorrhoea (Scanty Bleeding)
treatment.description: Diagnostic tools : - Scanty menstrual bleeding. Investigations : ± Ultra sonogram (USG) of Whole abdomen (W/A). Investigate for gynecological or medical diseases. At first give symptomatic treatment & must (must) exclude gynecological systemic diseases / Malnutrition. ☼ Cap. Folic Acid 5 mg / Folison (jay), ১ + ১ + ১ ...... ৭ দিন

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: PCOS is a hormonal disorder common among women of reproductive age usually with infrequent or prolonged menstrual periods or excess male hormone (androgen) levels. Hormonal profile in PCOS : Increased ↑ LH ↑ Androgens (Testosterone) ↑ Estrogen (E1) ↑ Prolactin ↑ LDL (cholesterol) Decreased ↓ FSH ↓ Progesterone ↓ SHBG ↓ HDL Symptoms and signs : May be asymptomatic or with – 1. Missed or irregular menstrual periods – more common in obese : oligomenorrhoea, amenorrhoea, dysfunctional uterine bleeding. 2. Acne. 3. Hirsutism (Excess facial/body hair growth). 4. Infertility. 5. Weight gain / central obesity. 6. Male pattern baldness. Common presentations : Less menstrual flow, irregular or infrequent menstruation, usually no dysmenorrhea, H/O weight gain, excess facial hair / hirsutism. Investigations : 1. USG of whole abdomen- defined as the presence of >12 follicles in each ovary measuring 2–9 mm in diameter and/or ovarian volume >10 cm 3 . 2. FSH, LH and testosterone : On 3 rd day of menstruation. S. testosterone – Increase [>2.5 nmol/L]. If >4.8 nmol/L, exclude other causes of androgen hypersecretion, e.g., tumour, cushing’s syndrome. Other hormone analysis : S. Prolactin, S. TSH. 3. FBS, 2HABF. PCOS requires the presence of two of the following three features : 1. Menstrual irregularity – oligomenorrhoea and/or anovulation. 2. Clinical or biochemical androgen excess. 3. Multiple cysts in the ovaries. Management : Non-pharmacological : ○ Weight loss & physical exercise. ○ Avoid oily, fatty food and fast foods. ○ Walking 30 minutes daily (or 5 day/week). Pharmacological : 1. If oligomenorrhoeic consider progestogens to induce a withdrawal bleed every 2-3 months to decrease risk of endometrial hyperplasia. 2. Consider the combine oral contraceptive (COC) pill to regulate menstruation; COC pills with anti-androgen (e.g. cocyprindiol) may decrease acne/hirsutism. 3. Clomiphene can be used to induce ovulation. When a patient with PCOS wants to conceive: Tab. Zinc + Folic acid, 0+1+0 ---- 3 months. Advice : coitus every alternative day from 9 th to 17 th day of menstrual cycle. 4. Metformin may be helpful for insulin sensitivity and menstrual disturbance. Also used for infertility if clomiphene has failed. 5. Hirsutism – cosmetic measures such as shaving, bleaching, waxing electrolysis and laser treatment are effective for small areas. Eflornithine cream may reduce hair growth when applied daily to affected areas of the face. If all above fails then anti-androgen therapy. Tab. Spironolactone 50 mg, 1+0+1 ----- 6 months.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Model Treatment : ► Tab. Norethisterone 5 mg / Normens (ren), 1+1+1 ----- 10 days ► Tab. Metformin/Comet XR(sq) 500 mg, 1+0+1 – 3 months (A/M) ► Tab. Inositol Nicotinate 500 or 750 mg / Myosit (ren) / Nicosit (incp) 750 mg, 1+0+1 or 1+1+1 ----- 3 months When a patient with PCOS wants to conceive : ► Tab. Folic Acid + Zinc / Zifolet (sq) / Folic-Z (ops), 0+0+1 ---- 3 month ► Tab. Metformin / Comet XR (sq) / Met XR (ops) 500 mg, 1+0+0 ---- continue (A/M) ► Tab. Clomiphene Citrate 50 mg or 100 mg / Ovulet (ren) / Fertil (bex) / Ovuclon (incp), 100 mg daily for 5 days. Note : Starting any day between 2 nd to 5 th day of the menstrual cycle & continue for next 5 days. Repeat for 3 menstrual cycles, but if pregnancy has not occurred after a total of about 6 cycles, further clomiphene therapy is not recommended. Refer to specialist (obstetrician or endocrinologist) if a PCOS patient wants to become pregnant, as they have low ovarian reserve. So judicial use of ovulation inducing drugs is mandatory. Advice : Coital act in between 10 th to 20 th day of menstrual cycle.

treatment.title: Vaginal Discharge
treatment.description: Consider abnormal vaginal discharge if associated with itching, sore, per vaginal bleeding, foul smelling etc. Or, amount increases than normal (leukorrhoea). Most causes of abnormal PV discharge : 1. Excessive normal secretions (leukorrhoea) 2. Bacterial vaginosis (BV) 3. Candida albicans 4. Cervicitis (gonococcal, chlamydial or herpetic) 5. Trichomonas vaginalis (TV)

treatment.title: Leukorrhoea
treatment.description: Leukorrhoea is excess of normal secretion. No other abnormality. C/F : ● P/V discharge, which is Non-irritating, Non-foul smelling. ● Usually increases before & after menstruation. Characteristics of normal vaginal fluid : It is watery, white in color, non-odorous with pH acidic around 4.0. Microscopically, it contains squamous epithelial cells and a few bacteria, Lactobacilli, few gram-negative bacteria and anaerobes are present without any white or red blood cells. Physiological discharge varies considerably & is affected by the menstrual cycle. ○ Before ovulation — mucus is Clear, watery & slippery. ○ After ovulation — mucus is thicker & sticker. When to consider abnormal vaginal discharge? 1. Amount increase than normal. 2. Foul smelling (offensive). 3. Associated with itching (pruritus) & per vaginal (PV) bleeding. No drug treatment indicated : ► Tab. Vitamin B Complex, 0+0+1 – 3 months ► Tab. Albendazole 400 mg, 1 tab stat & repeat after 7 days. Advise : ø Assure that its normal physiology. ø Maintain personal hygiene. ø Wash private parts with lukewarm water. ø Use cotton undergarments. ø Pill user may have to stop the pill temporarily. ø Cleaning of vagina after sexual intercourse. ø Urination before & after sexual intercourse. If leucorrhea is due to cervical cause like cervical ectopy or chronic cervicitis, polyp or ectropion : Electrocautery or cryosurgery.

treatment.title: D/D of vaginal discharge
treatment.description: Characteristics Tricho-moniasis Discharge Profuse Gray, yellow or green Consistency Thin, frothy, adherent Odor Unpleasant Pruritus +++ dysuria Diagnosis (Wet mount preparation) Motile tricho-monas Treatment T. Metro-nidazole, 1+1+1–7 days Characteristics Bacterial vaginosis Discharge Gray white to green yellow Consistency Thin, adherent homo-geneous Odor Fishy/Foul Pruritus Non-irritating Diagnosis (Wet mount preparation) Clue cells (>20%) Treatment T. Metro-nidazole, 1+1+1–7 days Characteristics Chlamydia Discharge Muco-purulent Consistency Thick Odor - Pruritus - Diagnosis (Wet mount preparation) Chlamydia NAAT Treatment T. Azithro-mycin 1g single dose Characteristics Candidiasis Discharge Small curdy white Consistency Thick Odor - Pruritus ++ Diagnosis (Wet mount preparation) Hyphae, buds & spores Treatment Oral & intra-vaginal antifungal

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic clue : 1. Scanty, thick, whitish vaginal discharge. 2. Irritating, Foul smelling P/V discharge. 3. Valval or vaginal burning 4. Itching. 5. Dyspareunia, Dysuria. Treatment : ► Tab. Metronidazole / Filmet (bex) / Flagyl (syn) / Metryl (ops) / Amodis (sq) / Metro (zis), 1+1+1 ---- 4 – 7 days Or, Tab. Ornidazole 500mg / Robic (sq) / Ornid (drg) / Ornil (ops) / Troniz (uni) / Xynor (bex), 1+0+1 ---- 5 days Or, Tab. Secnidazole 1000 mg / Secnid DS (sq) / Secnidal DS (syn) / Secnizol DS (incp), 2 tab single dose before meal for both partners (0+0+2 ---- 1 day). + ► Cap. Doxycycline 100 mg / Doxicap (ren) / Doxin (ops) / Doxy-A (acm) / Doxacil (sq), 1+0+1 ---- 14 days ± ► [if married] Vaginal Suppository Metronidazole + Neomycin + Polymyxin B + Nystatin / Gynomix (drg) / VCAP (ren) / Gynepro (sq) / Tricomix (incp) / Gynoril (ops), প্রত্যহ রাতে মাসিকের (শরীরের) পথে দিবেন ---- ১২ দিন ± ► Tab. Levocetirizine / Alcet (hc) / Seasonix (incp) / Sinacet (ibn), ০ + ০ + ১ বা, ১ + ০ + ১ ----- ৫ দিন Advice : ø Maintain personal hygiene. ø Avoidance of coitus for 2-3 weeks. ø Improved diet. ø Follow up of the Patient. ø Reassurance and counselling. ø Drug treatment for both female & male partners. PRURITUS VULVAE : 'Pruritus vulvae' simply means itching of the vulva.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : ● Curdy white vaginal discharge of small amount. ● Usually no or little itching & odor. Treatment : ► Vaginal Tablet Clotrimazole 200 mg / Neosten VT (bex) / Afun VT (sq), apply intravaginal 3 consecutive nights. Or, Dermasim VT (aci) single dose at night (500 mg). ► Cap. Fluconazole / Flugal (sq) 150 mg PO weekly for 6 weekly. Or, Tab. Terbinafine 250 mg / Terbex (bex) / Xfin (sq), PO weekly for 6 weeks.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Labial adhesion is the fusion of the labia minora or majora, e.g., no visual vaginal opening in old aged or newborn, child Treatment : ► Vaginal Cream Estriol 0.1% / Femastin (sq) / Gynest (acm), Apply 2 times daily for 1 week then, Apply 1 time daily for 2 weeks. ► Personal hygiene. ► If no improvement, then refer to gynecologist.

treatment.title: Cause & approach
treatment.description: Most common causes of lower abdominal pain (LAP) : 1. UTI 2. Dysmenorrhoea 3. Fibroid 4. Ovarian cyst 5. PID 6. Abortion 7. Ectopic pregnancy Approach to patient with lower abdominal pain in female : Associated with menstrual cycle Dysmenorrhoea With short H/O amenorrhoea & P/V/B Ectopic pregnancy, Abortion Associated with menorrhagia Fibroid Associated with fever, frequency, dysuria UTI Absolute constipation, abdominal pain, vomiting Intestinal obstruction

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Infection ascending from the endocervix causing endometritis, salpingitis, para metritis, oophoritis, tubo-ovarian abscess and/or pelvic peritonitis. PID is an infection of upper genital tract includes the uterus, ovaries, fallopian tubes and cervix. It's usually caused by a sexually transmitted infection (STI) like chlamydia or gonorrhea. PID often doesn't cause any obvious symptoms. Most women have mild symptoms that may include any of the followings : Usual Symptoms : • Pain around the pelvis or lower abdomen. • Discomfort or pain during sex (Deep dyspareunia - pain felt deep inside the pelvis). • Bleeding between periods and after sex. • Heavy periods, Painful periods. • Dysuria (Pain during urination). • Unusual vaginal discharge, especially yellow or green. • In acute cases : - severe lower abdominal pain & high. - fever / high temperature. - feeling and being sick. Criteria for diagnosis PID : Minimum criteria for diagnosis include : Must be present. Lower abdominal pain may worse when move. • Cervical motion tenderness and/or, • Uterine tenderness and/or, • Adnexal tenderness. Plus any of the following criteria : • Fever / Temperature greater than 38° C. • Abnormal cervical discharge. • Pelvic abscess or inflammatory complex on bimanual examination. • Leukocytosis > 10,000 WBC/mL. • Elevated ESR or elevated C-reactive protein (CRP). Definitive criteria • Histopathologic evidence of endometritis. • Imaging showing thickened fluid-filled tubes with or without free pelvic fluid or tubo-ovarian complex mass. • Laparoscopic abnormalities consistent with PID. Physical examination 1. Lower abdominal and pelvic tenderness remains the most consistent finding in PID. 2. Swelling of the adnexa or a true adnexal mass is noted in 10% of PID patients 3. Rebound lower abdominal tenderness suggesting pelvic peritonitis occurs in only 25% of PID patients. Investigation 1. CBC, RBS, Urine R/E. 2. Gram stain of the endocervical secretions. 3. High vaginal swab for C/S. 4. VDRL, TPHA. 5. Ultrasound evaluation is of limited value in diagnosing acute PID. However, it is 95% accurate for detecting pelvic abscesses and can help monitor response to medical therapy. 6. Laparoscopy or laparotomy provides immediate and accurate diagnosis.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Antibiotics & Supportive measures : a) Bed rest, sexual abstinence (until the results of pelvic examination become normal), hydration and analgesics should be encouraged. b) IUD should be removed (if in utero) after antibiotic therapy has been initiated. Contraceptive counseling should be offered at the time of IUD removal. Treatment (both husband & wife) : ► Tab. Ofloxacin 400 mg / Rutix (sq) / Oflacin (drg) / Flocet (ops), ১ + ০ + ১ ----- ১৪ দিন অথবা, ► Cap. Doxycycline 100 mg / Doxicap (ren) / Doxin (ops) / Doxy-A (acm) / Doxacil (sq), ১ + ০ + ১ ----- ১৪ দিন + ► Tab. Metronidazole 400mg / Metryl (ops) / Amodis (sq) / Filmet (bex) / Flagyl (syn) / Flamyd (incp) / Nidazyl (ori), ১ + ১ + ১ ----- ৫ – ১৪ দিন অথবা, ► Tab. Ornidazole 500mg / Robic (sq) / Ornid (drg) / Ornil (ops) / Troniz (uni) / Xynor (bex), ১ + ০ + ১ ----- ৫ – ১৪ দিন + ► Tab. Naproxen 500 mg + Esomeprazole 20 mg / Neso (aris) / Naprosyn Plus (rad) / Dinovo (bex) / Naparo-A Plus (acm) / Naproxen Plus (zen) / Solivo (hc) 500/20 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ----- ৫ দিন Treatment outlines : General measures : • Antipyretic and NSAID for fever and pain. • Counselling and contract tracing. • Concurrent treatment of partner. • Abstinence and contraceptive. Specific treatment : • Antibiotic therapy. • Surgical Intervention – ✓ Laparotomy and peritoneal lavage. ✓ TAH ± BSO (may be needed). Follow up : Close follow up is required at 72 hours or earlier if symptoms are failing to settle. Review at 2 weeks to assess for response to treatment and the development of any recent complications (eg- tubo-ovarian abscess.) Complications : • Infertility. • Chronic pelvic pain. • Increased incidence of ectopic pregnancy. • Increased risk of further episodes of PID. • Tubo-ovarian abscess and pelvic abscess. Standard Antibiotic therapy : • Ceftriaxone 500 mg IM as one dose plus • Doxycycline 100 mg orally twice a day for 14 days plus • Metronidazole 400 mg orally 12 hourly for 14 days.

treatment.title: Fitz~-Hugh-Curtis (FHC) syndrome
treatment.description: Fitz~-Hugh-Curtis (FHC) syndrome It occurs in 5% to 10% of cases of PID. It is associated with symptoms of pleuritic right upper quadrant abdominal pain referred to the corresponding shoulder or peri-hepatitis with mild liver function test abnormalities. FHC syndrome is secondary to either gonococcal or chlamydial infection spreading from the fallopian tubes along the paracolic gutters to the upper abdomen. A prompt response to antibiotics and the recognition of concurrent PID help distinguish FHC syndrome from cholecystitis.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: Abnormal Uterine Bleeding (AUB) / Dysfunctional uterine bleeding (DUB)
treatment.description: AUB is bleeding from uterine corpse that is abnormal in volume, regularity and/or timing and has present for the majority of the past 6 months (FIGO). Diagnostic clue : Excessive vaginal bleeding without any organic cause. Cause of / Classify as PALM-COEIN : • AUB-A-Adenomyosis. • AUB-L-Leiomyoma. • AUB-M- Malignancy & hyperplasia. • AUB-C-Coagulopathy. • AUB-O-Ovulatory dysfunction. • AUB-E- Endometrial dysfunction. • AUB-I- Iatrogenic. • AUB-N- Not otherwise classified. Diagnosis conferenced by investigation : • USG of lower abdomen (L/A). • TVS (if needed). • Coagulation profile. • Hormone assay : FSH, LH, prolactin, TSH. • Pap’s smear. • Histopathology of biopsy material. AUB Management – according to age groups Puberty / Adolescent Reproductive age group Baseline Investigations : • CBC with ESR • Blood grouping & Rh typing. • Thyroid function test – FT3, FT4, TSH • Coagulation Profile – BT, CT, PT, APTT • USG of L/A or W/A Exclude Pregnancy & it’s complications General and Pelvic Examination Exclude Cervical Polyp / Malignancy Observation for 3 months and supportive management : • Anemia : Hematinics (Iron, folate) • Pain : NSAID • Bleeding : Anti-fibrinolytic agents ↓ Poor Response If no need of contraceptives : • Progesterone • NSAIDs • Anti-Fibrinolytic drugs • GnRH analog (in severe cases) If need of contraceptives : • Combined OCP • Mirena (if available) ↓ Follow Up 6-9 months ↓ Poor Response / Failed Referral AUB in Peri-menopausal/ Post-menopausal women ↓ General and Pelvic Examination : Exclude Cervical Polyp / Malignancy ↓ Base line investigation as per suspect + USG of L/A or preferably TVS-assessment of endometrial thickness ↓ ● Endometrial thickness > 4mm : Endometrial Curettage for Histopathology : Refer if Endometrial Atypia/Malignancy. ● Endometrial thickness ↓ Observation for 3 months and supportive management : • Anemia : Hematinic. • Pain : NSAID. • Bleeding : Anti-fibrinolytic agents. ↓ If recurs Medical Treatment : Progesterone/ GnRH for 3-6 cycles. ↓ If recurs / Fails Referral & Surgical treatment : Hysterectomy / TAH ± BSO. Investigations : 1. USG of WA 2. VIA or PAP smear 3. S. TSH If symptom isn't resolved : Refer to gynecologist.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.disease_name: Abnormal Uterine Bleeding (AUB) / Dysfunctional uterine bleeding (DUB)
treatment.description: Drugs and Materials needed in AUB Hormonal drugs : • Progesterone – Norethisterone / Dydrogesterone : 5- 10mg tds for (1 to 3) cycles or, (3 to 6) cycles. • OCP – combined pill for 3 cycles. • GnRH analog- Inj. Luporin– one inj. monthly for 6 months • Tab. Thyroxine 50 microgram – if hypothyroidism, Dose – according to hormone level. Hematinics : • Inj. Feric Carboxymaltose 500mg – weekly for 2-3 weeks. (If Hb level Or, • Oral Iron polymaltose with folic acid and zinc with/without vit-B complex – (1-2) cap daily for 3 months. NSAIDs : • Mefenemic acid-(250-500) mg bd for 5 days. Or, • Ketorolac 10mg tds for 5 days. Or, • Naproxen 500mg bd for 5 days. Anti-fibrinolytic agents : • Inj. Tranexamic acid – 500mg tds for 24 hrs (If severe bleeding). • Cap. Tranexamic acid – 500mg tds for 2-3 days. C/C : 1. Excessive per vaginal bleeding. 2. Lower abdominal pain during menstruation. Model Treatment : ✔ Tab. Lynestrenol 5 mg / Orgatril (nuv), 2 + 2 + 2 ----- 5 days, Then, 1 + 1 + 1 ----- 5 th to 25 th day of menstruation for 3 month ✔ Tab. Mefenamic acid/Dysmen (ren) 500mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) – 5 days ✔ Cap. Omeprazole / Omep (ops) 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ------ 5 days ✔ Cap. Tranexamic Acid / Traxyl (nuv) 500 mg, 1+1+1 ----- 5 days ✔ Tab. Iron / Hemofix FZ (bex), 0+1+0 (A/M) ------ 3 months Surgical option : Post operative drugs after hysterectomy / TAH ± BSO : • Cap. Cefixime 400mg / Ciprofloxacin 500mg bd for 7 days & Tab. Metronidazole(400mg) tds for 5 days. • Tab ketorolac 10mg tds for 5 days. • Cap Omeprazole 20mg bd for 7 days. • Tab. Calcium with vitamins- 1 tab bd for 1 month. Advice : • Normal body movement except heavy lifting. • Abstinence for 6 weeks. • Follow up after 7 days.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Uterine Fibroid / Leiomyoma Benign smooth muscle tumors of the uterus. Risk factors : • Obesity • Reproductive years • Early menarche & late menopause • Osteogenic hormone therapy • ↑ Intake of Red Meat • Family history of Fibroids • Differing pregnancy / infertility Adverse pregnancy outcomes : • Miscarriage • Threatened abortion • Premature labor (if large tumor) • Mal-presentation • IUGR • Prolonged obstructed labour • PPH Diagnosis by Symptom & Signs : • Heavy Periods ± Pain / Heavy menstrual bleeding. • Urinary Frequency / Urgency (If large & push over the bladder base). • Pain during coitus. • Sub fertility / Recurrent abortion. • Abdominal Mass – firm, mobile, midline lower abdominal mass with negative get below the swelling. (when large > 12 weeks size) • Anemia (secondary to increased p/v bleeding). • Sometime asymptomatic. O/E : Anaemia (+/++/+++), Lump in lower abdomen : uterus is ‘___’ weeks in size, firm, mobile side to side. Investigations for diagnosis – ➢ USG of L/A ➢ TVS- sometimes needed when small fibroids or submucous type Investigations : 1. CBC 2. USG of WA 3. VIA or PAP smear Advice : Do TVS after 3 months. Doses of some important drugs Ulipristal acetate - (5mg) 1 tab daily for 3 months (check SGPT & S. creatinine before and after treatment). Cabergoline (0.5mg) : one-tab weekly for 2-3 months Danazol (200mg) : 1-tab bd for 3-6 months Aromatase inhibitor - tab Letrozole 2.5mg/day for 3-6 months GnRH analog - eg- Inj. Lucrin -3.75mg monthly for 6 month/ 11.25mg 3 monthly for 2 doses. Combined oral contraceptive pill (COC) [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 168]

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Treatment of Fibroid Uterus : A. • No treatment (Fibroids shrink after menopause). • Observation and Follow Up (F/U). • Surgical / drug treatment if fertility concern. B. If symptoms predominant : Control of bleeding : • Antifibrinolytic drug – e.g. Tranexamic acid. • Combined oral contraceptive pill (COC) • Progesterone • Ulipristal acetate Correction of anaemia : • Oral iron therapy- • Injectable iron therapy • Blood transfusion (BT) Control of pain : • NSAIDs : Ibuprofen / Mefenamic acid • Antispasmodic drug C. If large size cause mechanical difficulty : Medications aimed at shrinking tumors : • Ulipristal acetate • GnRH analogues D. If not improved : Recent Conservative approach – to control of symptoms. • Ultrasonic fibroid destruction / HIFU (High intensity focused ultrasound) • Radiofrequency ablation • Uterine artery embolization Or, Surgical treatment : Surgical approaches – • Open • Laparoscopic. Surgical technique – • Myomectomy – if fertility desired. • Hysterectomy – if fertility not desired. Model Prescription : If less than 5 – 7 cm : ✔ Tab. Ulipristal 5 mg / Uliroid (zis) / Utal (sq) / Preroid (ren) / Fibropress (nuv) / Uliprist (uni), 0+0+1 ------ 3 months ✔ Tab. Mefenamic Acid 500 mg, 1+1+1(A/M) ----- 5 days ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 ( ½ hr B/M) ----- 5 days ✔ Cap. Tranexamic acid 500 mg, 1+1+1 ----- 5 days Advice : Follow up (F/U) after 3 months with TVS report. If more than 5 – 7 cm : Refer to gynecologist.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Usually, >40 years of age. 2. Excessive per vaginal bleeding. 3. Moderate to severe dysmenorrhea. Investigations : 1. CBC 2. USG of WA 3. TSH 4. VIA or PAP smear During menstruation : ✔ Tab. Mefenamic Acid 500 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) ----- 5 days ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ----- 5 days ✔ Cap. Tranexamic acid 500 mg, 1+1+1 ----- 5 days ✔ Tab. Iron + Folic acid + Zinc / Hemofix FZ (bex) / Feglo-FZ (aci), 0+1+0 ---- 3 months Advice : 1. Continue the drugs for 3 months. 2. Repeat TVS after 3 months & come for F/U.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Lower abdominal pain (LAP). 2. Menorrhagia. 3. Dysmenorrhea. Investigations : 1. USG of W/A. 2. S. TSH (Tiny : Few mm) During menstruation : ✔ Tab. Mefenamic Acid 500 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) ----- 5 days ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ----- 5 days ✔ Cap. Tranexamic acid 500 mg, 1+1+1 ----- 5 days ✔ Tab. Iron + Folic acid + Zinc / Hemofix FZ (bex) / Feglo-FZ (aci), 0+1+0 ----- 3 months Follow up with : 1. If Married : TVS after 3 months. 2. If Unmarried: USG of pelvic organs with special attention to ovaries. OVARIAN CYST (3 – 7 cm) : C/C : Lower abdominal pain with lump in lower abdomen Per abdomen : - Soft / firm / hard mass in iliac region, - Mobile in all direction. Investigations : 1. USG of W/A : If unmarried & TVS : If married. 3. VIA : If >30 years. 4. CA 19-9. 5. CA 125. 6. LDH. Model Prescription : ► Tab. Drospirenone + Ethinylestradiol (0.02 mg + 3 mg) / Novelon Lite (ren) / Elisa (nuv) / Rosen Gold (incp), 0 + 0 + 1 ---- continue from 1 st day of menstruation ► Tab. Tiemonium methylsulphate 50 mg / Algin (ren) / Viset (hc) / Tinium (acm) / Onium (ori), 1+1+1 ----- 5 days, if pain present. ► Cap. Omeprazole / Omenix (incp) / Seclo (sq) 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ----- 5 days or till hyper-acidity present. Follow up : ○ If unmarried: USG of pelvic organs with special attention to ovaries. If married : TVS after 3 months. ○ Refer to gynecologist: If size increases. Must refer it >9 cm.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Irregular per vaginal bleeding for long time period. 2. Post coital bleeding. 3. Per vaginal discharge. P/V/E : Cauliflower like growth, bleeds on touch. Investigation : Screening : PAP smear. PAP smear of lady > 30 years of age with post coital & irregular bleeding is done in most govt. facilities. Investigation for confirmation : cervical biopsy and histopathology. Rx on admission : ● Tab. B/C, 1+1+1. ● Tab. F/S, 1+0+1. ● Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg, 1+0+1. ● Tab. Metronidazole 400 mg, 1+1+1. ● Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M). Specific treatment : Radiotherapy, Chemotherapy, Surgery or Combinations.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Genital prolapse is the descent of the pelvic organs secondary to the rupture or weakness of the pelvic floor support. It is usually associated anteriorly with prolapse of the urethra or bladder (urethrocele, cystocele) or posteriorly with rectal mucosa (rectocele) or both. Diagnosis clinically : Symptoms : • Something coming down P/V. • Usually postmenopausal & multipara. • Difficulty in micturition– Incomplete voiding, dysuria, frequency. • Difficulty in defecation – Pt may have to push the prolapsed area up for completion of defecation. • Backache – Dragging sensation in back. • P/V reddish discharge – if decubitus ulcer. Signs : Per abdominal exam visually normal Bimanual exam – prolapsed area can be seen on inspection and /or on cough reflex test, stage can be assessed also. Categories / Classification : A. 1st degree (I) – the uterus is in the upper half of the vagina. (only can be felt in bimanual exam). B. 2nd degree (II) – the uterus has descended nearly to the opening of the vagina. (+ve cough reflex test). C. 3rd degree (III) – the uterus protrudes out of the vagina. D. Procidentia (IV) – the uterus is completely out of the vagina. 1° = External os below the level of ischial spine. 2° = External os protrudes outside the vaginal introitus. 3° = Uterine body remain outside the vaginal introitus. Operative treatment needed in 3rd degree (III) & Procidentia (IV) and also in 2nd degree (II) prolapse (in symptomatic cases).

treatment.title: Management & Operative
treatment.description: Medical treatment : Ciprofloxacin & metronidazole at initial stage. ¤ Tab. Ciprofloxacin / Ciprocin (sq) 500 mg (1+0+1). ¤ Tab. Metronidazole / Metryl (ops) 400 mg (1+1+1). ¤ Cap. Omeprazole / Progut (pop) 20 mg (1+0+1) [B/M]. ¤ Vaginal pack if decubitus ulcer present. For Pack : Sanitary pad with Econazole 1% / Econate (incp) cream & Chlorhexidine / Hexitane (aci) cream. Specific Management : • Pre-anesthetic checkup for fitness to surgery. • Surgery – vaginal hysterectomy with pelvic floor repair under spinal anesthesia (G/A rarely needed). ○ Vaginal hysterectomy (VH) with anterior (if cystocele present) or posterior colpo-perineo-rrhaphy (if rectocele or lax perineum present). ○ Manchester repair ('Fothergill's operation') : Performed if family is incomplete or young patient & with tubectomy : if family complete. Pre-operative Investigation : • Complete Blood count • Blood sugar – post prandial/ OGTT • S. creatinine • HBs Ag • Blood grouping & Rh typing • Urine R/M/E • X-ray Chest PA view • USG of lower abdomen • ECG ± • Echocardiography – If needed ± • VIA/pap’s smear test Post-operative management ➢ Oral antibiotic after 24 hrs. ➢ Continuous catheterization – 3-5 days. ➢ Patient can be discharged on 4 th /5 th POD. ➢ Cap. Cefixime 400mg / Ciprofloxacin 500mg, bd for 7 days ± ➢ Cap. Flucloxacilin 500mg, qds (6 hrly) for 7 days ➢ Tab. Metronidazole 400mg, tds (8 hrly) for 5 days ➢ Tab ketorolac 10mg, tds (8 hrly) for 5 days ➢ Cap Omeprazole 20mg, bd (12 hrly) for 7 days ➢ Tab. Calcium with vitamins, 1-tab bd (1+1+0) for 1 month.

treatment.title: Definitions
treatment.description: It is a laceration of the skin and other soft tissue structures which, in women, separate the vagina from the anus (so called gynaecological perineum). • History of tear in vagina/perineum during delivery/rape/trauma. • Inability to hold feces (3rd & 4th degree). Degrees and severity of tears : First-degree tear – small, skin-deep tears which usually heal naturally. Second-degree tear – deeper tears affecting the muscle and fascia of the perineum as well as the skin. Third-degree tear – extends into the muscle that controls the anus (external anal sphincter). Fourth-degree tear – if the tear extends further into the lining of the anus or rectum it is known as a fourth-degree tear. 1° Laceration / injury to hymen. 2° hymen + Posterior vaginal wall. 3° hymen + Posterior vaginal wall + External & Internal anal sphincters. 4° hymen + Posterior vaginal wall + External & Internal anal sphincters + Rectal mucosa. • 1st & 2nd degree perineal tear need not usually to be repaired. Oral Antibiotics and daily hipbath with antiseptic solution is enough usually to heal this tear. Second degree tears sometimes need correction if patient demands / symptom present. • 3rd & 4th degree tear needs surgical correction. COMPLETE PERINEAL TEAR (CPT) : Inability to hold feces (3rd & 4th degree). Within 24 hours After 24 hours ↓ ↓ Hospitalization & repair. Oral Antibiotics for 7-10 days and daily hipbath with antiseptic solution until it heals. ↓ after 3 months Hospitalization for repair.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ CPT ▸ BT Demand (if severe or ongoing blood loss) ¤ Diet – Liquid. ¤ Syp. Lactulose 200 ml / DLAC (drg) / Actilac (hc), 15 to 30 ml orally stat & TDS. ± ¤ Inf. C/S / Acme’s C/S, 1 to 2 L, I/V stat @ 20 d/m (if loose stool). ¤ Inj. Ciprofloxacin 100 ml / Beuflox (incp) / Ciprox (ops), 1 bag I/V stat & BD. ¤ Inj. Metronidazole 100 ml / Flamyd (incp) / Metro (osl), 1 bag I/V stat & TDS. ¤ Cap. Esomeprazole 20mg / Esotid (ops) / Sergel (hc), 1 + 0 + 1 (½ hr B/M). ± ¤ Cap. Tranexamic acid 500mg/ Tracid (acm) / Xamic (ren), 1 + 1 + 1 (if bleeding present). ¤ Inj. Diclofenac (75 mg/3 ml) / Voltaren (nuv) / Diclofen (ops), 1 amp I/M OD or BD. If severe pain : ¤ Inj. Nalbuphine (20mg/2ml) / Raltrox-2 (ops) / Nalbun-2 (incp) / Newbain (sq), 1 amp I/M stat & sos. c̄ - ¤ Inj. Ondansetron (8mg/4ml) / Anset (ops), 1 amp IV stat & sos.

treatment.title: Operative
treatment.description: Investigation : • Complete blood count, • Random blood sugar/OGTT, • S Creatinine, HBsAg, • Blood grouping & Rh typing, • Urine R/M/E & C/S, • X-Ray chest P/A view, • ECG. Pre-operative preparation : • 2 days low residual diet, 2 days non-residual diet. • NPO before the day of operation. • Broad spectrum antibiotics. • Laxatives. • 1-day preparation may be effective. (Inj. Osmosol 1 glass, if dehydration – Inf. Cholera saline) Suture Material : • Vaginal Mucosa – 2/0 Vicryl (Round Body). • Rectal Mucosa – 3/0 Vicryl (Round Body). • Rectal Sphincter – 1/0 PDS. Post-operative & discharge management : • NPO for one day of operation, next 2 days- non-residual diet, next 2 days- low residual diet then normal diet. • Broad spectrum antibiotics for 7 days. • Abstinence for 2 months. • Laxatives for one month. • Stay free from constipation & hardening of stools. • Take more vegetables and water. Keep the wound clean.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Premenstrual syndrome (PMS) a combination of physical and emotional symptoms that many women get after ovulation and before the start of their menstrual period. Diagnostic tools : Hundreds of symptoms are linked with PMS. The most common are – a) Psychological – Emotional instability : mood swings, nervous, palpitation, tension, irritability etc. When severe, termed as premenstrual dysphoric disorder (PMDD). b) Physical – abdominal bloating, weight, breast tenderness, headache, body ache, feet cramming. c) Behavioural – reduce visuospatial and cognitive ability, increase chance of accidents. Ask the patient to keep a diary to establish cyclical unexplained symptoms & behavioral nature for more than 2 cycles. Investigation usually not necessary. Diagnosis is made by excluding medical or psychiatric disorders.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Management : ► If mild/moderate symptoms : Try lifestyle / dietary modification first – 1. Inform family members when symptoms are likely to be worst & seek their emotional support. 2. Wear loose clothes if feeling bloated. 3. Ensure adequate sleep and take regular exercise. 4. Eat regularly, small, frequent meals may help; avoid sweet. 5. Snacks between meals; make sure diet is low in fat / salt, caffeine, alcohol, and contains plenty of fruit / vegetables and complex carbohydrate (e.g. bread, pasta, rice, potatoes). 6. Decrease fluid intake or eat diuretic foods, (e.g. strawberries, watermelon, aubergines, prunes, figs, parsley) to ease fluid retention. ► Consider drug therapy or CBT – if symptoms are severe or do not respond to diet / lifestyle measures. 1) Hormonal drugs : a) Combine oral pill (COC) – first line treatment, given cyclically or continuously. b) Low dose estrogen – second-line treatment. c) GnRH analogues – reserved for specialist care. Usually given in combination with add-on HRT. 2) Antidepressants – SSRI is the first line treatment that can be given continuously or just in the luteal phase (days 15-28). Those drugs decrease physical as well as psychological symptoms. a) Fluoxetine has been studied in the most detail and is effective in doses as small as 5 mg and up to 20 mg per day. b) Paroxetine 5 to 30 mg/day, and c) Sertraline 25 to 150 mg/day are probably equally as effective. d) Diuretic – spironolactone is effective for bloating/breast tenderness. e) NSAIDs – particularly helpful for premenstrual pain and to decrease menstrual bleeding. f) Surgery – hysterectomy with oophorectomy is curative. Most women require HRT / testosterone replacement afterwards. g) Complementary therapy – pyridoxine 25 to 100 mg/day, vitamin E 400 to 600 IU/day, calcium and magnesium daily during the luteal phase. ► Refer to a Gynecologist – if symptoms are severe or primary care management is ineffective. NB : Wait for at least 3 to 6 months for switching from any treatment option.

treatment.title: Model Prescription
treatment.description: Model Prescription : ✔ Tab. Pyridoxine 20 or 25 mg / Sixvit (bacn) / Pyrovit (pdesh) / Pyrol (jay), 1+1+1+1 ----- 14 days (during luteal phase) ✔ Tab. Mefenamic acid / HPR-DC (pac) / Dysmen (ren) / Fenaton (drg) 500 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) ---- 5 days (during symptom) ✔ Cap. Fluoxetine 20 mg / Prodep (sun) / Prolert (sq) / Modipran (bex) / Nodep (gen), 0+0+1 ------ 2 weeks ± ✔ Tab. Spironolactone 100 mg/day / Spirocard 100 mg (pop), 1+0+0 --- 2 weeks, if bloatedness, irritability, swelling, weight gain, breast tenderness. If above measures fail : ✔ Tab. Medroxyprogesterone 10 mg / Medroxy (pop) / Medrogest (ren) / Mepogest (drg) 10 mg, 1+1+1 ----- 3 months with 7 days intervals after every 21 days Advice : 1. Daily charting of symptoms for 2 months. 2. Dietary modifications. 3. Regular exercise. 4. Counselling & psychological support.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: EARLY MENOPAUSE Stop menstruation/amenorrhea before 40 years of age. Menstrual history : LMP, Exclude pregnancy. Investigations : S. TSH, FSH, LH. Treatment : Counselling, Reassurance & symptomatic Tx. ○ Tab. Vit B Complex / Cap. B-50 forte (sq), 0+0+1 – 1-3 months ○ Tab. Calcium 500 mg / Ostocal (skf), 0+1+0 –– 1- 3 months HRT is recommended until the average age of menopause (51 yrs).

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Menopause : Menopause is defined as the point in time when menstrual cycles permanently cease due to the natural depletion of ovarian oocytes from aging (after 40 years of age). The diagnosis is typically made retrospectively after the woman has missed menses for 12 consecutive months. The average age of menopause in Bangladesh is 46.7 years. Diagnosis of menopause : ● More than 12 months of amenorrhoea with no other cause. ● Elevated follicle stimulating hormone ( ↑ FSH) and low estrogen ( ↓ ) are consistent with menopause. FSH >30 IU/L on 2 occasions, >1 month apart suggests the woman is postmenopausal. Period changes just before menopause : Changes in menstrual pattern are common in the years just before the menopause – 1. Typically cycle shortens after 40 years by up to 7-10 days. Cycle then lengthens periods may occur at 2-3 months intervals until stopping. 2. Dysfunctional uterine bleeding is common leading up to the menopause but investigate if very heavy, painful, irregular, intermenstrual or post-coital bleeding. 3. Late menstruation (>54 years) investigate as increase risk of malignancy. Symptoms of menopause : Immediate symptoms : Flushes and sweats : 80% have hot flushes during the menopause. Low estrogen can result in vasomotor instability (such as hot flushes and night sweats). Psychological symptoms : Psychological changes such as mood swings, insomnia, depression and difficulty in concentrating are common. Genital tract problems : Genital tract atrophy (such as vaginal dryness, painful intercourse and urinary incontinence). Lower androgen levels can cause loss of sex drive. Later medical problems : The lower reproductive hormones associated with menopause will increase the risk of osteoporosis, bone fractures and cardiovascular disease (such as myocardial infarction and stroke). Post Menopausal Complication : ○ Hot flushes and night sweats. ○ Dryness of vagina. ○ Joint pain & osteoporosis. ○ Stress incontinence. ○ Palpitation. ○ Mood changes, e.g., anxiety, depression, insomnia.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Management of menopausal symptoms : Non-pharmacological : 1. Low intensity exercise (e.g. yoga, deep breathing). 2. Avoiding trigger foods/drinks, (e.g. spicy foods, caffeine, alcohol). 3. Diet rich in calcium, if require supplementation of vitamin D and calcium. Pharmacological treatment : 1. HRT consists of estrogen, progesterone and possibly testosterone may help alleviate or reduce the menopausal symptoms. This is particularly important to prevent osteoporosis in premature menopause (menopause before 40 years of age). However, it is recommended to use the lowest possible dose for the shortest duration possible to reduce the risks of breast cancer, blood clots, myocardial infarction and stroke. 2. For vasomotor instability include low dose SSRI antidepressants (such as fluoxetine, paroxetine, citalopram and sertraline), gabapentin and clonidine. 3. For osteoporosis bisphosphonates (such as alendronate, risedronate and ibandronate) or selective estrogen receptor modulators (such as raloxifene). 4. Sexual dysfunction : Vaginal dryness and atrophy are common. Manage with vaginal lubricants or topical oestrogen. Loss of libido (especially after surgical removal of the ovaries) responds to administration of androgen (e.g. testosterone) with HRT, until libido is re-established.

treatment.title: Model Treatment
treatment.description: Model Treatment : ✔ Tab. Tibolone 2.5 mg / Tibilon (bex) / Ubilon (incp) / Menorest (ren) / Tibo (pop) / Renorma (sq), 0+0+1 ------ 6 months ✔ Cap. Evening primrose oil 1000 mg / Eprim Plus (sq) / PrimaVera (ren) / Primacap (drg) 1000 mg, 1+0+1 ----- 3 months ✔ Tab. Calcium+vit-D / Calbo D (sq), 1+0+0 ----- 1 month ✔ Tab. Multivitamin / Zovia silver (ops) 0+1+0 ----- 3 months ✔ Tab. Clonazepam 0.5 / Disopan (incp) 0.5 mg, 0+0+1 --- 15 days Nonspecific / Generalized Weakness / Body ache : ○ Tab. Vitamin B Complex, 0 + 0 + 1 ----- 1 month ○ Tab. Calcium+ Vitamin D, 0 + 1 + 0 ----- 3 months ○ Tab. Iron + Folic acid + Zinc, 0 + 1 + 0 (A/M) ---- 3 months Generalized/Focal Burning Sensation of Body : Vit-B1, B6 & B12 ○ Tab. Vit B1+B6+B12 / Neuro-B (sq) / Neurocare (bex) / TPC (acm) / Neurobest (ren) / B126 (pop) / Povital (aci) / Supra-B (drg), 1 + 0 + 1 (less acidity if taken after meal) ––– 1-3 months HRT : if necessary (see below for details). Advice : 1. Take adequate fruits and vegetables. 2. Drink milk and milk made foods. 3. Walk 30 minutes a day. Follow up : At 2-3 month then 6 monthly. Yearly screening of – mammography, PAP smear, pelvic USG, serum estradiol.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: HORMONE REPLACEMENT THERAPY (HRT) Short term use of HRT is used for relief of symptoms related to oestrogen deficiency peri and post menopausally. Indication of HRT : 1.
