Early menopause 2. Hysterectomy before menopause even if ovaries are conserved. 3. Second-line treatment of osteoporosis. Contraindication of HRT : 1. History of hormone dependent cancer e.g. ovarian cancer, breast cancer. 2. Thromboembolic disease (including AF). 3. Liver disease where LFTs have failed to return to normal (If past history of liver disease, gall stones, or taking liver enzyme inducing drugs, consider transdermal therapy). Drugs use for HRT : 1. For women without a uterus – give oestrogen alone, unless past history of endometriosis. 2. For women with an intact uterus – progestogen is needed for the last 12-14 days of the cycle or IUS to prevent endometrial proliferation. 3. Alternatively, use a continuous oestrogen / progestogen preparation (although not in the peri-menopause or 4. Tibolone – oestrogenic, progestogenic and weak androgenic action. Use in the same way as continuous combined HRT. Treatment with tibolone : • In natural menopause : After 12 months of last natural bleeding • In artificial menopause : Immediately • In case of HRT : ○ Estrogen only : after withdrawal bleeding. ○ Sequential HRT : after progesterone pill. ○ Continuous HRT : anytime. 5. Topical vaginal preparations : Oestrogen pessaries, creams or rings for vaginal dryness / atrophic vaginitis. Limit use for 3-6 months, consider prescribing a progestogen if given for longer periods or higher doses are used. Dose and duration of HRT : Start with a low dose and review the patient after 3 months, continue until age 51 years. Side effects of HRT : ► Oestrogen related – fluid retention, breast enlargement and tenderness, nausea, headache. ► Progestogen related – headache, increase weight, bloating and depression (decrease by changing to a preparation with a less androgenic progestogen e.g. dydrogesterone or medroxy-progesterone).

treatment.title: Model Prescriptions
treatment.description: Points to be noted before starting HRT : 1. Reason to start HRT. Why does the woman want to start HRT? What are her expectations of treatment? Has she had a hysterectomy? If not, ask about bleeding pattern. Investigate abnormal bleeding prior to starting HRT. 2. Any history / risk factor of osteoporosis, DVT, CVD, family history of breast cancer. 3. Contraceptive requirement – HRT does not provide contraception. If 4. Drug history – previous experience of HRT; levothyroxine (may need to increase dose of levothyroxine when start HRT-check TFTs); steroids (HRT decrease effectiveness of steroids); anti-epileptics (increase elimination of oestrogen). a 5. Examination – check BP, weight, breasts (check no lumps; demonstrate breast self-examination techniques); smear is up to date; consider examination for prolapse / vaginal abnormalities if symptoms. Follow up (F/U) of HRT : 1. Initially after 3 months, then review every 6-12 months and at the time of any problem. 2. Check BP, weight, breast, symptoms and bleeding pattern. 3. Reassess risks and benefits. 4. HRT is needed for 5. When stopping, withdrawal flushes may be distressing – stop in cold weather, reduce the dose to half for the first month, then gradually stop. Reasons to stop HRT immediately : 1 . Serious neurological effects e.g. severe headache, first convulsion/fit. 2. Severe chest pain. 3. Sudden breathlessness / cough with blood stained sputum. 4. Unexplained severe pain in calf. 5. Severe upper abdominal pain. 6. Hepatitis, jaundice, liver enlargement. 7. Systolic BP >160 mmHg and/or diastolic >95 mmHg. 8. Detection of a risk factor e.g. DVT, stroke. 9. Prolonged immobility after surgery or leg injury. CONVENTIONAL HORMONE REPLACEMENT THERAPY (HRT) Model Prescriptions : 1. In hysterectomized patient : Single therapy with estrogen. Tab. Estriol 1 mg / Ovestin (nuv) / Femastin (sq), 0 + 0 + 1 ----- continue. Or, Tab. Conjugated estrogen 0.625 mg / Premarin (rad, 60/-) / Estracon (ren, 20/-), 0+0+1 ---- continue. Firstly, prescribe it for 1 st – 25 th day then off for 5 days. Continue this cyclically, if symptoms present, then take continuously. Or, severe vasomotor symptoms associated with menopause and prevention of postmenopausal osteoporosis : Tab. Conjugated Estrogen + Bazedoxifene (0.45 mg+20 mg) / Bestron (incp, 80/-), 0 + 0 + 1 ------ continue. 2. In intact uterus : Combined therapy estrogen + progesterone ○ Tab. Estriol 1 mg / Ovestin (nuv), 0 + 0 + 1 ----- continue ○ Tab. Norethisterone 5 mg / Normens (ren) / Menoral (sq) / Mensil N (hc) / Menogia (aci) / Remens (pop) / Feminor (acm), 0 + 0 + ½ (on 13 th - 25 th day of the cycle) ----- continue 3. If conventional HRT contraindicated : ○ Tab. Tibolone 2.5mg (20/-) / Ubilon (incp) / Menorest (ren) / Renorma (sq) / Tibo (pop) / Tibilon (bex) / Tibonor (skf), 0 + 0 + 1 ----- 3 months ○ Cream Estriol 0.10% / Femastin (sq) / Gynest (acm) / Ovestin (nuv) / SRT (hc) Vaginal Cream, Apply daily for 3 weeks then twice daily for 3-4 weeks

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Dyspareunia means pain during coitus. It may be superficial (felt around the introitus) or deep (felt deep inside). There is a psychological element in most cases (a vicious cycle of pain leading to fear of intercourse which exacerbates symptoms). Address both physical and psychological aspects. Following lubricants may be used before & in-between coital act, when necessary (apply before each coital act) : ► Gel Glycerol 11.25% / K-Y Jelly (J&J) / R-Y Jelly (phar). Or, ► Topical Gel Hydroxyethyl Cellulose + Glycerin [(1 gm+1.3 gm)/50 gm] / Eazy Jelly (ren) / Gleazy (sq) / P-L Jelly (uni). [Treat post-menopausal symptoms (vaginal dryness is common), if any. Please see above.]

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: Emergency contraceptive pill (ECP) Drugs Emergency contraceptive dose & time Ulipristal Acetate 30 mg 30 mg PO within 120 hour (5 days). Levonorgestrel 0.75 mg or 1.5 mg 150 mg (single dose), Or, 0.75 mg stat and after 12 hours. Norgestrel 0.3 mg + Ethinyl Estradiol 0.03 mg Combination oral contraceptives (COC), 2 tab stat and 2 tab after 12 hrs. Copper IUDs (gold standard) Insertion within 5 days Mifepristone 200 mg 100 mg single dose. Failure (pregnancy) rate is less than 1% but not 0%. Single dose solution to prevent unplanned pregnancy : ► Tab. Ulipristal Acetate 30 mg / Peuli (zis) / 5X (ren) / Lily (uni), Only 1 tab is indicated for emergency contraception within 120 hours (5 days) of unprotected sexual intercourse or contraceptive failure. If vomiting occurs within 3 hours of intake, then another tablet needed to be taken. Ulipristal Acetate can be taken at any time of menstrual cycle. Or, ► Tab. Levonorgestrel 1.5 mg / Norix 1 (smc) / Emcon 1 (ren) / I-Pill DS (pop) / Norpill 1 (sq) / Setfree (nuv) / Nirvoy (hc) / EC-Pill (tech), For emergency contraception, preferably within 12 hours and no later than 72 hours (3 days) after the unprotected sexual intercourse; Levonorgestrel can be taken at any moment during the menstrual cycle. [strength 0.75 mg usually not used as ECP, if used then 2 tab is indicated] ¤ After using emergency contraception, it is recommended to use a local contraceptive mean (condom, spermicide, and cervical cap) until the next menstrual period resumes. ¤ The use of Levonorgestrel does not contraindicate the continuation of regular hormonal contraception. If you have used this medicine while you were using oral contraception (contraceptive pill), you should carry on taking the usual tablets until the end of the treatment. ¤ In case no menstrual period occurs in the next pill-free period following the use of Levonorgestrel a pregnancy test should be performed to rule out a pregnancy. Studies shows no adverse effects of progestogen on the malformation of a fetus. So if failed contraception, pregnancy can be continued up to term. Note on the other hand if taken MM Kit Tablet (Mifepristone + Misoprostol), has a risk of foetal malformation, recommended to terminate pregnancy. ► Prostaglandin analogues : Tab. MM Kit (zis) / MR Kit (euro) / AB kit (ren) / A-Kit (acm) / Terminex (sq) contains – 1 tablet of Mifepristone 200 mg + 5 tablet of Misoprostol 200 mcg. This kit is indicated for early Menstrual Regulation (MR) / termination of pregnancy up to 9 weeks ( Day 1 (First visit) : One tablet of Mifepristone (200 mg) intake. Day 2 or 3 (Second visit) : Misoprostol administration: 24-48 hours after ingesting the Mifepristone. Intake of four 200 microgram tablets (800 micrograms) of Misoprostol buccally or sublingually or 2 tab misoprostol (200) µg vaginally. Day 10 to 14 (Third visit) : USG of LA. Patients who have an ongoing pregnancy at this visit have a risk of fetal malformation resulting from the treatment. Surgical termination/MVA is recommended to manage Menstrual Regulation (MR)/termination of pregnancy failures. Refer if any complication or uncontrolled per vaginal bleeding (P/V/B).

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: Contraception options for married couple Educated couple : Oral contraceptive (female) ± Condom (male). Illiterate couple : Implants (Injection or IUCD). Aged couple with two or more children : permanent method (with consent) – bilateral tubal ligation (female) or vasectomy (male). ► Tab. Drospirenone + Ethinylestradiol (0.03 mg + 3 mg) / Valentino (hc) / Novelon (ren) / Droscon (acm) / Rosen (incp), 0 + 0 + 1 ----- continue Or, ► Tab. Drospirenone + Ethinylestradiol (0.02 mg + 3 mg) / Novelon Lite (ren) / Elisa (nuv) / Rosen Gold (incp), 0 + 0 + 1 ----- continue Counselling of married women on OCP : What to do if anyone missed combine oral pill : ► If 1 pill is missed (taken >24 hours late anywhere in the pack). Take the missed pill as soon as possible even if that means taking 2 pills in one day. Continue taking the rest of the pack as usual. No additional contraception is needed. Take the 7 days break as normal. ► If ≥ 2 pills are missed (pills are >48 hours late at anytime, anywhere in the pack). Take the most recent missed pill as soon as possible even if that means taking 2 pills in 1 day. Leave any earlier missed pills. Continue taking the rest of the pack as usual. Use extra contraceptive precautions or abstain from sexual intercourse for next 7 days.

treatment.title: Progesterone Only Pill (POP)
treatment.description: Suitable candidate for POP or Minipill : 1. When estrogen containing pills are contraindicated. 2. Older women. 3. Breast feeding women; 4. Patients with HTN, Valvular heart disease, DM or migraine. 5. Women with past history/predisposition to venous thrombo embolism. Reason not to prescribe POP : 1. Current breast cancer 2. Newly diagnosed Ischemic heart disease, stroke/TIA. 3. Liver disease. 4. Migraine with aura when taking POP. 5. Avoid if SLE with antiphospholipid antibodies. What to do if anybody missed POP? If a pill is missed or delayed >3 hours (in the same day), continue taking the POP at the usual time & use additional barrier methods for next 2 days.

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: Ovum implants outside the uterine cavity commonly (95%) in a fallopian tube. Other sites – ovary, mesentery, abdomen etc. Classic triad : Reproductive aged married/unmarried female with – ● Short period amenorrhea (positive pg strip test) + ● Severe lower abdominal pain (LAP) + ● Light per vaginal bleeding (P/V/B). Diagnosis by Clinical Feature : • Short period of amenorrhea (1 – 3 missed periods). • Severe abdominal pain. • Irregular vaginal bleeding. • Fainting attack. • Shock / BP usually low (shock). • Tender abdomen. • Tender fornixes, bulky uterus. • Tender adnexal mass. • P/V bleeding / spotting. General management : • Rapid evaluation: vital signs. • Resuscitation if shock is suspected / diagnosed. • IV access & IV fluid – normal saline / Ringers’ solution. • blood transfusion (BT) – 2-3 bags. • Analgesic : ➢ Inj. Pethidine / Nalbuphine – (1-2), if necessary. ➢ Inj. Tiemonium – (4-6) amp. Specific Management of Ectopic Pregnancy : Unruptured / Chronic Ruptured (Acute Emergency) or, Unruptured / Chronic : • Patient hemodynamically stable. • No intrauterine gestational sac. • No fluid in pouch (POD). • β -hCG • Adnexal mass • Patient hemodynamically unstable • Minimum fluid collection in POD. • β -hCG > 2000 mIU/ml. • Adnexal mass >3.5 cm. ↓ ↓ • Inj. Methotrexate 50 mg I/M single dose. • Follow up β -HCG level-Every 48-72 hrs until β -HCG –ve. • Fall of 15 % within (3- 7) days If unruptured / chronic : Laparotomy / Laparoscopy then – Salpingectomy / Salpingostomy / salpingotomy. If ruptured : emergency surgery : Laparotomy with Peritoneal toileting & Salpingectomy ↓ If no fall • 2nd dose of inj. Methotrexate [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page – 154] Definitive management : Emergency laparotomy & resuscitation side by side. 1. Extensive damage to tube : Salpingectomy. 2. Little tubal damage : Repair Salpingostomy.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Ruptured ectopic pregnancy Adv. : ▸ USG of lower abdomen. ▸ Blood grouping and Rh typing ▸ Strip test or urinary β -hCG. ▸ Counseling for 4 units of blood ready. ► Bed rest. ► NPO-TFO. ► Inj. Ceftriaxone / Oricef (hc) / Ceftron (sq) / Arixon (bex) 1 gm, 1 vial I/V state & BD. ► Inj. Nalbuphine (20mg/2ml) / Raltrox-2 (ops) / Nalbun-2 (incp) / Newbain (sq), 1 amp I/M stat & sos (or TDS). c̄ - ► Inj. Ondansetron (8mg/4ml) / Zofra (skf), 1 amp IV stat & sos (or TDS). ► Inj. Tiemonium (50mg/1ml) / Visceralgine (nuv) / Viset (hc), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS/QDS. ► Inj. Esomeprazole 40mg / Remmo (bex) / Nexum (sq), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. If patient is in shock, manage the shock first – ► Open IV channel by two wide bore cannula. ► Inf. H/S (1L), I/V running stat. ► Inf. Plasmasol (ori) /Plasmex (bacn) 500ml, I/V stat @ 30 drops/min. ► Continuous catheterization. Please see, post-operative portion (next chapter) for Post-OT & discharge notes.

treatment.title: Per vaginal bleeding in early pregnancy
treatment.description: D/Ds : 1. Abortions. 2. Ectopic pregnancy. 3. Molar pregnancy.

treatment.title: Miscarriage / Spontaneous Abortion
treatment.description: Occurs in 1 in 5 pregnancies, 80% occurs before 2 weeks of gestation. Risk factors of miscarriage/spontaneous abortion are : 1. Maternal age ≥ 35 years or paternal age > 40 years. 2. BMI > 29 kg/m 2 . 3. Smoking & alcohol. Causes of miscarriage / spontaneous abortion are 1. Fetal abnormality. 2. Multiple pregnancy. 3. Uterine abnormality – fibroids, polyps, congenital abnormality, cervical incompetence. 4. Systemic disease- renal, autoimmune or connective tissue disease – particularly SLE, PCOS, DM, systemic infection. 5. Drugs – cytotoxic, diethylstilbestrol. 6. Placental vascular abnormalities. Classification of miscarriage / spontaneous abortion : 1. Threatened abortion. 2. Inevitable abortion. 3. Complete abortion. 4. Incomplete abortion. 5. Missed Abortion. 6. Septic Abortion. Recurrent miscarriage When a patient has ≥ 3 miscarriages. Those patient must refer for further investigation. Consider checking antiphospholipid antibodies.

treatment.title: Therapeutic Termination of Pregnancy
treatment.description: Definition : Deliberate induction of abortion to terminate the pregnancy. Indication : • Continuation of pregnancy may cause life risk to pregnant woman-such as – ✓ grade III/IV cardiac disease, ✓ advanced renal disease, ✓ CVD/stoke. • advanced malignancy chromosomal / major congenital anomaly of fetus. • Possibilities of fetal anomalies- ingestion of teratogenic drug, grossly uncontrolled DM, badly controlled epilepsy etc. • Pregnancy caused by rape or failure of contraception. Confirmation of gestational age clinically by history & examination. Investigation : • CBC • RBS • S. Creatinine • Blood grouping & Rh typing • TSH • Coagulation profile • Liver function test • USG of pregnancy profile Refer for further management.

treatment.title: Features & Management
treatment.description: A threatened abortion is when vaginal bleeding occurs, and the diagnostic for a spontaneous abortion has not been met. C/C : 1. Pregnancy / Amenorrhea for less than 28 weeks 2. Light per vaginal bleeding (P/V/B) (common in 1 st trimester, known as spotting). 3. Dull pain in lower abdomen. 4. No expulsion of products of conception. Diagnostic tools of threatened abortion Diagnosis can be confirmed by hCG and an USG. The ultrasound can also help rule out an ectopic pregnancy and to evaluate for retained products of conception. A yolk sac is typically seen at 36 days and a heartbeat is seen on ultrasound at approximately 45 days after the last menstruation. Management of threatened abortion 1. Bed rest is the primary requirement to recover from a threatened abortion. Any kind of physical activities, including sexual intercourse, should be strongly avoided. 2. Repeat pelvic ultrasound weekly until a viable pregnancy is confirmed or excluded. 3. If the tests revealed reduce levels of hormones, treatment of threatened abortion with progesterone injections might also be undertaken to bring them to the required amount. ¤ Injection Hydroxyprogesterone Caproate 250mg/1ml or 500mg/2ml / HPC (pop) / Hydrogest DS (ren) / Caprogen DS (nuv), a dose of 500 mg or 250 mg I/M once weekly. 4. To stop the bleeding or any uterine contractions, haemostatic drugs or even tocolytic agent based medication might be given. 5. Treat associated DM, hypothyroidism and Rh incompatibility between mother and fetus.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ Threatened abortion Adv. : 1. USG of PP or pelvic organ. 2. Hb%. 3. Blood grouping and Rh typing. 4. RBS or FBS, 2HAFB. 5. S. TSH. ☼ Bed rest. ☼ Diet – Normal. ☼ Tab. Folic acid 5mg, 1+0+1 upto 12 weeks. ☼ Inj. Hydroxyprogesterone Caproate (HPC) (250mg/ml) or (500mg/2ml), 1 amp deep I/M (preferably on buttock) stat & 3 to 7 days interval upto 12 weeks to 17 weeks (maturation of placenta). ☼ Cap. Progesterone / Microgest (ren) / Mygest (drg) 200 mg, 1+0+1 upto 12 to 17 wks. ☼ Tab. Allystrenol 5mg / Gestrenol (ren) / Alia (acm) / Geston (sq) / Allygest (nuv) / Allynol (pop), 1+0+1 upto 12 to 17 weeks.

treatment.title: Missed Abortion
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Amenorrhea for ____. 2. Mild / Irregular PV bleeding / brownish discharge. 3. No pain, 4. No foetal heart beat & movement. Missed (or delayed) miscarriage – no PV bleeding. Usually discovered when no heart beat is seen on routine antenatal scan. Treatment is D&C. A ‘watch and wait’ approach is possible, but at 4 weeks only 66% are complete and associated with longer bleeding. Medical management with prostaglandin analogues + antiprogesterone priming is an alternative to D&C.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: ● Incomplete abortion / miscarriage- history of PV bleeding. Products of conception remain in the uterus but there is no fetal heart beat. Usually admit for evacuation of retained products of conception (D&C). Sometimes ‘watch and wait’ approach for spontaneous expulsion/abortion. C/C : 1. Pregnancy with heavy per vaginal bleeding / irregular bleeding (any time before 28 weeks of pg). 2. Cramping lower abdominal pain. 3. Partial expulsion fleshy mass per vagina. Δ Missed Abortion or, Incomplete abortion Adv. : ▸ USG of lower abdomen / pelvic organ. ▸ Blood grouping and Rh typing. ▸ FBS, 2HAFB. ▸ S. TSH. ▸ CBC, Hb% c̄ Platelet count. ▸ BT, CT. ▸ Counseling for 1 unit of blood ready. ☼ Diet : Normal. ☼ Tab. Ciprofloxacin / Cipro-A (acm) / Neofloxin (bex) / Ciprox (ops) / DFX (del) / Ciprozid-DS (drg) 500mg, 1+0+1. ☼ Tab. Metronidazole / Amodis (sq) / Filmet (bex) / Flagyl (syn) / 400mg, 1+1+1. ☼ Cap. Omeprazole 20mg / Cosec (drg) / Seclo (sq) / Losectil (skf), 1+0+1 (½ hr B/M). ☼ Cap. Tranexamic acid 500mg / Intrax (incp) / Tracid (acm) / Traxyl (nuv), 1+1+1. If patient is in shock, manage the shock first – ☼ Open IV channel by two wide bore cannula. ☼ Inf. H/S (1L), I/V running stat. ☼ Inf. Plasmasol (ori) /Plasmex (bacn) 500ml, I/V stat @ 30 drops/min. ☼ Continuous catheterization. Specific : ☼ Tab. Misoprostol / Cytomis (incp) / Isovent (sq) / Misotab (euro) 200 mcg, (dose depends on gestational age) Or, ☼ Tab. Mifepristone 200 mg + Misoprostol 200 mcg / MM Kit (zis) / AKit (acm) / MR Kit (euro) / Cytomis Kit (incp) [use within 1" trimester], 1 tab. Mifepristone (the bigger tablet) stat sub-lingually (S/L), after 24-48 hours 4 tab. Misoprostol (the smaller four tablets) S/L. Or, ☼ If, large POC (products of conception) or previous CS then do ICC.

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: Discharge Notes (Missed or, Incomplete abortion) : -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- ☼ Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500mg, 1+0+1 – 7 days. ☼ Tab. Metronidazole 400mg, 1+1+1 – total 5 days. ☼ Tab. Tiemonium 50mg, 1+1+1 – total 5 days. ☼ Tab. Misoprostol 200 microgram, 1 +1 +1 -3 days. ☼ Cap. Omeprazole 20mg, 1+0+1 (30 min B/M) – 15 days. ☼ Tab. Iron + Folic acid + Zinc, 1+0+0 – 3 months. উপদেশ : ১। নিয়মিত ওষুধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে কোন সমস্যা হলে গাইনি বহির্বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। ২। ১৪ দিন পর USG of lower abdomen (with special attention to uterus) রিপোর্টসহ গাইনি বহির্বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। ৩। ১৪ দিন স্বামী সহবাস নিষেধ ও ভারী কাজ করবেন না। তারপর থেকে জন্মনিয়ন্ত্রণ পদ্ধতি ব্যাবহার করবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন। ● Complete abortion / miscarriage – H/O PV bleeding, there is no products of conception in the uterus. USG of L/A – no POC. Discharge Notes (Complete abortion) : -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- ☼ Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500mg, 1+0+1 – 7 days. ☼ Tab. Metronidazole 400mg, 1+1+1 – total 5 days. ± ☼ Tab. Tiemonium 50mg, 1+1+1 – total 5 days. ☼ Cap. Omeprazole 20mg, 1+0+1 (30 min B/M) – 15 days. ☼ Tab. Iron + Folic acid + Zinc, 1+0+0 – 3 months. Adv. : USG of lower abdomen (c̄ special attention to uterus) : NAD. উপদেশ : ১। নিয়মিত ওষুধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে কোন সমস্যা হলে গাইনি বহির্বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। ২। ১৪ দিন স্বামী সহবাস নিষেধ ও ভারী কাজ করবেন না। তারপর থেকে জন্মনিয়ন্ত্রণ পদ্ধতি ব্যাবহার করবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন।

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Amenorrhea for ____. 2. Reduced level of consciousness. 3. H/O induced abortion / MR ____ days back. 4. Per vaginal bleeding or foul-smelling discharge. 5. Abdominal pain. 6. Fever. Specific management : 1. Uterine curettage : If patient's condition is stable after giving antibiotic. 2. Laparotomy : If any suspected injury to gut and uterus. Δ Septic abortion Adv. : ▸ CBC ▸ CRP ▸ S. electrolytes ▸ USG of W/A ▸ HVS for C/S ▸ Blood grouping and Rh typing ▸ X-ray abdomen erect posture. ▸ Urine R/M/E ▸ Counseling for 1 units of blood ready. ☼ Bed rest. ☼ NPO-TFO. ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Oricef (hc) / Rofecin (rad) / Ceftron (sq) 1gm, 1 vial I/V state & BD. ☼ Inj. Metronidazole 500 mg/100 ml / Metro (osl) / Flamyd (incp) / Filmet (bex), 1 bottle I/V state & TDS. ☼ Inj. Nalbuphine (20mg/2ml) / Raltrox-2 (ops) / Nalbun-2 (incp) / Newbain (sq), 1 amp I/M stat & sos (or TDS). c̄ - ☼ Inj. Ondansetron (8mg/4ml) / Emistat (hc), 1 amp IV stat & sos (or TDS). ☼ Inj. Tiemonium (50mg/1ml) / Norvis (sq) / Algin (ren), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS/QDS. ☼ Inj. Esomeprazole 40mg / Remmo (bex) / Nexum (sq), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. If patient is in shock, manage the shock first – ☼ Open IV channel by two wide bore cannula. ☼ Inf. H/S (1L), I/V @running stat. ☼ Inf. Plasmasol (ori) /Plasmex (bacn) 500 ml, I/V stat @ 30 drops/min. ☼ Continuous catheterization. Monitor : 1. Pulse 2. BP 3. Temperature 4. Respiratory rate 5. Urine output 6. Serum electrolytes

Cefixime 200mg, 1+0+1 – 7 days. ☼ Tab. Metronidazole 400mg, 1+1+1 – total 5 days. ☼ Tab. Tiemonium 50mg, 1+1+1 – total 5 days. ☼ Tab. Misoprostol 200 microgram, 1 +1 +1 -3 days. ☼ Cap. Omeprazole 20mg, 1+0+1 (30 min B/M) – 15 days. ☼ Tab. Iron + Folic acid + Zinc, 1+0+0 – 3 months. উপদেশ : ১। নিয়মিত ওষুধ খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে কোন সমস্যা হলে গাইনি বহির্বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। ২। ১৪ দিন স্বামী সহবাস নিষেধ ও ভারী কাজ করবেন না। তারপর থেকে জন্মনিয়ন্ত্রণ পদ্ধতি ব্যাবহার করবেন। ৩। ১৪ দিন পর USG of lower abdomen (with special attention to uterus) রিপোর্টসহ গাইনি বহির্বিভাগে যোগাযোগ করবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন।

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnosis by Clinical Feature (C/F) : • Vaginal bleeding during the first / second trimester. • Passage of fleshy mass / grape like structure P/V. • Pain in lower abdomen / cramping abdominal pain. • Signs of shock may present. • Severe nausea / vomiting – Hyperemesis gravidarum. • Uterus larger & softer than gestational period (50%). • Fetal part is not palpable • Early onset pre-eclampsia may occurs. O / E : - Pulse. - Blood pressure (BP). - Anemia (-) / (+). - Oedema (-) / (+). - TT immunization (-) / (+). Adv. : - USG : Snow-storm appearance. - S. hCG – Elevated. Investigation : • Complete Blood count, RBS. • X-ray Chest P/A view – to exclude lung involvement. • USG of pregnancy profile (PP): - Snow storm appearance. - No foetus; (in partial mole-fetus with mole). • Blood grouping & Rh typing. • Serum β -hCG - ( β -hCG) / Urinary pregnancy test +ve in high dilution (may need to be repeated at several times). • Histopathology of evacuated product (after suction evacuation). Δ Molar pregnancy / Hydaridiform mole Adv. : ▸ USG of lower abdomen. ▸ Blood grouping and Rh typing ▸ β -hCG. ▸ CXR P/A view. ▸ Counseling for 2 units of blood ready. ☼ NPO-TFO. ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Exephin (incp) / Traxon (ops) / Traxef (bacn) 1 gm, 1 vial I/V state & BD. Or, ☼ Inj. Ciprofloxacin 200mg/100ml / Neofloxin (bex), 1 bottle I/V stat and BD. Plus, ☼ Inj. Metronidazole 500mg/100ml / Metryl (osl), 1 bottle I/V stat & TDS. ☼ Inj. Ondansetron (8mg/4ml) / Ofran (sq) / Ondason (drg), 1 amp IV stat & sos (or TDS). ☼ Inj. Tiemonium (50mg/1ml) / Xelcom (rad) / Viset (hc), 1 amp I/M stat & TDS/QDS. ☼ Inj. Esomeprazole 40mg / Esonix (incp) / Othera (aci), 1 vial I/V stat & BD. ± ☼ Inj. Tranexamic acid 500mg/ Traxyl (nuv), 1 amp I/V stat & TDS. ± ☼ Inj. Nalbuphine (20mg/2ml) / Nalbun-2 (incp), 1 amp I/M stat & sos c̄ Inj. Ondansetron. If patient is in shock, manage the shock first – ☼ Open IV channel by two wide bore cannula. ☼ Inf. H/S (1L), I/V running stat. ☼ Inf. Plasmasol (ori) / Plasmex (bacn) 500ml, I/V stat @ 30 drops/min. ☼ Continuous catheterization.

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: General Management : • Rapid evaluation of vital signs. • Resuscitation if shock is suspected / diagnosed. • IV access & IV fluid : Normal saline (N/S) / Ringers’ solution (H/S) – (1-2 litre). • Blood transfusion (BT) – if necessary. • Analgesic : Inj. Pethidine, 1 amp, if necessary. • Counselling : Pre-procedure/during procedure / post procedure and assurance. • Oxytocin infusion 20 IU in 1liter normal saline for at least 6-8 hr. • Antibiotic – Inj. Ciprofloxacin & Inj. Metronidazole for 24 hrs then oral therapy for 7 days. Specific Management of Molar Pregnancy : • Start MVA/suction evacuation (SE) under spinal or general anesthesia. • When uterus becomes smaller - start Oxytocin infusion. • 20 IU in 1litre normal saline @60 drops/min. • Keep blood ready & transfuse if necessary. • Send evacuated product for histopathology. • Strict follow up spatially with pulse oximeter. • Evacuated. ↓ • Ensure immediate follow up (F/U) – Oxygen saturation to early diagnosis of embolism. • Contraception – Barrier methods until β -hCG (-) ve, then hormonal contraceptive for at least 1 year. ↓ Follow up (F/U) – • History – Amenorrhea / irregular bleeding, cough (warning signs). • Examination – size of uterus, adnexa (to exclude luteal cyst), metastasis in vulva, vagina cervix, urethra etc. • Serum beta HCG – weekly until 3 consecutive negative result then monthly for 1 year. • X-ray chest if necessary. ↓ • If β HCG does not fall to normal / remains plateau / rises within or after 6 wks of evacuation (S&E) refer the patient to tertiary center for suspected choriocarcinoma. ► Definitive management : MVA / suction & evacuation (SE). Send product of concept (POC) – for histopathology. ► Post-operative order is same as D&C. ► Advise regular follow up & chemotherapy.

treatment.title: Discharge notes
treatment.description: Discharge c̄ Notes (Following S&E or MVA) : ☼ Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500mg, 1+0+1 – total 7 days. ☼ Tab. Metronidazole 400mg1+1+1 – total 5 days. ☼ Tab. Misoprostol 200 microgram, 1+1+1 – 3 days. ☼ Cap. Omeprazole 20mg, 1+0+1 (30 mins before meal) – 15 days. ☼ Tab. Calcium Orotate 740 mg, 0+1+0 – 2 months. ☼ Cap. Iron + Folic acid + Zinc, 1+0+1 – 3 months.

treatment.title: Urinary Catheter Durations
treatment.disease_name: Drugs needed in Obs - Gyne surgery
treatment.description: ► Incase of prolong labour urinary catheter must be kept in situ for 5-7 days prevention of VVF. • In obstructed labour, during caesarean section surgery, prior to abdominal hysterectomy (for 1 day), at the end of vaginal hysterectomy (for 5 days), post-partum haemorrhage, during abortion often. • To check cervical incompetence 8F Foley’s catheter is used. • After vesicovaginal fistula repair it is kept for 7-10 days. • After Wertheim’s operation it is kept for 14 days. • After bladder injury repair it is kept for 10-14 days. ► Use Tab. Misoprostol / cytomis 200 mcg (4 tab P/R stat) under supervision of senior just after delivery.

treatment.title: OT & Confinement Note
treatment.disease_name: Drugs needed in Obs - Gyne surgery
treatment.description: OT (Surgery) Note Date & Time (Start to end) Name of operation (N/O) : LUCS / LUCS ē BLTL. (Lower uterine cesarean section with bilateral tubal ligation). Name of Indication (N/I) : __ th Gravida c̄ __ weeks Pg / FTP c̄ LAP / LM / Previous C/S ± Breech Presentation ± (Mild/Moderate/Severe) oligohydramnios. Name of Surgeon (N/S): Dr. ABC, Associate Professor, (MBBS, MS) Name of Assistant Surgeon (N/Asst) : - Dr. DEF (AR). - Dr. GHI (Intern). Name of Anesthesia / Block (N/B) : SAB (Sub Arachnoid Block). Name of Anesthesiologist (N/A) : Dr. JKL, AP (MBBS, DA). Name of Incision (N/I) : Pfannenstiel incision. Procedure & Findings : c̄ all aseptic precaution after proper painting & draping the abdomen was opened by pfannestial incision. A transverse incision was given on the lower uterine segment. A male / female healthy baby was delivered. Placenta & the membrane were removed by CCT. After maintaining proper haemostasis, counting instrument & mop, abdomen was closed layer by layer. There was less bleeding & no per-operative complication. Skin closed by vicryl / poly sorb intradermally / interrupted Mersilk suturing. OT note in short / traditional : OT (Surgery) Note (Date & Time) N/O – LUCS. N/I – ___ th Gravida c̄ xx weeks Pg / FTP c̄ LAP / LM / Previous C/S / Breech Presentation / (Mild/Moderate/Severe) oligohydramnios. N/S – Dr. ABC. N/Asst – Dr. DEF. N/B – SAB. N/A – Dr. GHI. TAH c̄ BLSO Procedure & Findings : c̄ all aseptic precaution after proper painting & draping the abdomen was opened by pfannestial incision. The uterus was 8 weeks in size, both ovaries were healthy. After clamping TAH c̄ BLSO was done. Bleeding was average. After maintaining proper haemostasis, counting instrument & mop, abdomen was closed layer by layer. There was no per-operative complication. Skin closed by vicryl / poly sorb intra-dermally / interrupted Mersilk suturing. Confinement Note (NVD) Date & Time : Clinical diagnosis : Procedure : With all aseptic precaution after proper draping (± ē the aid of episiotomy) a healthy (Male / Female) baby was delivered. Placenta ē its membrane was delivered by CCT (Control Cord Traction). Proper examination of placenta was done & there was no missing cotyledon. Proper exploration of genital tract there was no injury & no cervical. Vaginal or perineal tear or episiotomy wound was repaired by Catgut 1-0 R/B. There was no complication & bleeding was average. Conducted by – Dr. ABC

treatment.title: Special Notes (USG of W/A)
treatment.description: USG of Lower Abdomen : Urinary Bladder, Uterus & Adnexa (Female). Kidney, Ureter, Urinary bladder & Prostate (male). USG of Hepato-biliary System : Liver, Gall bladder, Billary tree & Pancreas. H/O Surgery : Gall Bladder (GB) : Not Found (H/O Cholecystectomy/Operation). Uterus : Not Found (H/O Hystrectomy / Operation). Special Notes : Impression : Fatty changes in the Liver. Liver : ● Normal in size, shape & echotexture. ● Parenchymal echogenicity is mildly increased. ● No focal or diffuse lesion seen. Impression : Suggestive of hepatitis . Liver : ● Mildly enlarged in size . ● Peri-portal echogenicity increased. Gall bladder : ● Normal in size & shape. ● Wall is thick (Reactionary). ● No stone or soft tissue mass is noted. Impression : Suggestive of Acute Hepatitis. Liver : ● Normal in size, shape. ● Echogenicity is mildly decreased. ● No focal or diffuse lesion seen. Gall bladder : ● Normal in size. ● Wall is little thick . ● No stone or soft tissue mass is noted. Impression : Cholelithiasis. Gall bladder : Normal in size & shape. Wall thickness is normal. Multiple bright echogenic structures casting posterior acoustic shadows are noted in the lumen of GB. Impression : 1. Suggestive of cholangiocarcinoma with billiary obstruction. 2. Thick walled contracted GB. Biliary tree : Both intrahepatic & extra hepatic biliary tree is dilated. CBD (14.4 cm). A soft tissue mass of size about 3.2 × 3.1 cm is noted at the terminal part of CBD. Impression : Right ureteric stone with mild hydronephrosis with hydroureter (Rt). Kidney : Both are normal in size, shape & position with well defined cortex and medulla. Mild hydronephrosis is seen in the right kidney. No hydronephrosis is seen in left kidney. No stone is seen in any kidney. Ureters : Upper part of the right ureter is dilated . A bright echogenic structure of length about 1.2 cm casting posterior acoustic shadow is seen at the lower end of the right ureter. Adv- X-ray KUB & IVU for further evaluation. Impression : Suggestive of renal parenchymal disease / Smaller right kidney. Kidney : The left kidney is normal in size (11.5×4.4 cm), shape & position with well defined cortex and medulla. The right kidney is smaller in size, measures about 7.2 × 3.0 cm. Right renal echogenicity is mild increased. No stone or hydronephrosis seen in any kidney. Impression : Left renal cortical cyst. Kidney : Both the kidneys appear normal in size, shape & position. Bi-polar diameter of RK is 9.8cm & LK is 10.3 cm. Cortico-medullary differentiation is well maintained. Pelvicaliceal systems are not dilated. No stone or hydronephrosis seen in any kidney. A cortical cyst (3.5cm×3.3cm) is noted in the mid-polar region of LK. Impression : Mild enlarged prostate. Prostate : Mild enlarged in size, measures about 3.7×4.0×4.5cm. Uniform in echotexture. Capsule is intact. Impression : Enlarged prostate with significant PVR. Prostate : Enlarged in size, measures about 5.5 cm × 5.9cm. Uniform & normal in echotexture. Median lobe is protruded into UB wall. Capsule is intact. PVR (Post Voidal Residue) : 75 ml. Impression : Chronic cystitis. Urinary Bladder (UB) : UB is well filled. Wall is thick & irregular. No intra-vesicle lesion is seen. Impression : Simple adnexal cyst (Rt). Adnexa : A round shaped homogeneous hypoechoic cyst of diameter about 3.1cm is seen in the right adnexa. No significant abnormality is noted in the left side. Impression : Fibroid uterus. Uterus : Normal in size & shape. Anteverted in position. A round shaped homogeneous hypoechoic lesion of diameter about 2.5 cm is seen in the myometrium. Endometrial echo is normal. Cervix appears normal. Impression : Suggestive of PID. Cul-de-sac : Mild fluid collection is seen.

Name Signature & Date of doctor, MBBS, DMRD.

treatment.title: Special Notes (USG of PP)
treatment.description: Impression : Early G. sac of 5 weeks + duration. Adv- Follow up USG after 3 weeks (to rule out Blight Ovum). Uterus : Normal in size & shape. Anteverted in position. Myometrial echotexture is homogenous. Cavity of the uterus contains a sac of diameter is about 1.7 cm, corresponding with 5 weeks & 3 days of pregnancy. No definite fetal pole like structure is detected. Cervix appears normal. Impression : Blighted Ovum. Uterine gestational sac without embryo is known as blighted ovum. USG uterine findings are same as early pregnancy, with following criteria – - G. Sac > 2.5cm / 25 mm without fetal pole. - Amenorrhoea > 2 months. - Lower abdominal pain (LAP). - P/V/B (Per vaginal bleeding). Impression : Early pregnancy of 8 weeks+ duration. Uterus : Uterus is gravid with single live fetus with regular cardiac activity. CRL is about 2.9 cm, corresponding with 9 weeks & 6 days of pregnancy. EDD= 26.11.2018. Cervix appears normal. Impression : Consistent with retained Gestational (G.) products / organized blood. Uterus : Normal in size & shape. Anteverted in position. Myometrial echotexture is homogenous. Cavity of the uterus contains a sac of diameter is about 2.7 cm corresponding with 8 weeks & 0 day of pregnancy. No definite fetal pole like structure is detected. Some non-specific structures are seen within the uterine cavity. Cervix appears normal. Or, Cavity of the uterus contains a few non-specific structures. Impression : IUD (Intra Uterine Dead Fetus). Fetal cardiac activity & Fetal movement : Absent. USG for pregnancy : 5+ wks → Gestational sac appeared. 5 wks to 8 wks → Heart Beat. 6+ wks → Fetal pole appeared. 7+ wks to 12 wks → CRL (Crown Rump Length). 13+ wks & onwards → BPD (Bi-parietal diameter), FL (Femoral length).

treatment.title: Fluid Management 1
treatment.description: Age Suitable Fluid with composition Available in market 1 st Day – 2 nd Day 10 % DA Inf. DA/Dextrose 10% Inf. Dexaqua 10% Inf. Libott 10% 2 nd Day – 2 nd Month 10% DA + 0.225% NaCl ( ¼ strength fluid) Inf. APN Inf. Pedisol DS Imf. ADS-10 2 nd Month – 2 nd Year 5 % DA + 0.225% NaCl ( ¼ strength fluid) Inf. Babysol Inf. Baby Saline Inf. Pedisol Inf. Neosol Inf. Libott S 2 nd Year – 10 th Year 5 % DA + 0.45% NaCl ( ½ strength fluid) Inf. Babysol Junior Inf. Neosol DS Inf. Libott S Junior ≥ 10 th Year 5% DNS / 5% DA (adult fluids) Inf. 5% DA / DA (Acme) / Dexaqua. Or, Inf. 5% DNS / DNS (Acme) / Dexoride / Solodex. Fluid requirements : according to age : Amount of fluids in pediatrics : Age Amount of fluid (ml/Kg/day) wt > 1.5 Kg wt 1-1.5 Kg wt Day – 1 60 80 100 Day – 2 80 95 115 Day – 3 100 110 130 Day – 4 120 125 145 Day – 5 140 140 160 Day – 6 150 155 175 Day – 7 up to 2 years 150 170 190 2 – 4 years 100 × × 4 – 8 years 80 × × 8 – 12 years 60 × × Fluid requirements : according to weight : (after 1 month of age) : 100 ml/kg for first 10 kg + 50 ml/kg for next 10 kg + 25 ml/kg for rest of the body weight Cut down 20 – 30 % of total fluid in – Add more 20 – 30 % of total fluid in – 1. Birth Asphyxia / PNA. 1. In phototherapy. 2. Septicaemia (Sepsis). 2. Radiant Warmer. 3. Meningitis / Encephalitis. 3. Preterm LBW. 4. Pneumonia. 4. Shock, if capillary refill time (CRT) > 3 sec or more. 5. Heart failure. 6. Kwashiorkor. * ANY PEDIATRIC DOSE SHOULD NEVER EXCCED ADULT DOSE. * This fluids according to age may not be followed by experienced pediatricians. But intern & trainee should follow it. * This amount of fluids is not calculated with the exact numbers. * 2 / 3 / 4 times/day better than 12 / 8 / 6 hourly respectively, not necessary to count hour, interfere sleep, as ensue same results. * Must know , Flow rate (d/m) = ml 4 × hr , Easy equation ( © Intern’s & GP Diary), Flow rate ≈ [ Fluid amount ( ml ) per day / 100] drops/min Drop/min calculation : The amount of fluid (in ml) to be administered in 24 hours, divide it by 100. e.g., If 1200 ml fluid to be infused in 24 hour, no. of drops/min (1200 ÷ 100) = 12 drop/min or 48 μ D/min [1 drop = 4 micro drops].

treatment.title: Calculation
treatment.description: Choose types, Calculate amount & drops / min of fluids : Example : A baby of 4 hours of life with weight 2.2 kg. Select & calculate the appropriate fluid ? Fluid choice : 4 th hours of life = 1 st day = Inf. 10 % DA ↓ Amount/day : Weight 2.2 kg, then total fluid = 60 ml × 2.2 = 132 ml / day. So, here we want to infuse 130 ml of 10% DA within 24 hours. ↓ Flow rate (drops/min) = 132/ 100 = 1.32 d/m = 1.3 × 4 μ d/m [1 d = 4 μ d] = 5.28 μ d/m ≈ 6 μ d/m. ↓ Order on treatment sheet , ☼ Inf. 10% DA 130 ml, I/V stat @ 6 μ d/m. Usually microburette & 24 G (yellow) IV cannula are used when weight is less than 10 Kg. Example : A boy of 3 years with weight 14.6 kg come to you with acute abdomen. You want to keep him nothing per oral (NPO). Now select & calculate the appropriate fluid ? Fluid choice : A boy of 3 years = Inf. Babysol Junior. ↓ Amount/day : Weight 14.6 kg, then total fluid = 10 Kg + 4.6 Kg = (10 × 100 ml) + (4.6 × 50 ml) = 1000 + 230 ml = 1230 ml. So, here we want to infuse 1230 ml of Babysol Junior within 24 hrs. ↓ Flow rate (drops/min) = 1230/ 100 = 12.3 d/m ≈ 13 d/m. ↓ Order on treatment sheet, ☼ Inf. Babysol Junior 1230 ml, I/V stat @ 13 d/m. Example : In an adult we want to give 3L daily, then, Flow rate (drops/min) = 3000/ 100 = 30 d/m . Here, 30 d/m means the 3L or 3000 ml or three saline bag of 1L will take approx. 24 hours or 1 day. Example : In an adult we want to give 1L daily, then, Flow rate (drops/min) = 1000/ 100 = 10 d/m . Here, 10d/m means the 1 L or 1000 ml saline bag will take approx. 24 hours or 1 day.

treatment.title: Stages of life
treatment.description: Premature Preterm Term Full term : 37 to 42 completed weeks of gestation. Post term > 42 completed weeks’ gestation. Neonate Newborn baby up to 28 days (1 month) of age. Infant Up to 1 year of age. Child All ages up to 16 years, but often divided into – Preschool child Child > 5 years to puberty. Adolescent Puberty up to 16 years. In our hospitals pediatric wards receive patient up to age 12. After age of 13 all patients are sent to medicine or allied wards. [Everywhere, wt = Weight of the baby in Kg.]

treatment.title: Criteria of healthy new born
treatment.description: 1. Gestational age: 37 to 42 completed weeks. 2. Birth weight: 2.5 to 4 Kg. 3. Length : 50 cm approximately. 4. Occipito-frontal circumference (OFC) : 35 cm (30 – 40 cm) approximately. 5. Colour : Pink, but slight peripheral cyanosis soon after birth may be present and is considered normal. 6. Breathing: Spontaneous, regular and respiratory rate between 30-40 breaths/min. 7. Heart rate (HR) : 100-160 beats per minute. 8. Axillary temperature: 97.5 ° to 99 ° F (36.5 ° - 37.5 ° C). 9. Normal muscle tone and the baby will be in a flexed position. 10. Good Primitive reflexes (see at least Moro reflex). 10. Sucking Reflex : Able to suck breast milk soon after birth. 11. Urination : Pass urine within 24 hours of birth but not more than 48 hours of birth. 12. Defecation : Pass meconium within hours of birth. 13. Sleeping Time : Sleeps around 18 hours a day. 14. Absence of Congenital Anomalies : Observe oral cavity & look for openings of anal canal & urethra. [Ref – (Simplified) Essence of Pediatrics, Prof MR Khan and Prof Rahman, Chapter 2 (Neonatology)]

treatment.title: APGAR Score
treatment.description: The “ APGAR Score” is a quick way for health professionals to evaluate the health of all newborns at 1 and 5 minutes after birth and in response to resuscitation. Acronym Point 2 Point 1 Point 0 A Appearance Normal over entire body Normal except extremities Cyanotic or pale all over P Pulse > 100 bpm Absent G Grimace Sneeze/cough/cry Grimaces No response A Activity Active Arms and legs flexed Absent R Respiration Good, crying Gasping, irregular Absent Comment : • Apgar score 7 – 10 : No further resuscitation. • Apgar score 5 – 6 : Bag and mask ventilation. • Apgar score 0 – 4 : Intubation.

treatment.title: NICU criteria
treatment.description: UNWELL CHILD : 1. H/O PNA, birth injury, LBW. 2. Convulsion. 3. Respiratory difficulty. 4. Cyanosis. 5. Poor primitive reflexes e.g., Moro reflex. 6. Unable to suck. 7. Severe abdominal distension. ADMISSION CRITERIA IN NICU : 1. Weight 2. Gestational age 3. Major congenital malformation. 4. Per-natal asphyxia (PNA). 5. Breathing difficulty. 6. Suspected early onset neonatal sepsis. 7. Neonatal jaundice requiring phototherapy or exchange transfusion. 8. IDM baby requiring IV glucose infusion. 9. Post operative newborn requiring intensive care. 10. Not able to take feed from breast/cup and spoon. 11. Any sick neonate irrespective of birth weight/gestation.

treatment.title: History Taking in Neonates
treatment.description: *1. Appearance / Colour : Pink (but mild peripheral cyanosis soon after birth is normal.) *2. Heart rate (HR) / Pulse : Normally 100 – 160 beats / min. 3. Grimace (response to catheter put into nostril). 4. Activity and tone. *5. Respiratory rate (RR) : Normally 30 – 60 breaths / min. 6. APGAR score. *7. Lung : Clear / Creps. *8. Primitive Reflexes : Good / Average / Poor. (Clinically Moro & sucking reflexes are more important) *9. Tissue perfusion : Capillary refill time (CRT), Note weather more than (>) 3 sec or less than ( *10. Umbilical condition – Normal / Meconium stained / Herniated / infected. 11. Temperature. 12. Congenital anomaly. B/H (Birth History) – NVD / Instrumental NVD / LUCS. Birth History : ¤ Age of mother : ¤ Received ANC : ¤ Number of Issue : ¤ Number of pregnancy : ¤ H/O TT or Td vaccination : ¤ Mother's history : DM, HTN, hypothyroidism, pre-eclampsia, eclampsia ¤ Mother's blood group & Rh incompatibility. Delivery history : ¤ Date of delivery (DOD): ¤ Time of delivery (TOD): ¤ Mode of delivery (MOD): ¤ Place of delivery (POD): IB or OB. ○ OB = Outborn (born outside tertiary centers) & ○ IB = Inborn (born within hospital facility). ¤ Gestational age (GA) : Preterm/Term/Post term. ¤ H/O maternal fever or any infection sign. ¤ Prolonged/ Obstructed labour (PL/OL ≥ 12hrs) ¤ PROM : Yes/No General Examination : ¤ APGAR score at 1 st & 10 th minutes. ¤ Delayed crying : Yes/No ¤ Convulsion : Yes/No ¤ Appearance: Normal / Ill looking / excessive crying / gasping / coma. ¤ Color : Pink / Central pink / Peripheral cyanosis / Cyanosed / Pale. ¤ Heart Rate (HR) : ¤ Respiratory Rate (RR) : ¤ Temperature : ¤ Heart : NAD / Any positive finding. ¤ Lungs : Clear / Creps. ¤ Muscle tone : Normal/Hypo or Hypertonic ¤ Reflex : Good / moderate / poor ¤ Fontanelle : Normal / Bulged / Depressed. ¤ Meconium stain on aspiration : Yes/No. ∆ Diagnosis : ● Male / Female ● Term / Preterm (PT) ● Inborn / Outborn with ● Exact diagnosis (dx). ► Keep the baby warm / Prevent hypothermia : • Wrap the baby with adequate clothing including cap and socks. • Adequate clothing covering head, neck, truck. • Kangaroo mother care (KMC) : Skin to skin contract with mother. Bedding with mother. • Keep the room warm ( ≥ 25°C) [with room heater if needed]. • Avoid early bathing. ► Protects against infection : 1. Use hand sanitizers before handling the baby. 2. Handle baby as minimum as possible. 3. Don't give prelacteal feed. 4. Keep the umbilical stump bare, clean and dry. 5. Avoid overcrowding.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – No specific complaint from mother/near relatives & come only for evaluation. May have distended abdomen, but no lethargy, able to suck breast milk effectively (sucking reflex). O/E – ▸ Moro reflex – NAD [Spreading out of the arms (abduction)]. ▸ Lung & Heart – NAD. ▸ Activity – well alert. ▸ Effectively suck feedings. Δ Apparently Healthy Baby ☼ Diet : EBF. ☼ O2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Keep the baby warm. ☼ Apply 7.1 % Chlorhexidine / Hexicord (aci) / Germicord (sq) around umbilicus stat. ☼ Inj. Vitamin K1 2 mg / K-one MM (sq) / K MM (incp) / Babykion (che), 1 amp PO on D1, D4 & D28. ☼ Simethicone Pediatric Drops 67 mg/ml / Flacol (sq) / Pedicon (ori)/ Pedigas (glob), 5 drops three or four times daily --- 5-30 days ► Apparently healthy babies should not be released without observation or evaluation by a senior doctor. Some silly points or findings can be missed by an inexperienced doctor. ► These are noninvasive medications for each and every newborn. Then add other drugs, fluid or investigations according senior or professor in next scheduled round.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.disease_name: PNA c̄ HIE (Perinatal asphyxia with Hypoxic Ischaemic Encephalopathy)
treatment.description: PNA c̄ HIE (Perinatal asphyxia with Hypoxic Ischaemic Encephalopathy) Perinatal Asphyxia (PNA) : It is a clinical condition resulting from impairment of gas exchange in the fetus leading to hypoxia; hypercarbia and acidosis. Approximately 10% of newborns require some assistance to begin breathing at birth after drying. About 1% needs extensive resuscitative measure to survive. Diagnosis by clinical features : • No respiration, no cry (apnoea). • Gasping respiration with long pauses or absent of spontaneous breathing after birth. • Bradycardia ( • Cyanosis. • Convulsions / seizures. • Skin appears bluish grey (asphyxia livida) or death like pallor (asphyxia pallid). • Less tissue perfusion (capillary refill time >3 sec). • Muscular hypotonia. • Lethargic / comatose & absent reflex. • Oliguria. Stage Symptoms HIE - I Delayed crying (only) - - HIE - II Delayed crying Convulsion - HIE - III Delayed crying ± Convulsion Lethargy / Coma • 5 min APGAR score → 0-3 severe. • 5 min APGAR score → 4-7 mild to moderate. Easy staging of Hypoxic Ischemic Encephalopathy (HIE) [only term babies of > 36 weeks] : Signs Stage 1 (Mild) Stage 1 (Moderate) Stage 3 (Severe) *Level of consciousness Hyper-alert Lethargic Stuporous, Coma *Moro reflex Strong Weak Absent Pupils Mydriasis Miosis Unequal, poor to light reflex Clonus / Tendon reflex Hyperactive Hyperactive Absent Seizures None Common Decerebration For more please see Sarnat & Sarnat stages of HIE from Essence of Pediatrics by Prof. MR Khan & M Ekhlasur Rahman. Caution : These are the not exact drugs & doses. There are hundreds of drugs with varying dose for same indication. These are only a pro-forma. Supervision of experienced consultants is must. Note : Treatment plan may vary patient to patient according to condition. There is different treatment protocol for the same disease. C/F : ➢ 5-min APGAR score of 0 – 3. ➢ Hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy (HIE) – altered tone, depressed level of consciousness, and seizure. ➢ Signs of hypoxic-ischemic injury to multiple organs. ➢ Foetal acidosis (pH Prognosis : • Hypoxic–ischemic encephalopathy (HIE) is an important cause of permanent damage to CNS tissues. 20 – 30% Infants with HIE die in the neonatal period. • Permanent neurodevelopmental abnormalities (cerebral palsy, mental retardation) occur in 33-50%.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.disease_name: PNA c̄ HIE (Perinatal asphyxia with Hypoxic Ischaemic Encephalopathy)
treatment.description: Δ PNA c̄ HIE-II Adv. : ▸ RBS ▸ Serum Calcium ▸ Serum electrolytes ▸ Serum creatinine Sepsis screening : ▸ CBC ▸ CRP ☼ NPO-TFO / EBF. ☼ O2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Keep the baby warm. ☼ If ☼ Apply 7.1 % Chlorhexidine / Hexicord (aci) / Germicord (sq) around umbilicus stat. ☼ Inf. 10% DA / Libott-10% (libra) / Dexaqua DS (bex) / 10% Dextrose (osl), I/V stat @ [ml/25] μ d/m. [80 × wt] ml in total – Day-1 Then, ☼ Inf. (¼) 0.225% Sodium Chloride + 10% Dextrose/ADS-10 (libra)/APN (osl)/Pedisol DS (pop)/ Electrodex (ori) [2nd Day to 2nd Month], I/V stat @ [ml/25] μ d/m. [100 × wt] ml in total – Day-2 [110 × wt] ml in total – Day-3 [120 × wt] ml in total – Day-4 [130 × wt] ml in total – Day-5 [140 × wt] ml in total – Day-6 [150 × wt] ml in total – Day-7 & afterwards ☼ Inj. Ceftazidime (250mg/2.5ml) or (500mg /5 ml) / Tazid (sq) / Serozid (ops) / Ceftazid (aci) / Trum 3 (drg) 250 or 500 mg, [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Amikacin (100 mg / 2 ml) / Kacin (aci) / Akicin (sq) / Amikin (incp) 100mg/2ml, [0.15 × wt] ml IV stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Vitamin K1 2 mg / K-one MM (sq) / K MM (incp) / Babykion (che), 1 amp I/V or PO on D1, D4 & D28. Add, if severe respiratory distress or Gasping preterm (PT) baby : ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone (5mg/1ml) + 4ml D/W, [0.15 × wt] IV stat & TDS

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.disease_name: PNA c̄ HIE (Perinatal asphyxia with Hypoxic Ischaemic Encephalopathy)
treatment.description: Things to be given to buy at admission : ╬ IV cannula 24 G --- (4) ╬ JMS Micropore 1” ---- (1) ╬ JMS Microburette --- (1) ╬ Inf. 10% DA / Libott-10 ---- (1) ╬ IV Inj. Serozid (ops) / Tazid (sq) 250 mg -- (1) ╬ Inj. Kacin 100 mg/2ml ----- (1) ╬ Inj. K-one MM (Sq) / K-MM (inp) ---- (1) ╬ Hexicord ---- (1) ╬ Inj. Filin ---- (1) ╬ Inj. Adrenaline ---- (1) ╬ Suction tube ---- (1) ╬ ১ গজ রেক্সিন ---- (১) ╬ বসার বড় টুল ---- (১) ╬ প্লাস্টিক গামলা ---- (১) ► NPO – TFO is must to prevent aspiration pneumonia. Babies with PNA or LBW are usually associated with poor or absent sucking reflex or impaired shallow inspite of good sucking reflex. We may suggest apparently healthy baby to suck breast milk (EBF) in case of excessive crying. But NPO is always the safe line. ► After keeping the baby NPO, trophic feeding should be given only to ready the gut for milk & enzyme secretion. Term baby = 1 ml / 6 hr or only 4 ml / day. Pre term baby = 0.5 ml / 6 hr or only 2 ml / day. Increase the amount of milk. ▸ O-P suction is a must & it is the prior step of O 2 inhalation for resuscitation. As O 2 will not reach lung alveoli if airway is blocked. ▸ Immediate O 2 inhalation should be given 2L/min through nasal cannula or 5-6 L/min through head box. ▸ Nebulization may be suggested. But neonates has no prominent respiratory muscles so bronchodilators poorly act. ☼ Nebulization with Budesonide (0.5mg/2ml) / Budicort, 0.3 ml + 1 ml N/S stat & 6 hourly. ▸ Recent studies has shown 7.1% chlorhexidine application as part of routine ENC for umbilical cord care to reduce infection. Apply 7.1% chlorhexidine within 24 hours for single time. ▸ Give infusion fluid as the baby is kept NPO. 10 % DA for first 24 hours of birth & APN /ADS–10 for next 2 months after first 24 hours. ▸ Another antibiotic combinations : Please see below (LBW discussion portion). ▸ Antibiotic should be routinely use, even in an apparently healthy baby to prevent hospital acquired / nosocomial infections. ▸ Vitamin K supplement : Please see below (LBW discussion portion).

treatment.title: Helping Babies Breath (HBB)
treatment.disease_name: PNA c̄ HIE (Perinatal asphyxia with Hypoxic Ischaemic Encephalopathy)
treatment.description: Immediate resuscitation by WHO 2005 guideline : Helping Babies Breath (HBB) : Neonatal Resuscitation (WHO) Dry the baby with clean cloth and place the baby where it will be warm → Draw Blood and send for RBS, Ca 2+ , Mg 2+ levels ↓ (30 sec) Look For : • Breathing/ Crying • Good Muscle Tone • Colour : Pink. Check : • Position of the head of the baby in neutral position to open the airway. • Airway (Clean if necessary). • Stimulate and reposition. • Give Oxygen (if necessary). Yes → Breathing & Pink. ↓ Routine Care & Close Observation. (30 sec) ↓ Not breathing & cyanosed Use a Correctly fitting mask & give the baby 5 slow ventilations with bag. • Check position & fit of mask and adjust if needed. • Provide ventilation with bag and mask. • If chest wall is not moving provide airway suction. → If Breathing, Close Observation (30 sec) ↓ Not breathing & cyanosed CALL FOR HELP : Check Heart rate by cord pulsation or by stethoscope. If HR Compress the chest with ventilations at a ratio of 3:1. If HR >60/min : • Continue to bag at 40 breaths/min. Use Oxygen if available. • Make sure the chest is moving adequately. • Every 1-2 min, stop to see if the pulse or breathing has improved. • Stop compression once the HR >100/min. • Stop Bagging when RR >30/min. • Continue O 2 until pink and active. ↓ Not breathing & cyanosed No Breathing & Absent Pulse. After 20 minutes of resuscitation → Cease all resuscitation efforts & counsel the parents that the baby has died. [Ref – Clinical Management Protocol, SSK, MH&FW, Draft, Page– 20 & 21] Post resuscitation care : • Maintain body temperature. • Support respiration by – ✓ Supplemental oxygen by nasal cannula (2L/min), head box (5-6 L/min). ✓ Continuing artificial ventilation if already in the machine. • Regular monitoring (4-6 hourly) : ✓ Respiratory status : respiratory rate, cyanosis, SPO 2 . ✓ Circulatory status : Heart rate, BP, capillary refilling time (CRT). ✓ Renal status : Urine volume, S. creatinine, electrolytes. ✓ Haematological status : Any bleeding, anaemia. ✓ Capillary blood glucose status. ✓ Arterial blood gas (ABG) analysis, when needed.

treatment.title: Complications
treatment.disease_name: PNA c̄ HIE (Perinatal asphyxia with Hypoxic Ischaemic Encephalopathy)
treatment.description: Consequences of PNA : • Brain- Hypoxic ischemic encephalopathy(HIE) • Kidney-Acute kidney injury • Heart-Hypoxic cardiomyopathy/CCF • Lungs- Pulmonary hemorrhage (PPHN), RDS • Adrenal hemorrhage • Liver- Hepatocellular necrosis • SIADH, Hypoglycemia, Hypocalcemia, Hyponatremia • DIC Effect of hypoxia : ➢ CNS- HIE, infarction, intracranial hemorrhage, seizures, cerebral edema, hypotonia, hypertonia. ➢ CVS-Myocardial ischemia, poor contractility, cardiac stunning, tricuspid insufficiency, hypotension. ➢ Pulmonary - Pulmonary hypertension (HTN), pulmonary hemorrhage (Hg), RDS ➢ Renal – ATN or cortical necrosis → AKI → CKD. ➢ Adrenal hemorrhage ➢ GIT–perforation, ulceration c̄ hemorrhage, necrosis ➢ Metabolic – SIADH, hyponatremia, hypoglycemia, hypocalcemia, myoglobinuria. ➢ Subcutaneous fat necrosis. ➢ DIC.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: • Capillary refilling time (CRT) >3 sec (press over sternum gently), • H/R • BP low. Management : ¤ Infuse normal saline bolus @ 10 ml/kg over 30 min, then Give dopamine / dobutamine @ 5-10 µgm/kg/min. • Inf. N/S [10 × wt] ml, I/V bolus stat, then, • Inj. Dopamine 200mg/5ml, 0.5 ml in each 100ml of fluid baby already gets. ¤ Neck extended, O-P suction & O 2 inhalation. ¤ Stimulation (Rubbing over vertebral column, chest, palm & sole, CPR).

treatment.title: Types of seizures
treatment.description: Usually not detected by parents, its job of physician to diagnosis convulsion & used appropriate anticonvulsants. Classification : a) Subtle seizures (50%) : 1. Ocular : Tonic horizontal deviation of eyes or sustained eye opening with ocular fixation or cycled fluttering. (- পিউপিল নিচের দিকে থাকতে পারে। - একদৃষ্টিতে তাকিয়ে থাকতে পারে। - ঘন ঘন চোখের পলক ফেলতে পারে।) 2. Oral-facial-lingual movements : Chewing, tongue thrusting, lip smacking. (- কিছু চাবানোর মত মুখ নাড়াতে পারে। - বার বার জিহবা বের করতে পারে। - খাবার জন্য ঠোঁট নাড়াতে পারে।) 3. Limb movement : Cycling, Padding, boxing-jabs. (সাইকেলিং এর মত পা নাড়াতে পারে বা নড়ীরহম এর মত হাত নাড়াতে পারে।) 4. Autonomic phenomena : Tachycardia or bradycardia. 5. Apnea with accelerated or normal heart rate when evaluated 20 seconds after onset. b) Clonic seizures : rhythmic movements of muscle groups. c) Tonic seizures : sustained flexion of axial or appendicular muscle groups. d) Myoclonic seizures : single or multiple jerks of the upper or lower limbs.

treatment.title: Anti-convulsants & subsequent treatment
treatment.description: Anti-convulsants for neonates : • Phenobarbitone (PB) lV 20 mg/kg over 20 minutes (slowly @ 1mg/kg/min); if not controlled repeat 10/mg/kg/dose up to 40mg/dose. • Check & correct hypoglycaemia or hypocalcaemla, if present. • Convulsion continues : Start Fosphenytoin 30 mg/kg/dose lV diluted in Normal Saline slowly @ 1mg/kg/min. • Convulsion continues : Midazolam initiation bolus 0.2 mg/kg then, 0.01-2.0 mg/kg/hour in drip; increase every I5 minute up to 2 mg/kg/hour, if no response. • When controlled : PB 5 mg/kg/12 hourly for maintenance. • If convulsion continues : Refer to tertiary hospital. Choice : If age is less than 3 months then, 1. Inj. Phenobarbitone / Barbit (I/V). ↓ 2. Inj. Phosphophenytoin / Fosfen (I/V). ↓ 3. Inj. Diazepam / Sedil (P/R). ↓ 4. Inj. Midazolam / Dormitrol (P/R). If age is more than 3 months then, 1. Inj. Diazepam / Sedil (P/R). ↓ 2. Inj. Phosphophenytoin / Fosfen (I/V). ↓ 3. Inj. Phenobarbitone / Barbit (I/V). Age of child ☼ Inj. Phenobarbitone / Barbit (incp) 1 ml + 9 ml D/W, then, [wt] ml I/V stat over 20 mins. after 15 mins ↓ uncontrolled ☼ Inj. Phenobarbitone / Barbit (incp) 1 ml + 9 ml D/W, then, [wt/2] ml I/V stat over 10 mins. after 15 mins ↓ uncontrolled If after 15 mins convulsion is uncontrolled then switch to Fosphenytoin. ☼ Inj. Fosphenytoin (150 mg/2 ml) / Fosfen (pop) 2 ml + 8 ml D/W or N/S (total 10 ml), [2 × wt] ml I/V stat. after 15 mins ↓ uncontrolled ☼ Inj. Midazolam 15mg/3ml / Hypofast (incp) / Milam (skf) + 7 ml D/W [Dose: 0.2mg/kg/IV stat], [wt/7.5] ml I/V stat. ↓ If not controlled with midazolam : Use others e.g., levetiracetam, topiramate, sodium valproate. ↓ When convulsion controlled, Maintenance dose : ☼ Inj. Phenobarbitone / Barbit (incp) 1 ml + 9 ml D/W, then, [wt/8] ml I/V stat & BD. ☼ Check & correct hypoglycaemia or hypocalcaemla, if present. Advices for evaluation the cause of convulsion : 1. Serum Calcium (S. Ca ++ ). 2. S. Electrolytes. 3. RBS. Diazepam is used in older infants, ☼ Inj. Diazepam (10mg/2ml) + 8 ml DW or NS, then [wt/2] ml per rectal (PR) though NG tube or rubber tube during active convulsion. Up to 3 times in 10 min interval. Dose : 0.5 mg/kg per rectally (P/R). If convulsion free for 48 hours : 1. Omit Phosphophenytoin / Fosfen first & observe 48-72 hours. 2. If no convulsion then start, syp or tab phenobarbital. ○ Tab. Phenobarbitone / Barbit 30 mg, 0+0+ ¼ for 6 weeks. If baby develops distended abdomen : Cause : Neurogenic bladder due to phenobarbitone. Evacuate the bladder by pressing on the UB or abdomen. If hypoglycaemia (Glucose ☼ Inf. 10% DA 10 ml IV @60 micro drops/min stat [2ml/kg]. If convulsion free for 48 hours (2 days) : 1. Omit anti-convulsant first & observe next 48-72 hours. 2. If no convulsion then start, oral PB : ☼ Syp. Phenobarbital (20mg/5ml)/ Barbit Elixir (incp)/ Emer (ops), [wt] ml once at night or in two divided dose for 6 weeks. Subsequent treatment : • If sepsis suspects : Do septic screen & per-enteral broad-spectrum antibiotics as in sepsis. • Keep the baby NPO for 48-72 hours. • Appropriate IV fluid - (30% curtail) 10% DA first day then 10% dextrose in baby saline. • Treatment of other complications : Anuria, DIC, dyselectrolytemia etc.

treatment.title: Discharge Treatment
treatment.description: Discharge criteria : • Baby on full oral feed. • Vital signs are stable. • Convulsion control with or without drugs. Rx on Discharge : HIE stage I : No discharge medication. HIE stage II / III : Tab. Phenobarbital / Emer / Barbit 30mg, 0 + 0 + ⅓ for 6 weeks. Advice : Follow up at pediatrics OPD or ‘Shishu Bikash Kendra’ after 6 weeks. Discharge Treatment for PNA c̄ HIE – II / III -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- Rx, ᴏ Syp. Phenobarbital / Babit Elixir (incp) / Emer (ops), [wt/2] মিলি × দিনে ২ বার ..... ৬ সপ্তাহ, অথবা, [wt] মিলি × দিনে ২ বার ..... ৬ সপ্তাহ উপদেশ : ১. ৬ মাস বয়স পর্যন্ত শিশুকে শুধুমাত্র বুকের দুধ খাওয়াবেন। এরপর বুকের দুধের পাশাপাশি অন্যান্য স্বাভাবিক খাবার যেমন : নরম খাবার, সুজি, খিচুরি ইত্যাদি খাওয়াবেন। ২. দেড় মাস (১.৫ মাস) বয়স থেকে ইপিআই (EPI) শিডিউল অনুযায়ী টিকা দেওয়া শুরু করবেন। টিকাদান কার্ড অনুযায়ী সময়মত সবগুলো টিকি দিবেন। ৩. বাচ্চাকে ঠান্ডা লাগাবেন না। মাথায় তেল ও চোখে কাজল দিবেন না। ৪. পরবর্তীতে কোনো সমস্যার জন্য “শিশু বিকাশ কেন্দ্র” বা শিশুরোগ বিষেশজ্ঞ বা বহি: বিভাগে দেখা করবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন। ▸ Phenobarbitone / Phenobarbital is indicated in HIE – II / III (c̄ H/O convulsion), use according to weight & under supervision of senior doctors. Usually no drug is used on discharge for neonates if not highly indicated. ▸ Here multivitamin / vitamin B complex can also be prescribed : ☼ P/D Multi-vitamin / V Plex (acm) / Tynisol (bex) / Multisina (ibn) / Biovit (bio) / Vita-M (ops) Pedi Drop, ১০ ফোটা করে দিনে ১ বার ...... ১ মাস

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Low Birth Weight (LBW) – due to PT or IUGR
treatment.description: Low Birth Weight (LBW) – due to Preterm (PT) or IUGR C/C – 1. Preterm (PT) or IUGR LBW. Diagnosis : ∆ ● Male / Female ● Term / Preterm (PT) ● Inborn / Outborn with ● LBW / VLBW / Extremely LBW / Incredible LBW. Here, - LBW = - VLBW (very LBW) = - Extremely LBW = - Incredible LBW = Δ PT – LBW Adv. : ▸ CBC c̄ PBF ▸ IT ratio ▸ CRP ▸ RBS ▸ Blood grouping & Rh typing Gestational age : Weight : ☼ NPO-TFO / EBF. ☼ O2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Keep the baby warm. ☼ If ☼ Apply 7.1 % Chlorhexidine / Hexicord (aci) / Germicord (sq) around umbilicus stat. ☼ Inf. 10% DA / Libott-10% (libra) / Dexaqua DS (bex) / 10% Dextrose (osl), I/V stat @ [ml/25] μ d/m. [80 × wt] ml in total – Day-1 Then, ☼ Inf. (¼) 0.225% Sodium Chloride + 10% Dextrose / ADS-10 (libra) / APN (osl) / Pedisol DS (pop) / Electrodex (ori) [2nd Day to 2nd Month] I/V stat @ [ml/25] μ d/m. [100 × wt] ml in total – Day-2 of age [120 × wt] ml in total – Day-3 [130 × wt] ml in total – Day-4 [140 × wt] ml in total – Day-5 [150 × wt] ml in total – Day-6 [160 × wt] ml in total – Day-7 [170 × wt] ml in total – Day-8 & afterwards ☼ Inj. Ceftazidime (250mg/2.5ml) or (500mg /5 ml) / Tazid (sq) / Serozid (ops) / Ceftazid (aci) / Trum 3 (drg) 250 or 500 mg, [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Amikacin (100 mg / 2 ml) / Kacin (aci) / Akicin (sq) / Amikin (incp) 100mg/2ml, [0.15 × wt] ml IV stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Vitamin K1 2 mg / K-one MM (sq) / K MM (incp) / Babykion (che), 1 amp PO on D1, D4 & D28. Add, if severe respiratory distress or Gasping preterm (PT) baby : ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone (5mg/1ml) + 4ml D/W, [0.15 × wt] IV stat & TDS

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.disease_name: Low Birth Weight (LBW) – due to PT or IUGR
treatment.description: ► If there is convulsion : ☼ Inj. Phenobarbitone 200mg/1ml + 9 ml D/W, [wt] ml IV stat & [wt/8] ml IV BD. [Phenobarbital 200mg/ml / Barbit (incp) : 20mg/Kg stat as loading dose & 2.5mg/kg BD as maintenance dose.] ► Things to be given to buy at admission (if needed) : ╬ IV cannula 24 G ---- (4) ╬ JMS Micropore 1” ---- (1) ╬ JMS Microburette ----- (1) ╬ Inf. 10% DA / Libott-10 ---- (1) ╬ IV Inj. Serozid (ops)/Tazid (sq) 250 mg--(1) ╬ Inj. Kacin 100 mg/2ml ---- (1) ╬ Inj. K-one MM (Sq) / K-MM (inp) ---- (1) ╬ Hexicord ----- (1) ╬ Hexisol 50 ml ----- (1) ╬ Inj. Filin ----- (1) ╬ Inj. Adrenaline ---- (1) ╬ Suction tube ---- (1) ╬ ১ গজ রেক্সিন ---- (১) ╬ বসার বড় টুল ---- (১) ╬ প্লাস্টিক গামলা ---- (১) ► If weight less than 18 Kg & Warmer / incubator is not available – ╬ কটন / তুলা ----- (১) ╬ টেবিল ল্যাম্প সেট ---- (১) ╬ ২০০ ওয়ার্ট বাল্ব ---- (১) ╬ কালো সুতি কাপড় ---- ১ গজ ╬ সেফটিপিন ----- ১ ডজন ╬ ছোট কেচি ---- (১) ► If weight less than 10 kg IV cannula 24 G & microburette should be given. As failure rate is higher so amount should be 3 to 5. IV cannula 22G is given to child more than 10 kg. Suction tube is given for O-P suction. Inj. Filin & Adrenalin should be given for emergency condition like apnea & cardiac arrest. Usually 2 – 4 neonates are kept on a single bed in a crowded hospital. So a tool / chair (বসার বড় টুল) is to buy for siting purpose. টেবিল ল্যাম্প সেট ও ২০০ ওয়ার্ট বাল্ব to keep the baby warm. কালো সুতি কাপড়, সেফটিপিন, ছোট কেচি to prevent direct light exposure to eye & testis. Cotton to wrap the baby. Hexisol 50 ml for medical stuffs & guardians to disinfect their hands before touching the baby. Money / Taka notes should never be touched by neonates. No bathing till 2 nd / 3 rd day of life. Antibiotics & saline may be supplied in the ward, please ask the SSN (senior stuff Nurse) which are supplied & which are not. ► Exclusive breastfeeding (EBF) refers to the practice of giving an infant breast milk not a drop of water only up to the age of six months (180 days), except for oral rehydration (ORS) in diarrhoea. ► NPO-TFO is must to prevent aspiration pneumonia. Babies with PNA or LBW are usually associated with poor or absent sucking reflex or impaired shallow in spite of good sucking reflex. We may suggest apparently healthy baby to suck breast milk (EBF) in case of excessive crying. But NPO is always the safe line. ▸ After keeping the baby NPO, trophic feeding should be given gradually only to ready the gut for milk & enzyme secretion. Term baby = 1 ml / 6 hr or only 4 ml / day. Pre term baby = 0.5 ml / 6 hr or only 2 ml / day. Increase the amount of milk gradually. ► O-P suction is a must & it is the prior step of O 2 inhalation for resuscitation. As O 2 will not reach lung alveoli if airway is blocked. ▸ Immediate O 2 inhalation should be given 2L/min through nasal cannula or 5-6 L/min through head box. ▸ Nebulization may be suggested. But neonates have no prominent respiratory muscles so bronchodilators poorly act. ☼ Nebulization with Budesonide (0.5mg/2ml) / Budicort, 0.3 ml + 1 ml N/S stat & 6 hourly. ► Recent studies has shown 7.1% chlorhexidine application as part of routine ENC for umbilical cord care to reduce infection. Apply 7.1% chlorhexidine within 24 hours for single time. ► Chlorhexidine 7.1% (10 ml) এর ড্রপার বোতল চেপে সম্পূর্ণ সলিউশনটি নবজাতকের জন্মের পরপর কাটা নাড়ীর অগ্রভাগ, চারপাশ ও গোড়ায় এমনভাবে লাগান যেন সম্পূর্ণ নাড়ীটি ভালো ভাবে ভিজে যায়। Chlorhexidine লাগানোর পর নাড়ী কোন কিছু দিয়ে মুছবেন না। একবার Chlorhexidine লাগানোর পর নাড়ী শুষ্ক রাখুন ও নাড়ীতে অন্য কোন কিছুই লাগাবেন না। চোখে যেন কোনভাবেই না লাগে। ► Give infusion fluid as the baby is kept NPO. 10 % DA for first 24 hours of birth & APN / ADS–10 for next 2 months after first 24 hours. ► Another antibiotic combinations (first line antibiotics) : The following combination should be given first but we empirically use second line antibiotics at first (Ceftazidime & Amikacin). ☼ Inj. Ampicillin / Ampicin (500 mg/5 ml) / Ampexin (ops), [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD (unavailable). ☼ Inj. Gentamycin / Gentin (ops) 20 mg/2 ml, [wt/4] ml I/V stat & BD. [wt/16 if 80mg/2ml ampule] ▸ Another antibiotic combination : ☼ Inj. Cefotaxime / Maxcef (sq) / Cefotax (ren) 250 mg/2.5 ml, [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Amikacin / Kacin (aci) 100mg/2ml, [0.15 × wt] ml I/V stat & BD. ▸ Antibiotic should be routinely use (c̄ consultation of seniors), even in an apparently healthy baby to prevent hospital acquired / nosocomial infections. ► Vitamin K supplement : Inj. Vitamin K 1 2 mg / K-one MM is routinely administered orally to each baby. But administered I/V if IV cannula is done. [Mnemonic, 3 doses at ‘4 th hour, 4 th day, 4 th week’.] Dose of Inj. Phytomenadione 2mg / 0.2 ml : ○ 1 st dose at within 4 hours / first day, ○ 2 nd dose at 4 th day & ○ 3 rd dose at 4 th week / 28 th day. Vitamin E capsule / Cap. E 200 mg 2 drop orally stat & 1 drop in each morning for 14 days can also be given. ► Steroid (IV dexamethasone) should be used for LBW (specially in PT) under supervision of seniors.

treatment.title: Discharge Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Low Birth Weight (LBW) – due to PT or IUGR
treatment.description: ► Discharge criteria : 1. Able to maintain temperature. 2. No apnoea and bradycardia in last 5 days. 3. Able to take and tolerate full feeding from breast or cup spoon without respiratory discomfort. 4. Parents are confident enough to take care. 5. Crossed birth weight & shows a steady weight gain for 3 consecutive days. Discharge Treatment for LBW -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- Rx, ১৫ দিন বয়স পূর্ণ হলে, 1. Multivitamin Pediatric Drop / V-Plex (acm) / Tynisol (bex) / Multisina (ibn) / Biovit (bio) / Vita-M (ops) P/D, ১০ ফোটা করে দিনে ১ বার ...... ৩ – ৬ মাস 2. Syp. Folic Acid (2.5 mg/5 ml) / B-9 (sq) / Foli Kidz (acm), [wt] মিলি দিনে ১ বার ...... ৩ – ৬ মাস [or, Tab. Folison (jay), ¼ or ⅓ + 0 + 0 ... ৩ – ৬ মাস] দেড় মাস / ৪৫ দিন বয়স পূর্ণ হলে, 3. Iron Pediatric Drop (50mg/ml) / Compiron (incp) P/D, ৩ ফোটা করে দিনে ১ বার ..... ৩ মাস ± 4. Cap. Vit-E / E-gel (ren) / Evit (sq) / E-cap (drg) 200 IU, ২ ফোটা করে দিনে ২ বার ...... ১ মাস উপদেশ : ১. ৬ মাস বয়স পর্যন্ত শিশুকে শুধুমাএ বুকের দুধ খাওয়াবেন। এরপর বুকের দুধের পাশাপাশি অন্যান্য স্বাভাবিক খাবার যেমন : সুজি, নরম খিচুরি ইত্যাদি খাওয়াবেন। ২. ইপিআই (EPI) শিডিউল অনুযায়ী টিকা দেওয়া শুরু করবেন। ৩. বাচ্চাকে ঠান্ডা লাগাবেন না। মাথায় তেল ও চোখে কাজল দিবেন না। ৪. পরবর্তীতে কোনো সমস্যার জন্য শিশু বহি: বিভাগে দেখা করবেন। ৫. ১ মাস পর চক্ষু বিশেষজ্ঞের কাছে বাচ্চার চোখ ও ই.এন.টি বিশেষজ্ঞের কাছে বাচ্চার কান পরীক্ষা করাবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন। Note : Iron store in the body in last (3 rd ) trimester, but premature baby lacks in last trimester. So LBW has no or less iron storage. So multivitamins & folic acid are given to stimulate erythropoiesis. As gut can’t absorb medication in first 15 days, so these drugs are given after 15 days of age. Iron needs at least 45 days of age to be absorbed.

treatment.title: Complications
treatment.disease_name: Low Birth Weight (LBW) – due to PT or IUGR
treatment.description: ► Problems with LBW : ➢ Early : o PNA. o Hypothermia (Temperature ° F). o Hypoglycaernia (RBS: o Respiratory distress syndrome (RDS). o Apnoeic spells (cessation of respiration for >20 sec). o Infections. o Haemorhage e.g. minor to fatal intraventricular, GI, pulmonary haemorrhage. o Feeding difficulty, intolerance. o Problems of gut :NEC, GERD. o Exaggeration of physiological jaundice. o Anemia of prematurity. o PDA. ➢ Late : o Metabolic bone diseases e.g. Rickets of prematurity. o Retinopathy of prematurity (ROP). o Delayed growth and development. o CP.

treatment.title: Respiratory distress at 1st day
treatment.description: 1. Tachypnea (> 60/min). 2. Grunting / Expiratory grunting. 3. Sternal and intercostals recession. 4. Cyanosis. Meconium Aspiration Syndrome (MAS) Respiratory distress syndrome (RDS) Usually occurs within 1 hr of life. Usually within first 6 hr of life. Term, post term & IUGR male babies are affected. Most common cause of respiratory distress in first day of life. Pre-term babies due to surfactant deficiency. May be in perinatal asphyxia & maternal DM. O/E : Meconium stained umbilical cord, skin, nail. Distended chest or, ↑ AP diameter of chest due to emphysema. Coarse crackles over lung field. Chest X-Ray : Early; Reticulo-granular pattern, mottling. Late: Bilateral confluent opacification ( air bronchogram)

treatment.title: Features & Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Medical referral note of H/O – Aspiration of meconium contaminated amniotic fluid or information from patient near relatives – H/O newborn’s defecation within womb. New born may be apparently healthy but should start IV antibiotics & respiratory supports. Risk factors : • Term/post term/IUGR male baby, • Delivered by CS, • Oligohydramnios. C/F : • Resp. Distress within first hours : Tachypnea(> 60/min). • Grunting. • Sternal and intercostals recession/retraction. • Cyanosis. • Hypotonia, seizures. • Concomitant asphyxia present. O/E — • Meconium stained umbilical cord, skin, nail. • Distended chest – ↑ AP diameter of chest due to emphysema. • Coarse crackles over lung field. CXR— • Patchy infiltration, • Coarse irregular streaking of both lung field, • ↑ antero- posterior (AP) diameter. • Horizontal ribs. • Flattening of diaphragm. • Pneumothorax (may be). • PO 2 – ↓. Δ MAS (Meconium Aspiration Syndrome) Adv. : ▸ CXR. Sepsis screening : ▸ CBC ▸ CRP If convulsion : ▸ RBS ▸ Serum Calcium ▸ Serum electrolytes ▸ Serum creatinine ☼ NPO-TFO (if RR > 60/min) / EBF. ☼ O2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Keep the baby warm. ☼ If ☼ Apply 7.1 % Chlorhexidine / Hexicord (aci) / Germicord (sq) around umbilicus stat. ☼ Inf. 10% DA / Libott-10% (libra) / Dexaqua DS (bex) / 10% Dextrose (osl), I/V stat @ [ml/25] μ d/m. [80 × wt] ml in total – Day-1 Then, ☼ Inf. (¼) 0.225% Sodium Chloride + 10% Dextrose/ADS-10 (libra)/APN (osl)/Pedisol DS (pop)/ Electrodex (ori) [2nd Day to 2nd Month] I/V stat @ [ml/25] μ d/m. [100 × wt] ml in total – Day-2 [110 × wt] ml in total – Day-3 [120 × wt] ml in total – Day-4 [130 × wt] ml in total – Day-5 [140 × wt] ml in total – Day-6 [150 × wt] ml in total – Day-7 & afterwards ☼ Inj. Ceftazidime (250mg/2.5ml) or (500mg /5 ml) / Tazid (sq) / Serozid (ops) / Ceftazid (aci) / Trum 3 (drg) 250 or 500 mg, [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Amikacin (100 mg / 2 ml) / Kacin (aci) / Akicin (sq) / Amikin (incp) 100mg/2ml, [0.15 × wt] ml IV stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Vitamin K1 2 mg / K-one MM (sq) / K MM (incp) / Babykion (che), 1 amp I/V or PO on D1, D4 & D28. ► Ventilatory support & administration of exogenous surfactant to infants with MAS and hypoxemic respiratory failure, or pulmonary hypertension. If hypotonia, bradycardia, apnea then endotracheal intubation+ suction under direct laryngoscope. ± ► Here, treatment on admission is same as PNA / ARDS. Just should add the followings under senior supervision : ☼ Inj. Furosemide (20 mg / 2 ml), (wt/10) ml I/V stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone (5 mg / 1 ml), ¼ ml / amp I/V stat & BD.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – Respiratory distress occurring within 1 hour of birth. O/E – RR > 60/min. – Expiratory grunting. – Sternal and intercostal recession / retraction. – Cyanosis present. Note : Treatment on admission is same as PNA. Nebulization & steroid may also be used in PNA with consultation of senior physicians. Specific & exact treatment of ARDS is surfactant administration. Δ ARDS Adv. : ▸ CXR ☼ NPO-TFO. ☼ O2 inhalation & O-P stat. ☼ IV fluid. ☼ IV antibiotics. ☼ Oral vitamin-K. ± ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone (5mg/1ml) + 4 ml D/W, [0.15 × wt] IV stat & TDS ± ☼ Nebulization with Budesonide (0.5mg / 2ml) / Budicort, 0.3ml + 1ml N/S stat & 6 hrly. ☼ If presented within ½ hour of delivery : Intrathecal surfactant, (4ml/Kg in 4 divided doses). ☼ If significant respiratory distress, hypoxemia, hypercarbia or acidosis : Ventilatory support or CPAP. ► Advice : CXR A/P view (Chest x-ray Antero-posterior view) may be advised to older neonate for further evaluation. CXR A/P view is given to child under 3 years & CXR P/A view is given to child above 3 years. • Early findings : Reticulogranular pattern, mottling. • Late findings : Air bronchogram.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Usually term male baby delivered by C/S. C/F — • Tachypnea; usually 2 -3 hrs after birth and persists upto 24 hrs and within 72 hrs neonate becomes well. • Child remains alert and pink in color. • Retractions respiratory grunting. • Cyanosis (relieved by 2 ). • No crackle/wheeze on chest auscultation. Chest X-ray (CXR) : • Hyper-inflated chest. • Flat diaphragm. • Interlobar or pleural fluid. • Prominent pulmonary. • vascular markings. • No air bronchogram. Management — No specific Rx. • O 2 inhalation stat to maintain SaO 2 (90- 95%). • Oral feeding (EBF) when resp. rate • Prognosis —good and no residual morbidity.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : 1. R/R 2. Capillary refilling time >3 sec. Management : ☼ Inj. Aminophylline (125mg/5ml) / Filin (ops) / Aminophylline (ambee), 0.3 ml or [wt/6] mL I/V stat then, 0.5 ml in each 100 ml of fluid or, [wt] mL I/V throughout 24 hrs in drip (fluids that baby gets) Critical cases : Give AMBU resuscitation. If not improved : with permission of senior : ☼ Inj. Adrenalin (1mg/1ml) / Adrin (gaco) / Adrinor (incp) / G-Adrenaline (gono), 0.1 ml IV stat.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Yellow discolouration of sclera & whole body. 2. Physiological jaundice (if within 3 – 10 days of age). Risk factor for developing significant jaundice : 1. Blood group incompatibility: Baby of Rh negative or O blood group mother. 2. Preterm (PT) baby. 3. H/O previous sibling receiving phototherapy. 4. Concealed haemorrhage. 5.
