Failure of exclusive breast feeding. 6. Weight loss >3%/day. Physiological Jaundice : 1. Jaundice appears on 2nd day. 2. Bilirubin level rises slowly. 3. level rarely goes above 15mg/dl. 4. Baby remains otherwise healthy. 5. Jaundice clears spontaneously within 7-10 days of life. Tx : No drug treatment needed for Physiological Jaundice. Only EBF ± mild sunlight exposure & observation is advocated. বাবা-মা ’ র প্রতি উপদেশ - ১। সাধারণত বাচ্চা ভালোভাবে ও ঠিকমতো দুধ টেনে খেতে পারে, তাই বাচ্চাকে ঘন ঘন বুকের দুধ খাওয়াবেন। ২। হলদেটে ভাব চোখ, গায়ে ও উভয় থাই পর্যন্ত থাকবে এবং পায়খানা হলুদ হবে। তবে পায়ের আর হাতের পাতাও যদি হলদেটে হয়ে যায় এবং পায়খানা সাদা হলে শিশু হাসপাতাল বা চিকিৎসকের শরণাপন্ন হবেন। ৩। বাচ্চাকে ভোরের ও বিকালের হাল্কা সূর্যালোকে অল্প সময়ের জন্য রোদ তাপাবেন। ৪। সাধারনত এই জন্ডিস জন্মের ১০ দিনের মধ্যে কমে যায়, তবে টার্ম বেবিতে ১৪ দিন ও প্রি টার্মে ২১ দিন পর্যন্ত থাকতে পারে। ► Simple sunlight exposure is useful, artificial light sources are better in lowering unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia. But sunlight therapy is under study. Now a group of pediatricians do not recommend sunlight exposure for treatment purpose. Physiological jaundice needs no treatment. Vitamin B complex, Folic acid, Domperidone, Simethicone pediatric drops are placebo. ► Refer pathological jaundice to neonatologists. In case of pathological jaundice need phototherapy accordingly to jaundice management chart. Phototherapy is indicated when indirect s. bilirubin level is high. In case of raised direct S. bilirubin phototherapy is contraindicated. If not improving and bilirubin level is increasing gradually, then need – exchange transfusion. Features of pathological jaundice : 1. Jaundice appeared on 1 st day of life. 2. Jaundice persisting more than 14 days in term baby or, more than 21 days in preterm baby or rises fast (10mg/dl/day). 3. Jaundice extending upto palm and sole. 4. Jaundice with any sign of sepsis. 5. Jaundice with pale stool. Tx : Phototherapy → Exchange transfusion, Treatment of cause. Rx on admission : ☼ NPO-TFO. ☼ O 2 inhalation & O-P stat. ☼ Chlorhexidine on umbilicus. ☼ IV fluid. ☼ IV antibiotics. ☼ Oral vitamin-K. ☼ Continuous phototherapy. Adv. 1. S. total bilirubin. 2. S. direct bilirubin. 3. S. indirect bilirubin. 4. Blood grouping & Rh typing. (both baby and mother) 5. Reticulocyte count or CBC 6. Direct coombs test 7. Sepsis screening

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Things to be given to buy at admission : ╬ IV cannula 24 G ---- (4) ╬ JMS Micropore 1” ----- (1) ╬ JMS Microburette ---- (1) ╬ Inf. 10% DA / Libott-10 ---- (1) ╬ IV Inj. Serozid (ops) / Tazid (sq) 250 mg -- (1) ╬ Inj. Kacin 100 mg/2ml ---- (1) ╬ Inj. K-one MM (sq) / K-MM (incp) --- (1) ╬ Hexicord ---- (1) ╬ Inj. Filin ----- (1) ╬ Inj. Adrenaline ---- (1) ╬ Suction tube ----- (1) ╬ ১ গজ রেক্সিন ----- (১) ╬ বসার বড় টুল ---- (১) ╬ প্লাস্টিক গামলা ----- (১) For phototherapy – ╬ কালো সুতি কাপড় ---- ১ গজ ╬ সেফটিপিন ---- ১ ডজন ╬ ছোট কেচি ----- (১) ► Actually EBF, phototherapy & phenobarbitone (PHB) is the treatment, no antibiotics are indicated but we use antibiotics to prevent hospital acquired infections. Hospital acquired infections are deadly. Ceftriaxone & amikacin should be avoided. If there is convulsion : ☼ Inj. Phenobarbitone 200mg/1ml / Barbit (incp) 1 ml + 9 ml D/W, [wt] ml IV stat & [wt/8] ml IV BD. [20 mg/Kg stat & 2.5 mg/Kg BD] ► If direct bilirubin is more than 20% of total (liver disease / Obstructive jaundice) then phototherapy is not indicated. Phototherapy is given when serum bilirubin is, 10 – 12 mg/dL in preterm babies & 15 mg/dL in term babies. ► Rough measurement of serum bilirubin (Kramer’s rule) : 1. Jaundice terminates at neck = 6 mg/dl. 2. Jaundice terminates at umbilicus & upper arm = 10 mg/dl. 3. Jaundice from umbilicus to knees = 12 mg/dl. 4. Jaundice terminates at forearm-wrist & leg-angle = 15 mg/dl. 5. Jaundice from top to toe = above 16 mg/dl. Modified Kramer's Scale (rough estimation) : Dermal Zone Area of body Approx. level of bilirubin 1 Face 4 – 6 mg/dl 2 Chest, upper abdomen 8 – 10 mg/dl 3 Lower abdomen, thighs 12 – 14 mg/dl 4 Arms, lower legs 15 – 18 mg/dl 5 Palms, soles 16 – 20 mg/dl ► Baby should be naked but eye & male genitalia are covered with black cover to prevent retinal & testicular damage during photo therapy. ► Distance should be kept 45 cm from light source. Preferably blue (420 – 470 nm) light is used. ► Position should be changed frequently. Babies should be taken out only for feeding or changing wet napkins. But if baby cries then he/she can be taken out for 15 mins in every hour. ► Usually 1 day / 24 – 28 hours of exposure is enough. Evaluate the baby after 1 day. ► Indications of exchange transfusion : 1. If intensive phototherapy has failed to reduce bilirubin to a safe range. 2. If the risk of kernicterus exceeds the risk of the procedure. 3. If the initial bilirubin level is too high.

treatment.title: Breast Milk Vs Breast Feeding Jaundice
treatment.description: Breast Milk Jaundice Breast feeding jaundice or, Lactation Failure Jaundice Late onset : Most commonly presents in the 2 nd week of life. Early onset : Usually presents in the 1 st week of life. Moderate to severe. Mild to moderate. T. Bilirubin may 20 – 30 mg/dl. Not so high but may >12 mg/dl. Baby healthy, well thriving. Baby dehydrated. Affected infants are thriving and weight gain is adequate. Affected infants are often dehydrated and have weight loss or poor weight gain. If breast feeding stop for 24 hrs, Jaundice decreases ( ↓). If breast feeding stop for 24 hrs, Jaundice increases ( ↑). Breastfeeding is adequate. Breastfeeding is NOT providing sufficient nutrition. No treatment required. Supplement breastfeeding & specialist consultation. Rx : observation, EBF, assurance. EBF, no artificial feeding, breast feeding at night. Spontaneous resolution usually occurs within 12 th weeks of age. Spontaneous resolution usually occurs by the 3 rd week of life.

treatment.title: Features & Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: [Neonatal sepsis is suspected in case of no obvious history & findings. No presented few of the following symptoms & signs.] Symptoms – 1. Unable suck / poor feeding / refusal to suck. 2. Lethargic, poor cry. 3. Hypothermia / fever. 4. Poor muscle tone / Hypotonia. 5. Less alert, vomiting. 6. Convulsion. 7. Respiratory distress. 8. Fever. Sign – • Hypothermia or fever • Temperature – high / Normal, even hypothermia. • CRT > 3s (Poor perfusion / shock) • Absent / Poor primitive reflexes • Hypotonia, poor reflex • Apnea, fast breathing • Gasping respiration • Chest indrawing, grunting, central cyanosis • Brady/tachycardia • Bleeding • Distended abdomen, vomiting • Poor cry or high pitch cry • Bulged fontanel • Skin pustules > 10 • Periumbilical redness or draining Pus • Hypo/hyperglycemia ╬ Convulsion (indicate meningitis, encephalitis). ╬ Reddish spot over different parts of the body (indicate DIC). ╬ Sclerema – hardening of skin which indicates end stage of sepsis (managed by BT / blood transfusion). Diagnosis : ∆ - Male / Female - Term / Preterm - Inborn / Outborn with - EONS / LONS / LLONS. ● EONS (Early onset neonatal sepsis) : Within 72 hrs (3 days) of life ● LONS (Late onset neonatal sepsis) After 72 hrs (3 days) of life. ● LLONS : (Long late onset neonatal sepsis) > 28 Days. Investigations : Sepsis screening : CBC c̄ PBF : ≥ 2 is positive screen : • Total leucocyte count - 3 or 3 . • Absolute neutrophil count(ANC) 3 . • IT ratio > 0.2. • Micro ESR >15mm in 1st hr. • CRP ≥ 6 mg/l. • PBF – toxic granules. • Platelet – low. Culture & sensitivity : blood, urine, CSF or other body discharge. CSF study – in LONS & in EONS if baby have convulsion, drowsy, other CNS symptoms. Others : o RBS o S. electrolytes o S. creatinine o Coagulation profile (BT, CT) o ABG o X-ray : CXR (if respiratory distress) o Abdominal X-ray – (if suspected NEC, huge abdominal distension) o If convulsion advices for : Serum Calcium (S. Ca ++ ), S. electrolytes, RBS. Δ EONS / LONS ☼ NPO-TFO / EBF. ☼ O2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Keep the baby warm. ☼ If ☼ Apply 7.1 % Chlorhexidine / Hexicord (aci) / Germicord (sq) around umbilicus stat. ☼ Inf. 10% DA / Libott-10% (libra) / Dexaqua DS (bex) / 10% Dextrose (osl), I/V stat @ [ml/25] μ d/m. [80 × wt] ml in total – Day-1 Then, ☼ Inf. (¼) 0.225% Sodium Chloride + 10% Dextrose / ADS-10 (libra) / APN (osl) / Pedisol DS (pop) / Electrodex (ori) [2nd Day to 2nd Month], I/V stat @ [ml/25] μ d/m. [100 × wt] ml in total – Day-2 [110 × wt] ml in total – Day-3 [120 × wt] ml in total – Day-4 [130 × wt] ml in total – Day-5 [140 × wt] ml in total – Day-6 [150 × wt] ml in total – Day-7 & afterwards ☼ Inj. Ceftazidime (250mg/2.5ml) or (500mg /5 ml) / Tazid (sq) / Serozid (ops) / Ceftazid (aci) / Trum 3 (drg) 250 or 500 mg, [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Amikacin (100 mg / 2 ml) / Kacin (aci) / Akicin (sq) / Amikin (incp) 100mg/2ml, [0.15 × wt] ml IV stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Vitamin K1 2 mg / K-one MM (sq) / K MM (incp) / Babykion (che), 1 amp I/V or PO on D1, D4 & D28.

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: Supportive Treatment : o Hypothermia: Wrap the baby with warm clothes, keep under radiant warmer or in incubator. o Hypoglycaemia: Correct by 2ml/kg of 10% DA I/V o Hypocalcemia: I/V by 2ml/kg of 10% Calcium gluconate. o If CRT > 3s, manage shock with 20ml/kg NS over 30 min. o Blood & blood product if needed. o Convulsion: By IV/IM phenobarbitone 20mg/kg loading then if persist, 10mg/kg/dose upto 2 times. o Hypoxia: Give oxygen by nasal cannula, face musk or head box. Keep SpO 2 > 95% o Ensure BF, nutrition, avoid enteral feeding if very sick. o Nutrition : I/V alimentation, nasogastric or orogastric feeding when indicated with 20% fluid restriction. Specific Treatment : antimicrobial therapy (Empirical) : o 1st line – ampicillin + gentamicin. o 2nd line – ceftazidime + amikacin. o 3rd line – meropenem, ciprofloxacin, vancomycin, cefipime, colistin, piperacilin + tazobactum, netilmicin. o In case of suspected meningitis – cefotaxim + amikacin or meropenem + amikacin. N.B.: Antibiotic may be changed after 3 days if improvement does not occur or according to C/S. Duration of antibiotic therapy : Risk factor positive (clinically well, culture negative, screen negative) 2 – 3 days Risk factor positive, screen positive (clinically well, culture negative) 5 – 7 days Clinically sepsis (screen negative) 7 days Clinically sepsis, screen positive (culture negative) 7 – 10 days Blood culture positive (no meningitis) 14 days Meningitis (with or without positive blood / CSF culture) 21 days Osteomyelitis or septic arthritis 3 – 4 weeks ▸ Paracetamol suppository, syrup etc. are less effective for neonatal fever. Tepid sponging with luke warm water & paracetamol pediatric drop can be recommended. ▸ If condition of baby does not improve then higher antibiotics are used. As – ☼ Inj. Meropenam 500mg/5ml, (0.4 × wt) ml I/V stat & TDS. Or, ☼ Inj. Vancomycin 500mg/10 ml D/W, [0.3 × wt]ml + 10 ml saline baby gets, I/V stat @ 10 μ d/m, then, [0.2 × wt] ml + 10 ml saline baby gets, I/V TDS @ 10 μ d/m. ▸ If change antibiotic & give : ☼ Inj. Meropenem / Merocon 500mg + 10ml DW, [2 × wt] ml IV stat and TDS [60mg/kg/day TDS] ☼ Inj. Amikacin / Kacin 100mg/2ml + 8ml DW, [0.75 × wt] ml IV/IM stat and BD. [15-25mg/kg/day 8-12 hourly]

treatment.title: Discharge Treatment
treatment.description: Discharge Treatment for Neonatal sepsis -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- ► Inj. Ceftazidime / Serozid (ops) / Tazid (sq) / Zitum (aci), Or, Inj. Cefotaxime / Maxcef (sq) / Cefotax (ren) 250 mg, ½ ভায়াল মাংশপেশীতে (থাই) × দিনে ২ বার ..... ২ – ৫ দিন Or, Paediatric drop. Cefaclor 125mg/1.2ml / P/D Loracef (sq) / Navacef (nav) / Clorocef (ibn), ১০ – ১২ ফোটা করে দিনে ৩ বার ..... ১০ – ১৫ দিন উপদেশ : ১. ৬ মাস বয়স পর্যন্ত শিশুকে শুধুমাএ বুকের দুধ খাওয়াবেন। এরপর বুকের দুধের পাশাপাশি অন্যান্য স্বাভাবিক খাবার, যেমন : নরম খাবার, সুজি, খিচুরি ইত্যাদি খাওয়াবেন। ২. দেড় মাস (১.৫ মাস) বয়স থেকে ইপিআই (EPI) শিডিউল অনুযায়ী টিকা দেওয়া শুরু করবেন। টিকাদান কার্ড অনুযায়ী সময়মত সবগুলো টিকি দিবেন। ৩. বাচ্চাকে ঠান্ডা লাগাবেন না। মাথায় তেল ও চোখে কাজল দিবেন না। ৪. পরবর্তীতে কোনো সমস্যার জন্য শিশু বহি: বিভাগে দেখা করবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন। ▸ Oral antibiotic drop does not act to neonates less than 15 days of age. However, the above drugs may be prescribed on discharge. ▸ Actually neonatal sepsis need complete IV antibiotic & supportive treatment in the hospital, no drugs should be given. If IV antibiotic therapy is less than optimum duration according to indication (above) then we can suggest outdoor IM antibiotic injections. ▸ IV Ceftriaxone in baby below 2 months is not practiced, but specialist pediatricians can use IM Ceftriaxone to neonates.

treatment.title: Rabies Update 2025
treatment.description: Treatment : Category I : No vaccination. Category II : ARV only. Category III : ARV + RIG. ARV / Anti-Rabies Vaccination (Post exposure & Re exposure) : Doses - 3 if intra-dermal (I/D) & 4 if intra-muscular (I/M). Intradermal (ID) Schedule – Days: 0, 3, 7. Dose: 0.1 mL at 2 sites per day (usually in both deltoid regions). Intramuscular (IM) Schedule – Days: 0, 3, 7, 14. Dose: one full ampoule (1 mL) in deltoid or anterolateral thigh in small children. Nowadays ID is more preferred to IM. Rabies Immunoglobulin (RIG) : for Category III exposures (e.g., bites, scratches with bleeding, licking on broken skin): Given on Day 0 only (as soon as possible). May be given late if patient seeks treatment late, even after 1-2 weeks. Dose : Human RIG (HRIG) : 20 IU/kg or, Equine RIG (ERIG): 40 IU/kg, upto 3000 IU in total. Do not exceed recommended total dose. Infiltrate around the wound as much as anatomically feasible. Remaining dose (if any) given I/M at a distant site away & opposite from ARV. Re-exposure / new bite within 3 months : ৩ মাসের মধ‍্যে আবার Expose হলে কোন টিকা লাগবেনা। তবে ৩ মাসের পরে কিন্তু ৫ বছরের আগে হলে শুধু ২ ডোজ নিবে। (D0, D3). ৫ বছরের পরে treat as a new case (full course of ARV ± RIG). What if the prior vaccination status is uncertain or incomplete? -Treat as primary exposure (full course of ARV ± RIG). Δ Dog / Cat Bite (Category III) : ☼ Rinse & wash the wound with soap water thoroughly for 15 mins. ☼ Inj. ARV 2.5 IU/1mL / Rabix VC (incp) / Rabivax (pop), 1 amp I/M on left arm, on – D0 – 1/6/2025 D3 – 4/6/2025 D7 – 8/6/2025 D14 – 15/6/2025 ☼ Inj. RIG 1000 IU/5 mL / Rabix VC (incp) / Rabivax IG (pop), [wt/5] ml (max 15 ml) infiltrate around the wounds & remaining on right arm/buttock. ± ☼ Cap. Flucloxacillin / Flupen (drg) 500 mg, 1+1+1+1 for 7 days ± ☼ Tab. Naproxen + Esomeprazole / Solivo (hc) 500/20 mg, 1+0+1 for 5 days.

treatment.title: Physiological Vs Pathological Jaundice
treatment.description: 60% of term and 80% of preterm infants develop jaundice in 1 st wk of life. Among them about 5-10% require treatment. There are two main type of jaundice in neonates. They are – 1. Physiological jaundice & 2. Pathological jaundice. Physiological jaundice : Physiological process of HbF → HbA. Normally every child develop jaundice on second day and disappear on tenth day. Physiological one can be treat by a qualified GP but pathological one needs neonatologist or pediatric specialist. Physiological Jaundice Pathological Jaundice Appears on 2nd day of life. Starts on 1st day of life. Bilirubin level rises slowly. Level rarely goes above l5 mg/dl. A rise in serum bilirubin level over 0.5 mg/dl/hr or 10 mg/dl/day. Up to trunk & thigh. Jaundice extends up to palms & soles. Jaundice with yellow stool. Jaundice with pale stool. Baby remains otherwise healthy. Presence of signs of sepsis. (Reluctant to feed, convulsion, hypo/hyperthermia, chest infection, abdominal distension etc.) Jaundice clears spontane-ously within 7-10 days of life. Jaundice lasts longer than 14 days in term & 21 days in preterm. Normal stool colour. Jaundice with pale stool. [Ref – (Simplified) Essence of Pediatrics, Prof MR Khan and Prof Rahman, Chapter 2 (Neonatology)] Physiological jaundice : • Appear 2nd - 3rd day. • Bilirubin rise slowly not more than 15 mg/dl. • Jaundice disappear 7-10 days. Pathological jaundice : • Jaundice appear 1st day • Jaundice rise 0.5mg/dl/hrs or 10 mg/dl/day • Jaundice lasting more > 14 days

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discussion
treatment.description: Δ Baby of HBsAg (+) Mother ☼ Diet : EBF. ☼ O 2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Keep the baby warm. ☼ Apply 7.1 % Chlorhexidine / Hexicord (aci) / Germicord (sq) / Gentian Violet / Viola 1% to 2 % around umbilicus (A/U) stat. ☼ Inj. Vitamin K 1 2 mg / K-one MM (sq) / K MM (incp) / Babykion (che), 1 amp PO stat [on D1, D4 & D28]. Must on admission : ☼ Inj. Hepatitis B Immune Globulin (HBIG) [human] / Inj. Hepabig (viz) (100IU/0.5ml) [3000/-] / IMMUNOHBs (zas) (180U/1 ml) [5430/-] , 0.5 ml I/M stat on anterolateral aspect of one (left) thigh / arm. ☼ Inj. Hepatitis B Vaccine 0.5 ml / Hepavax (pop) (1ml) / Inj. Hepa-B (incp) (10 μ g/0.5ml) or, (20 μ g/1ml) / [0.5 ml–400/- & 1ml–500/-], 0.5 ml I/M stat on anterolateral aspect of another (right) thigh / arm. ► Antibody & antigen should be given simultaneously on two different sites. If given on same site the antibodies will be neutralized by the antigens. So should be given on different arms in adult & different thighs on infants. ► These Hepatitis B Immunoglobulin & Hepatitis B vaccine both should be given on the day of birth, at the same time but in separate sites. Infants should receive HBIG immediately after birth - preferably within 12 hours of birth and certainly within 48 hours. Its efficacy decreases markedly if given more than 48 hours after birth. But should be given by doctor, if found non-immunized, even after 2 days of life. ► Antibody [Hepatitis B Immune Globulin (HBIG) / Hepabig (100IU/0.5ml)] must be given within first 12 hours of life. But less accurately can be given within 5 days (preferably within 48 hours) to the neonates (0.5 ml IM in the anterolateral thigh). Inj. Hepabig 200IU/1 ml should be given to adult (medical stuff) within 7 days (preferably within 48 hours) after exposure/suspected needle prick. ► Antigens : Inj. Hepatitis B (rDNA) Vaccine / Engerix-B (gsk) / Hepa-B (incp) / Hepavax (pop) should be given at 0, 1 & 6 th month of age or on 0, 1, 2 & 12 th month. In adult (more than 20 years) 20 μ g of Engerix-B is given on – ¤ 1 st dose : on the day / 0 th day. ¤ 2 nd dose : after 1 month of 1 st dose. ¤ 3 rd dose : after 6 month of 1 st dose. ► Give the dose of monovalent hepatitis B vaccine preferably within 24 hours of birth, and definitely within 7 days. This regimen results in seroconversion rates of more than 90% in neonates, even with concurrent administration of HBIG. ► Do not delay vaccination beyond 7 days after birth, because vaccination alone is reasonably effective in preventing infection, if it is given early enough. Infants must receive 3 subsequent doses of a hepatitis B-containing vaccine along with EPI schedule. (Pentavalent 6 wks, 10 wks and 14 wks). A booster dose of hepatitis B vaccine should be given after 1 year.

treatment.title: Evaluation
treatment.description: ► Further HBsAg and Anti HBs determination is advised at 9 month of age. Test Interpretation HBsAg –ve Anti HBs > 10 U/L Baby is protected. HBsAg –ve Anti HBs –ve or, Not protected, consider vaccination accordingly. HBsAg +ve Anti HBs –ve Baby became carrier. Declare as non-responder to vaccine : If at 9 month anti HBs ↓ If anti HBs ↓ If anti HBs ↓ If anti HBs Consider the baby as genetically non-responder. • If report suggestive of active state –› Treat with Lamivudine / Tenofovir / Entecavir / Interferron. • If Dormant/immune-tolerant HBV –› only follow up

treatment.title: Infant of Diabetic Mother (IDM)
treatment.description: Δ IDM Adv. : ▸ RBS : At delivery, ½ th hour, 2, 4, 8, 12, 24, 36 and 48th hours of age. ☼ Diet : EBF. ☼ O2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Keep the baby warm. ☼ Apply 7.1 % Chlorhexidine / Hexicord (aci) / Germicord (sq) / Gentian Violet / Viola 1 to 2 % around umbilicus (A/U) stat. ☼ Inj. Vitamin K1 2 mg / K-one MM (sq) / K MM (incp) / Babykion (che), 1 amp PO stat [on D1, D4 & D28]. ☼ Inj. Ceftazidime (250 mg/2.5 ml) / Serozid (ops) / Tazid (sq) 250 mg, [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Amikacin / Kacin (aci) 100 mg / 2 ml, [0.15 × wt] ml I/V stat & BD. Must on admission : ☼ Inf. 10% DA 10 ml, IV @ 60 μ d/m stat. (micro drops/min) [2ml/kg @1ml/min] [May increase the dose up to 12 ml/kg I/V bolus to maintain blood glucose >50mg/dl or 2.8 mmol/L. If convulsion, start with 4ml/kg] ► Diet: Early start of EBF ½ hour after capillary blood glucose (CBG) measurement. If normal: Perform CBG 3-4 hourly 3-4 times. ► Check RBS At delivery, ½ , 2, 4, 8, 12, 24, 36 and 48th hours of age, by heel prick. ► Hypoglycaemia is the main danger. Baby of DM mother is prone to develop hypoglycemia due to hypertrophied islets of Langerhans. ► May add, Inj. Hydrocortisone (100 mg/2 ml) / Cotson, [wt/10] mL I/V stat & 12 hourly or 2 – 4 times daily. Or, may add : Inj. Glucagon 100 – 300 mg/kg, Inj. Diazoxide. ► Hormonal investigation : 1. Serum cortisol level. 2. Serum insulin level. 3. CT scan of pancreas. 4. Screening for inborn error of metabolism.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Rh incompatibility (For Mother) H/O – Rh negative mother & Rh positive baby. ► Supportive treatment & if symptomatic & hemolysis : specific treatment is exchange transfusion. Investigations to be given : - Blood grouping & Rh typing. - CBC c̄ PBF. - Total reticulocytes count. - Serum bilirubin. - Coomb’s test (Direct & indirect). ► If baby is Rh positive then mother should receive Anti-D within 48 hours. ¤ Inj. Human Anti-D Immunoglobulins (300 mcg/2 ml) / Inj. Rhophylac (csl) [4,720/-] / RhoGAM (zas) [4700/-], 1 PFS or 250 mcg should be given deep IM immediately or as soon as possible after delivery, or abortion of a Rh-positive child, preferably within 48 hours, but not later than 72 hours post-partum. Also following any potentially sensitizing episode (e.g. stillbirth, amniocentesis) up to 20 weeks of gestation 125 mcg per episode (after 20 weeks of gestation, 250 mcg) immediately or within 72 hours. Do not inject intravenously.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: TOXIC ERYTHEMA / URTICARIA NEONATORUM ● Erythematous rash with central pale papule appearing on the second or third day in term babies. The rash starts on the face & spreads to the trunk & extremities in about 24hours. Disappear spontaneously within a few days without any treatment. Advice: Don't use any mustard oil. ¤ Cream Fusidic acid 2% Cream + Hydrocortisone 1% / FusitopHC (sq)/ Facid HC (skf)/ Fusibac-H (drg) / Fusicort (ops) Cream, Apply locally 2 – 3 times daily for 7 – 30 days.

treatment.title: Features & Discussion
treatment.description: Thick meconium obstructs the ileum and manifests as intestinal obstruction. C/C : 1. Failure to pass meconium beyond 24 hours of birth. 2. Bile-stained vomiting. 3. Abdominal distension. D/D: Hirschsprung's disease, malrotation. Investigations : 1. Plain X-ray abdomen : a. Hazy or ground glass appearance in right lower quadrant. b. Small bubbles of gas trapped in meconium. 2. Sweat chloride/Na test : >60meq/l on 2 occasions. Management : N/G suction, NPO, TPN (total parental nutrition) followed by – Uncomplicated meconium ileus – o Gastrograffin enema (hypertonic). o Wash out inspissated meconium. o Repeat after 8-10 hours interval. o Adequate hydration mandatory. Complications: o Due to disease – perforation, peritonitis, adhesion. o Due to enema – dehydration, shock. Refer to pediatric surgery for laparotomy. o Laparotomy (if enema fails) – Mucus plug relieved by manipulation or direct enteral irrigation by N-actylcystein followed by enterotomy. o Complicated MI : Laparotomy followed by Bowel resection end to end anastomosis with distal ileotomy abdominal lavage (peritoneal).

treatment.title: Features & Discussion
treatment.description: Usually occurs in first 2 weeks of life. Triad of NEC : ● Abdomen distension. ● Feed intolerance. ● Blood in stool. D/D : Septicemia, intestinal perforation/obstruction. Staging (Bell’s staging) of NEC : Staging I (suspected) → abd. distension, poor feeding vomiting ± GI bleedings, → mild ileus and intestinal dilatation on x-ray Staging II (definite) → Stage I + absent bowel sound → pneumatosis intestinalis, ileus, portal vein gas Staging I I I (advanced) → Stage II + generalized peritonitis, shock → all above with definite ascites pneumoperitoneum. Investigation : X ray Abdomen — X-Ray should be done 6 hrly – AP/left lateral/cross table lateral. - Abnormal gas shadow. - Intestinal dilatation, ileus, fixed distended loop. - Pneumatosis intestinalis (pathognomonic). - Portal vein gas/ hepatic vein gas. - Pneumo-peritonium. Blood : Thrombocytopenia, persistent metabolic acidosis, refractory hyponatraemia. Stool : occult blood (+) ve. USG of W/A – gas in portal vein. Management : Medical: Immediate hospitalization, NPO and I/V infusion, Inj. antibiotics, monitoring of metabolic derangements/vital sign. Surgical: (if bowel perforation, full thickness necrosis, peritonitis) Resection of necrotic loop → Enterostomy → end to end anastomosis.

treatment.title: Features & Discussion
treatment.description: Widespread intravascular fibrin deposition; ischemia, necrosis, hemorrhagia and hemolysis. C/C : Baby may bleed per nose or mouth. Inv : ▪ ↑ PT, APTT. ▪ PBF : fragmented, burr, helmet shaped RBC. (broken RBC of different name). ▪ Increased FDP, ↑ D-dimers. Rx: ☼ Inj. Vit-K 2mg/0.2ml / K-one MM, 1 amp IV / PO stat. ☼ Antibiotic (Ceftazidime & Amikacin). ☼ Replacement therapy : (Blood transfusion) ➢ FFP (for decrease coagulation factor). ➢ Cryoprecipitate (for decrease fibrinogen). ➢ Platelet infusion (for decrease platelet). ➢ Correction of shock, hypoxia, acidosis.

treatment.title: Excessive crying
treatment.description: ● Caressing & EBF / sucking breast. ● Inj. Phenobarbitone / Barbit (1ml + 9 ml D/W), [wt/8] ml I/V stat & sos. (only if no respiratory depression & other organic cause). ● Hold phototherapy for 15 – 30 min.

treatment.title: Apnoea / Grasping respiration
treatment.description: ● Neck extended & O – P suction. ● O 2 inhalation. ● Stimulation (Rubbing over vertebral column, chest, palm & sole, CPR ). ● Inj. Aminophylline / Filin 125mg/5ml, (0.2 × wt ml + D/A D/W) I/V stat then, 0.3 × wt ml in each 100 ml fluid that baby gets (slowly).

treatment.title: Cardiac Arrest / HR < 60 b/m
treatment.description: 1. CPR : Put both thumbs over front of the chest & grab the side chest wall with rest 4 finger. Press 3 times then press the AMBU once (3:1) by another doctor / nurse. Stop if heart rate (HR) becomes more than 100. Please correlate with CPR training. 2. Extended neck O – P suction & O 2 inhalation. 3. Stimulation (Rubbing over vertebral column, chest, palm & sole). 4. Inj. Adrenaline (1ml + 9 ml D/W), then, [wt/10] ml I/V stat (slowly).

treatment.title: Abdominal Distension & belching
treatment.description: ● Semithicone pediatric drop / Flacol P/D, 5 – 10 drops × 8 hourly. Or, Domperidone pediatric drop / Domin P/D, 5 – 10 drops × 8 hourly. Or, 5. Supp. Glycerol / Glysup 1.15gm, 1 stick P/R stat & sos (consult with senior).

treatment.title: Amount of blood & PCV
treatment.description: Amount of blood – [20ml/kg] to be given within 4 hours from taking out of refrigerator. Each 20ml consists of 3ml Anti-coagulant + 17 ml blood. In case of heart failure : Packed cell [10ml/kg] to be given. Amount of Whole Blood Demand : = Weight in Kg × 6 × Hb Deficit. Here, Hb Deficit = Standard Hb% (12 gm/dL) – Recent Hb%. Example : A 10 kg thalassemic patient has Hb% 7 gm/dL. So, BT amount = Weight in Kg × 6 × Hb Deficit. = 10 × 6 × (12-7) = 10 × 6 × 5 = 300 ml. [Note, standard Hb% is 12 gm/dL though it varies according to age & sex.] Amount of PCV (Packed cell volume) Demand : = wt in Kg × 4 × Hb Deficit. Here, Hb Deficit = Standard Hb% (12 gm/dL) – Recent Hb%. Example : A 10 kg thalassemic patient has Hb% 7 gm/dL. So, PCV amount = Weight in Kg × 4 × Hb Deficit. = 10 × 4 × (12-7) = 10 × 4 × 5 = 200 ml. [PCV transfusion is the best option for thalassemia patient. But we use whole blood.]

treatment.title: BT order
treatment.description: BT order (10 September, 2024, 8:00 AM) In case of neonates & child BT must use diuretics for volume correction – ☼ Inj. Furosemide (20 mg / 2 ml), [wt/10] ml I/V on mid transfusion. Blood group : Regi No : Donor No. : With all aseptic precautions please transfuse ……. ml / this unit / bag of fresh human blood initially @ 5 d/m for 5 minutes. If no reaction occurs then transfuse @ 10 d/m. [In case of neonate: 4 drops/min as blood volume is small]. But in case of transfusion reaction please do the followings – ᴏ Stop blood transfusion (BT). ᴏ Inj. Hydrocortisone / Cotson (100 mg/2 ml), [wt/10] ml I/V stat. ᴏ Inj. Pheniramine / Avil (45.5 mg/2 ml), ¼ amp I/M stat. ᴏ Call the DOD / AR. Signature & Date Note : Dexamethasone (Oradexon) & Prochlorperazine (Vergon) may also be given. But hydrocortisone is best as it is short acting. Administration of Ca ++ gluconate : If hypocalcaemia, ☼ Inj. Calcium Gluconate 5ml, [2 × wt] ml + D/A D/W slowly I/V over 20 min c̄ cardiac monitoring. [ ► Dose must according to seniors (2.4 mEq/Kg/day). ► Before giving Calcium Gluconate, some D/W should be given bolus to see any extravasation or swelling of the cannula site. If any extravasation is present the extravasated Calcium Gluconate will burn the site. ► Rapid infusion causes bradycardia & arrhythmia, so auscultation of precordium is a must. ► BT of 3 unit / 100mL, needs 0.45mEq/Kg/100mL BT of Calcium gluconate.]

treatment.title: Baby can’t get mother’s milk
treatment.description: [See “Basic Pedi for all” portion in GP Diary] - Demonstrate appropriate attachment & positioning of baby during breast feed (senior stuff nurse / Mid-wife are trained in this). (Attachment & positioning for breast feeding is very important & mother starts to milking for sucking reflex.) - Assure that only a teaspoonful (TSF) colostrum will enough for baby in a day. Avoid formulated food for neonate. - Not to press off honey color milk / colostrum rather feed the baby that milk by turn from both breast. - Multivitamin (Tab. Momvit, 0+1+0 for 3 / 4 months) & Domperidone (Tab. Deflux, 2+2+2 for 1 months) can be advised.

treatment.title: Operative orders
treatment.description: Preoperative order (Date & Time) • Please take informed written consent. • Inf. H/S (1L) I/V @ 30d/min before sending patient to OT. • Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g 1vial I/V stat just before OT. • Inj. Omeprazole 40mg 1vial I/V stat just before OT. • Inj. Ondansetron 8mg/4m 1 amp I/V stat just before OT. Postoperative management : • Please keep the patient in left lateral position. • Maintain good hydration. • Frequent check of vital signs – pulse, BP, respiration, temperature etc. • Look for swallow reflex – If present indicates bleeding in tonsillar fossa. • Nothing by mouth for 4 hours – then soft cold diet. • Antibiotic – Inj. Ceftriaxone – 1-2 gm daily for 3 days then, Cap. Cefixime (200-400) mg BD for 7 days. • Analgesic and anti-ulcerant – Inj. Ketorolac / Diclofenac suppository followed by Tab. Ketorolac or Diclofenac orally for 5-7 days & Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg BD for 7 days. • Diluted hydrogen peroxide gurgling may be advised to keep the operation area clean. Δ Acute Tonsillitis Postoperative order (Date & Time) ¤ NPO for 6 hours then sips of water followed by soft, liquid diet (ice cream). ¤ Left lateral position. ¤ Inf. H/S 1L, I/V stat @ 20 d/m. ¤ Inf. Ceftriaxone / Oricef (hc) 1g, 1 vial I/V stat & 12 hourly ¤ Inf. Ketorolac 30 mg / Toradolin (rad), 1 amp I/M stat & 8 hourly ¤ Inf. Omeprazole 40 mg / Losectil (skf), 1 vial I/V stat ¤ Supp. Diclofenac / Voltalin (syn) 50 mg, 1 stick P/R SOS ¤ H₂O₂ mouth wash 2 TSF in ½ glass of water TDS 6 hourly. ¤ Please monitor the vital signs.

treatment.title: Discharge Notes
treatment.description: After 6 hours & Discharge of Tonsillitis (Operated) : Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g 1vial IV stat & 12 hourly for next 2-3 days, then – ► Cap. Cefixime / T-cef (drg) 200mg 1+0+1 for 14 days. ► Tab. Fexofenadine 120 mg / Fenadin (ren) 0+0+1 for 14 days. ► H₂O₂ (Hydrogen peroxide) Mouth Wash, Gurgle with 2 TSF in ½ glass of water 6 hourly for 14 days. Or, Povidine Iodine 1% / Viodin Mouth Wash, Gurgle with 2 – 3 TSF in ½ glass of water 6 hourly for 14 days. ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) – 5-10 days (if pain present). ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M)- If taking ketorolac. উপদেশ : ঝাল , শক্ত , কাঁটাযুক্ত , খাবার থেকে বিরত থাকুন ( ১৪ দিন ) । নরম খাবার খাবেন। পরবর্তীতে একই সমস্যার জন্য প্রেসকিপ্টশন সহ নাক - কান - গলা বহি : বিভাগে দেখা করবেন। Complication of tonsillectomy : • Hemorrhage : ▸ Reactionary hemorrhage - within 24 hours of surgery. ▸ Secondary hemorrhage - within 2-14 days of surgery, commonly within 5-7 days. • Aspiration of blood or saliva. • Injury to teeth, lips, gum, palate.

treatment.title: Haemorrhage after tonsillectomy
treatment.description: Management of haemorrhage after tonsillectomy : Hemorrhage from tonsillar fossa after 24 hours until 2 weeks following tonsillectomy is called secondary hemorrhage. Usually occurs between 3rd to 10th post operative day (POD). Reactionary haemorrhage : • Inspection of tonsillar fossa for any clot or bleeding point. • Remove the clot & apply pressure with small pack held in artery forceps. • Monitoring of the vital signs like-pulse, respiration, BP etc. • Gurgle with hydrogen peroxide. ↓ → If bleeding Stops : Patient is kept under watch, hypovolemia and blood loss are treated. ↓ → If bleeding persists : call for consultant for Ligation or Cauterization of bleeding vessel under GA. Secondary haemorrhage : This is due to infection of the tonsillar fossa. • Swab from tonsillar fossa for C/S. • Parenteral broad-spectrum antibiotics – Inj. Ceftriaxone 1-2 gm IV for 7 days. • Cold liquid diet. • General management for reactionary haemorrhage. If bleeding persists then patient is shifted to operation theater & inter pillar suturing may be required.

treatment.title: Treatment principle
treatment.description: Treatment principle in ENT diseases : ● Antibiotic ● Anti-histamine ● Nasal decongestant ● Anti-pyretic ● Gurgling agent ● Antibiotic : Alternative antibiotics (empirically for Gram +ve bacteria) : Co-amoxiclav or, Amoxicillin & Clavulanic Acid / Tyclav (bex) / Augment (skf) 665 mg (1+1+1--- 7 days). ● Anti-pyretic or, Pain killer c̄ anti-ulcerant. ● Anti-histamine : ○ Tab. Cetirizine 10 mg / Alatrol (sq) / Atrizin (bex) / Citin (ops) / Acitrin (aci), 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 5 to 10 days) Or, ○ Tab. Levocetirizine 5 mg / Alcet (hc), 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 5 to 10 days Or, Low price : ○ Tab. Chlorphenamine 4mg / Histal (ops) / Tab. Histacin (jay) / Sinamin (ibn), (1+1+1 or 1+1+1+1 ---- 5 days) Or, High price : ○ Tab. Bilastine 20 mg / Biltin (skf) / Blast (gen) / Bilastin (pop) / Bilamin (ibn) / Bilan (acm) / Billi (bex) 20 mg, 0+0+1 ---- 5 to 10 days ● Nasal decongestant : ○ N/D Oxymetazoline 0.05 % (adult, child > 6 yr) / 0.025% (child of 2-6yrs) / Afrin (aris) / Rynex (incp) / Oxynex (ops) / Xylocon (sq), 2 – 3 drops in each nostril twice daily for 3 – 5 days. Or, ○ N/D Xylometazoline 0.1% (adult & >6 yr) or 0.05% (½ - 6 years) / Antazol (sq) / Rhinozol (acm) / Xylomet (ops), 2 or 3 drops in each nostril two to three times daily for 3 – 7 days. ● Gurgling agent : Used for local irritation relief. ○ Listacare Blue Mint Mouthwash, twice daily for 10 days. Other home remedy gurgling agent : ○ আধা গ্লাস কুসুম গরম পানিতে ২ - ৩ চামচ লবণ বা আদা বা লবণ - আদা মিশিয়ে দিনে ৩ - ৪ বার গড়গড়া করবেন ------ ৭ দিন

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: ● Infection of the glottic and subglottic regions. ● Common acute upper respiratory obstruction. ● 3 months – 5 years, peak 2nd year. ● 15% have family history. ● By parainfluenza viruses (types 1, 2, and 3) – 75%, influenza A and B, adenovirus, RSV, measles, mycoplasma pneumonia. Features : Usually child with hoarseness of voice / muffled / hot potato voice with fever ± Respiratory distress. If croupy cough then dx is croup. ● Prodrome: rhinorrhea, pharyngitis, low-grade fever ± cough over 12-48 hours. ● Gradual development of barking cough, hoarseness, varying inspiratory stridor, ± fever (may be 102 – 104°F). ● May develop more severe obstruction with inspiratory stridor at rest, increased HR / RR, nasal flaring, retractions, progressive hypoxia and cyanosis. ● Symptoms may worsen at night/with crying. ● Mild course 3-7 days, more severe 7-14 days. ● The child who is hypoxic, cyanotic, pale, or obtunded needs immediate airway management. Diagnosis is mainly clinical diagnosis. Direct laryngoscopy / FOL : Epiglottis is thick, swollen, inflamed. Adv. : X-ray soft tissue neck lateral view or X-ray of the neck P/A view – steeple sign. Treatment : ● Mainstay of treatment – airway management. ● Home management – cool mist or night air, keep child calm. ● Nebulized racemic epinephrine – 0.25-0.5 mL of 2.25% racemic epinephrine + 3 mL normal saline every 20 min. ● L-epinephrine (5 mL of 1 : 1,000 solution) is equally effective as racemic epinephrine. ● Corticosteroids – dexamethasone 0.6 mg/kg, oral/IM – single dose or nebulized budesonide. ● Antibiotics – if secondary bacterial infection. ● Severe croup – intubation. Indications for Admission : ● Progressive stridor ● Severe stridor at rest. ● Respiratory distress ● Hypoxia (SPO2 ● Cyanosis ● Depressed mental status. ● Poor oral intake. ● Need for reliable observation. Criteria for discharge after racemic epinephrine – ● Observed for 3-4 hours. ● No stridor at rest. ● Normal air entry. ● Normal color. ● Normal level of consciousness. ● Have received a dose of dexamethasone. Spasmodic Croup ● 1-3 years of age. ● Clinically similar to acute laryngotracheobronchitis. ● No viral prodrome and fever. ● No family history. ► NG tube feeding & ICU support for more severe condition. Refer : Indicators of serious chronic respiratory disease : o Persistent fever. o Failure to thrive. o Digital clubbing. o Persistent dyspnea. o Chronic purulent sputum. o Limitation of activity.

treatment.title: Earache / Otalgia
treatment.description: Most common causes : 1. ASOM 2. Impacted Wax 3. Otomycosis 4. Furunculosis 5. Otitis Externa Referred Pain : 1. Tempro - mandibular Joint 2.Throat Pain

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C – ● Pain, exacerbated by moving the tragus or opening the jaw. ● Local swelling & ear blockade. ► Ear drop Gentamycin 0.3% / E/E drop Genacyn / Gentin, ২ - ৩ ফোটা আক্রান্ত কানে ( ডান / বাম ) দিনে ৩ - ৪ বার ----- ৭ দিন , নির্দেশনা - কাত হয়ে শুয়ে আক্রান্ত কানে ( ডান / বাম ) দিয়ে কানে আঙ্গুল দিয়ে হালকা চাপ দিয়ে ৫ মিনিট ধরে রাখবেন। ► Tab. Ibuprofen / Inflam / Profen 400 mg, ১ + ০ + ১ ---- ৫ দিন ( ব্যাথা বা জ্বর থাকলে ভরাপেটে খাবেন ) ► Tab. Pantoprazole 20 mg / Pantonix (incp) / Pantobex (bex), ১ + ০ + ১ ( খাওয়ার আধাঘন্টা পূর্বে ) ----- ৫ দিন ► Tab. Levocetrizine 5 mg / Alcet, ০ + ০ + ১ ---- ৭ দিন উপদেশ : ○ কানে পানি ঢুকাবেন না। গোসলের সময় সরিষার বা নারিকেলের তেলে ভিজানো তুলা দিয়ে আক্রান্ত কান বন্ধ রাখবেন। সাঁতার কাটা যাবে না। ○ হাঁচি কাশির সময় মুখ খোলা রাখবেন। ○ দশ দিনে মধ্যে অসুখের উন্নতি না পেলে প্রেশক্রিপশনসহ দেখা করবেন। Surgical toileting (incision & drainage) may need if non healing & recurrent.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: C/F : 1. Earache (common in 4 – 12 years). 2. Hearing impairment (decreased). 3. H/O Fever & cold, ± Headache. Acute otitis media / AOSM in pediatric patients : ● Peak during the 1st 2 year. ● 80% of children have at least 1 episode by 3 year of age. ● More in winter. ● High in children of day care, living with smokers and using pacifiers ( চুষনি ). Features : ● Young children-irritability, change in sleeping, eating habits. ● Crying incessantly. ● Holding or tugging at the affected ear. ● Fever ● Pain in the ear. ● Purulent discharge from ear – means rupture of tympanic membrane (TM). O/E : Tympanic membrane (TM) is dull, congested and retracted. Otoscopy : Moderate to severe bulging of the TM or new-onset otorrhea not caused by otitis externa, Mild bulging of the TM and recent ( ± INV : ● CBC: TC↑, N↑. ● Aural swab for gram stain and culture.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment : promptly before rupture of TM, to avoid CSOM which may impair hearing. Refer to ENT/pedi-specialists before any complications, like – CSOM, meningitis, mastoiditis. Antibiotic : Cephradine / amoxicillin / cefuroxime / azithromycin / clarithromycin. Amoxicillin or cefuroxime with clavulanic acid (wt/10) tsf BD – 7-10 days. ✔ Tab. Cefuroxime + Clavulanic Acid (500 mg+125 mg) / Axim CV (aris) / Cerox CV (aci) / Turboclav (bex) / Secoclav (gen) / Kefuclav (skf) / Famiclav (acm), 1 + 0 + 1 or, 1 + 1 + 1 ------ 10 – 14 days Or, ✔ Tab. Amoxicillin + Clavulanic Acid 625 mg or 1000 mg / Augment (skf) / Fimoxyl (syn) / Moxaclav (sq) / Tyclav (bex) / Avloclav (aci) / Clacido (hc), 1 + 1 + 1 ------ 10 – 14 days Or, ✔ In pediatric patients : Syp. Cefixime / Cef-3 (sq) / Denver (hc) / Orcef (ren) / Rofixim (rad), wt/20 TDF BD for 7 – 14 days. Anti-histamine : ✔ Tab. Bilastine 10 or, 20 mg / Bilan (acm) / Billi (bex) / Biltin (skf) / Blast (gen) / Zilas (hc), 0 + 0 + 1 ---------- 7 – 14 days Or, ✔ In pediatric patients : Syp. Fexofenadine / Fexofast (drg) / Fixal (ops) / Telfast (syn), wt/20 TSF BD for 7 – 14 days. Or, ✔ Antihistamine - 0.3mg/kg/day chlorpheniramine (Histacin) TDS or cetrizin for 7-10 days. Nasal decongestant : ✔ N/D Oxymetazoline 0.05% (adult) or 0.025% (child of 2-6yrs), Nasal Drop / Afrin (aris) / Rynex (incp) / Zoli (hc) / Xylocon (sq) / Oxynex (ops) 0.05% N/D, 2 or 3 drops in each nostril 2-3 times daily for 5–7 days. Or, ● Nasal decongestants 2 drops 3-4 times/day 7-10 days. Analgesic & anti-pyretic : ✔ Tab. Paracetamol 665 mg / Napa Extend (bex) / Renova XR (ops) / Xpa XR (aris), 1 + 1 + 1 ± 1 (after meal) ------- 5 – 10 days Or, Syp. P/C / Napa (bex)/ Ace (sq), wt/8 TSF TDS / QDS for 7 days. ● Paracetamol (60 mg/kg/day QID) if fever, pain. ± ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Ometid (ops) / Prazole (ren) / Inpro (bio) / OP-20, 1 + 0 + 1 (30 min before meal) --------- 7 – 14 days ± ✔ Tab. Clonazepam 0.5 mg / Leptic (acm) / Rivo (ori) / Epiclon (gen) / Lonapam (del) / Xetril (bex) 0.5 mg, 0+0+1 ---- 7 – 14 days Advice : 1. Avoid water entry in ear. Keep ear dry. 2. Avoid pricking in ear. 3. No swimming in the pond/pool. 4. Use oil-soaked cotton during bathing. 5. Keep mouth open during sneezing & coughing. 6. Counselling – reassurance of the parents. উপদেশ : ○ কানে পানি ঢুকাবেন না। গোসলের সময় সরিষার বা নারিকেলের তেলে ভিজানো তুলা দিয়ে আক্রান্ত কান বন্ধ রাখবেন বা ইয়ার মাফ ব্যবহার করবেন । ○ সাঁতার কাটা যাবে না। ○ হাঁচি কাশির সময় মুখ খোলা রাখবেন। ○ দশ দিনে মধ্যে অসুখের উন্নতি না পেলে প্রেশক্রিপশনসহ দেখা করবেন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.disease_name: Otitis Media with Effusion (OME) / Secretory otitis media / Glue ear
treatment.description: C/C : ● Mostly Children (2-11 yr). ● Fluctuating hearing loss. ● Poor school performance & non-attentive. ● Occasional Earache, ear blockade. ● DUH/Tympanic membrane ● Snoring/mouth breathing. Investigation : ● PTA, SRT (conductive hearing loss). ● X-ray Nasopharynx lateral view (L/V) – to see adenoid). Prognosis : 90% of cases spontaneous resolution. Treatment : ● Antibiotic : Syp. Amoxicillin/Cefuroxime± clavunate/Cefixime. ● Tab. Montelukast 4 or 5 mg OFT, 0+0+1 ---- 2 – 3 months. ● Nasal Spray Fluticasone 50 mcg/spray ---- 2 – 3 months. ● Chewing gum for 14 days. If not improved 2-3 months, refer to ENT specialist. Definitive surgery (if indicated) : Myringotomy and insertion of ventilation tube (grommets) ± adenoidectomy.

treatment.title: Types & Features
treatment.description: Types of CSOM : • Tubo tympanic (TT) type & • Attico antral (AA) type. Examination by otoscope : TT variety : Central perforation of tympanic membrane. AA variety : Perforation seen in attic region. E.g., Marginal or attic perforation of tympanic membrane, retracted pocket or cholesteatoma, granulation tissue may be seen. Diagnosis : Mainly by history and clinical exam : ● Aural discharge / Otorrhea. ● Deafness / Hearing impairment / Hearing loss. ● Feeling blockage in ear / aural fullness. ● Tinnitus / vertigo. ● Earache / Otalgia Difference between discharges : Tubo tympanic (TT) type Recurrent / Intermittent Profuse Thin/watery or purulent/mucoid No blood staining Non foul smelling Attico antral (AA) type Continuous Scanty Thick ± Blood stained Foul smelling Treatment guideline : • Topical antibiotics – (e.g. quinolones, aminoglycosides, polymyxins) are more effective. • Oral or parenteral antibiotics are useful in patients with systemic sepsis or inadequate response to topical antibiotics. • Tympanoplasty is an option in chronic perforation and hearing loss. • Mastoidectomy is often recommended for patients with chronic mastoiditis. Investigations : (as per need / GA fitness) • CBC • HBs Ag • S. Creatinine, • Urine R/M/E • Chest X-ray P/A view, • X ray Mastoid -Towne’s view • ECG- if patient is > 40 yrs • PTA (pure tone audiometry) • CT scan of tympano-mastoid region.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Management of CSOM : (refer to ENT specialist) Model Prescription : ✔ Tab. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg / Ciprocin (sq) / Neofloxin (bex) / Cipro-A (acm) / Ciprox (ops), 1 + 0 + 1 ---- 7 – 14 days ✔ Ophthalmic Solution Ciprofloxacin 0.3% + Dexamethasone 0.1% / E/D Beuflox-D (incp) / Bactin D (ibn) / Cipro-D (acm) / Aprodex (aris) / Ciprozid-DX (drg) / Neofloxin-D (bex) E/D, 4 drops instilled into the affected ear 2 times daily for 7 – 14 days ✔ Tab. Fexofenadine 120mg / Axodin (bex) / Dinafex (skf) / Fexo (sq) / Fexofast (drg) / Telfast (syn), 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 7 – 14 days ✔ Oxymetazoline 0.05% (adult) or 0.025% (child) Nasal Drop / Afrin (aris) / Rynex (incp) / Zoli (hc) / Xylocon (sq) / Oxynex (ops) 0.05% N/D, 2 or 3 drops in each nostril 2-3 times daily for 5–7 days ± ✔ Tab. Paracetamol 665 mg / Napa Extend (bex) / Renova XR (ops) / Xpa XR (aris), 1 + 1 + 1 ± 1 (after meal) ---- 5 – 10 days ± ✔ Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg / Ometid (ops) / Prazole (ren) / Inpro (bio) / OP-20, 1 + 0 + 1 (30 min before meal) ---- 7 – 14 days Advice : 1. Avoid water entry in ear. Keep ear dry. 2. Avoid pricking in ear. 3. No swimming in the pond/pool. 4. Use oil-soaked cotton during bathing. 5. Keep mouth open during sneezing & coughing. Anti-fungal or anti-bacterial ear drops combination : ✔ Ear Drop Ofloxacin + Clotrimazole + Beclomethasone + Lidocaine (0.3%+1%+0.025%+2%) / Candistin (uni) / Otic-4 (sq) / Audicare (aris) E/D, Instill 2-5 drops three to four times daily into the affected ear for 7-14 days.

treatment.title: Modified radical mastoidectomy (MRM)
treatment.description: Tubo tympanic (TT) type : Medical : Systemic antibiotics & decongestants in case of sinusitis. Local : • Aural toileting • Aural Antibiotic eardrops -ciprofloxacin with dexamethasone or gentamycin with hydrocortisone. • Decongested nasal drop – e.g., oxymetazoline. • Chemical cautery for small perforations. Surgical : • Myringoplasty. • Cortical mastoidectomysome cases with chronic mastoiditis. Attico Antral (AA) Type : Medical : Systemic antibiotics prior to surgery. Surgical : • Modified radical mastoidectomy / MRM (Usually). • Radical mastoidectomy (in case of extended cholesteatoma or intracranial complication) Postoperative care : • Bed rest, NPO for 4-6 hours then liquid/soft diet. • Analgesic and antibiotic. • Removal of stitches after 7 days. • Removal of aural pack after 14 days. Modified radical mastoidectomy Preoperative order (Date & Time) ► Please take informed written consent. ► Inf. H/S (1L), I/V @ 30d/min before sending patient to OT. ► Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g 1vial, I/V stat just before OT. ► Inj. Omeprazole 40mg, 1vial I/V stat just before OT. ► Inj. Ondansetron 8mg/4m, 1 amp I/V stat just before OT. Δ COSM (AA) OT note (Date & Time) N/O surgery : Indication: N/O surgeon: N/O assistant: N/O anaesthesia: N/O anesthesiologist : Postoperative order (Date & Time) ¤ NPO for 6 hours then sips of water followed by soft, liquid diet (ice cream). ¤ Left lateral position. ¤ Inf. H/S 1L, I/V stat @ 20 d/m. ¤ Inf. Ceftriaxone / Oricef (hc) 1 g, 1 vial I/V stat & 12 hourly ¤ Inj. Flucloxacillin / Flupen (drg) 500 mg, 1 vial IV stat and QDS ¤ Inf. Ketorolac 30 mg / Toradolin (rad), 1 amp I/M stat & 8 hourly ¤ Inf. Omeprazole 40 mg / Losectil (skf), 1 vial I/V stat ¤ Supp. Diclofenac / Voltalin (syn) 50 mg, 1 stick P/R SOS ¤ H₂O₂ mouth wash 2 TSF in ½ glass of water TDS 6 hourly. ¤ Please monitor the vital signs. After 6 hours & Discharge : Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g 1vial IV stat & 12 hourly for next 2-3 days then – ► Cap. Cefixime / T-cef (drg) 200mg 1+0+1 for 14 days. ► Cap. Flucloxacillin 500 mg, 1+1+1+1 for 7 – 14 days. ► Tab. Fexofenadine 120 mg / Fenadin (ren) 0+0+1 for 14 days. ► Tab. Ketorolac 10 mg, 1+1+1 (A/M) – 5-10 days (if pain present). ► Cap. Omeprazole 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M)- If taking ketorolac. ► Nasal drop Xylometazoline 0.05% (in children) / 0.1% (in adult), 2 – 3 drops in each nostril TDS for 14 days. ► After removal of pack on 7th POD : Ear drop Ciprofloxacin 0.3% +Dexamethasone 0.1% 3 drops TDS 7 – 14 days উপদেশ : নিয়মিত ঔষধ খাবেন। ৭ দিন পর প্যাক (pack) খুলতে আসবেন। কটন বাড দিয়ে কান খোচাবেন না। পানিতে ডুব দিয়ে গোসল করা যাবে না। গোসলের পুর্বে কানে তুলা দিয়ে রাখবেন। ১৪ দিন পর ফলোআপে আসবেন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Most common cause is slapping over ear/ any trauma. C/C : Aural dullness, tinnitus, Earache. Refer to ENT specialist, if large perforation or non-responsive to medical treatment. Treatment : ► Antibiotic : Tab. Amoxicillin + Clavulanic Acid (500 mg+125 mg) / Augment (skf) / Fimoxyl (syn) / Moxaclav (sq) / Tyclav (bex) / Avloclav (aci) 625 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 — 7 – 14 days ► Anti-histamine : Tab. Fexofenadine 120 mg / Fenadin (ren) / Fexo (sq) / Fexofast (drg) / Fixal (ops) 120 mg , 0 + 0 + 1 — 7 – 14 days ► Nasal decongestant : N/D Oxymetazoline 0.05 % (adult, child > 6 yr) / 0.025% (child of 2-6yrs) / Afrin (aris) / Rynex (incp) / Oxynex (ops) / Xylocon (sq), 3 drops in each nostril thrice daily for 7 days Advice : ○ Use oil soaked cotton or ear plug to prevent water entry into the ear. Must cautious during bathing. ○ Avoid swimming, flight journey in any type of ear drum perforation (COSM, traumatic, etc.). ○ Don't use any cotton bud or stick in ear. ○ Don't instil any liquid (oil, ear drop, water) in affected ear. ○ Keep mouth open during sneezing or coughing. ○ Follow-up within 7 – 14 days. Dos & Don'ts (for GP) : ○ No ear drop. No topical (drop) antibiotic or steroid. ○ Caution about medico-legal importance – physical assault. ○ Otoscopy to see TM is mandatory for treatment & followup.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : 1. Blockage Sensation in Ear. 2. Mild to Moderate Pain. 3. Hearing Impairment (Long Standing Wax). Treatment : Ear Wax softener : ✔ Ear drop Waxsol (uni) / Waxnil (sq), 3 drops 4 times daily ...... 7 days Or, ✔ Olive oil / Coconut oil, 3 drops 4 times daily ...... 7 days Then suction after 7 days. ✔ Antihistamine : Syp. Fexomin (ibn), wt/20 tsf BD for 7 days. ✔ Syp. P/C / Tamen (skf), wt/8 tsf TDS for 3 – 7 days.

treatment.title: Parotid Swelling
treatment.description: Swelling (unilateral) in front of pre or post auricular region. Inv. : ○ FNAC from the parotid swelling. ○ USG of the parotid region. Ref to ENT specialist & surgery.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Perichondritis is an infection of the skin and tissue surrounding the cartilage of the outer ear. Usually caused by injury to the ear as a result of ear piercing, sports, injury. C/C : Moderate to severe pain in pinna ± Fever. Prognosis is very poor. Cartilages are devoid of blood supply & responsive is not satisfactory. Even ENT specialists (in pvt chamber) refer for hospitalization. As frequently total external ear cartilage destruction occurs, results in pinna deformities. Model Prescription : ✔ Tab. Cefuroxime / Furocef (ren) 500 mg, 1+0+1 — 7 day. ✔ Tab. Naproxen / Naprosyn (rad) 500 mg, 1+0+1 (A/M) — 7 day. ✔ Cap. Esomeprazole / Sergel (hc) 20mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) — 7 day. ✔ Refer to ENT surgeon. ✔ Never try any incision & drainage if external ear cartilage is involved, even any simple dressing or aspiration should be avoided in GP setup.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Occurs after 1-2 year of ear piercing. Avoid excision (though may be tiny) in GP setup. Usually people (especially female) already having a keloid, are more prone to formation of new keloids (e.g., surgery / injury site). ✔ Inj. Triamcinolone (intralesional, usually 21 – 28 days interval until it disappears.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/F : Ringing, chirping, hissing, buzzing, clicking, roaring and maybe even shrieking sounds — or sometimes a combination of sounds at varying degrees of loudness. Causes of tinnitus : Presbyacusis, Otosclerosis, Meniere’s disease, Noise induced hearing loss, Acoustic neuroma, Impacted wax, Drugs (gentamycin, aspirin, quine). Treatment : ✔ Cap. Ginkgo Biloba 120 mg / Giloba (sq) / Gingoba (ibn) / Gobison (bronson) 120 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ---- 2 months ✔ Tab. Vitamin B1 100 mg + B6 200 mg + B12 200 mcg / Bost (gen) / Neurocare (bex) / TPC (acm) / Neucos-B (rad), 1+0+1 --- 2 months Alternate options : ± ✔ Tab. Nortriptyline / Moodnor (ibn) 25 mg, 0+0+2 – 15-30 days ± ✔ Tab. Paroxetine / Oxat (sq) 20 mg, 0+0+1 ----- 15 – 30 days

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Meniere's disease is an inner car disorder of unknown aetiology marked by episodic vertigo, fluctuating sensorineural hearing loss, aural fullness, and tinnitus. C/C : ● Fluctuations hearing loss. ● Tinnitus. ● Vertigo. Inv. : PTA – Sensory-neuronal hearing loss. Model Prescription : ► Tab. Betahistine 8 mg or 16 mg / Menaril (incp) / Veserc (uni) / Vectra (hc) 8 mg or 16 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 — 10 days. ► Tab. Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg or 50 mg / Acuren (incp) / HTZ (uni) 25 mg, 1 + 0 + 0 — 10 days. Advice : Avoid salt, excessive water intake. Refer to ENT Specialist.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Vertigo is the hallucination of movement. It is the cardinal symptom of lesions of the vestibular apparatus including it's central connections. Proper assessment of the patient for underlying cause of vertigo is a must. Features → Suspected Dx : ● Precipitated by movement of head in direction (rolling over or getting into or out of bed) → Benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV) ● Bed-bound by vertigo (starts on waking) + Middle aged + vomiting [no tinnitus or deafness] → Labyrinthitis ● Sudden severe persistent vertigo, drowsiness and neurological features like lower motor type 7th nerve palsy, hemipheresis / Cannot stand or walk → Cerebellar stroke / Brainstem stroke ● Vertigo associated with specific situation e.g. height → Phobic disorder ● Any tinnitus or hearing loss + Unilateral slowly progressive hearing loss, tinnitus → Acoustic neuroma ● Any tinnitus or hearing loss + Bilateral progressive hearing loss, tinnitus → Otosclerosis The commonest cause of vertigo in GP, is idiopathic and not due to serious pathology.

treatment.title: Treatment
treatment.description: Treatment : ► Explanation and reassurance : Self-limiting within few weeks. Teach the patient to minimize symptoms by sitting and lying-in stages. Habituation may occur by maintaining the trigger position until vertigo settles. ► Tab. Cinnarizine 20 mg + Dimenhydrinate 40 mg / Cinaron Plus (sq) / Revert (incp) / Cinarzin Plus (ibn) / Cinazin Plus (acm), 1 + 1 + 1 (after meals) ----- 10 days [to 1 month] ► Tab. Flunarizine / Flurizin (sq) / Minium (ops) / Fluzin (acm) / Fluver (aci) / Norium (skf) 10 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 ----- 10 days [to 1 month] Discussion : ● Instead of Cinnarizine 20 mg + Dimenhydrinate 40 mg, Cinnarizine 15 mg or Prochlorperazine 5mg (vergon) can be used. ¤ Tab. Cinnarizine 15 mg / Cinaron (sq) / Cinaryl (ops) / Cinarzin (ibn) / Cinazin (acm), 1+1+1 ---- 10 – 30 days Or, ¤ Prochlorperazine Maleate 5 mg / Vergon (ops) / Stemetil (syn) / Promtil (sq), 1 + 1 + 1 ---- 10 – 30 days ¤ Procyclidine may be used to prevent extrapyramidal effect incase of Prochlorperazine. Tab. Procyclidine 5 mg / Cyclid (incp) / Perkinil (sq), ½ + ½ + ½ ---- 10 – 30 days

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV) characterized by brief, sudden attack of vertigo associated with head movement. Common in women & aged (>50 yr). C/F : There is no abnormality on otoscopy or audiometry. The diagnosis is made on the history and by positional test or Hallpike test. Treatment : 1. Avoidance of provoking position. 2. Particulate removal manoeuvre / Epley manoeuvre / Repositioning exercise. 3. Labyrinthine sedative (prochlorperazine). 4. Posterior semicircular canal obliteration. 5. Singular neurectomy.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic features : Abrupt onset of vertigo and vomiting. Nystagmus may be present. Labyrinthitis & Vestibular Neuronitis : Labyrinthitis is an inflammatory or infectious condition that affects the labyrinth (the balance and hearing organs within the inner ear) and causes prolonged vertigo and hearing loss. Vestibular neuritis is a condition that affects the vestibular nerve, which connects the inner ear to the brain, causing prolonged vertigo usually in the absence of significant hearing loss. The onset of both conditions is typically abrupt over minutes to hours, and may involve vertigo, imbalance, jumping vision due to eye movements, nausea and vomiting. Treatment : ¤ Tab. Cinnarizine 15 mg / Cinaron (sq) / Cinaryl (ops) / Cinarzin (ibn) / Cinazin (acm), 2 + 2 + 2 ------ 21 – 30 days

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Epistaxis : Fresh blood / bleeding from interior of nasal cavity and nasopharynx. Active bleeding from the nose ↓ History of present illness : General Clinical Exam & Rhinoscopy ↓ Identification of bleeding site and nasal pinching about 15 mins ↓ Anterior nasal packing upto 36-72 hours ↓ If unsuccessful, Repacking of nose. Successful stop bleeding : Remove Nasal Pack after 48-72 hours ↓ If failed, next endoscopic cauterization. ↓ Unsuccessful & continue bleeding : ● Surgical Interventions: Ligation of maxillary artery and branches. ● Ligation of Ant. Ethmoidal artery. ● Angiographic arterial embolization of maxillary artery and branches. ↓ Investigations : Routine : CBC c̄ Blood film, RBS, BT, CT & Blood grouping. Special : APTT, PT, Factor assay, X-ray PNS, CT scan of nose & Nasopharynx. ↓ Correct coagulopathies (if applicable) : FFP, Vit-K, Cryoprecipitate, Platelets Check BP, anemia & manage accordingly, antibiotic. Control HTN (if any) : Anti-hypertensive. Rx on admission (Time & Date) ¤ Diet : Normal / Full. ¤ Pressure over the ala of the nose / Ice over the nasal bridge (If H/O trauma). ¤ Inj. Tranexamic Acid 500 mg / Traxyl (nuv), 2 amp I/V stat & sos. ¤ Xylometazoline 0.1% / Antazol (sq) 0.1% N/Drop, 3 -5 drop in each nostril 3 times daily. ±¤ Cap. Amoxicillin + Clavulanic Acid / Moxaclav (sq) 500 mg, 1+1+1. ±¤ Anti-hypertensive. In severe cases, adv. : Blood grouping, Rh typing, Screening, Cross matching & BT demand. Specific Rx : Local cautery / anterior nasal packing (ANS pack) may consider, in case of ongoing bleeding. Intermittent pressure over nose for 10 min each time in leaning forward ± Ice compression. If bleeding stopped : ANS pack for up to 48 hour. If bleeding stopped yet : find out the nasal pathology and send the patient for cauterization.

treatment.title: Operative (DNS)
treatment.description: DNS : Deviation of cartilage and/or bony framework of nasal septum from midline associated with nasal symptoms. Types of DNS : • Deviation - C Shaped, S Shaped • Anterior Dislocation • Spurs - Angulation at the bony-cartilaginous junction • Thickening of septum Diagnosis by clinical features : • Nasal obstruction • Headache, usually frontal • Recurrent Upper Respiratory Tract Infections • Epistaxis • Hyposmia / Anosmia • External deformity-may present • Septum deviated to right or left • Nasal cavity- narrow or roomy On Anterior Rhinoscopy : Shows the deviations and its type. Compensatory hypertrophy of inferior terminate is seen opposite to deviated side Investigations : (GA fitness) 1. CBC 2. Random blood sugar 3. BT, CT 4. Serum Creatinine 5. Chest X-ray P/A view 6. X-Ray PNS O/M view (to see associated sinusitis) 7. ECG – if age >40yrs Treatment : Minor degrees - no treatment. Surgical treatment includes - • Septoplasty ±SMD/Cautery of inferior turbinate under G/A (All ages) • SMR under G/A (>17 years) Post operative follow up : 1. Patient kept in left lateral position. 2. Monitoring of P/T/R & bleeding. 3. Nothing per oral for about 4 hours then soft cold diet. 4. Adequate analgesics & antibiotics should be given. 5. ANS pack is usually removed after 24 hours. 6. Nasal splint usually removed after 7 days. 7. Patient is advised for nasal cleansing by saline water. Post operative complications : • Bleeding • Septal hematoma • Septal abscess • Septal perforation • Synechiae / adhesions • Depression of nasal bridge • Retraction of columella • Saddle nose deformity

treatment.title: DNS c̄ HIT
treatment.description: Continues nasal obstruction (Unilateral, irrespective of cold). O/E : ○ Deviation of nasal septum (DNS). ○ Hypertrophied in turbinate (HIT). Tx, ✔ Tab. Fexofenadine / Fexo (sq) 120 mg, 0+0+1 ---- 14 days. ✔ Nasal Spray Mometasone 50 mcg/spray / Nasomet (bex) / Momeson (incp) / Metaspray (sq) / M-Son (drg) / Metamist (uni) nasal spray, 2 spays each nostril twice daily ---- 14 days. If non-responsive : Refer to ENT Specialist for Septoplasty with SMD.

treatment.title: Nasal Polyps
treatment.description: Nasal polyp : ● Nasal stuffiness leading to nasal obstruction. ● Partial and total loss of sense of smell. ● Headache, facial heaviness & pain. ● Sneezing and watery discharge. Antrochoanal polyp : Less common, seen in adolescents & children. Etiology is infection Single Unilateral Shape is tri-lobed (dumbbell) Grows backward Treatment is surgical Recurrence is uncommon Ethmoid polyp : Common nasal polyp & Adult Allergic Multiple Bilateral Grape like Forward Medical + Surgical Common Surgical Treatment : a. FESS (Functional Endoscopic Sinus surgery). b. Polypectomy under GA. Refer to ENT Specialist.

treatment.title: Rhinitis medicamentosa
treatment.description: Rhinitis medicamentosa (RM), also known as; rebound congestion is inflammation of the nasal mucosa caused by the overuse of topical nasal decongestants. When a patient come with nasal obstruction please ask about nasal drop use. Many long standing nasal drop use comes with rhinitis medicamentosa. ✔ Avoid xylometazoline & oxymetazoline nasal drop. ± Steroid nasal spray. Refer to ENT Specialist.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: C/C: - Frontal headache / facial pain. - Typically worse on movement / bending. - Facial pain & tenderness/pressure over the maxillary sinus. Headache particularly frontal headache. - Purulent nasal discharge, blockage & congestion. - ± Fever & ± Loss of smell. -May with H/O URTI or tooth infection. O/E: Temperature: Raised In nose: Congested nasal mucosa, in the middle meatus pus. In face: Tenderness over sinuses. Adv: • X-ray PNS (O/M view) • ± CBC X-ray para-nasal sinus occipito- mental view: may reveal mucosal thickening, air-fluid levels, and complete opacification of the involved sinus. CBC can be done. Maxillary sinusitis is more common. Diagnostic Symptoms : •Facial pain • Purulent & profuse nasal discharge • ± Headache • ± Fever

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : - Unilateral nasal strawberry like mass. - Recurrent epistaxis. - Sneezing, rhinorrhea, discharge. Treatment : Excision & histopathology. Rx, Ø Tab. Dapsone / Lepsone 100 mg, প্রতিদিন ১০০ মি . গ্রা . ১ টি ট্যাবলেট খাবেন ....... ৬ - ১২ মাস Ø Tab. Desloratadine 5 mg / Deslor, ০ + ০ + ১ ....... ১৪ দিন Ø Oxymetazoline 0.05% / Afrin 0.05% Nasal drop, ২ - ৩ ফোটা ডান ও বাম নাসারন্ধ্রে দিনে ৩ বার ....... ১৪ দিন Ø Antibiotics + Analgesics with PPIs for post-operative cases. উপদেশ : ঔষধের জন্য ইনফেকশাস ডিজিস (ID) হাসপাতালে বা কুষ্ঠ নিরাময় কেন্দ্র বা ( সরকারী ) লেপ্রসী ক্লিনিকে যোগাযোগ করবেন। পরবর্তীতে একই সমস্যার জন্য প্রেসক্রিপশন সহ নাক - কান - গলা বহি : বিভাগে দেখা করবেন।

treatment.title: Foreign body (FB) in Ear or Nose
treatment.disease_name: Foreign body (FB) in Ear or Nose
treatment.description: H/O of FB or foul smelling nasal discharge from single nostril, in children, suspect old FB in nose. Should be removed with FB hook with Proper illumination, ±Antibiotic, ± Nasal drop. Holding a child during FB removal : (any one method to restrict any movement of the child during procedure.) Expert attendant hold the child’s head & arms tightly. Or, Wrap the towel / bed sheet around the trunk with both hands within it. Foreign body (FB) Hook is an important (cheaper) ENT tool for GP. FB or objects within ear or nose can easily be removed by FB hook & Crocodile forceps. ▸ Living (moving) insect trapped within auditory canal. First instil 5 drops of olive oil or coconut oil to kill the moving insect. Then pull it back with the instrument. ▸ Small fish bone stuck within throat : Swallow cooked rice without chewing or vigorous cough may expel it out. But in case of large fish bone or coin within oesophagus, must refer to ENT surgeon. ▸ Coin, meat bolus, fishbone, dentures are common FB. ▸ GA given then post-operative order : - NPO for 6 hours. - IV fluid (according to weight). - IV antibiotic. - IV analgesic / Supp. - IV antiulcerant.

treatment.title: Advice after Tooth Extraction
treatment.description: Advice after Tooth Extraction : দাঁত তোলার পর করণীয় : * দাঁত তোলার পর তুলা বা গজ ৩০ মিনিট থেকে ১ ঘন্টা কামড় দিয়ে ধরে রাখুন। এ সময় গজ বা তুলার কামড়ের উপর চাপ থাকবে যাতে দ্রুত রক্তপড়া বন্ধ হয় । * যদি ঠান্ডার সমস্যা না থাকে তবে তুলা বা গজ ফেলে দেয়ার পর আইসক্রিম বা ঠান্ডা জাতীয় কিছু খান। * দাঁত তোলার পর ২৪ ঘন্টা পর্যন্ত কোন প্রকার গরম ও শক্ত জাতীয় খাবার খাবেন না। এ সময় ঠান্ডা বা স্বাভাবিক তাপমাত্রার নরম খাবার , যা আঠালো নয় , এমন খাবার খান। * দাঁত তোলার পর ২৪ ঘন্টা পর্যন্ত অযথা জোরে জোরে কুলকুচি এবং বারবার থুথু ফেলা থেকে বিরত থাকুন । * দাঁত তোলার ১ - ২ দিন পর থেকে পরবর্তী ৫ - ৭ দিন হালকা গরম পানিতে লবণ মিশিয়ে তা দিয়ে দিনে ৩ থেকে ৪ বার কুলকুচি করুন । * দাঁত তোলার পর ক্ষতস্থানে আঙ্গুল বা অন্য কিছু দিয়ে খুচাখুচি করবেন না । * দাঁত তোলার পর ১ - ২ দিন স্ট্র বা পাইপ দিয়ে কিছু না খাওয়াই ভালো। তাছাড়া যেসব জিনিষ চুষে খেতে হয় যেমন ললীস বা লজেন্স না খাওয়াই উচিৎ। আঠালো খাবার ও চকোলেটও বর্জনীয়। * দাঁত তোলার পর বেশি বেশি প্রোটিন ও ভিটামিন - সি সমৃদ্ধ খাবার ( যেমন মাছ , মাংস , দুধ , ডিম ইত্যাদি এবং লেবুজাতীয় ও টক ফল ) খাবেন। * দাঁত তোলার আগে অবশকারক ইঞ্জেকশন দেয়া হয়। এসময় আপনার ঠোঁট ও অবশ হয় কিছু সময়ের জন্য। এই অবস্থায় দাঁত দিয়ে ঠোঁট ও ভেতরের গাল কামড়িয়ে ক্ষতসৃষ্টি করা থেকে সাবধান থাকুন । * দাঁত তোলার পর সেলাই দেয়া হলে , ৭ দিন পর সেলাই কাটার জন্য আসবেন । * কোন ঔষধ দেয়া হলে ডাক্তারের পরামর্শ মত তা সেবন করবেন । * দাঁত তোলার পর দীর্ঘ সময় পর্যন্ত ক্ষতস্থান থেকে রক্তপড়া বন্ধ না হলে অথবা অন্য যেকোন সমস্যায় ডাক্তারের সাথে যোগাযোগ করুন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Fever under evaluation / Pyrexia of unknown origin (PUO) : persistent fever above 38.3°C (100°F) that evades diagnosis for at least 3 weeks, including 1 week of hospital evaluation & investigations. Adv. : As per suspect. ● Syrup Paracetamol 120mg/5ml, [wt/8] TSF 6 hourly [15mg/Kg 6 hourly] If unconscious or if temp ≥ 101 ° F : ● Suppository Paracetamol 60 / 125 / 250 / 500 mg, [wt × 15] stick P/R stat & SOS [15mg/Kg/dose]

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: C/C : ● Fever (high to moderate) for more than 1 week. Starts at low grade and then shows stepwise increase & continued, with chills and rigor, toxic look, prostration. ● Headache, anorexia, nausea. ● Abdominal pain, diarrhea. Advice : 1. CBC : Leucopenia. Thrombocytopenia is a marker of severe illness. 2. Blood culture (Gold standard) : within 1st week. 3. Urine/stool for C/S: In 2nd week 4. Widal test / Triple antigen : after 1st week. Little value in diagnosis of typhoid or enteric fever. 5. Urine R/M/E. 6. CXR P/A view. Δ Fever under evaluation (?) • Diet – Normal. • Inj. Ceftriaxone / Arixon (bex) / Oricef (hc) (500mg/5ml) or (1g/10ml) [100mg/kg/day], [wt] ml I/V stat & OD or, [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD (for 7 – 14 days) • Syrup. Paracetamol 120mg/5ml, [wt/8] TSF QDS. [15mg/Kg 6 hourly] • Supp. Paracetamol 60 / 125 / 250 / 500 mg, [15 × wt] mg stick P/R sos (if > 101°F) If the patient is in shock, obtundation, stupor or coma or Prolonged symptoms. : • Inj. Dexamethasone 5mg/1ml, (3 amp for each 5 kg) IV stat then, (1 amp for every 5 kg) IV 6 hrly for 2 days [Dose : 3 mg/kg IV stat then, 1 mg/kg 6 hourly for 48 hours] Vaccination : • Inj. Typhoid polysaccharide vaccine (25µg/0.5ml) / Vaxphoid (incp) / Typhim Vi (syn), 1 amp I/M stat [after 2 years of age] Booster : 2 yearly in high risk group. [Vaccines should be injected intramuscularly either in the deltoid or the vastus lateralis, but not into the gluteal areas.]

treatment.title: Drug list for Circumcision
treatment.description: 1. 5-0 plain Catgut (1) 2. Inj. Jasocain 2% (1) 3. Povisep/Viodin 10% solution (1) 4. Gloves 6/6.5"/7" (1+1) 5. Diclofenac Suppository (25mg) (1) 6. Mupirocin 2% Ointment (1) 7. Duoderm bandage (1) 8. Inj. Ultracaine (Bupivacaine) 5mg/ml (1) 9. Syringe 5cc/3cc (2+2)

treatment.title: Discharge Notes
treatment.description: Discharge (circumcision) : ► Syrup. Cefixime 100mg/5ml, 1 TSF for every 20kgs BD – 7 days [1-4 years: 1 TSF OD; 5-10years: 1 TSF BD] ► Syrup. Flucloxacillin 125mg/5ml, 1 TSF for every 5kgs QDS – 7 days [100mg/Kg/day 6 hourly] ► Syrup. Paracetamol 120mg/5ml, 2 TSF for every 8 hours QDS – If pain ► Tab./Cap. Rabeprazole 10 mg, 20kg: 1+0+1. ► Ointment Mupirocin 0.2%, Apply locally BD- 7 days

treatment.title: Juvenile Colonic Polyp
treatment.description: Juvenile Colonic Polyp : Usually baby of 2-10 years, Peak age 2-5 years. Painless per rectal bleeding, drop by drop at the end of defecation. Treat conservatively & refer to pediatric surgery unit.

treatment.title: Hemorrhoids (Piles)
treatment.description: Hemorrhoids (Piles) Per rectal fresh bleeding, Prolapse, incarceration, thrombosis, Peak onset: 12-18 years of age. Most patients recover by conservative (medical) management. Treat conservatively & refer to pediatric surgery unit. Indoor Tx, Δ Haemorrhoid ± Anal fissure (?) DRE : Findings or not done. ● Diet: Normal. ● Ointment Anustat (bex) / Anorel (pop) / Erian (sq) / Retocain (drg), Apply per rectally BD-for 2-4 weeks ● Syrup. Lactulose (3.35mg/5ml) / Lactu (bio) / Avolac (aris) / D-lac (drg) / Osmolax (sq), 1 TSF 5kgs BD [0.5 1mg/kg/day BD] ● Hip bath / Sitz bath : Take 1 bowl lukewarm water + 3 TSF 10% viodin (sq) or Povisep (jay) or Betadine (mundi) + 3 TSF table salt, then get the anus in water for 10-15 minutes 2 to 3 times daily for 2-4 weeks. DischargeTreatment : ▪ Avoidance of constipation – use stool softeners. ▪ Fruits, vegetable, fixed time daily bowel emptying. ▪ Anti inflammatory [Proctosedyl (san) / Hedensa /Anustat (bex) / Erian (sq)] – P/R application for 2-4 wks ▪ Sitz (hip) bath, 2/3 times daily for 1-2 wks. ▪ Failure of medical management may need surgery like excision (hemorrhoidectomy), rubber banding, stapling. (see general surgery portion for more).

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Acute appendicitis ● Umbilical pain shifting to Mcburney's point. [Mcburney's point : Junction of medial ⅔ to lateral ⅓ of a line from umbilicus to right anterior superior iliac spine.] ● Rebound tendernes on Macburney ꞌ s point. ● Rovsing, psoas, obturator signs are less useful. ● peaks at 1 2-18 yrs. ● Misdiagnosis is common. ● Perforation as high 80% in younger age group, occurs in 48 hrs. ● Complication : Burst, lump, perforation, peritonitis, abscess, ileus. Feature Score Fever > 38 ° C (100 ° F) 1 Anorexia 1 Nausea / vomiting 1 Cough / percussion / hopping tenderness 1 Right lower quadrant tenderness 1 Migration of pain 1 Leukocytosis >10,000 (10⁹/L) 1 Polymorphonuclear – Neutrophilia >7,500 (10⁹/L) 1 Score ► ≥ 8 : high possibility, ► 3 – 7 : further evaluation, ► ≤ 2 : low Possibility Total 10

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Acute abdomen C/C : • Lower abdominal pain (LAP) or Peri-umbilical for ___ hours. • H/O Constipation / Vomiting / Fever. O/E : Abdomen : • Soft / rigid, tender / non tender, • Bowel sound present / absent. • Mac Burney’s point tender ness (- / +). Δ Acute abdomen Adv. : ▸ USG of W/A. ▸ X-ray abdomen/ KUB (as per suspect) ▸ CBC (if fever) ☼ NPO TFO. ☼ Inf. Babysol Junior 1600 ml, I/V stat @ 16 d/m ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone / Ceftron (sq) / Oricef (hc)/ Arixon (bex) / Rofecin (rad) / Triject (skf) 1 g, [wt] ml I/V stat & OD. ☼ Inj. Metronidazole/Flagyl500 mg/100ml, [wt × 1.5] ml I/V stat & TDS. ☼ Inj. Nalbuphine 10 mg/1 ml / Nalbun-1 (incp) / Raltrox-1 (ops) / Newbain (sq), 1 ml (1 amp) I/M stat & sos. ☼ Syp. Famotidine 40 mg/5 ml / Famomax (bex) / Famotid (drg) / Famodin (acm) / Famogen (gen) / Famotack (sq) / Peptid (ops), ½ TSF BD (if needed). ▸ Pain other than abdominal pain paracetamol or diclofenac may use. Supp. Diclofenac or Voltalin (syn) 12.5 or 25 mg, 1 or ¾ stick P/R stat & sos.

treatment.title: Baby Come for any elective surgery
treatment.description: Come for Any Elective Surgery Δ Dx ☼ Diet : ● ● > 6 moths = Full. Investigation & drugs according to seniors. Signature (DOD) & Date

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Bronchiolitis Inflammation and obstruction of the small airway (bronchioles) of the lungs. The children of less than 2 years are affected commonly, mostly 2-6 months in epidemics. Diagnostic Clue : ● Age : ● হাসি / Playful ● কাশি / Cough ● বাঁশি / Ronchi / Wheeze ● ± Fever C / C c̄ Duration : - Cough and difficulty in breathing. - Low grade fever. - Runny nose for 7 days. - Poor feeding for 6 hours. O/E : ● Appearance : apparently healthy. ● Lung : Ronchi. ● Dyspnic / Cyanotic. ● Temperature (+ / ++ / +++ / -). ± Investigations : ● CBS with ESR. ● CXR PA view. O/E : Appearance : apparently healthy. Lung : Ronchi. Δ Bronchiolitis Adv. : ± CXR. ☼ Diet : EBF / Normal. ☼ Bed rest in propped up position. ☼ O 2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Nebulization c̄ Windel plus & Budicort (if cough) three times daily. Age Windel plus Budicort N/S 0.5 ml 0.5 ml 2 ml 1 – 4 yrs 1 ml 1 ml 1 ml > 4 yrs 2 – 3 ml 1 ml - [May repeat 2 times at 20 minute interval if no improvement upto 6 – 8 times daily.] ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone (1g/10 ml) or (500mg/5 ml), [wt] ml I/V stat & OD [50 – 100 mg / Kg / Day, use only if age more than 3 months] ☼ Nasal Drop Normal Saline 0.9% / Solo (sq) / Norsol (osl) / Nosomist (ops), 1-2 drops into each nostril 3 to 4 times daily. ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone 5mg/1ml / Dexa (ren), ○ ¼ amp BD ○ 5 – 10 Kg, ⅓ amp BD ○ 10 – 20 Kg, ½ amp BD ○ > 20 Kg, 1 amp BD ☼ Syrp. Paracetamol 120mg/5ml, [wt/8] TSF 6 hourly. [15mg/Kg 6 hrly] ☼ Supp. Paracetamol 120 or 250 or 500 mg, [15×wt] mg P/R sos, if temp > 101ºF. ☼ Tepid sponging. ► Antibiotics has no role in bronchiolitis. But conventionally antibiotics are used in wards. If age ☼ Inj. Cefazidime (1g/10 ml) or (500mg/5 ml), [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD. ☼ Inj. Amikacin (100 mg / 2 ml), [wt × 0.15] ml I/V stat & BD. If age > 3 months : ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone (1g/10 ml) or (500mg/5 ml), [wt] ml I/V stat & OD (once daily). Or, [wt/2] ml I/V stat & BD. If wheeze & cough : Nebulization is beneficial. Budesonide Nebuliser Suspension / Budicort 0.5 mg/2 ml ► Now a days salbutamol + Ipratropium is preferred over solo salbutamol. Budesonide is very effective in wheeze, cough & runny nose. Nebulization with racemic epinephrine is also beneficial. ► To overcome anatomic respiratory dead space, total amount of nebulization solution should be 3-5 mL, below 3 ml solution will not reach distant bronchioles. ► Salbutamol + Ipratropium can be used 3 to 6 times daily as per need. Budesonide can be mixed with morning & night doses of nebulization solution (Salbutamol + Ipratropium). So extra episode of nebulization can be avoided. Amount of budesonide should also be made 5 ml by adding N/S, if used alone. Usually a bit less in decimal of ml of wt in kg; e.g. 10 kg child needs 0.4-0.5 ml salbutamol respiratory solution in each time. ► Severe case Acute Bronchiolitis needs hospitalization & steroid. ☼ Inj. Dexamethasone 5 mg/1 ml / Roxadex (nuv), (wt/0.04) ml IV stat and BD [0.4mg/kg/day] ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone 1gm [No role, used conventionally], (wt/2) ml IV stat and BD, or, (wt) ml IV stat and OD [50-100mg/Kg/Day]

treatment.title: Discharge / Outdoor Treatment
treatment.description: D c̄A (Bronchiolitis) : TREATMENT : If cough – ► Pediatric Drop (P/D) Ambroxol 6 mg/ml / Lytex (ibn) / Boxol (ops) / Ambrox (sq), If P/D : (wt × 2) drops BD for 7 – 30 days or, If syrup : (wt/15) TSF BD ( 2yr) ± ► Tab. Montelukast 4 mg / Monas OFT / Aeron FT / M-Kast (drg) / Trilock OFT / Lumona FT / Montene (sq) / Monocast (bex), ০+০+১ (চুষে খাওয়া যাবে) ---- ৫ – ৪৫ দিন If fever – ► Pedi drop. Paracetamol (Acetaminophen) 80 mg/ml / Tamen (skf) / Napa (bex) / Ace (sq), [wt × 4] ফোটা দিনে (০৩ - ০৪) তিন থেকে চার বার ---- ৫ দিন [Syrup হলে : wt/8 চামচ দিনে (০৩) তিনবার ---- ৫ দিন] If breathing difficulty – ► Syp. Levosalbutamol 1 mg/5ml / Brodil Levo (aci) / Purisal (incp), [wt/12] চামচ দিনে (০৩) তিনবার ---- ৫ – ১৪ দিন ► Syp. Iron Polymaltose + Vitamin B Complex + Zinc / Bicozin I (sq)/ Aritone ZI (incp)/ Beconex ZI (ren), Infant : [wt/3] ml once daily for 1 – 3 months Child : 5 ml (1 TSF) once daily for 1 – 3 months If vomiting – ► Syp. Ondansetron 4 mg/5 ml / Anset (ops) / Emistat (hc) / Zofra (skf), [wt/12] মিলি বা ⅓ বা ½ চামচ দিনে (০৩) তিনবার ---- ৩ দিন অথবা, ৪ বৎসরের বেশি হলে, ► Tab. Zofra ODT 4 mg ---- বমি / বমিভাব হলে খাওয়াবেন। If runny nose – ► Syrup. Diphenhydramine 10mg / 5ml / Pedeamin (bex), [wt/ 8] TSF for TDS / QDS --- 7 days. Practical GP note : Standard protocols never use LTRA (montelukast) as a primary drugs in Bronchiolitis or BA, rather bronchodilator inhaler or nebulization or oral is the primary treatment. Salbutamol ↑↑ HR & older child commonly complaints of feeling unwell (palpitation). There is no role of multi-vitamins & multi-minerals in bronchiolitis & asthma, but in GP prevention of recurrence/attack can be observed. But first visit GP may prefer montelukast 4 mg chewable / flash tablet with iron containing multi-vitamins.

treatment.title: Diagnosis & Classification
treatment.description: Bronchial Asthma [Please see Pulmonology (Medicine) portion] Asthma is a reactive airway disease & commonest childhood medical problem at private practice, 12% in USA, 7% in Bangladesh. Majority of the asthma in children remit spontaneously by 2-20 yrs of age, some percentage can continue as adult asthma with normal longevity in well controlled subject. Usual presentation : Following symptoms must be reversible with bronchodilator : ● Recurrent cough. ● Cough at night ● Tightness of chest. ● Respiratory distress. ● Wheeze / Whistling sound at chest Others – ● Fast breathing and secondary infection common : Wheeze (ronchi) and prolonged expiration, coarse crackles. ● Daytime symptoms: less than once per week. ● Night symptoms: less than or equal to twice per month. ● Family history of atopy.
