O/E : Appearance: apparently healthy, Lung : Ronchi. Investigations : CXR P/A view. In clinical practice in pedi age group blood test should be avoided if no concrete suspect. Non invasive investigation like CXR, Stool R/M/E with reducing substance can be done. Diagnosis is mainly clinical. Asthma is diagnosed after 2 years of age. Differentiation between wheezy child and asthma is not required. In fact, second attack of bronchiolitis is 1st attack of asthma. Presentation and treatment remain same. Well controlled asthma has no complication. Following guideline, using meter dose inhaler / nebulized bronchodilator are corner stone of management. In countries like Bangladesh, antibiotic is required in most of the cases. ➢ Chest X-ray : Useful in child with dyspnea to exclude pneumothorax, pneumomediastinum, foreign body aspiration, pneumonia ,TB, others. ➢ Other investigation depending on suspicion of differential diagnosis clinically and radiologically, eg. CBC, MT, Gene X pert, HRCT, total circulating eosinophil count. Avoid asthma precipitants : ● Respiratory viruses. ● Indoor : dust, mite, pet, insect, cockroach, cooking fumes, less ventilation. ● Outdoor : temperature fluctuation >2º C, dust, pollen, gases, smoke. ● Tobacco, perfume, hairspray. ● Exercise, emotion. ● Drugs (NSAID, Aspirin, ẞ blocker). ● Risk factors : Atopy, pneumonia, male gender, LBW, poor, black, polluted environment, obesity. Classification of asthma severity : Traits Intermittent Asthma Persistent Asthma Mild Moderate Severe Day time symptom ≤ 2 days/wk >2 days/wk Daily Throughout day SABA use ≤ 2 days/wk >2 days/wk Daily Normal activity limitation None Minor Some Extreme FEV ₁ % predicted (>5 yrs) Normal ≥ 80% 60 – 80% Clinically frequent, severe acute attack means severe disease and less frequent, mild attack means less severe disease.

treatment.title: Principle of treatment
treatment.description: Relievers(bronchodilator) : ► Salbutamol dose: 0.03 ml/kg/dose (max 1 ml) dilute with 1 ml normal saline 3-6 hourly (3-8 times/day). ► >5 yrs: MDI/DPI of Salbutamol ± Ipratropium : In both MDI/DPI forms action starts after 5 min, lasts for 4-6 hours, fewer side effects, right amount of medicine enters into lungs, does not enter to rest of the body and achieving therapeutic level is not needed. It can be repeated in acute asthma, 5 inhalations at a time, if necessary after 5 min, total up to 5 (rule of 5). ▪ Ipratropium acts as a bronchodilator by blocking muscarinic receptor, hence reduce bronchial smooth muscle tone and secretion. Salbutamol and other molecules are β 2 agonists. ▪ Salbutamol dose : 1-2 inhalation (100 μ g/inhalation) 3-6 hourly (4-6 times/day) till no cough. ▪ Ipratropium dose : 1-2 inhalation (20 μ g/inhalation) 2-3 times/day, till no cough. ▪ In emergency 2.5-5 mg salbutamol mixed with 2 ml normal saline can be repeated at an interval of 20 min (3 doses in - 1st hour); then 1-4 hours interval as per need, sometimes continuous nebulization as 0.5 mg/kg/hr, max 15 mg/hr). Oral medication takes 30 min to start acting, less efficacious, has more toxicity. Now a days not in practice in developed world. Preventers (anti-inflammatory) : ➢ Inhaled corticosteroid (ICS) : ICS dose depends upon disease severity and frequency, e.g. Fluticasone 50 μ g to 250 μ g. Usual dose is 1-2 inhalation b.i.d. ➢ Leukotriene antagonists (montelukast) : Prevention by inhaled corticosteroid (ICS) and montelukast ( ⅟₂ - 5 yrs : 4 mg tablet, 5-15 yrs: 5 mg tab, >15 yrs : 10 mg tab) are useful. Corticosteroid can be given in nebulized form (budesonide) as preventer in ➢ Cromones (Sodium cromoglycate). ➢ Concomitant allergic rhinitis may be treated with intranasal steroid spray (fluticasone) 1 – 2 puff 2 – 3 times daily till symptom free. Rinse the oral cavity after steroid inhaler use is advised. ➢ Long term use of preventer usually beneficial in terms of less attack, less severity, less school failure and lower rate of antibiotic use. Protectors (symptom controllers) : are long acting bronchodilator with weak anti-inflammatory properties which prevent recurrent attacks, e.g. salmeterol (25 µg), femoterol, bambuterol, fixed dose e.g. 25/50 µg – BD. Rescuer / Rescue Therapy : o Rescue steroid for 2-5 days is useful, in moderate to severe case. o Prednisolone 1-2 mg/kg PO for 2-5 days o In acute severe asthma: Parenteral hydrocortisone (Inj. Cotson) 4-6 mg/kg/dose, 4 times daily, 2-3 days followed by oral prednisolone. ❖ Antibiotic is used in productive cough, red throat, continuous high fever >3 days, lung crackles, toxic child. Severe cases (fast breathing, subcostal recession) require parenteral antibiotic. Table : Step care management – Step-1 SABA ↓ Step -2 SABA + LDICS ↓ Step- 3 SABA + LDICS ± LABA/LTRA ↓ Step-4 SABA + MDICS + LABA ↓ Step-5 SABA + HDICS + LABA ↓ Step-6 SABA + HDICS + LABA/LRTA + OCS Abbreviation : ● SABA = Short-acting β 2 Agonists (Salbutamol). ● ICS = Inhaled corticosteroids (Fluticasone) : o LDICS = Low dose ICS o MDICS = Moderate dose ICS o HDICS = High dose ICS ● LABA = Long-acting β 2 Agonists (Salmeterol). ● LTRA = Leukotriene Receptor Antagonist (Montelukast). ● OCS = Oral corticosteroid (Prednisolone). *If preventer drugs keep asthma in control for 3 months, then step down. Again step up if severity / frequency is increased. Doses of Inhaled corticosteroids (ICS) & LABA : LD-ICS >5 yrs: Salmeterol + Fluticasone (25/50) : 50 μ g/puff, 1 – 2 puff BD. Nebulized Budesonide : 0.25-0.5 mg/day. MD-ICS >5 yrs: Salmeterol + Fluticasone (25/125) : 125 µg/puff, 1 – 2 puff BD. Nebulized Budesonide : 0.5-1 mg/day. HD-ICS >5 yrs: Salmeterol + Fluticasone (25/250) : 250µg/puff : 1 – 2 puff BD. Nebulized Budesonide: 1-2 mg/day. * Oral corticosteroid (OCS) : Prednisolone 1-2 mg/kg/d- for 2-5 days as rescue therapy /relief symptom, even in earlier step without any harm, practiced globally. * Fluticasone (0.5mg/2ml) respiratory solution for nebulization can also be as preventer in * Cromolyn Sodium inhaler is a weak alternative to ICS, less used now a days. *Omalizumab S/C 2-4 weeks for patient >12 yrs with allergic asthma as immunotherapy in step 2-6. It is human monoclonal antibody binds with IgE. Benefit, high cost, risks are limiting factors to use it routinely. *MDI/DPI can be difficult to inhale in full done by children. Hence space device, respochamber, acuhaler, uptake go spacer can ensure full dose desired medication into lung.

treatment.title: Intermittent Asthma
treatment.description: ✔ Tab. Montelukast 4 mg / Monas OFT / Aeron 4 mg FT / M-Kast (drg) / Trilock OFT / Lumona FT / Montene (sq) / Monocast (bex) , 2-5 years: 4mg tab at night, 5-15 years: 5mg tab at night, >15 years: 10mg tab at night.] + ✔ Syrup. Ambroxol 15mg/5ml (if cough) / Ambrox (sq) / X-cold (acm) / Ambroxol (bio) / Ambolyt (incp) / Boxol (ops), 5 years: 1 TSF TDS + ✔ Inhaler Salbutamol HFA 100 mcg / Azmasol (bex) / Sultolin (sq) / Brodil (aci) / Salburen (ren) / PulmoCare (hc) / Salmolin (acm) / Ventil (drg), 1 puff 2-3 times daily with spacer for 1 – 3 months.

treatment.title: Persistent Asthma
treatment.description: ✔ Tab. Montelukast 4 mg / Monas OFT / Aeron 4 mg FT / M-Kast (drg) / Trilock OFT / Lumona FT / Montene (sq) / Monocast (bex), [2-5 years: 4mg tab at night, 5-15 years: 5mg tab at night, >15 years: 10mg tab at night.] + Bronchodilator : ✔ Inhaler. Salbutamol 100 mcg mcg / Azmasol (bex) / Sultolin (sq) / Brodil (aci) / Salburen (ren) / PulmoCare (hc) / Salmolin (acm) / Ventil (drg), 4-14 years : 2 puffs 2 – 3 times in case of breathlessness / cough / wheeze & as per need. First use salbutamol then 5 – 30 minutes later use preventor & protector inhaler 2-4 times. + Preventor & Protector : ✔ Inhaler Budesonide+ Formoterol (80 mcg+4.5 mcg)/puff / Inh. Renafor-B (ren) / Symbion (bex) / Budison F (aris) ○ 2-5 years : 1 puff BD with spacer – continue ○ 5-10 years : 1-2 puffs BD with spacer – continue ○ 10-14 years : 2 puffs BD with spacer – continue (Gurgle after steroid containing steroid use) Or, ✔ Inhaler Salmeterol + Fluticasone (25/125) μ g / Bexitrol F (bex) / Ticamet (sq) / Saltica (ren) / Aroflo (aris) / Salflu (acm), 1 – 2 puff BD for 2 – 3 months Strength as per severity (Fluticasone Propionate) : Low dose (LDICS) → Moderate dose → High dose (HDICS) (25+50 mcg)/puff (25+125 mcg)/puff (25+250 mcg)/puff Practical GP note : Standard protocols never use LTRA (montelukast) as a primary drugs in BA, rather SABA inhaler or nebulization is the primary treatment. But there is social stigma against inhaler that once an inhaler taken, then must be taken for life long. So, in first visit GP may prefer montelukast 4 mg chewable / flash tablet with iron containing multi-vitamins. There is no role of multi-vitamins & multi-minerals in bronchiolitis & asthma, but in GP prevention of recurrence/attack can be observed. শ্বাস কষ্টের রোগীর জন্য উপদেশ : ১। আইসক্রীম, ফ্রিজের ঠান্ডা পানি, কোল্ড ড্রিংক, ফ্রিজে। রাখা খাবার খাওয়ানো নিষেধ। ২। ঠান্ডা পানিতে গোসল, ঠান্ডা পানি হাতানো, ঠান্ডা জায়গায় বা ধুলা-বালিতে খেলা-ধূলা নিষেধ। ৩। শরীরে কোন অবস্থাতেই ঘাম শুকাতে দিবেন না। ঘাম তোয়ালে দিয়ে সাথে সাথে মুছে ফেলবেন। ৪। ডাইরেক্ট ফ্যান বা ফ্যানের পাশে শোয়াবেন না। ৫। ঘরে কার্পেট, পাপোষ, মশার কয়েল এবং স্প্রে ব্যবহার নিষেধ। ৬। হালকা গরম পানি খাবে। হালকা উষ্ণ পানিতে গোসল ও হাত-মুখ ধোবে। ৭। বাচ্চার সামনে ঘর - উঠান ঝাড়ু এবং ধুমপান নিষেধ। ৮। গরুর মাংস, হাঁসের ডিম, ইলিশ মাছ, চিংড়ি মাছ, বেগুন, কচু বা কচুর লতা খেলে শ্বাস কষ্ট বেশী হয় কিনা লক্ষ্য করুন। ৯। বাড়িতে কুকুর, বিড়াল রাখা নিষেধ। ১০। সকালে ও বিকালে বেলুন ফোলাবে অথবা মাউথ অরগান বাঁশি বাজাবে। ১১। কখন কি করলে বা কি খেলে বা কি অবস্থায় কাশি, শ্বাসকষ্ট বেশী হয় লক্ষ্য রাখুন এবং এড়িয়ে চলুন। Please see pulmonology (medicine) portion for more suggestion & advise in bangla.

treatment.title: Acute asthma & Status asthmaticus
treatment.description: Acute severe asthma : ● Advice hospitalization. ● O 2 inhalation – 40% (2-5 L/min). ● IV Hydrocortisone (4-5 mg/kg/dose) 6 hourly (if can’t swallow) OR, ● IV Methyl prednisolone (30 mg/kg/dose) BD for 1-2 days, followed by oral prednisolone 1-2 mg/kg for 3-7 days. ● Nebulized Salbutamol + Ipratropium – 6-8 times daily until no cough. ● IV antibiotic 5-7 days, followed by P.O – if severity reduces ● ± SC / IM Adrenaline – not much used now a days. Status asthmaticus Hospitalization in PICU + Intubation and mechanical ventilation + above measures ± Immunomodulatory agents. Asthma control : Can be checked by spirometry periodically/annually. o PEFR- 80-100% of personal best: Good control. o PEFR- 50-80% of personal best: Suboptimal control (yellow zone) o PEFR- o Requiring 1 MDI (200 inhalation/MDI) per month or, requiring 3 MDI per year : Inadequate control. Vaccination : annual Influenza, 3-5 yearly pneumococcal, measles can prevent to some extent.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discharge
treatment.description: C / C c̄ Duration : - Respiratory distress. - Runny nose. - Cough. - H/O same type of attack previously, eczema. - Positive (+ve) family history. O / E : - Lung : Rhonchi. - Respiratory rate (R/R) : ____ breaths / min (b/m). - Heart rate (H/R) : ____ beats / min (b/m). - Dyspnic / Cyanotic. - Dehydration (+ / -). - Temperature (+ / ++ / +++ / -). Δ Acute BA Adv : ▸ CXR PA view. ☼ Diet : EBF / Full. ☼ Bed rest in propped up position. ☼ O 2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Bed rest in propped up position. ☼ Nebulization c̄ Windel plus & Budicort (if cough) three times daily. Age Windel plus Budicort N/S 0.5 ml 0.5 ml 2 ml 1 – 4 yrs 1 ml 1 ml 1 ml > 4 yrs 2 – 3 ml 1 ml - ☼ Inj. Hydrocortisone 2ml / 100 mg, [wt/10] ml I/V stat & TDS / QDS. ± ☼ Inj. Omeprazole 40 mg +10ml DW, [4 × wt] ml I/V stat and BD. [Used if ≥ 7 years] [1mg/kg/day BD] ± ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone (1g/10ml) or (500mg/5ml), [wt] ml I/V stat & once daily. ☼ Tab. Montelukast 4 mg / Monas 4 mg OFT, 0 + 0 + 1. ► If not improved : Add, ☼ Inj. Adrenaline 0.01 mg/kg and repeat after ½ hour if not improved yet [Max 0.5 mg] ☼ Inj. MgSO 4 4g/100ml / Nalepsin (bex), [wt] ml IV (25 – 75 mg/kg) very slowly over 20 min stat. ► In refractory cases : Mechanical ventilation and PICU may be necessary. ► Antibiotics should not be used routinely. Indications of antibiotic : 1. Productive cough. 2. Continuous high fever >3 days. 3. Toxic child. 4. Lung crackles. 5. Red throat. Dc̄A (BA) -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- ☼ Syrup. Levosulbutamol 1mg/5ml [0.1mg/kg/dose TDS], [wt/12] TSF TDS for 5 – 30 days. ☼ Inhaler Salmeterol + Fluticasone (25 mcg+125 mcg)/puff, 1 – 2 puff BD with spacer for 60 days. ☼ Tab. Montelucast OFT / FT 4 mg, 0 + 0 + 1 for 15 – 60 days. বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন।

treatment.title: Classification, Diagnosis & Treatment
treatment.description: Diagnostic Clue : NO PNEUMONIA : ● Cough or cold ± Fever PNEUMONIA : ● Cough or cold ● Fever ● Chest indrawing ● Fast breathing SEVERE PNEUMONIA or, VERY SEVERE DISEASE : ● Any general danger sign or, ● Stridor in calm child On examination : 1. Patient is febrile 2. Respiratory rate increase (tachypnea is first sign of pneumonia). a) 0 – 2 months ≥ 60 breaths/minute b) 2 – 12 months ≥ 50 breaths/minute c) 1 – 5 year ≥ 40 breaths/minute d) 6 – 12 year ≥ 30 breaths/minute e) 13 year or older ≥ 20 breaths/minute SEVERE PNEUMONIA : • Any general danger sign (below), or, • Stridor in a calm child, or, • Oxygen saturation SPO Antibiotics used in pneumonia : Neonate and age Ampicillin and gentamicin 7-10 days or, 3rd generation of cephalosporin (cefixime) and Amikacin- 7-14 days Age 2 month – 5 years Inj Ampicillin 6 hourly 50 mg /kg/dose plus Inj Gentamicin 7.5 mg /kg/day-7 days If no response within 48 hours, change Ampicillin to Cloxacillin or Inj ceftriaxone (80-100 mg) IM/IV for7-10 days Age > 5 years : Duration of Rx : 10-14 days First line : 3rd generation Cephalosporin (e.g., Ceftriaxone 80 mg /kg day) plus Gentamicin (5 mg /kg day) Second line : 3rd generation Cephalosporin (ceftriaxone) plus Gentamicin (5 mg /kg day) plus Vancomycin 40 mg/kg/day Treatment : ► Syrup. Cefixime 100mg/5ml / Cefim-3 (aci) / Denver (hc), 1 TSF 12 hourly for each 20 kg for 5 – 7 days ► Syrup. Paracetamol 120mg/5ml (if fever) / Fast (acm), 1 TSF for each 8kg TDS or QDS for 3 – 7 days ► Syrup. Levosalbutamol 1mg/5ml (if breathing difficulty) / Levostar (sq) / Purisal (incp) / Brodil Levo (aci), 1 TSF TDS for each 10kg for 5 – 7 days ► Syrup. Ambroxol 15mg/5ml (if cough) for 5 – 7 days. Syp. Lytex (ibn)/ Mucosol (bex)/ Ambolyt (incp)/ Boxol (ops) >5 years: 1 TSF TDS ► Normal saline drop or Oxymetazoline (0.25%) or Xylo-metazoline (0.05%) nasal drop (if less than 6 years of age), 1 – 2 drop in each nostril two or three times daily for 5 days. If vomiting, ► Syrup. Ondansetron 4mg/5ml ( ½ or ⅓ TSF) for 3 – 5 days Syp. Ofran (sq) / Ondason (drg) / Zofra (skf) / Periset (acm) >4years: 1 TSF TDS ► Alternate bronchodilator & steroid : Nebulization stat & TDS. With nebulizer or respiratory solution : windel plus (incp) / Sulprex (sq) / Ipsavent (uni) / Iprasol (bex) / Salpium (acm) 1 – 2 ml. (Salbutamol 3 – 4 times daily) + Nebuliser Suspension Budesonide 0.5 mg/2 ml / Budicort (incp) / Buditide (uni) / Budison (aris), 1 ml. (steroid 2 – 3 times daily) + Add N/S 1 ml.

treatment.title: Severe Pneumonia
treatment.description: ► Urgent hospitalization ► O ₂ inhalation stat & SOS. ► NPO TFO (if severe tachypnea / RR ↑↑↑ ) ► If on NPO, type & amount of fluid according to age. ► Syp. Paracetamol 120mg/5ml [15mg/Kg 6 hourly], 1 TSF for every 8 Kg 6 hourly ► N/S Nasal Drop/ Norsol, 1 drop in each nostril 6 hourly. ► Nebulization with Salbutamol / Windel Plus (incp) / Sulprex (sq) (little role of bronchodilators in pneumonia but given conventionally) stat & 6 hourly. If nebulization is not possible : Syp. Salbutamol 2mg/5ml / Brodil (aci) / Salmolin (acm) / Ventisal (ibn), (wt/10) ml P/O stat and TDS [0.1mg/Kg TDS] Age ► Inj. Ceftazidime 250mg/5ml / Tazid (sq) / Cefazid (ren) / Trum 3 (drg) / Serozid (ops) [100mg/Kg/Day], [wt in kg] ml IV stat & BD ► Inj. Gentamycin 20mg/2ml / Gentin (ops) / Intamycin (incp), [wt/4] ml IV stat and BD [5mg/Kg/Day] Age >2 months : ► Inj. Ceftriaxone 500 mg or 1 gm [50-100mg/Kg/Day], [wt in kg] ml IV stat and OD or, (wt/2) ml IV BD. P/A : If consolidation on CXR : ► Inj. Flucloxacillin 500 mg + 5 ml DW, [wt/4] ml stat and 6 hourly [100mg/Kg/day 6 hourly]

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discharge
treatment.description: C / C c̄ Duration : - Fever (high to moderate grade). - Respiratory distress & Cough. O / E : - Appearance : Dyspneic. - Temperature: Raised. - Flaring of nose : + - Cyanosis: +/-. - Heart rate (HR) : Tachycardia. - Respiratory rate : Tachypnoea. - Chest indrawing: +/-. Respiratory system examination : - Breath sound : Bronchial over consolidation. - Wheezing : +++ - Crepitation : +/-. If Tachypnoea (RR >70 b/m) + Tachycardia (HR >100 b/m) + hepatomegaly + Cardiomegaly (On chest X-ray) → Dx : Pneumonia or Bronchopneumonia c̄ heart failure (HF). ► Classification of pneumonia : No pneumonia : ● Cough only. ● No fast breathing and no indicators of severe or very severe pneumonia. Pneumonia : ● Fast breathing : ○ ≥ 60/min, if age 0 – 2 months, ○ ≥ 50/min, if age 2 – 12months, ○ ≥ 40/min, if age 1 – 5 years. ● Chest indrawing (+). Severe pneumonia : ● Any general danger sign (convulsion, lethargic or unconscious, not able to drink or breastfeed, vomits everything). ● Severe lower chest indrawing. ● Stridor in calm child. Danger sings in any children : • Cyanosis. • Very high respiratory rate (> 70/min). • Unable to feed / breastfeed. • Unable to drink or eat. • Vomiting everything / Very severe persistent vomiting. • Convulsion. • Lethargic or unconsciousness. • Severe malnutrition. • Stridor in calm child. Δ Pneumonia / Severe pneumonia Adv. : ▸ CXR A/P view : Homogenous opacity. ± ▸ CBC : Neutrophilic Leucocytosis [Avoid any blood test in child if not strongly indicated] ☼ Diet : EBF / Normal / NPO-TFO (if RR ↑↑↑ ) ☼ Bed rest in propped up position. ☼ O 2 inhalation stat & sos. ☼ O-P suction stat & sos. ☼ Nebulization c̄ Windel plus & Budicort (if cough) three times daily. Age Windel plus Budicort N/S 0.5 ml 0.5 ml 2 ml 1 – 4 yrs 1 ml 1 ml 1 ml > 4 yrs 2 – 3 ml 1 ml - ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone (1g/10 ml) or (500mg/5 ml) [wt] ml I/V stat & OD [50 – 100 mg / Kg / Day, use only if age more than 3 months] ☼ Syrp. Paracetamol 120mg/5ml, [wt/8] TSF 6 hourly. [15mg/Kg 6 hrly] ☼ Supp. Paracetamol 120 or 250 or 500 mg, [15×wt] mg P/R sos, if temp > 101ºF. ☼ Tepid sponging. ► Nebulization has no role but given conventionally. If nebulization is not possible : P/A : ☼ Syp. Salbutamol 2mg/5ml [0.1 – 0.2 mg/Kg TDS], [wt/15] TSF TDS ► Antibiotc : If age less than 3 months : ☼ Inj. Ceftazidime (250mg/5ml) or (500mg/5 ml) or (1g/10 ml), [wt in kg] ml IV stat & BD [100mg/Kg/Day]. + Plus, ☼ Inj. Kacin 100mg/2ml, [0.15 × wt] ml IV stat and BD. Or, ☼ Inj. Ampicillin 200mg/2.5ml, [wt/2] ml IV stat and BD. If consolidation on CXR : Add – ☼ Inj. Flucloxacillin (500mg/5ml) [100mg/Kg/day 6 hourly], [wt/4] ml IV stat and 6 hourly. ► Nasal Decongestant : ☼ Nasal Drop N/S 0.9% / Solo (sq) / Norsol (osl) / Nosomist (ops), 1 – 3 drops into each nostril 3 to 4 times a day for 3 – 7 days. Or, ☼ N/D Oxymetazoline 0.05 % ( ≥ 6 yr) / 0.025% (child of 2-6yrs) / Afrin (aris) / Rynex (incp) / Oxynex (ops) / Xylocon (sq), 1 – 2 drops in each nostril twice daily for 3 – 5 days. Or, ☼ N/D Xylometazoline 0.1% ( ≥ 6 yr) or 0.05% (½ - 6 years) / Antazol (sq) / Rhinozol (acm) / Xylomet (ops), 1 – 2 drops in each nostril 2 to 3 times daily for 3 – 7 days. ► If patient develop heart failure (HF) : C/F : 1. Tachypnoea. 2. Tachycardia. 3. Hepatomegaly. 4. Cardiomegaly. Treatment : ☼ Propped up position. ☼ High flow O ₂ inhalation stat and sos. ☼ Inf. Frusemide 20mg /2ml, [wt/10] ml IV stat and BD. ☼ Inj. Digoxin 0.5mg/2ml, [0.03mg/kg/dose] (administer through 100 U insulin syringe), ○ (1.5 × wt) unit IV stat, ○ 8 hour later (0.75 × wt) unit, ○ next another 8 hour later (0.75 × wt) unit. ☼ Tab. Captopril 25 mg, 0.25mg/kg/day (at night). Dc̄A (Pneumonia) : -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- ☼ Syrup. Cefixime 100mg/5ml, [wt/10] TSF × BD – 7 days ☼ Syrup. P/C 120mg/5ml, [wt/8] TSF × QDS – If fever presents. বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন।

treatment.title: Diagnosis & Hospitalization Criteria
treatment.description: SEVERE ACUTE MALNUTRITION (SAM) A. In 6 – 60 months : more than one of the followings : ○ MUAC ○ WLZ ○ Bilateral oedema. B. In one or more of the followings : ○ WLZ ○ Bipedal oedema. ○ Visible wasting. • MUAC = Mid upper arm circumference. • WLZ = Weight-for-length z-score. • WHZ = Weight-for-length z-score. Indication of hospitalization in SAM : 1. Edema : Grade ++/+++ 2. Edema with wasting 3. Unable to eat 4. Persistent vomiting 5. Fever (>39ºC or 102.2ºF axillary) or hypothermia ( 6. Rapid breathing : ○ ≥ 60 /min ○ 2-12 months ≥ 50 /min ○ 12-59 months ≥ 40 /min 7. Severely pale 8. Infection requiring parenteral treatment 9. Very weak, apathetic, unconscious/convulsions. 10. Dehydration 11. No urine for last 12 hours 12. Infants 13. Caregiver requests

treatment.title: Ten essential steps
treatment.description: Ten essential steps for management : 1.Treat/prevent Hypoglycaemia : if ○ Blood sugar level is ○ Hypothermia. ○ Lethargy, limpness, convulsion and loss of consciousness. 2.Treat / prevent hypothermia. 3. Treat / prevent dehydration. 4. Correct electrolyte imbalance ( ↓ or ↑ Na + , ↓ K + , ↓ Ca 2+ , ↓ Mg 2+ ). 5. Treat / prevent infection. 6. Correct micronutrient deficiencies. 7. Breast feeding with oral / NG feeding. (calorie 100 kcal/kg/d, protein 1-1.5 g/kg/d, fluid 100ml/kg/d if with oedema; 120ml/kg/d if without oedema). 8. Achieve catch-up growth with rapid weight gain of ≥ 10 g/kg/d. 9. Cheerful, loving care, structured play therapy 15-30 min/d. 10. Prepare for discharge and follow-up, if achieved WLZ or WHZ > -2. Phases of management : 1. Stabilization : 1 st week. 2. Rehabilitation: 2 nd to 6 th weeks.

treatment.title: Step 1 to 10
treatment.description: Step 1 : Treatment / Prevent Hypoglycemia : • 50 ml of 10% glucose oral/NG tube or, • 5 ml of 10% glucose IV slowly. • Continue 2 hourly feed day and night through NG tube. If the child is unconscious or convulsing give : • 10% glucose or DA (5 ml/kg) IV followed by 50 ml of 10% glucose or DA by NG tube. If persists – give P/R diazepam (0.5mg/kg body weight) or intranasal midazolam. Keep the child warm. Step 2 : Treatment / Prevent Hypothermia : If axillary Step 3 : Treatment and prevention of dehydration : by Rehydration Solution for Malnutrition (ReSoMal). ● 1 st 2 hours : Give ReSomal, 5 ml/kg, orally / through NG tube, at 30 minutes interval. ● Next 4-10 hours : Give ReSomal, 5 -10 ml/kg/hour, alternatively with F-75 therapeutic milk. Step-4: Correct electrolyte imbalance : • K + 3-4 mmol/kg/day (1 ml contains 1.5 mmol). • Inj. 50% MgSO4: 0.1 ml/kg/day IM for 7 days. • Prepare food without salt. • ReSoMal for rehydration. Step 5 : Treat and Prevent Infection : Uncomplicated SAM : Home treatment : • Oral amoxicillin 15 mg/kg 8 hourly for 5 days or Cotrimoxazole (Trimethoprim- 5mg/kg) 12 hourly for 5 days. Complicated SAM : • IV/IM Ampicillin (50 mg/kg) 6 hourly & IV/IM Gentamycin (7.5 mg/kg) once daily for 7 days. • Change to Ceftriaxone (50-100 mg/kg/day), ± Inj. Gentamicin (7.5 mg/kg) once daily when – no improvement e.g. lethargy, persistence of fever by 48 hours or deterioration after 24 hours or present with septic shock or meningitis. Step 6 : Correct micronutrient deficiencies : High potency Vit-A cap. On day 1, 2, 14. ○ 0 – 5 months: 50,000IU (2 drops), ○ 6 – 12 months : 100,000 IU (4 drops), ○ >12 months : 200000 IU (8 drops). Give the following for 2 weeks – • Multivitamin, usually 1-2 tsf/day. • Folic acid 1 tablet (5 mg) on day 1, then 1 mg/day. • Syrup Zinc : 2 mg/kg/day (1 tsf = 10 mg). • Copper : 0.3 mg/kg/day (if available). • Elemental Iron, 3mg /kg/day usually, after 2weeks of starting treatment. Step 7 : Start feeding cautiously : Allocate daily fluid @ 120-130 ml/kg. But restrict to 100 ml/kg/day, if the child is oedematous with F75. Step 8: Achieve Catch-up Growth : About 1 week after admission, when appetite returns, oedema disappears. Change F-75 to F-100 therapeutic milk gradually. Step 9 : Provide sensory stimulation and emotional support. Step 10 : Prepare for discharge and follow-up after recovery.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Δ SAM ✔ Diet : NG Feeding F-75(75 kcal/100 ml fluid). ○ Day 1 – 2 : 11 ml/kg/feed 2 hourly. ○ Day 3 – 5 : 16 ml/kg/feed 3 hourly. ○ Day 6 & onwards : 22 ml/kg/feed 4 hrly. ✔ Inf. 10% DA 50ml orally or by NG tube stat. ✔ Inj. Ceftriaxone 1 gm/10 ml or 0.5g/5ml, [wt] ml IV stat and OD [100mg/Kg/Day] ✔ Inj. Gentamicin 20mg/2ml, [wt/4] ml IV stat & BD. ✔ Syrup. Potassium Chloride (500mg/5ml) or (6.7mmol/5ml) / Electro K, 4mmol/kg/day (3ml/Kg/Day) for 1 week. ± ✔ Inj. MgSO4 (2.47 mg/5ml), 0.1 ml/kg/day OD for 5 – 7 days. ✔ Tab. Folic acid 5mg, 5 mg at day 1 then, ⅕ + 0 + 0 for 2 weeks. Or, Syp. Folic Acid 2.5 mg/5 ml, 10 ml at day 1 & 2 ml daily for next 14 days. ✔ Syrup. Zinc 10mg/5ml [2mg/kg/day] ○ ½ TSF stat & BD ○ > 6 month : 1 TSF stat & BD ✔ Cap. Vitamin A ○ 0 – 6 months: 50,000 IU ○ 6 months – 1 year : 1,00,000 IU ○ 1 year – 2 years : 2,00,000 IU ○ If eye involvement : 1 st , 2 nd & 14 th day → 3 doses. ✔ Multivitamin : Syrup. V plex ○ ½ TSF ○ 6 months – 2years : 1 TSF ○ > 2 years :2 TSF OD for 2 weeks ✔ If dehydration present : ReSoMal. ○ 5ml/kg in every 30 minutes for 2 hours, ○ Then, Alternate hours for 4-10 hours. F-75 is given in alternate hours. ✔ Keep the baby warm. ✔ If fever : No sponging, no paracetamol. ► Start feeding with F-75 therapeutic milk 2-3 hourly orally through NG tube. F-75 diet: [75 kcal/100ml, protein 0.9g/100ml] F-100 diet: [100 kcal/100ml, protein 2.9 g/100ml]

treatment.title: Treatment of associated Conditions
treatment.description: Vitamin A deficiency : • Vitamin A capsule to all children with SAM (single dose); If photophobia / Xeropthalmia present, give vitamin A on day 1, 2 and 14. • Persistent diarrhea – Give vitamin A on day 1. • In case of Measles – Give vitamin A on day 1 & 2. Dermatosis : For fungal infections. • Skin lesions : give Clotrimazole cream to apply twice daily for 2 weeks. Or, • 1% potassium permanganate solution soaked gauze over affected area. • ± Syrup. Zinc 10mg/5ml 1 TSF for every 5 kg [2 mg/kg/day] – 2 weeks. • Keep the perineum dry. • If candidiasis present : Cream clotrimazole, apply locally 2 times daily – 2 weeks. • If oral candidiasis : Drop Nystatin 1 lac unit, 15 – 30 drops 4 times daily – 14 days Helminthiasis : • Albendazole single dose for children (12-23 months) : 200 mg, Children ≥ 24 months : 400 mg, OR, • Mebendazole : 100 mg twice daily for 3 days (>24 months of age). Diarrhoea : • Giardiasis – Oral Metronidazole (7.5 mg/kg) 8 hourly for 7 days. • Dysentery – Oral Ciprofloxacin (10 mg/kg/dose) 12 hourly for 5 days or Pivmecillinum. • ORS & Zinc. Corneal ulceration/clouding : • Eye drop Moxifloxacin 0.5% / Chloramphenicol 0.5%, 1 drop in each eye 2-3 hourly for 7-14 days. • Oint Tetracycline 1% or 3%, Apply at night for 7-14 days. • E/D Atropine 1gm/100ml, 1 drop TDS for 3-5 days. • Cover eye with eye pads soaked with N/S and bandage.

treatment.title: Treatment of complications
treatment.description: SHOCK : C/F : 1. Lethargy or unconsciousness. 2. Cold extremities. 3. CRT > 3 secs. 4. Pulse : Weak or fast pulse. [Age 2-12 months: ≥ 160/min, Age 1-5 years: ≥ 140/min] • Give oxygen 2-4 L/min., Give 10% glucose (5ml/kg) IV. • Antibiotic- Ampicillin & Gentamycin. • Give IV fluid (e.g. 5% dextrosin 0.45% NaCl) or Ringer lactate – 20 ml /kg /hour over one hour, • AWD- cholera saline 20 ml/kg /hour for 2 nd hours, If sign of improvement present repeat IV cholera saline 15 ml/kg over 1 hour (3 rd hour). Switch to ReSoMal, 10 ml/ kg / h in alternate hours with F-75 for up to 10 hours, then start F-75. • If fails to improve – continue IV fluids (3 ml/kg/hr), wait for blood. Blood transfusion 10 ml/ kg slowly over 3 hours. Treatment of Shock : ☼ O ₂ inhalation ☼ Inf. 10% DA 5 ml/kg IV stat. ☼ Inf. Libott S IV junior (5% dextrose +0.45% NS), 20ml/kg over 1 hour [If shock is due to AWD infuse C/S 20ml/kg/hour for 2 hours]. ☼ Inj. Ceftriaxone, [wt] ml IV stat and OD [100mg/Kg/Day]. ☼ Keep the baby warm ☼ Monitor pulse and respiratory rates at every 30 min. If improvement occurs : Heart rate and respiratory rates fall : ☼ Inf. Libott S IV junior (5% dextrose + 0.45% NS), 15ml/kg over 1 hour then switch to oral feed. ↓ If there is no improvement : Heart rate and respiratory rates remain high, assume septic shock. ☼ Inf. Libot S IV junior (5% dextrose + 0.45% NS), 3ml/kg/hour till blood is available then Blood transfusion (BT) 10ml/kg over 3 hrs. If RR increases by ≥ 5/min, HR increases by ≥ 25 beats/min : Stop the infusion or BT & urgent call to seniors / ICU. If very severe anaemia present : If Hb Treatment : • Blood transfusion 10ml/kg over 3 hours. • Inj. Frusemide 20mg/2ml+8ml D/W, [wt/2] IV stat just before BT. • If signs of cardiac failure present : Transfuse packed cells 5-7 ml/kg. Discharge criteria : If criteria for discharge fulfilled – • WHZ ≥ -2 SD. • Oedema has resolved. • Gaining weight. • Eating an adequate amount of nutritious food that the mother can prepared at home. • All infection and complication have been treated. • Provided with micronutrients. • Immunization is updated. Child : 1. WHZ ≥ -2 SD. 2. Oedema has resolved. 3. No more infection or medical complication. 4. Immunization is completed or planned. Mother : Knows how to 1. Prepare homemade balanced diet and to feed the child. 2. How to make appropriate toys and to play with the baby. 3. How to provide home Rx of fever, diarrhoea and ARI, and knows when to seek medical assistance. 4. Advice for follow up Follow up : F/U visit – 1 week, 2 weeks, 1 month, 3 months, then 3 monthly until WHZ become >-1 or WHM >90% , which point the baby was discharged. Switching to catch up formula from starter formula : 1. Provide the same amount of F-100 in stead of F-75; every 4 hour for 48 hours. 2. Increase each successive feed by 10 ml until some foods remain uneaten. Assessment of weight gain : 1. Good: >10g/kg/day (Praise the staff and mother). 2. Moderate: 5-10 g/kg/day (Check whether intake is appropriate, or infection has been overlooked). 3. Poor:

treatment.title: Child Feeding
treatment.description: 1. 1 st 6 months : ○ Colostrum is the first effective vaccine. ○ Exclusive breast feeding is gold standard. 2. 6 th to 8 th months : ○ Breast feeding plus- ○ 3 servings : Smashed / liquid family food like rice, vegetables, pulses, fish, meat (blend / mixed). ○ Fruit juice (homemade). 3. 9 th month to 12 th months : ○ 5 servings of family food including 2-3 staple food (3 meals +2 snacks). ○ Snacks: e.g., noodles, banana, firni, payesh, egg, haluya, semai, chira etc. ○ Breast feeding will be continued. 4. By 2 to 3 years : Accustomed to family foods and stop breast feeding.

treatment.title: Treatment (RAP) - Syrups
treatment.disease_name: Recurrent Abdominal Pain (RAP) & Irritable bowel syndrome (IBS)
treatment.description: RECURRENT ABDOMINAL PAIN (RAP) Treatment : • Diet : avoid milk and milk products if IBS is suspected. • Tab. Amitryptyline 10mg, 1 mg/kg/day, maximum 25 mg at bed time-2 months or longer If dyspeptic symptoms present: • Tab. Rabeprazole 10mg Img/kg/day BD – 2 months. If IBS is suspected : • Cap. Peppermint oil 1+1+1 – 2 months. In abdominal migraine : • Tab. Pizotifen 0.5mg 0+0+1 – 3 months. Symptomatic treatment – ¤ Syp. Famotidine (40mg/5ml) /Famomax (bex) / Famotack (sq) / Famogen (gen) / Famotid (drg) / Famodin (acm), [wt/20] TSF BD for 5 – 60 days. ¤ Syp. Domperidone (5mg/5ml) / Motigut (sq) / Deflux (bex) / Domin (ops) / Don-A (acm) / Omidon (incp), [wt/12] TSF BD for 15 – 60 days. ¤ Syp. Multivitamin & Cod Liver Oil / Pogo (hc) / Dorakid (bacn) / Filwel Kids (sq) / Zovia kids (ops) / Bextrum kids (bex) / Kiddi (ren), [wt/20] TSF OD for 30 – 90 days.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Acute Viral Hepatitis C/C : – Fever – Malaise & anorexia – Yellow discoloration of sclera. – Child become icteric when bilirubin >2 mg/dl and in neonate >5 mg/dl. Adv. 1. SGPT 2. PT If needed : 3. S. bilirubin 3. Anti-HAV 4. HBsAg Tx, Laxative : ► Syp. Lactulose 3.35 gm/5 ml / Lactu (bio) / Avolac (aris) / D-lac (drg) / Osmolax (sq) / Actilac (hc) / Sinalac (ibn), [wt/5] চামচ দিনে ২ থেকে ৩ বার ---- ১৪ দিন ► Syp. Ursodeoxycholic Acid 250mg/5ml / Liconor (ops) / Ursolic (drg), [wt/20] চামচ দিনে ২ বার ---- ৭ থেকে ১৪ দিন [Until ALT is normal] ± ► Syp. Famotidine 40mg/5ml / Famomax (bex) / Famotack (sq) / Famogen (gen) / Famotid (drg) / Famodin (acm), [wt/20] চামচ দিনে ২ বার ---- ৭ থেকে ১৪ দিন If itching present (hepatitis) : ± ► Sachet Cholestyramine / Hepacol 4 gm (৬ বৎসরের উপরের বাচ্চাদের) ½ স্যাসেট / অর্ধেক পাতা /প্যাকেট আধাগ্লাস (১২৫ মিলি) পানিতে গুলিয়ে খাওয়বেন ---- ৫ দিন (Cholestyramine 250 mg/Kg/day in divided dose) উপদেশ : ১। প্রোটিন জাতীয় খাবার (যে কোনো মাংস, মাছ, ডাল) কম বা অল্প পরিমাণে খাওয়া যাবে। ২। রেজিষ্ট্রার্ড চিকিৎসকের পরামর্শ ছাড়া কোনো ঔষধ খাবেন না। ব্যাথার ও ঘুমের ঔষধ, হারবাল ঔষধ খাওয়া যাবে না। ৩। পর্যাপ্ত পানি খাবেন। তবে মিষ্টি জাতীয় খাবার, অতিরিক্ত পানি ও ফলের রস বা জুস খাওয়া থেকে বিরত থাকতে হবে। ৪। ১৪ দিন পর দেখা করবেন। এর মধ্যে বাচ্চা অজ্ঞান হয়ে গেলে বা অতিরিক্ত জ্বর বা ডায়রিয়া হলে অতিসত্ত্বর নিকটস্থ হাসপাতালে ভর্তি করবেন।

treatment.title: Discussion
treatment.description: ► Multivitamin & Cod Liver Oil is often useful. • Syp. Pogo (hc) / Dorakid (bacn) / Filwel Kids (sq) / Zovia kids (ops) / Bextrum kids (bex) / Kiddi (ren) 1 – 12 Months 2.5 ml ( ½ teaspoon) Once daily 1 – 4 Years 5 ml (1 teaspoon) Once daily 4+ years 7.5 ml (1 ½ teaspoon) Once daily Adult 10 ml (2 teaspoons) Once daily ► Hepatitis in children by hepatitis ‘B’ virus is uncommon, as majority children are vaccinated against HBV by EPI schedule. Commonly by A & E virus, transmitted by feco-oral route. ► Laxative syp. Lactulose may be given in higher dose to prevent constipation. Constipation may lead to ammonia intoxication & hepatic encephalopathy. So prevention of constipation is a must. ► Common side effects of Cholestyramine (Hepacol) is constipation, so high doses of laxatives may need. Multivitamins has regenerative & laxative effects. ► Paracetamol is hepatotoxic, so P/C is not used if liver is compromised, but Tab. Paradote (Paracetamol + Methionine) (renata, doubtly authorized) may use to reduce the toxicity. Syp. / Tab. Ibuprofen is used as anti-pyretic & analgesic in liver diseases. ► Predictor of impending hepatic failure : • Persistence of prodormal symptoms for >3 wks • Reappearance of prodormal symptoms • Gradual deepening of jaundice • Ascites • Sudden shrinkage of liver • Bilirubin >17.5 mg/dl • PT ↑ • SGPT ↓ markedly • RBS, BUN, cholesterol ↓ ► Indication of hospitalization in acute viral hepatitis : • Hepatic failure • Impending failure • Severe nausea • Intractable vomiting • High grade fever

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Acute Hepatitis [Please see “gastroenterology” portion.] C/C – 1. Reluctant to feed for 3 – 7 days. 2. Nausea and vomiting. 3. Yellow discoloration of sclera, skin, urine etc. 4. Upper (right sided) abdominal pain. 5. Fever. O/E – 1. Jaundice. 2. Tender hepatomegaly. 3. Abdomen – soft but tender. Δ Acute Hepatitis Advice : ± ▸ CBC c̄ PDF ▸ S. bilirubin ▸ SGPT : ↑↑↑ ▸ ALP ▸ PT : ↑↑ ▸ Anti-HAV IgM ▸ HBsAg : If not vaccinated. ▸ Anti- HEV IgM ☼ Complete bed rest. ☼ Diet : high caloric c̄ protein restricted. ☼ Fluid : Inf. (2-10 yr : ½ Strength) Libott-S JR (libra) / Babysol Junior (osl) [100 × wt] ml, I/V stat @ [total amount in ml/100] d/m. ☼ Syrup. Ursodeoxycolic acid 250mg/5ml [wt/20] TSF BD. [10-30mg/kg/day] Ursodeoxycholic acid / Ursocol 150 mg, 1 + 0 + 1 ☼ Sus. Domperidone (5 mg/5 ml) / Deflux (bex) / Don-A (acm) / Vave (aci), [wt/12] TSF TDS (10 – 30 min B/M) ☼ Syp. Lactulose 68%/ Avolac (aris) / Dlac (drg), [wt/5] TSF BD/ TDS. [0.5 – 1.0 ml/kg/day] [Titrated (increase / decrease dose) until 2-3 loose stool/day] ± ☼ Inj. Vitamin K 2mg/0.2ml / K-one MM (sq), 1 amp IV stat & daily if PT is >15 sec. ☼ Syp. Ibuprofen 100 mg/5 ml / Flamex (aci) / Advel (ops) / Profen (acm) / Inflam (syn), [wt/15] TSF TDS/QDS. ☼ Tepid sponging (if temp raised). ☼ Avoid paracetamol, NSAIDs, sedatives, centrally acting analgesics, antiviral, diuretics & I/M injections. ► Fluid option : If age ≥ 10 yrs : Inf. 5% DNS, [50 × wt] ml to max [150 × wt]/day. If age 2 – 10 yrs : [50 × wt] ml to maximum [150 × wt]/day. ½ Strength : Sodium Chloride 0.45% + Dextrose 5% / Libott-S JR (libra) / Babysol Junior (osl) / Neosol DS (bex) / Dextrosal (ori) / Solodex JR (sq) / Kidsol DS (acm). If age ≤ 2 yrs : [50 × wt] ml to maximum [150 × wt]/day. ¼ Strength : Sodium Chloride 0.225% + Dextrose 5% / Baby saline (libra) / Babysol (osl) / Pedisol (pop) / Dextrosal (ori) / Solodex Baby (sq). ► Antibiotic options : 1. Ciprofloxacin + Metronidazole 2. Cefotaxime 3. Ampicillin + Metronidazole 4. Rifaximin ► Ibuprofen is the antipyretic & analgesics in liver impairment instead of paracetamol (P/C).

treatment.title: Treatment of Chronic Hepatitis B Infection
treatment.description: Antiviral should be given after fulfilling the following criteria : A. Chronic viral hepatitis : 1. HBSAg positive : for ≥ 6 months. 2. HBSAg positive and anti-HBc lgM negative in one occasion. B. Active hepatic inflammation : 1. Raised ALT for 6 months >1.5 ULN or > 60 IU/L whichever is lower. 2. Histological evidence of chronic hepatitis : Moderate to severe inflammation and fibrosis. C. Viral replication : 1. HBV DNA > 2000IU/ml and/or 2. HBcAg positive. Drugs : Lamivudine : Dose : 3 mg/kg/day. Highest dose : 10omg/day. Adefovir : ○ ○ 6-12 years: 0.25 mg/kg/day ○ >12 years: 10mg/day Tenofovir : >12 years: 300 mg OD. Entecavir : 0.015 mg/kg/day, highest dose 0.5 mg/day. Oral antiviral drugs should be continued for at least 1 year and maintained for at least 12 months after HBcAg seroconversion if there is no evidence of resistance or any drug reaction. Interferon : 6 mIU/m² thrice weekly S/C for 6 months. Vaccination : Schedule : ○ 0, 1 st month & 6 th months. ○ 4 th dose (booster dose) : If antibody titre ○ In high risk group : booster dose 5 yearly. Dose : ○ ○ >18 Years : 20 µm, ○ Immunocompromised: 40 μ m. HBIg : Should be given within 24 hours of identified blood exposure, within 14 days of sexual exposure.

treatment.title: Treatment of Chronic Hepatitis C Infection
treatment.description: Treatment : Ribavirin + Sofosbuvir for 12-24 weeks. ● Tab. Ribavirin 200mg, 15 mg/kg/day in 2 divided doses (BD). ● Tab. Sofosbuvir 400mg, 200mg/day. Follow up : For screening of HCC : 1. USG of HBS. 2. Alpha fetoprotein

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: C/F : 1. Progressive jaundice. 2. Fever, abdominal pain, vomiting, anorexia. 3. Swelling of abdomen. 4. Bleeding. Drug history : Ask about taking herbal medication, acetaminophen, sodium valproate, isoniazid etc. History of taking mushroom. Family history : Liver/Kidney disease. O/E : ○ Fetor hepaticus : +. ○ Jaundice : Progressive. ○ Raised ICP caused by cerebral oedema causes – ○ BP : Hypotension ○ Pulse : Bradycardia Per abdomen O/E : ○ Liver : May be palpable. ○ Fluid thrill/Shifting dullness : +. Δ Acute Liver Failure ● Bed rest. ● Diet: Protein restriction in stage III and IV. ● Inf. 10% DA, 60ml/kg/day. [to avoid aggravating factor hypoglycemia] ● Syrup. Lactulose3.35gm/5ml, 10-50 ml 2-4 hourly sufficient to cause loose stool. [to avoid aggravating factor constipation] ● Inj. Omeprazole 40mg [0.5-1mg/kg/dose BD], [wt/4] ml IV stat and BD. ± ● Tab. Rifaximin 200 mg. ● Avoid NSAIDs, sedatives and diuretics. ► For infection : For 7 days. ● Inj. Cefuroxime 250mg/vial, 20mg/kg/dose TDS. ● Inj. Amoxicillin 500mg, 15mg/kg/dose TDS. ● Tab. Metronidazole 400mg, 10mg/kg/dose TDS. ● Cap. Fluconazole 50mg, 3-6 mg/kg/day OD. ► If bleeding manifestation present : Inj. Konakion, FFP, cryoprecipitate, platelets, recombinant factor VIII. ► Investigations : 1. CBC : ↓ Hb, thrombocytopenia. 2. PBF : Features of hemolysis. 3. S. bilirubin : Raised. 4. SGPT, SGOT : Raised. 5. PT with INR : Prolonged, not improved after administration of Vit-K. 6. RBS : Hypoglycaemia 7. Serum electrolytes : Hyponatremia, Hypokalemia. 8. Blood ammonia. 9. HBsAg. 10. Anti HAV IgM, anti HEV IgM. 11. Autoantibodies: Anti LKM1, anti-SMA, ANA, total IgG.

treatment.title: Hepatic encephalopathy
treatment.description: Types of hepatic encephalopathy Stage 0: Minimal HE. Slight changes in memory and concentration. Stage 1: Mild HE. Mood changes and sleep problems. Stage 2: Moderate HE. Inappropriate behavior, slurred speech, trouble doing basic math. Stage 3: Severe HE. Disorientation, extreme sleepiness, or anxiety. Stage 4: Coma. Four stages of encephalopathy : Grade-I Symptoms : ● Periods of euphoria ● Lethargy ● Reversal of day night sleeping; Signs : ● Trouble in drawing figure and performing mental tasks EEG : Normal Grade-II Symptoms : ● Drowsiness ● Inappropriate behavior ● Agitation ● Wide mood swing ● Disorientation Signs : ● Asterixis ● Fetor hepaticus ● Incontinence EEG : Generalized slowing, q waves Grade-III Symptoms : ● Stupor but arousable ● Confused ● Incoherent speech Signs : ● Asterixis ● Hyper reflexia ● Extensor reflexes ● Rigidity EEG : Markedly abnormal, triphasic waves Grade-IV Symptoms : ● Coma IVa : Respond to noxious stimuli. IVb : No response. Signs : ● Areflexia ● No asterixis ● Flaccidity EEG : Markedly abnormal Bilateral slowing, d waves, electric cortical silence.

treatment.title: Urinary Catheterization
treatment.description: Requirements : Sterile gloves, Foley’s catheter, 2% xylocaine jelly, Povidone iodine solution, 10 mL syringe, distilled water, urobag, sterile dressing, sterile draping sheet. Procedure : 1. Explain to the patient that a catheter will be introduced through his urethral orifice. Reassure that it will be a painless procedure. 2. Scrub and put on sterile gloves. 3. Patient supine with legs apart 4. Clean the genitalia with an antiseptic solution and drape the area 5. The uncircumcized foreskin is retracted, and the glans penis and the corona exposed. 6. The glans penis is cleaned with antiseptic solution. 7. Take 20 mL of 2% xylocaine in the syringe and introduce it through the external urethral meatus into the urethra and press the external urethral meatus to prevent the jelly spilling out and massage the undersurface of the penis to allow the jelly to go further down. Or, Introduce almost the whole tube of jesocain jelly into urethra. Usually no jelly needed for female. 8. Wait for 5 minutes 9. The penis is held vertically upwards to straighten the penile urethra by encircling a sterile gauge around the penis. 10. The (lubricated) Foley's catheter is then pushed gently through the external urethral meatus and gradually advanced till it reaches the bladder when urine will be seen coming through the catheter. 11. The catheter is advanced a little further. 12. Aspiration of urine through straight channel with a empty 10 cc syringe to see urine for comfirmation. Thus we may exclude false passage. 13. About 10 mL of water is introduced through the side channel of the catheter to inflate the balloon or the amount (2.4 ml / 3 ml) of water written in the oblique channel of sillicon catheters for children. Distilled water should be used for inflation of balloon, never use normal saline. Tap water may be used in this purpose, if D/W is unavailable. 14. After the balloon is inflated the catheter is pulled outward to confirm that the balloon is properly inflated. 15. The catheter is then connected to an urobag. 16. The preputial skin is brought back over the glans penis to prevent development of paraphimosis (Hindu patients) in case of chronic retention the bladder should be emptied slowly.

treatment.title: Insertion of a nasogastric (NG) tube
treatment.description: Indications for inserting a nasogastric tube : - Decompression of the stomach during upper abdominal surgery. - To empty stomach in intestinal obstruction. - To decompress the stomach postoperatively. - For gastric lavage for preparation of patients of gastric outlet obstruction (GOO) for operation. - To monitor gastric bleeding. - For feeding. Requirements : Sterile gloves, Paraffin / Jesocain jelly, Micropore, NG tube, Stethoscope, 50cc syringe. Procedure : 1. Explain the patient that a tube needs to be inserted through his nose. Patient has to swallow the tube. There may be some cough during insertion of the tube. 2. Lubricate the Ryle’s tube (Usually no.16 Fr or 14 Fr.) with 2% xylocaine jelly / Paraffin (supplied in the ward). 3. Choose the nostril which has the wider channel. 4. Pass the Ryle’s tube horizontally through the nose. As the Ryle’s tube touches the posterior pharyngeal wall the patient will gag. 5. Patient may be given a little water to sip. The tube is then slowly advanced as the patient is asked to swallow the tube. During swallowing the cricopharyngeus muscle will relax and the tube will enter into the esophagus. The tube is further advanced till the second ring in the tube lies at the level of nostril when the tip will lie in the stomach. Actually at first we have to measure the NG tube & mark with a little micropore piece. Measure angle of the mouth to angle of the mandible then angle of the mandible to xiphisternum or base of the nose to umbilicus. 6. The tube is secured to the nose with an adhesive tape / micropore. 7. The tube is then connected to a plastic drainage bag to siphon out the stomach contents. if required the stomach content may be sucked out hourly. How to confirm that the tube is in stomach : 1. Inject about 20 mL of air through the tube and listen with a stethoscope over the epigastrium — audible gurgling sound in the epigastrium will confirm that the tube is in stomach. 2. Put the outer end of the tube within a cup / glass full of water. If gurgling presents with respiration then the tube is in the trachea / lung. 3. Aspirate the tube with a syringe — aspiration of greenish gray fluid confirms that the tube is in stomach. Advise to patient attendants : 1. First to bring the patient in sitting position by holding from back or, by cushion bring the patient to propped up position. 2. Then attach 50 cc syring to the NG tube port. 3. Pour liquid rice / jaw vat, suji, milk, soften powder / biscuit, water etc, avoid high price formulated canned foods. 4. Pour liquid food full of 50 cc syring 4 times after each 2 hour. 5. Finally in each time pour 20 cc freash water to prevent tube blockage. 6. During feeding the 50 cc syringe is to hold up to the nose level, so that the liquid food will spontaneously go into stomach, never to use piston of the syringe for pushing. 7. If liquid food does not go spontaneously then a mild push with the piston to the liquid food filled syringe. 8. Keep the patient even after finishing the 4 syringe food for 15 min to prevent aspiration. 9. Opening port of NG tube is opened or covered is not important, but kept close to avoid aspiration outside. 10. If patient tries to pull off the NG tube then make balls of paper / take small balls, then tie then with both plam of patient, so that patient can not make fist or grasp anything. Or, Tie with bed.

treatment.title: Measuring Blood Pressure
treatment.description: When measuring BP : - Patients should not have smoked, or ingested caffeine-containing beverages or food in the preceding 30minutes. - Patient should be seated for at least 5 minutes before measurement with the back supported, and arm bared and resting on a surface at heart-level. - Use a standard bladder (12-13 cm long and 35 cm wide) but have a larger and a smaller bladder available for fat and thin arms, respectively. Use smaller bladder in children. The bladder within the cuff should encircle 80% of the arm. - Have the cuff at the heart level, whatever the position of the patient. - SBP should be estimated first by palpation to avoid missing the auscultatory gap. - Take at least two measurements spaced by 1-2 minutes, and additional measurements if the first two readings differ by >5 mmHg. The BP recorded should be the average of 2 readings. - Use phase I and V (disappearance) Korotkoff sounds to identify systolic and diastolic BP, respectively. - Measure BP in both arms at first visit to detect possible differences due to peripheral vascular disease. In this instance, take the higher value as the reference one. - Measure BP 1 and 5 min after assumption of the standing position in elderly subjects, diabetic patients, and in other conditions in which postural hypotension may be frequent or suspected.

treatment.title: Chest Drain Tube Management
treatment.description: Post-Procedure Care : • Prescribe oral analgesia. • In order to avoid the rare complication of re-expansion pulmonary oedema, do not drain off more than 2 L of pleural fluid in 24 hours (e.g. 1 L mane, 1 L nocte). • For the underwater seal, it is imperative that the bottle is kept below the level of the insertion site at all times (to prevent passage of the drainage bottle contents into the pleural cavity). The bottle must also be kept upright, and adequate sterile water should be present in order to submerge the end of the tube. • Keep the tip (that is into bag) of drain tube into distilled / savlon water. Keep the valve of the bag open. • Record regularly the amount of drainage/bubbling and the presence of respiratory swing on a dedicated chest drain chart. Bubbling during cough is monitorted, if no bubling then the tube is blocked.

treatment.title: Ascitic Fluid Aspiration / Abdominal Paracentesis
treatment.description: Abdominal Paracentesis : In rupture ectopic pregnancy, blant trauma over abdomen / spleen, abdominal paracentesis is indicated to detect hemo-peritonium. Ascitic fluid aspiration to study ē ADA & malignat cells is to detect medical diseases. Requirement : ╠ Hand gloves 7”. ╠ Blood set needle. ╠ Sterile empty saline bag / Container. ╠ Saline set (Never tranfusion set, as they will filter pus cells). ╠ Micropore 1”. ╠ Spirit / Viodin 10% / Lidocaine as per need. Steps / Procedure : 1. Select safe area for punture. ‘Safe area’ of the abdomen for performing paracentesis: the right or left iliac fossae inferior to the umbilicus. (A) Line drawing of the surface anatomy of the abdomen for safe abdominal paracentesis. ‘Safe area’ of the abdomen for performing paracentesis : the right or left iliac fossae inferior to the umbilicus. (B) Photo demonstrating the safe areas for paracentesis in a patient with gross ascites. 2. Connect saline set with sterile empty saline bag & other end with sterile blood set needle. 3. Position : Supine. Empty saline bag below the bed. Patient on floor can not be drained. 4. Anti septic wash with spirit of safe area & wear hand gloves. 5. Prick needle in “Z pattern / track”. Oblique direction is termed the ‘Z track’, ensuring the sites of needle entry at the skin and at the peritoneum do not overlie one another. this reduces the risk of continuing leak from the puncture site following the procedure. 6. Fix the neddle with micropore & cotton. 7. Do not aspirtae more than 2-3 L, e.g. 2-3 saline bag of 1L. For more aspiration you must supply human albumin. 8. Preserve first saline bag & sent for “ Ascitic fluid study c̄ ADA ”, (Adenosine De-Aminase) if not done before. Note : Encysted / Uable to aspirate fluid, Adv. USG guided ascitic fluid aspiration & Ascitic fluid study c̄ ADA.

treatment.title: Pleural Fluid Aspiration / Paracentesis Thoracis
treatment.description: Requirement : ╠ Hand gloves 7”. ╠ Blood set needle. ╠ Sterile empty saline bag / Container. ╠ Saline set (Never tranfusion set, as they will filter pus cells). ╠ Micropore 1”. ╠ Spirit / Viodin 10% / Lidocaine as per need. Steps / Procedure : 1. Select safe area for punture. • The site of aspiration should be between the posterior-axillary line and the inferior angle of the scapula. • Alternatively chest auscultation / percussion should allow detection of the effusion and the needle entered into the pleural space some 2–3 rib spaces below the highest level of the effusion. Roughly lower part 9 th intercostal space, that is upper border to 11 th rib to avoid neurovascular bundle of intercostal grove. 2. Connect saline set with sterile empty saline bag & other end with sterile blood set needle. 3. Position : Sit the patient upright and leaning forward. Empty saline bag below the bed. Patient on floor can not be drained. 4. Anti septic wash with spirit of safe area & wear hand gloves. 5. Prick needle in “Z pattern / track”. Oblique direction is termed the ‘Z track’, ensuring the sites of needle entry at the skin and at the peritoneum do not overlie one another. this reduces the risk of continuing leak from the puncture site following the procedure. 6. Fix the neddle with micropore & cotton. 7. Do not aspirtae more than 2L, e.g. 2 saline bag of 1L to avoid Re-expansion edema. 8. Preserve first saline bag & sent for “ Pleural fluid study c̄ ADA ”, (Adenosine De-Aminase) if not done before. Note : Encysted / Uable to aspirate fluid Adv. USG guided pleural fluid aspiration & pleural fluid study c̄ ADA.

treatment.title: Tension Pneumothorax – Emergency Mx
treatment.description: British thoracic society (bts) guidelines for managing pneumothoraces Pleural aspiration has a role in the management of pneumothoraces if certain criteria are met: • Primary pneumothorax (no evidence of underlying lung disease): — if patient is not breathless or there is — if patient breathless or >2 cm rim of air on chest X-ray, then aspirate. If unsuccessful, consider repeat aspiration up to a total of 2.5 L. If an air leak persists, insert a chest drain. • Secondary pneumothorax (known underlying lung disease): — if patient is not breathless, or there is — if patient breathless or > 2 cm rim of air on chest X-ray, then insert a chest drain. TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX – EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT • This is a medical emergency requiring immediate recognition and treatment. If left untreated the increasing size of the tension pneumothorax compresses the mediastinum, ultimately obstructing venous return to the right heart and causing subsequent haemodynamic collapse and death. Do not waste time with local anaesthetic – warn the patient of the procedure and the importance of performing it quickly. Procedure : • Upon diagnosing a tension pneumothorax, immediately insert a large-bore cannula over the upper border of the third rib in the second anterior intercostal space, mid-clavicular line. Fig. Position for decompression of tension pneumothorax: the second intercostal space, mid-clavicular line. • Upon removing the trocar a ‘hiss’ of air should be heard, signifying decompression of the tension and reversion to a simple pneumothorax. • The purpose of needle thoracocentesis is to convert a tension pneumothorax into a simple pneumothorax. Inserting the cannula allows air to escape to atmosphere during expiration rather than accumulating and placing the intrathoracic structures under tension. Therefore formal drainage of the residual simple pneumothorax will still be required with an intercostal drain. Note – Spongy ( প্যাচ প্যাচ করে এমন ) skin over chest / upper jaw / leg / any where is Subcutaneous Emphysema.

treatment.title: Lumber Puncture (LP)
treatment.description: Identification of space for SAB / Lumber puncture : Anatomy : Spinal cord extends from foramen magnum to Adult : lower border of L1 or, upper border of L2 or in L2. Infants / Children : L3. It is about 45 cm long. Spinal cord gives rise to 31 pairs of spinal nerve. Dura-mater, Sub-arachnoid space & subdural space : From foramen magnum & extends up to S2 in adults (S3 in children). Limber cistern : CSF within the L1 (end of conus medullaris) to S2 level (end of dura & arachonoid mater), L3 to S3 in children, where lumber puncture should be done. An extension of pia-mater, the filum terminale penetrate the dura and attach the terminal end of spinal cord [conus medullaris] to the periosteum of the coccyx. Site identification Adult : L3-L4 or L4-L5 (Commonly at L3-L4). Infant : L4-L5. Aim is to inject LA below L1 in adult & L3 in children at any point below spinal cord (conus medullaris) even in L2-L3 & the largest interspace is L5-S1. But these spaces are rarely practiced. Tuffier’s line / Inter-cristine line : A line drawn between the highest point of iliac crests (not posterior superior spine of iliac crest) usually cross either body of L4 or the L4-L5 interspace. Needle insertion Keep the bevel pointer on left or right side in case of sitting position & upwards or downwards in case of lateral position. Cutting needles should be inserted with the bevel parallel to the longitudinal fibers of the dura. This helps to reduce cutting fibers and enhances tactile sensation as anatomical structures are crossed. Needle face two (02) resistance / POP during insertion – - 1 st POP due to Ligamentum flavum / flava. - 2 nd POP due to Dura-Arachonoid Mater. Approach Mainly two types. They are – Midline & Paramedian / Lateral. *** Structure pierced during LP Midline Approach *** Paramedian approach Skin Skin Subcutaneous fat Subcutaneous fat Supraspinous ligament × Interspinous ligament (It is not × Infra spinous) × Ligamentum flavum / flava (1 st POP) Ligmentum flava Areolar Tissue Areolar Tissue Dura mater Dura mater Subdural space Subdural space Arachnoid mater Arachnoid mater Subarachnoid space Subarachnoid space ✔ What is Bupivacaine Heavy in SAB ? Available injection is Bupivacaine 5 mg/mL + 5% Dextrose 80 mg/mL. It is 0.5% bupivacaine in 5% dextrose. Dextrose is to make LA (Bupivacaine) heavier (hyperbaric) than CSF. ✔ Why to use only Bupivacaine Heavy during LP ? To prevent high block & Total spinal block (TSB). ✔ We must use only hyperbaric solution intrathecally (LP) to prevent high block. Block more than C4 level may cause cardiac arrest and respiratory arrest (respiratory center paralysis in pons). ✔ Adjuvant opioid (fentanyl) is used to potentiate the action of LA & for analgesia. ✔ Indication of Lumber Puncture / Spinal Tap : 1. Anesthesia (regional or SAB) : Surgical procedures carried out on the lower half of the body (lower abdomen / below umbilicus) & lower extremities. This blocks spinal nerve not substances of spinal cord. 2. Diagnostic : CSF study (Total 6mL, 2 mL is collected in 3 vials each). 3. Treatment : Intrathecal opioids as analgesic. ✔ Steps of Lumber Puncture (LP) / Spinal Tap : 1. Site identification & indentation by mild nail indentation. 2. Antiseptic wash (central to periphery). 3. Insertion of needle with beveled side laterally. 4. Stop when 2 nd POP is felt. 5. Removal of trocar & observe clear CSF on the hub. 6. Diagnostic : CSF study (Total 6mL, 2 mL is collected in 3 vials each). ✔ Endotracheal (ET) intubation & CPR should be learnt, these are life saving procedures. These procedures will be added in next edition.

treatment.title: BLS : CPR Procedure
treatment.description: Basic Life Support (BLS) : CPR Procedure • CPR হচ্ছে একমাত্র পদ্ধতি যার মাধ্যমে কার্ডিয়াক এ্যারেস্ট বা হঠাৎ হৃদযন্ত্র বন্ধ হয়ে যাওয়া কার্যক্রমকে সচল করতে পারে। • CPR দেয়া না হলে মাত্র তিন থেকে চার মিনিটের মধ্যে মস্তিষ্কে রক্তসঞ্চালন সম্পূর্ণ বন্ধ হয়ে যায় এবং সাত মিনিটের মধ্যে মৃত্যু অনিবার্য । • CPR শুরু করলে সমগ্র শরীরে রক্তসঞ্চালন পুনরায় শুরু হয় এবং শরীরের কোষগুলো পুনরায় অক্সিজেন পায়, এর ফলে আমাদের মস্তিষ্ক এবং শরীরের কিছু সময়ের পুনরায় সচল রাখা যায়। এই সময়ের মধ্যে রুগীকে হাসপাতালে স্থানান্তর করা গেলে তার বাঁচার সম্ভবনা বাড়ে। কার ক্ষেত্রে CPR প্রদান করত হয় : • কোন ব্যক্তি যদি অজ্ঞান থাকে ও হৃদস্পন্দন শোনা না গেলে। • কোন ব্যক্তি যদি শ্বাস নিতে না পারে অথবা নি:শ্বাস নিতে মারত্বক কষ্ট হয়। • কোন ব্যক্তি যদি কথা শব্দ ব্যথামূলক অনুভূতিতে সাড়া না দেয়। CPR এর ধাপসমূহ : • কোন শক্ত স্থানের উপর অজ্ঞান ব্যক্তিকে রেখে টানা ৩০ বার বুকের সামনে দিকে হাতের তালু দিয়ে চাপ প্রয়োগ করা এবং তারপর ২ বার অজ্ঞান ব্যক্তির মুখে সরাসরি বাতাস দেয়া, এভাবে মিনিটে মোট ১০০ বার বুকের সামনে চাপ দেয়া এবং কমপক্ষে ৮ বার মুখে বাতাস প্রদান করা। • যদি অজ্ঞান ব্যক্তির মুখে বাতাস দেয়া সম্ভব না হয় তবে মিনিটে কমপক্ষে ১০০ বার বুকের সামনের অংশে চাপ প্রদান করা নিশ্চিত করতে হবে। • যে ব্যক্তিকে সিপিআর প্রদান করতে হবে তার মাথা পেছনের দিকে হেলান দিয়ে এবং থুতনির দিকটি উপরের দিকে তুললে শ্বাসনালী উন্মুক্ত হবে। • তারপর আঙুল দিয়ে তার নাকের ফুটো বন্ধ করতে হবে। এরপর সেই ব্যক্তির মুখের সাথে মুখ লাগিয়ে দ্রুত কমপক্ষে ২ বার শ্বাস দিতে হবে। এক্ষেত্রে লক্ষা রাখতে হবে যেন অজ্ঞান ব্যক্তির নাক দিয়ে বাতাস বেরিয়ে না যায় এবং তার বুক সঠিকভাবে ওঠানামা করে। • প্রায়শই সামনের অংশে চাপ প্রদানের ফলে বুকের সামনের হাড় ভেঙে যেতে পারে। সেক্ষেত্রে হাতের অবস্থান পরিবর্তন করে পুনরায় চাপ প্রদান করতে হবে। কখন CPR দেয়া বন্ধ করতে হবে : • যদি অজ্ঞান ব্যক্তি নিজে থেকে শ্বাসপ্রশ্বাস নেয়া শুরু করে এবং তার হৃদস্পন্দন ফিরে আসে। • যদি সিপিআর চলাকালীন অবস্থায় অজ্ঞান ব্যক্তিকে হাসপাতালে অথবা এম্বুলেন্স স্থানান্তর করা সম্ভব হবে।

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discharge
treatment.description: C/C – [Age : 6 months – 6 years, peaks at 14-18 months] Fever & Convulsion 1 time; followed by normal level of consciousness, playful, eating well. O/E : – Appearance : Normal / sleepy during convulsion. – HR : Tachycardia. – RR : Tachypnea. – Temperature : Raised. – Systemic examination: – Fontanelle : Normal. – Signs of meningeal irritation : Absent. Δ Febrile convulsion : • Diet : Full. • Syp. P/C / Tamen (skf) / Fast (acm) / Ace (sq), [wt/8] TSF TDS. • Supp. P/C / Renova (ops) 125/250/500 mg, [15×wt] mg P/R sos (if temp > 101°F) • Syp. Cefixime / Cef-3 (sq) / Denver (hc), [wt/20] TSF BD. Or, • Inj. Ceftriaxone (500mg/5ml), [wt] ml I/V stat & OD. During active convulsion : •Inj. Diazepam (10mg/2ml) + 8 ml DW or NS, then, [wt/2] ml per rectal (PR) though NG tube or rubber tube during active convulsion. Up to 3 times in 10 min interval. Then, • Tab. Diazepam 5 mg / Sedil (sq) / Easium (ops) / Relaxen (sonera), [0.3mg/kg TDS for 3 days], [wt/3] mg + [wt/3] mg + [wt/3] mg ► Indications of CSF study : 1. Any doubt of meningitis / encephalitis. 2. First attack is 3. Age 12-18 months associated with complex seizure / altered sensorium. 4. If recovery is slow or undue prolongation of post-ictal sleep. Dc̄A (FS) -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- ● Syrup Cefixime (100mg/5ml), 8mg/kg/day BD – 7 days ● Syrup Paracetamol (125/5ml), [wt/8] TSF TDS – 5 days (If fever present). ● Tab. Diazepam 5 mg, [0.3mg/kg TDS for 3 days], [wt/17] mg + [wt/17] mg + [wt/17] mg – 3 days উপদেশ : ১) বাচ্চাদের বয়স ৬ বছর হওয়া পর্যন্ত জ্বর হলে এমন খিঁচুনি হতে পারে, এতে দু:চিন্তার কোনো কারণ নাই। ২) বাসায় নিমাক্ত ঔষুধ দুটি রাখবেন ও বাচ্চার জ্বর আসলে খাওয়াবেন। বাচ্চার বয়সরে সাথে তার ওজন অনুযায়ী ওষুদ্ধরে পরমিাণ বাড়াতে হবে। • Syrup. Paracetamol / Napa, [wt/8] TSF TDS/QDS for 3-5 days. • Tab. Diazepam / Sedil, ½ + ½ + ½ or, ⅓ + ⅓ + ⅓ for 3 days. ৩) এরপরও জ্বর না কমলে দ্রুত চিকিৎসকের / হাসপাতালের শরণাপন্ন হবেন। বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Typically : ● Usually 6 months – 6 years, peak age 18 months. ● Males are affected slightly more commonly than females. ● Seizure occurs with a rapid rise of temperature. ● Onset is within 24 hour of illness, mostly within 12 hour of development of fever. Type is generalized tonic-clonic, duration is ● Not due to CNS infection (meningitis - bulging fontanelle, neck stiffness, stupor, irritability) or metabolic imbalance. Atypical / Complex : 1. Other age group ( 6 year) 2. Multiple fits per day. 3. Non tonic clonic, not generalized. 4. Lasts > 15min. Criteria for Prophylactic Anti-convulsant Therapy : ● Patients under 18 months of age with previous abnormal development or abnormal neurological signs. ● Atypical febrile seizures. ● Recurrent febrile seizures. ● High level of parental anxiety. Continuous daily prophylaxis can be given with sodium valproate 30-60 mg/kg/day in 2 divided doses, to be continued for at least 2 year fit-free or until the child is 6 year old, whichever comes earlier. ● Syrup Sodium Valproate 200 mg/5 ml / Valex / Valoate, [wt/8] চামচ দিনে ২ বার ---- চলবে ► Not to treat a case of FS in GP if atypical / complex seizure & typical seizure below 1 years. Febrile seizure lasting longer than 30 minutes is called febrile status epilepticus. Typical Febrile Convulsion Exclude other causes by history : • Meningitis • Encephalitis • Cerebral malaria • Metabolic derangement Examinations : • Level of consciousness • Sign of meningeal irritation • Sign of infection – Otitis media, skin rash, sore throat, tonsillitis. Treatment : ● Suspension Paracetamol 120 mg/5 ml / Apa / Fast / ATP / Reset / Renova, [wt/8] চামচ দিনে ৩ - ৪ বার ---- ৩ - ৫ দিন ● Tab. Diazepam 5 mg / Sedil / Azepam / Easium, 0.33 mg/Kg 8 hourly ---- 3 – 5 days Correction of IDA (Iron ± Vit-C) : ● Syrup Iron Polymaltose Complex 50 mg/5 ml / Compiron, [wt/10] চামচ দুপুরে ১ বার (ভরাপেটে) --- ৩ / ৬ / ১২ মাস Prophylaxis : The risk of reverence can be reduced by diazepam or clobazam or clonazepam for 2-3 days, with paracetamol for fever. Counseling to parents : ১. আবার জ্বর আসলে এমন খিঁচুনী হতে পারে। কিছু কিছু বাচ্চাদের জ্বরের সাথে খিচুনি হয়, ভয়ের কিছু নাই, ৬ বা ৭ বছর বয়স পর্যন্ত যখন বেশি জ্বর আসবে, তখন এমন খিচুনি হতে পারে। তবে যখন জ্বর হবে, তখন কিছু মেডিসিন খেলে সাধারণত খিঁচুনি অআর হয় না। ২. জ্বর আসার সাথে সাথে ঔষধ খাওয়াতে হবে যাতে শরীরের তাপমাত্রা হঠাৎ বৃদ্ধি না হয়। ফ্রিজে ডায়াজিপাম ও প্যারাসটিামল সাপোজিটার রাখবেন। বয়স বাড়ার সাথে সাথে ডোজ পরর্বিতন করে নিবেন। ● Tab. Diazepam (0.3-0.5 mg/Kg/dose TDS) & ● Syp. Paracetamol (15 mg/Kg/dose TDS or QDS). ৩. জ্বর আসলে খিঁচুনী হোক বা না হোক কমপক্ষে ৩ দিন ডায়াজিপাম ও প্যারাসটিামল খাওয়াবেন। ৪. ভবিষ্যতে ১ % বাচ্চার মৃগী রোগ হতে পারে। ফিভরাইল কনভালশন রোগের বাচ্চার সাধারণত পরবর্তী জীবনে বুদ্ধিমত্তার কোন ত্রুটি বা সমস্যা হয় না। জ্বরের সাথে খিচুনি থাকলে (Febrile Convulsion) : ১। Suppository Paracetamol /Renova (125mg) ব্যবহার করবেন। ২। Tab. Sedil (5 mg), ( ½ + ½ + ½ ) বা ( ⅓ + ⅓ + ⅓ ) ---- ৩ দিন। ৩। মুখে কিছু খাওয়াবেন না। ৪। ডান বা বাম কাতে শুয়ে রাখবেন। ৫। মুখে ফেনা আসলে মুছে দিবেন। বি: দ্র: Tab. Sedil, Paracetamol Suppository ঔষধগুলি ৫ বছর বয়স পর্যন্ত সব সময় ঘরে জমা রাখবেন। ► Though there is no indication of antibiotic in any case except acute infection & prophylaxis but practically a full course antibiotic helps to reduce frequency of illness (ARI / AWD).

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Cause : most common cause viral. C/Fs.: • Mild infection, coma or sudden death. • High fever, violent, convulsion. • Bizarre movement. • Hallucination with brief recovery followed by recovery. • Non-specific – fever, headache, hyperaesthesia, irritability, lethargy, nausea, vomiting. • Altered consciousness, stupor. • Focal neurologic sign. • Flaccid paralysis. Treatment of viral encephalitis — • Supportive except HSV (IV Acyclovir, Gancyclovir, Valacyclovir). • Analgesic. • Anti convulsant. • IV fluid & feeding. See meningitis portion (just above). Prevention — • Vaccination against MMR and varicella, Rabies, Influenza, HBV. • Vector control-by spray at breeding site. • Insect repellants on exposed part. • Long sleeves, pants, socks.

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discharge
treatment.description: C/C – ● High grade fever for more than 7 days. ● Convulsion & excessive cry. O/E : – Fontanelle ( ½ yr) : Bulged – Signs of meningeal irritation : Positive. – Kernig sign : Positive. – Signs of meningeal irritation: negative (in encephalitis). O/G/E : – Appearance : Ill looking / semiconscious/ unconscious. – HR : Tachycardia. – RR : Tachypnea. – Temperature: Raised. Δ Meningitis (?) Adv. : ▸ CBC : Neutrophilic Leucocytosis ▸ CRP: Raised ▸ CXR PA view ▸ CSF study : See lab value portion. ▸ Blood culture. • NPO TFO (if semi-conscious). • Fluid according to age & weight. • ± NG tube feeding. • Inj. Ceftriaxone / Arixon (bex) / Oricef (hc) (500mg/5ml) or (1g/10ml) [100mg/kg/day], [wt] ml I/V stat & OD or BD. • Inj. Vancomycin / Covan (ren) / Vancomin (ops) / Vanmycin (incp) [60mg/kg/day], [0.3 × wt] ml + 20 ml N/S or D/W, I/V stat @ 25 μ d/m & QDS. ± • Inj. Acyclovir / Virux (sq) / Xovir (bacn) / Acerux (ops) (250 mg / 50ml) or (500 mg / 100ml) [10mg/kg/dose TDS], [wt × 2] ml IV stat @ 30-50 μ d/m & TDS. • Inj. Dexamethasone 5mg/1ml [0.4mg/kg/day], [wt× 0.03] to [wt× 0.08] ml IV stat & QDS. (IV steroids should be given before giving IV antibiotic). • Inj. Phenobarbitone 200mg/1ml + 9 ml D/W, then, [wt] ml IV stat & then, [wt/8] ml IV BD. • Syrup. Paracetamol 120mg/5ml, [wt/8] TSF QDS. [15mg/Kg 6 hourly] • Supp. Napa (P/C) 60 / 125 / 250 / 500mg, [15 × wt] mg stick P/R sos (if > 101°F) • Tepid sponging. • Change posture 2 hourly. • Continuous catheterization. • Care of bowel, skin, oral cavity and eyes. ► IV antibiotic for 10-14 days. In Gram (-ve) bacteria for 21 days. Choice of antibiotic depends upon organism sensitivity pattern. • Inj. Cefotaxim(300mg/kg/day) for 6 hrly. • Inj. Ceftriaxon(100mg/kg/day) + Inj. Vancomycin (60mg/kg/day) 6 hrly. • Inj. Ceftazidim + Inj aminoglycoside. • Inj. Meropenam (30mg/kg/dose) TID. • Inj. Ampicillin (200mg/kg/day) QID. • Inj. Vancomycin + Rifampicin. ► Gram +ve case CSF become sterile within 1-2 days, Gram (-) ve bacteria by 2-10 days. ► Steroid – IV Dexamethasone 0.15 mg/kg/dose (0.6mg/kg/day) QID for 2 days (48 hours) which reduces cerebral edema and neurological injury. Dexamethasone should be given 15 minutes before antibiotic. ► Feeding (fluid and calorie) maintain, restriction of fluid ½ to ⅓ to avoid raised ICP and SIADH. ► Convulsion – IV anti convulsant → Phenobarbitone 20mg/kg/dose as loading dose, following by maintenance 5mg/kg/dose TDS until no convulsion for 1 – 2 days. ► Rx of complication : Increase intracranial pressure ( ↑ ICP) : • IV Mannitol 0.5-1g/kg infusion. • Inj. Furosemide / Lasix : 1mg/kg/dose (BD). • Intubation and hyperventilation. Discharge [Meningitis (bacterial / viral)] -: হাসপাতাল ত্যাগকালে উপদেশ ও ব্যবস্থাপত্র :- • Syrup. Cef-3 (Cefixime) 100mg/5ml, [wt/10] TSF × BD for 14 – 21 days. • Syrup. Napa (PC) 125/5ml, [wt/8] TSF × TDS – if fever present. বিঃদ্রঃ পুনরায় ভর্তি ও উপদেশের জন্য এই ছাড়পত্র অবশ্যই সঙ্গে আনিবেন। ► Outcome : Complete recovery in most children. But – • Mortality- 10% • Neurodevelopment sequelae-10- 20 % • Sensory-neural hearing loss (common) • Cognitive impairment • Visual impairment • Behaviour problem • Recurrent seizure • Delayed speech ► Prevention : Vaccination for against pneumo-coccus, meningo-coccus, haemophyllus influenzae. ► Antibiotic prophylaxis for high risk (TB) contacts - Rifampicin 10- 20 mg/kg for 2-4 days.

treatment.title: Features
treatment.description: Nephrotic syndrome It is the commonest renal disease, (50-70% of all pediatric renal disease). More common in male child, 2-15 years of age. Classification : ▪ Idiopathic nephrotic syndrome (90%) ▪ Secondrynephrotic syndrome (10%) ▪ Congenital nephrotic syndrome ( ˂ 1%) C/C : • Facial puffiness –› Swelling of whole body. • Face – puffiness, edematous. • Reduce urine output. • BP (blood pressure) is normal. • Bed side heat coagulation test-positive (+++ proteinuria). Investigation – • In first attack – all investigations. • In relapse – infection screening and few relevant. For diagnosis : ▪ 24 hrs urinary total protein: ˃ 1 gm/day – significant. [Spot urinary protein creatinine / PCR ratio, confirmatory diagnosis if it is > 2 (in case of relapse)] ▪ S. albumin : ˂ 25 gm/L (normally 34 to 54 g/L). ▪ S. cholesterol : ˃ 220 mg/dl (normally For infection screening : ▪ Hb%, TC, DC, ESR, PBF : ESR ↑ (prognostic value). ▪ Urine for RME and c/s: rule out UTI. ▪ CXR : rule out pleural effusion / consolidation. ▪ MT : rule out TB. To see renal function : ▪ S. creatinine ▪ Blood urea ▪ S. Electrolytes (Na ⁺ , K ⁺ , Cl ⁻ , HCO ₃) To identify secondary cause : ▪ HBsAg, Anti HCV. ▪ C3, C4, ANA, Anti dsDNA. ▪ Renal biopsy : in steroid resistance, secondary NS. NS diagnostic criteria ¤ Massive Proteinuria (>1g/m 2 /day or spot urinary PCR > 2:1, > 50mg/kg/day) ¤ ↓ Albumin ( ¤ Oedema ¤ ↑ Cholesterol (because of ↓ Lipase, ↑ hepatic synthesis, ↓ receptor) Progression of oedema Facial edema (S. Albumin ↓ Swelling of whole body / Anasarca (S. Alb ↓ Genital swelling (S. Albumin ≤ 1 gm/dl)

treatment.title: Management
treatment.description: Principle of NS management : ✔ Hospitalization if huge edema or other complication and 1st attack : ascites, effusion, genital edema, severe infection, asthma. ✔ Diet – Normal diet with protein rich diet & salt restricted. ✔ Fat restriction. ✔ Salt restricted (2meq/kg/day) & no added salt. ✔ Fluid restricted = Previous Day Output (PDO) + (400 ml×BSA). ✔ Maintain input output (IO) chart. Control of infection / complication (asthma) : ✔ If UTI then give antibiotic (before that do urine C/S). ✔ Treatment of PEM, Ca ++ and vitamin deficiency. Reduce edema : ✔ Albumin : 0.5 to 1 gm/kg/dose, fresh frozen plasma ± IV furosemide at the end if volume is normal or high / ↑ BP. ✔ Diuretics : cautiously with close monitoring if no hypovolemia – furosemide (1-2 mg/kg/d), spironolactone (3-5 mg/kg/d). Treatment of 1st attack : ✔ Prednisolone : 60 mg/m 2 /day or 2 mg/kg/day – single morning dose or 2-3 divided doses for 6 weeks followed by 40 mg/m 2 /day or 1.5mg/kg/day on alternate on day single morning dose for-6 weeks ± followed by tapering over 1-2 months. o Usually 60-80% relapse but in longer duration of treatment of 1 st attack, reduce the relapse rate of 20-40% with increase side effect of steroid. o 80-90% respond within 3 weeks, vast majority by 5 weeks. Treatment of FRNS and SDNS : Find threshold value, lowest dose on alternate day, if no response then, consider daily dose of prednisolone which keeps in remission – continue the dose for 9 months to 1 year : Prednisolone 60 mg/m 2 /day → remission for 3 days → 40 mg/m 2 /day (1.5 mg/kg/day) → 1 mg/m 2 /day → 0.50 mg/kg/day → 0.25 mg/kg/day. Other options : Adding 2nd line drug – levamisole, cyclophosphamide, MMF, after remission. o Levamisole – 2-2.5 mg/kg on alternate day/daily for 1-2 years or longer. It is almost free of toxicity and cheap. o Cyclophosphamide – used in FRNS and SDNS, good for steroid toxic NS, maintains prolonged remission, 2-3 mg/kg on daily dose for 3 months along with alternate prednisolone at 1.5 mg/kg for 1 month. Toxicities are many but rarely seen except transient alopecia. Others : ✔ Calcium supplementation if more than 3 months of steroid therapy. ✔ If upper GI discomfort add famotidine (PPI use should be limited). ✔ Ramipril (ACEi) to reduce proteinuria. ✔ Atrovastatin (above 10 years of age) if hypercholesterolemia (Please see “Dyslipidemia” portion). ✔ Treatment of the underlying cause like DM, SLE etc. ► Vaccination : ➢ All vaccine after 1-3 month of prednisolone therapy. ➢ Live vaccine OPV, MMR, varicella, DPT, BCG to be avoided during Rx with steroid/CNI. ➢ Pneumococcal vaccine, Hib, HBV and influenza vaccination be given but response may be blunted, double dose for HBV recommended. ➢ Pneumococcal vaccine – - 3 doses of hepatavalent conjugate vaccine for less than 2 years. - 2-5 years – one dose of conjugate vaccine – after 8 weeks 1 dose of 23 valent polysaccharide vaccine - For more than 5 years 1 dose of polysaccharide vaccine, revaccinations after 5 years. ➢ Varicella and measles contact / infection – Vericella zoster immunoglobulin (125 U/10kg, minimum 125U, max 625U) if not available IV IG-400 mg/kg, IV Acyclovir 1500 mg/kg/m 2 /day.

treatment.title: Classification, outcome & referral
treatment.description: ► Clinical Classification : A. Steroid sensitive nephrotic syndrome (SSNS) : 80-90% • IFRNS (Infrequent relapse nephritic syndrome) : 20-25%, ≤ 2 in 6 months or ≤ 4 in 12 months. • FRNS (Frequent relapse nephritic syndrome) : 40-45%, ≥ 2 in 6 months or ≥ 4 in 12 months. • SDNS (Steroid dependent nephrotic syndrome) : 2 consecutive relapses during alternate day or, within 2-4 weeks after stopping of prednisolone therapy. B. Steroid-resistant nephrotic syndrome (SRNS) : (Rare 10-20%) No response after 4 weeks of steroid in adequate doses (60mg/m 2 /day) + 3 pulses intravenous methylprednisolone @ 30 mg/m 2 /dose. ► Counseling / Outcome : 1) Natural history → 70-80% relapse, daily heat coagulation test is essential to detect proteinuria and relapse. 2) Relapses are linked to mainly infection (should be promptly treated) - Urine infection - Asthma, cough and cold - Septicemia - Diarrhea 3) Toxicity due to drugs (steroids or others) will be abolished when disease will burn out. 4) 80-85% patients will be free from disease at around 14 to 20 years of age and they will live as normal child/adult, - 10-20% will continue as adult nephrotic syndrome and - 5% turns to CKD. ► Refer to pediatric nephrologists, if atypical / bad prognostic features : • Age 15 years. • ↑ BP – 20%. • Azotemia. • Hematuria. • ↓ C3, C4. • Extra renal features: suggest vasculitis/secondary causes. • +ve family history. • S. cholesterol > 500 mg/dl.

treatment.title: D/D points & heat coagulation test
treatment.description: ► Practical differentiating points among D/D : ➢ AGN : In AGN usually 5-15 yrs, sudden onset, Hematuria (100%), ↑ BP (60-80%),Turgid mild edema and dyspnea (due to volume overload). ➢ Angioneurotic edema : itching, no proteinuria or hematuria and normal BP. ➢ AKI : oliguria, azotemia. ➢ PEM: th centile, bed side albumin nil, hair, skin mental changes present in PEM. ➢ Liver disease : stigmata of chronic liver disease (CLD) – jaundice, (BT) ↑ bleeding time etc. ➢ Cardiac failure (HF) : tachypnea, tachycardia, dyspnea, gallop, basal crackles, pedal edema, murmur, ↑ JVP and tender liver. ➢ Protein loosing enteropathy : history of chronic diarrhea. ► Heat coagulation test : Procedure : 1. Fill ⅔ rd of the test tube with urine. 2. Add few drops of 2% acetic acid in it. 3. Heat the upper portion of the test tube up to boiling. Observation : The thick white coagulum is formed at the upper part of the test tube. Interference : Albumin and globulin is present. ► Interpretation of the heat coagulation test : • Hold a written paper opposite side of test tube : ✓ + means one can read paper with difficulties. ✓ ++ means cannot read but line can be detected. ✓ +++ means nothing is seen. ✓ ++++ means urine is curd. • Estimating amount of protein losing by doing heat coagulation Test : ✓ Trace means 10-20 mg/dl. ✓ + means closest to 30 mg/dl. ✓ ++ means 30- 100 mg/dl. ✓ +++ means closest to 100-300 mg/dl. ✓ ++++ means closest to 1-2 gm/dl. [Discussions are not intended to encourage treatment of NS in GP setup, rather to guide & counselling of the patient & relatives. Refer any complicated case to pedi-nephrologists]

treatment.title: Indoor Treatment & Discharge
treatment.description: C/C – 1. Usually 2-10 years. 2. Generalized swelling of the body for ___ days. 3. Scanty, normal coloured urine for ___ days. Past history: May H/O revious attack. Drug history: Prednisolone/ Diuretics. O/E – - Appearance : Puffy face - Oedema: +++. - Bed side urine heat coagulation test +. Diagnostic criteria : 1. Massive proteinuria. (>1g/m²/day or spot urinary PCR > 2:1, >50g/dl) 2. Hypoalbuminaemia ( 3. Oedema. 4. ↑ Cholesterol. Indications of hospitalization : 1. 1st attack. 2. Huge oedema, genital oedema, effusion, ascites. 3. Severe infection. 4. Asthma or other complications. Δ Nephrotic syndrome (NS) Advice : 1. Heat Coagulation test 2. Urine R/M/E 3. S. albumin 4. S. Cholesterol 5.
