Serum creatinine 6. 24 hours total urinary protein • Diet : Normal c̄ heavy protein diet. • Fluid : PDO + 250ml (Insensible fluid loss). • Please maintain input output chart. • Fluid, fat, salt & fruit restricted. ○ Protein rich diet. (2-2.5gm/Kg if persistent proteinuria) ○ Fat restriction: Not >30% from fat. ○ Salt restriction : No added salt. (2 meq/kg/day) If persistent edema and weight gain of 7-10% : • Tab. Furosemide 20 mg + Spironolactone 50 mg [Furosemide : 1 – 2 mg/kg/day BD, Spironolactone : 2 – 4 mg/Kg/Day], [wt] mg + [wt] mg + 0 Or, • Inj. Frusemide (20mg / 2 ml), [wt/10] ml I/V stat & BD (8 am & 4pm). In refractory edema : [0.5-1 g/Kg over 2-4 hr] • Inf. Human Albumin 25% / Albutein (alpha) / AlbuRx (csl) 25%, [wt×4 ml] IV stat@10d/m. Or, • Inf. Human Albumin 20% (csl) / (city) / Uman Albumin (zas), [50 ml 3,700/- to 4,500/, 100 ml 7,500/- to 9,500/-], [wt×5 ml] IV stat @ 10 d/m. Steroid : [60 mg/ (BSA) m² / day in single morning dose or 2 mg / kg / day (max 60 mg/day)] -- 6 weeks. • Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan (incp) 5 or 10 or 20 mg, [wt] mg + [wt] mg + 0 (after meal). • Tab. Rabeprazole 10 mg, 1+0+1. (as taking Tab. Cortan) If patient is febrile : • Inj. Ceftriaxone (500mg/5ml) or (1000mg/ 10ml), [wt] ml I/V stat & once daily. ► Fluid: 400ml/(BSA)m²+ previous days output (PDO) if marked edema or ascites. No restriction or normal in mild edema. Fluid, salt & fruit should be restricted to prevent edema & electrolyte imbalanced. Diuretics is must to resolve oedema. ► Specific treatment : Steroid / Prednisolone : 60 mg/m 2 body surface area/day in 3 divided dose until urine become protein free. Then, 60mg/m 2 /day single dose every alternate day for 3-6 months. But steroid must be given if there is no UTI or other infection, otherwise infection will be flare up. Discharge (NS) : Initial Attack: - Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan (2/5/10/20) mg, --+0+0 (A/M) [60mg/m2/day in single or divided dose] for 6 weeks. Then, 40mg/m2 as single dose in alternate days - next 6 weeks. Then, Taper over 4-8 weeks. উপদেশ : প্রতিদিন প্রস্রাব জ্বাল দিবেন। পরপর ৩ দিন প্রস্রাব ঘোলা হলে হাসপাতালে যোগাযোগ করবেন।

treatment.title: Features & Indoor Treatment
treatment.description: Acute Glomerulonephritis (AGN) / Nephritis syndrome (ANS) C/C – ● Usually 5-12 years, swelling of the whole body, first appearing on the face for ___ days. ● Scanty high coloured urine for ___ days. O/E – – H/O skin infection/sore throat. – Appearance : Puffy face/eyelid swelling. – HR : Tachycardia – BP : Hypertension – RR : Tachypnoea – Oedema : +++ – Scratch mark : Present. AGN characterized by – o Sudden onset of hematuria (microscopic or macroscopic). o Hypertension. o Mild azotemia. o Oliguria. o Edema Δ AGN • Diet : ○ Protein 0.5 gm/kg/day. ○ Salt restriction. ○ Fluid : PDO + 400ml/m²(BSA). • Please maintain input output chart. • Tab. Furosemide 40 mg [1-3 mg/kg/day BD], [wt] mg + [wt] mg + 0. Or, • Inj. Frusemide (20mg / 2 ml), [wt/10] ml I/V stat & BD (8 am & 4pm). • Tab. Phenoxymethyl Penicillin 250 mg, [Penicillin V] [50mg/Kg/Day, QDS], [12.5×wt] + [12.5×wt] + [12.5×wt] + [12.5×wt] • Tab. Captopril 25 or 50 mg / Acetor (drg) / Cardopril (bex) / Angiten (ibn) / Capotril (alco), [0.25-6mg/Kg/day in 2-4 dose], [wt] mg + [wt] mg + [wt] mg ± [wt] mg Or, • Tab. Nifedipine 10 or 20 mg / Nidipine SR 20 mg (sq) / Nifin 10 mg (acm) [0.25 – 0.5 mg/Kg/day in 2-4 dose], [wt/4] mg + 0 + [wt/4] mg ► Prophylactic antibiotic should be Phenoxymethyl penicillin 50mg/kg/day (6 hrly). • Tab. Oracyn-K (syn) / Penvik (sq) / Open (ops) / Biopen VK (bio) / Penicillin (bristol) 250 mg, [wt × 12.5] mg QDS (4 times daily). ► Treatment of the cause : 1. Hypertension. 2. HTN encephalopathy. 3. Heart failure (HF). 4. Renal failure (RF). ► Add antibiotics after investigations. Advice : 1. Heat Coagulation test 2. Urine R/M/E 3. S. Creatinine 4. S. Albumin 5. S. Cholesterol

treatment.title: Calculations theory
treatment.description: Pediatric Oral Drug Doses – © “Diary of Intern Life”, © “GP Diary”, By – Dr. Abu Taher Sohan. [Here, wt = Baby’s Weight in Kg] Formulations for pediatric uses Suitable age group Short form Different formulation Below 6 months P/D Paedriatic drop 6 months to 6 years Syp. & Sus. Syrup & Suspension Above 6 years Tab. & Cap. Tablet & Capsule * 1 TSF = ১ চামচ = ৫ মি লি * ½ TSF = 1 2 চামচ = ২.৫ মি লি * TSF = Tea spoonful & TBSF = Table spoonful * 1 Tbsf = 3 tsf = 15 mL. * 30 mL = 1 oz, 8 oz = 1 cup ≈ 250 mL or, 1 Glass ≈ 250 ml or 200 ml. * 1 Kg = 2.2 lbs (pounds). * 1 inch = 2.5 cm, 12 inches = 1 foot & 18 inches = 1 hand. * 15 drops or 20 drops = 1 ml, 1 drop = 4 μ d (microdrop). * mL = ml = cc = মি. লি. * ১ মি লি = ১ সি সি ≈ ১৫ ফোটা (অনেক ক্ষেত্রে ২০ ফোটা ধরা যায়) = 10 drop of blood transfusion set for blood products. = 60 microdrop ( μ d) or pedi drip set (microburette is used, weight > 10kg). * Syrup (Syp or Syrp) and suspension (sus or susp) are used synonymously. Shaking before intake is mandatory for any suspension . * Pediatric drop = P/D (age * Ear drop & Eye drop = E/D, Nasal drop = N/D; * I/V formulation or Injection = Inj. * PFS = Pre-filled syringe. * ২ বা ২.৫ চামচের বেশি হলে অ্যাডাল্ট ডোজের হিসাবে অর্ধেক বা পুরো ট্যাবলেট দেওয়া হয়। ভর (ওজন) ৩০ কেজির বেশি হলে অ্যাডাল্ট ডোজের অর্ধেক হিসাবে ঔষধ দেওয়া হয়।

treatment.title: Child Feeding option & frequency
treatment.description: 1. 1 st 6 months : ○ Colostrum is the first effective vaccine. ○ Exclusive breast feeding is gold standard. 2. 6 th to 8 th months : ○ Breast feeding plus- ○ 3 servings : Smashed / liquid family food like rice, vegetables, pulses, fish, meat (blend / mixed). ○ Fruit juice (homemade). 3. 9 th month to 12 th months : ○ 5 servings of family food including 2-3 staple food (3 meals +2 snacks). ○ Snacks: e.g., noodles, banana, firni, payesh, egg, haluya, semai, chira etc. ○ Breast feeding will be continued. 4. By 2 to 3 years : Accustomed to family foods and stop breast feeding.

Atropine 0.6mg/1ml, 0.01 – 0.05mg/kg/dose. ● Inj. Calcium gluconate 10% [wt] ml + same amount D/W, I/V very slowly over 20 min. If age 3months – ceftriaxone : ● Inj. Ceftazidime (250mg/2.5ml) or (500mg/5 ml) or (1g/10 ml), Neonate : [wt/2] ml I/V – BD Pedi : [wt/2] ml – TDS ● Inj. Ceftriaxone (1g/10 ml) or (500mg/5 ml), [wt/2] ml – 12 hourly or, [wt] ml IV OD. Inj. Ceftriaxone may be used 1ml/kg IV Stat & once daily (OD) dose may be divided into two doses (if IV cannula in situ). In hospitalized patient increased dose frequency has a positive psychological effect on the patient’s parents. More the Health Care Personnel attends the patient more the psychological to the patient near relatives, though increased dose frequency (of some drugs) has no beneficial pharmacological effect. ● Inj. Cefuroxime 150mg/kg/dose. ● Susp. Cefixime (100 mg / 5 ml), (wt/20) TSF BD for 7-14 days. ● Susp. Azithromycin (200mg/5ml), (wt/20) TSF once daily for 5 days. ● Inj. Ciprofloxacin (200mg/100ml bottle) 5mg/kg/dose – BD, [7.5 × wt] mL I/V stat – BD. ● Susp. Ciprofloxacin (250 mg/5 ml), [wt/20] TSF BD for 5 – 7 days. ● Inj. Flucloxacillin 250mg/2.5ml, [wt × 0.25] ml IV QDS. ● Inj. Gentamycin 20mg/2ml, [wt × 0.25] ml IV BD. [May also dilute 80mg/2ml injection + 6 ml D/W to make 20 mg/2 ml injection]. ● Inj. Amikacin 100mg/2ml, [wt × 0.15] ml IV BD. ● Inj. Lasix (20mg/2ml), [wt × 0.1] ml IV stat and BD. ● Inj. Metronidazole (500mg/100ml), [wt × 1.5] ml IV TDS. ● Susp. Metronidazole (200 mg / 5 ml), [wt/20] TSF TDS for 3 – 7 days. ● Inj. Meropenem (1000mg/20ml) (500mg/10ml) (250mg/5ml), 20-40 mg/kg/dose, [wt × 0.6] mL I/V – TDS. ● Inj. Omeprazole (40mg/10ml), [wt × 0.2] ml – BD. ● Inj. Ranitidine 50mg/100ml, [wt × 2] ml IV BD – QDS. [Not available now, banned generic worldwide]. ● Inj. Ondansetron (8mg/4ml), 0.15mg/kg/dose-TDS ● Inj. Dexamethasone (5mg/1ml) + 4ml D/W, [wt × 0.15] ml-TDS. ● Inj. Pralidoxime (1gm/20ml), 20-30mg/kg/dose. ● Inj. Sodibicarb (7.5mg/100ml), 0.5 – 1 ml/kg + same amount D/W I/V slowly stat and daily. ● Inj. Tranexamic acid (500mg/5ml), 10mg/kg/dose – TDS ● Inj. Timonium 5mg/2ml, [wt × 0.01] ml IV – TDS. ● Inj. Vancomycin 500mg/10ml Loading Dose (LD) : 0.3 × wt + 10 ml D/W, then, Maintenance Dose (MD) : 0.2 × wt + 10 ml D/W – TDS. All forms of paracetamol (P/C) taken 3-4 times daily for 3-7 days. ● Tab. Paracetamol, 15 mg/Kg/dose. ● Syp. Paracetamol 120 mg/ml, (wt/8) TSF. ● P/D Paracetamol 80 mg/ml, [3 × wt] drops ( ● Inj. Aminophylline (125 mg / 5 ml), Loading dose (LD) : 5 mg/kg, [wt/6] mL I/V stat then, Maintenance dose (MD) : 0.5 – 1 mg/kg/hr (Max rate: 25 mg/min). [wt] mL I/V throughtout 24 hours in drip (fluids that baby gets). ● Inj. Hydrocortisone 100 mg/2 ml, [wt/10] mL I/V stat & TDS / QDS. ● Inj. Diazepam (10 mg/2 ml), [wt/10] mL+ DA D/W (Double amount distilled water), per rectally (P/R) through rubber tube stat & sos. Up to 3 times in 10 min interval. Or, ● Inj. Diazepam (10mg/2ml) + 8 ml DW or NS, then (wt/2) ml per rectal (PR) though NG tube or rubber tube during active convulsion. Up to 3 times in 10 min interval. ● Inj. Diazepam (10mg/2ml), 0.5 mg/kg per rectally (P/R) & Inj. Diazepam (10mg/2ml), 0.2 – 0.3 mg/kg slowly I/V. ● Inj. Phenobarbitone (200 mg) 1 ml + 9 ml D/W, (wt) ml I/V stat then, (wt/8) mL I/V stat & BD. ● Inj. Pethidine 100 mg / 2 ml, Usually used for post-operative pain : 1.5 mg / Kg / Dose – TDS. [3 × wt] U (of 100 U insulin syringe) I/V stat & TDS. 1 U of insulin syringe (100 IU) contains 0.5 mg. treatment.title: Nebule / Nebuliser Solution
treatment.description: Salbutamol : Orally : 0.1 mg / Kg / Dose QDS. Tab. 2 mg, 4 mg, Syp. 2 mg/tsf. Nebulization : 0.25 mg / Kg / Dose QDS. Nebulization : Add normal saline to make solution total 3 ml. Salbutamol : 0.03 ml/kg Ipratropium : 0.1 ml/kg Salbutamol + Ipratropium : 0.15m/kg Salbutamol 2.5 mg + Ipratropium 500 mcg / 3 ml Windel Plus / Sulprex / Iprasol / Salpium Nebuliser Solution (0.15 × wt) ml + N/S to make total 3 ml; 6 or 8 hourly Another solution may contain (2.5 mg+500 mcg)/ml, then use 0.03 ml/kg. Add normal saline to make the total solution 3 – 5 ml. Nebulization with at least 3 to 5 ml solution is needed to reach the narrowest lung bronchioles & alveoli otherwise it will occupy only anatomical dead space. Distilled water should not be used. Sodium chloride inhalation is used to produce or liquefy sputum (mucus or phlegm) from the mouth to help improve lung function in people with cystic fibrosis, or to collect sputum for medical testing. Nebulization with 3% Hypertonic Normal Saline Solution is a treatment of bronchiolitis in infants. Steroid : Nebuliser Suspension Budesonide 0.5 mg/2 ml / Nebuliser Suspension Budicort / Budison 0.5 mg/2 ml ¤ 6 months – 12 years : 1 ml + 2 ml N/S ¤ >12 years : 2 ml + 1 ml N/S Salbutamol Respirator Solution 5 mg/ml : Sultolin / Azmasol 5 mg/ml Respirator Solution Note : Salbutamol in – ● Respirator Solution 5 mg/ml & ● Nebule / Nebuliser Solution 2.5mg/3ml 1 Nebule = 2.5 mg in 3 mL 6 months – 1 yr = 0.3 ml/dose 1 yr – 5 yr = 0.5 ml/dose > 5 yr = 1 ml/dose 1 ml Solution = 5mg 0.15 – 0.3 mg/kg/dose ● Nebulization with Windel Plus (2.5mg+500mcg) 3ml : ● Nebulization c̄ Windel Plus (wt×0.1 ml) TDS/QDS. : ○ ○ 1 – 4 year = 1 ml + 2 D/W stat & 6 hourly. ○ > 4 year = 1 amp stat & 6 hourly. ○ + N/S : 1 – 2 ml (to make total 3 ml solution) ● Nebulization with Budicort 0.5mg/2ml only, 0.3 to 0.5 ml +1 – 2 ml Windel plus or N/S stat & 12 to 8 hourly, If neonate ( ● Nebulization (stat and 8 hourly) with – ○ Windel Plus : wt×0.1 ml ○ Budicort : wt×0.5 ml Or, ● Nebulization (stat and 6 hourly) with – ○ Salbutamol 5 mg/ml (20 ml) / Sultolin (sq) : wt×0.03 ml ○ Ipratropium Bromide 250 mcg/ml (20 ml) / Iprex (sq) : wt×0.1 ml ○ Add 1 – 2 ml normal saline (N/S) to make total 3 ml fluid.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Tinea Pedis / Athlete's foot Types of tenia pedis : Type Lesions Chronic Intertrigenous Fissuring, scaling or maceration of the interdigital or sub-digital areas Chronic papulosquamous Inflammation and or patchy or diffuse moccasin like scaling over the soles Vesicular or vesiculobullous Vesicles and vesiculopustules near insteps and on mid-anterior planter surface. Acute ulcerative Maceration, weaping, denudation and ulceration of sizeable areas on the soles. Pungent odor is characteristics. Clinical feature : - Itching and burning between toes. - Skin becomes flakey, loose, and breaks. Difference between psoriasis (feet) : Bilateral, keratoderma (thick sole), thick plaque/scale, non itchy. Treatment : Any one topical anti-fungal : ● Benzoic Acid 6% + Salicylic Acid 3% (better efficacy) / ☼ Oint. Whitfield (unique/millat)/ Dermin (jay)/ Bensal (gaco), দিনে ২ – ৩ বার ........... ২১ – ৩০ দিন অথবা, ● Cream Miconazole 2% / Miconex (aci) / Dakrin (gen) / ☼ Topicazole (incp) / Unigal (ops) / Fungidal (sq) cream, দিনে ৩ বার ........... ১৫ দিন অথবা, ● Cream Terbinafine 1% / Telfin (uni) / Elvina (hc) / ☼ Terbex (bex) / Xfin (sq) / Mycofree (drg) / Skinabin (aci), দিনে ৩ বার .......... ১৫ - ৩০ দিন উপদেশ : টাইট মোজা ও জুতা পড়বেন না। আঙ্গুলের ফাঁকগুলো শুষ্ক রাখবেন, ঘামতে দিবেন না। গোসল বা পা ধোয়ার পর পরই মুছে শুকিয়ে নিবেন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Barber's itch (Folliculitis) Tinea Barbae Barber's itch is a staph infection of the hair follicles in the beard area, usually the upper lip. Shaving makes it worse. Tinea barbae usually causes superficial, circular patches & the infection is caused by a fungus. Tinea barbae is similar to barber's itch, but most skin infections in the beard area are caused by bacteria, not fungi. Treatment, ● Topical anti-fungal with antibiotic activity : Benzoic Acid 6% + Salicylic Acid 3% / ☼ Oint. Whitfield (unique/millat) / Dermin (jay) / Bensal (gaco), দিনে ৩ বার ................. ২১ – ৩০ দিন উপদেশ : দাড়ি কাটার সময় উল্টা টান দেওয়া থেকে বিরত থাকবেন।

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Bell's palsy / Facial nerve palsy Possible symptoms of Bell's palsy : ▪ Droopy eyelid, dry eye, or excessive tears. ▪ Facial paralysis twitching, or weakness. ▪ Drooping corner of mouth, dry mouth, impaired taste. Treatment : ● Patient should wear spectacle when go outside. ● Eye should be tapped shut overnight. ● Artificial tear & ointment. ¤ Tearfresh liquigel 10mg/ml, 1 drop in affected eye 4-6 times daily or if dry eye sensation ---- 4 weeks ● Prednisolone 40-60 mg (1 mg kg in morning) daily for 7 – 14 days (should be started within 72 hours) : ¤ Tab. Prednisolone / Cortan (incp) / Deltasone (ren) / Precodil (ops) / Predimax (acm) 20 mg, 2+1+0 (A/M) ----- 7 – 14 days ¤ Cap. Omeprazole / Seclo (sq) / Ometid (ops) / Losectil (skf) / Procetin (bex) 20 mg, 1+0+1 (B/M) ----- 7 – 14 days ± ● Anti-viral (traditional but not recommended) : ¤ Tab. Acyclovir 400 mg / Virus (sq) / Novirux (drg) / Xovir (bacn) 400 mg, 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 ---- 10 days If no recovery within 3 weeks or being complicated refer to neurologist or ophthalmologist or plastic surgeon.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Alzheimer's disease / Dementia Alzheimer's disease is the most common cause of dementia — a gradual decline in memory, thinking, behavior and social skills. - Common in old age ( - Unable to remember recent events. - Temporary loss of remembering address, phone number etc. Investigations : - CT scan : cerebral senile atrophy. Treatment, ► Tab. Donepezil 5 mg / Dimenta (hc) / Ameloss (incp), 0+0+1 --- 1 month, then, 0+0+2 ---- continue. ► Antidepressant in depressive patient.

treatment.title: Features & Treatment
treatment.description: Parkinson's Disease (Refer to a specialist) ¤ Older patient with insidious onset of resting tremor, bradykinesia, rigidity and festinate gait. Patient present with weakness, difficulty in walking. ¤ Rigidity of the limbs present, patient walk by short strides and turning of the patient will be slow. On examination – Appearance: Masked face, Cogwheel rigidity, normal sensory, cerebellar and planter responses. Treatment : For rigidity and bradykinesia : ► Tab. Levodopa 100 mg + Carbidopa 10 mg / Syndopa (sun) / Perkidopa (sq) / Co-dopa (uni) / D-dopa (drg), 1 + 1 + 1 ----- 1 month then titrate according to response 8 or 12 hourly increased up to 1000 mg/day (May start with 50 mg). For tremor : Anticholinergic to improve tremor & rigidity. ► Tab. Trihexyphenidyl / Hexinor (bacn) / Trihexy (incp) 2 mg, 1 + 0 + 0 --- 5 days, then, 1 + 0 + 1 --- 5 days, then, 1 + 1 + 1 ---- continue, then titrate according to 6-10 mg/day, max up to 15 mg/day. Discussion & options – ● Physiotherapy & speech therapy. ● Anticholinergic-improve tremor & rigidity. ¤ Trihexyphenidyl (1-4 mg 8 hourly) [available 1 mg, 2 mg, 4 mg]. ¤ Orphenadrine 50-100 mg 8 hourly. ● Dopamine receptor agonist : ¤ Bromocryptine 1 mg initially then 2.5 mg 8 hourly up to 30 mg/day. ¤ Pergolide 50 µgm (starting dose) increased to 250 µgm 8 hourly up to 3000 μ g/day. ● Anti-dyskinetic medicine : Amantadine (dose 100 mg, 8 or 12 hrly), to control dyskinesia produced by dopaminergic treatment. ● COMT inhibitors : Entacapone 200 mg with each dose of levodopa. This prolonged the effect of levodopa & allows levodopa dose to be reduced & given less frequently.

treatment.title: Dark spot in face or Scar mark of injury or surgery
treatment.description: ► Cream Fluocinolon 0.01%+Hydroquinone 4%+Tretinoin 0.05% / Cream Malasin (phar) / Melatrin (zis) / Melano (sq) / Nospot (skf) Apply BD over the affected part –––––– 2 to 6 months May use twice daily (BD) if scar area is covered beneath clothing but if in exposed area better to use once daily (OD) at night, as it is advised to avoid sunlight after use of that cream.

treatment.title: Hair shedding
treatment.description: Common Hair Fall Disorder Most commonly – ¤ Telogen effluvium / Excessive hair shedding. ¤ Alopecia Areata. ¤ Androgenic Alopecia. Telogen effluvium / Hair shedding In a normal healthy person's scalp, about 85% of the hair follicles are actively growing hair (anagen hair) and about 15% are resting hair (telogen hair). It is normal to lose up to 100 hairs a day on one's comb, brush, in the basin or on the pillow, as a result of the normal scalp hair cycle. Telogen effluvium is excessive hair shedding from all over scalp and non-scarring form of diffuse hair loss with no clinical or histological evidence of inflammation and can affect up to 50% of the scalp hair. Complaints : Ø Hair thinning involves the entire scalp ± loss of other body hair. Ø Short hairs of normal thickness. Ø Diffuse thinning without focal areas of total alopecia. Ø A gentle hair pull test reveals an increased number of hairs. Telogen effluvium is self-correcting. Recommendations include : ● Gentle handling of the hair, avoiding over-vigorous combing, brushing and any type of scalp massage. Avoid parlor treatment. ● Take treatment of any underlying scalp disorder or hormonal problem determined, if any. ● Correct any abnormality in thyroid function, or levels of iron, vitamin B12 and folic acid. ● Ensure a nutritious diet, with plenty of protein, fruit and vegetables. Avoid crush dieting and keto-diet. ● Avoid stress & adopt relaxation therapy.

treatment.title: Alopecia areata
treatment.description: Complaints : ● Alopecia areata is a sudden, well-defined, localised patchy loss of hair, reversible, recurrent, non-scarring type, occurring in sharply defined areas and usually involving the scalp or beard, eyebrows, eyelashes but can affect any hair bearing area. ● Usually the hair loss is asymmetric and it may be accompanied by burning, stinging, tenderness or pain. Investigation : Alopecia areata is an autoimmune disease, so rule out DM, Thyroid abnormalities / Grave's disease, Nutritional deficiency or Pernicious anaemia. Treatment of alopecia areata : ► Oint Clobetasol Propionate 0.05% / Exovate (bex) / Dermasol (sq)/ Clovate (aci)/ Xenovate (uni)/ Topiclo (skf)/ Nyclobate (incp) Apply in affected part BD ––––––––––––––––––––– 3 – 6 months ► Scalp Solution Minoxidil 2% or 5% / Splendora (sq) / Trugain (zis) / Minoxil (ops) / Xenogrow (incp) / Hairgain (uni) / Regrow (tech) 5% Apply 1 ml (7 sprays) of solution twice daily ––––– 3 – 6 months [Minoxidil foam formulation is less irritant than solution. But costly & less available.] ± ¤ Multivitamins : acts placebo, effective only if true nutritional deficiencies present. But traditionally may use any of these – ► Tab. Biotin (Vitamin B7) 1000 mcg / Floriz (rad) / Biotin (bacn) / Biotab (ibn) / Cap. Bioprem (sq), 0 + 1 + 0 ––––––– 1 to 12 weeks Or, ► Cap. Cholecalciferol [Vit-D3] 20k IU / D-Rise (bex) / D-Cap (drg)/ D-Balance (sq)/ D-Vine (hc)/ D3 (aci)/ Defrol (acm) 20000IU One capsule weekly (on friday) –––––––––– 6 to 12 weeks Or, ► Cap. Vit-E/ Tocopherol 400 IU / E-Cap (drg) / Evit (sq) / E-Gel (ren) / Nature-E (uni) / E 400 (bris), 0+1+0 –––– 1 to 3 weeks ¤ Intra lesional Triamcinolone Acetonide in severe case / failed cases : Inj. Trialon (drg) / Cenolon (incp) / Trimcort (che) / Alfacort (zis) ----- Inject on the lesion using insulin syringe once in a month (4 weeks apart) for 1 – 5 times as per response. ¤ Minoxidil foam formulation is less irritant than solution. But costly & less available. Systemic treatments : 1. Psoralen plus UV-A 2. Systemic steroids 3. Methotrexate 4. Cyclosporine 5. Tacrolimus. Topical Tacrolimus 0.1% / Remus (sq) can be also used, apply at affected part BD --------- 1 month

treatment.title: Androgenic Alopecia / Male Type Baldness
treatment.description: Features of baldness : ¤ Androgenic alopecia starts gradually. Men present with gradual thinning in the temporal areas, producing a reshaping of the anterior (frontal) part of the hairline. ¤ Women with androgenic alopecia usually present with diffuse thinning on the crown (along midline). In general women maintain a frontal hairline. Types : Two types : Male-pattern hair loss (MPHL) & Female-pattern hair loss (FPHL). Androgenetic alopecia is a hair losing condition that primarily affects the top (crown / vertex) and front (frontal) of the scalp in male-pattern hair loss (MPHL). Female-pattern hair loss (FPHL) typically presents as a diffuse thinning of the hair across the crown / midline of entire scalp. Investigation ¤ If a thyroid disorder is suspected – Serum TSH, T3, T4. ¤ Serum De-hydro-epi-androsterone sulfate (DHEAS) and Serum Testosterone is needed to confirm virilization evident (androgens) in women. Management ► Scalp Solution Minoxidil 2% or 5% / Splendora (sq) / Trugain (zis) / Minoxil (ops) / Xenogrow (incp) / Hairgain (uni) / Regrow (tech) 5% Apply 1 ml (7 sprays) of solution twice daily ––––– 1 – 3 months ► Tab. Finasteride / Pronor 5 mg (sq) / Recur 1 mg (bex)-unavailable, Tab. Finasteride 1 mg once daily for 1 to 3 months, if improve then Continue, stop if side effects observed (Like gynecomastia, Low libido, erectile dysfunction etc.). Better to avoid in males. ± ¤ Multivitamins : acts placebo, effective only if true nutritional deficiencies present. But traditionally may use any of these – ► Tab. Biotin (Vitamin B7) 1000 mcg / Floriz (rad) / Biotin (bacn) / Biotab (ibn) / Cap. Bioprem (sq), 0 + 1 + 0 ––––––– 1 to 12 weeks Or, ► Cap. Cholecalciferol [Vit-D3] 20k IU / D-Rise (bex) / D-Cap (drg)/ D-Balance (sq)/ D-Vine (hc)/ D3 (aci)/ Defrol (acm) 20000IU One capsule weekly (on friday) –––––––––– 6 to 12 weeks Or, ► Cap. Vit-E / Tocopherol 400 IU / E-Cap (drg) / Evit (sq) / E-Gel (ren) / Nature-E (uni) / E 400 (bris), 0+1+0 –––– 1 to 3 weeks Refer to dermatologist : ¤ Non responsive hair fall / baldness cases. ¤ Seeking aesthetic procedures. ¤ Psychological burdensome cases. ¤ Hair fall / baldness thyroid or connective tissue disorders. ¤ Alopecia totalis & alopecia universalis. Complete loss of scalp hair is called alopecia totalis and complete loss of all hair as alopecia universalis. If not improved procedural treatment : ¤ Autologous hair transplants ¤ Platelet rich plasma therapy (PRP) ¤ Micro needling therapy (MNT) ¤ Stem cell therapy ¤ Mesotherapy ¤ Micropigmentation

treatment.title: Premature graying of hair (PGH)
treatment.description: Graying of hair also called canities or achromotrichia occurs with normal aging. As graying of hair is perceived as a sign of old age, premature graying of hair (PGH) can bear an adverse effect on the self-esteem of the individual. Particular diseases can cause premature graying, including vitiligo and alopecia areata, treatment of underlying condition is needed. Treatment : Natural hair dye + Multivitamin (especially B7, B12 & vit-E), if applicable avoid smoking.

treatment.title: Trichology : Common Hair Fall Disorder
treatment.description: Most commonly – ¤ Telogen effluvium / Excessive hair shedding. ¤ Alopecia Areata. ¤ Androgenic Alopecia.
